english_verse: “A virgin will have a baby boy, and he will be called Immanuel,” which means “God is with us.”
translation_verse: “Hwɛ, babunu he bɛfa afuro na wawo ba beema, na bɛato ne dini sɛ Immanuel,” asekyerɛ ne sɛ, .

english_verse: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
translation_verse: na Asor woo Sadok, na Sadok woo Akim, na Akim woo Elihud,

english_verse: This is how Jesus Christ was born. A young woman named Mary was engaged to Joseph from King David's family. But before they were married, she learned that she was going to have a baby by God's Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Afei kwane a bɛfaa so woo Yesu Kristo di: Berɛ a bɛde ne maame Maria maa Yosef sɛ asiwa, na bennya ntenaa sɛ awarefoɔ he, behuu sɛ Maria afa afuro a ofiri Honhom Kronkron.

english_verse: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.
translation_verse: Ɔnam sɛ na Yosef a ɔyɛ n'asiwa yerɛ he yɛ nipa tenenee na ɔmpɛ sɛ obegu Maria anim ase badwam nti, ɔbɔɔ ne tim pɔ sɛ obegyae no kokoam.

english_verse: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
translation_verse: Nso berɛ a ɔyɔɔ sɛ obetu saa anammɔn he pɛ ne Nyankopɔn bɔfoɔ yii ne ho adi kyerɛɛ no daeɛso mu, kaa sɛ, “Yosef, Dawid ba, nsuro sɛ wobɛfa Maria a ɔyɛ w'asiwa-yerɛ he akɔ wo fie, efiri sɛ, abɔfra a ɔnyem no he firi Honhom Kronkron.

english_verse: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
translation_verse: Ɔbɛwo ba beema, na to ne dini Yesu, efiri sɛ ɔno ne bɛgye ne nkurofoɔ afiri bɛ bɔne mu.”

english_verse: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
translation_verse: Yei nyinaa siiɛ sɛdeɛ asɛm a Awurade nam diyifoɔ he so kaeɛ he bɛbam sɛ,

english_verse: After Joseph woke up, he and Mary were soon married, just as the Lord's angel had told him to do.
translation_verse: Berɛ a Yosef nyane firii ne nna mu he, ɔyɔɔ sɛdeɛ Awurade bɔfoɔ he hyɛɛ no sɛ ɔnyɔ he; na ɔfaa Maria baa ne nkyɛne sɛ ne yerɛ,

english_verse: But they did not sleep together before her baby was born. Then Joseph named him Jesus.
translation_verse: nso wamfa ne ho anka no kosii sɛ ɔwoo ba beema; na ɔtoo ne dini Yesu.

english_verse: When Jesus was born in the village of Bethlehem in Judea, Herod was king. During this time some wise men from the east came to Jerusalem
translation_verse: Hene Herode berɛ so he, bɛwoo Yesu Betlehem a ɔya Yudea. Na yei akyi he, nsoromma mu animdefoɔ bi firi apieeɛ baa Yerusalem,

english_verse: and said, “Where is the child born to be king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
translation_verse: bebisaa sɛ, “Hefa ne Yudafoɔ hene a bɛawo no he ya? Yɛahu ne nsoromma apieɛ, na yɛaba sɛ yɛɛbɛkoto no.”

english_verse: When King Herod heard about this, he was worried, and so was everyone else in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Berɛ a Herode a ɔyɛ hene he teeɛ he, ɔne Yerusalemfoɔ nyinaa akoma tuiɛ;

english_verse: Herod brought together the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses and asked them, “Where will the Messiah be born?”
translation_verse: na ɔfrɛɛ asɔfoɔ mpanyimfoɔ ne mane he mu atwerɛfoɔ nyinaa bisaa bɛ baabi a ɔsɛ sɛ bɛwo Kristo he.

english_verse: They told him, “He will be born in Bethlehem, just as the prophet wrote,
translation_verse: Bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, ɔsɛ sɛ bɛwo no Betlehem a ɔya Yudea, sɛnea adiyifoɔ he bɛatwerɛ ato hɔ sɛ:

english_verse: ‘Bethlehem in the land of Judea, you are very important among the towns of Judea. From your town will come a leader, who will be like a shepherd for my people Israel.’ ”
translation_verse: “Na wo, Betlehem a woya Yuda asaase so, wonyɛ kumaa Yuda amane mufoɔ mu, efiri sɛ wo mu ne bɛbɛfrɛ safohene afiri a ɔbɛyɛ dwanhwɛfoɔ ama me mane Israel.”

english_verse: Herod secretly called in the wise men and asked them when they had first seen the star.
translation_verse: Afei Herode frɛɛ nsoromma mu animdefoɔ he kokoam bisaa bɛ berɛ pɔtee a saa nsoromma he pieeɛ.

english_verse: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsomaa bɛ Yerusalem, kaa sɛ, “Honkɔhwehwɛ abɔfra he yie; na sɛ hohu no a, hommɛka nkyerɛ me na me nso menkɔkoto no bi.”

english_verse: The wise men listened to what the king said and then left. And the star they had seen in the east went on ahead of them until it stopped over the place where the child was.
translation_verse: Na betiee hene he nsɛm he wieeɛ he, befirii hɔ; na hɔaa, nsoromma a opieeɛ he dii bɛ anim, kosii sɛ okogyinaa baabi a abɔfra he ya.

english_verse: They were thrilled and excited to see the star.
translation_verse: Behuu sɛ nsoromma he agyina he, bɛ ani gye boroo so.

english_verse: When the men went into the house and saw the child with Mary, his mother, they knelt down and worshiped him. They took out their gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh and gave them to him.
translation_verse: Bewuraa fie a na abɔfra he yam he mu he, behuu ɔne ne maame Maria, na bɛkoto sɔree no. Afei, bebiee bɛ kotokuo mu yii sikakɔkɔɔ, krobo, ne aduhwam de yɔɔ no ayɛ.

english_verse: Later they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, and they went back home by another road. The Escape to Egypt
translation_verse: Na bɛbɔɔ bɛ kɔkɔ daeɛ mu sɛ bɛnnsane nkɔ Herode hɔ bio he, bɛfaa kwane foforɔ so kɔɔ bɛ kurom. Yosef de abɔfra he dwane kɔɔ Misraim

english_verse: After the wise men had gone, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up! Hurry and take the child and his mother to Egypt! Stay there until I tell you to return, because Herod is looking for the child and wants to kill him.”
translation_verse: Na nsoromma mu animdifoɔ he firii hɔ he, Nyankopɔn bɔfoɔ yii ne ho adi kyerɛɛ Yosef ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Sɔre, fa abɔfra he ne ne maame, na honnwane nkɔ Misraim, na hontena hɔ nkosi sɛ mɛka bibi akyerɛ ho bio ɔfa yei ho; efiri sɛ Herode ɔɔpɛ abɔfra he aku no.”

english_verse: That night, Joseph got up and took his wife and the child to Egypt,
translation_verse: Na Yosef sɔreeɛ, na ɔfaa abɔfra he ne ne maame anadwo he, na bɛkɔɔ Misraim,

english_verse: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
translation_verse: na bɛtenaa hɔ kosii sɛ Herode wuiɛ. Yei maa deɛ Awurade nam diyifoɔ he so kaeɛ he baa mu sɛ, “Misraim ne mafrɛ me ba he afiri.” Bekuu mmɔfra

english_verse: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
translation_verse: Na Herode huu sɛ nsoromma mu animdefoɔ he adaadaa no he, ne bo fuu kɛse paa, na ɔsoma maa bekokuu mmɔfra mmeema a benni mmoroo mfie mmienu nyinaa a bɛya Yerusalem ne ne mpɔtamu, sɛnea berɛ a ohu firii nsoromma mu animudefoɔ he hɔ kyerɛ he.

english_verse: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
translation_verse: Na diyifoɔ Yeremia asɛm he baam sɛ:

english_verse: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
translation_verse: “Bɛtee nne bi Rama, su ne adwotwa dendene, Rahel oosu ne mma, ɔmma kwane mma ne werɛ nkyekyere, efiri sɛ benni hɔ bio.” Yosef ne abɔfra he firii Misraim sane baeɛ

english_verse: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
translation_verse: Na Herode wuiɛ he, hwɛ, Awurade bɔfoɔ yii ne ho adi daeɛ mu kyerɛɛ Yosef Misraim na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ,

english_verse: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
translation_verse: “Sɔre, fa abɔfra he ne ne maame, kɔ Israel mane mu, bɛ a bɛɛpɛ abɔfra he aku no he nyinaa awuwu.”

english_verse: Joseph got up and left with them for Israel.
translation_verse: Na Yosef sɔre faa abɔfra he ne ne maame, de bɛkɔɔ Israel asaase so.

english_verse: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
translation_verse: Nso ɔtee sɛ Arkelao asi ne papa Herode anane mu oodi adeɛ Yudea he, osuroo sɛ ɔbɛkɔ hɔ. Na afei, bɛbɔɔ no kɔkɔ daeɛ mu he, ɔkɔɔ Galilea mansinim.

english_verse: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “He will be called a Nazarene.”
translation_verse: Na ɔkɔtenaa baabi a bɛfrɛ hɔ Nasaret, nti deɛ diyifoɔ he kaeɛ he baam sɛ, “Bɛbɛfrɛ no Nasareni.”

english_verse: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
translation_verse: “Honsakra ho adwene, na soro ahennie he abɛne.”

english_verse: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
translation_verse: Na Yohane fira ntoma a bɛde yoma nwii ayɔ a nwoma aboomu bɔ n’asenem, na aduane a odi ne mmɛbɛ ne wuram woɔ.

english_verse: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.
translation_verse: Na Yerusalemfoɔ ne Yudeafoɔ nyinaa ɔne bɛ a bɛte Yordan agya nyinaa kɔɔ ne nkyɛne,

english_verse: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
translation_verse: na bɛba bɛkaa bɛ bɔne ma ɔbɔɔ bɛ asu Asubɔntene Yordan mu.

english_verse: Many Pharisees and Sadducees also came to be baptized. But John said to them: You bunch of snakes! Who warned you to run from the coming judgment?
translation_verse: Nso ohuu Farisifoɔ ne Sadukifoɔ sɛ bɛɛba abɛbɔ asu he, ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Ho nankatia mma! Hwane ne bɔɔ ho kɔkɔ sɛ honnwane mfiri abufuo a ɔɔba he ho?

english_verse: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
translation_verse: Mpo seesei bɛde akuma ato dua he nhini ase; nti dua biaa a ɔnnso aba he bebetwa na bɛato atwene gya mu.

english_verse: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: Okita ne huhuammoa ne nsam, na ɔbɛpra n’ayuporobea hɔ na woaboaboa n’ayuo ano akogu pata so; nso ntɛ he deɛ, ɔde gya a onnum da bɛhye ne nyinaa.” Yesu asubɔ (Marko

english_verse: Jesus left Galilee and went to the Jordan River to be baptized by John.
translation_verse: Afei Yesu firii Galilea baa Yohane nkyɛne Yordan ho, sɛ Yohane mmɔ no asu.

english_verse: But John kept objecting and said, “I ought to be baptized by you. Why have you come to me?”
translation_verse: Nso Yohane pɛɛ sɛ osi no kwane, na ɔkaa sɛ, “Me ne ɔsɛ sɛ wobɔ me asu, ne wo mmom wooba me nkyɛne yi?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “For now this is how it should be, because we must do all God wants us to do.” Then John agreed.
translation_verse: Nso Yesu buaa no sɛ, “Ma ɔnyɛ saa, ɔyɛ ma yɛ sɛ yɛhyɛ tenenee nyinaa ma.” Na Yohane penee so maa no.

english_verse: So Jesus was baptized. And as soon as he came out of the water, the sky opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down on him like a dove.
translation_verse: Na ɔbɔɔ Yesu asu wieeɛ he, na opie firii nsuo he mu a pɛ, amonom hɔaa ne soro bie maa no na ohuu sɛ Nyankopɔn Honhom oosiane sɛ abuburo ɔɔba ne so.

english_verse: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
translation_verse: Na nne bi firi soro kaa sɛ, “Yei ne me dɔ Ba a ɔsɔ m’ani.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
translation_verse: Na afei Yesu tee sɛ bɛakye Yohane he, osiim kɔɔ Galilea.

english_verse: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
translation_verse: Ofirii Nasaret na ɔkɔtenaa Kapernaum po he ho, Sebulon ne Naftali, mpɔtamu,

english_verse: After Jesus had gone without eating for
translation_verse: Odii mmuada adaduannan, awia ne anadwo, na yei akyi he kɔm dee no.

english_verse: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
translation_verse: Na sɔhwɛfoɔ he ba bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Sɛ wo ne Nyankopɔn ba he a, hyɛ ma aboɔ yi nnane paanoo.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “The Scriptures also say, ‘Don't try to test the Lord your God!’ ”
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Bɛatwerɛ bio sɛ, ‘Nsɔ Awurade wo Nyankopɔn nhwɛ.’ ”

english_verse: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
translation_verse: Afei nso, Bronsam faa no de no kosii bepɔ tentene bi atifi, na ɔkyerɛɛ no wiase ahennie ne mu afɛfɛdeɛ nyinaa,

english_verse: The devil said to him, “I will give all this to you, if you will bow down and worship me.”
translation_verse: na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Sɛ wobɛkoto asɔre me a, nka mede saa nnoɔma yi nyinaa bɛma wo.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Go away Satan! The Scriptures say: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve only him.’ ”
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ: Firi hɔ, Satan! Na bɛatwerɛ sɛ: “Awurade wo Nyankopɔn nkoaa ne sɔre no, na ɔno nkoaa nso ne som no.”

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said,
translation_verse: sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a nkɔmhyɛ a ɔnam diyifoɔ Yesaia so baeɛ he bɛba mu sɛ:

english_verse: “Listen, lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, lands along the road to the sea and across the Jordan. Listen Galilee, land of the Gentiles!
translation_verse: “Sebulon asaase, Naftali asaase, po ho kwane so, Yordan agya amanamane mufoɔ Galilea—

english_verse: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
translation_verse: Amonom hɔaa begyaa bɛ asau he hɔ kodii n’akyi.

english_verse: While Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw two brothers. One was Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting their net into the lake.
translation_verse: Na Yesu nam Galilea po ho he, ohuu anuanom mmienu, Simon, a bɛfrɛ no Petro, ɔne ne nua Andrea, sɛ beegu asau po he mu—efiri sɛ na bɛyɛ apofofoɔ.

english_verse: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
translation_verse: Na ofirii hɔ he, ohuu anuanom mmienu bi, Yakobo, Sebedeo ba ne ne nua Yohane, sɛ bɛ ne bɛ papa Sebedeo te kodoɔ mu a beesiesie bɛ asau, na ɔfrɛɛ bɛ.

english_verse: At once they left the boat and their father and went with Jesus. Jesus Teaches, Preaches, and Heals (Luke
translation_verse: Amonom hɔaa begyaa bɛ kodoɔ ne bɛ papa hɔ, na bekodii n’akyi. Yesu kaa Nyankopɔn asɛm na ɔsaa yareɛ ya Galilea

english_verse: Jesus went all over Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and preaching the good news about God's kingdom. He also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
translation_verse: Yesu kyinkyinii Galilea nyinaa, kyerɛkyerɛɛ bɛ hyia adane mu, na ɔpae mu kaa ahennie he ho asɛmpa na ɔsaa bɛ a bɛyare nyinaa yadeɛ.

english_verse: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
translation_verse: Nti ne ho asɛm trɛɛ Siria nyinaa, na bɛde ayarefoɔ, bɛ a bɛyare yadeɛ ahodoɔ ne bɛ a bɛ honam tutu bɛ, ne bɛ a honhom fi ne twerɛ ya bɛ so, ɔne mmubuafoɔ nyinaa, baa ne nkyɛne. Na ɔsaa bɛ nyinaa yadeɛ.

english_verse: Large crowds followed Jesus from Galilee and the region around the ten cities known as Decapolis. They also came from Jerusalem, Judea, and from across the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Na nkurofoɔ akuoakuo pii firi Galilea, Dekapoli, Yerusalem, Yudea, ne Yordan agya nyinaa dii n’akyi.

english_verse: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on the side of a mountain and sat down. Blessings (Luke
translation_verse: Berɛ a Yesu huu nnipadɔm he, ɔkɔɔ bepɔ he so; na ɔtenaa ase he, n’asuafoɔ baa ne nkyɛne.

english_verse: You must obey God's commands better than the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law obey them. If you don't, I promise you will never get into the kingdom of heaven. Anger
translation_verse: Meeka akyerɛ ho, sɛ ho tenenee ammoro atwerɛfoɔ ne Farisifoɔ bɛ deɛ so a, honnya soro ahennie mu kwane da. Abufuo ho nkyerɛkyerɛ

english_verse: So if you are about to place your gift on the altar and remember that someone is angry with you,
translation_verse: Nti sɛ wode afɔrebɔdeɛ ba afɔrebokyia ho, na wokae sɛ wo nua wɔ bibi tia wo a,

english_verse: God blesses those people who depend only on him. They belong to the kingdom of heaven!
translation_verse: “Nhyira ne bɛ a ahia bɛ honhom mu, efiri sɛ bɛ ne soro ahennie he yɛ bɛ dea.

english_verse: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
translation_verse: Nhyira ne bɛ a bedi awerɛhoɔ, efiri sɛ bɛ ne bebenya awerɛkyekyerɛ.

english_verse: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
translation_verse: Nhyira ne bɛ a bɛdwo, efiri sɛ bɛ ne bebedi asaase he so.

english_verse: God blesses those people who are merciful. They will be treated with mercy!
translation_verse: Nhyira ne bɛ a behu afoforɔ mmɔbɔ, efiri sɛ bɛ ne bebehu bɛ mmɔbɔ.

english_verse: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
translation_verse: Nhyira ne bɛ a bɛ akoma mu te, efiri sɛ bɛ ne bebehu Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
translation_verse: Hoyɛ wiase hann. Kuro a ɔda mmepɔ so ntumi nhinta.

english_verse: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
translation_verse: Na me deɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, Nka ntam koraa, mfa soro nka ntam, efiri sɛ ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn ahennwa,

english_verse: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
translation_verse: saa nso ne mfa asaase nka ntam, efiri sɛ ɔyɛ ne nane ntiasoɔ; mfa Yerusalem nso nka ntam, efiri sɛ ɔyɛ Hene kɛseɛ he ahenkuro.

english_verse: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
translation_verse: Hone asaase yi so nkyene; nso sɛ nkyene dɛ yera a, ɔbɛyɛ dɛ na ayɛ nkyene bio? Bɛmmfa nnyɔ hwee bio, gye sɛ bɛto gu ma betiatia so.

english_verse: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
translation_verse: Nipa biaa nsɔ kanea mmfa nnhyɛ atam ase, na mmom bɛde si kanea dua so, na ahyerɛn ama nnipa a bɛya fie hɔ nyinaa.

english_verse: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
translation_verse: Honnwene sɛ maaba sɛ meebeyi mmara ne adiyifoɔ he hɔ; mamma sɛ meebeyi no hɔ, na mmom maaba sɛ meebɛma no aba mu pefee.

english_verse: You know our ancestors were told, “Do not murder” and “A murderer must be brought to trial.”
translation_verse: “Hoate sɛ bɛka kyerɛɛ tetefoɔ he sɛ, ‘Honni awu; na nea odi awu biaa bedi fɔ atemmuo mu.’

english_verse: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
translation_verse: Na me deɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, sɛ wo bo fu wo nua a, wobedi fɔ atemmuo mu; wodi wo nua atɛm sɛ nipahunu a wobedi fɔ mpanyimfoɔ atitire he asɛniiɛ; na sɛ woka sɛ, ‘Woyɛ kwasea’ a, wobedi fɔ akɔ gya a onnum da mu.

english_verse: leave your gift there in front of the altar. Make peace with that person, then come back and offer your gift to God.
translation_verse: nea odi kane a ɔsɛ sɛ woyɔ ne sɛ, gya w’afɔrebɔdeɛ he afɔrebokyia he so na wo ne no nkobom, na ɔno akyi he woasane aba abɛbɔ w’afɔdeɛ.

english_verse: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
translation_verse: Sɛ wo ne wo soboɔbɔni nam kwane so ɔɔkɔ asennibea a, yɛ ntɛm na wo ne no mmom ansa na wo ne no aduru asennibea hɔ, anyɛ saa a wo soboɔbɔni he de wo bɛhyɛ temufoɔ nsa, na temufoɔ he so de wo ahyɛ wɛmfoɔ nsa, na ɔde wo akɔto afiase.

english_verse: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
translation_verse: Nɔkorɛ, meeka akyerɛ ho, wonnya kwane mmfiri kosi sɛ wobetua wo kaprɛ a aka wo nsam he ansa.” Awaresɛeɛ ho nkyerɛkyerɛ

english_verse: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
translation_verse: “Afei nso, bɛkaa sɛ, ‘Biaa a obegyae ne yerɛ he, ɔmma no awaregyaeɛ krataa.’

english_verse: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
translation_verse: “Bio, hoate sɛ bɛka kyerɛɛ tetefoɔ he sɛ, ‘Nka ntanhunu, nso fa wo ntankadeɛ ma Awurade.’

english_verse: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
translation_verse: Na ma nea wobɛka sɛ ɔyɛ ‘ɛɛ, he, nyɛ ɛɛ’, na nea wobɛka sɛ ‘daabi, he nso nyɛ daabi’; yei akyi ade foforɔ biaa firi bɔne mu. Awerɛtɔ ho nkyerɛkyerɛ

english_verse: You know you have been taught, “An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.”
translation_verse: “Hoate sɛ bɛkaa sɛ, ‘ani nsi ani ananem, na sẽ nso nsi sẽ ananem.’

english_verse: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
translation_verse: Na me deɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, Ho ne bɔnefoɔ nni asie. Nso sɛ bi pae w’aso nifa mu a, dane baako he nso ma no;

english_verse: If someone sues you for your shirt, give up your coat as well.
translation_verse: sɛ bi pɛ sɛ ɔne wo di asɛm na ɔfa w’ataadeɛ ngusoɔ a, fa deɛ ɔhyɛ aseɛ he so ka no ho;

english_verse: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
translation_verse: na sɛ bi hyɛ wo sɛ wo ne no nkɔ borɔfo-kwansini baako a, kɔ borɔfo-kwansini mmienu.

english_verse: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
translation_verse: Adeɛ biaa a bi bɛsrɛ afiri wo hɔ he, fa ma no, na deɛ ɔfɛm bibi firi wo hɔ biaa nkame no. Dɔ w’atamfoɔ

english_verse: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
translation_verse: “Hoate sɛ bɛkaa sɛ, ‘Dɔ wo yɔnko na tane w’atamfoɔ.’

english_verse: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
translation_verse: Na me deɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, Honnɔ ho atamfoɔ na hommɔ mpaeɛ mma bɛ a bɛtaa ho,

english_verse: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
translation_verse: saa ne bɛma ho ayɛ ho Agya a ɔya soro he mma; efiri sɛ ɔma awia pie abɔnefoɔ ne ateneneefoɔ so, na ɔtɔ nsuo gu ateneneefoɔ ne bɛ a bɛnnyɛ ateneneefoɔ so.

english_verse: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
translation_verse: Na sɛ hodɔ bɛ a bɛdɔ ho a, mmɛ akatua ne hobenya? Togyefoɔ mpo nnyɔ saa anaa?

english_verse: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
translation_verse: Na sɛ ho kyea ho nuanom nkoaa a, mmɛ ne ho ayɔ akyɛne binom? Amanamane mufoɔ mpo nnyɔ saa?

english_verse: But you must always act like your Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Honyɛ pɛ, efiri sɛ, ho Agya ɔya soro he yɛ pɛ.

english_verse: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
translation_verse: “Honhwɛ yie na hoannyɔ ho tenenee nnipa anim sɛ bebehu ho; sɛ hoyɔ saa a hoonnya akatua ho Agya a ɔya soro he hɔ.

english_verse: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
translation_verse: Na sɛ wooyɔ adɔeɛ a, mma wo nsa benkum nnhu deɛ wo nsa nifa ɔɔyɔ,

english_verse: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
translation_verse: “Na sɛ hoobɔ mpaeɛ a honnyɔ sɛ nyaatwomfoɔ he; efiri sɛ bɛpɛ sɛ begyina hyiadane mu ne mmɔntene ntweaso bɔ mpaeɛ, sɛ nnipa behu bɛ. Nɔkorɛ, meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, bɛanya bɛ akatua dada.

english_verse: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
translation_verse: Na wo deɛ, sɛ woobɔ mpaeɛ a, kɔhyɛ wo dane mu na to wo ho pono mu na bɔ mpaeɛ kyerɛ w’Agya a ɔya kokoam he; na w’Agya a ohu kokoam he betua wo ka.

english_verse: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
translation_verse: “Sɛ woobɔ mpaeɛ a, nka nsɛm pii kwa sɛ abosonsomfoɔ he; efiri sɛ besusu sɛ bɛkasaa dodoɔ nti bebetie bɛ.

english_verse: Don't be like them. Your Father knows what you need even before you ask.
translation_verse: Na ho deɛ honnyɔ sɛ bɛ, efiri sɛ ho Agya nim deɛ ohia ho ansa na hoobisa no.

english_verse: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
translation_verse: Nti hommɔ mpaeɛ sɛ: Yɛ Agya a woya soro, anidie nka wo dini,

english_verse: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
translation_verse: W’ahennie mmra. Wopɛ nyɛ hɔ, asaase yi so sɛdeɛ ɔteɛ soro.

english_verse: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
translation_verse: Na sɛ hode bɛ a bɛfom ho he mfomsoɔ kyɛ bɛ a, ho Agya a ɔya soro he nso de ho mfomsoɔ bɛkyɛ ho;

english_verse: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
translation_verse: nso sɛ hoammfa afoforɔ bɛ mfomsoɔ ankyɛ bɛ a, ho Agya a ɔya soro he nso ɔmmfa ho mfomsoɔ nkyɛ ho. Mmuadadie ho nkyerɛkyerɛ

english_verse: Instead, comb your hair and wash your face.
translation_verse: Nso sɛ woodi mmuada a, sra wo tim, na hohoro w’anim,

english_verse: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
translation_verse: sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a nnipa onnhu sɛ woodi abuada gye sɛ w’Agya a ɔya kokoam he; na w’Agya a ohu kokoam he betua wo ka. Soro Agyapadeɛ ho nkyerɛkyerɛ

english_verse: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
translation_verse: Nso honni kane nhwehwɛ Nyankopɔn ahennie ne ne tenenee, na ɔde yeinom nyinaa nso bɛka ho ho.

english_verse: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
translation_verse: “Nti honnwene kyena ho, efiri sɛ haw a ɔya kyena mu he, dɔɔso ma kyena heaa. Haw a ɔya da biaa mu he dɔɔso ma da heaa.”

english_verse: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
translation_verse: “Honnhyehyɛ agyapadeɛ asaase yi so, baabi a mfɔteɛ ne naakyee sɛe no na akorɔmfoɔ bubu kowia;

english_verse: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
translation_verse: na mmom honhyehyɛ ho agyapadeɛ soro baabi a mfɔteɛ anaa naakyee ntumi nsɛe no anaa akorɔmfoɔ ntumi mmubu nkowia,

english_verse: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
translation_verse: efiri sɛ baabi a w’agyapadeɛ ya he, hɔ ne w’akoma nso ya. Akoma mu hann

english_verse: But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. If the light inside you is dark, you surely are in the dark. Money (Luke
translation_verse: nso sɛ w’ani nyɛ a nipadua he nyinaa bɛyɛ sum. Nti sɛ hann a ɔya ho mu he yɛ sum a, he deɛ sum he bɛyɛ kɛse paa! Nyankopɔn ne ahonyadeɛ

english_verse: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
translation_verse: “Akoa biaa ntumi nsom awuranom mmienu; efiri sɛ ɔbɛtane baako na wadɔ baako, anaa ɔde ne ho bɛbata baako na waabu baako animtia. Hontumi nsom Nyankopɔn ne ahonyadeɛ.” Honnwennwene (Luka

english_verse: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
translation_verse: Ne saa nti honnwennwene sɛ, Mmɛ ne yebedi anaa Mmɛ ne yɛbɛnom anaa Ɛɛne ne yebefira?

english_verse: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
translation_verse: Nti meeka akyerɛ ho, honnwennwene ho kra ho, deɛ hobedi anaa deɛ hobɛnom, anaa ho nipadua ho, ɔfa deɛ hobɛhyɛ ho. Nkwa nsom bo nkyɛne aduane, na nipadua nkyɛne ntoma anaa?

english_verse: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
translation_verse: Honhwɛ nnomaa a bɛya wiem; bennua, bentwa, na bɛmmoa hwee ano ngu pata so, nso ho Agya a ɔya soro he mma bɛ aduane di. Honsom bo nkyɛne bɛ anaa?

english_verse: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
translation_verse: Mmɛ nti ne hodwene nea hobefira ho? Honhwɛ wuram asukooko, sɛdeɛ benyini; bɛnnyɔ adwuma na bɛnnto asaawa,

english_verse: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
translation_verse: nso meeka akyerɛ ho, Salomo animuonyam nyinaa akyi he, wanhyehyɛ ne ho sɛ bɛ mu baako mpo.

english_verse: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
translation_verse: Nso sɛ Nyankopɔn fira wura a, ɔya hɔ nnɛ na kyena onni hɔ na bɛatwa ato fononoo mu ahye no, ntoma a, he deɛ ontumi mfira ho ntoma mma no mmoro so anaa, ho a ho gyidie sua?

english_verse: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛ amanamane mufoɔ ne bɛpre yeinom nyinaa ho; na nɔkorɛ, ho Agya a ɔya soro he nim sɛ hohia yeinom nyinaa.

english_verse: Don't condemn others, and God won't condemn you.
translation_verse: Hommmu atɛne, na bɛammu ho atɛne.

english_verse: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
translation_verse: Na atɛne a hobu he ɔnoaa ne bɛde bebu ho, na deɛ wode susu he, ɔno ne bɛde besusu ama ho.

english_verse: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
translation_verse: Mmɛ nti ne wohwɛ wo yɔnko ani ase mfrɛ, na wonnwene mpuna a ɔda w’ani so he ho?

english_verse: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
translation_verse: Anaa mmɛ nti ne wotumi ka kyerɛ wo yɔnko sɛ, Mma menyi w’ani ase mfrɛ berɛ a mpuna da so da w’ani so?

english_verse: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
translation_verse: Wo nyaatwomni, di kane yi mpuna a ɔda w’ani so he, na ɔbɛma woahu yie na woatumi ayi wo yɔnko ani ase mfrɛ. Honngu ade kronkron ho fi

english_verse: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
translation_verse: Na afei sɛ ho a hoyɛ nnebɔnyɛfoɔ mpo de nnepa mma ho mma a, ɔbɛyɛ dɛ ne ho Agya a ɔya soro he ɔmmfa nnoɔma pa mma bɛ a bebisa no! Mmara titire

english_verse: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
translation_verse: Ho mu hwane ne ne ba bisa no paanoo a ɔbɛma no boɔ?

english_verse: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
translation_verse: Hommisa, na bɛde bɛma ho; honhwehwɛ, na hobehu; hommom, na bebebie pono he ama ho.

english_verse: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
translation_verse: Na biaa a obisa he, ne nsa ka, na biaa a ɔhwehwɛ he, ohu, na biaa a obom he bebie pono he ma no.

english_verse: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
translation_verse: Anaa ne ba bisa no adwene a ɔbɛma no wɔ?

english_verse: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
translation_verse: Nti adeɛ biaa a hopɛ sɛ nnipa yɔ ma ho he, honyɔ saa mma bɛ; na yei ne nea mmara he ne adiyifoɔ he ka. Akwane ahodoɔ mmienu

english_verse: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.
translation_verse: “Nipa biaa a otie me nsɛm yi na ɔyɔ bɛyɛ sɛ nyansani a osii ne dane abotane so.

english_verse: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
translation_verse: Na pono teaa, ɔne kwane teaa he so fa ɔdene na ɔno ne kɔ nkwa mu, na nnipa kakraa bi ne hu saa pono ne kwane he. Dua ne n’aba

english_verse: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.
translation_verse: Honhwɛ yie atorɔ adiyifoɔ ho, bɛ a bɛfira dwane nwoma ba ho nkyɛne nso bɛ mu paa deɛ bɛyɛ mpataku.

english_verse: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
translation_verse: Bɛ aduaba ne hode behu bɛ. Bɛtete bobe nkasɛe so anaa bɛtete borɔdoma hwerɛmo so?

english_verse: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
translation_verse: Saa nso ne dua pa biaa so aba pa, na dua bɔne so aba bɔne.

english_verse: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
translation_verse: Dua pa ntumi nso aba bɔne, saa nso ne dua bɔne ntumi nso aba pa.

english_verse: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
translation_verse: Dua biaa a ɔnso aba pa he betwa to twene gya mu.

english_verse: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke
translation_verse: Nti bɛ aba ne hode behu bɛ. Setie ho nkyerɛkyerɛ

english_verse: Anyone who hears my teachings and doesn't obey them is like a foolish person who built a house on sand.
translation_verse: Nso nipa biaa a otie me nsɛm yi na ɔnnyɔ he bɛyɛ sɛ bi a onnim nyansa a osii ne dane anweam.

english_verse: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
translation_verse: Nsuo tɔeɛ, nsuhyiri baeɛ, mframa bɔ bɛbɔɔ no, na obu guu fam tim bubuu pasaa!”

english_verse: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
translation_verse: Ɔnyɛ biaa a ɔfrɛ me sɛ, Awurade, Awurade, ne bɛkɔ soro ahennie mu, na mmom deɛ ɔyɔ m’Agya a ɔya soro he apɛdeɛ.

english_verse: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
translation_verse: Saa da he, bebree bebisa me sɛ, Awurade, Awurade, yɛamfa wo dini anhyɛ nkɔm, na yɛamfa wo dini antutu ahonhommɔne, na yɛamfa wo dini annyɔ tumideɛ pii anaa?

english_verse: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
translation_verse: Na mɛpae mu aka akyerɛ bɛ sɛ, Mennim ho da; homfiri me so nkɔ, ho a hoyɔɔ nnebɔne. Abotane ne anwea so dansie

english_verse: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
translation_verse: Nsuo tɔeɛ, nsuhyiri baeɛ, na mframa bɔ bɛbɔɔ no, nso ammu angu, efiri sɛ otoo ne fapem he abotane so.

english_verse: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were surprised at his teaching.
translation_verse: Na afei, berɛ a Yesu kaa nsɛm yi wieeɛ he, nnipadɔm he ho dwirii bɛ ne nkyerɛkyerɛ he ho,

english_verse: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ ɔkyerɛkyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ bi a ɔwɔ tumi, na ɔnyɛ sɛnea bɛ atwerɛfoɔ he kyerɛkyerɛ he.

english_verse: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
translation_verse: na ahennie he mma paa deɛ bɛbɛto bɛ atwene sum kabii mu, baabi a suu ne benabɔ ya.”

english_verse: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
translation_verse: Saa anwummerɛ he bɛde nnipa bebree a bɛwɔ ahohommɔne braa no; ɔde tumi a ɔya n’asɛm mu tutuu ahohommɔne he, na ɔsaa bɛ a bɛyare nyinaa yadeɛ.

english_verse: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
translation_verse: Na hwɛ, kwatani bi baa ne hɔ bebuu nkotodwe n’anim, kaa sɛ, “Awurade, sɛ wopɛ a, wobetumi ama me ho afiri.”

english_verse: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
translation_verse: Na Yesu tenee ne nsa de kaa no, kaa sɛ, “Mepɛ. Wo ho mfiri!” Amonom hɔaa ne kwata he firii ne ho.

english_verse: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Mɛba abɛsa no yadeɛ.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
translation_verse: Na Yesu tee deɛ ɔkaeɛ he, ne ho dwirii no, na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ a bedi n’akyi he sɛ, “Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho, menhu biaa Israel mu a ɔwɔ gyidie sei.

english_verse: “Lord, my servant is at home in such terrible pain that he can't even move.”
translation_verse: sɛ, “Awurade, m’akoa abubu gu fie a ohu amane paa.”

english_verse: Many people will come from everywhere to enjoy the feast in the kingdom of heaven with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
translation_verse: Meeka akyerɛ ho, nnipa bebree firi apieeɛ ne atɔeɛ bɛba ne Abraham ne Isak ne Yakob abɛtena soro ahennie mu abedidi,

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the officer, “You may go home now. Your faith has made it happen.” Right then his servant was healed. Jesus Heals Many People (Mark
translation_verse: Na Yesu ka kyerɛɛ asraafoɔ ha so panyini he sɛ, “Kɔ; ɔnyɔ hɔ ma wo sɛdeɛ wo gyidie teɛ.” Na akoa he ho tɔɔ no saa dɔnhwere heaa mu. Yesu saa nnipa bebree yadeɛ ya Petro fie (Marko

english_verse: Suddenly they shouted, “Jesus, Son of God, what do you want with us? Have you come to punish us before our time?”
translation_verse: Amonom hɔaa bɛteateam sɛ, Mmɛ ne wo ne yɛ wɔ yɔ, Nyankopɔn Ba? Woaba ha sɛ wobɛha yɛ ansa na mmerɛ he aduru anaa?

english_verse: Everyone in town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their part of the country.
translation_verse: Na afei kuro mu he nyinaa ba behyiaa Yesu; na behuu no he, bɛsrɛɛ no sɛ ommfiri bɛ mpɔtamu hɔ nkɔ.

english_verse: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
translation_verse: Na afei Yesu kɔɔ Petro fie, na abunu akye Petro ase baa da hɔ.

english_verse: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
translation_verse: Yesu sɔɔ ne nsa, na abunu he gyaeɛ, na ɔsɔreeɛ, na ɔyɔɔ no ahɔhoyɛ.

english_verse: When Jesus saw the crowd, he went across Lake Galilee.
translation_verse: Afei Yesu huu nnipadɔm pii sɛ bɛatwa ne ho ahyia he, ɔhyɛɛ sɛ bentwa nkɔ fa nohoaa.

english_verse: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
translation_verse: Twerɛfoɔ bi baa ne nkyɛne sɛ, “Kyerɛkyerɛni, medi w’akyi baabi a wobɛkɔ biaa.”

english_verse: Another disciple said to Jesus, “Lord, let me wait till I bury my father.”
translation_verse: Na suani foforɔ nso kaa sɛ, “Awurade, mma me kwane na menni kane nkosie me papa.”

english_verse: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
translation_verse: Na asuafoɔ he baa ne nkyɛne bɛyanee no, sɛ, “Awurade, gye yɛ! Yɛɛhwere yɛ nkwa!”

english_verse: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
translation_verse: Na Yesu tenaa kodoɔ he mu he, n’asuafoɔ dii n’akyi.

english_verse: a terrible storm suddenly struck the lake, and waves started splashing into their boat. Jesus was sound asleep,
translation_verse: Ahum kɛseɛ bi bɔ faa po he so, na ano dene nti asorɔkye he baa kodoɔ he mu; nso na Yesu ada.

english_verse: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
translation_verse: Na yei yɛɛ bɛ nwanwa, na bɛkaa sɛ, “Abɛ nipa di a, mframa ne po mpo yɔ setie ma no yi?” Yesu tutuu ahonhommɔne ya Gadarene (Marko

english_verse: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
translation_verse: Na Yesu duruu asuogya hɔ he, Gadarenefoɔ asaase so he, nnipa mmienu a bɛwɔ honhommɔne firi afunsieɛ behyiaa no. Na bɛ ho hu nti, na nnipa biaa ntumi ntwam saa kwane he so.

english_verse: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
translation_verse: Saa mmerɛ he na mprakokuo bebree bi oodidi nkyɛne hɔ.

english_verse: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
translation_verse: Na ahohommɔne he srɛɛ no sɛ, “Sɛ wotutu yɛ a, ma yɛnkɔ mprakokuo yi mu.”

english_verse: Jesus told them to go, and they went out of the men and into the pigs. All at once the pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Honkɔ!” Nti bɛkɔ kɔ wuraa mprakokuo he mu; na hɔaa, mprako he nyinaa bɔɔ bum sianee monku he mu koguu po he mu ma bɛ ase hyeeɛ.

english_verse: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
translation_verse: Na bɛ a bɛɛhwɛ mprakokuo he dwane kɔɔ kurom hɔ, kaa deɛ asi fa bɛ a na bɛwɔ ahohommɔne he ho kyerɛɛ bɛ.

english_verse: Jesus heard them and answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
translation_verse: Na ɔtee yei he, ɔkaa sɛ, “Bɛ a bɛte apɔ he benhia yaresafoɔ, na mmom gye sɛ bɛ a bɛyare he ne hia yaresafoɔ.

english_verse: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. Both the skins and the wine will then be safe. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Mark
translation_verse: Saa nso ne bɛmmfa bobesa foforɔ ngu nsakotokuo dadaa mu; anyɛ saa a, nsakotokuo he bɛpae ama bobesa he ahwie agu, na kotokuo he asɛe. Na mmom bɛde bobesa foforɔ gu nsakotokuo mono mu, na ne mmienu nyinaa ansɛe.” Abaayaa bi baa nkwa mu bio na maame bi nso nyaa ayaresa (Marko

english_verse: News about this spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men
translation_verse: Na yei ho amanebɔ trɛɛ mansini mu hɔ nyinaa. Yesu saa anifirafoɔ mmienu yadeɛ

english_verse: Some teachers of the Law of Moses said to themselves, “Jesus must think he is God!”
translation_verse: Na atwerɛfoɔ bi keka kyerɛɛ bɛ ho bɛ ho sɛ, “Beema yi ɔɔka abususɛm.”

english_verse: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
translation_verse: Na Yesu huu bɛ adwene he, ɔkaa sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne ho dwene bɔne ho akoma mu?

english_verse: But I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
translation_verse: Na sɛ hobehu sɛ Nipa Ba he wɔ tumi asaase so sɛ ɔde bɔne kyɛ nti a.” Na afei ɔka kyerɛɛ bubuani he sɛ, “Sɔre, fa wo kɛtɛ, na kɔ wo fie.”

english_verse: The man got up and went home.
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔreeɛ na ɔkɔɔ ne fie.

english_verse: Go and learn what the Scriptures mean when they say, ‘Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others.’ I didn't come to invite good people to be my followers. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Mark
translation_verse: Honkɔ nkosua nea Nyankopɔn kyerɛ, sɛ ‘Mepɛ mmɔborɔhunu, na nyɛ afɔrebɔ.’ Na mamma sɛ meebɛfrɛ ateneneefoɔ, na mmom mebaa sɛ meebɛfrɛ nnebɔnyɛfoɔ.” Akɔnkyene ho nkyerɛkyerɛ (Marko

english_verse: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Na Yesu firii hɔ kɔeɛ he, anifirafoɔ mmienu dii n’akyi teateam dendene sɛ, “Hu yɛ mmɔbɔ, Dawid ba!”

english_verse: As Jesus and his disciples were on their way, some people brought to him a man who could not talk because a demon was in him.
translation_verse: Bɛkɔeɛ akyi he, bɛde mum bi a honhommɔne ya ne mu braa no.

english_verse: As Jesus was leaving, he saw a tax collector named Matthew sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.” Matthew got up and went with him.
translation_verse: Na Yesu nam he, ohuu beema bi a bɛfrɛ no Mateo sɛ ɔte togyebea, na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Di m’akyi.” Na ɔsɔre dii n’akyi.

english_verse: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Matthew's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners were also there.
translation_verse: Na Yesu ne n’asuafoɔ tenaa adidiiɛ fie hɔ he, hwɛ, atogyefoɔ ne nnebɔnyɛfoɔ bebree nso bɛtenaa bɛ nkyɛne.

english_verse: Some Pharisees asked Jesus' disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and other sinners?”
translation_verse: Berɛ a Farisifoɔ he huu yei he, bɛka kyerɛɛ n’asuafoɔ he sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne ho kyerɛkyerɛni yi ne atogyefoɔ ne nnebɔnyɛfoɔ didi yi?”

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking, an official came and knelt in front of him. The man said, “My daughter has just now died! Please come and place your hand on her. Then she will live again.”
translation_verse: Berɛ a Yesu gu so ɔɔka yeinom akyerɛ bɛ he, mpofiremuaa hyiadane mu panyini bi ba bebuu nkotodwe n’anim kaa sɛ, “Me ba baa awu seseiaa; na bra na fa wo nsa begu ne so na obenya nkwa.”

english_verse: Jesus turned. He saw the woman and said, “Don't worry! You are now healed because of your faith.” At that moment she was healed.
translation_verse: Yesu danee ne ho, na ohuu no he, ɔkaa sɛ, “Babaa, ma w’akoma ntɔ wo yam, wo gyidie agye wo.” Na amonom hɔaa baa he nyaa ayaresa.

english_verse: After Jesus had forced the demon out, the man started talking. The crowds were so amazed they began saying, “Nothing like this has ever happened in Israel!”
translation_verse: Na berɛ a otuu honhommɔne he, mum he kasaeɛ; na nnipadɔm he ho dwirii bɛ, na bɛkaa sɛ, “Biaa nhuu bibiaa a ɔte sei Israel da.”

english_verse: Some followers of John the Baptist came and asked Jesus, “Why do we and the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
translation_verse: Afei Yohane asuafoɔ he baa ne nkyɛne bɛkaa sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne yɛ ne Farisifoɔ he taa kyene kɔm, na wo asuafoɔ yi deɛ nkyene kɔm?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom aren't sad while he is still with them. But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
translation_verse: Na Yesu ka kyerɛ bɛ sɛ, “Ayefohyia he ahɔhoɔ ntumi nni awerɛhoɔ berɛ a ayefokunu he ya bɛnkyɛne, ɔnte saa? Nna bi bɛba a bɛbɛfa ayefokunu he afiri bɛnkyɛne, na afei bɛbɛkyene kɔm.

english_verse: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
translation_verse: Biaa mfa ntoma mono sini ntɛ ataadeɛ dada mu, anyɛ saa a, ntɛmuu he bɛte afiri mu ama ne nteɛ he ayɛ kɛse paa.

english_verse: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, ɔkaa ne ne tim sɛ, “Sɛ mesɔ n’ataadeɛ ano nkoaa mpo a, menya ayaresa.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples got up and went with the man.
translation_verse: Na Yesu sɔre ne n’asuafoɔ he dii hyiadane mu panyini he akyi.

english_verse: A woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and barely touched his clothes.
translation_verse: Afei mpofiremuaa, baa bi a mogyaa atu no mfirinhyia dummienu firii Yesu akyi bɛsɔɔ n’ataadeɛ ano,

english_verse: When Jesus went into the home of the official and saw the musicians and the crowd of mourners,
translation_verse: Berɛ a Yesu duruu panyini he fie na ohuu atɛntɛmmɛne hyɛnefoɔ ne nnipadɔm a bɛɛyɔ gyegyeegye he,

english_verse: he said, “Get out of here! The little girl isn't dead. She is just asleep.” Everyone started laughing at Jesus.
translation_verse: ɔkaa sɛ, “Homfiri ha nkɔ; na abaayaa he nwuiɛ, na waada.” Na bɛseree no twee.

english_verse: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
translation_verse: Na bɛmaa nnipadɔm he kɔɔ abɔntene he, ɔkɔɔ dane he mu na ɔsɔɔ ne nsa, na abaayaa he sɔreeɛ.

english_verse: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
translation_verse: Berɛ a ɔkɔɔ fie hɔ he, anifirafoɔ he baa ne nkyɛne, na Yesu bisaa bɛ sɛ, “Hogye di sɛ metumi ayɔ yei?” Bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Ɛɛ, Awurade.”

english_verse: Jesus touched their eyes and said, “Because of your faith, you will be healed.”
translation_verse: Afei ɔde ne nsa kaa bɛ ani, na ɔkaa sɛ, “Sɛdeɛ ho gyidie teɛ he, ɔnyɔ saa mma ho.”

english_verse: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.
translation_verse: Na bɛ ani biebieɛ. Afei Yesu bɔɔ bɛ kɔkɔ dendenndene sɛ, “Honhwɛ na biaa ante yei.”

english_verse: But they left and talked about him to everyone in that part of the country. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Talk
translation_verse: Nso befirii hɔ kɔeɛ a ne bɛde ne ho asɛm trɛɛ mansini he mu nyinaa. Yesu saa mum bi yadeɛ

english_verse: But the Pharisees said, “The leader of the demons gives him the power to force out demons.” Jesus Has Pity on People
translation_verse: Nso Farisifoɔ he kaa sɛ, “Ɔde ahonhommɔne panyini tumi ne tu ahonhommɔne he.” Twa adwuma he so, nso apaafoɔ he sua

english_verse: Jesus went to every town and village. He taught in their synagogues and preached the good news about God's kingdom. Jesus also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
translation_verse: Afei Yesu kyinkyinii nkuro ne nkuraa nyinaa ase, kyerɛkyerɛ bɛ hyiadane mu, na ɔpae mu kaa ahennie he ho asɛmpa he, na ɔsaa yadeɛ ne ahoɔmmerɛ nyinaa.

english_verse: When he saw the crowds, he felt sorry for them. They were confused and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
translation_verse: Berɛ a ohuu nnipadɔm he, ohuu bɛ mmɔbɔ, efiri sɛ na bɛɛteetee a na bɛabrɛ nso, na begu hɔ sɛ nwane a benni hwɛfoɔ.

english_verse: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛ n’asuafoɔ he sɛ, “Twa he so, nso apaafoɔ he sua;

english_verse: Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.”
translation_verse: nti hommisa twawura he na ɔnsoma apaafoɔ nkɔ ne twa adwuma he mu.”

english_verse: Philip, Bartholomew, Thomas, Matthew the tax collector, James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles.
translation_verse: Filipo ne Bartolomeo; Toma ne Mateo a na ɔyɛ togyeni; Alfeo ba Yakobo, ne Tadeo;

english_verse: But when someone arrests you, don't worry about what you will say or how you will say it. At that time you will be given the words to say.
translation_verse: Sɛ beyi ho mma a, honnwene sɛnea hobɛkasa anaa deɛ hobɛka; efiri sɛ bɛde deɛ hobɛka he bɛma ho saa berɛ heaa mu;

english_verse: Go only to the people of Israel, because they are like a flock of lost sheep.
translation_verse: na mmom honkɔ Israelfoɔ he mu, nwane a bɛayera he hɔ.

english_verse: I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves. So be as wise as snakes and as innocent as doves.
translation_verse: “Hwɛ, meesoma ho sɛ nnwane akɔ mpataku mfimfini; nti honya nyansa sɛ wɔ, na honyɛ nnipa a ho ho nni asɛm te sɛ mmuburo.

english_verse: The others were Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus. Instructions for the Twelve Apostles (Mark
translation_verse: Simon a ofiri Kana, ne Yuda Iskariot a oyii Yesu maeɛ he. Asuafoɔ dummienu he dwumadie (Marko

english_verse: Jesus sent out the twelve apostles with these instructions: Stay away from the Gentiles and don't go to any Samaritan town.
translation_verse: Saa nnipa dummienu yi ne Yesu somaa bɛ hyɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Hommmane nkɔ Amanamane mufoɔ hɔ, na honnwura Samariafoɔ kuro biaa mu,

english_verse: And don't carry a traveling bag or an extra shirt or sandals or a walking stick. Workers deserve their food.
translation_verse: hommfa akwantuo bɔtɔ, anaa ntaadeɛ mmienu, anaa mpaboa anaa poma; na mmom adwumayɔni akatua fata no.

english_verse: So when you go to a town or a village, find someone able and willing to have you as their guest and stay with them until you leave.
translation_verse: Kuro anaa akuraa biaa a hobɛkɔ mu he, hommisa deɛ ɔfata a ɔya hɔ, na hontena hɔ kosi sɛ hobefiri hɔ.

english_verse: I promise you the day of judgment will be easier for the towns of Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town. Warning about Trouble (Mark
translation_verse: Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, atemuda he Nyankopɔn behu Sodom ne Gomora mpo mmɔbɔ akyɛne saa kuro he. Taa ho nkɔmhyɛ (Marko

english_verse: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
translation_verse: Nua beyi nua ama wuo, na agya ayi ne ba ama, na mma bɛsɔre atia awofoɔ ama bɛaku bɛ;

english_verse: Watch out for people who will take you to court and have you beaten in their synagogues.
translation_verse: Honhwɛ ho ho yie bɛ ho, efiri sɛ bɛde ho bɛma badwafoɔ na bɛahwe ho bɛ hyia adane mu;

english_verse: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
translation_verse: na me nti bɛbɛtwe ho akɔ amradofoɔ ne ahemfo anim, na ayɛ adansedie ama bɛ ne Amanamane mufoɔ.

english_verse: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
translation_verse: na nyɛ ho ne hobɛkasa, na mmom ɔyɛ ho Agya Honhom ne bɛfa ho mu akasa.

english_verse: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you remain faithful until the end, you will be saved.
translation_verse: na me dini nti nnipa nyinaa bɛtane ho. Na nea obegyina akosi awieeɛ he, ɔno ne bɛbɛgye no nkwa.

english_verse: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
translation_verse: Sɛ bɛtaa ho kuro baako so a, honwane nkɔ kuro foforɔ so; na nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, honnkɔ Israel nkuro nyinaa mu na Nipa Ba he aba.

english_verse: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
translation_verse: “Suani nkyɛne ne kyerɛkyerɛni, saa ara nso ne akoa nkyɛne ne wura;

english_verse: It is enough for students to be like their teacher and for slaves to be like their master. If people call the head of the family Satan, what will they say about the rest of the family? The One To Fear (Luke
translation_verse: ɔdɔɔso ma suani sɛ ɔbɛyɛ sɛ ne kyerɛkyerɛni, anaa akoa sɛ ne wura. Sɛ bɛfrɛɛ fie he wura he Beelsebul a, mprɛ ahe ne bɛmfrɛ fie he mufoɔ saa bi! Deɛ ɔsɛ sɛ besuro no (Luka

english_verse: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
translation_verse: Honnsuro bɛ a beku honam nso bentumi nku kra; na mmom honsuro deɛ obetumi asɛe honhom ne honam bronsam gya mu.

english_verse: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
translation_verse: Bɛnntɔno mpatuprɛ mmienu nnye kaprɛ anaa? Nso bɛ mu baako mpo nkwati ho Agya ntɔ fam.

english_verse: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
translation_verse: Na ho tinwii mpo bɛakane ne nyinaa.

english_verse: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
translation_verse: Saa nti biaa a ɔbɛgye me atum nnipa anim he, me nso mɛgye no atum m’Agya a ɔya soro he anim;

english_verse: But if you reject me, I will tell my Father in heaven you don't belong to me. Not Peace, but Trouble (Luke
translation_verse: nso deɛ ɔbɛpo me nipa anim he, me nso mɛpo no m’Agya a ɔya soro he anim. Ko di asomdwie anim (Luka

english_verse: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
translation_verse: “Honnwenne sɛ mede asomdwie ne baa asaase so; mammfa asomdwie amma, na mmom mede sekane.

english_verse: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.
translation_verse: Na maaba sɛ mede mpaepaemu ɔɔbɛto babeema ne n’agya ntam, babaa ne ne maame ntam, na ase asɔre atia n’ase baa;

english_verse: Your worst enemies will be in your own family.
translation_verse: na nipa fiefoɔ bɛyɛ n’atamfoɔ.

english_verse: If you love your father or mother or even your sons and daughters more than me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
translation_verse: Biaa a ɔdɔ Agya anaa na kyɛne me he mfata sɛ ɔbɛyɛ me suani; na biaa a ɔdɔ babeema anaa babaa kyɛne me he nso mfata sɛ ɔbɛyɛ me suani;

english_verse: And unless you are willing to take up your cross and follow me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
translation_verse: na biaa a ɔmmfa n’asennua he na onni m’akyi he mfata sɛ ɔbɛyɛ me suani.

english_verse: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
translation_verse: Deɛ ne nsa aka ne kra he, obefiri ne nsa, na bɛ a me nti bɛbɛhwere bɛ kra he bɛ nsa bɛka. Akatua

english_verse: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
translation_verse: Deɛ ɔgye diyifoɔ ya diyifoɔ dini mu he benya diyifoɔ akatua; na deɛ ɔgye nipa tenenee ya nipa tenenee dini mu he benya nipa tenee akatua;

english_verse: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me also welcomes the one who sent me.
translation_verse: “Biaa a ɔgye ho he gye me, na deɛ ɔgye me he gye deɛ ɔsomaa me he nso.

english_verse: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
translation_verse: na deɛ ɔbɛma nkumaa yi bi nsuo nnwunu kuruwaa baako mpo he suani dini mu he, nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ n’akatua ɔmmɔ no da.”

english_verse: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
translation_verse: Na bɛɛkɔ he, Yesu firii aseɛ kaa Yohane ho asɛm kyerɛɛ nnipadɔm he sɛ: “Mmɛ ne hokɔɔ serɛ so kɔhwɛeɛ? Nnɛm a mframa ohim no anaa?

english_verse: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
translation_verse: Na Yohane baeɛ, na onnidi, na ɔnnom nso, na bɛkaa sɛ, ‘Ɔwɔ honhommɔne’;

english_verse: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
translation_verse: Berɛ a Yohane da afiase a ɔtee deɛ Kristo ɔɔyɔ he, ɔsomaa n’asuafoɔ

english_verse: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
translation_verse: ma bɛ bebisaa Yesu sɛ, “Wone deɛ ɔɔba he, anaa yɛnhwɛ foforɔ kwane?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Go and tell John what you have heard and seen.
translation_verse: Yesu buaa bɛ sɛ, “Honkɔ na honkɔka deɛ hoate ne deɛ hoahu nkyerɛ Yohane:

english_verse: The blind are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People with leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
translation_verse: anifirafoɔ hu adeɛ, mmubuafoɔ nante, akwatafoɔ ho firi, asotifoɔ te asɛm, awufoɔ nyane firi wuom, na bɛka asɛmpa he kyerɛ ahiafoɔ.

english_verse: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
translation_verse: Nhyira ne deɛ mennto bɛ sintidua.” Yesu kamfõõ Suboni Yohane

english_verse: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
translation_verse: Sɛ onte saa a ɛɛne ne hokɔhwɛeɛ? Bi a ɔhyɛ ntaade pa? Hwɛ, bɛ a bɛhyɛ ntaade pa he bɛya ahemfie.

translation_verse: na okohuu deɛ ne boɔ ɔdene paa he, ɔkɔtɔnoo deɛ ɔwɔ nyinaa de kɔtɔeɛ. Asau ho mfantoho

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a net is thrown into a lake and catches all kinds of fish.
translation_verse: “Bio, soro ahennie te sɛ asau a bɛde guu po mu na benyaa nam ahodoɔ bebree;

english_verse: When the net is full, it is dragged to the shore, and the fishermen sit down to separate the fish. They keep the good ones, but throw the bad ones away.

english_verse: That's how it will be at the end of time. Angels will come and separate the evil people from the ones who have done right.
translation_verse: Saa ara ne ɔbɛyɛ awieeɛ berɛ mu. Abɔfoɔ he bɛba abɛfa abɔnefoɔ he afiri teneneefoɔ mu

translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Yei nti twerɛfoɔ biaa a bɛatete ne sɛ suani ma soro ahennie he te sɛ fiewura a oyi n’agyapadeɛ foforɔ ne dadaa nyinaa firi n’adekoradane mu.”

english_verse: When Jesus had finished telling these stories, he left
translation_verse: Berɛ a Yesu wiee saa mfantoho yi, ofirii hɔ kɔeɛ. Bɛpoo Yesu ya Nasaret (Marko

translation_verse: Na afei Yesu baa ɔnoaa ne kurom, na ɔhyɛɛ aseɛ sɛ ɔɔkyerɛkyerɛ nkurofoɔ he bɛ hyiadane mu hɔ, na bɛ ho dwirii bɛ maa bɛkaa sɛ, “He ne saa nipa yi konyaa nyansa ne tumi nnwuma a ɔte sei?

english_verse: Isn't he the son of the carpenter? Isn't Mary his mother, and aren't James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?
translation_verse: Ɔnyɛ duadwumfoɔ he ba he di? Ɔnyɛ ne maame ne bɛfrɛ ne Maria he? Ɔnyɛ ne nua mmeemmaanom ne Yakobo ne Yosef ne Simon ne Yuda?

english_verse: Don't his sisters still live here in our town? How can he do all this?”
translation_verse: Na ɔnyɛ yɛ ne ne nua mmaanom nso ne te yi? Na afei he fa ne okonyaa yeinom nyinaa?”

english_verse: So the people were upset because of what he was doing. But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their own family.”
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛɛ sintidua maa bɛ. Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Anidie mmɔ diyifoɔ biaa gye sɛ ne mane mu ne ne fie.”

english_verse: And because the people did not have any faith, Jesus did not work many miracles there.
translation_verse: Na gye a bɛannye anni nti, wannyɔ tumi nnwuma bebree hɔ.

english_verse: “Come on!” Jesus said. Peter then got out of the boat and started walking on the water toward him.

english_verse: so much he swore to give her whatever she wanted.

english_verse: But the girl's mother told her to say, “Here on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”

english_verse: But when Peter saw how strong the wind was, he was afraid and started sinking. “Save me, Lord!” he shouted.
translation_verse: Nso ohuu mframa dendene he, osuroeɛ, na ofirii aseɛ sɛ ɔɔmmem he, ɔteaam sɛ, “Awurade, gye me!”

english_verse: But they said, “We have only five small loaves of bread and two fish.”

english_verse: After Jesus heard about John, he crossed Lake Galilee to go to some place where he could be alone. But the crowds found out and followed him on foot from the towns.
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a Yesu teeɛ he, ɔtenaa kodoɔ mu firii nnipadɔm he nkyɛne kɔɔ baabi a biaa nni. Nso nnipadɔm he teeɛ he, befirii nkuro so nam fam tii no.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “They don't have to leave. Why don't you give them something to eat?”
translation_verse: Nso Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Ɔnsɛ sɛ bɛkɔ; homma bɛ bibi nni.”

english_verse: Jesus asked his disciples to bring the food to him,
translation_verse: Na ɔkaa sɛ, “Homfa mmra me.”

english_verse: and he told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they gave it to the people.
translation_verse: Afei ɔhyɛɛ nnipadɔm he sɛ bɛntenatena serɛ he so. Ɔfaa paanoo nnum ne nkyenam mmienu he, ɔhwɛɛ soro, hyiraa so bubuu paanoo he, na ɔde maa asuafoɔ he, na asuafoɔ he de maa nkurofoɔ he.

english_verse: After everyone had eaten all they wanted, Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftovers.
translation_verse: Na biaa diiɛ na bɛmeeɛ; na bɛsesaa aseɛ mporoporowa nkɛntɛmma dummienu.

english_verse: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and start back across the lake. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
translation_verse: Amonom hɔaa Yesu maa asuafoɔ he kɔɔ kodoɔ he mu sɛ bentwa nni n’anim kane nkɔ asuogya nohoa, na onnya nnipadɔm he kwane.

english_verse: Then he went up on a mountain where he could be alone and pray. Later in the evening, he was still there.
translation_verse: Na ogyaa bɛ kwane wieeɛ he, ɔkɔɔ bepɔ he so kɔbɔɔ mpaeɛ. Nnwunu dwoeɛ he, na ɔno nkoaa ne ya hɔ.

english_verse: By this time the boat was a long way from the shore. It was going against the wind and was being tossed around by the waves.
translation_verse: Saa berɛ yi na kodoɔ he mmɛne asaase koraa, na na asorɔkye he ama nnoɔma mu ayɛ dene, efiri sɛ na mframa he bɔ tia bɛ.

english_verse: When they saw him, they thought he was a ghost. They were terrified and started screaming.
translation_verse: Na berɛ a asuafoɔ he huu sɛ ɔnam po he so he, bɛ akoma tuiɛ, na bɛkaa sɛ, “Ɔyɛ samane!” Na bɛde hu teateam.

english_verse: At once, Jesus said to them, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
translation_verse: Nso amonom hɔaa Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Homa ho akoma ntɔ ho yam, ɔyɛ me a; honnsuro koraa.”

english_verse: Peter replied, “Lord, if it really is you, tell me to come to you on the water.”

translation_verse: Berɛ a beduruu kodoɔ he mu he, mframa he gyaeɛ.

english_verse: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.” What Really Makes People Unclean (Mark
translation_verse: bɛsom me kwa, bɛkyerɛkyerɛ nipa ahyɛdeɛ sɛ ɔyɛ nkyerɛkyerɛ pa.’ ” Nnoɔma a ogu nipa ho fi ankasa

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
translation_verse: Na Yesu buaa no sɛ, “Baa, wo gyidie so! Ɔnyɔ hɔ mma wo sɛ deɛ wopɛ.” Amonom hɔaa ne ba he nyaa ayaresa. Yesu saa nnipa bebree yadeɛ

english_verse: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
translation_verse: Na Yesu frɛɛ n’asuafoɔ he baa ne nkyɛne na ɔkaa sɛ, “Nnipadɔm yi yɛ me mmɔbɔ paa, efiri sɛ bɛatena me nkyɛne nnansa nso benni hwee a bebedi; na mempɛ sɛ mɛma bɛde kɔm akɔ, anyɛ saa a bebetumi atwa ato kwanem.”

english_verse: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
translation_verse: Na asuafoɔ he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “He fa ne yebenya paanoo a ɔbɛso nnipa bebree yi serɛ so ha?”

english_verse: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
translation_verse: Yesu bisaa bɛ sɛ, “Howɔ paanoo dodoɔ mmeɛhe?” Na bɛkaa sɛ, “Nson, ne nkyenam nketewa kakraa bi.”

translation_verse: Na ɔhyɛɛ nkurofokuo he ma bɛtenaa ase,

translation_verse: na afei ɔfaa paanoo nson ne nkyenam he; na ɔdaa ase wieeɛ he, obubuu mu mmaa asuafoɔ he, na asuafoɔ he nso de maa nnipadɔm he.

english_verse: Everyone ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
translation_verse: Na bɛ nyinaa di meeɛ; na bɛsesaa aseɛ mporoporowa nkɛntɛmma nson.

english_verse: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
translation_verse: Mmɛ nti ne ho nteaseɛ sɛ ɔnyɛ paanoo asɛm ne meeka? Honhwɛ yie Farisifoɔ ne Sadukifoɔ mmɔreka ho!”

english_verse: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
