english_verse: Finally, the disciples understood that Jesus wasn't talking about the yeast used to make bread, but about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees. Who Is Jesus? (Mark
translation_verse: Na bɛtee aseɛ sɛ ɔnyɛ mmɔreka paanoo ho ahwɛyie ho asɛm ne ɔɔka na mmom, Farisifoɔ ne Sadukifoɔ nkyerɛkyerɛ ho ahwɛyie. Petro kyerɛɛ nipa ko a Yesu yɛ (Marko

english_verse: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
translation_verse: Na obuaa bɛ sɛ, “Sɛ awia ɔkɔtɔ a, hoka sɛ, ‘Wiem bɛte, efiri sɛ anim ayɛ kɔkɔɔ.’

english_verse: But if the sky is red and gloomy in the morning, you say it is going to rain. You can tell what the weather will be like by looking at the sky. But you don't understand what is happening now.
translation_verse: Na anɔpa, ‘Ahum betu, efiri sɛ anim ayɛ kɔkɔɔ ne kusuu.’ Honim sɛ deɛ bɛkyerɛ wiem tebea ase, nso honnim sɛnea bɛkyerɛ mmerɛ ho nsɛnkyerɛnne ase.

english_verse: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
translation_verse: Na bebuaa sɛ, “Binom dɛ ɔyɛ Yohane Suboni, binom so dɛ ɔyɛ Elia, na binom nso dɛ ɔyɛ Yeremia anaa adiyifoɔ he mu baako.”

english_verse: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
translation_verse: Ofiri saa mmerɛ he ɔɔkɔ he, Yesu firii aseɛ kyerɛɛ n’asuafoɔ he sɛ ɔsɛ sɛ ɔkɔ Yerusalem na okohu amane, mpanyimfoɔ ne asɔfoɔ mpanyimfoɔ ne atwerɛfoɔ nsam na beku no, na ne nnansa so he ɔsɔre.

english_verse: Jesus then warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Honhwɛ yie Farisifoɔ ne Sadukifoɔ mmɔreka ho.”

english_verse: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
translation_verse: Bɛka kyerɛɛ bɛ ho bɛ ho sɛ, “Sesɛɛ yɛamfa paanoo yi nti a.”

english_verse: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
translation_verse: “Na Nipa Ba he de n’abɔfoɔ bɛba n’Agya animuonyam mu, na obetua nipa biaa ka sɛdeɛ n’adwuma teɛ.

english_verse: So I will call you Peter, which means “a rock.” On this rock I will build my church, and death itself will not have any power over it.
translation_verse: Na meeka akyerɛ wo, wo ne Petro, na botane yi so ne mesi m’asafo, na Asamando pono ontumi mmu mmfa so da.

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
translation_verse: Obisaa bɛ sɛ, “Ho nso hodɛ me ne hwane?”

english_verse: Simon Peter spoke up, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
translation_verse: Simon Petro buaa sɛ, “Wone Kristo he, Nyankopɔn teasefoɔ he Ba.”

english_verse: Jesus told him: Simon, son of Jonah, you are blessed! You didn't discover this on your own. It was shown to you by my Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Na Yesu buaa no sɛ, “Nhyira ne wo, Simon, Yona ba! Ɔnyɛ honam ne mogyaa ne yi kyerɛɛ wo, na mmom m’Agya a ɔya soro.

english_verse: I will give you the keys to the kingdom of heaven, and God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth. But he will not allow anything you don't allow.
translation_verse: Mɛma wo soro ahennie he safoa, na deɛ wobɛkyekyere no asaase yi so he na bɛakyekyere no soro dada, na deɛ wobɛsane no asaase so he na bɛasane no soro dada.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples not to tell anyone he was the Messiah. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Mark
translation_verse: Na ɔhyɛɛ asuafoɔ he ketee sɛ bɛnnka nkyerɛ biaa sɛ ɔno ne Kristo he. Yesu hyɛɛ ne wuo ne ne wusɔreɛ ho nkɔm (Marko

english_verse: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
translation_verse: Na Petro de no gyinaa nkyɛne na ofirii aseɛ kaa n’anim sɛ, “Awurade! Yei mpare wo. Yei ntumi nto wo da!”

english_verse: Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Satan, get away from me! You're in my way because you think like everyone else and not like God.”
translation_verse: Na ɔdanee ne ho ka kyerɛɛ Petro sɛ, “Firi me so kɔ, Satan! Woyɛ sintidua ma me; efiri sɛ woamfa w’adwene ansi Nyankopɔn so na mmom honam nnwuma so.” Suaniyɛ ho asodie

english_verse: Then Jesus said to his disciples: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
translation_verse: Na Yesu ka kyerɛɛ n’asuafoɔ he sɛ, “Sɛ bi pɛ sɛ odi m’akyi a, ma no mpa ne ho akyi na ɔmfa n’asennua na onni m’akyi.

english_verse: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will find it.
translation_verse: Na bɛ a bɛpɛ sɛ bɛkora bɛ kra he, obefiri bɛ nsa, na bɛ a me nti bɛbɛhwere bɛ kra he, bɛ nsa bɛka.

english_verse: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself? What would you give to get back your soul?
translation_verse: Na sɛ bi nya wiase nyinaa na ohwere ne kra a mmɛ mfasoɔ ne obenya? Anaa mmɛ ne bi bɛma de asesa ne kra?

english_verse: I promise you some of those standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming with his kingdom.
translation_verse: Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho, binom gyina ha a, bennwu kosi sɛ bebehu sɛ Nipa Ba he firi n’ahennie mu ɔɔba.”

english_verse: There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. His face was shining like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
translation_verse: Na ɔsakraa bɛ ani so, na n’anim hyerɛnn te sɛ awia, na ne ntaadeɛ yɛɛ fitafita te sɛ hann.

english_verse: Then the disciples understood Jesus was talking to them about John the Baptist. Jesus Heals a Boy (Mark
translation_verse: Na asuafoɔ he tee aseɛ sɛ Suboni Yohane ho asɛm ne ɔɔka akyerɛ bɛ he. Yesu saa abranteɛ bi a na ɔwɔ honhommɔne yadeɛ (Marko

english_verse: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the brothers James and John with him. They went up on a very high mountain where they could be alone.
translation_verse: Nnansia akyi he, Yesu faa Petro ne Yakobo ne ne nua Yohane kaa ne ho kɔɔ bepɔ tentene bi so.

english_verse: All at once Moses and Elijah were there talking with Jesus.
translation_verse: Na hwɛ, Mose ne Elia yii bɛ ho adi, na bɛ ne no kasaeɛ.

english_verse: So Peter said to him, “Lord, it is good for us to be here! Let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
translation_verse: Na Petro ka kyerɛɛ Yesu sɛ, “Awurade, ɔyɛ sɛ yɛya ha; sɛ wopɛ a, mɛbɔ apata mmeɛnsa, wo deɛ baako, Mose deɛ baako, na Elia so deɛ baako.”

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!”
translation_verse: Berɛ a ogu so ɔɔkasa he, amonom hɔaa mununkum bi a ɔhyerɛn bɛkataa bɛ so, na nne bi firi mununkum he mu kaa sɛ, “Yei ne me Ba, deɛ medɔ no he; ɔno ne sɔ m’ani; hontie no!”

english_verse: When the disciples heard the voice, they were so afraid they fell flat on the ground.
translation_verse: Berɛ a asuafoɔ he tee saa he, bɛde bɛ ani butubutuu fam, na besuroeɛ paa.

english_verse: But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, “Get up and don't be afraid!”
translation_verse: Nso Yesu ba de ne nsa bɛkaa bɛ, na ɔkaa sɛ, “Honsɔre na honnsuro.”

english_verse: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
translation_verse: Na bɛmaa bɛ ani so he, bɛanhu biaa ka Yesu ho.

english_verse: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
translation_verse: Na asuafoɔ he bisaa no sɛ, “Sɛ ɔte saa deɛ a mmɛ nti ne atwerɛfoɔ he ka sɛ ɔsɛ sɛ Elia ba ansa?”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “Elijah certainly will come and get everything ready.
translation_verse: Oyii ano sɛ, “Ampa, Elia ɔɔba abɛtoto nnoɔma nyinaa yie;

english_verse: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
translation_verse: nso meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ Elia aba dada, na bɛanhu no, na bɛyɔɔ no deɛ bɛpɛ biaa. Saa ara nso ne ɔnkyɛre biaa, Nipa Ba he nso behu amane bɛ nsam.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples returned to the crowd. A man knelt in front of him
translation_verse: Berɛ a bɛbaa nnipadɔm he nkyɛne he, beema bi baa ne nkyɛne bebuu nkotodwe n’anim,

english_verse: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
translation_verse: kaa sɛ, “Awurade, hu me ba yi mmɔbɔ, na ɔyare twerɛ, na oohu amane; na ɔtaa tɔ gya ne nsuo mu.

english_verse: I brought him to your disciples, but none of them could heal him.”
translation_verse: Na mede no braa w’asuafoɔ he, nso bɛantumi ansa no yadeɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said, “You people are too stubborn to have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy here.”
translation_verse: Yesu buaa sɛ, nneemmafoɔ a honni gyidie na hoamane, mentena ho nkyɛne nkosi da bɛ? Onkosi da bɛ ne menya ho ho aboterɛ? Homfa no mmra me.”

english_verse: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
translation_verse: Na Yesu teatea honhommɔne he, na ofirii ne mu, na abɔfra he nyaa ayaresa saa berɛ heaa.

english_verse: Later the disciples went to Jesus in private and asked him, “Why couldn't we force out the demon?”
translation_verse: Na asuafoɔ he baa Yesu nkyɛne kokoam bebisaa no sɛ, “Mmɛ nti na yɛantumi antutu no yi?”

english_verse: While Jesus and his disciples were going from place to place in Galilee, he told them, “The Son of Man will be handed over to people
translation_verse: Berɛ a bebehyiaa Galilea he, Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Bebeyi Nipa Ba he ahyɛ nnipa nsa,

english_verse: who will kill him. But three days later he will rise to life.” All of this made the disciples very sad. Paying the Temple Tax
translation_verse: na bebeku no, na nnansa so he ɔbɛsɔre.” Na ɔyɛɛ bɛ awerɛhoɔ pii. Yesu tuaa asɔrefie ho toɔ

english_verse: When Jesus and the others arrived in Capernaum, the collectors for the temple tax came to Peter and asked, “Does your teacher pay the temple tax?”
translation_verse: Beduruu Kapernaum he, bɛ a bɛgyegye asɔredane toɔ he baa Petro hɔ bebisaa no sɛ, “Nti wo kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ ntua asɔredane toɔ he bi anaa?”

english_verse: “Yes, he does,” Peter answered. After they had returned home, Jesus went up to Peter and asked him, “Simon, what do you think? Do the kings of this earth collect taxes and fees from their own people or from foreigners?”
translation_verse: Na ɔkaa sɛ, “Ɛɛ, otua.” Na Petro baa fie he, Yesu ne pagyaa ho asɛm baeɛ bisaa sɛ, “Simon, Wodwene ho sɛ? Hefoɔ hɔ ne asaase yi so ahemfo gye adwadeɛ anaa toɔ? Bɛgye no bɛ mma hɔ anaa afoforɔ hɔ?”

english_verse: Peter answered, “From foreigners.” Jesus replied, “Then their own people don't have to pay.
translation_verse: Na Petro kaa sɛ, “Bɛgye firi afoforɔ hɔ,” he, Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Saa deɛ a, na mma he de bɛ ho.”

english_verse: But we don't want to cause trouble. So go cast a line into the lake and pull out the first fish you hook. Open its mouth, and you will find a coin. Use it to pay your taxes and mine.”
translation_verse: Nso sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɔ na yɛnnto sintidua nti, kɔ po so koto doraa; na adwene a wobedi kane ayi no he bie n’anum a, na wobehu dwetɛ; fa kɔma bɛ sɛ me ne wo toɔ.”

english_verse: If your eye causes you to sin, poke it out and get rid of it. You would be better off to go into life with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fires of hell. The Lost Sheep (Luke
translation_verse: Na sɛ w’ani to wo sintidua a, tu, na to twene; ɔyɛ sɛ wode ani baako bɛkɔ nkwam sene sɛ wowɔ ani mmienu na bɛbɛto wo atwene amanehunu gya mu. Dwane a wayera ho mfantoho (Luka

english_verse: Don't you think you should show pity to someone else, as I did to you?”
translation_verse: Ɔnsɛ sɛ nka wohu wo yɔnko akoa mmɔbɔ, sɛdeɛ mehuu wo mmɔbɔ he anaa?’

english_verse: But if you are as humble as this child, you are the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
translation_verse: Nipa biaa a ɔbrɛ ne ho ase sɛ abɔfra he, ɔno ne deɛ ɔyɛ kɛseɛ paa soro ahennie mu.

english_verse: And when you welcome one of these children because of me, you welcome me. Temptations To Sin (Mark
translation_verse: “Deɛ ɔgye abɔfra a ɔte sei baako me dini mu he, ɔgye me. Sintiduatoɔ

english_verse: If the follower refuses to listen to them, report the matter to the church. Anyone who refuses to listen to the church must be treated like an unbeliever or a tax collector. Allowing and Not Allowing
translation_verse: Sɛ saa nipa he anntie bɛ a, ka kyerɛ asafo he; na sɛ saa nipa he antie asafo he nso a, fa no sɛ bi a ɔnnyɛ gyidini, na mmom togyeni.

english_verse: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the deepest part of the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks!
translation_verse: Sɛ ho mu bi to mmɔfra nketewa a bɛgye me di yi mu baako sintidua a, nsunsuansoɔ a ɔbɛba ne so he so kyɛne sɛ bɛde ayuyammoɔ bɛsɛne ne kɔno mu de no akɔto po bunu mu.

english_verse: If your hand or foot causes you to sin, chop it off and throw it away! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and be thrown into the fire that never goes out.
translation_verse: Sɛ wo nsa anaa wo nane to wo sintidua a, twa firi hɔ, na to twene; ɔyɛ mma wo sɛ wode nsa sini anaa nane sini bɛkɔ nkwa mu sene sɛ wowɔ nsa mmienu anaa nane mmienu na bɛbɛto wo ato daa amanehunu gya mu.

english_verse: If one of my followers sins against you, go and point out what was wrong. But do it in private, just between the two of you. If that person listens, you have won back a follower.
translation_verse: “Sɛ wo nua fom wo a, kɔ na kɔka ne mfomsoɔ kyerɛ no, berɛ a ho mmienu nkoaa hoahyia. Sɛ otie wo a, woanya wo nua.

english_verse: I am sure that finding it would make you happier than having the
translation_verse: Sɛ ohu no a, nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, n’ani gye ne ho kyɛne aduokro-nkron a bɛanyera he.

english_verse: That's how it is with your Father in heaven. He doesn't want any of these little ones to be lost. When Someone Sins (Luke
translation_verse: Saa ara nso ne ɔnyɛ ho Agya a ɔya soro he pɛ sɛ nketewa yi mu baako bɛyera. Sɛdeɛ beyi bi a w’ayɛ bɔne atɛne

english_verse: But if that one refuses to listen, take along one or two others. The Scriptures teach that every complaint must be proven true by two or more witnesses.
translation_verse: Na sɛ wanntie wo a, fa nipa baako anaa mmienu ka wo ho, na asɛm he nyina adansefoɔ mmienu anaa mmeɛnsa so.

english_verse: I promise you God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth, but God will not allow anything you don't allow.
translation_verse: Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho, deɛ hobɛkyekyere no asaase so he yɛ deɛ bɛakyekyere no soro, na deɛ hobɛsane no asaase so he yɛ deɛ bɛasane no soro.

english_verse: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
translation_verse: Bio, nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, ho mu mmienu yɛ anokorɔ adeɛ biaa a hobebisa no asaase yi so a, m’Agya a ɔya soro he bɛyɔ ama ho.

english_verse: Whenever two or three of you come together in my name, I am there with you. An Official Who Refused To Forgive
translation_verse: Baabi a nnipa mmienu anaa mmeɛnsa ahyia me dini mu he, na meka bɛ ho.” Bɔnefakyɛ

english_verse: Peter came up to the Lord and asked, “How many times should I forgive someone who does something wrong to me? Is seven times enough?”
translation_verse: Na Petro baa ne nkyɛne bebisaa no sɛ, “Awurade, mprɛ dodoɔ sɛ ne me nua yɔ bɔne tia me a me mfa nkyɛ no? Onkoduru mprɛ nson?”

english_verse: This story will show you what the kingdom of heaven is like: One day a king decided to call in his officials and ask them to give an account of what they owed him.
translation_verse: “Ne saa nti bɛde soro ahennie he bɛtoto hene bi a ɔpɛɛ sɛ ɔne ne nkoa bu akonta ho.

english_verse: As he was doing this, one official was brought in who owed him
translation_verse: Berɛ a ɔhyɛɛ nkotabuo ase he, bɛde bi a ɔde no ka a ɔboro paani mfie dunnum akatua begyinaa n’anim;

english_verse: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
translation_verse: esiane sɛ ontumi ntua nti, ne wura he hyɛɛ sɛ bɛntɔno ɔne ne yerɛ ne ne mma ne n’agyapadeɛ nyinaa metua ka he.

english_verse: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
translation_verse: Ne saa nti akoa he buu nkotodwe hwee ase n’anim, srɛɛ no sɛ, ‘Nya aboterɛ ma me, na metua ne nyinaa.’

english_verse: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
translation_verse: Na akoa he wura he nyaa tema maa no, na ogyaa no de ne ka he kyɛɛ no.

english_verse: The man got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you back.”
translation_verse: Afei ne yɔnko akoa he de ne ho too fam srɛɛ no sɛ, ‘Nya aboterɛ ma me, na metua wo ka.’

english_verse: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
translation_verse: Nso wampene; na ɔde no kɔtoo afiase sɛ ɔnna hɔ nkosi sɛ obetua ne ka he.

english_verse: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
translation_verse: Berɛ a nkoaa a bɛka ne ho he huu deɛ asi he, bɛ werɛ hoo paa, na bɛkɔkaa deɛ asi nyinaa kyerɛɛ bɛ wura he.

english_verse: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
translation_verse: Na ne wura he frɛɛ no ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, ‘Wo akoa timuɔdenfoɔ! Mede wo ka a wo de me nyinaa kyɛɛ wo esiane sɛ wosrɛɛ me nti.

english_verse: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
translation_verse: Na ne wura he de abufuo de no hyɛɛ ahohiahiafoɔ nsa, kosi sɛ obetua ne ka a ɔde no nyinaa.

english_verse: That is how my Father in heaven will treat you, if you don't forgive each of my followers with all your heart.
translation_verse: Saa ne m’Agya a ɔya soro he so bɛyɔ ho mu bi a wamfiri n’akoma mu amfa ankyɛ ne nua.”

english_verse: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Yesu ka yeinom wiee he, ofirii Galilea kɔɔ Yudea mpɔtamu hɔ Yordan akyi.

english_verse: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
translation_verse: Na binom ya hɔ a bɛwoo bɛ he na bɛ ho nni hɔ, na binom nso ya hɔ a, nnipa ne ama bɛ ayɛ saa, na binom so ya hɔ a bɛ ankasa, ne soro ahennie he nti, bɛayɔ ama bɛ ho nni hɔ. Deɛ obetumi agye yei he, ɔnye.” Yesu hyiraa mmɔfra (Marko

english_verse: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
translation_verse: Na nnipa nnipadɔm pii dii n’akyi, na ɔsaa bɛ yadeɛ hɔ.

english_verse: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
translation_verse: Na Farisifoɔ baa ne nkyɛne bɛsɔɔ no hwɛeɛ, sɛ, “Mmara ma ho kwane sɛ asɛm bi nti beema bi gyae ne yerɛ anaa?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
translation_verse: Obuaa sɛ, “Honkenkane sɛ, mfitiaseɛ he, beema ne baa ne Nyankopɔn bɔɔ bɛ

english_verse: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
translation_verse: na ɔkaa sɛ, ‘Yei nti ne beema begya ne papa ne ne maame, na ɔde ne ho akɔbata ne yerɛ, na bɛ mmienu ayɛ honam baako’?

english_verse: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
translation_verse: Ne saa nti bɛnna so nyɛ mmienu bio, na mmom bɛyɛ honam korɔ. Nti deɛ Nyankopɔn de abom he, ɔnsɛ sɛ biaa tete mu.”

english_verse: The Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why did Moses say a man could write out divorce papers and send his wife away?”
translation_verse: Afei bebisaa no sɛ, “Hedeɛ, mmɛ nti ne Mose hyɛɛ yɛ sɛ, bi betumi ama awaregyaeɛ krataa na wagyae baa he?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You are so heartless! That's why Moses allowed you to divorce your wife. But from the beginning God did not intend it to be that way.
translation_verse: Ɔka kyerɛ bɛ sɛ, “Ɔyɛ ho akomadene nti ne Mose maa ho kwane sɛ honyae ho yerɛnom, na ofiri mfitiaseɛ he, na ɔnte saa.

english_verse: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
translation_verse: Na meeka akyerɛ ho, sɛ ɔnyɛ adwamammɔ nti, na bi gyae ne yerɛ na ɔsane ware foforɔ a, wasɛe awareɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Ɔnyɛ biaa ne betumi agye saa nkyerɛkyerɛ yi, gye sɛ bɛ a bɛde ho nteaseɛ ama bɛ nkoaa.

english_verse: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Afei bɛde mmɔfra braa Yesu, sɛ ɔmfa ne nsa ngu bɛ so mmɔ mpaeɛ mma bɛ. Na Asuafoɔ he kaa bɛ a bɛde bɛ baeɛ he anim;

english_verse: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Afei, nipa bi baa Yesu nkyɛne bebisaa no sɛ, “Kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ, mmɛ nwuma pa ne menyɔ na manya daa nkwa?”

english_verse: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
translation_verse: Ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Mu deɛ ɔya he?” Na Yesu kaa sɛ, “Nni awu; Nsɛe awareɛ; Mmɔ korɔno; Nni adansekunumu;

english_verse: After Jesus had placed his hands on the children, he left. A Rich Young Man (Mark
translation_verse: Na ɔde ne nsa guu bɛ so hyiraa bɛ na ɔkɔɔ ne kwane. Daa nkwa (Marko

english_verse: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
translation_verse: Na obisaa no sɛ, ‘Mmɛ nti ne wobisa me papa ho asɛm? Baako pɛ ne yɛ papa. Sɛ wopɛ sɛ wo nya nkwa a, di ahyɛdeɛ he so.”

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Nso Yesu kaa sɛ, “Homma mmɔfra he mmra me nkyɛne, na ho nsi bɛ kwane; na bɛ a bɛte saa ne soro ahennie he yɛ bɛ dea.”

english_verse: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
translation_verse: Nso adikanfoɔ he mu pii bedi akyire, na akyikafoɔ mmom abedi kane.

english_verse: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: Di w’agya ne wo na nni; na afei, Dɔ wo yɔnko sɛ wo ho.”

english_verse: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
translation_verse: Abranteɛ he ka kyerɛɛ Yesu sɛ, “Maadi yeinom nyinaa so; mmɛ bio ne ɔsɛ sɛ meyɔ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Sɛ wo pɛ sɛ wo woyɛ pɛ a, kɔ, tɔno w’ahonyadeɛ, na fa sika he ma ahiafoɔ, na wobenya agyapadeɛ soro; na afei bra, bedi m’akyi.”

english_verse: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
translation_verse: Berɛ a abranteɛ he tee saa asɛm he, ɔde awerɛhoɔ firii hɔ kɔeɛ, efiri sɛ na ɔwɔ ahonyadeɛ pii.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
translation_verse: Na Yesu ka kyerɛɛ asuafoɔ he sɛ, “Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho, ɔbɛyɛ dene ama sikani sɛ ɔbɛkɔ soro ahennie mu.

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Bio, meeka akyerɛ ho, ɔbɛyɛ mmerɛ ama yoma sɛ ɔbɛfa paneɛ tokuro mu sene sɛ sikani bɛkɔ Nyankopɔn ahennie mu.”

english_verse: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Asuafoɔ he tee yei he, ɔyɛɛ bɛ nwanwa kɛse, na bɛkaa sɛ “Sei ne ɔteɛ deɛ a, hwane ne betumi anya nkwa?”

english_verse: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Na Yesu hwɛɛ bɛ kaa sɛ, “Nnipa fam deɛ ɔnyɛ yie, nso Nyankopɔn fam deɛ bibiaa nni hɔ a ontumi nyɛ hɔ.”

english_verse: Peter replied, “Remember, we have left everything to be your followers! What will we get?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Yesu buaa bɛ sɛ, “Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho, sɛ Nipa Ba he tena n’animuonyam adwa so a, na nnoɔma nyinaa yɛ foforɔ a, ho a hoadi m’akyi yi nso ho bɛtena ahennwa dummienu so, abu Israel mmusuakuo dummienu he atɛne.

translation_verse: Na biaa a me dini nti, wagya afie anaa nuammeema anaa nuammaa, anaa papa anaa maame anaa mma, anaa nsaase hɔ he, obenya no mmɔho ha, na obenya daa nkwa nso.

english_verse: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
translation_verse: “Na soro ahennie he te sɛ afuowura bi a ɔkɔɔ abɔntene anɔpatutuutu sɛ ɔɔkɔpɛ apaafoɔ akɔ ne bobe turo mu.

english_verse: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
translation_verse: Ɔne bɛ dii ano maa osii sɛ obetua paani da korɔ akatua ama he, ɔmaa bɛkɔɔ ne bobe turo mu.

english_verse: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
translation_verse: Berɛ a ofirii abɔntene bɛyɛ nnɔn nkron he, ohuu apaafoɔ foforɔ dwaso sɛ begyina hɔ a bɛnnyɔ hwee.

english_verse: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, ‘Ho nso ho nkɔ bobe turo mu hɔ, na metua ho ka sɛdeɛ ɔsɛ.’ Nti bɛkɔeɛ.

english_verse: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
translation_verse: Na ɔbɔɔ bɛyɛ nnɔn num he ɔkɔɔ abɔntene kohuu sɛ binom gyinagyina hɔ; na obisaa bɛ sɛ, ‘Mmɛ nti ne hogyina hɔ kwa da mu nyinaa?’

english_verse: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
translation_verse: Bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, ‘Nipa biaa mmɔ yɛ paa nti a.’ Ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, ‘Ho nso honkɔ bobe turo mu hɔ.’

english_verse: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
translation_verse: Nnwunu dwoeɛ he, bobe turo wura he ka kyerɛɛ ne hwɛsoni sɛ, ‘Frɛ apaafoɔ he na tua bɛ ka, firi deɛ otwa toɔ so kosi deɛ odi kane so.’

english_verse: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
translation_verse: Ɔfrɛɛ apaafoɔ a ɔfaa bɛ nnɔn num he baeɛ, na ɔmaa bɛ mu biaa sɛnea paani dakorɔ akatua teɛ.

english_verse: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
translation_verse: Na bɛ nsa kaeɛ he, benwinwini tiaa bobe turo wura he,

english_verse: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
translation_verse: kaa sɛ, ‘Yeinom a bɛbaa akyire yi yɔɔ adwuma dɔnhwere baako pɛ, nso woama yɛ a yɛayɔ adwuma da mũ nyinaa ama awia ahye yɛ ne bɛ nyinaa pɛ.’

english_verse: The owner answered one of them, “Friend, I didn't cheat you. I paid you exactly what we agreed on.
translation_verse: Nso obuaa bɛ mu baako sɛ, ‘Damfo, wotee me aseɛ sɛ wobɛgye dakorɔ akatua; nti menyɔɔ wo bɔne biaa.

english_verse: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
translation_verse: Fa wo akatua kɔ; ɔyɛ mepɛ sɛ mɛma bɛ a bɛbaa akyire yi sɛ wo.

english_verse: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
translation_verse: Menni ho kwane sɛ mede m’agyapadeɛ yɔ deɛ meaa me pɛ? Anaa m’ayamyɛ nti na w’ani abere yi?

english_verse: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
translation_verse: Saa ne akyidifoɔ bedi kane, na adikanfoɔ mmom abedi akyire.” Yesu wuo ne ne wusɔreɛ ho nkɔmhyɛ a ɔtɔ so mmeɛnsa (Marko

english_verse: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”

english_verse: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.

english_verse: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.

english_verse: Jesus asked her what she wanted, and she said, “When you come into your kingdom, please let one of my sons sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
translation_verse: Na obisaa no sɛ, “Mmɛ ne woopɛ?” Na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Hyɛ sɛ w’ahennie mu hɔ me mma mmienu yi bɛtena wo nifa ne wo benkum so.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Not one of you knows what you are asking. Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from?” James and John said, “Yes, we are!”
translation_verse: Nso Yesu buaa sɛ, “Wonnim deɛ woobisa he. Hobetumi anom kuruwaa a mɛnom he bi?” Bebuaa sɛ, “Yebetumi.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Kuruwaa a mɛnom he deɛ ho nso hobɛnom, nso sɛ hobɛtena me nsa nifa ne benkum so deɛ, ɔnnyɛ me ne mede bɛma, na mmom, ɔya hɔ ma bɛ a m’ Agya asiesie de ama bɛ.”

english_verse: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with the two brothers.
translation_verse: Berɛ a asuafoɔ du he tee yei he, bɛ bo fuu anuanom mmienu yi paa.

english_verse: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know foreign rulers like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over everyone they rule.

english_verse: But don't act like them. If you want to be great, you must be the servant of all the others.

english_verse: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.

translation_verse: Bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Awurade, ma yenhu adeɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said: I will tell you a story about a man who had two sons. Then you can tell me what you think. The father went to the older son and said, “Go work in the vineyard today!”
translation_verse: Yesu bisaa sɛ, “Hodwene yei ho sɛ? Beema bi wɔ mma mmienu; ɔkɔɔ deɛ odi kane he hɔ, na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, ‘Me ba, kɔ na kɔyɔ bobe turo he mu adwuma nnɛ.’

english_verse: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: Nso berɛ a nhwɛsofoɔ he huu ne ba he, bɛka kyerɛɛ bɛ ho bɛ ho sɛ, ‘Yei ne nea obedi adeɛ he; homma yenku no na yɛmfa n’agyapadeɛ he.’

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs them.’ He will at once let you have the donkeys.”
translation_verse: Sɛ bi bisa ho asɛm a, ho deɛ honka sɛ, ‘Bɛ ho hia ma Awurade.’ Na ntɛmaa ɔbɛma ho de bɛ aba.”

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,

translation_verse: “Honka nkyerɛ Sion babaa, Hwɛ, wo hene ɔɔba wo nkyɛne, ɔdwo, na ɔte afunumu so, afunumu ba a ɔsoa nnoɔma so.”

english_verse: When Jesus came to Jerusalem, everyone in the city was excited and asked, “Who can this be?”

translation_verse: Bibiaa a hode gyidie bɛsrɛ mpaebɔ mu he, ho nsa bɛka.” Bebisaa tumi a Yesu de di dwuma (Marko

translation_verse: na bebisaa no sɛ, “Nti wonte deɛ yeinom bɛɛka yi?” Yesu buaa bɛ sɛ, “Ɛɛ! honkenkanee no atwerɛ he mu sɛ, ‘Ofiri mmɔfra ne bɛ a betua nofoɔ ano na woasiesie ayeyie ama wo ho’?”

translation_verse: Na afei ogyaa bɛ hɔ, firii hɔ kɔɔ Betania, na ɔdaa hɔ anadwo he. Yesu domee borɔdoma dua bi

english_verse: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,

translation_verse: Saa nti bebuaa Yesu sɛ, “Yennim.” Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Saa ara ne me nso mennka tumi korɔ a mede yɔ yeinom nkyerɛ ho.” Setie ne asoɔdene ho asɛm

english_verse: Jesus told the chief priests and leaders to listen to this story: A land owner once planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it and dug a pit to crush the grapes in. He also built a lookout tower. Then he rented out his vineyard and left the country.
translation_verse: Afei Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Hontie mfantoho foforɔ yi, afuowura bi yɔɔ bobe turo, too bane faa ho, tuu mu nsa-kyi-amena, na osii abane tentene. Na ɔde hyɛɛ nhwɛsofoɔ nsa firii hɔ kɔɔ mane foforɔ so.

english_verse: Jesus had gone into the temple and was teaching when the chief priests and the leaders of the people came up to him. They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: Na afei berɛ a Yesu wuraa asɔredane mu hɔ he, asɔfoɔ mpanyimfoɔ ne mane he mu mpanyimfoɔ baa ne nkyɛne, berɛ a ɔɔkyerɛkyerɛ he. Na bebisaa no sɛ, “Mmɛ tumi ne wode yɔ saa nnoɔma yi, na hwane ne maa wo saa tumi yi?”

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?” They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
translation_verse: Na Yohane asubɔ he firi soro, anaa ofiri nnipa?” Na besusuu ho sɛ, “Sɛ yɛka sɛ, ‘Ofiri soro’ a, obebisa yɛ sɛ, ‘Mmɛ nti ne hoannye no anni?’

english_verse: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
translation_verse: Nso sɛ yɛka sɛ, ‘Ofiri nnipa,’ a yesuro nea nnipadɔm he bɛyɔ; efiri sɛ bɛ nyinaa bebu Yohane sɛ diyifoɔ.”

english_verse: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.

translation_verse: Hohwɛ a bɛ mmienu mu hwane ne yɔɔ ne papa he apɛdeɛ?” Bebuaa sɛ, “Deɛ odi kane he a.” Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho, togyefoɔ ne adwamammɔfoɔ oodi ho anim kane Nyankopɔn ahennie mu,

translation_verse: efiri sɛ Yohane baa ho nkyɛne tenenee kwane so, nso hoanye no anni, na togyefoɔ ne nwamanmɔfoɔ gyee no diiɛ; mpo akyire yi a hohuiɛ yɛ nso, hoannsesa ho adwene anye no anni. Nhwɛsofoɔ bɔne he (Marko

english_verse: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
translation_verse: Berɛ a twaberɛ duruiɛ he, ɔsomaa ne nkoa kɔɔ nhwɛsofoɔ he hɔ sɛ bɛnkɔgye n’afudeɛ mmra no.

english_verse: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.

english_verse: He then sent more servants than he did the first time. But the renters treated them in the same way.
translation_verse: Na ɔsane somaa nkoa foforɔ a bɛ dɔɔso kyɛne kane deɛ he; na bɛde bɛfaa saa kwane heaa so.

english_verse: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.

english_verse: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”

english_verse: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
translation_verse: Bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Ɔbɛsɛe saa nnipa bɔnefoɔ he pasaa, na ɔde bobe turo he ama nhwɛsofoɔ foforɔ a bɛde afudeɛ bɛbra no twaberɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Honkenkanee no twerɛ nsɛm he mu pɛn sɛ: ‘Boɔ a abantofoɔ apo he; abɛyɛ tweaso tiboɔ titire; yei yɛ deɛ Awurade ayɔ, na ɔyɛ yɛ ani so nwanwa’ anaa?

english_verse: I tell you God's kingdom will be taken from you and given to people who will do what he demands.
translation_verse: Nti meeka akyerɛ ho, bɛbɛgye Nyankopɔn ahennie he afiri ho nsam na bɛde ama bɛ a bɛsõ ahennie he ho aba.”

english_verse: Anyone who stumbles over this stone will be crushed, and anyone it falls on will be smashed to pieces.”
translation_verse: [Deɛ ɔbɛhwe saa boɔ yi so he mu bɛpaepae; na nea boɔ he bɛhwe ne so biaa nso ɔbɛyam no muhumuhu.]

english_verse: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.
translation_verse: Na bɛpɛ sɛ bɛkye no, nso na besuro nnipadɔm he, efiri sɛ na nnipadɔm he gye no tom sɛ diyifoɔ.

english_verse: Many are invited, but only a few are chosen. Paying Taxes (Mark
translation_verse: Na bɛafrɛ bebree nso kakraa bi na bɛayi bɛ.” Toɔtua ho asɛmmisa (Marko

english_verse: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Na afei Farisifoɔ he kotuu agyina sɛ bebesum Yesu fidie asɛm a ɔbɛka ho.

english_verse: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: “Kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ, Mose kaa sɛ, ‘Sɛ beema bi ware na wanwo ba na owu a, ne nua nware kunafoɔ he, na wawo mma ama ne nua a waawu he.’

english_verse: Once again Jesus used stories to teach the people:
translation_verse: Bio, Yesu de mfantoho bi kyerɛkyerɛ bɛ sɛ:

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a king gave a wedding banquet for his son.
translation_verse: “Soro ahennie he te sɛ hene bi a otoo ayefohyia pono maa ne ba.

english_verse: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
translation_verse: Ɔsomaa ne nkoa sɛ bɛnkɔfrɛ bɛ a bɛtoo nsa frɛɛ bɛ ayefohyia he ase nyinaa, nso bɛampɛ sɛ bɛba.

english_verse: He sent other servants to say to the guests, “The banquet is ready! My cattle and prize calves have all been prepared. Everything is ready. Come to the banquet!”
translation_verse: Nti ɔsane somaa nkoa foforɔ, kaa sɛ, ‘Honka nkyerɛ bɛ a bɛato nsa afrɛ bɛ he sɛ: Hwɛ masiesie me nnuane, maaku m’anantwie ne mmoa a bɛadɔre sradeɛ, mawie nnoɔma nyinaa; hommra ayefohyia apontoɔ he ase.’

english_verse: But the guests did not pay any attention. Some of them left for their farms, and some went to their places of business.
translation_verse: Nso bɛamfa n’asɛm he annyɛ hwee na bɛkɔɔ bɛ baabi, baako kɔɔ n’afuom, bi nso kɔɔ n’adwuma,

english_verse: Others grabbed the servants, then beat them up and killed them.
translation_verse: binom nso kyee nkoa he, bɔɔ bɛ adapaa kukuu bɛ.

english_verse: This made the king so furious that he sent an army to kill those murderers and burn down their city.
translation_verse: Na hene he bo fuiɛ. Ɔsomaa ne dɔm maa bɛkɔsɛe saa wudifoɔ he hyee bɛ kuro.

english_verse: Then he said to the servants, “It is time for the wedding banquet, and the invited guests don't deserve to come.
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ ne nkoa he sɛ, ‘Bɛasiesie ayefohyia apontoɔ he awie, nso bɛ a bɛtoo nsa frɛ bɛ he, amfata bɛ.

english_verse: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”
translation_verse: Nti honkɔ mmɔntene so, na homfrɛ biaa a hobehu no ma no mmra ayefohyia apontoɔ he ase.’

english_verse: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
translation_verse: “Nso berɛ a hene he behuu ahɔhoɔ he, ohuu beema bi a na ɔnhyɛ ayefohyia ataadeɛ he bi,

english_verse: Tell us what you think! Should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
translation_verse: Kyerɛ yɛ nea wodwene fa yei ho. Ɔsɛ sɛ yetua toɔ ma Kaesare, anaa sɛ ɔnsɛ yetua ma no?”

english_verse: The same thing happened to the second and third brothers and finally to all seven of them.
translation_verse: Deɛ ɔtɔ so mmienu he nso wuiɛ a wannya ba. Asɛm korɔ heaa too nea ɔtɔ so mmeɛnsa ne bɛ a bɛaka he nyinaa kosii nea ɔtɔ so nson he so.

english_verse: They sent some of their followers and some of Herod's followers to say to him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Nti bɛsomaa bɛ asuafoɔ ne Herodefoɔ kɔɔ ne nkyɛne, kaa sɛ, “Kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ, yenim sɛ woyɛ nɔkwafoɔ, wonam nɔkorɛ so kyerɛkyerɛ Nyankopɔn kwane, na wonsuro nipa biaa, saa nso ne wonhwɛ nnipa anim.

english_verse: Let me see one of the coins used for paying taxes.” They brought him a silver coin,
translation_verse: Homfa dwetɛ deɛ yɛde tua toɔ he bi nkyerɛ me.” Na bɛde denare braa no.

english_verse: “The Emperor's,” they answered. Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
translation_verse: Bebuaa sɛ, “Kaesare dea.” Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Saa deɛ a, homfa deɛ ɔyɛ Kaesare deɛ mma Kaesare, na homfa deɛ ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn deɛ mma Nyankopɔn.”

english_verse: His answer surprised them so much that they walked away. Life in the Future World (Mark
translation_verse: Berɛ a bɛteeɛ he, bɛ ho dwirii bɛ; na begyaa ne hɔ kɔeɛ. Wusɔreɛ ho asɛmmisa (Marko

english_verse: All the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets are based on these two commandments. About David's Son (Mark
translation_verse: Saa mmaransɛm mmienu yi so ne mmara ne adiyifoɔ he nyinaa gyina.” Dawid ba ho asɛmmisa

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
translation_verse: Saa da heaa Sadukifoɔ a bɛ gyidie ne sɛ wusɔreɛ biaa nni hɔ he mu bi baa Yesu nkyɛne; na bebisaa no sɛ,

english_verse: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
translation_verse: Yesu buaa bɛ sɛ, “Nea hooka yi, te ne honte aseɛ yie, efiri sɛ honnim atwerɛeɛ he anaa Nyankopɔn tumi.

english_verse: When God raises people to life, they won't marry. They will be like the angels in heaven.
translation_verse: Na wusɔreɛ akyi he mmeema nware, na bɛmfa mma nso mma awareɛ, sɛnea abɔfoɔ a bɛya soro nware he, saa ara ne bɛ nso bɛnware.

english_verse: “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living.
translation_verse: ‘Meyɛ Abraham Nyankopɔn, Isak Nyankopɔn, ne Yakob Nyankopɔn anaa’? Ɔnnyɛ awufoɔ Nyankopɔn, na mmom ɔyɛ ateasefoɔ Nyankopɔn.”

english_verse: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
translation_verse: Na berɛ a nnipadɔm he tee saa he, ne nkyerɛkyerɛ he yɛɛ bɛ ahodwiri. Dɔ ne mmara he mu kɛseɛ paa (Marko

english_verse: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a Farisifoɔ he tee sɛ Yesu aka Sadukifoɔ he ano atum he, behyia faako.

english_verse: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
translation_verse: “Kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ, mmaransɛm he mu nea ɔya he ne yɛ kɛseɛ paa?”

english_verse: This is the first and most important commandment.
translation_verse: Yei ne mmaransɛm he mu kɛseɛ paa ne deɛ odi kane.

english_verse: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: Na deɛ ɔtɔ so mmienu a ɔte sɛ ɔno ne sɛ: ‘Dɔ wo yɔnko sɛ wo ho.’

english_verse: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a Farisifoɔ he hyiaeɛ he, Yesu bisaa bɛ asɛm yi sɛ:

english_verse: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
translation_verse: ‘Awurade ka kyerɛɛ m’Awurade sɛ, “Tena me nsa nifa so, kosi sɛ mede w’atamfoɔ bɛhyɛ wo nane ase” ’?

english_verse: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
translation_verse: “Ɛɛne ne hodwene fa Kristo he ho? Ɔyɛ hwane ba?” Bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Ɔyɛ Dawid ba.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “How then could the Spirit lead David to call the Messiah his Lord? David said,
translation_verse: Ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Saa deɛ a mmɛ nti ne Dawid nam Honhom he so frɛ no Awurade, berɛ a ɔkaa sɛ,

english_verse: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
translation_verse: Sɛ Dawid frɛ no Awurade a, ɔyɛɛ dɛ na ɔyɛ ne ba?”

english_verse: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
translation_verse: Biaa antumi ammua no asɛm biaa, na ofiri saa da he biaa ammisa no asɛm biaa bio.

english_verse: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
translation_verse: Biaa a ɔma ne ho so he bɛbrɛ no ase, na biaa a ɔbrɛ ne ho ase he bɛbɛma no so.

english_verse: And now your temple will be deserted.
translation_verse: Hwɛ, obegya wo fie ama no ada mpan.

english_verse: Whoever is the greatest should be the servant of the others.
translation_verse: Deɛ ɔyɛ kɛseɛ paa ho mu he bɛyɛ ho somfoɔ.

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: “Yerusalem, Yerusalem, kuro a oku adiyifoɔ na osi bɛ a bɛasoma bɛ ne nkyɛne aboɔ! Hwɛ mprɛ dodoɔ a meepɛ sɛ mɛboaboa wo mma ano sɛdeɛ akokɔ baatane boaboa ne mma ano ne ntabane ase, nso hoampɛ!

english_verse: Anyone who swears by the altar also swears by everything on it.
translation_verse: Nti biaa a ɔde afɔrebokyia ka ntam he ka ɔne deɛ ɔya so nyinaa ntam;

english_verse: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
translation_verse: Afei Yesu ka kyerɛɛ nnipadɔm he ne n’asuafoɔ he sɛ,

english_verse: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: “Atwerɛfoɔ ne Farisifoɔ he atena Mose akonnwa so;

english_verse: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
translation_verse: Ne saa nti honyɔ deɛ bɛbɛkyerɛkyerɛ ho biaa na honni so.

english_verse: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
translation_verse: Bɛkyekyere nnesoa duruduru a ne soa ɔdene, na bɛde ato afoforɔ mmati so; nso bɛ ankasa deɛ bɛmpɛ sɛ bɛde bɛ nsa teaa mpo ka nnesoa he.

english_verse: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
translation_verse: Na bɛyɔ bɛ nnwuma nyinaa sɛ ɔbɛyɛ a nipa behu bɛ. Na bɛyɔ bɛ nkaeɛ nwoma atɛtrɛtɛ, na bɛyɔ bɛ ntaadeɛ ano nso akɛseɛ.

english_verse: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
translation_verse: Bɛpɛ tenabea a anidie ya mu ya apontoɔ ase, ɔne hyiadane mu nkonnwa pa,

english_verse: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
translation_verse: ne dwa so nkyea a anidie yam, ɔne sɛ nipa bɛfrɛ bɛ Rabi!

english_verse: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
translation_verse: Nso ɔnsɛ sɛ bɛfrɛ ho Rabi, efiri sɛ ho kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ yɛ baako, na ho nyinaa hoyɛ asuafoɔ.

english_verse: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Na hommfrɛ biaa ho Agya asaase so, efiri sɛ, howɔ Agya baako, deɛ ɔya soro he.

english_verse: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
translation_verse: Afei homma bɛmmfrɛ ho akyerɛkyerɛfoɔ nso, efiri sɛ, howɔ kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ baako, ɔno ne Kristo he.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
translation_verse: [Ho atwerɛfoɔ ne Farisifoɔ, nyaatwomfoɔ honnue! Hofa akunafoɔ nnoɔma na sɛdeɛ bebehu ho nti hobɔ mpaeɛ atentene; nti hobenya afobuo a mu oduru.]

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
translation_verse: Ho, atwerɛfoɔ ne Farisifoɔ, nyaatwomfoɔ, honnue! Efiri sɛ, hotware po ne asaase kɔ sɛ hokɔsakra nipa baako, na homa saa nipa he bɛyɛ ho fekuoni; na sɛ ɔyɛ a, homma ɔbɛfata brosamgyam amanehunu mprenu sɛ ho ankasa.

english_verse: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
translation_verse: “Akwankyerɛfoɔ anifirafoɔ, honnue! Ho a hoka sɛ, ‘Sɛ bi de hyiadane ka ntam a, ntam he nyɛ hwee, na mmom sɛ bi de hyiadane mu hɔ sikakɔkɔɔ ka ntam a ntam he kyekyere no.’

english_verse: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
translation_verse: Ho anifirafoɔ ne nkwaseafoɔ! Deɛ ɔya he ne sombo, sikakɔkɔɔ he anaa hyiadane he a woama sikakɔkɔɔ he ho ate he?

english_verse: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
translation_verse: Na hoka sɛ, ‘Deɛ ɔde afɔrebokyia ka ntam he, hwee nkyekyere no, nso biaa a ɔde akyɛdeɛ a ɔda afɔrebokyia he so ka ntam he, ntam he kyekyere no.’

english_verse: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
translation_verse: Ho ani afira paa! Na deɛ ɔya he ne sombo, akyɛdeɛ he anaa afɔrebokyia a ɔma akyɛdeɛ he ho te he?

english_verse: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
translation_verse: na biaa a ɔde hyiadane he ka ntam he, ɔde ɔne deɛ ɔte mu he ne ka ntam;

english_verse: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
translation_verse: na biaa a ɔde soro ka ntam he, ka Nyankopɔn adwa ne deɛ ɔte so he ntam.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
translation_verse: “Honnue, atwerɛfoɔ ne Farisifoɔ, nyaatwomfoɔ! Efiri sɛ, hoyi mmee ne nunum ne kumin mma ho ntotosoɔ du du, na hobu ho ani gu mmara he mu nnoɔma deɛ ohia kyɛne yeinom he so: atɛntenenee ne mmɔborɔhunu ne gyidie. Yeinom a ohia kɛseɛ he ne nka ɔsɛ sɛ hoyɔ berɛ a hommu ho ani ngu deɛ aka he nso so.

english_verse: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.
translation_verse: Ho, akwankyerɛfoɔ anifirafoɔ! Hosɔne anonneɛ so yi nwansena to twene nso homene yoma!

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
translation_verse: “Atwerɛfoɔ ne Farisifoɔ, nyaatwomfoɔ honnue! Efiri sɛ, hohohoro kuruwaa ne ayowa ho, nso mu deɛ, adufudepɛ ne pɛsɛmenkomenya ahyɛ ma.

english_verse: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
translation_verse: Ho Farisifoɔ anifirafoɔ! Honhohoro kuruwaa he mu kane na ho he nso afiri.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
translation_verse: “Honnue, atwerɛfoɔ ne Farisifoɔ, nyaatwomfoɔ! Efiri sɛ, hote sɛ nna a bɛatwa ho fitafita a ɔyɛ fɛ abɔntene, nso mu deɛ funu nnompe ne fi ahodoɔ ahyɛ no ma.

english_verse: That's what you are like. Outside you look good, but inside you are evil and only pretend to be good.
translation_verse: Saa ne ho nso, afoforɔ hu ho ateneneefoɔ abɔntene, nso nyaatwom ne mmarato ahyɛ ho mu ma.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
translation_verse: “Honnue, atwerɛfoɔ ne Farisifoɔ, nyaatwomfoɔ! Efiri sɛ, hoyɔ adiyifoɔ bɛ nna na hosiesie ateneneefoɔ adamena,

english_verse: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
translation_verse: na hoka sɛ, ‘Sɛ na nka yɛya hɔ yɛ nananom berɛ so a, nka yɛ ne bɛ ommom nhwie adiyifoɔ he mogyaa ngu.’

english_verse: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
translation_verse: Nti hodi adanseɛ tia ho ho sɛ hoyɛ bɛ a bekuku adiyifoɔ he asefoɔ.

english_verse: So keep on doing everything they did.
translation_verse: Saa deɛ a honwie deɛ ho agyanom hyɛɛ aseɛ he.

english_verse: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
translation_verse: Awɔ, anankatia mma! Hobɛyɛ dɛ adwane atemmuo a ɔde hobɛkɔ bronsam gya mu he?

english_verse: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
translation_verse: Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho, yei nyinaa bɛba nnɛ mma yi so. Yerusalem ho kwadwom

english_verse: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Na meeka akyerɛ ho, honhu me bio de bɛkɔ akosi sɛ hobɛka sɛ, ‘Nhyira ne deɛ ɔɔba Awurade dini mu he.’ ”

english_verse: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
translation_verse: Na Yesu firii asɔredane mu a na ɔɔkɔ he, n’asuafoɔ he baa ne nkyɛne bɛkyerɛɛ no asɔrefie he mu adane ahodoɔ.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
translation_verse: Obisaa bɛ sɛ, “Hoahu yeinom nyinaa anaa? Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho, bennya boɔ baako mpo mma ɔnka a benwiri ngu.” Awieeɛ mmerɛ ho nsɛnkyerɛnne

english_verse: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
translation_verse: Efiri sɛ bebree bɛba me dini mu, abɛka sɛ, ‘Me ne Kristo he,’ na bɛbɛdaadaa nnipa bebree.

english_verse: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
translation_verse: Na hobɛte ko ne akosɛm; honhwɛ yie na ho akoma antu; efiri sɛ otwa sɛ yeinom nyinaa ba, nso na awieeɛ he nya mmaeɛ.

english_verse: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
translation_verse: Berɛ a ɔte Ngo Bepɔ so he, n’asuafoɔ he baa ne nkyɛne bebisaa no kokoam sɛ, “Ka kyerɛ yɛ, da bɛ ne yeinom besi, na mmɛ nsɛnkyerɛnne ne bɛda adi fa wo mmaeɛ ne awieeɛ berɛ he mu?”

english_verse: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
translation_verse: Na amane bɛsɔre atia amane, na ahennie bɛsɔre atia ahennie, kɔm bɛba na asaase wosoɔ nso bɛba baabi bebree.

english_verse: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
translation_verse: Bɛ a bɛafa afuro ne bɛ a bɛma mma nofoɔ nnue saa da he!

english_verse: Jesus answered: Don't let anyone fool you.
translation_verse: Yesu buaa bɛ sɛ, “Honhwɛ yie na bi annaadaa ho.

english_verse: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
translation_verse: Na yeinom nyinaa te sɛ awoko yea mfitiaseɛ. Taa ho nkɔmhyɛ

english_verse: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
translation_verse: “Saa mmerɛ he bebeyi ho ama sɛ bɛmfa homfa ahohiahia mu, na bɛaku ho, na me dini nti amane nyinaa bɛtane ho.

english_verse: Many will give up and will betray and hate each other.
translation_verse: Na bebree bɛpa bɛ gyidie, na bebeyiyi bɛ ho bɛ ho ama na bɛatentane bɛ ho bɛ ho.

english_verse: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
translation_verse: Na atorɔ adiyifoɔ bebree bɛsɔre abɛdaadaa nnipa bebree.

english_verse: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
translation_verse: Na sɛ bi ka kyerɛ ho sɛ, ‘Hwɛ, Kristo he di!’ Anaa ‘Ɔya ha a!’ Honnnye nnni.

english_verse: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
translation_verse: Hommɔ mpaeɛ sɛ saa da yi ɔmma awɔ anaa sutɔberɛ anaa homeda.

english_verse: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
translation_verse: “Nti sɛ hohu sɛ akyiwadeɛ a ɔbɔ mane he gyina kronkronbea hɔ a, sɛdeɛ diyifoɔ Daniel kaeɛ he a ,

english_verse: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
translation_verse: hedeɛ bɛ a bɛya Yudea he nnwane nkɔ bepɔ so;

english_verse: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
translation_verse: deɛ ɔya afuom nsane mma fie mmɛfa ataadeɛ.

english_verse: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
translation_verse: Sɛ bɛantwa saa nna he so a, biaa nni hɔ a obenya nkwa; nso bɛ a bɛayi bɛ he nti bebetwa nna he so tia.

english_verse: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
translation_verse: Saa ara nso ne sɛ hohu yeinom nyinaa a, honhu sɛ Kristo mmaeɛ he abɛne, ogyina pono ano.

english_verse: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
translation_verse: Na sɛdeɛ Noa nna he yɛeɛ he, saa ne Nipa Ba he so maeɛ bɛ yɛ.

english_verse: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
translation_verse: “Nso saa da ne dɔnhwere he deɛ biaa nnim, soro abɔfoɔ, anaa Ɔba he mpo nnim, gye sɛ Agya he nkoaa.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
translation_verse: Saa mmerɛ he, ansa na nsuhyiri he ɔɔba he, nnipa didiiɛ, na bɛnomeeɛ, mmeema wareeɛ na bɛde maa maa awareɛ, kosii sɛ Noa wuraa adaka he mu.

english_verse: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: Na bɛanhu hwee kosi sɛ nsuhyiri he baeɛ bɛfaa bɛ nyinaa kɔeɛ. Na sɛdeɛ ɔyɛeɛ he, saa ne ɔbɛyɛ Nipa Ba he mmaeɛ mu.

english_verse: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Ɔte sɛ nnipa mmienu ya afuom; bɛbɛfa baako na bɛagya baako hɔ.

english_verse: Two women will be together grinding grain, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Saa ara nso ne ɔte sɛ mmaa mmienu ɔɔyam wiyammoɔ so a; bɛbɛfa baako na bɛagya baako hɔ.

english_verse: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
translation_verse: Saa nti ho nkɔ so nwɛne, efiri sɛ honnim da korɔ a ho Wura bɛba.

english_verse: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
translation_verse: Honte aseɛ: Sɛ nka fiewura nim berɛ pɔtee dasuom a korɔmfoɔ bɛba a, nka ɔbɛnwɛne, na korɔmfoɔ annya kwane ammebu ne dane anwura mu.

english_verse: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
translation_verse: Nti ho nso honna ho ho so, efiri sɛ dɔnhwere a ho ani nna so mu na Nipa Ba he bɛba. Akoa nɔkwafoɔ ne akoa a onni nɔkorɛ

english_verse: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
translation_verse: Nso sɛ akoa timɔdenfoɔ he ka no ne tim sɛ, ‘Me wura akyɛre,’

english_verse: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
translation_verse: “Afei, hwane ne akoa nɔkwafoɔ ne nyansafoɔ a, ne wura de no asi ne fie so, sɛ ɔma nkoa a bɛhyɛ n’ase aduane ne berɛ mu?

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
translation_verse: Nhyira ne akoa a ne wura bɛba abehu no sɛ ɔɔyɔ saa.

english_verse: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
translation_verse: Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho, ɔbɛma saa akoa he adi n’agyapadeɛ nyinaa so.

english_verse: Suppose this evil servant starts beating the other servants and eats and drinks with people who are drunk.
translation_verse: na ofiri aseɛ boro nkoa a bɛhyɛ n’ase na ɔne nsaborofoɔ didi na bɛnom nsa a,

english_verse: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
translation_verse: ne wura he bɛba da korɔ a n’ani nna so ne dɔnhwere a onnim mu.

english_verse: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
translation_verse: Obetwitwa ne mu na ɔde no aka nyaatwomfoɔ ho ya baabi a suu ne setwerɛ ya.

english_verse: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
translation_verse: Berɛ a bɛ a bɛnyɛ mmadwemma he faa bɛ nkanea he bɛamfa ngo anka ho;

english_verse: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
translation_verse: nso mmadwemma he deɛ bɛfaa ngo kaa bɛ nkanea he ho.

english_verse: Then in the middle of the night someone shouted, “Here's the groom! Come to meet him!”
translation_verse: Nso anadwo dasuom bɛtee nteamu, ‘Hwɛ, ayefokunu he di! Hommra mmehyia no.’

english_verse: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
translation_verse: Nti mmabaa he nyinaa sɔre sosɔɔ bɛ nkanea he.

english_verse: the foolish ones said to the others, “Let us have some of your oil! Our lamps are going out.”
translation_verse: Bɛ a bɛnyɛ mmadwemma he ka kyerɛɛ mmadwemma he sɛ, ‘Homma yɛ ho ngo yi bi, na yɛ nkanea he oodumdum.’

english_verse: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
translation_verse: Nso mmadwemma he buaa bɛ sɛ, ‘Daabi, ɔnso yɛ ne ho; honkɔ adetɔnfoɔ hɔ nkɔtɔ bi.’

english_verse: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
translation_verse: Saa nti honwɛne, efiri sɛ honnim da anaa dɔnhwere korɔ he a Akontabuo ho mfantoho (Luka

english_verse: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
translation_verse: Mmerɛ tentene bi akyi he, nkoa he wura he baeɛ na ɔne bɛ bebuu akonta.

english_verse: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
translation_verse: Saa deɛ a nka ɔsɛ sɛ wode me sika he kɔto sika korabea, na mesane ba a, manya me sika he ne ho ba.

english_verse: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
translation_verse: “Na ɔte sɛ beema bi a ootu kwane, na ɔfrɛɛ ne nkoa na ɔde n’agyapadeɛ hyɛɛ bɛ nsa;

english_verse: As soon as the man had gone, the servant with the
translation_verse: Deɛ onyaa sika dwetire nnum he ntɛmaa ɔde kodii dwa, na ɔnyaa sika dwetire mmɔho nnum kaa ho.

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: Ne wura he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, ‘Mo ne yɔ, akoa pa ne nɔkwafoɔ; woadi kakra yi ho nɔkorɛ, mede nnoɔma bebree bɛhyɛ wo nsa; nya wo wura anigye he bi.’

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: Ne wura he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, ‘Mo ne yɔ, akoa pa nɔkwafoɔ; woadi kakra yi ho nɔkorɛ, mede nnoɔma bebree bɛhyɛ wo nsa; nya wo wura anigye he bi.’

english_verse: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
translation_verse: nti mesuroeɛ, na mede wo sika he kosiee fam. W’adeɛ di, gye.’

english_verse: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
translation_verse: Nso ne wura he buaa sɛ, ‘Wo akoa bɔne ne anihafoɔ! Wonnim sɛ metwa baabi a mennguiɛ, na meboaboa ano baabi a memmfa mmpeteɛ?

english_verse: Then the master said, “Now your money will be taken away and given to the servant with
translation_verse: Afei honye mfiri ne nsam, homfa mma nea ɔnyaa dwetire du he.

english_verse: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
translation_verse: Na bɛ a bɛanya bi aka nea bɛde ama bɛ ho he, bɛde bebree bɛka bɛ ho, na bɛanya ama no abu so; na bɛ a bɛanya hwee anka nea bɛde ama bɛ ho he deɛ, kakra a bɛwɔ he mpo, bɛbɛgye afiri bɛ nsam.

english_verse: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
translation_verse: Akoa a ne so nni mfasoɔ yi deɛ, homfa ne nto sum kabii mu, baabi a suu ne setwerɛ ya.’ Atemmuda kɛseɛ he

english_verse: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
translation_verse: “Sɛ Nipa Ba he ba n’animuonyam mu a, na abɔfoɔ nyinaa ka ne ho a, ɔbɛtena n’animuonyam adwa so.

english_verse: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
translation_verse: Amane nyinaa bɛboaboa bɛ ano n’anim, na ɔbɛpaepae nnipa mu sɛ dwanhwɛfoɔ paepae nnwane firi mmirekyie mu,

english_verse: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
translation_verse: na ɔde nnwane he begyina ne nsa nifa so na mmirekyie he nso ɔde bɛ agyina ne nsa benkum so.

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
translation_verse: Na hene he bɛka akyerɛ bɛ a bɛya ne nifa so he sɛ, ‘Hommra, ho a m’Agya ahyira ho, hommɛfa ahennie a bɛasiesie ama ho firi wiase mfitiaseɛ he nni;

english_verse: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,
translation_verse: efiri sɛ kɔm dee me na homaa me aduane diiɛ, nsukɔm dee me na homaa me nsuo nomeeɛ, meyɛɛ hɔhoɔ na hode me kɔɔ fie,

english_verse: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
translation_verse: medaa adagya na hofiraa me ntoma, meyareeɛ, na hohwɛɛ me, mekɔdaa afiase na hobɛsraa me.’

english_verse: Then the ones who pleased the Lord will ask, “When did we give you something to eat or drink?
translation_verse: Na teneneefoɔ he bebua sɛ, ‘Awurade, da bɛ ne yehuu sɛ kɔm de wo na yɛmaa wo aduane diiɛ, anaa nsukɔm de wo na yɛmaa wo nsuo nomeeɛ?

english_verse: When did we welcome you as a stranger or give you clothes to wear
translation_verse: Na da bɛ ne yehuu sɛ woyɛ hɔhoɔ na yɛfaa wo kɔɔ fie, anaa woda adagya na yefiraa wo ntoma?

english_verse: or visit you while you were sick or in jail?”
translation_verse: Na da bɛ ne yehuu sɛ woyare anaa woda afiase na yɛbaa wo nkyɛne?’

english_verse: The king will answer, “Whenever you did it for any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you did it for me.”
translation_verse: Na hene bebua bɛ sɛ, ‘Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho, sɛ hoyɔ maa me nuanom nkumaa yi mu baako he, hoayɔ ama me nso.’

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
translation_verse: Na ɔbɛka akyerɛ bɛ a bɛya ne nsa benkum so he sɛ, ‘Ho a bɛadome ho, homfiri me so nkɔ daa gya a bɛasiesie ama Bronsam ne n’abɔfoɔ he mu;

english_verse: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ kɔm dee me nso hoamma me aduane, nsukɔm dee me, nso hoamma me hwee annom,

english_verse: I was a stranger, but you did not welcome me, and I was naked, but you did not give me any clothes to wear. I was sick and in jail, but you did not take care of me.”
translation_verse: meyɛɛ hɔhoɔ nso hoamfa me ankɔ fie, medaa adagya nso hoamfira me ntoma, meyareeɛ na mekɔdaa afiase nso hoammɛsra me.’

english_verse: Then the people will ask, “Lord, when did we fail to help you when you were hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in jail?”
translation_verse: Hɔ ne bɛ so bɛbɛka sɛ, ‘Awurade, da bɛ na yehuu sɛ kɔm de wo anaa sukɔm de wo, anaa woyɛɛ hɔhoɔ anaa wodaa adagya anaa woyareɛ anaa wokɔdaa afiase, na yɛannyɔ wo yie?’

english_verse: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
translation_verse: Na obebua bɛ sɛ, ‘Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, sɛdeɛ hoannyɔ amma nkumaa yi mu baako he, saa ne hoannyɔ amma me nso.’

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”
translation_verse: Na yeinom befiri hɔ akɔ daa asotwe mu, na ateneneefoɔ he bɛkɔ daa nkwa mu.”

english_verse: “You know two days from now will be Passover. This is when the Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies and nailed to a cross.”
translation_verse: “Honim sɛ, aaka nnanu na Twam afahyɛ he aduru, na bebeyi Nipa Ba he ama, na bɛabɔ no asennua mu.”

english_verse: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
translation_verse: Na afei ɔbaa asuafoɔ he nkyɛne bɛka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Nti hogu so ɔɔda gye ho ahome? Hwɛ, berɛ he abɛne, sɛ bebeyi Nipa Ba he ahyɛ nnebɔnyɛfoɔ nsa.

english_verse: They planned how they could sneak around and have Jesus arrested and put to death.
translation_verse: na bɛbɔɔ pɔ sɛ bɛbɛfa nyansa kwane so akye Yesu aku no.

english_verse: But they said, “We must not do it during Passover, because the people will riot.” At Bethany (Mark
translation_verse: Nso bɛkaa sɛ, “Ɔnyɛ afahyɛ berɛ he mu ne ɔsɛ sɛ bɛyɔ saa, anyɛ saa basabasa bɛba mane he mu.” Bɛsraa Yesu ngo ya Betania (Marko

english_verse: We could have sold this perfume for a lot of money and given it to the poor.”
translation_verse: Nka bebetumi atɔno agye sika kɛseɛ, na bɛde sika he ama ahiafoɔ.”

english_verse: But when his disciples saw this, they became angry and complained, “Why such a waste?
translation_verse: Nso asuafoɔ he huiɛ he, bɛ bo fuiɛ, na bɛkaa sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne woasɛe aduhwam yi sei?

english_verse: Then they sang a hymn and went out to the Mount of Olives. Peter's Promise (Mark
translation_verse: Na bɛtoo ayeyie nnwom wieeɛ he, befiri adi kɔ Ngo Bepɔ he so. Yesu hyɛɛ pa a Petro bɛpa no he ho nkɔm (Marko

english_verse: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
translation_verse: na beedidi he, ɔkaa sɛ, “Meeka akyerɛ ho nɔkorɛ mu sɛ, ho mu baako beyi me ama.”

english_verse: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
translation_verse: Berɛ a beedidi he, Yesu faa paanoo, ohyiraa so wieeɛ he, obubu de maa asuafoɔ he kaa sɛ, “Honnye, honni; me nipadua di.”

english_verse: This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven.
translation_verse: na yei ne me mogyaa a ɔyɛ apam he mogyaa a, nnipa bebree nti beehwie agu ama bɔne fakyɛ.

english_verse: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Meeka akyerɛ ho, mennom saa bobesa yi bi bio kosi da a me ne ho bɛnom no kwane foforɔ so m’ Agya ahennie mu.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples, “During this very night, all of you will reject me, as the Scriptures say, ‘I will strike down the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’
translation_verse: Afei Yesu ka kyerɛɛ asuafoɔ he sɛ, “Anadwo yi, deɛ ɔbɛba me so he bɛto ho nyinaa sintidua; efiri sɛ bɛatwerɛ sɛ, ‘Mɛbɔ dwanhwɛfoɔ he, na nnwane he bɛhwete.’

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I promise you before a rooster crows tonight, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Meeka akyerɛ wo nɔkorem, anadwo yi, ansa na akokɔ bɛbɔno he, na woapa me mprɛnsa.”

english_verse: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
translation_verse: Afei Yesu ne n’asuafoɔ he kɔɔ bea bi a bɛfrɛ hɔ Getsemane; na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Hontena ha, na menkɔ nohoa nkɔbɔ mpaeɛ.”

english_verse: and he said to them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛ bɛ sɛ, “Awerɛhoɔ ahyɛ me kra so de ɔɔkɔ wuo mu; hontwɛne ha na ho ne me nwɛne.”

english_verse: Peter spoke up, “Even if all the others reject you, I never will!”
translation_verse: Petro ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Sɛdeɛ ɔbɛba wo so he bɛto biaa sintidua a, me deɛ ɔnto me sintidua koraa.”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you.” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Mark
translation_verse: Petro ka kyerɛ no sɛ, “Sɛ otwa sɛ me ne wo wu mpo a, mempa wo da.” Na saa ne asuafoɔ he nyinaa nso kaeɛ. Yesu bɔɔ mpaeɛ ya Getsemane (Marko

english_verse: Again Jesus went to pray and said, “My Father, if there is no other way, and I must suffer, I will still do what you want.”
translation_verse: Na ofirii hɔ kɔɔ ne mprenu so na ɔkɔbɔɔ mpaeɛ sɛ, “M’Agya, sɛ yei ntumi ntwa me ho na otwa sɛ menom a, ma wopɛ nyɛ hɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt with his face to the ground and prayed, “My Father, if it is possible, don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
translation_verse: Na ɔkɔɔ anim kakra he, ɔde n’anim kobutuu fam bɔɔ mpaeɛ sɛ, “M’Agya sɛ obetumi a, ma kuruwaa yi ntwam me ho, nso ɔnyɛ deɛ mepɛ na mmom deɛ wopɛ .”

english_verse: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Mark
translation_verse: Honsɔre, mma yɛnkɔ. Hwɛ, deɛ obeyi me ama he abɛne.” Yuda Iskariot de asraafoɔ baa Turom hɔ (Marko

english_verse: Jesus took along Peter and the two brothers, James and John. He was very sad and troubled,
translation_verse: Ɔfaa Petro ne Sebedeo mma mmeema mmienu he kaa ne ho, na ɔhyɛɛ aseɛ dii awerɛhoɔ na ne ho yera no.

english_verse: He came back and found his disciples sleeping. So he said to Peter, “Can't any of you stay awake with me for just one hour?
translation_verse: Afei ɔsane baa asuafoɔ he nkyɛne behuu sɛ bɛɛda; na ɔka kyerɛɛ Petro sɛ, “Nti wantumi ne me anwɛne dɔnhwere baako mpo?

english_verse: Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.”
translation_verse: Honwɛne na hommɔ mpaeɛ na hoankɔ sɔhwɛ mu; honhom he pɛ deɛ nso honam he ɔmmerɛ.”

english_verse: He left them and prayed the same prayer once more.
translation_verse: Nti ogyaa bɛ hɔ bio he, ɔkɔ kɔbɔɔ mpaeɛ ne mprɛnsa so na ɔkaa asɛm korɔ heaa.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “My friend, do what you came for.” The men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Damfo, yɔ deɛ wobaa ha sɛ woobɛyɔ he ntɛm.” Na afei bɛba bɛsɔɔ Yesu mu na bɛkyee no.

english_verse: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.

translation_verse: sɛ, “Berɛ he awie duru, na Nyankopɔn ahennie he abɛne; honsakra ho adwene na honye asɛmpa he nni.” Yesu Frɛɛ N’asuafoɔ a Bedi kane he

translation_verse: Amonom hɔaa, ɔfrɛɛ bɛ, na begyaa bɛ papa Sebedeo ne apaafoɔ he ne kodoɔ he hɔ bedii n’akyi. Beema bi a na Honhommɔne ya ne mu (Luka

english_verse: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”

translation_verse: Na ɔkɔɔ Galilea nyinaa kɔkaa asɛmpa he ya bɛ hyiadane ahodoɔ mu, na otutuu ahonhommɔne. Yesu saa Kwatani bi Yadeɛ (Mateo

translation_verse: Nso ofirii adi, na ɔhyɛɛ aseɛ kekaa ho asɛm, ma ɔtrɛɛ maa afei deɛ Yesu antumi anyi n’anim ankɔ kuro biaa mu bio, na ɔtenaa nkuro agya, na nnipa firii mmeammea baa ne nkyɛne.

english_verse: It was during the time of Abiathar the high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his followers.”

translation_verse: Nti nnipa bebree behyiaam kyeree so kosii kwane he ano nyinaa, na ɔɔka asɛm he akyerɛ bɛ.

english_verse: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.

translation_verse: Na berɛ a nnipa he nti bɛantumi amfa no amma Yesu nkyɛne he, bɛpaa dane he so, tuu tokuro dane he atifi, na begyeegyaa kɛtɛ he a bubuani he da so he mu besii fam.

english_verse: I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man,

english_verse: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
