translation_verse: Obuaa sɛ, “Ɔsɛ sɛ wode wo akoma nyinaa, ne wo kra nyinaa, ne wo ahoɔdene nyinaa, ne w’adwene nyinaa dɔ Awurade wo Nyankopɔn, na wodɔ wo yɔnko sɛ wo ho.”

english_verse: At that same time, Jesus felt the joy that comes from the Holy Spirit, and he said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people. Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
translation_verse: Saa dɔno heaa mu he, anigye hyɛɛ Yesu mã Honhom Kronkron mu, na ɔkaa sɛ, “Agya, soro ne asaase Wura, meyi wo ayɛ, efiri sɛ yeinom ahinta anyansafoɔ ne animdefoɔ, na woayi no adi akyerɛ mpapahwekwaa. Ɔyɛ ampa, Agya, efiri sɛ ɔyɛ wo pɛ sɛ ɔba saa.

english_verse: Jesus then turned to his disciples and said to them in private, “You are really blessed to see what you see!
translation_verse: Afei ɔdanee ne ho kyerɛɛ n’asuafoɔ he kaa sɛ, “Nhyira ne ani a ohu nnoɔma a hohu yi!

english_verse: Jesus answered, “What is written in the Scriptures? How do you understand them?”
translation_verse: Yesu bisaa no sɛ, “Ɛɛne ne bɛatwerɛ Mmara he mu? Mmɛ ne wokenkane firii Mmara he mu?”

english_verse: Jesus said, “You have given the right answer. If you do this, you will have eternal life.”
translation_verse: Ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Woabua no yei; kɔ so yɔ yei na wobenya nkwa.”

english_verse: But the man wanted to show that he knew what he was talking about. So he asked Jesus, “Who are my neighbors?”
translation_verse: Nso, beema he pɛɛ sɛ obu ne ho bem nti, obisaa Yesu sɛ, “Na hwane ne me yɔnko?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: As a man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, robbers attacked him and grabbed everything he had. They beat him up and ran off, leaving him half dead.
translation_verse: Yesu buaa sɛ, “Na beema bi firi Yerusalem oosiane akɔ Yeriko, na ɔkɔtɔɔ akwamukafoɔ mu ma bɛworɔɔ ne ho ntaadeɛ bobɔɔ no, gyaa no hɔ kɔeɛ a na watɔ baha.

english_verse: A priest happened to be going down the same road. But when he saw the man, he walked by on the other side.
translation_verse: Afei, ɔbaa sɛ na sɔfo bi ootwam saa kwane he so, nso berɛ a ohuu no he, ɔfaa kwane he fã baabi kɔeɛ.

english_verse: Later a temple helper came to the same place. But when he saw the man who had been beaten up, he also went by on the other side.
translation_verse: Saa nso ne berɛ a Lewini bi nso duruu hɔ a ohuu no he, ɔfaa kwane he fã baabi kɔeɛ.

english_verse: A man from Samaria then came traveling along that road. When he saw the man, he felt sorry for him
translation_verse: Yei akyi he, Samariani bi a ɔnam kwane he so ootu kwane bɛtoo no, na ohuu no he, ɔyɛɛ no mmɔbɔ.

english_verse: and went over to him. He treated his wounds with olive oil and wine and bandaged them. Then he put him on his own donkey and took him to an inn, where he took care of him.
translation_verse: Nti ɔkɔɔ ne nkyɛne de aduro kekaa n’akuro he mu kyekyeree so. Afei ɔde no tenaa n’ankasa aboa so de no kɔɔ ahɔhodane mu kɔhwɛɛ no.

english_verse: The next morning he gave the innkeeper two silver coins and said, “Please take care of the man. If you spend more than this on him, I will pay you when I return.”
translation_verse: Na adeɛ kyeeɛ he, oyii sika a ne dodoɔ bɛyɛ paani nnaanu akatua de maa ahɔhodane sohwɛfoɔ he kaa sɛ, ‘Hwɛ no, na ka biaa a wobɛbɔ yei akyi he, mesane ba a, metua ama wo.’

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “Which one of these three people was a real neighbor to the man who was beaten up by robbers?”
translation_verse: Saa nnipa mmeɛnsa yi mu deɛ ɔya he ne wosusu sɛ wada ne ho adi sɛ beema a ɔkɔtɔɔ akwamukafoɔ mu he yɔnko paa?”

english_verse: The expert in the Law of Moses answered, “The one who showed pity.” Jesus said, “Go and do the same!” Martha and Mary
translation_verse: Ɔkaa sɛ, “Nea ohuu no mmɔbɔ he a.” Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Kɔ na wo nso kɔyɔ saa.” Marta ne Maria

english_verse: The Lord and his disciples were traveling along and came to a village. When they got there, a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home.
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a bɛɛkɔ he, ɔkɔɔ akuraa bi ase. Ha ne baa bi a bɛfrɛ no Marta gyee no kɔɔ fie sɛ hɔhoɔ.

english_verse: She had a sister named Mary, who sat down in front of the Lord and was listening to what he said.
translation_verse: Na saa baa yi wɔ nuabaa bi a bɛfrɛ no Maria, na ɔno deɛ ɔtenaa Awurade nane ase tiee asɛm a na ɔɔka he.

english_verse: Martha was worried about all that had to be done. Finally, she went to Jesus and said, “Lord, doesn't it bother you that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her to come and help me!”
translation_verse: Nso Marta deɛ, na nnwuma bebree agye n’adwene. Nti ɔba bɛkaa sɛ, “Awurade, wonhu sɛ me nuabaa he agya me hɔ a me nkoaa ne meeyɔ adeɛ nyinaa? Nti ka kyerɛ no na ɔmmɛboa me.”

english_verse: The Lord answered, “Martha, Martha! You are worried and upset about so many things,
translation_verse: Nso Awurade buaa no sɛ, “Marta, Marta, wodwendwene bebree na wode nnoɔma pii ha wo ho.

english_verse: but only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen what is best, and it will not be taken away from her.”
translation_verse: Nso, adeɛ baako pɛ ne hia. Maria deɛ, waayi nea ɔyɛ he, ɔno ne bɛnnye mfiri ne nsam.”

english_verse: So I tell you to ask and you will receive, search and you will find, knock and the door will be opened for you.
translation_verse: Nti meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, hommisa, na bɛde bɛma ho; honhwehwɛ, na hobehu; hommom na bebebie ho.

english_verse: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give the Holy Spirit to anyone who asks. Jesus and the Ruler of Demons (Matthew
translation_verse: Nti, sɛ ho a hoyɛ abɔnefoɔ mpo nim sɛnea hode akyɛdeɛ pa ma ho mma a, mprɛ dodoɔ sɛ ne ho Agya a ɔya soro he mfa Honhom Kronkron mma bɛ a bebisa no?” Nkɔnkɔnsa bi ho mmuaeɛ (Mateo

english_verse: Which one of you fathers would give your hungry child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
translation_verse: Ho mu hwane koraa ne ɔyɛ agya a sɛ ne ba bisa no adwene a, ɔde wɔ bɛma no na ɔmma no adwene?

english_verse: So Jesus told them, “Pray in this way: ‘Father, help us to honor your name. Come and set up your kingdom.
translation_verse: Nti ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Berɛ biaa a hobɛbɔ mpaeɛ he, honka sɛ, “Agya, anidie nka wo dini. W’ahennie mmra.

english_verse: Forgive our sins, as we forgive everyone who has done wrong to us. And keep us from being tempted.’ ”
translation_verse: Na fa yɛ bɔne kyɛ yɛ, sɛnea yɛ ankasa nso yɛde akyɛ bɛ a bɛde yɛ ka. Na nnyae yɛ mma yɛnkɔ sɔhwɛ mu.” Mpaebɔ ho nsiyɛ

english_verse: Everyone who asks will receive, everyone who searches will find, and the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
translation_verse: Na biaa a obisa he onya, na biaa a ɔhwehwɛ he ohu, na biaa a obom he bebebie no.

english_verse: Just as Jonah was a sign to the people of Nineveh, the Son of Man will be a sign to the people of today.
translation_verse: Na sɛnea Yona bɛyɛɛ nsɛnkyerɛnne maa Ninewefoɔ he, saa ne nipa Ba he nso bɛyɛ nsɛnkyerɛnne ama awo ntoatoaso yi.

english_verse: While Jesus was still talking, a woman in the crowd spoke up, “The woman who gave birth to you and nursed you is blessed!”
translation_verse: Na Yesu gu so ɔɔka yeinom he, baa bi firi nnipakuo he mu teaam ka sɛ, “Nhyira ne yafunu a ɔnyinsɛne wo ne nofoɔ a wonomeeɛ!”

english_verse: Jesus forced a demon out of a man who could not talk. And after the demon had gone out, the man started speaking, and the crowds were amazed.
translation_verse: Afei na Yesu ootu honhommɔne afiri mum bi mu. Honhommɔne he firiiɛ he, beema a ɔyɛ mum he kasaeɛ. Na ɔyɛɛ nnipadɔm he nwanwa.

english_verse: Others wanted to put Jesus to the test. So they asked him to show them a sign from God.
translation_verse: Nso, binom a na bɛpɛ sɛ bɛsɔ no hwɛ firii aseɛ hwehwɛɛ nsɛnkyerɛnne a ofiri soro firii ne hɔ.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he said: A kingdom where people fight each other will end up in ruin. And a family that fights will break up.
translation_verse: Na ohuu bɛ nsusuiɛ he, ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ. “Ahennie biaa a ɔne ne ho di asie he dane amamfõõ, na fie a mpaapaemu yam he nso bɔ.

english_verse: If Satan fights against himself, how can his kingdom last? Yet you say that I force out demons by the power of Beelzebul.
translation_verse: Na sɛ Satan nso ne ne ho di asie a, ɔbɛyɛ dɛ na n’ahennie agyina? Meeka yei, efiri sɛ hodɛ menam Beelsebul so ne metu ahonhommɔne.

english_verse: If I use his power to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? They are the ones who will judge you.
translation_verse: Sɛ menam Beelsebul so ne metu ahonhommɔne a, ɔnam hwane so ne ho nkurofoɔ nso tu bɛ? Yei nti hoaa ho nkurofoɔ he bebu ho atɛne.

english_verse: When a strong man arms himself and guards his home, everything he owns is safe.
translation_verse: Sɛ beema hoɔdenfoɔ bi hyehyɛ ne ho wɛne n’aban a, n’agyapadeɛ nya ahobammɔ.

english_verse: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You love the front seats in the synagogues, and you like to be greeted with honor in the market.
translation_verse: Honnue, ho Farisifoɔ, efiri sɛ hopɛ hyia adane mu nkonnwa a osisi animu ne dwa so nkyea!

english_verse: A teacher of the Law of Moses spoke up, “Teacher, you said cruel things about us.”
translation_verse: Afei mmarakyerɛfoɔ he mu baako buaa no sɛ, “Kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ, sɛ woka saa a yɛ nso woadidi yɛ atɛm.”

english_verse: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when it doesn't find a place, it says, “I will go back to the home I left.”
translation_verse: “Sɛ Honhom fĩ bi firi nipa mu a, okokyinkyini akwaweɛ so kɔhwehwɛ homebea, na sɛ wannya bi a, ɔka sɛ ‘Mɛsane akɔ me fie a metu firii mu he mu.’

english_verse: When it gets there and finds the place clean and fixed up,
translation_verse: Na ɔba a, obehu sɛ bɛaprapra mu kama de nnoɔma asiesie mu.

english_verse: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and that person ends up in worse shape than before. Being Really Blessed
translation_verse: Nti ɔsane kɔ kɔfa Honhom nson foforɔ a bɛyɛ werɛmfoɔ sene no ka he ho, na bɛba mu bɛtena hɔ; na saa nipa he awieeɛ sɛe sene kane he.” Setie

english_verse: Jesus replied, “That's true, but the people who are really blessed are the ones who hear and obey God's message!” A Sign from God (Matthew
translation_verse: Nso ɔkaa sɛ, “Daabi, mmom, nhyira ne bɛ a betie Nyankopɔn asɛm na bedi so!” Yudafoɔ fɔdie kyɛne amanamane mufoɔ deɛ (Mateo

english_verse: The Pharisee was surprised that he did not wash his hands before eating.
translation_verse: Nso, berɛ a Farisini no huu sɛ wanni kane anhohoro ne nsa ansa na oodidi he, ɔyɛɛ no nwanwa.

english_verse: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your gardens, such as mint and rue. But you cheat people, and you don't love God. You should be fair and kind to others and still give a tenth to God.
translation_verse: “Na ho Farisifoɔ deɛ, honnue, efiri sɛ hoyi mẽẽ, nunum ne kumin nyinaa so ntotosoɔ du du, nso hototo atɛntenenee ne Nyankopɔn dɔ ase! Saa nnoɔma yi ne ɔsɛ sɛ nka hoyɔ, na nea aka he nso ɔnsɛ sɛ hoyi firi mu.

english_verse: As crowds were gathering around Jesus, he said: You people of today are evil! You keep looking for a sign from God. But what happened to Jonah is the only sign you will be given.
translation_verse: Berɛ a nnipakuo he ɔɔboaboa bɛ ho ano he, ofirii aseɛ kaa sɛ, “Awoɔ ntoatoaso yi yɛ awoɔ ntoatoasoɔ bɔne; bɛhwehwɛ nsɛnkyerɛnne. Nso bɛmma bɛ sɛnkyerɛnne biaa ka Yona nsɛnkyerɛnne he ho.

english_verse: When the judgment comes, the Queen of the South will stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something far greater than Solomon.
translation_verse: Bebenyane anafoɔ fam hemmaa he ne saa awo ntoatoasoɔ yi mu nnipa atemmuo he mu na obebu bɛ fɔ; efiri sɛ ofiri asaase ano betiee Salomo nyansa, nso hwɛ, nea ɔsene Salomo ya ha.

english_verse: The people of Nineveh will also stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah. Light (Matthew
translation_verse: Bebenyane Ninewefoɔ ne awo ntoatoaso yi atemmuo mu; na bebebu ntoatoaso yi fɔ, efiri sɛ Ninewefoɔ he sakraa bɛ adwene berɛ a Yona bɔɔ bɛ kɔkɔ he; nso, hwɛ, nea ɔsene Yona ya ha. Kanea adwuma (Mateo

english_verse: No one lights a lamp and then hides it or puts it under a clay pot. A lamp is put on a lampstand, so everyone who comes into the house can see the light.
translation_verse: “Sɛ bi sɔ kanea a, ɔmfa nsi kokoam, saa ara nso ne ɔmfa nhyɛ kodoɔ ase, na mmom ɔde si kaneadua so, sɛnea bɛ a bɛba mu he behu hann he.

english_verse: Your eyes are the lamp for your body. When your eyes are good, you have all the light you need. But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark.
translation_verse: Nipadua he kanea ne ani. Nti sɛ w’ani yɛ a, wo nipadua nyinaa nso hyerɛn; na sɛ ɔnyɛ a, wo nipadua nso bɛyɛ kusuu.

english_verse: You fools! Didn't God make both the outside and the inside?
translation_verse: Nnipa a honni adwene! Ɔnyɛ nea ɔyɔɔ akyi heaa ne yɔɔ mu he nso?

english_verse: Yes, you are really in for trouble. You build monuments to honor the prophets your own people murdered long ago.
translation_verse: “Honnue sɛ hoto adiyifoɔ a ho agyanom ne kuu bɛ nna so!

english_verse: When Jesus finished speaking, a Pharisee invited him home for a meal. Jesus went and sat down to eat.
translation_verse: Berɛ a Yesu kaa yei he, Farisini bi srɛɛ no sɛ ɔne no mmedidi. Nti ɔkɔɔ hɔ kɔtenaa adidipono ho.

english_verse: So the Lord said to him: You Pharisees clean the outside of cups and dishes, but on the inside you are greedy and evil.
translation_verse: Na Awurade ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Ho Farisifoɔ, ho hohoro kuruwa ne ayowa ho, na ho mu deɛ, amim ne nnebɔne ahyɛ mã:

english_verse: If you would only give what you have to the poor, everything you do would please God.
translation_verse: Nso, homfa nea ɔya mu he nyɔ adɔeɛ, na hwɛ, nnoɔma a aka nyinaa ho ate ho fam.

english_verse: But you are in for trouble! You are like unmarked graves that people walk on without even knowing it.
translation_verse: Honnue, efiri sɛ hote sɛ adamena a ahinta na nnipa nante so a bennim!”

english_verse: Jesus replied: You teachers are also in for trouble! You load people down with heavy burdens, but you won't lift a finger to help them carry the loads.
translation_verse: Na ɔkaa sɛ, “Ho nso Honnue, ho a honim mmara he, efiri sɛ hode nnesoa a mu oduru soa nnipa, nso ho ankasa hommfa ho nsateaa baako mpo nka nnesoa he!

english_verse: You must think that was the right thing for your people to do, or else you would not have built monuments for the prophets they murdered.
translation_verse: Nɔkorem he, hoyɛ ho agyanom nnwuma ho adansefoɔ, nso hofoa bɛ so, efiri sɛ yeinom ne bekuu adiyifoɔ he, na ho ne hooto bɛ nna so.

english_verse: Because of your evil deeds, the Wisdom of God said, “I will send prophets and apostles to you. But you will murder some and mistreat others.”
translation_verse: Yei nti na Nyankopɔn nyansa nso kaa sɛ, ‘Mɛsoma adiyifoɔ ne asomafoɔ akɔ bɛ nkyɛne, na bebeku bɛ mu binom ataa binom.

english_verse: You people living today will be punished for all the prophets who have been murdered since the beginning of the world.
translation_verse: Nti ne bebebisa adiyifoɔ he nyinaa mogyaa a bɛahwie agu firi wiase ahyaseɛ afiri awo ntoatoaso yi hɔ,

english_verse: This includes every prophet from the time of Abel to the time of Zechariah, who was murdered between the altar and the temple. You people will certainly be punished for all of this.
translation_verse: efiri Habel mogyaa so kosi Sakaria a bekuu no afɔrebokyia he ntomadane he ntam he mogyaa so.’ Ɛɛ, meka akyerɛ ho sɛ bebebisa afiri awo ntoatoaso yi hɔ.

english_verse: You teachers of the Law of Moses are really in for trouble! You carry the keys to the door of knowledge about God. But you never go in, and you keep others from going in.
translation_verse: “Honnue, ho mmarakyerɛfoɔ, efiri sɛ hoagye nimdeɛ safoa he, nso ho ankasa hoankɔ mu, na bɛ a bɛɛkɔ mu he nso hosi bɛ kwane!”

english_verse: Jesus was about to leave, but the teachers and the Pharisees wanted to get even with him. They tried to make him say what he thought about other things,
translation_verse: Nti ofirii hɔ he, Atwerɛfoɔ ne Farisifoɔ he befumm ne ho na bebisabisaa no nsɛm foforɔ pii de hyɛɛ no abufuo

english_verse: so they could catch him saying something wrong.
translation_verse: na bɛtɛɛ no hwɛɛ sɛ bebenya asɛm bi afiri n’anum anaa.

english_verse: As thousands of people crowded around Jesus and were stepping on each other, he told his disciples: Be sure to guard against the dishonest teaching of the Pharisees! It is their way of fooling people.
translation_verse: Afei, berɛ a nnipakuo mpempem pii he dɔɔso ama bɛakyere so ama beetiatia bɛ ho bɛ ho he, Yesu dii kane ka kyerɛɛ n’asuafoɔ he sɛ. “Honhwɛ yei Farisifoɔ mmɔreka a ɔyɛ nyaatwom he ho.

english_verse: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. Won't he do even more for you? You have such little faith!
translation_verse: Nti sɛ Nyankopɔn fira wuram nhahane a ɔya hɔ nnɛ na kyena bɛtwa de agu fononoo mu ahye no he ntoma saa a, mmɛ nti ne ommfira ho mmom a ho ho hia he ntoma? Oo! ho a ho gyidie sua!

english_verse: But put God's work first, and these things will be yours as well. Treasures in Heaven (Matthew
translation_verse: Na mmom honhwehwɛ Nyankopɔn ahennie, na bɛde yeinom nyinaa bɛka ho ho. Soro ahonyadeɛ (Mateo

english_verse: My friends, don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but after that, there is nothing else they can do.
translation_verse: Bio nso, me nnamfonom, meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, honnsuro bɛ a beku nipadua na ɔno akyi he bentumi nyɛ no hwee bio.

english_verse: Even the hairs on your head are counted. So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Matthew
translation_verse: Na ho ti nwii mpo bɛakane ne nyinaa. Nti honnsuro; hosom bo paa kyɛne nkasanoma bebree. Ɔnsɛ sɛ yɛpa Kristo

english_verse: My little group of disciples, don't be afraid! Your Father wants to give you the kingdom.
translation_verse: “Honnsuro! Nwankuo ketewa, efiri sɛ ɔsɔ ho Agya ani sɛ ɔde ahennie he bɛma ho.

english_verse: Sell what you have and give the money to the poor. Make yourselves moneybags that never wear out. Make sure your treasure is safe in heaven, where thieves cannot steal it and moths cannot destroy it.
translation_verse: Hontɔno nnoɔma a howɔ na homfa nyɔ adɔeɛ. Honyɔ sika nkotokuo a ɔnsɛe mfa, na hompɛ agyapadeɛ a ɔya soro, baabi a akorɔmfoɔ ntumi nkowia na nwewemmoa nsɛe no,

english_verse: At that time the Holy Spirit will tell you what to say. A Rich Fool
translation_verse: na dɔno heaa mu Honhom Kronkron bɛkyerɛ ho nnoɔma a ɔsɛ sɛ hoka.” Yesu mpɛ sɛ oyi atɛne

english_verse: If you tell others that you belong to me, the Son of Man will tell God's angels that you are my followers.
translation_verse: “Nti, meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, biaa a ɔgye me tom nnipa anim he, Nipa Ba he nso bɛgye no atom Nyankopɔn abɔfoɔ anim.

english_verse: But if you reject me, you will be rejected in front of them.
translation_verse: Na nea ɔpa me nnipa anim he, Nipa Ba he nso bɛpa no Nyankopɔn abɔfoɔ anim. Honhom Kronkron ne n’akwankyerɛ

english_verse: When you are brought to trial in the synagogues or before rulers or officials, don't worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say.
translation_verse: Na sɛ bɛde ho kɔ hyia adane mu, mpanyimfoɔ ne atumfoɔ anim a, honnwennwene nea hode beyi ho ho ano anaa nea hobɛka ho;

english_verse: A man in a crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, tell my brother to give me my share of what our father left us when he died.”
translation_verse: Afei bi firi nnipakuo he mu ka kyerɛɛ Yesu sɛ, “Kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ, ka kyerɛ me nua na ɔne me nkyɛ awunnyadeɛ he.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Who gave me the right to settle arguments between you and your brother?”
translation_verse: Ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Beema! Hwane ne de me sii ho so temmufoɔ anaa dekyɛfoɔ?”

english_verse: and he said to himself, “What can I do? I don't have a place large enough to store everything.”
translation_verse: Nti osusuu ne mu sɛ, ‘Ɛɛne ne menyɔ? Efiri sɛ, menni baabi a mede me nnɔbae besie.’

english_verse: Later, he said, “Now I know what I'll do. I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, where I can store all my grain and other goods.
translation_verse: Nti ɔkaa sɛ, ‘Nea meyɔ di. Medwiri m’apata agu na masisi akɛseɛ, na hɔ ne mɛboaboa m’ayuo ne me nnoɔma pa nyinaa ano agu;

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: I tell you not to worry about your life! Don't worry about having something to eat or wear.
translation_verse: Afei Yesu ka kyerɛɛ n’asuafoɔ he sɛ, “Yei nti, meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, honnwennwene ho kra ho, nea hobedi anaa ho nipadua anaa nea hobɛhyɛ ho,

english_verse: Look how the wild flowers grow! They don't work hard to make their clothes. But I tell you Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of these flowers.
translation_verse: Honhwɛ sɛnea asukooko nyini; ɔnyɛ adwuma na ɔnto asaawa; nso meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, Salomo animuonyam nyinaa mu mpo he, wanhyehyɛ ne ho kamakama sɛ yeinom mu baako mpo.

english_verse: Don't keep worrying about having something to eat or drink.
translation_verse: Nti, honnhwehwɛ nea hobedi ne nea hobɛnom, na honnwennwene nnha ho ho;

english_verse: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father knows what you need.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ yeinom nyinaa ne nnoɔma a amane a ɔya wiase he di akyi dendenndene, na ho Agya nim sɛ hohia saa nnoɔma yi.

english_verse: But suppose one of the servants thinks that the master won't return until late. Suppose that servant starts beating all the other servants and eats and drinks and gets drunk.
translation_verse: Nso sɛ ɔkɔba sɛ akoa he ka no n’akoma mu sɛ, ‘Me wura akyɛre ba, na ofiri aseɛ boro nkoa ne mmaawa he, na odidi nom, na ɔboro a,

english_verse: Do you think that I came to bring peace to earth? No indeed! I came to make people choose sides.
translation_verse: Hosusu sɛ mede asomdwie ne aba asaase so anaa? Ɔnte saa, na mmom mpaepaemu ne mede aba.

english_verse: Be ready and keep your lamps burning
translation_verse: “Hommɔ abɔsoɔ, na homma ho nkanea nnɛre,

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master finds them awake and ready when he comes! I promise you he will get ready and let his servants sit down so he can serve them.
translation_verse: Nhyira ne nkoa a bɛ wura he bɛba abehu bɛ sɛ begu so ɔɔwɛne! Nɔkorɛ, meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, ɔbɛbɔ n’abɔsoɔ, na wama bɛatenatena didipono ho na waba abɛsom bɛ.

english_verse: Those servants are really fortunate if their master finds them ready, even though he comes late at night or early in the morning.
translation_verse: Na sɛ ɔba anadwo dasuom anaa nhemadeakyeɛ na ɔbɛto bɛ saa a, nhyira ne bɛ!

english_verse: You would surely not let a thief break into your home, if you knew when the thief was coming.
translation_verse: Nso honhu yei sɛ, sɛ fiewura nim dɔno korɔ a korɔmfoɔ bɛba a, nka wamma no ammetu ne dane ase ammewura mu.

english_verse: So always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come.
translation_verse: Ho nso honsiesie ho ho, efiri sɛ dɔno a honsusu koraa mu ne Nipa Ba he bɛba.”

english_verse: Peter asked Jesus, “Did you say this just for us or for everyone?”
translation_verse: Afei Petro bisaa sɛ, “Awurade, wode mfantoho yi ɔɔma yɛ anaa wode ɔɔma bɛ nyinaa nso?”

english_verse: The Lord answered: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
translation_verse: Na Awurade buaa sɛ, Hwane koraa ne fiepanyini nɔkwafoɔ ne badwemma a ne wura de no bedi ne fiefoɔ so, sɛ ɔmma bɛ bɛ aduane berɛ a ɔsɛ mu?

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
translation_verse: Nhyira ne saa akoa he a ne wura bɛba abehu no sɛ ɔɔyɔ saa!

english_verse: A servant who is always faithful will surely be put in charge of everything the master owns.
translation_verse: Nɔkorem, meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, ɔde saa akoa he besi nea ɔwɔ nyinaa so.

english_verse: If that happens, the master will come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him. That servant will then be punished and thrown out with the servants who cannot be trusted.
translation_verse: saa akoa he wura bɛba da a ɔnhwɛ no kwane ne dɔno a onnim sɛ ɔbɛba mu, na ɔbɛtwe n’aso dendenndene na ɔde no afra bɛ a bɛnnye nni mu.

english_verse: If servants are not ready or willing to do what their master wants them to do, they will be beaten hard.
translation_verse: Nti akoa he a onim nea ne wura pɛ nso wansiesie ne ho anaa wanyɔ nea ɔpɛ he, bɛbɛbɔ no mmaa pii.

english_verse: But servants who don't know what their master wants them to do will not be beaten so hard for doing wrong. If God has been generous with you, he will expect you to serve him well. But if he has been more than generous, he will expect you to serve him even better. Not Peace, but Trouble (Matthew
translation_verse: Nso nea onnim a ɔyɔɔ nnoɔma a ɔsɛ hweɛ he bɛbɛhwe no kakraa bi. Nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, biaa a bɛma no pii he, bebebisa pii afiri ne hɔ, na nea bɛde pii hyɛ ne nsa he, ɔno ne bebebisa pii nso afiri ne hɔ. Akodie ho nkɔmhyɛ (Mateo

english_verse: I came to set fire to the earth, and I wish it were already on fire!
translation_verse: “Mebaa sɛ meebɛma gya asɔ asaase so, na sɛ asɔ dada a, mmɛ ne meehwehwɛ kyɛne yei?

english_verse: I am going to be put to a hard test. And I will have to suffer a lot of pain until it is over.
translation_verse: Nɔkorɛ di, mewɔ asubɔ bi da m’anim, na hwɛ sɛnea me ho yera me kosi sɛ bebewie!

english_verse: A family of five will be divided, with two of them against the other three.
translation_verse: Na ofiri nnɛ, fie baako mu nnipa mmienu bedi mpaepaemu, na mmeɛnsa ne mmienu anya, na mmienu ne mmeɛnsa anya.

english_verse: Fathers and sons will turn against one another, and mothers and daughters will do the same. Mothers-in-law and daughters-in-law will also turn against each other. Knowing What To Do (Matthew
translation_verse: Bebedi mpaepaemu, agya bɛsɔre atia ne ba na ba asɔre atia n’agya, na na bɛsɔre atia ne babaa na babaa asɔre atia ne na, asebaa bɛsɔre atia n’ase, na ase nso asɔre atia n’asebaa.”

english_verse: Jesus said to all the people: As soon as you see a cloud coming up in the west, you say, “It's going to rain,” and it does.
translation_verse: Afei Yesu toaa so ka kyerɛɛ nnipakuo he nso sɛ, “Sɛ hohu sɛ nsuo amuna atɔeɛ fam a, hoka ntɛmaa sɛ, ‘Nsuo ɔɔbɛtɔ’; na ɔba saa.

english_verse: When the south wind blows, you say, “It's going to get hot,” and it does.
translation_verse: Na sɛ hohu sɛ anafoɔ fam mframa ɔɔbɔ a, hoka sɛ, ‘Ahuhuro ɔɔbɛba,’ na ɔbam.

english_verse: Are you trying to fool someone? You can predict the weather by looking at the earth and sky, but you don't really know what's going on right now.
translation_verse: Nyaatwomfoɔ, honim sɛnea bɛkyerɛ asaase ne wiem nsɛnkyerɛnne ase, afei, mmɛ nti ne honnim sɛnea bɛkyerɛ saa mmerɛ yi mu?

english_verse: Why don't you understand the right thing to do?
translation_verse: “Afei, ɛɛne nti ne ho ankasa honntumi nhu nea ɔfata sɛ hoyɔ?

english_verse: When someone accuses you of something, try to settle things before you are taken to court. If you don't, you will be dragged before the judge. Then the judge will hand you over to the jailer, and you will be locked up.
translation_verse: Sɛ wo ne wo nkurobɔfoɔ ookodi asɛm tumfoɔ bi hɔ a, honam kwane so ɔɔkɔ he, bɔ mmɔdene sɛ wobesiesie wo ne no ntam, na wantwe wo ankɔ temmufoɔ he anim, na temmufoɔ he amfa woanhyɛ asennibea sohwɛfoɔ nsa, na asennibea sohwɛfoɔ he amfa wo ankɔto afiase.

english_verse: You won't get out until you have paid the last cent you owe.
translation_verse: Meeka akyerɛ wo sɛ, womfiri hɔ nkɔ gye sɛ woatua ka biaa a ɔsɛ sɛ wo tua ansa.”

english_verse: About this same time Jesus was told that Pilate had given orders for some people from Galilee to be killed while they were offering sacrifices.
translation_verse: Saa berɛ heaa, nnipa bi a bɛya hɔ kaa sɛnea Pilato de Galileafoɔ bi mogyaa fraa bɛ afɔrebɔ mu he ho asɛm kyerɛɛ no.

english_verse: Jesus replied: Do you think that these people were worse sinners than everyone else in Galilee just because of what happened to them?
translation_verse: Nti obisaa bɛ sɛ, “Nti hosusu sɛ na saa Galileafoɔ yi yɛ abɔnefoɔ kyɛne Galileafoɔ a aka nyinaa esiane saa amaneɛ a behuiɛ yi nti anaa?

english_verse: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also be killed.
translation_verse: Nɔkorem, meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ ɔnte saa koraa; nso sɛ hoansakra a, saa ne bebekuku ho nyinaa nso.

english_verse: Jesus said, “What is God's kingdom like? What can I compare it with?
translation_verse: Afei Yesu toaa so sɛ, “Sɛ ne Nyankopɔn ahennie teɛ? Na ɛɛne ho ne mede bɛto?

english_verse: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also die. A Story about a Fig Tree
translation_verse: Nɔkorem, meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ ɔnte saa; nso sɛ hoansakra a, bebekuku ho nyinaa saa nso.” Borɔdoma a ɔnso aba

english_verse: Jesus then told them this story: A man had a fig tree growing in his vineyard. One day he went out to pick some figs, but he didn't find any.
translation_verse: Afei ɔtoaa so maa mfantoho yi sɛ, “Na beema bi wɔ borɔdoma si ne bobe turom, na ɔbaa sɛ ɔɔbɛhwɛ sɛ aduaba ya so a, nso wannya bi.

english_verse: The gardener answered, “Master, leave it for another year. I'll dig around it and put some manure on it to make it grow.
translation_verse: Obuaa no sɛ, ‘Owura, gyae no afe yi nso, na memfuntum aseɛ na memfa sumina ngu aseɛ.

english_verse: Maybe it will have figs on it next year. If it doesn't, you can have it cut down.” Healing a Woman on the Sabbath
translation_verse: Na sɛ ɔsõ aba afe sesɛɛ a ɔyɛ asɛmpa, na mmom sɛ anso a, hedeɛ wobetumi atwa atwene.’ ” Yesu saa baa bi yadeɛ Homeda bi

english_verse: One Sabbath, Jesus was teaching in a synagogue,
translation_verse: Afei na Yesu ɔɔkyerɛkyerɛ hyia adane he baako mu homeda bi.

english_verse: and a woman was there who had been crippled by an evil spirit for
translation_verse: Saa berɛ he, na baa bi a honhom bi ama no ayare mfie dunwɔtwe ama ne mu akuntunu a na ontumi mmae ne mu.

english_verse: When Jesus saw the woman, he called her over and said, “You are now well.”
translation_verse: Berɛ a Yesu huu no he, ɔfrɛɛ no ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Baa, bɛayi wo afiri wo w’ahoɔmmerɛ mu.”

english_verse: The Lord replied, “Are you trying to fool someone? Won't any one of you untie your ox or donkey and lead it out to drink on a Sabbath?
translation_verse: Nso, Awurade buaa no sɛ, “Nyaatwomfoɔ, nti ho mu biaa nsane ne nantwie anaa n’afunumu mfiri buom homeda nkɔma no nsuo nnom?

english_verse: This woman belongs to the family of Abraham, but Satan has kept her bound for
translation_verse: Ɔmfata sɛ baa yi a ɔyɛ Abraham babaa a Satan akyekyere no mfie dunwɔtwe he, bɛsane no firi mpokyerɛ yi mu homeda anaa?”

english_verse: Jesus' words made his enemies ashamed. But everyone else in the crowd was happy about the wonderful things he was doing. A Mustard Seed and Yeast (Matthew
translation_verse: Na ɔkaa yeinom he, n’atamfoɔ he nyinaa ani wuiɛ; nso nnipakuo he nyinaa firii aseɛ dii ahurusie anwanwadeɛ a ɔyɔeɛ he nyinaa ho. Sinapi aba ne mmɔreka ho mfantoho (Mateo

english_verse: At that time some Pharisees came to Jesus and said, “You had better get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you!”
translation_verse: Dɔno heaa mu he Farisifoɔ bi ba bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Firi ha kɔ, efiri sɛ Herode pɛ sɛ oku wo.”

english_verse: But I am going on my way today and tomorrow and the next day. After all, Jerusalem is the place where prophets are killed. Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Matthew
translation_verse: Nso, otwa sɛ menante nnɛ ne kyena ne adekyeeɛ, efiri sɛ ɔnsɛ sɛ beku diyifoɔ bi baabi foforɔ biaa gye Yerusalem.’

english_verse: There the ones who are now least important will be the most important, and those who are now most important will be least important. Jesus and Herod
translation_verse: Nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, binom aka akyi nso bebedi kane, saa nso ne binom adi kane nso bɛbɛka akyi.” Herode Antipa

english_verse: Jesus said to them: Go tell that fox, “I am going to force out demons and heal people today and tomorrow, and three days later I'll be through.”
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Honkɔka nkyerɛ saa dadaafoɔ he sɛ, ‘Hwɛ, meetu ahonhommɔne na masa yadeɛ nnɛ ne kyena, na n’adekyeɛ he mawie. Yesu twa Yerusalem ho adwo (Mateo

english_verse: It is like what happens when someone plants a mustard seed in a garden. The seed grows as big as a tree, and birds nest in its branches.”
translation_verse: Na ɔmaa mfantoho yi sɛ: Nyankopɔn ahennie he te sɛ sinapi aba a bi duaa no ne turom, na onyini bɛyɛɛ dua, na soro nnomaa tenatenaa ne mmane so.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “What can I compare God's kingdom with?
translation_verse: Na ɔkaa bio sɛ, “Mmɛ ho ne mede Nyankopɔn ahennie bɛto?

english_verse: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he taught the people in the towns and villages.
translation_verse: Na Yesu tutuu akwane kɔɔ nkuro mu ne nkuraa ase kyerɛkyerɛeɛ na ɔtoaa n’akwantuo he so kɔɔ Yerusalem.

english_verse: Do all you can to go in by the narrow door! A lot of people will try to get in, but will not be able to.
translation_verse: “Honyere ho ho dendenndene nwura pono teateaa he mu, na meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ nnipa beberee bɛhwehwɛ sɛ bewura mu nso bentumi.

english_verse: Once the owner of the house gets up and locks the door, you will be left standing outside. You will knock on the door and say, “Sir, open the door for us!” But the owner will answer, “I don't know a thing about you!”
translation_verse: Na sɛ fiewura he nya sɔre to pono he mu a, afei hobegyina abɔntene abɔ pono he mu aka sɛ, ‘Owura, bie yɛ.’ Nso obebua ho sɛ, ‘Mennim baabi a hofiri.’

english_verse: Then you will start saying, “We dined with you, and you taught in our streets.”
translation_verse: Na hobefiri aseɛ aka sɛ, ‘Yɛ ne wo didi nomeeɛ, na womaa nkyerɛkyerɛ yɛ mmɔntene so.’

english_verse: But he will say, “I really don't know who you are! Get away from me, you evil people!”
translation_verse: Nso obebie n’ano aka akyerɛ ho sɛ, ‘Mennim baabi a hofiri. Homfiri me so nkɔ, ho a hoyɔ nea ɔntene nyinaa!’

english_verse: Then when you have been thrown outside, you will weep and grit your teeth because you will see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God's kingdom.
translation_verse: Saa berɛ he hobesu na hoatwɛre ho sẽ, berɛ a hobehu Abraham ne Isak ne Yakob ne adiyifoɔ he nyinaa sɛ bɛya Nyankopɔn ahennie mu, na ho deɛ, bɛapam ho kɔ abɔntene he.

english_verse: People will come from all directions and sit down to feast in God's kingdom.
translation_verse: Bio nso, nkurofoɔ befiri apieɛ fam ne atɔeɛ fam, atifi fam ne anafoɔ fam aba, na bɛbɛtenatena adidiiɛ Nyankopɔn ahennie mu.

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: Yerusalem! Yerusalem! Wo a woku adiyifoɔ na wosi bɛ a bɛasoma bɛ wo nkyɛne aboɔ; hwɛ mprɛ dodoɔ a mepɛɛ sɛ meboaboa wo manfoɔ ano sɛnea akokɔbaatane boaboa ne mma ano hyɛ ne ntabane ase, nso woampene so!

english_verse: Now your temple will be deserted. You won't see me again until the time when you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Hwɛ! Bɛagyaa ho fie fituo ama ho. Meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, honhu me bio kosi sɛ hobɛka sɛ, ‘Nhyira ne nea ɔba Awurade dini mu!’ ”

english_verse: Jesus turned and asked the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses, “Is it right to heal on the Sabbath?”
translation_verse: Nti Yesu bisaa Mmaranimfoɔ ne Farisifoɔ he sɛ, “Mmara ma kwane sɛ bɛsa yadeɛ homeda anaa ɔmma kwane?”

english_verse: But they did not say a word. Jesus took hold of the man. Then he healed him and sent him away.
translation_verse: Nso bɛyɛɛ komm. Nti Yesu sɔɔ ne mu saa no yadeɛ na ɔmaa no kɔeɛ.

english_verse: There was nothing they could say. How To Be a Guest
translation_verse: Bɛantumi anyi saa asɛm yi ano. Ahobrɛaseɛ

english_verse: Jesus saw how the guests had tried to take the best seats. So he told them:
translation_verse: Afei ɔtoaa so maa mfantoho bi kyerɛɛ nnipa a bɛato nsa afrɛ bɛ he, berɛ a ohuu sɛnea na bɛɛhwehwɛ mu ayi mpanyini tenabea he, ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ,

english_verse: When you are invited to a wedding feast, don't sit in the best place. Someone more important may have been invited.
translation_verse: “Sɛ bi to nsa frɛ wo ayefohyia ase a, ntena mpanyini tenabea, ebia na waato nsa afrɛ bi a odi mu kyɛne wo,

english_verse: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
translation_verse: Na biaa a ɔma ne ho so he bɛbɛbrɛ no ase, na nea ɔbrɛ ne ho ase he bɛbɛma no so.” Ahiafoɔ adidifrɛ

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the man who had invited him: When you give a dinner or a banquet, don't invite your friends and family and relatives and rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return, and you will be paid back.
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ beema a ɔtoo nsa frɛɛ no he nso sɛ, “Sɛ woto pono, mfrɛ wo nnamfo anaa wo nuanom anaa w’abusuafoɔ anaa wo mfɛfoɔ adefoɔ nkoaa, ɔnam sɛ wodwene sɛ bɛ nso da bi bɛbɛto nsa afrɛ wo bɛ apontoɔ asɛ de atua wo ka nti.

english_verse: They cannot pay you back. But God will bless you and reward you when his people rise from death. The Great Banquet (Matthew
translation_verse: na wobenya nhyira, efiri sɛ bɛ deɛ benni bibiaa a bɛde betua wo so ka. Na wobenya akatua ateneneefoɔ wusɔreɛ he mu.” Apontoɔ kɛseɛ he (Mateo

english_verse: When the servant returned, he said, “Master, I've done what you told me, and there is still plenty of room for more people.”
translation_verse: Akoa he yɔɔ saa wieeeɛ he, ɔkaa sɛ, ‘Me wura, maayɔ nea wo hyɛɛ me he, nso kwane da so ya hɔ.’

english_verse: After Jesus had finished speaking, one of the guests said, “The greatest blessing of all is to be at the banquet in God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Berɛ a bɛ a bɛ ne no tete adidiiɛ hɔ he mu baako tee nsɛm yi he, ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Nhyira ne nea obedidi Nyankopɔn ahennie mu!”

english_verse: Jesus told him: A man once gave a great banquet and invited a lot of guests.
translation_verse: Na afei Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Nipa bi too pono kɛseɛ anwummerɛ bi, na ɔtoo nsa frɛɛ nnipa bebree.

english_verse: When the banquet was ready, he sent a servant to tell the guests, “Everything is ready! Please come.”
translation_verse: Na dɔno a bɛde bedi aduane he duruiɛ he, ɔsomaa n’akoa kɔɔ bɛ a ɔtoo nsa frɛɛ bɛ he hɔ sɛ, ‘Hommra na afei bɛasiesie bibiaa awie.’

english_verse: One guest after another started making excuses. The first one said, “I bought some land, and I've got to look it over. Please excuse me.”
translation_verse: Nso bɛ nyinaa befiri aseɛ yiyii bɛ ho ano. Nea odi kane he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, ‘Maatɔ asaase na ehia sɛ mekɔhwɛ; mesrɛ wo, yi me firi mu.’

english_verse: Another guest said, “I bought five teams of oxen, and I need to try them out. Please excuse me.”
translation_verse: Na foforɔ nso kaa sɛ, ‘Maatɔ anantwie mpamho nnum na meehwɛ sɛnea bɛteɛ; mesrɛ wo, yi me firi mu.’

english_verse: The servant told his master what happened, and the master became so angry he said, “Go as fast as you can to every street and alley in town! Bring in everyone who is poor or paralyzed or blind or lame.”
translation_verse: Nti akoa he bɛkaa nsɛm yi kyerɛɛ ne wura he. Na fiewura he bo fuiɛ na ɔka kyerɛɛ n’akoa he sɛ, ‘Yɔ ntɛm kɔ kuro he akwane ne mmorɔnoo so kɔfa ahiafoɔ ne mmubuafoɔ ne anifirafoɔ ne mpakye bra.’

english_verse: His master then told him, “Go out along the back roads and make people come in, so my house will be full.
translation_verse: Na ne wura he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, ‘Kɔ akwane ne mmɔntene so na hyɛ amanfoɔ ma bɛ mmra mu, sɛ ɔbɛyɛ na fie ha bɛyɛ ma.

english_verse: Not one of the guests I first invited will get even a bite of my food!” Being a Disciple (Matthew
translation_verse: Na meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, nnipa a too nsa frɛɛ bɛ he mu biaa nni hɔ a m’aduane he bi bɛka n’ano.’ ” Kristo frɛ a ɔfrɛ nnipa (Mateo

english_verse: Large crowds were walking along with Jesus, when he turned and said:
translation_verse: Afei na nnipadɔm pii bi ne no nam, na ɔdanee ne ho ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ,

english_verse: You cannot be my disciple, unless you love me more than you love your father and mother, your wife and children, and your brothers and sisters. You cannot follow me unless you love me more than you love your own life.
translation_verse: “Sɛ bi ba me nkyɛne na w’annɔ me asene ne papa ne ne maame ne ne yerɛ ne ne mma ne ne nuammeemanom ne ne nuammaanom ne n’ankasa mpo kra a, ontumi nyɛ me suani.

english_verse: You cannot be my disciple unless you carry your own cross and follow me.

english_verse: Suppose one of you wants to build a tower. What is the first thing you will do? Won't you sit down and figure out how much it will cost and if you have enough money to pay for it?

translation_verse: Afei ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Sɔre kɔ; wo gyidie agye wo.” Nyankopɔn ahennie he mmaeɛ

translation_verse: Nso nea odi kane he, ɔsɛ sɛ Nipa Ba he hu amaneɛ bebree na nneemmafoɔ po no. Noa ne Lot nna ho mfatoho

translation_verse: Meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, obebu atɛntenenee ama bɛ ntɛm so. Nso sɛ Nipa Ba he ba a, obehu gyidie ankasa asaase so anaa?” Farisini ne togyeni he

translation_verse: Meka kyerɛ ho sɛ, togyeni he de bembuo sane kɔɔ ne fie sene Farisini he, efiri sɛ, biaa a ɔma ne ho so he bɛbɛbrɛ no ase, na nea ɔbrɛ ne ho ase he bɛbɛma no so.” Yesu hyira mmɔfra (Mateo

translation_verse: Yesu tee yei he, ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Nso aka wo adeɛ baako: kɔtɔno nea wowɔ nyinaa na kyekyɛ ma ahiafoɔ, na wobenya agyapadeɛ soro; na bra bedi m’akyi.”

english_verse: When the man heard this, he was sad, because he was very rich.
translation_verse: Berɛ a temmufoɔ he tee yei he, ne werɛ hoo paa, efiri sɛ, na ɔyɛ defoɔ ankasa. Ahonyadeɛ mu asiane

english_verse: When the crowd heard this, they asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Bɛ a bɛtee yei he kaa sɛ, “Hedeɛ hwane ne betumi anya nkwa?”

english_verse: When Jesus was coming close to Jericho, a blind man sat begging beside the road.

english_verse: Jesus stopped and told some people to bring the blind man over to him. When the blind man was getting near, Jesus asked,
translation_verse: Afei Yesu gyinaeɛ na ɔhyɛɛ sɛ bɛmfa beema he mmra ne nkyɛne. Oduruu Yesu nkyɛne he, Yesu bisaa no sɛ,

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Look and you will see! Your eyes are healed because of your faith.”
translation_verse: Nti Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Hu adeɛ; wo gyidie agye wo.”

english_verse: Jesus was heading his way, and Zacchaeus wanted to see what he was like. But Zacchaeus was a short man and could not see over the crowd. So he ran ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree.

english_verse: But the people of his country hated him, and they sent messengers to the foreign country to say, “We don't want this man to be our king.”

translation_verse: Na berɛ a ne ho yera no he, ɔbɔɔ mpaeɛ dendenndene; na ne ho mfifire yɛɛ sɛ mogyaa a ɔɔsosɔ gu fam.]

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd came up. It was led by Judas, one of the twelve apostles. He went over to Jesus and greeted him with a kiss.
translation_verse: Yesu gu so ɔɔkasa he, mpofirem, nnipakuo bi baeɛ a na dummienu he mu baako a bɛfrɛ no Yuda di bɛ anim; na ɔbaa Yesu nkyɛne bɛfee n’ano.

english_verse: and a servant girl saw him. Then after she had looked at him carefully, she said, “This man was with Jesus!”
translation_verse: Na abaawa bi huu no sɛ ɔte gya he ho; na ɔhwɛɛ no gyee kaa sɛ, “Na beema yei nso ka ne ho.”

english_verse: One of the disciples even struck at the high priest's servant with his sword and cut off the servant's right ear.
translation_verse: Bɛ mu baako mpo twee sɔfo panyini akoa he so sekane twaa n’aso nifa firii hɔ.

english_verse: Jesus asked Judas, “Are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?”
translation_verse: Na Yesu bisaa no sɛ, “Yuda, wode mfeano ooyi Nipa Ba he ama?”

english_verse: When Jesus' disciples saw what was about to happen, they asked, “Lord, should we attack them with a sword?”
translation_verse: Berɛ a bɛ a bɛka ne ho he huu nea oobesi he, bebisaa sɛ, “Awurade, yɛntwe sekane nkɔ bɛ so anaa?”

english_verse: “Enough of that!” Jesus said. Then he touched the servant's ear and healed it.
translation_verse: Na Yesu kaa sɛ, “Homma no nso ha yiaa!” Na ɔde ne nsa kaa aso he maa ne ho tɔɔ no.

english_verse: Jesus spoke to the chief priests, the temple police, and the leaders who had come to arrest him. He said, “Why do you come out with swords and clubs and treat me like a criminal?
translation_verse: Afei Yesu bisaa asɔfoɔ mpanyini ne asɔrefie ahwɛfoɔ ne mpanyimfoɔ a bɛaba ɔɔbɛkye no he sɛ. “Hode sekane ne nkontimaa aba me so te sɛ meyɛ dwotwafoɔ?

english_verse: Peter said, “Woman, I don't even know that man!”
translation_verse: Nso Petro sanee sɛ, “Baa, mennim no.”

english_verse: A little later someone else saw Peter and said, “You are one of them!” “No, I'm not!” Peter replied.
translation_verse: Na berɛ tiaa bi akyi he, nnipa foforɔ bi huu no kaa sɛ, “Wo nso woka bɛ ho.” Na Petro kaa sɛ. “Beema, menka bɛ ho.”

english_verse: They said, “Tell us! Are you the Messiah?” Jesus replied, “If I said so, you wouldn't believe me.
translation_verse: “Sɛ wone Kristo he a, ka kyerɛ yɛ.” Nso ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Sɛ meka kyerɛ ho mpo a, ɔnyɛ adeɛ a hobɛgye adi.

english_verse: Jesus was arrested and led away to the house of the high priest, while Peter followed at a distance.
translation_verse: Afei bɛkyee Yesu de no kɔeɛ, na bɛde no kɔɔ sɔfo panyini he fie, na Petro di bɛ akyi ya akyirikyiri.

english_verse: At daybreak the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses got together and brought Jesus before their council.
translation_verse: Na adeɛ kyeeɛ he, mane he mpanyimfoɔ abadwakuo he, asɔfoɔ mpanyimfoɔ ne atwerɛfoɔ he nyinaa ka boom, na bɛde no kogyinaa bɛ asennibea kaa sɛ.

english_verse: Peter replied, “I don't know what you are talking about!” Right then, while Peter was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
translation_verse: Nso Petro kaa sɛ, “Damfo, mennim nea wooka he.” Na hɔaa, berɛ a ogu so ɔɔkasa he, akokɔ bɔneeɛ.

english_verse: The Lord turned and looked at Peter. And Peter remembered that the Lord had said, “Before a rooster crows tomorrow morning, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Na Awurade danee n’ani hwɛɛ Petro, na Petro kae asɛm a Awurade ka kyerɛɛ no he sɛ, “Ansa na akokɔ bɛbɔno nnɛ he, na woapo me mprɛnsa.”

english_verse: Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
translation_verse: Na ofirii adi de awerɛhoɔ kosuu yayaaya. Yesu ho agodie (Mateo

english_verse: The men who were guarding Jesus made fun of him and beat him.
translation_verse: Afei mmeema a na bekita ne mu he firii aseɛ dii ne ho agorɔ bobɔɔ no;

english_verse: They kept on insulting Jesus in many other ways. Jesus Is Questioned by the Council (Matthew
translation_verse: Na bɛkɔɔ so kekaa abususɛm bebree tiaa no.

english_verse: And if I asked you a question, you wouldn't answer.
translation_verse: Afei nso, sɛ mebisa ho asɛm a, hommua me nso.

english_verse: But from now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right side of God All-Powerful.”
translation_verse: Nso, efiri sesɛɛ ɔɔkɔ he nipa Ba he bɛtena Nyankopɔn nsa nifa a tumi yam he so.”

english_verse: Then they asked, “Are you the Son of God?” Jesus answered, “You say I am!”
translation_verse: Na bɛ nyinaa bebisaa no sɛ, “Nti, wone Nyankopɔn Ba he?” Ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Hoaa ne hooka sɛ mene no.”

english_verse: They replied, “Why do we need more witnesses? He said it himself!”
translation_verse: Bɛkaa sɛ, “Abɛ adanseɛ bio ne yehia? Yɛate afiri ɔno ankasa n’anum.”

english_verse: Everyone in the council got up and led Jesus off to Pilate.
translation_verse: Nti nnipadɔm he nyinaa sɔreeɛ, na bɛde Yesu kɔmaa Pilato.

english_verse: They started accusing him and said, “We caught this man trying to get our people to riot and to stop paying taxes to the Emperor. He also claims that he is the Messiah, our king.”
translation_verse: Afei befirii aseɛ bɔɔ no soboɔ sɛ, “Yɛakye beema yi sɛ ɔɔsɛe yɛ mane, na ɔbra sɛ bentua toɔ mma Kaesare na ɔka sɛ ɔno ne hene Kristo he.”

english_verse: Herod and his soldiers made fun of Jesus and insulted him. They put a fine robe on him and sent him back to Pilate.
translation_verse: Afei Herode ne n’asraafoɔ he bɔɔ no adapaa, goroo ne ho, na bɛhyɛɛ no ahentaadeɛ bi de no kɔmaa Pilato bio.

english_verse: He told them, “You brought Jesus to me and said he was a troublemaker. But I have questioned him here in front of you, and I have not found him guilty of anything that you say he has done.
translation_verse: na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Hode nipa yi braa me sɛ bi a ɔma nkurofoɔ te atua, na hwɛ, mehwehwɛɛ n’asɛm mu ho anim ha yiaa, nso manhu nnyinasoɔ biaa a hode bɔɔ no soboɔ.

english_verse: But they all kept on saying, “He has been teaching and causing trouble all over Judea. He started in Galilee and has now come all the way here.” Jesus Is Brought before Herod
translation_verse: Nso bɛkaa no dendenndene sɛ, “Ɔkyerɛkyerɛ Yudea nyinaa de hwanyane mane he mu, ofirii aseɛ mpo firii Galilea ne ɔde abeduru ha yi.”

english_verse: But the whole crowd shouted, “Kill Jesus! Give us Barabbas!”
translation_verse: Na nnipadɔm he nyinaa teaam kaa sɛ, “Fa yei kɔ, na gyaa Baraba ma yɛ!”

english_verse: Pilate asked Jesus, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words,” Jesus answered.
translation_verse: Afei Pilato bisaa no sɛ, “Wone Yudafoɔ hene he ampa?” Na obuaa no sɛ, “W’ankasa ne wooka.”

english_verse: Pilate told the chief priests and the crowd, “I don't find him guilty of anything.”
translation_verse: Afei Pilato ka kyerɛɛ asɔfoɔ mpanyimfoɔ he ne nnipakuo he sɛ, “Menhu bɔne biaa beema yi ho.”

english_verse: After Pilate learned that Jesus came from the region ruled by Herod, he sent him to Herod, who was in Jerusalem at that time.
translation_verse: Na ohuu sɛ Yesu hyɛ Herode tumi ase he, ɔma bɛde no kɔmaa Herode a nna he mu na n’ankasa ya Yerusalem he.

english_verse: For a long time Herod had wanted to see Jesus and was very happy because he finally had this chance. He had heard many things about Jesus and hoped to see him work a miracle.
translation_verse: Berɛ a Herode huu Yesu he n’ani gyee yie, efiri sɛ na ofiri mmerɛ tentene apɛ sɛ ohu no esiane ne ho asɛm a waate nti, na na ɔpɛ sɛ ɔyɔ nsɛnkyerɛnne bi ma ohu.

english_verse: Herod asked him a lot of questions, but Jesus did not answer.
translation_verse: Afei ofirii aseɛ bisaa no nsɛm bebree; nso Yesu ammua no.

english_verse: Then the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses stood up and accused him of all kinds of bad things.
translation_verse: Na asɔfoɔ mpanyimfoɔ he ne atwerɛfoɔ he kɔɔ so sɔre gyinaeɛ de abufuɔ bɔɔ no soboɔ aniberɛ so.

english_verse: That same day Herod and Pilate became friends, even though they had been enemies before this. The Death Sentence (Matthew
translation_verse: Afei Herode ne Pilato bɛyɛɛ nnamfoɔ saa da heaa; kane he, na nka bɛyɛ atamfoɔ. Pilato di maa Yesu

english_verse: As Jesus was being led away, some soldiers grabbed hold of a man named Simon who was from Cyrene. He was coming in from the fields, but they put the cross on him and made him carry it behind Jesus.
translation_verse: Na bɛde no ɔɔkɔ he, bɛsɔɔ Simon, Kireneni bi a na ofiri akuraa bi ase ɔɔba kurom, na bɛde asennua he soaa no dii Yesu akyi.

english_verse: One of the criminals hanging there also insulted Jesus by saying, “Aren't you the Messiah? Save yourself and save us!”
translation_verse: Na nnebɔnyɛfoɔ a bɛasɛne bɛ he mu baako firii aseɛ kasa tiaa Yesu sɛ, “Wone Kristo he, anaa ɔnyɛ wo a? Gye wo ho, na gye yɛ nso.”

english_verse: Pilate called together the chief priests, the leaders, and the people.
translation_verse: Afei Pilato frɛɛ asɔfoɔ mpanyimfoɔ he, mane sodifoɔ he ne ne manfoɔ he hyiaa mu

english_verse: Pilate wanted to set Jesus free, so he spoke again to the crowds.
translation_verse: Pilato maa ne nne so ne nnipadɔm he kasaa bio, efiri sɛ na ɔpɛ sɛ ogyaa Yesu.

english_verse: The people kept on shouting as loud as they could for Jesus to be put to death.
translation_verse: Afei bɛde nne kɛseɛ kaa no dendenndene sɛ ɔmmɔ no asennua mu; na bɛ nne bunkamm ne deɛ so.

english_verse: Finally, Pilate gave in.
translation_verse: Nti Pilato buu atɛne sɛnea bɛɛhwehwɛ he.

english_verse: He freed the man who was in jail for rioting and murder, because he was the one the crowd wanted to be set free. Then Pilate handed Jesus over for them to do what they wanted with him. Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross (Matthew
translation_verse: Na ogyaa deɛ sakasaka ne awudie nti bɛde no kɔtoo afiase maa bɛ sɛnea bɛsrɛɛ no sɛ ɔnyɔ he; nso oyii Yesu maeɛ sɛdeɛ na bɛɛpɛ he. Yerusalem mmaa

english_verse: If this can happen when the wood is green, what do you think will happen when it is dry?
translation_verse: Na sɛ bɛde yei ɔɔyɔ dua mono a, mmɛ ne bɛde bɛyɔ dua wuiɛ?”

english_verse: Around noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until the middle of the afternoon.
translation_verse: Na aabɔ bɛyɛ awia dummienu, afei sum baa asaase nyinaa so kosii awie nnɔnmmeɛnsa,

english_verse: Jesus said, “Father, forgive these people! They don't know what they're doing.” While the crowd stood there watching Jesus, the soldiers gambled for his clothes. The leaders insulted him by saying, “He saved others. Now he should save himself, if he really is God's chosen Messiah!”
translation_verse: Na nkurofoɔ he gyina hɔ ɔɔhwɛ. Na atumfoɔ he dii ne ho agorɔ kaa sɛ, “Ɔgyee binom; sɛ yei ne Kristo a Nyankopɔn ayi no he a, ma onnye ne ho ɛ!” Yesu ho agodie

english_verse: The soldiers made fun of Jesus and brought him some wine.
translation_verse: Asraafoɔ he mpo dii ne ho agorɔ, na bɛbaa ne nkyɛne de nsa kekakeka maa no

translation_verse: kaa sɛ, “Sɛ wone Yudafoɔ hene he a, gye wo ho!”

translation_verse: Na nea ɔka ho he buaa no kaa n’anim sɛ, “Nti wonsuro Nyankopɔn koraa berɛ a woya afobuo korɔ yiaa mu yi?

translation_verse: Na Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Nɔkorem meeka akyerɛ wo sɛ, nnɛ wobɛka me ho Paradise hɔ.” Yesu wuo

english_verse: Then they went to prepare some sweet-smelling spices for his burial. But on the Sabbath they rested, as the Law of Moses commands.
translation_verse: Na bɛsane besiesiee nnuro ne ngo hwamhwam. Na homeda he, bɛhomeeɛ sɛnea mmara he teɛ he.

translation_verse: Na berɛ a nnipakuo a bɛahyiam ɔɔhwɛ nea ɔɔkɔ so he nyinaa huu nea asi he, bɛde bɛ nsa bobɔɔ bɛ koko so firii hɔ kɔeɛ.

english_verse: All of Jesus' close friends and the women who had come with him from Galilee stood at a distance and watched. Jesus Is Buried (Matthew
translation_verse: Bio nso, na bɛ a benim no he nyinaa gyinagyina akyiri. Na mmaa a bedii n’akyi firii Galilea he nso gyinagyina hɔ ɔɔhwɛ yeinom. Yesu sie (Mateo

english_verse: He took the body down from the cross and wrapped it in fine cloth. Then he put it in a tomb that had been cut out of solid rock and had never been used.

translation_verse: Na da he yɛ ahoboaboa da ma homeda a na ɔɔba he.

translation_verse: Na mmaa he nso a bɛne no firi Galilea baeɛ he dii akyire kɔhwɛɛ da he ne sɛdeɛ bɛde n’amu he too hɔ faeɛ.

english_verse: Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and some other women were the ones who had gone to the tomb. When they returned, they told the eleven apostles and the others what had happened.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛ Maria Magdalene, ne Yohana, ne Yakobo maame Maria. Afei nso, mmaa a aka a bɛka bɛ ho he kaa yeinom kyerɛɛ asomafoɔ he.

english_verse: When they found the stone rolled away from the entrance,
translation_verse: Nso behuu sɛ bɛamini boɔ a na ɔda nna he ano he afiri hɔ,

english_verse: they went in. But they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus,
translation_verse: na berɛ a bɛkɔɔ mu he, bɛanhu Awurade Yesu amu he.

english_verse: ‘The Son of Man will be handed over to sinners who will nail him to a cross. But three days later he will rise to life.’ ”
translation_verse: sɛ otwa sɛ beyi Nipa Ba he hyɛ abɔnefoɔ nsa na bɛbɔ no asennua mu na da a ɔtɔ so mmeɛnsa he wasɔre.”

english_verse: That same day two of Jesus' disciples were going to the village of Emmaus, which was about eleven kilometers from Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Na hwɛ, da heaa na bɛ mu mmienu ɔɔkɔ Emaus a ɔyɛ akuraa bi a ofiri hɔ kɔ Yerusalem bɛyɛ akwansini nson,

english_verse: Jesus asked them, “What were you talking about as you walked along?” The two of them stood there looking sad and gloomy.
translation_verse: Obisaa bɛ sɛ, “Abɛ asɛm ne honam kwane so oosusu ho yi?” Na begyinaeɛ a na bɛ anim ayɛ bosaa.

english_verse: We had hoped that he would be the one to set Israel free! But it has already been three days since all this happened.
translation_verse: Na yɛ deɛ, yɛ ani daa so sɛ nka ɔno ne ɔɔbɛgye Israel. Nso yei nyinaa akyi he, nnansa ni a nsɛm yi siiɛ.

english_verse: Some women in our group surprised us. They had gone to the tomb early in the morning,
translation_verse: Bio nso, mmaa bi a befiri yɛ mu maa yɛ ho dwirii yɛ, efiri sɛ bɛyɔɔ ntɛm kɔɔ da he so

english_verse: Some men from our group went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said. But they didn't see Jesus either.
translation_verse: Afei, bɛ a bɛne yɛ ya hɔ he binom kɔɔ da he so; na bekohuu sɛ ɔte saa, sɛnea mmaa he bɛkaeɛ he pɛpɛɛpɛ, nso bɛ deɛ bɛanhu no.”

english_verse: Then Jesus asked the two disciples, “Why can't you understand? How can you be so slow to believe all that the prophets said?
translation_verse: Nti ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “O adwenharefoɔ a hoyɛ nyaa ho akoma mu sɛ hobɛgye deɛ adiyifoɔ he aka nyinaa adi!

english_verse: Didn't you know that the Messiah would have to suffer before he was given his glory?”
translation_verse: Nti na ɔnsɛ sɛ Kristo he hu amaneɛ yi nyinaa na ɔkɔ n’Animuonyam mu anaa?”

english_verse: Jesus then explained everything written about himself in the Scriptures, beginning with the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets.
translation_verse: Na ɔde firi Mose ne Adiyifoɔ he nyinaa so kyerɛkyerɛɛ bɛ nsɛm a ɔfa ne ho a ɔya Twerɛsɛm he nyinaa mu.

english_verse: They begged him, “Stay with us! It's already late, and the sun is going down.” So Jesus went into the house to stay with them.
translation_verse: Nso bɛhyɛɛ no dendenndene sɛ “Tena yɛ nkyɛne, efiri sɛ nwunu adwo, na adeɛ anya asa.” Nti ɔkɔtenaa bɛ nkyɛne.

english_verse: After Jesus sat down to eat, he took some bread. He blessed it and broke it. Then he gave it to them.
translation_verse: Na ɔne bɛ te adidiiɛ he ɔfaa paanoo hyiraa so bubuiɛ, na ofirii aseɛ de maa bɛ.

english_verse: At once they knew who he was, but he disappeared.
translation_verse: Afei bɛ ani biebie koraa ma behuu sɛ ɔyɛ ɔno a; na ɔyera firii bɛ nkyɛne.

english_verse: They said to each other, “When he talked with us along the road and explained the Scriptures to us, didn't it warm our hearts?”
translation_verse: Na bɛbisabisa bɛ ho bɛ ho sɛ, “Berɛ a ɔne yɛ ɔɔkasa kwane so na ɔɔkyerɛ Twerɛsɛm he mu kyerɛ yɛ he, ampusu yɛ akoma anaa?” Asuafoɔ mmienu he sane kɔ Yerusalem

english_verse: So they got up and returned to Jerusalem. The two disciples found the eleven apostles and the others gathered together.
translation_verse: Na dɔno heaa mu he, bɛsɔre sane kɔɔ Yerusalem, na bekohuu dubaako he ne bɛ a bɛka bɛ ho he sɛ bɛahyiam,

english_verse: And they learned from the group that the Lord was really alive and had appeared to Peter.
translation_verse: ɔɔka sɛ “Ampa, Awurade anyane, na waayi ne ho adi akyerɛ Simon!”

english_verse: Then the disciples from Emmaus told what happened on the road and how they knew he was the Lord when he broke the bread. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Matthew
translation_verse: Afei bɛ ankasa nso bɛkaa nsɛm a osii kwane so ne sɛdeɛ behuu no paanoobubuo mu he kyerɛɛ bɛ. Yesu yi ne ho kyerɛɛ asuafoɔ he (Marko

english_verse: While Jesus' disciples were talking about what had happened, Jesus appeared and greeted them.
translation_verse: Na bɛɛkasa saa he, ɔno ankasa begyinaa bɛ mfimfini ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Asomdwie nka ho.”

english_verse: They were frightened and terrified because they thought they were seeing a ghost.
translation_verse: Nso esiane sɛ na hu aka bɛ, na besuro nti, besusuu sɛ bɛahu samane bi.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Why are you so frightened? Why do you doubt?
translation_verse: Nti ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Ɛɛne nti ne ho akoma tutu, na mmɛ nti ne ho adwene yɛ ho naa yi?

english_verse: Look at my hands and my feet and see who I am! Touch me and find out for yourselves. Ghosts don't have flesh and bones as you see I have.”
translation_verse: Honhwɛ me nsa ne me nane, na hoahu sɛ ɔyɛ meaa; homfa ho nsa nso me mu nhwɛ, na samane nni honam ne nnompe sɛnea hohu sɛ mewɔ yi.”

english_verse: After Jesus said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.
translation_verse: Na ɔkaa yei he, ɔde ne nsa ne ne nane kyerɛɛ bɛ. Yesu didii asuafoɔ he anim

english_verse: The disciples were so glad and amazed that they could not believe it. Jesus then asked them, “Do you have something to eat?”
translation_verse: Nso berɛ a anigyeɛ nti bɛda so nnye nni na asɛm he yɛ bɛ nwanwa he, obisaa bɛ sɛ, “Howɔ aduane bi ya hɔ anaa?”

english_verse: They gave him a piece of broiled fish.
translation_verse: Na bɛmaa no apataa sini bi a bɛatoto;

english_verse: He took it and ate it as they watched.
translation_verse: ɔgyeeɛ, na odii no bɛ anim. Yesu kyerɛkyerɛ anyamesɛm ase

english_verse: Jesus said to them, “While I was still with you, I told you that everything written about me in the Law of Moses, the Books of the Prophets, and in the Psalms had to happen.”
translation_verse: Afei ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Yei ne me nsɛm a meka kyerɛɛ ho berɛ a na meda so ka ho ho he, sɛ otwa sɛ nea bɛatwerɛ afa me ho ya Mose mmara ne Adiyifoɔ he ne Nnwom he mu nyinaa ba mu.”

english_verse: Then he helped them understand the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Afei obiee bɛ adwene mu yie ma bɛtee Twerɛsɛm he ase,

english_verse: He told them: The Scriptures say that the Messiah must suffer, then three days later he will rise from death.
translation_verse: na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Saa kwane yi so ne bɛatwerɛ sɛ Kristo he behu amaneɛ na da a ɔtɔ so mmeɛnsa he wasɔre afiri awufoɔ mu,

english_verse: They also say that all people of every nation must be told in my name to turn to God, in order to be forgiven. So beginning in Jerusalem,
translation_verse: na ne dini so ne bebegyina aka adwensakra ne bɔne fakyɛ ho asɛm amansa nyinaa mu, na bɛbɛhyɛ aseɛ afiri Yerusalem.

english_verse: you must tell everything that has happened.
translation_verse: Hobɛyɛ nnoɔma yi ho adansefoɔ.

english_verse: I will send you the one my Father has promised, but you must stay in the city until you are given power from heaven. Jesus Returns to Heaven (Mark
translation_verse: Na hwɛ, meesoma nea m’Agya ahyɛ ho bɔ he aba ho so. Ho deɛ, hontena kuro he mu nkosi sɛ bɛbɛma ho ahoɔdene a ofiri soro.” Yesu sorokɔ (Marko

english_verse: Jesus led his disciples out to Bethany, where he raised his hands and blessed them.
translation_verse: Na ɔde bɛ firii adi dii bɛ anim kɔɔ Betania, na ɔmaa ne nsa so hyiraa bɛ.

english_verse: As he was doing this, he left and was taken up to heaven.
translation_verse: Na oohyira bɛ he ɔne bɛ ntam teteɛ na bɛmaa no so kɔɔ soro.

english_verse: After his disciples had worshiped him, they returned to Jerusalem and were very happy.
translation_verse: Na bɛkotoo no sane de anigye kɛseɛ baa Yerusalem.

english_verse: They spent their time in the temple, praising God.
translation_verse: Na bɛtenaa asɔre fie hɔ daa yii Nyankopɔn ayɛ.

english_verse: From the very beginning the Word was with God.
translation_verse: Ɔnoaa ne mfitiaseɛ he na ɔne Nyankopɔn ya hɔ.

english_verse: received its life from him, and his life gave light to everyone.
translation_verse: Ne mu ne nkwa ya, na nkwa he ne nnipa hann.

english_verse: The light keeps shining in the dark, and darkness has never put it out.
translation_verse: Na hann he hyerɛn sum mu, na sum he ntumi mmunkam mfa ne so.

english_verse: who came to tell about the light and to lead all people to have faith.
translation_verse: Ɔbaa sɛ oobedi hann he ho adanseɛ, sɛ ɔbɛyɛ a nnipa nyinaa nam hann he so bɛgye adi.

english_verse: The true light that shines on everyone was coming into the world.
translation_verse: Saa berɛ he, na hann trodoo a ɔhyerɛn ma nnipa nyinaa he ɔɔba wiase.

english_verse: The Word was in the world, but no one knew him, though God had made the world with his Word.
translation_verse: Na ɔya wiase deɛ, na ɔnam ne so ne bɛbɔɔ wiase, nso wiase anhu no.

english_verse: He came into his own world, but his own nation did not welcome him.
translation_verse: Ɔbaa ɔno ankasa ne deɛ mu, nso ne deɛ annye no antom.

english_verse: They were not God's children by nature or because of any human desires. God himself was the one who made them his children.
translation_verse: Yeinom yɛ mma a omfiri mogyaa anaa nea honam pɛ anaa nea beema pɛ so ne awo bɛ, na mmom Nyankopɔn pɛ so ne ɔnam a bɛawo bɛ.

english_verse: The Word became a human being and lived here with us. We saw his true glory, the glory of the only Son of the Father. From him the complete gifts of undeserved grace and truth have come down to us.
translation_verse: Na Asɛm he bɛyɛɛ honam bɛtenaa yɛ mu, na yɛahu n’animuonyam sɛ Agya he Ba a ɔwoo no koro maa yɛ, adom ne nɔkorɛ ahyɛ no ma.

english_verse: Because of all that the Son is, we have been given one blessing after another.
translation_verse: Ɔnam sɛ adom ahyɛ no ma nti, yɛ nyinaa yɛanya adom aka adom ho.

english_verse: No one has ever seen God. The only Son, who is truly God and is closest to the Father, has shown us what God is like. John the Baptist Tells about Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Biaa nni hɔ a wahu Nyankopɔn da. Nyankopɔn a ɔyɛ Ba koro he, nea ɔya Agya he kokom he, ɔno ne wada no adi. Suboni Yohane adanseɛ a odi faa Yesu ho

english_verse: Then when they asked him if he were Elijah, he said, “No, I am not!” And when they asked if he were the Prophet, he also said “No!”
translation_verse: Na bebisaa no sɛ, “Wone Elia anaa?” Na ɔkaa sɛ, “Daabi, menyɛ Elia.” “Wone diyifoɔ he?” Obuaa sɛ, “Daabi.”

english_verse: Finally, they said, “Who are you then? We have to give an answer to the ones who sent us. Tell us who you are!”
translation_verse: Nti bebisaa no sɛ, “Hedeɛ, wone hwane? Ma yɛ mmuaeɛ nkɔ ma bɛ a bɛsomaa yɛ he. Ɛɛne ne yɛnka mfa wo ho nkyerɛ bɛ?”

english_verse: John answered in the words of the prophet Isaiah, “I am only someone shouting in the desert, ‘Get the road ready for the Lord!’ ”
translation_verse: Ɔkaa sɛ, “Me ne deɛ ɔya serɛ he so a ɔɔteam sɛ, ‘Homma Awurade kwane he ntene,’ sɛnea diyifoɔ Yesaia kaeɛ he.”

english_verse: Some Pharisees had also been sent to John.
translation_verse: Na bɛasoma bɛ firi Farisifoɔ mu.

english_verse: They asked him, “Why are you baptizing people, if you are not the Messiah or Elijah or the Prophet?”
translation_verse: Na bebisaa no sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne wonyɛ Kristo he, Elia anaa diyifoɔ he, nso wobɔ asu yi?”

english_verse: John told them, “I use water to baptize people. But here with you is someone you don't know.
translation_verse: Yohane buaa bɛ sɛ, “Mede nsuo ne bɔ asu. Nso bi gyina ho mu a honnim no

english_verse: Even though I came first, I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
translation_verse: a odi m’akyi; memfata sɛ mesane ne mpaboa ahoma mpo.”

english_verse: John said this as he was baptizing east of the Jordan River in Bethany. The Lamb of God
translation_verse: Yei siiɛ ya Betania a ɔya Yordan agya, baabi a na Yohane bɔ asu he. Nyankopɔn Adwamaa

english_verse: The next day, John saw Jesus coming toward him and said: Here is the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
translation_verse: Na adeɛ kyeɛ he, Yohane huu sɛ Yesu ɔɔba, na ɔkaa sɛ, “Hwɛ! Nyankopɔn adwamaa a oyi wiase bɔne kɔ he di!

english_verse: He is the one I told you about when I said, “Someone else will come, who is greater than I am, because he was alive before I was born.”
translation_verse: Yei ne nea mekaa no ho asɛm sɛ, ‘Nipa bi di m’akyi a ɔɔba a ɔkyɛne me, efiri sɛ ɔya hɔ ansa na meeba.’

english_verse: I didn't know who he was. But I came to baptize you with water, so that everyone in Israel would see him.
translation_verse: Me deɛ, na mennim no; nso mede nsuo bɛbɔɔ asu sɛ ɔbɛda no adi akyerɛ Israel.”

english_verse: I was there and saw the Spirit come down on him like a dove from heaven. And the Spirit stayed on him.
translation_verse: Na Yohane dii adanseɛ sɛ, “Mehuu Honhom he sɛ ofiri soro ɔɔba sɛ abuburo abesi ne so

english_verse: Before this I didn't know who he was. But the one who sent me to baptize with water had told me, “You will see the Spirit come down and stay on someone. Then you will know that he is the one who will baptize with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Me deɛ na mennim no, nso nea ɔsomaa me maa mede nsuo bɔɔ asu he ka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, ‘Nea wobehu sɛ Honhom he abesi ne so he, ɔno ne de Honhom Kronkron bɔ asu he.’

english_verse: I saw this happen, and I tell you that he is the Son of God. The First Disciples of Jesus
translation_verse: Na meaa maahu na maadi ho adanseɛ sɛ ɔno ne Nyankopɔn Ba he.” Yesu Asuafoɔ a Bedi kane

english_verse: The next day, John was there again, and two of his followers were with him.
translation_verse: Adeɛ kyeeɛ he, na Yohane ne n’asuafoɔ mmienu gyina hɔ,

english_verse: When he saw Jesus walking by, he said, “Here is the Lamb of God!”
translation_verse: na ohuu Yesu sɛ ɔɔkɔ he, ɔteaam sɛ, “Hwɛ! Nyankopɔn adwamaa he di!”

english_verse: John's two followers heard him, and they went with Jesus.
translation_verse: Na asuafoɔ mmienu he tee sɛ ɔɔka yei he, bedii Yesu akyi

english_verse: When Jesus turned and saw them, he asked, “What do you want?” They answered, “Rabbi, where do you live?” The Hebrew word “Rabbi” means “Teacher.”
translation_verse: Yesu danee ne ho a ohuu sɛ bedi n’akyi he, obisaa bɛ sɛ, “Mmɛ ne hoopɛ?” Bebisaa no sɛ, “Rabuni” , “Wote he?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Come and see!” It was already about four o'clock in the afternoon when they went with him and saw where he lived. So they stayed on for the rest of the day.
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Hommra mmɛhwɛ.” Bɛba behuu baabi a na ɔte, na bɛ ne no tenaa hɔ saa da he. Na aabɔ bɛyɛ awia nɔnnan.

english_verse: One of the two men who had heard John and had gone with Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Simon Petro nua Andrea yɛ nnipa mmienu a bɛtee nea Yohane kaeɛ na bedii Yesu akyi he mu baako

english_verse: The first thing Andrew did was to find his brother and tell him, “We have found the Messiah!” The Hebrew word “Messiah” means the same as the Greek word “Christ.”
translation_verse: Ohuu ɔnoaa ne nua Simon Petro kane, na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Yɛahu Mesaia he” .

english_verse: Andrew brought his brother to Jesus. And when Jesus saw him, he said, “Simon son of John, you will be called Cephas.” This name can be translated as “Peter.” Jesus Chooses Philip and Nathanael
translation_verse: Ɔde Simon baa Yesu nkyɛne, na Yesu hwɛɛ no kaa sɛ, “Wone Simon, Yohane ba. Bɛbɛfrɛ wo Kefa” . Yesu frɛɛ Filipo ne Natanael

english_verse: Philip then found Nathanael and said, “We have found the one that Moses and the Prophets wrote about. He is Jesus, the son of Joseph from Nazareth.”
translation_verse: Filipo huu Natanael, na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Yɛahu nea Mose kaa ne ho asɛm ya mmara he mu na adiyifoɔ he nso kaa ne ho asɛm he, Yesu, Yosef ba a ofiri Nasaret he.”

english_verse: Nathanael asked, “Can anything good come from Nazareth?” Philip answered, “Come and see.”
translation_verse: Natanael kaa sɛ, “Nti, adepa bi betumi afiri Nasaret aba?” Filipo ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Bra bɛhwɛ.”

english_verse: When Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him, he said, “Here is a true descendant of our ancestor Israel. And he isn't deceitful.”
translation_verse: Berɛ a Yesu huu sɛ Natanael ɔɔba he, ɔka kyerɛ no sɛ, “Hwɛ! Israelni ankasa a nnaadaa biaa nni ne mu di!”

english_verse: “How do you know me?” Nathanael asked. Jesus answered, “Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.”
translation_verse: Natanael bisaa no sɛ, “Wonim me he?” Yesu buaa sɛ, “Mehuu wo sɛ woya borɔdoma dua he ase ansa na Filipo ɔɔfrɛ wo mpo.”

english_verse: Nathanael said, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God and the King of Israel!”
translation_verse: Natanael kaa sɛ, “Ampa, Rabuni, wone Nyankopɔn Ba he! Wone Israelfoɔ Hene he!”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Did you believe me just because I said that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see something even greater.
translation_verse: Yesu kaa sɛ, “Meka kyerɛ wo sɛ mehuu wo borɔdoma dua he ase ne wogyedi yi? Wobehu nnoɔma akɛseɛ a ɔkyɛne yeinom.”

english_verse: I tell you for certain you will see heaven open and God's angels going up and coming down on the Son of Man.”
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Nɔkwasɛm di, meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, hobehu soro sɛ abie na Nyankopɔn abɔfoɔ oodi aforosiane Nipa Ba he so.”

english_verse: Three days later Mary, the mother of Jesus, was at a wedding feast in the village of Cana in Galilee.
translation_verse: Yei akyi da a ɔtɔ so mmeɛnsa he, na ayefohyia bi ɔɔkɔ so Kana a ɔya Galilea. Na Yesu maame ya hɔ.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples had also been invited and were there.
translation_verse: Na bɛtoo nsa frɛɛ Yesu ne n’asuafoɔ he nso sɛ bɛmmra ayefohyia he ase.

english_verse: When the wine was all gone, Mary said to Jesus, “They don't have any more wine.”
translation_verse: Na nsa he saeɛ he, Yesu maame ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Bɛ nsa asa.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Mother, my time hasn't yet come! You must not tell me what to do.”
translation_verse: Na Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ “Maame, mmɛ ne wo pɛ sɛ meyɔ? Me berɛ nnya nnuruiɛ.”

english_verse: Mary then said to the servants, “Do whatever Jesus tells you to do.”
translation_verse: Na ne maame ka kyerɛɛ asomfo he sɛ, “Honnyɔ nea ɔbɛka akyerɛ ho biaa.”

english_verse: At the feast there were six stone water jars that were used by the people for washing themselves in the way that their religion said they must. Each jar held about
translation_verse: Na nsuhina nsia a bɛde aboɔ ayɔ sisi hɔ sɛnea Yudafoɔ ahodwira ho nhyehyɛeɛ teɛ he. Na mu biaa betumi afa galɔn aduonu anaa aduasa.

english_verse: Jesus told the servants to fill them to the top with water. Then after the jars had been filled,
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Homfa nsuo nhyehyɛ nhina yi mama.” Nti bɛde nsuo guguu nhina he mu na ne ɔyɛɛ ma tɛntɛm.

english_verse: he said, “Now take some water and give it to the man in charge of the feast.” The servants did as Jesus told them,
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Afei honsesa bi nkɔ ma adidieɛhwɛfoɔ panyini he.” Nti bɛsesaa bi kɔeɛ.

english_verse: and the man in charge drank some of the water that had now turned into wine. He did not know where the wine had come from, but the servants did. He called the bridegroom over
translation_verse: Adidieɛhwɛfoɔ panyini he kaa nsuo a na adane nsa he bi hwɛeɛ he na onhu baabi a nsa he firi . Ɔfrɛɛ ayefokunu he

english_verse: and said, “The best wine is always served first. Then after the guests have had plenty, the other wine is served. But you have kept the best until last!”
translation_verse: na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Biaa de nsa pa ma n’ahɔhoɔ kane, na sɛ bɛnom pii wie a ɔde nea ɔnyɛ papa he nso ama bɛ. Na wo deɛ wode nsa pa he asie abesi sesɛɛ.”

english_verse: But Jesus was talking about his body as a temple.
translation_verse: Nea ɔteɛ ne sɛ, ɔno mmom deɛ, na ɔyɛ ne nipadua asɔredane ho asɛm na ɔɔka.

english_verse: Not long before the Jewish festival of Passover, Jesus went to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Yudafoɔ Twam Afahyɛ he bɛneeɛ he, Yesu kɔɔ Yerusalem.

english_verse: So he took some rope and made a whip. Then he chased everyone out of the temple, together with their sheep and cattle. He turned over the tables of the moneychangers and scattered their coins.
translation_verse: Ɔde nhoma yɔɔ mmaa de pamoo bɛ nyinaa, nnwane ne anantwie he firi asɔredane mu hɔ, na ohwiee sikasesafoɔ he sika guiɛ, na ɔkaa bɛ apono he nso butubutuiɛ.

english_verse: The disciples then remembered that the Scriptures say, “My love for your house burns in me like a fire.”
translation_verse: Na n’asuafoɔ he kae sɛ bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Wofie ho mmɔdenmmɔ bɛhyɛ me ma.”

english_verse: “Destroy this temple,” Jesus answered, “and in three days I will build it again!”
translation_verse: Yesu buaa bɛ sɛ, “Honnwiri asɔredane yi ngu, na mede nnansa besi no bio.”

english_verse: There he found people selling cattle, sheep, and doves in the temple. He also saw moneychangers sitting at their tables.
translation_verse: Ohuu binom sɛ bɛɛtɔno anantwie, nnwane ne mmorɔnoma, na sika sesafoɔ nso te bɛ pono ho ya asɔrefie hɔ.

english_verse: Jesus said to the people who had been selling doves, “Get those doves out of here! Don't make my Father's house a marketplace.”
translation_verse: Ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ a bɛɛtɔno mmorɔnoma he sɛ, “Homfa yeinom mfiri ha, honnyɛ m’Agya fie dwadibea.”

english_verse: The Jewish leaders asked Jesus, “What miracle will you work to show us why you have done this?”
translation_verse: Afei Yudafoɔ he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Mmɛ nsɛnkyerɛnne ne wode kyerɛ yɛ a nti wooyɔ yei yi?”

english_verse: And when he was raised from death, his disciples remembered what he had told them. Then they believed the Scriptures and the words of Jesus. Jesus Knows What People Are Like
translation_verse: Ɔsɔre firii awufoɔ mu he, n’asuafoɔ he kae sɛ na ɔtaa ka saa; na bɛgyee twerɛsɛm he ne asɛm a Yesu kaeɛ he diiɛ.

english_verse: In Jerusalem during Passover many people put their faith in Jesus, because they saw him work miracles.
translation_verse: Na berɛ a ɔya Yerusalem Twam afahyɛ ase he, nnipa bebree a behuu nsɛnkyerɛnne a ɔyɔeɛ he gyee no diiɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus knew what was in their hearts, and he would not let them have power over him.
translation_verse: Nso, Yesu deɛ wamfa ne ho anto bɛ so, efiri sɛ na onim bɛ nyinaa.

english_verse: No one had to tell him what people were like. He already knew.
translation_verse: Afei nso na onhia sɛ biaa di nipa ho adanseɛ kyerɛ no, efiri sɛ na ɔno ankasa nim nea ɔya nipa mu.

english_verse: There was a man named Nicodemus who was a Pharisee and a Jewish leader.
translation_verse: Na afei na nipa bi ya hɔ a na ɔka Farisifoɔ he ho a na bɛfrɛ no Nikodemo, a na ɔyɛ Yudafoɔ panyini.

english_verse: One night he went to Jesus and said, “Rabbi, we know that God has sent you to teach us. You could not work these miracles, unless God were with you.”
translation_verse: Ɔbaa Yesu hɔ anadwo bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Rabuni, yenim sɛ woyɛ kyerɛkyerɛni a wofiri Nyankopɔn nkyɛne ne aba, na bi ntumi nyɔ nsɛnkyerɛnne a woyɔ yi, gye sɛ Nyankopɔn ka ne ho.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I tell you for certain that you must be born from above before you can see God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Yesu buaa no sɛ, “Meeka nɔkwasɛm paa akyerɛ wo sɛ, bɛamfiri soro anwo bi a ontumi nnya kyɛfa biaa Nyankopɔn ahennie he mu.”

english_verse: Nicodemus asked, “How can a grown man ever be born a second time?”
