translation_verse: Nikodemo bisaa no sɛ, “Ɔbɛyɛ dɛ na nipa a woanyini he, bɛatumi awo no bio? Obetumi akɔ ne maame yam ama bɛ awo no bio anaa?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that before you can get into God's kingdom, you must be born not only by water, but by the Spirit.
translation_verse: Yesu buaa sɛ, “Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ wo sɛ, bɛamfa nsuo ne honhom anwo bi a ontumi nkɔ Nyankopɔn ahennie mu.

english_verse: Humans give life to their children. Yet only God's Spirit can change you into a child of God.
translation_verse: Nea befiri honam mu awo no he yɛ honam, na nea befiri Honhom mu awo no he yɛ honhom.

english_verse: Don't be surprised when I say that you must be born from above.
translation_verse: Mma no nyɛ wo nwanwa sɛ meeka akyerɛ wo sɛ, ɔsɛ sɛ befiri soro wo ho.

english_verse: Only God's Spirit gives new life. The Spirit is like the wind that blows wherever it wants to. You can hear the wind, but you don't know where it comes from or where it is going.
translation_verse: Mframa bɔ kɔ baabi a ɔpɛ, na hote sɛ ɔɔbɔ, nso honnim baabi a ofiri ne baabi a ɔɔkɔ. Saa ne bi a bɛawo no honhom mu he teɛ.”

english_verse: “How can this be?” Nicodemus asked.
translation_verse: Nikodemo buaa sɛ, “Ɔbɛyɛ dɛ na yeinom atumi aba mu?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: How can you be a teacher of Israel and not know these things?
translation_verse: Yesu buaa sɛ, “Nti wo a woyɛ Israel kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ he, wonnim yeinom?”

english_verse: I tell you for certain we know what we are talking about because we have seen it ourselves. But none of you will accept what we say.
translation_verse: Meeka akyerɛ wo nɔkorem paa sɛ, “Nea yenim ne yɛka, na nea yɛani ahu ne yedi ho adanseɛ, nso honnye yɛ adanseɛ he nni.

english_verse: If you don't believe when I talk to you about things on earth, how can you possibly believe if I talk to you about things in heaven?
translation_verse: Sɛ maaka asaase so nsɛm akyerɛ ho na honnye nni a, ɔbɛyɛ dɛ na meka soro nsɛm kyerɛ ho a hoagye adi?

english_verse: No one has gone up to heaven except the Son of Man, who came down from there.
translation_verse: Nipa biaa mforo nkɔɔ soro gye sɛ nea wasiane afiri soro, Nipa Ba he.

english_verse: And the Son of Man must be lifted up, just as the metal snake was lifted up by Moses in the desert.
translation_verse: Na sɛnea Mose maa wɔ he so serɛ so he, saa ne otwa sɛ bɛma Nipa Ba he so,

english_verse: Then everyone who has faith in the Son of Man will have eternal life.
translation_verse: na biaa ɔbɛgye no adi he, benya daa nkwa.”

english_verse: God loved the people of this world so much that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who has faith in him will have eternal life and never really die.
translation_verse: Na sɛnea Nyankopɔn dɔɔ wiase di, sɛ ɔde ne Ba ɔwoo no koro he maeɛ, na biaa a ɔbɛgye no adi he anyera, na mmom wanya daa nkwa.

english_verse: God did not send his Son into the world to condemn its people. He sent him to save them!
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn ansoma ne Ba he amma wiase sɛ ommebu wiase atɛne, na mmom sɛ bɛmfa no so nnye wiase nkwa.

english_verse: No one who has faith in God's Son will be condemned. But everyone who doesn't have faith in him has already been condemned for not having faith in God's only Son.
translation_verse: Nea ɔgye Nyankopɔn Ba he di he, bemmu no atɛne, na nea ɔnnye no nni he, bɛabu no atɛne dada efiri sɛ wannye Nyankopɔn Ba koro pɛ he anni.

english_verse: The light has come into the world, and people who do evil things are judged guilty because they love the dark more than the light.
translation_verse: Na yei ne atemmuo he, sɛ hann he aba wiase na nnipa dɔ sum kyɛne hann he, efiri sɛ bɛ nnwuma yɛ bɔne.

english_verse: People who do evil hate the light and won't come to the light, because it clearly shows what they have done.
translation_verse: Biaa a ɔyɔ bɔne he tane hann he, na ɔmma hann he ho, sɛ ɔbɛyɛ a ne nnwuma he onna adi.

english_verse: But everyone who lives by the truth will come to the light, because they want others to know that God is really the one doing what they do. Jesus and John the Baptist
translation_verse: Na nea ɔyɔ setie ma nɔkorɛ he ba hann he ho sɛ ne nnwuma mmɛda adi, efiri sɛ ɔyɔ no Nyankopɔn mu. Yesu ne Suboni Yohane

english_verse: Later, Jesus and his disciples went to Judea, where he stayed with them for a while and was baptizing people.
translation_verse: Yeinom akyi he, Yesu ne n’asuafoɔ he kɔɔ Yudea asaase so. Ɔne bɛ tenaa hɔ kakra na ɔbɔɔ nkurofoɔ asu.

english_verse: John's followers got into an argument with a Jewish man about a ceremony of washing.
translation_verse: Na akyinnyegyeɛ bi a ɔfa ahodwira ho sɔree Yohane asuafoɔ ne Yudani bi ntam.

english_verse: They went to John and said, “Rabbi, you spoke about a man when you were with him east of the Jordan. He is now baptizing people, and everyone is going to him.”
translation_verse: Nti bɛbaa Yohane nkyɛne bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ, beema a na ɔne woya Yordan agya a wodii ne ho adanseɛ he, ɔɔbɔ asu ha, na nnipa nyinaa ɔɔkɔ ne nkyɛne.”

english_verse: John replied: No one can do anything unless God in heaven allows it.
translation_verse: Yohane buaa sɛ, “Nipa ntumi nya bibi gye sɛ bɛde firi soro ama no.

english_verse: You surely remember how I told you that I am not the Messiah. I am only the one sent ahead of him.
translation_verse: Ho ankasa hodi adanseɛ sɛ mekaa sɛ, ‘Ɔnyɛ mene Kristo he, na mmom bɛsomaa me dii n’anim.’

english_verse: At a wedding the groom is the one who gets married. The best man is glad just to be there and to hear the groom's voice. That's why I am so glad.
translation_verse: Nea ayeforɔ he wɔ no he ne ayefokunu. Na ayefokunu adamfo a ogyina hɔ na otie ayefokunu he deɛ, n’ani gye paa. Nti m’anigyeɛ awie pɛyɛ.

english_verse: Jesus must become more important, while I become less important. The One Who Comes from Heaven
translation_verse: Ɔno deɛ, otwa sɛ ɔkɔ so yɛ kɛse, na me deɛ, me so hwane.” Nea Ofiri Soro ba

english_verse: God's Son comes from heaven and is above all others. Everyone who comes from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks about earthly things. The one who comes from heaven is above all others.
translation_verse: Nea ofiri soro ba he boro biaa so. Ampa nea ofiri asaase so he firi asaase so, na ɔka asaase so nsɛm. Nea ofiri soro ba he boro biaa so.

english_verse: He speaks about what he has seen and heard, and yet no one believes him.
translation_verse: Odi nea wahu na wate ho adanseɛ, nso biaa nnye n’adanseɛ he ntom.

english_verse: But everyone who does believe him has shown that God is truthful.
translation_verse: Nea ɔgye n’adanseɛ he tom he asɔ ano sɛ Nyankopɔn yɛ nɔkwafoɔ.

english_verse: The Son was sent to speak God's message, and he has been given the full power of God's Spirit.
translation_verse: Na nea Nyankopɔn asoma no he ka Nyankopɔn asɛm, na Nyankopɔn nsusu Honhom ansa na ɔde ama.

english_verse: The Father loves the Son and has given him everything.
translation_verse: Agya he dɔ Ba he, na ɔde nnoɔma nyinaa ahyɛ ne nsa.

english_verse: Everyone who has faith in the Son has eternal life. But no one who rejects him will ever share in that life, and God will be angry with them forever.
translation_verse: Nea ɔgye ba he di he wɔ daa nkwa, na nea ontie ba he onhu nkwa, na Nyankopɔn abufuo ya ne so.

english_verse: But Jesus' disciples were really the ones doing the baptizing, and not Jesus himself. Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
translation_verse: Nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, Yesu ankasa ammɔ asu, na n’asuafoɔ he ne ɔmaa bɛbɔeɛ.

english_verse: Jesus left Judea and started for Galilee again.
translation_verse: Ofirii Yudea na ɔsane n’akyi baa Galilea.

english_verse: This time he had to go through Samaria,
translation_verse: Nso na otwa sɛ ɔfa Samaria.

english_verse: “You are a Jew,” she replied, “and I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink of water when Jews and Samaritans won't have anything to do with each other?”
translation_verse: Samariani baa he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne wo a woyɛ Yudani woobisa me Samariani baa nsuo?”

english_verse: “Sir,” the woman said, “you don't even have a bucket, and the well is deep. Where are you going to get this life-giving water?
translation_verse: Na baa he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Owura, wonni adeɛ a bɛde sa nsuo mpo, na abura he nso mu dɔ, nti hefa ne woya nkwa nsuo yi firi?

english_verse: Our ancestor Jacob dug this well for us, and his family and animals got water from it. Are you greater than Jacob?”
translation_verse: Woyɛ kɛseɛ kyɛne yɛ nana Yakobo a ɔde abura he maa yɛ na ɔno ankasa ne ne mma ne ne mmoa nom bi he anaa?”

english_verse: But no one who drinks the water I give will ever be thirsty again. The water I give will become in that person a flowing fountain that gives eternal life.”
translation_verse: na nea ɔbɛbɛnom nsuo a mede bɛma no he, sukɔm ɔnne no da. Na mmom nsuo a mede bɛma no he, bɛyɛ abura a ofifiri ne mu kɔ daa nkwam.”

english_verse: The woman replied, “Sir, please give me a drink of that water! Then I won't get thirsty and have to come to this well again.”
translation_verse: Na baa he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Owura, ma me saa nsuo yi bi nnom, na sukɔm anne me bio, na mankɔ so amma ha ammɛsa nsuo.”

english_verse: Jesus told her, “Go and bring your husband.”
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ baa he sɛ, “Kɔfrɛ wo kunu, na hommra ha.”

english_verse: The woman answered, “I don't have a husband.” “That's right,” Jesus replied, “you're telling the truth. You don't have a husband. You have already been married five times, and the man you are now living with isn't your husband.”
translation_verse: efiri sɛ woaware kununom num, na nea wo ne no wɔ hɔ seesei mpo, ɔnyɛ wo kunu. Nea woaka he yɛ nɔkorɛ.”

english_verse: The woman said, “Sir, I can see that you are a prophet.
translation_verse: Na baa he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Owura, afei maahu sɛ woyɛ diyifoɔ.

english_verse: My ancestors worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews say Jerusalem is the only place to worship.”
translation_verse: Yɛ nananom som bepɔ yi so, nso ho deɛ hoka sɛ Yerusalem ne baabi a ɔsɛ sɛ bɛsom.”

english_verse: Jesus said to her: Believe me, the time is coming when you won't worship the Father either on this mountain or in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Baa, gye me di, berɛ bi ɔɔba a honnsom Agya he bepɔ so anaa Yerusalem.

english_verse: You Samaritans don't really know the one you worship. But we Jews do know the God we worship, and by using us, God will save the world.
translation_verse: Hosom nea honnim no, na yɛ deɛ yɛsom nea yenim no, efiri sɛ nkwagyeɛ firi Yuda.

english_verse: But a time is coming, and it is already here! Even now the true worshipers are being led by the Spirit to worship the Father according to the truth. These are the ones the Father is seeking to worship him.
translation_verse: Na berɛ he ɔɔba, na anya aba sɛ nɔkorɛ asomfoɔ bɛsom Agya he honhom ne nɔkorem, efiri sɛ nnipa saa ne Agya he ɔɔhwehwɛ.

english_verse: God is Spirit, and those who worship God must be led by the Spirit to worship him according to the truth.
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn yɛ honhom, na ɔsɛ sɛ bɛ a bɛsom no he som no honhom ne nɔkorem.”

english_verse: The woman said, “I know that the Messiah will come. He is the one we call Christ. When he comes, he will explain everything to us.”
translation_verse: Na baa he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Menim sɛ Mesaia he ɔɔba” . “Sɛ ɔba a, ɔbɛka yei nyinaa akyerɛ yɛ.”

english_verse: “I am that one,” Jesus told her, “and I am speaking to you now.”
translation_verse: Na Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Me a meekasa akyerɛ wo yi, mene no.”

english_verse: The disciples returned about this time and were surprised to find Jesus talking with a woman. But none of them asked him what he wanted or why he was talking with her.
translation_verse: Saa berɛ heaa ne n’asuafoɔ he baeɛ, na ɔyɛɛ bɛ nwanwa sɛ ɔne baa he bɛɛkasa. Nso biaa anka sɛ “Mmɛ ne woohwehwɛ?” Anaa “Mmɛ nti ne wo ne no ɔɔkasa?”

english_verse: The woman left her water jar and ran back into town, where she said to the people,
translation_verse: Na afei baa he gyaa n’asuhina hɔ kɔɔ kuro he mu kɔka kyerɛɛ nnipa he sɛ,

english_verse: “Come and see a man who told me everything I have ever done! Could he be the Messiah?”
translation_verse: “Hommra mmɛhwɛ beema bi a waka m’asumasɛm nyinaa akyerɛ me! Ɔnyɛ yei ne Kristo he anaa?”

english_verse: Everyone in town went out to see Jesus.
translation_verse: Befirii kuro he mu baa ne nkyɛne.

english_verse: While this was happening, Jesus' disciples were saying to him, “Teacher, please eat something.”
translation_verse: Saa berɛ he na n’asuafoɔ he gu so ɔɔka sɛ, “Rabuni, didi.”

english_verse: But Jesus told them, “I have food you don't know anything about.”
translation_verse: Nso ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Mewɔ aduane bi di a honnim.”

english_verse: His disciples started asking each other, “Has someone brought him something to eat?”
translation_verse: Nti asuafoɔ he bisaa bɛ ho bɛ ho sɛ, “Bi abra no bibi adi anaa?”

english_verse: Jesus said: My food is to do what God wants! He is the one who sent me, and I must finish the work that he gave me to do.
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “M’aduane ne sɛ mɛyɔ nea ɔsomaa me he apɛdeɛ, na mawie n’adwuma.

english_verse: You may say there are still four months until harvest time. But I tell you to look, and you will see that the fields are ripe and ready to harvest.
translation_verse: Ɔyɛ a honka sɛ, ‘Aaka bosome nnan, na twaberɛ aduru’? Hwɛ, meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ homma ho ani so nhwɛ mfuo he sɛ aho sɛ betwa.

english_verse: Even now the harvest workers are receiving their reward by gathering a harvest that brings eternal life. Then everyone who planted the seed and everyone who harvests the crop will celebrate together.
translation_verse: Nea otwa he ɔɔgye akatua dada na ɔɔboaboa aba ano akɔ daa nkwam; na nea oduaeɛ ne nea otwaeɛ nyinaa ani agye abom.

english_verse: So the saying proves true, “Some plant the seed, and others harvest the crop.”
translation_verse: Na yei ma asɛm he yɛ nɔkorɛ sɛ, ‘Bi dua, na bi twa.’

english_verse: I am sending you to harvest crops in fields where others have done all the hard work.
translation_verse: Mesomaa ho sɛ ho nkotwa nea hoanyɔ ho adwuma. Binom ayɔ adwuma, na ho ne hoanya bɛ adwuma he ho mfasoɔ.”

english_verse: A lot of Samaritans in that town put their faith in Jesus because the woman had said, “This man told me everything I have ever done.”
translation_verse: Ɔnam baa he adanseɛ sɛ, “Ɔkaa m’asumasɛm nyinaa kyerɛɛ me” nti, Samariafoɔ bebree a na bɛya saa kuro he mu gyee Yesu diiɛ.

english_verse: They came and asked him to stay in their town, and he stayed on for two days.
translation_verse: Nti berɛ a Samariafoɔ he baa ne nkyɛne he, bɛsrɛɛ no sɛ ɔntena bɛ nkyɛne nnaanu.

english_verse: Many more Samaritans put their faith in Jesus because of what they heard him say.
translation_verse: Na nnipa bebree a bɛboro kane nnipa he gye diiɛ, ɔnam nea baa he kaeɛ nti.

english_verse: They told the woman, “We no longer have faith in Jesus just because of what you told us. We have heard him ourselves, and we are certain that he is the Savior of the world!” Jesus Heals an Official's Son (Matthew
translation_verse: Bɛka kyerɛɛ baa he sɛ, “Afei, ɔnyɛ nea wokaeɛ he nti ne yɛagye adi, efiri sɛ yɛ ankasa yɛate na yɛahu sɛ nipa yi ne wiase Agyenkwa he ampa.” Yesu sane baa Galilea

english_verse: and went to Galilee. The people there welcomed him, because they had gone to the festival in Jerusalem and had seen everything he had done.
translation_verse: Berɛ a ɔbaa Galilea he, Galileafoɔ he gyee no efiri sɛ bɛkɔɔ afahyɛ he ase bi, na behuu nea ɔyɔeɛ he. Yesu saa Ahenkwaa Panyini bi ba Yadeɛ

english_verse: While Jesus was in Galilee, he returned to the village of Cana, where he had turned the water into wine. There was an official in Capernaum whose son was sick.
translation_verse: Afei Yesu sane baa Kana a ɔya Galilea, baabi a ɔdanee nsuo nsa he bio. Na ahenkwaa panyini bi ya hɔ a ne ba yare ya Kapernaum.

english_verse: And when the man heard that Jesus had come from Judea, he went and begged him to keep his son from dying.
translation_verse: Berɛ a beema he tee sɛ Yesu firi Yudea aba Galilea he, ɔkɔɔ ne nkyɛne kɔsrɛɛ no sɛ ɔmɛsa ne ba yadeɛ, efiri sɛ na ɔda wusu.

english_verse: Jesus told the official, “You won't have faith unless you see miracles and wonders!”
translation_verse: Na Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Ho deɛ, hoanhu nsɛnkyerɛnne ne anwanwadeɛ a honnye nni.”

english_verse: The man replied, “Lord, please come before my son dies!”
translation_verse: Ahenkwaa panyini he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Owura, bra na me ba he anwu ansa.”

english_verse: Jesus then said, “Your son will live. Go on home to him.” The man believed Jesus and started back home.
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ “Kɔ, wo ba he ate apɔ!” Na nipa he gyee asɛm a Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no he diiɛ, na ɔkɔeɛ.

english_verse: Some of the official's servants met him along the road and told him, “Your son is better!”
translation_verse: Na ɔɔkɔ he, ne nkoa behyiaa no, na bɛkaa sɛ, “Wo ba he ate apɔ!”

english_verse: He asked them when the boy got better, and they answered, “The fever left him yesterday at one o'clock.”
translation_verse: Na obisaa bɛ berɛ pɔtee a ne ho yɛɛ no dene he, na bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Nnora bɛyɛ awia dummienu ne abunu he kɔeɛ.”

english_verse: The boy's father realized that at one o'clock the day before, Jesus had told him, “Your son will live!” So the man and everyone in his family put their faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Na papa he huu sɛ ɔyɛ saa berɛ heaa ne Yesu kaa sɛ, “Wo ba he onnwu.” Nti ɔne n’abusuafoɔ nyinaa gye diiɛ.

english_verse: This was the second miracle that Jesus worked after he left Judea and went to Galilee.
translation_verse: Yei nso ne nsɛnkyerɛnne a ɔtɔ so mmienu a Yesu firi Yudea baa Galilea he, ɔyɔeɛ.

english_verse: Later, Jesus went to Jerusalem for another Jewish festival.
translation_verse: Yei akyi he na Yudafoɔ ɔɔhyɛ fa, na Yesu kɔɔ Yerusalem.

english_verse: In the city near the sheep gate was a pool with five porches, and its name in Hebrew was Bethzatha.
translation_verse: Na tadeɛ bi ya Yerusalem “Nnwantene Pono” kɛseɛ he ho a bɛfrɛ no Hebri kasa mu sɛ Betesda a ɔwɔ abranaa num.

english_verse: Many sick, blind, lame, and paralyzed people were lying close to the pool.
translation_verse: [Nea ɔteɛ ne sɛ, sɛ Awurade bɔfoɔ he hono nsuo he wie a, yarefoɔ a obedi kane akɔtɔ nsuo he mu he nya ayaresa.]

english_verse: Beside the pool was a man who had been sick for
translation_verse: Na abranteɛ bi ya hɔ a wayare mfie aduasa-nwɔtwe.

english_verse: You have not believed his message, because you refused to have faith in the one he sent.
translation_verse: na n’asɛm nni ho mu, efiri sɛ nea ɔsomaa me he mpo honnye no nni.

english_verse: When Jesus saw the man and realized that he had been crippled for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to be healed?”
translation_verse: Berɛ a Yesu huu beema he sɛ ɔda hɔ he, na onim sɛ wayare akyɛre he, obisaa no sɛ, “Wopɛ sɛ wo ho yɛ wo dene?”

english_verse: The man answered, “Sir, I don't have anyone to put me in the pool when the water is stirred up. I try to get in, but someone else always gets there first.”
translation_verse: Yarefoɔ he buaa no sɛ, “Owura, menni nipa biaa a sɛ nsuo he hono a ɔde me bɛkɔ akɔtɔ mu, na sɛ meyɛ sɛ meekɔtɔ mu a na bi adi m’anim kane akɔtɔ mu.”

english_verse: Jesus told him, “Pick up your mat and walk!”
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Sɔre fa wo kɛtɛ na nante.”

english_verse: Right then the man was healed. He picked up his mat and started walking around. The day on which this happened was a Sabbath.
translation_verse: Amonom hɔaa beema he ho yɛɛ no dene, na ɔfaa ne kɛtɛ firi aseɛ nanteeɛ. Nso na saa da he yɛ homeda.

english_verse: When the Jewish leaders saw the man carrying his mat, they said to him, “This is the Sabbath! No one is allowed to carry a mat on the Sabbath.”
translation_verse: Nti Yudafoɔ he ka kyerɛɛ beema a Yesu saa no yadeɛ he sɛ, “Ɔyɛ homeda, na mmara mma kwane sɛ wobɛsoa wo kɛtɛ.”

english_verse: But he replied, “The man who healed me told me to pick up my mat and walk.”
translation_verse: Na obuaa bɛ sɛ, “Nea ɔsaa me yadeɛ heaa ne ka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, ‘Fa wo kɛtɛ na nante.’ ”

english_verse: They asked him, “Who is this man that told you to pick up your mat and walk?”
translation_verse: Bebisaa no sɛ, “Nipa hwane ne ka kyerɛɛ wo sɛ, ‘Fa wo kɛtɛ na nante?’ ”

english_verse: But he did not know who Jesus was, and Jesus had left because of the crowd.
translation_verse: Nso na nea ne ho ayɛ no dene he nnim nipa korɔ a ɔsaa no yadeɛ he, efiri sɛ na Yesu atwe ne ho kɔ, ɔnam nnipakuo a na bɛya hɔ nti.

english_verse: Later, Jesus met the man in the temple and told him, “You are now well. But don't sin anymore or something worse might happen to you.”
translation_verse: Yei akyi he, Yesu huu no asɔredane he mu, na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Hwɛ, wo ho ayɛ dene, nyɔ bɔne bio na nea ɔkyɛne yei amma wo so.”

english_verse: The man left and told the leaders that Jesus was the one who had healed him.
translation_verse: Beema he kɔka kyerɛɛ Yudafoɔ he sɛ, Yesu ne saa no yadeɛ.

english_verse: They started making a lot of trouble for Jesus because he did things like this on the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Yei nti Yudafoɔ he hyɛɛ aseɛ sɛ bɛɛtaa Yesu, efiri sɛ ɔyɔɔ yeinom homeda.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “My Father has never stopped working, and this is why I keep on working.”
translation_verse: Na Yesu buaa bɛ sɛ, “M’Agya gu so ɔɔyɔ adwuma besi sesɛɛ, na me nso meeyɔ adwuma.”

english_verse: Now the leaders wanted to kill Jesus for two reasons. First, he had broken the law of the Sabbath. But even worse, he had said God was his Father, which made him equal with God. The Son's Authority
translation_verse: Na yei nti afei Yudafoɔ he hwehwɛɛ sɛ bebeku no, efiri sɛ ɔnyɛ homeda nkoaa ne wabu so, na mmom ɔfrɛ Nyankopɔn sɛ ɔno ankasa n’Agya, de yɛ ne ho sɛ ɔne Nyankopɔn yɛ pɛ. Tumi a Ba he wɔ

english_verse: Jesus told the people: I tell you for certain the Son cannot do anything on his own. He can do only what he sees the Father doing, and he does exactly what he sees the Father do.
translation_verse: Yesu buaa bɛ sɛ, “Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, Ba he ankasa ntumi mmɔ ne ti mu nyɔ hwee, gye sɛ nea ohu sɛ Agya he yɔ, efiri sɛ bibiaa Agya he yɔ he, ɔno ne Ba he nso yɔ.

english_verse: The Father loves the Son and has shown him everything he does. The Father will show him even greater things, and you will be amazed.
translation_verse: Agya he dɔ Ba he, na ɔbɛkyerɛ no nnoɔma a ɔno ankasa yɔ nyinaa. Na ɔbɛkyerɛ no nnwuma akɛseɛ asene yeinom na ayɛ ho nwanwa.

english_verse: Just as the Father raises the dead and gives life, so the Son gives life to anyone he wants to.
translation_verse: Na sɛnea Agya he nyane awufoɔ na ɔma bɛ nkwa he, saa ne Ba he nso de nkwa ma bɛ a ɔno ankasa pɛ.

english_verse: The Father doesn't judge anyone, but he has made his Son the judge of everyone.
translation_verse: Na Agya he mmu biaa atɛne, na mmom ɔde atemmuo nyinaa ahyɛ Ba he nsa,

english_verse: The Father wants all people to honor the Son as much as they honor him. When anyone refuses to honor the Son, this is the same as refusing to honor the Father who sent him.
translation_verse: na ama biaa adi Ba he ni sɛnea bedi Agya he ni heaa. Nea onni Ba he ni he, onni Agya a ɔsomaa no he nso ni.

english_verse: I tell you for certain that everyone who hears my message and has faith in the one who sent me has eternal life and will never be condemned. They have already gone from death to life.
translation_verse: Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, nea otie m’asɛm na ɔgye nea ɔsomaa me he di he wɔ daa nkwa, na ɔmma atemmuom, na mmom waafiri wuom aba nkwam.

english_verse: I tell you for certain the time will come, and it is already here, when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God. And those who listen to it will live!
translation_verse: “Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, berɛ he ɔɔba, na anya aduru, sɛ awufoɔ bɛte Nyankopɔn Ba he nne, na bɛ a bebetie no he benya nkwa.

english_verse: The Father has the power to give life, and he has given that same power to the Son.
translation_verse: Na sɛnea Agya he wɔ nkwa ɔnoaa ne mu he, saa na ɔde ama Ba he nso sɛ onya nkwa ɔno ankasa ne mu.

english_verse: And he has given his Son the right to judge everyone, because he is the Son of Man.
translation_verse: Na waama no tumi sɛ ommu atɛne nso, efiri sɛ ɔno ne Nipa Ba he.

english_verse: Don't be surprised! The time will come when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of Man,
translation_verse: Hommma yei nyɔ ho nwanwa, efiri sɛ berɛ he ɔɔba a bɛ a bɛya adakamenam nyinaa bɛte ne nne,

english_verse: and they will come out of their graves. Everyone who has done good things will rise to life, but everyone who has done evil things will rise and be condemned.
translation_verse: na bɛafiri adi, na bɛ a bɛayɔ papa he bɛkɔ nkwa wusɔreɛ mu, na bɛ a bɛayɔ bɔne he bɛkɔ fobuo wusɔreɛ mu. Yesu ho Adansedie

english_verse: I cannot do anything on my own. The Father sent me, and he is the one who told me how to judge. I judge with fairness, because I obey him, and I don't just try to please myself. Witnesses to Jesus
translation_verse: “M’ankasa mentumi mmɔ me tim nyɔ hwee. Nea mete heaa ne megyina so bu atɛne, na atɛne a mebu he tene, efiri sɛ menhwehwɛ nea m’ankasa mepɛ, na mmom nea ɔsomaa me he apɛdeɛ.

english_verse: If I speak for myself, there is no way to prove I am telling the truth.
translation_verse: “Sɛ m’ankasa medi me ho adanseɛ a, m’adanseɛ he nyɛ nɔkorɛ.

english_verse: But there is someone else who speaks for me, and I know what he says is true.
translation_verse: Bi ya hɔ a odi me ho adanseɛ na menim sɛ adanseɛ a odi fa me ho he yɛ nɔkorɛ.

english_verse: You sent messengers to John, and he told them the truth.
translation_verse: Hosomaa binom kɔɔ Yohane nkyɛne, na odii nɔkorɛ adanseɛ,

english_verse: I don't depend on what people say about me, but I tell you these things so that you may be saved.
translation_verse: na me deɛ, mennye adanseɛ nipa biaa nkyɛne, na meeka yeinom na hoanya nkwa.

english_verse: John was a lamp that gave a lot of light, and you were glad to enjoy his light for a while.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛ kanea a ɔdɛre na ɔhyerɛn, na ho deɛ, na hopɛ sɛ ho ani gye mmerɛ kakra ne hann mu.

english_verse: But something more important than John speaks for me. I mean the things that the Father has given me to do! All of these speak for me and prove that the Father sent me.
translation_verse: Nso mewɔ adanseɛ a ɔkyɛne Yohane deɛ he, efiri sɛ nnwuma a m’Agya de ahyɛ me nsa sɛ menyɔ he, nnwuma a meeyɔ heaa di me ho adanseɛ sɛ Agya heaa ne asoma me.

english_verse: The Father who sent me also speaks for me, but you have never heard his voice or seen him face to face.
translation_verse: Na Agya he a ɔsomaa me he ankasa di me ho adanseɛ. Hontee ne nne, na honhuu ne su nso,

english_verse: You search the Scriptures, because you think you will find eternal life in them. The Scriptures tell about me,
translation_verse: “Hohwehwɛ twerɛsɛm he mu, efiri sɛ hogye di sɛ ɔno ne bɛma ho nkwa, na ɔnoaa nso ne di me ho adanseɛ.

english_verse: but you refuse to come to me for eternal life.
translation_verse: Nso, hommpɛ sɛ ho ba me nkyɛne na hoanya nkwa.

english_verse: I don't care about human praise,
translation_verse: Mennye animuonyam mfiri nnipa nkyɛne.

english_verse: but I do know that none of you love God.
translation_verse: Na menim yie sɛ honni Nyankopɔn dɔ ya ho mu.

english_verse: I have come with my Father's authority, and you have not welcomed me. But you will welcome people who come on their own.
translation_verse: Maaba m’Agya dini mu, nso honnye me, sɛ foforɔ ba ɔno anakasa ne dini mu a, ɔno ne hobɛgye no.

english_verse: How could you possibly believe? You like to have your friends praise you, and you don't care about praise that the only God can give!
translation_verse: Hobɛyɔ dɛ na hoagye adi berɛ a hogye animuonyam firi hoaa ho ho, na honnye animuonyam mfiri Nyankopɔn korɔ he nkyɛne?

english_verse: Don't think that I will be the one to accuse you to the Father. You have put your hope in Moses, yet he is the very one who will accuse you.
translation_verse: Honnsusu sɛ mɛbɔ ho soboɔ akyerɛ Agya he, na nea ɔbɔ ho soboɔ he ya hɔ, ɔno ne Mose a hode ho ani ato ne so he.

english_verse: Moses wrote about me, and if you had believed Moses, you would have believed me.
translation_verse: Sɛ hogye Mose di a, hobegye me nso adi, efiri sɛ Mose twerɛɛ me ho asɛm.

english_verse: But if you don't believe what Moses wrote, how can you believe what I say?
translation_verse: Na sɛ honnye asɛm a ɔtwerɛɛ he nni a, ɔbɛyɛ dɛ na hoagye me nsɛm adi?”

english_verse: The disciples gathered them up and filled twelve large baskets with what was left over from the five barley loaves.
translation_verse: Nti bɛboaboa ano, na atokoɔ paanoo mproprowa he yɛɛ nkɛntɛne dummienu ma ma.

english_verse: The disciples wanted to take him into the boat, but suddenly the boat reached the shore where they were headed. The Bread That Gives Life
translation_verse: Afei deɛ na bɛpɛ sɛ ɔbɛtena kodoɔ he mu, na ankyɛre koraa ne kodoɔ he duruu asaase a bɛɛkɔ so he so. Paanoo a ofiri Soro baeɛ

english_verse: They replied, “What miracle will you work, so that we can have faith in you? What will you do?
translation_verse: Nti bebisaa no sɛ, “Abɛ nsɛnkyerɛnne ne wobɛyɔ na yɛagye wo adi? Mmɛ adwuma ne wooyɔ?

english_verse: No one can come to me, unless the Father who sent me makes them want to come. But if they do come, I will raise them to life on the last day.
translation_verse: Biaa ntumi mma me nkyɛne gye sɛ Agya a ɔsomaa me he twe no ba; na menyane no nna odi akyire he.

english_verse: “There is a boy here who has five small loaves of barley bread and two fish. But what good is that with all these people?”
translation_verse: “Abeemaa bi ya ha a ɔwɔ atokoɔ paanoo nnum ne nkyenam nketewa mmienu. Nso yei bɛyɛ nnipadɔm yi dɛ?”

english_verse: The ground was covered with grass, and Jesus told his disciples to tell everyone to sit down. About
translation_verse: Na Yesu kaa sɛ, “Homma nnipa he ntenatena ase!” Nso na serɛ pii ya hɔ. Nti mmeema a bɛ dodoɔ bɛyɛ mpem num tenatenaa ase.

english_verse: They got into a boat and started across for Capernaum. Later that evening Jesus had still not come to them,
translation_verse: na bɛforo tenaa kodoɔ bi mu sɛ beetwa po he akɔ Kapernaum. Saa berɛ he na sum ahyɛ dada, na Yesu nnya mmaa bɛ nkyɛne.

english_verse: Jesus crossed Lake Galilee, which was also known as Lake Tiberias.
translation_verse: Yeinom akyi he, Yesu kɔɔ Galilea po, nea bɛsane frɛ no Tiberia po, he agya.

english_verse: A large crowd had seen him work miracles to heal the sick, and those people went with him.
translation_verse: Na nnipakuo kɛseɛ kɔɔ so dii n’akyi, efiri sɛ behuu nsɛnkyerɛnne a na ɔɔyɔ ma ayarefoɔ.

english_verse: Philip answered, “Don't you know that it would take almost a year's wages just to buy only a little bread for each of these people?”
translation_verse: Filipo buaa sɛ, “Paanoo a ne boɔ bɛyɛ paani bosome nsia akatua mpo ɔnso bɛ sɛ bɛ mu biaa benya bi adi.”

english_verse: Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter, was one of the disciples. He spoke up and said,
translation_verse: Na asuafoɔ he mu baako, Andrea, Simon Petro nua beema, ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ,

english_verse: Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave thanks to God. Then he passed the bread to the people, and he did the same with the fish, until everyone had plenty to eat.
translation_verse: Na Yesu faa paanoo ne nkyenam he, na ɔdaa ase he, ɔkyɛ maa bɛ a bɛtete hɔ he; saa nso ne ɔmaa bɛ nkyenam he dodoɔ a biaa hia.

english_verse: The people ate all they wanted, and Jesus told his disciples to gather up the leftovers, so that nothing would be wasted.
translation_verse: Na bɛmemeeɛ he, ɔka kyerɛɛ n’asuafoɔ he sɛ, “Hommoaboa mproprowa he ano, na bi anyera.”

english_verse: Jesus realized that they would try to force him to be their king. So he went up on a mountain, where he could be alone. Jesus Walks on the Water (Matthew
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a Yesu huu sɛ bɛpɛ sɛ bɛkye no si no hene he, ɔno ankasa twee ne ho kɔɔ bepɔ he so bio. Yesu nantee Nsuo so (Mateo

english_verse: The people who had stayed on the east side of the lake knew that only one boat had been there. They also knew that Jesus had not left in it with his disciples. But the next day
translation_verse: Adeɛ kyeeɛ he, nnipakuo a na begyina po he agya he huu sɛ kodoɔ biaa nni hɔ gye sɛ baako a n’asuafoɔ he kɔɔ mu he, nso Yesu ne bɛ ankɔ kodoɔ he mu, na n’asuafoɔ he nkoaa ne bɛkɔeɛ.

english_verse: Don't work for food that spoils. Work for food that gives eternal life. The Son of Man will give you this food, because God the Father has given him the right to do so.”
translation_verse: Honnyɔ aduane a ɔsɛe ho adwuma, na mmom honyɔ aduane a ɔtena hɔ de kɔ daa nkwam a Nipa Ba he de bɛma ho he ho adwuma, efiri sɛ, ɔno ne Agya Nyankopɔn ankasa de ne nsɔano agye no atom.”

english_verse: One of the prophets wrote, “God will teach all of them.” And so everyone who listens to the Father and learns from him will come to me.
translation_verse: Bɛatwerɛ ya adiyifoɔ he mu sɛ, ‘Nyankopɔn bɛkyerɛkyerɛ bɛ nyinaa.’ Biaa a wate bibi afiri Agya he nkyɛne na wasua he ne ba me nkyɛne.

english_verse: They started arguing with each other and asked, “How can he give us his flesh to eat?”
translation_verse: Na afei Yudafoɔ he firi aseɛ kasakasaa bɛ mu sɛ, “Ɔbɛyɛ dɛ na saa beema yi de ne honam ama yɛ adi?”

english_verse: That evening, Jesus' disciples went down to the lake.
translation_verse: Na nwunu doeɛ he, n’asuafoɔ he kɔɔ mpoano,

english_verse: and a strong wind was making the water rough.
translation_verse: Na mframa kɛseɛ bɔ maa po he huruiɛ.

english_verse: When the disciples had rowed for five or six kilometers, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He kept coming closer to the boat, and they were terrified.
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a bɛharee bɛyɛ nkwansini nnan he, behuu sɛ Yesu nam po he so ɔɔba kodoɔ he ho, na besuroeɛ.

english_verse: But he said, “I am Jesus! Don't be afraid!”
translation_verse: Nso ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Ɔyɛ me a, honnsuro.”

english_verse: some boats from Tiberias sailed near the place where the crowd had eaten the bread for which the Lord had given thanks.
translation_verse: Na afei akodoɔ firi Tiberia baa baabi a Awurade daa ase, na bedii paanoo he.

english_verse: They saw that Jesus and his disciples had left. Then they got into the boats and went to Capernaum to look for Jesus.
translation_verse: Na nnipakuo he huu sɛ Yesu ne n’asuafoɔ he mu biaa nni hɔ he, bɛkɔtenatenaa kodoɔ nketewa mu baa Kapernaum bɛhwehwɛɛ Yesu.

english_verse: They found him on the west side of the lake and asked, “Rabbi, when did you get here?”
translation_verse: Na berɛ a behuu no po he agya he, bebisaa no sɛ, “Kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ, mmɛ berɛ ne wobaa ha?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that you are not looking for me because you saw the miracles, but because you ate all the food you wanted.
translation_verse: Yesu buaa bɛ sɛ, “Nɔkwasɛm a ɔyam ne sɛ, ɔnyɛ nsɛnkyerɛnne a hohuiɛ nti ne hoohwehwɛ me, na mmom paanoo a hodi meeɛ he nti a.

english_verse: “What exactly does God want us to do?” the people asked.
translation_verse: Nti bebisaa no sɛ, “Ɛɛne ne ɔsɛ sɛ yɛ yɔ na yɛayɔ Nyankopɔn nnwuma?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “God wants you to have faith in the one he sent.”
translation_verse: Yesu buaa bɛ sɛ, “Nyankopɔn adwuma ne sɛ hobɛgye nea ɔsomaa no he adi.”

english_verse: For example, when our ancestors were in the desert, they were given manna to eat. It happened just as the Scriptures say, ‘God gave them bread from heaven to eat.’ ”
translation_verse: Yɛ nananom dii mana serɛ he so sɛnea bɛatwerɛ he, ‘Ɔmaa bɛ paanoo a ofiri soro ma bediiɛ.’ ”

english_verse: Jesus then told them, “I tell you for certain that Moses wasn't the one who gave you bread from heaven. My Father is the one who gives you the true bread from heaven.
translation_verse: Na Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Meeka akyerɛ ho nɔkorem paa sɛ, ɔnyɛ Mose ne maa ho paanoo a ofiri soro he, na mmom m’Agya ne maa ho paanoo kann a ofiri soro he.

english_verse: And the bread that God gives is the one who came down from heaven to give life to the world.”
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn paanoo ne nea osiane firi soro ba na ɔma wiase nkwa he.”

english_verse: The people said, “Sir, give us this bread and don't ever stop!”
translation_verse: Bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Owura, ma yɛ paanoo he bi da biaa.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: I am the bread that gives life! No one who comes to me will ever be hungry. No one who has faith in me will ever be thirsty.
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Mene nkwa paanoo he. Nea ɔba menkyɛne he kɔm ɔnne no da, na nea ɔgye me di he, sukɔm ɔnne no da.

english_verse: I have told you already that you have seen me and still do not have faith in me.
translation_verse: Nso maka akyerɛ ho sɛ, hoahu me nso honnye nni.

english_verse: Everything and everyone that the Father has given me will come to me, and I won't turn any of them away.
translation_verse: Bibiaa a Agya he de bɛma me he bɛba me nkyɛne, na nea ɔba me nkyɛne he, mempam no da,

english_verse: I didn't come from heaven to do what I want! I came to do what the Father wants me to do. He sent me,
translation_verse: efiri sɛ mefiri soro ne aba sɛ hobɛyɔ nea ɔsomaa me he apɛdeɛ, na ɔnyɛ m’ankasa me deɛ.

english_verse: and he wants to make certain that none of the ones he has given me will be lost. Instead, he wants me to raise them to life on the last day.
translation_verse: Nea ɔsomaa me he apɛdeɛ ne sɛ memma nea ɔde ama me he mu biaa nyera, na mmom sɛ menyane no da a odi akyi he.

english_verse: My Father wants everyone who sees the Son to have faith in him and to have eternal life. Then I will raise them to life on the last day.
translation_verse: Na m’Agya apɛdeɛ ne sɛ biaa a ohu Ba he na ɔgye no di he benya daa nkwa, na menyane no da a odi akyi he.”

english_verse: The people started grumbling because Jesus had said he was the bread that had come down from heaven.
translation_verse: Afei Yudafoɔ he firi aseɛ nwiinwii sɛ waka sɛ, “Mene paanoo a osiane firii soro he.”

english_verse: They were asking each other, “Isn't he Jesus, the son of Joseph? Don't we know his father and mother? How can he say that he has come down from heaven?”
translation_verse: Na bɛkaa sɛ, “Ɔnyɛ yei ne Yesu, Yosef ba, nea yenim ne papa ne ne maame he? Ɔyɛɛ dɛ ne afei deɛ ɔdɛ, ‘Mene paanoo a osiane firii soro he’?”

english_verse: Jesus told them: Stop grumbling!
translation_verse: Yesu buaa bɛ sɛ, “Honwiinwii ho mu.

english_verse: The only one who has seen the Father is the one who has come from him. No one else has ever seen the Father.
translation_verse: Biaa nhuu Agya he, gye sɛ nea ofiri Nyankopɔn hɔ he; ɔno ne wahu Agya he.

english_verse: I tell you for certain that everyone who has faith in me has eternal life.
translation_verse: Meeka akyerɛ ho nɔkorem sɛ, biaa a ɔgye di he benya daa nkwa.

english_verse: I am the bread that gives life!
translation_verse: Mene nkwa paanoo he.

english_verse: Your ancestors ate manna in the desert, and later they died.
translation_verse: Ho nananom dii mana serɛ he so, na bewuwuiɛ.

english_verse: But the bread from heaven has come down, so that no one who eats it will ever die.
translation_verse: Yei ne paanoo a osiane firi soro he, sɛ biaa nni na wanwu.

english_verse: I am that bread from heaven! Everyone who eats it will live forever. My flesh is the life-giving bread I give to the people of this world.
translation_verse: Mene paanoo a nkwa yam a mesiane firi soro baeɛ he, sɛ bi di paanoo yi bi a, ɔbɛtena ase daa. Na paanoo a mede bɛma wiase nkwa he ne me honam.”

english_verse: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that you won't live unless you eat the flesh and drink the blood of the Son of Man.
translation_verse: Nti Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Meeka akyerɛ ho nɔkorem sɛ, sɛ hoanni Nipa Ba he honam na hoannom ne mogyaa a, honnya nkwa ya ho mu.

english_verse: But if you do eat my flesh and drink my blood, you will have eternal life, and I will raise you to life on the last day.
translation_verse: Bɛ a bedi me honam na bɛnom me mogyaa he wɔ daa nkwa, na menyane bɛ nna odi akyire he,

english_verse: My flesh is the true food, and my blood is the true drink.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ me honam yɛ aduane ampa na me mogyaa yɛ anonneɛ ampa.

english_verse: If you eat my flesh and drink my blood, you are one with me, and I am one with you.
translation_verse: Bɛ a bedi me honam na bɛnom me mogyaa tena me mu, na me nso metena bɛ mu.

english_verse: The living Father sent me, and I have life because of him. Now everyone who eats my flesh will live because of me.
translation_verse: Sɛnea Agya a ɔte ase he somaa me he, na Agya he nti mete ase he, saa ara nso ne nea odi me honam he, me nti ɔbɛtena ase.

english_verse: The bread that comes down from heaven isn't like what your ancestors ate. They died, but whoever eats this bread will live forever.
translation_verse: Yei ne paanoo a osiane firii soro baeɛ a ɔnte sɛ nea ho nananom diiɛ na bewuiɛ he; nea odi paanoo yi bɛtena ase daa.”

english_verse: Jesus was teaching in a synagogue in Capernaum when he said these things. The Words of Eternal Life
translation_verse: Ɔkaa saa nsɛm yi berɛ a na ɔɔkyerɛkyerɛ Kapernaum hyiadane mu. Daa Nkwa Nsɛm

english_verse: Many of Jesus' disciples heard him and said, “This is too hard for anyone to understand.”
translation_verse: Nti berɛ a n’asuafoɔ he mu bebree tee yei he, bɛkaa sɛ, “Asɛm yi ano ɔdene, hwane ne betumi agye?”

english_verse: Jesus knew that his disciples were grumbling. So he asked, “Does this bother you?
translation_verse: Nso esiane sɛ na Yesu nim sɛ n’asuafoɔ he nwiinwii yei ho nti, obisaa bɛ sɛ, “Yei to ho sintidua anaa?

english_verse: What if you should see the Son of Man go up to heaven where he came from?
translation_verse: Na afei, sɛ hohu Nipa Ba he sɛ ɔɔkɔ soro, baabi a na ɔya kane he nso ɛ?

english_verse: The Spirit is the one who gives life! Human strength can do nothing. The words that I have spoken to you are from that life-giving Spirit.
translation_verse: Honhom ne ma nkwa; honam deɛ nka hwee koraa. Nsɛm a maka akyerɛ ho yi yɛ honhom ne nkwa.

english_verse: But some of you refuse to have faith in me.” Jesus said this, because from the beginning he knew who would have faith in him. He also knew which one would betray him.
translation_verse: Nso ho mu binom nnye nni.” Na ofiri mfitiaseɛ he na Yesu nnim bɛ a bɛnnye nni ne nea obeyi no ama he.

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “You cannot come to me, unless the Father makes you want to come. That is why I have told these things to all of you.”
translation_verse: Na ɔkaa sɛ, “Yei nti ne meka kyerɛ ho sɛ biaa ntumi mma me nkyɛne gye sɛ Agya he ama no kwane.”

english_verse: Because of what Jesus said, many of his disciples turned their backs on him and stopped following him.
translation_verse: Yei nti n’asuafoɔ he bebree sane kɔɔ bɛ akyi, na bɛne no annante bio.

english_verse: Jesus then asked his twelve disciples if they also were going to leave him.
translation_verse: Nti Yesu bisaa dummienu he sɛ, “Ho nso hopɛ sɛ ho kɔ anaa?”

english_verse: Simon Peter answered, “Lord, there is no one else that we can go to! Your words give eternal life.
translation_verse: Simon Petro buaa sɛ, “Awurade, hwane nkyɛne ne yɛbɛkɔ? Wo nkoaa ne wowɔ daa nkwa nsɛm.

english_verse: We have faith in you, and we are sure that you are God's Holy One.”
translation_verse: Na yɛagye adi, na yɛahu sɛ wone Kronkronni a wofiri Nyankopɔn hɔ he.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples, “I chose all twelve of you, but one of you is a demon!”
translation_verse: Yesu buaa bɛ sɛ, “Nyɛ mene maayi ho nnipa dummienu yi? Nso ho mu baako yɛ bronsam.”

english_verse: Jesus was talking about Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot. He would later betray Jesus, even though he was one of the twelve disciples.
translation_verse: Na ɔɔka Yuda Iskariot, Simon ba he ho asɛm. Ewom sɛ na ɔyɛ dummienu he mu baako deɛ, nso na oobeyi Yesu ama.

english_verse: Jesus decided to leave Judea and to start going through Galilee because the leaders of the people wanted to kill him.
translation_verse: Yei akyi he, Yesu kɔɔ Galilea. Na ɔmpɛ sɛ ɔbɛkɔ Yudea efiri sɛ na Yudafoɔ he ɔɔhwehwɛ kwane bi afa so aku no.

english_verse: It was almost time for the Festival of Shelters,
translation_verse: Afei na Yudafoɔ Apata Afahyɛ he abɛne.

english_verse: and Jesus' brothers said to him, “Why don't you go to Judea? Then your disciples can see what you are doing.
translation_verse: Nti ne nuanom ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Firi ha kɔ Yudea sɛ ɔbɛyɛ na w’asuafoɔ he nso behu nnwuma a wooyɔ,

english_verse: No one does anything in secret, if they want others to know about them. So let the world know what you are doing!”
translation_verse: efiri sɛ biaa a ɔpɛ sɛ nnipa bebree behu no he nni dwuma kokoam. Sɛ hoyɔ saa nnoɔma yi a, homfa ho ho nkyerɛ wiase.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “My time hasn't yet come, but your time is always here.
translation_verse: Nti Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Me berɛ nnuruiɛ, na ho deɛ, berɛ biaa yɛ ma ho.

english_verse: The people of this world cannot hate you. They hate me, because I tell them that they do evil things.
translation_verse: Wiase nni nnyinasoɔ biaa de tane ho, na me deɛ ɔtane me, efiri sɛ medi ne ho adanseɛ sɛ ne nnwuma yɛ bɔne.

english_verse: Go on to the festival. My time hasn't yet come, and I am not going.”
translation_verse: Honkɔ afahyɛ he ase, me deɛ mennya nkɔɔ afahyɛ yi ase, efiri sɛ berɛ he nnuruiɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said this and stayed on in Galilee. Jesus at the Festival of Shelters
translation_verse: Na ɔkaa yeinom wieeɛ he, ɔtenaa Galilea. Yesu kɔɔ afahyɛ he ase

english_verse: After Jesus' brothers had gone to the festival, he went secretly, without telling anyone.
translation_verse: Na ne nuammeemanom kɔɔ afahyɛ he ase he, ɔno nso kɔeɛ a wannyi ne ho adi na mmom ɔde ne ho sieeɛ.

english_verse: During the festival the leaders of the people looked for Jesus and asked, “Where is he?”
translation_verse: Na Yudafoɔ ɔɔhwehwɛ no afahyɛ he ase; na bebisaa sɛ, “Ɔya he?”

english_verse: The crowds even got into an argument about him. Some were saying, “Jesus is a good man,” while others were saying, “He is lying to everyone.”
translation_verse: Na bɛyɛɛ ne ho huhuhuhu pii nnipadɔm he mu. Binom kaa sɛ. “Ɔyɛ nipa pa.” Binom nso kaa sɛ, “Daabi, ɔɔdaadaa nkurofoɔ he.”

english_verse: But the people were afraid of their leaders, and none of them talked in public about him.
translation_verse: Nso, Yudafoɔ he ho hu nti, biaa anyi n’anim anka ne ho asɛm.

english_verse: When the festival was about half over, Jesus went into the temple and started teaching.
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a afahyɛ he duruu mfimfini he, Yesu kɔɔ asɔredane he mu, na ofirii aseɛ kyerɛkyerɛeɛ.

english_verse: The leaders were surprised and said, “How does this man know so much? He has never been taught!”
translation_verse: Na yei yɛɛ Yudafoɔ he nwanwa ma bɛkaa sɛ, “Ɔyɛɛ dɛ ne nipa a onsuaa nwoma biaa yi nim nwoma saa?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: I am not teaching something I thought up. What I teach comes from the one who sent me.
translation_verse: Na Yesu buaa bɛ sɛ, “Nea me kyerɛkyerɛ he nyɛ me dea, na mmom ɔyɛ nea ɔsomaa me he dea.”

english_verse: If you really want to obey God, you will know if what I teach comes from God or from me.
translation_verse: Sɛ bi pɛ sɛ ɔyɛ n’apɛdeɛ a, obehu sɛ nkyerɛkyerɛ he firi Nyankopɔn hɔ anaa m’ankasa ne mebɔ me tim kasa.

english_verse: If I wanted to bring honor to myself, I would speak for myself. But I want to honor the one who sent me. This is why I tell the truth and not a lie.
translation_verse: Bɛ a bɛbɔ betim kasa he hwehwɛ bɛ ankasa bɛ animuonyam; na nea ɔhwehwɛ nea ɔsomaa no he animuonyam he yɛ nɔkwafoɔ a nea ɔntene biaa nni ne mu.

english_verse: Didn't Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you obey it! So why do you want to kill me?
translation_verse: “Ɔnyɛ Mose ne maa ho mmara he? Nso ho mu baako mpo nni mmara he so. Ɛɛne nti ne hoopɛ kwane afa so aku me?”

english_verse: The crowd replied, “You're crazy! What makes you think someone wants to kill you?”
translation_verse: Nnipa he buaa sɛ, “Wowɔ honhommɔne; hwane ne ɔɔhwehwɛ wo aku wo?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: I worked one miracle, and it amazed you.
translation_verse: Yesu buaa bɛ sɛ, “Maayɔ adwuma baako, na yei yɛ ho nyinaa nwanwa.

english_verse: in order to obey the Law of Moses. Why are you angry with me for making someone completely well on the Sabbath?
translation_verse: Sɛ hotumi twa nipa twetia homeda sɛ ɔbɛyɛ a ɔnto Mose mmara he a, ɛɛne nti ne ho bo afu me sɛ mama bi ho ayɛ ne dene homeda?

english_verse: Don't judge by appearances. Judge by what is right.
translation_verse: Hommfa nea hohu mmmu atɛne, na hommu atɛntenenee.” Kristo he di?

english_verse: Some of the people from Jerusalem were saying, “Isn't this the man they want to kill?
translation_verse: Afei Yerusalemfoɔ he binom kaa sɛ, “Ɔnyɛ beema yi ne bɛɛpɛ no aku no yi?

english_verse: Yet here he is, speaking for everyone to hear. And no one is arguing with him. Do you suppose the authorities know he is the Messiah?
translation_verse: Nso, hwɛ ɔɔkasa peteem, nso bɛnka hwee nkyerɛ no. Nti mpanyimfoɔ he ahu sɛ ɔno ne Kristo he anaa?

english_verse: But how could that be? No one knows where the Messiah will come from, but we know where this man comes from.”
translation_verse: Yei deɛ yenim nea ofiri, nso Kristo he deɛ sɛ ɔba a, biaa onhu baabi a ofiri.”

english_verse: As Jesus was teaching in the temple, he shouted, “Do you really think you know me and where I came from? I didn't come on my own! The one who sent me is truthful, and you don't know him.
translation_verse: Nti berɛ a Yesu ɔɔkyerɛkyerɛ asɔredane mu hɔ he, ɔteaam sɛ, “Honim me, na honim baabi a mefiri nso. Na ɔnyɛ m’ankasa mepɛ so na me baeɛ, na nea ɔsomaa me he yɛ nɔkwafoɔ a honnim no.

english_verse: But I know the one who sent me, because I came from him.”
translation_verse: Menim no efiri sɛ, mefiri ne nkyɛne ne baeɛ, na ɔno ne somaa me.”

english_verse: Some of the people wanted to arrest Jesus right then. But no one even laid a hand on him, because his time had not yet come.
translation_verse: Nti bɛhwehwɛɛ kwane a bɛbɛfa so akye no nso biaa amfa ne nsa anka no, efiri sɛ na berɛ he nnuruiɛ.

english_verse: A lot of people in the crowd put their faith in him and said, “When the Messiah comes, he surely won't perform more miracles than this man has done!” Officers Sent To Arrest Jesus
translation_verse: Na nnipakuo he mu pii gyee no diiɛ, na bebisaa sɛ, “Sɛ Kristo he ba a, ɔbɛyɔ nsɛnkyerɛnne asene nea nipa yi ayɔ yi anaa?” Bɛsomaa Mpanyimfoɔ sɛ bɛnkɔ kye Yesu

english_verse: When the Pharisees heard the crowd arguing about Jesus, they got together with the chief priests and sent some temple police to arrest him.
translation_verse: Farisifoɔ he tee sɛ nnipakuo he oonwinwii keka yeinom fa ne ho, na Farisifoɔ ne asɔfoɔ mpanyimfoɔ he somaa asɔrefie awɛmfoɔ sɛ bɛnkɔ kye no.

english_verse: But Jesus told them, “I will be with you a little while longer, and then I will return to the one who sent me.
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Meya ho nkyɛne berɛ tiaa bi, na meebɛkɔ nea ɔsomaa me he nkyɛne.

english_verse: You will look for me, but you won't find me. You cannot go where I am going.”
translation_verse: Hobɛhwehwɛ me, na honhu me, na nea meya he, hontumi mma hɔ.”

english_verse: The people asked each other, “Where can he go to keep us from finding him? Is he going to some foreign country where our people live? Is he going there to teach the Greeks?
translation_verse: Yudafoɔ he bisaa bɛ ho bɛ ho sɛ, “Hefa ne nipa yi pɛ sɛ ɔkɔ a nti yenhu no? Ɔpɛ sɛ ɔkɔ Yudafoɔ a bɛahwete Helafoɔ mu he nkyɛne na ɔkɔkyerɛkyerɛ Helafoɔ he anaa?

english_verse: What did he mean by saying that we will look for him, but won't find him? Why can't we go where he is going?” Streams of Life-Giving Water
translation_verse: Abɛ asɛm ne ɔɔka yi sɛ, ‘Hobɛhwehwɛ me, na honhu me, na nea meya he, hontumi mma hɔ’?” Nkwa Nsuo

english_verse: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, “If you are thirsty, come to me and drink!
translation_verse: Da a otwa toɔ afahyɛ he mu he, Yesu gyinaa hɔ teaam sɛ, “Nea sukɔm de no he mmra me nkyɛne mɛnom nsuo.

english_verse: Have faith in me, and you will have life-giving water flowing from deep inside you, just as the Scriptures say.”
translation_verse: Sɛnea twerɛsɛm he aka he, ‘Nea ɔgye me di he, nkwa asubɔntene bɛtene afiri n’akomam.’ ”

english_verse: Jesus was talking about the Holy Spirit, who would be given to everyone that had faith in him. The Spirit had not yet been given to anyone, since Jesus had not yet been given his full glory. The People Take Sides
translation_verse: Nso ɔkaa yei faa Honhom a na bɛ a bɛbɛgye no adi he benya he ho, saa berɛ he na Honhom he nnya mmaeɛ, efiri sɛ na bennya nhyɛɛ Yesu animuonyam. Nnipa he mu ntete mu

english_verse: When the crowd heard Jesus say this, some of them said, “He must be the Prophet!”
translation_verse: Na bɛtee saa nsɛm yi he, nnipa bi firi nnipakuo he mu kaa sɛ, “Diyifoɔ he di ampa.”

english_verse: Others said, “He is the Messiah!” Others even said, “Can the Messiah come from Galilee?
translation_verse: Na binom nso kaa sɛ, “Kristo he di.” Na binom nso bisaa sɛ, “Kristo he befiri Galilea anaa?”

english_verse: The Scriptures say that the Messiah will come from the family of King David. Doesn't this mean that he will be born in David's hometown of Bethlehem?”
translation_verse: Twerɛsɛm he nkaa sɛ Kristo he befiri Dawid ase ne Betlehem, akuraa a na Dawid te mu he ne bɛba?”

english_verse: The people started taking sides against each other because of Jesus.
translation_verse: Afei Yesu nti nnipakuo he mu kyekyɛeɛ.

english_verse: Some of them wanted to arrest him, but no one laid a hand on him. The Leaders Refuse To Have Faith in Jesus
translation_verse: Na bemu binom pɛɛ sɛ bɛkye no, nso biaa amfa ne nsa anka no. Mpanyimfoɔ he Annyeanie

english_verse: When the temple police returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, they were asked, “Why didn't you bring Jesus here?”
translation_verse: Nti awɛmfoɔ he sane kɔɔ asɔfoɔ mpanyimfoɔ ne Farisifoɔ he hɔ, na bebisaa awɛmfoɔ he sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne hoankye no?”

english_verse: They answered, “No one has ever spoken like this man!”
translation_verse: Awɛmfoɔ he buaa sɛ, “Nipa biaa nkasaa sɛ beema yi da!”

english_verse: The Pharisees said to them, “Have you also been fooled?
translation_verse: Afei Farisifoɔ he kaa sɛ, “Wadaadaa ho nso anaa?

english_verse: Not one of the chief priests or the Pharisees has faith in him.
translation_verse: Mpanyimfoɔ he bi anaa Farisifoɔ he bi agye no atom anaa?

english_verse: And these people who don't know the Law are under God's curse anyway.”
translation_verse: Na nnipakuo yi a bennim mmara he—bɛadome bɛ!”

english_verse: Nicodemus was there at the time. He was a member of the council, and was the same one who had earlier come to see Jesus. He said,
translation_verse: Nikodemo a ɔbaa Yesu nkyɛne dabi he a na ɔyɛ bemu baako bisaa sɛ,

english_verse: “Our Law doesn't let us condemn people before we hear what they have to say. We cannot judge them before we know what they have done.”
translation_verse: “Sɛ wonnii kane ntiee bi anum asɛm nhuu nea ɔɔyɔ a, nti yɛ mmara he tumi bu no atɛne?”

english_verse: Then they said, “Nicodemus, you must be from Galilee! Read the Scriptures, and you will find that no prophet is to come from Galilee.” A Woman Caught in Sin
translation_verse: Bebuaa sɛ, “Womfiri Galilea, anaa wofiri hɔ? Hwehwɛ mu hwɛ na wobehu sɛ diyifoɔ biaa nni hɔ a obefiri Galilea asɔre.” Baa he a bɛkyee no adwammammɔ mu he

english_verse: Everyone else went home,
translation_verse: [Nti biaa kɔɔ fie.

english_verse: but Jesus walked out to the Mount of Olives.
translation_verse: Na Yesu kɔɔ Ngo Bepɔ he so.

english_verse: Then early the next morning he went to the temple. The people came to him, and he sat down and started teaching them.
translation_verse: Na anɔpa tutuutu he ɔsane baa asɔredane he mu. Na mane mũ he nyinaa baa ne nkyɛne na ɔtenaa ase kyerɛkyerɛ bɛ.

english_verse: Then they said, “Teacher, this woman was caught sleeping with a man who isn't her husband.
translation_verse: Na bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ, bɛakye baa yi adwamammɔ so pɛɛ.

english_verse: The Law of Moses teaches that a woman like this should be stoned to death! What do you say?”
translation_verse: Mose hyɛɛ yɛ mmara he mu sɛ, yensi mmaa a bɛ te saa he aboɔ. Na wo nso ɛɛne ne wooka?”

english_verse: They asked Jesus this question, because they wanted to test him and bring some charge against him. But Jesus simply bent over and started writing on the ground with his finger.
translation_verse: Bɛkaa yei de sɔɔ no hwɛeɛ, sɛ ɔbɛyɛ na bebenya soboɔ bi abɔ no. Na Yesu bɔɔ ne mu ase de ne nsateaa twerɛtwerɛɛ fam.

english_verse: They kept on asking Jesus about the woman. Finally, he stood up and said, “If any of you have never sinned, then go ahead and throw the first stone at her!”
translation_verse: Na bɛkɔɔ soaa bisaa no he, ɔmaa ne mu so ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Ho mu nea ɔnyɔɔ bɔne da he nni kane nto boɔ mmɔ no.”

english_verse: Once again he bent over and began writing on the ground.
translation_verse: Na ɔsane bɔɔ ne mu ase bio de ne nsateaa twerɛtwerɛɛ fam.

english_verse: The people left one by one, beginning with the oldest. Finally, Jesus and the woman were there alone.
translation_verse: Na bɛtee yei he, , nti befirii adi mmaako mmaako firi mpanyimfoɔ so. Na ɔkaa Yesu ne baa he nkoaa a ogyina mfinfini hɔ.

english_verse: Jesus stood up and asked her, “Where is everyone? Isn't there anyone left to accuse you?”
translation_verse: Yesu maa ne mu so bisaa baa he sɛ, “Baa, bɛya he? Biaa ammu wo fɔ?”

english_verse: “No sir,” the woman answered. Then Jesus told her, “I am not going to accuse you either. You may go now, but don't sin anymore.” Jesus Is the Light for the World
translation_verse: Na baa he kaa sɛ, “Me wura, daabi.” Na Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Me nso memmu wo fɔ, kɔ na nkɔyɔ bɔne bio.”] Yesu ne wiase hann

english_verse: Once again Jesus spoke to the people. This time he said, “I am the light for the world! Follow me, and you won't be walking in the dark. You will have the light that gives life.”
translation_verse: Na Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ bio sɛ, “Mene wiase hann. Nea odi m’akyi he nnante sum mu da, na obenya nkwa hann he.”

english_verse: The Pharisees objected, “You are the only one speaking for yourself, and what you say isn't true!”
translation_verse: Afei Farisifoɔ he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Wo deɛ wodi w’ankasa wo ho adanseɛ; w’adanseɛ he nyɛ nɔkorɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: Even if I do speak for myself, what I say is true! I know where I came from and where I am going. But you don't know where I am from or where I am going.
translation_verse: Yesu buaa sɛ, “Mpo sɛ medi meaa me ho adanseɛ a, m’adanseɛ he yɛ nɔkorɛ, efiri sɛ, menim baabi a mefiri baeɛ ne baabi a meekɔ. Na ho deɛ honnim baabi a mefiri ne baabi a meekɔ.

english_verse: You judge in the same way that everyone else does, but I don't judge anyone.
translation_verse: Ho deɛ hobu atɛne honam mu, na me deɛ memmu biaa atɛne.

english_verse: If I did judge, I would judge fairly, because I would not be doing it alone. The Father who sent me is here with me.
translation_verse: Na sɛ mebu atɛne mpo a, m’atemmuo he yɛ nɔkorɛ efiri sɛ nyɛ me nkoaa ne mebu atɛne, na mmom me ne Agya a ɔsomaa me he ne bu atɛne.

english_verse: Your Law requires two witnesses to prove that something is true.
translation_verse: Bɛatwerɛ no ho mmara he mu sɛ, “Nnipa mmienu adanseɛ yɛ nɔkorɛ.”

english_verse: I am one of my witnesses, and the Father who sent me is the other one.
translation_verse: Mene medi me ho adanseɛ, na Agya a ɔsomaa me he nso di me ho adanseɛ.”

english_verse: “Where is your Father?” they asked. “You don't know me or my Father!” Jesus answered. “If you knew me, you would know my Father.”
translation_verse: Na bebisaa no sɛ, “W’Agya ya he?” Yesu buaa sɛ, “Honnim me, na honnim m’Agya nso; sɛ honim me a, nka honim m’Agya nso.”

english_verse: Jesus said this while he was still teaching in the place where the temple treasures were stored. But no one arrested him, because his time had not yet come. You Cannot Go Where I Am Going
translation_verse: Ɔkaa nsɛm yi berɛ a na ɔɔma nkyerɛkyerɛ ya asɔrefie sikakorabea hɔ; na biaa ankye no, efiri sɛ na ne mmerɛ nya nnuruiɛ. Yesu hyɛɛ ne Wuo ho Nkɔm

english_verse: Jesus also told them, “I am going away, and you will look for me. But you cannot go where I am going, and you will die with your sins unforgiven.”
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ bio sɛ, “Me deɛ, meekɔ, na hobɛhwehwɛ me, na hobewu ho bɔne mu; na baabi a meekɔ he, ho deɛ, hontumi mma hɔ.”

english_verse: The people asked, “Does he intend to kill himself? Is that what he means by saying we cannot go where he is going?”
translation_verse: Nti Yudafoɔ he kaa sɛ, “Oobeku ne ho anaa? Nti na ɔdɛ, ‘Baabi a meekɔ he, honntumi mma hɔ yi’?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “You are from below, but I am from above. You belong to this world, but I don't.
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Ho deɛ hofiri fam, na me deɛ, mefiri soro. Hofiri wiase; na me deɛ, memmfiri wiase yi mu.

english_verse: This is why I said you will die with your sins unforgiven. If you don't have faith in me for who I am, you will die, and your sins will not be forgiven.”
translation_verse: Meka kyerɛɛ ho sɛ hobewu ho bɔne mu, na sɛ hoannye anni sɛ mene no a, hobewu ho bɔne mu.”

english_verse: “Who are you?” they asked Jesus. Jesus answered, “I am exactly who I told you at the beginning.
translation_verse: Bebisaa no sɛ, “Hwane ne wo”? Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Mene nipa korɔ a maka sɛ meyɛ ofiri me dwumadie ahyɛaseɛ he.

english_verse: There is a lot more I could say to condemn you. But the one who sent me is truthful, and I tell the people of this world only what I have heard from him.”
translation_verse: Mewɔ ho ho nsɛm pii a mɛka, na mabu atɛne, na nea ɔsomaa me he yɛ nɔkwafoɔ. Na nea mate afiri ne hɔ ne meka kyerɛ wiase.”

english_verse: No one understood that Jesus was talking to them about the Father.
translation_verse: Bɛante aseɛ sɛ Agya he ho asɛm ne ɔɔka kyerɛ bɛ.

english_verse: Jesus went on to say, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, you will know who I am. You will also know that I don't do anything on my own. I say only what my Father taught me.
translation_verse: Nti Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Sɛ honya ma nipa ba he so a, saa berɛ he ne hobehu sɛ mene no, na memmɔ m’ankasa me tim nyɔ bibiaa, na nea Agya he kyerɛkyerɛ me he, ɔno ne meka.

english_verse: The one who sent me is with me. I always do what pleases him, and he will never leave me.”
translation_verse: Na nea ɔsomaa me he ka me ho, na onnyae me nko, efiri sɛ meyɔ nea ɔsɔ n’ani daa.”

english_verse: After Jesus said this, many of the people put their faith in him. The Truth Will Set You Free
translation_verse: Na ɔɔka saa nsɛm yi he, nnipa bebree gyee no diiɛ. Asuafoɔ kann

english_verse: Jesus told the people who had faith in him, “If you keep on obeying what I have said, you truly are my disciples.
translation_verse: Na afei Yesu ka kyerɛɛ Yudafoɔ a bɛagye adi he sɛ, “Sɛ hotena m’asɛm mu a, hoyɛ m’asuafoɔ ampa;

english_verse: You will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
translation_verse: na hobehu nɔkorɛ he, na nɔkorɛ he ama ho ayɛ adehyeɛ.”

english_verse: They answered, “We are Abraham's children! We have never been anyone's slaves. How can you say we will be set free?”
translation_verse: Bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Yɛ yɛ Abraham asefoɔ, na yɛ nyɛɛ bi nkoa da. Ɛɛne nti ne wooka sɛ, ‘Hobenya adehyeɛ’?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: I tell you for certain that anyone who sins is a slave of sin!
translation_verse: Yesu buaa bɛ sɛ, “Meeka akyerɛ ho nɔkorem sɛ, biaa a ɔyɔ bɔne he yɛ bɔne akoa.

english_verse: And slaves don't stay in the family forever, though the Son will always remain in the family.
translation_verse: Afei nso akoa ntena fie daa, na ba deɛ ɔtena fie hɔ daa.

english_verse: If the Son gives you freedom, you are free!
translation_verse: Nti sɛ Ba he ma ho fahodie a hobɛyɛ adehyeɛ ampa.

english_verse: I know that you are from Abraham's family. Yet you want to kill me, because my message isn't really in your hearts.
translation_verse: Menim sɛ hoyɛ Abraham asefoɔ, nso hopɛ sɛ hoku me, efiri sɛ m’asɛm nsene ho mu.

english_verse: I am telling you what my Father has shown me, just as you are doing what your father has taught you. Your Father Is the Devil
translation_verse: Nea mahu no Agya he nkyɛne ne meka, na ho deɛ honyɔ nea hoate firi Agya he nkyɛne.” Yesu ne Abraham

english_verse: The people said to Jesus, “Abraham is our father!” Jesus replied, “If you were Abraham's children, you would do what Abraham did.
translation_verse: Bebuaa no sɛ, “Yɛ Agya ne Abraham.” Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Sɛ ho Agya ne Abraham a, nka hobɛyɔ nea Abraham yɔeɛ,

english_verse: Instead, you want to kill me for telling you the truth that God gave me. Abraham never did anything like that.
translation_verse: nso seesei hopɛ sɛ hoku me, nipa a maaka nɔkorɛ a mete firi Agya he nkyɛne akyerɛ ho he. Nso Abraham deɛ wanyɔ saa adeɛ yi.

english_verse: But you are doing exactly what your father does.” “Don't accuse us of having someone else as our father!” they said. “We just have one father, and he is God.”
translation_verse: Hoyɔ nea ho agya yɔ ampa.” Na bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Bɛanwo yɛ adwamammɔ mu, yɛwɔ Agya baako, ɔno ne Nyankopɔn.”

english_verse: Jesus answered: If God were your Father, you would love me, because I came from God and now I am here. He sent me. I did not come on my own.
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Sɛ Nyankopɔn yɛ ho Agya a nka hobɛdɔ me, efiri sɛ mefiri Nyankopɔn hɔ ne baeɛ. Ɔnyɛ mene mede mepɛ baeɛ na mmom ɔno ne somaa me.

english_verse: Why can't you understand what I am talking about? Can't you stand to hear what I am saying?
translation_verse: Mmɛ nti ne ho nte nea meka he ase yi? Efiri sɛ hontumi ntie m’asɛm.

english_verse: Your father is the devil, and you do exactly what he wants. He has always been a murderer and a liar. There is nothing truthful about him. He speaks on his own, and everything he says is a lie. Not only is he a liar himself, but he is also the father of all lies.
translation_verse: Hofiri ho agya bronsam hɔ, na hopɛ sɛ hoyɔ ho Agya he apɛdeɛ. Ɔyɛ wudini firi mfitiaseɛ, na wannyina nɔkorɛ he mu efiri sɛ nɔkorɛ nni ne mu. Sɛ odi torɔ a, ofiri ɔno ankasa ne mu ne ba, efiri sɛ ɔyɛ torɔfoɔ ne atorɔ agya.

english_verse: Everything I have told you is true, and you still refuse to have faith in me.
translation_verse: Na esiane sɛ mekaa nɔkorɛ kyerɛ ho nti, honnye me nni.

english_verse: Can any of you accuse me of sin? If you cannot, why won't you have faith in me? After all, I am telling you the truth.
translation_verse: Ho mu hwane ne bu me bɔne bi ho atɛne? Sɛ meka nɔkorɛ a, mmɛ nti ne honnye me nni?

english_verse: Anyone who belongs to God will listen to his message. But you refuse to listen, because you don't belong to God. Jesus and Abraham
translation_verse: Nea ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn deɛ biaa tie Nyankopɔn asɛm. Nea nti a hontie ne sɛ, ho nyɛ Nyankopɔn deɛ.”

english_verse: The people told Jesus, “We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and that you have a demon in you!”
translation_verse: Yudafoɔ he buaa no sɛ, “Sɛ yɛka sɛ woyɛ Samariani na wowɔ honhommɔne a, ɔnyɛ nɔkorɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I don't have a demon in me. I honor my Father, and you refuse to honor me.
translation_verse: Yesu buaa sɛ, “Menni honhommɔne, na mmom medi m’Agya ni, na ho deɛ honni me ni.

english_verse: years old!” they said. “How could you have seen Abraham?”
translation_verse: Nso menhwehwɛ animuonyam mma me ho, bi ya hɔ a ɔhwehwɛ na obu atɛne.

english_verse: I tell you for certain that if you obey my words, you will never die.”
translation_verse: Meeka nɔkwasɛm paa akyerɛ ho sɛ, biaa a obedi me nsɛm so he, onwu da.”

english_verse: Then the people said, “Now we are sure that you have a demon. Abraham is dead, and so are the prophets. How can you say that no one who obeys your words will ever die?
translation_verse: Yudafoɔ he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Afei deɛ yɛahu sɛ wowɔ honhommɔne. Abraham wuiɛ, na adiyifoɔ he nso wuiɛ. Nso wo dɛ, ‘Biaa a obedi me nsɛm so he, onwu da.’

english_verse: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died, and so did the prophets. Who do you think you are?”
translation_verse: Wo so kyɛne yɛ agya Abraham a owuiɛ he anaa? Adiyifoɔ he nso wuiɛ. Afei wo dɛ wo ne hwane?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “If I honored myself, it would mean nothing. My Father is the one who honors me. You claim that he is your God,
translation_verse: Yesu buaa sɛ, “Sɛ m’ankasa mehyɛ me ho animuonyam a, m’animuonyam he nyɛ hwee. M’Agya a hoka sɛ ɔyɛ ho Nyankopɔn he ne hyɛ me animuonyam

english_verse: even though you don't really know him. If I said I didn't know him, I would be a liar, just like all of you. But I know him, and I do what he says.
translation_verse: nso honnim no. Na me deɛ menim no. Na sɛ meka sɛ mennim no a, na meyɛ torɔfoɔ te sɛ ho. Menim no na medi n’asɛm so.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that even before Abraham was, I was, and I am.”
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Meeka akyerɛ ho nɔkorem paa sɛ, Abraham nnya mmaeɛ he, ne meya hɔ.”

english_verse: The people picked up stones to kill Jesus, but he hid and left the temple.
translation_verse: Yei nti bɛsesaa aboɔ sɛ bɛɛbɛtoto abɔ no, nso Yesu de ne ho sieeɛ, na ofirii asɔrefie hɔ kɔeɛ.

english_verse: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who had been blind since birth.
translation_verse: Na afei Yesu ootwam baabi he, ohuu beema bi a bɛwoo no he na n’ani afira.

english_verse: Jesus' disciples asked, “Teacher, why was this man born blind? Was it because he or his parents sinned?”
translation_verse: N’asuafoɔ he bisaa no sɛ, “Kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ, hwane ne yɔɔ bɔne, beema yi anaa n’awofoɔ, sɛ bɛwoo no a na n’ani afira yi?”

english_verse: “No, it wasn't!” Jesus answered. “But because of his blindness, you will see God work a miracle for him.
translation_verse: Yesu buaa sɛ, “Ɔnyɛ beema yi anaa n’awofoɔ ne yɔɔ bɔne, na mmom bɛwoo no anifra mu senea ɔbɛyɛ a Nyankopɔn nnwuma bɛda adi ne mu.

english_verse: As long as it is day, we must do what the one who sent me wants me to do. When night comes, no one can work.
translation_verse: Adekyeɛ ya hɔ yi ɔsɛ sɛ yɛyɔ nea ɔsomaa yɛ he nnwuma; anadwo ɔɔba a biaa ntumi nnyɔ adwuma.

english_verse: While I am in the world, I am the light for the world.”
translation_verse: Berɛ a meya wiase yi, mene wiase hann.”

english_verse: After Jesus said this, he spit on the ground. He made some mud and smeared it on the man's eyes.
translation_verse: Ɔkaa saa he, ɔtoo ntasuo too fam, na ɔde ntasuo he fraa nnɛteɛ, na ɔde nnɛteɛ he faa nifirani he ani so.

english_verse: Then he said, “Go wash off the mud in Siloam Pool.” The man went and washed in Siloam, which means “One Who Is Sent.” When he had washed off the mud, he could see.
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Kɔhohoro so ya tadeɛ Siloa mu.” Na ɔkɔhohoroo so, na ɔbaeɛ he na ohu adeɛ.

english_verse: The man's neighbors and the people who had seen him begging wondered if he really could be the same man.
translation_verse: Ne mpɔtamufoɔ ne bɛ a na benim no sɛ na ɔyɛ srɛsrɛfoɔ he kaa sɛ, “Ɔnyɛ beema a na ɔte hɔ srɛsrɛ adeɛ he di?”

english_verse: Some of them said he was the same beggar, while others said he only looked like him. But he told them, “I am that man.”
translation_verse: Afei binom kaa sɛ, “Ɔyɛ ɔno a.” Na binom nso kaa sɛ, “Ɔsɛ no.” Na ɔnoaa deɛ, ɔkaa sɛ, “Ɔyɛ me a.”

english_verse: “Then how can you see?” they asked.
translation_verse: Na bebisaa no sɛ: Ɔyɛɛ dɛ ne w’ani abie yi?

english_verse: He answered, “Someone named Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. He told me to go and wash it off in Siloam Pool. When I did, I could see.”
translation_verse: Obuaa sɛ, “Nipa bi a bɛfrɛ no Yesu ne potɔɔ nnɛteɛ de sraa m’ani so, na ɔka kyerɛ me sɛ, ‘Kɔ tadeɛ Siloa mu kɔ hohoro so.’ Na mekɔ hohoroo so he, mehuu adeɛ.”

english_verse: “Where is he now?” they asked. “I don't know,” he answered. The Pharisees Try To Find Out What Happened
translation_verse: Na bebisaa no sɛ, “Ɔya he?” Na ɔkaa sɛ, “Mennim.” Farisifoɔ he hwehwɛɛ Ayaresa he mu

english_verse: The day when Jesus made the mud and healed the man was a Sabbath. So the people took the man to the Pharisees.
translation_verse: Na nea na ɔteɛ ne sɛ, na saa da a Yesu potɔɔ nnɛteɛ de biee n’ani he yɛ homeda.

english_verse: They asked him how he was able to see, and he answered, “Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. Then after I washed it off, I could see.”
translation_verse: Na Farisifoɔ he nso bisaa no bio sɛnea ɔyɔeɛ a ohuu adeɛ. Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Nnɛteɛ ne ɔde sraa m’ani so a, mekɔ hohoroo so a, mehuu adeɛ yi.”

english_verse: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man Jesus doesn't come from God. If he did, he would not break the law of the Sabbath.” Others asked, “How could someone who is a sinner work such a miracle?” Since the Pharisees could not agree among themselves,
translation_verse: Na Farisifoɔ he mu bi kaa sɛ, “Nipa yi mfiri Nyankopɔn nkyɛne, efiri sɛ onni homeda.” Nso binom bisaa sɛ, “Ɔbɛyɛ dɛ na nipa debɔneyɛni atumi ayɔ nsɛnkyerɛnne a ɔte sɛ yi?” Afei mpaepaemu baa bɛ mu.

english_verse: they asked the man, “What do you say about this one who healed your eyes?” “He is a prophet!” the man told them.
translation_verse: Nti bɛsane bisaa nea na ɔyɛ nifirani he sɛ, “Wone wabie w’ani, na wo nso mmɛ ne wowɔ ka fa ne ho?” Ɔkaa sɛ, “Ɔyɛ diyifoɔ.”

english_verse: But the Jewish leaders would not believe that the man had once been blind. They sent for his parents
translation_verse: Nso na Yudafoɔ he nnye ne ho nsɛm he nni, sɛ na nka ɔyɛ nifirani na seesei ohu adeɛ, kosii sɛ bɛfrɛɛ deɛ ohuu adeɛ he awofoɔ,

english_verse: and asked them, “Is this the son that you said was born blind? How can he now see?”
translation_verse: na bebisaa bɛ sɛ, “Ho ba a hoka sɛ howoo no nifirani he di? Afei ɔyɛɛ dɛ ne ohuu adeɛ yi?”

english_verse: The man's parents answered, “We are certain that he is our son, and we know that he was born blind.
translation_verse: N’awofoɔ he ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Yenim sɛ yɛba di, na yɛwoo no nifirani;

english_verse: But we don't know how he got his sight or who gave it to him. Ask him! He is old enough to speak for himself.”
translation_verse: na sɛnea ɔyɔeɛ a ohuu adeɛ yi deɛ yennim. Afei nso nipa korɔ a obiee n’ani he, yɛdeɛ, yennim no; waanyini, hommisa no, na ɔno ankasa betumi ayi ano.”

english_verse: The leaders called the man back and said, “Swear by God to tell the truth! We know that Jesus is a sinner.”
translation_verse: Na bɛfrɛɛ nipa a na nka ɔyɛ nifirani he ne mprenu so ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Fa animuonyam ma Nyankopɔn! Yɛ deɛ, yenim sɛ nipa yi yɛ debɔnyɛni.”

english_verse: The man replied, “I don't know if he is a sinner or not. All I know is that I used to be blind, but now I can see!”
translation_verse: Na ɔkaa sɛ, “Sɛ ɔyɛ debɔnyɛni a, mennim; adeɛ baako pɛ ne menim, na nka meyɛ nifirani, na afei deɛ maahu adeɛ.”

english_verse: “What did he do to you?” they asked. “How did he heal your eyes?”
translation_verse: Na bebisaa no sɛ: Mmɛ ne ɔyɔ maa wo? Obiee w’ani he sɛ?

english_verse: The man answered, “I have already told you once, and you refused to listen. Why do you want me to tell you again? Do you also want to become his disciples?”
translation_verse: Ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Maaka akyerɛ ho dada, na hoantie? Ɛɛne ne hopɛ sɛ hote bio? Ho nso hopɛ sɛ hoyɛ n’asuafoɔ anaa?”

english_verse: The leaders insulted the man and said, “You are his follower! We are followers of Moses.
translation_verse: Bɛyeyaa no, na bɛkaa sɛ, “Wone woyɛ ne suani; na yɛ deɛ, yɛyɛ Mose asuafoɔ.”

english_verse: We are sure God spoke to Moses, but we don't even know where Jesus comes from.”
translation_verse: Yenim sɛ Nyankopɔn kasa kyerɛɛ Mose; na yei deɛ, yennim baabi a ofiri.

english_verse: “How strange!” the man replied. “He healed my eyes, and yet you don't know where he comes from.
translation_verse: Beema he buaa sɛ, “Yei ne ɔnwanwa he, sɛ honnim faako a ofiri, nso wabie m’ani.

english_verse: We know that God listens only to people who love and obey him. God doesn't listen to sinners.
translation_verse: Yenim sɛ Nyankopɔn ntie abɔnefoɔ, na otie bɛ a bɛsom no ne bedi n’apɛdeɛ so.

english_verse: And this is the first time in history anyone has ever given sight to someone born blind.
translation_verse: Ofiri tete de besi nnɛ yɛntee sɛ bi abie bi a bɛwoo no nifirani ani da.

english_verse: Jesus could not do anything unless he came from God.”
translation_verse: Sɛ beema yi mfiri Nyankopɔn hɔ a, nka ontumi nyɔ hwee.”

english_verse: The leaders told the man, “You have been a sinner since the day you were born! Do you think you can teach us anything?” Then they said, “You can never come back into any of our synagogues!”
translation_verse: Bebuaa no sɛ, “Wo a bɔne mu ne bɛwoo wo yi ne wookyerɛkyerɛ yɛ yi?” Na bɛpamoo no firi hɔ. Honhom mu Anifira

english_verse: When Jesus heard what had happened, he went and found the man. Then Jesus asked, “Do you have faith in the Son of Man?”
translation_verse: Yesu tee sɛ bɛapam no, na ohuu no he, obisaa no sɛ, “Wogye Nipa Ba he di?”

english_verse: He replied, “Sir, if you will tell me who he is, I will put my faith in him.”
translation_verse: Obuaa sɛ, “Owura, ɔno ne hwane? Ka kyerɛ me na mennye no nni.”

english_verse: “You have already seen him,” Jesus answered, “and right now he is talking with you.”
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Woahu no, na ɔnoaa ne ɔne wo ɔɔkasa yi.”

english_verse: The man said, “Lord, I put my faith in you!” Then he worshiped Jesus.
translation_verse: Ɔkaa sɛ, “Awurade, megye no di.” Na ɔsɔree no.

english_verse: Jesus told him, “I came to judge the people of this world. I am here to give sight to the blind and to make blind everyone who can see.”
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Atemmuo nti ne mebaa wiase, na bɛ a benhu adeɛ he ahu adeɛ, na bɛ a behu adeɛ he bɛadane anifirafoɔ.”

english_verse: When the Pharisees heard Jesus say this, they asked, “Are we blind?”
translation_verse: Na Farisifoɔ he mu bi a na bɛ ne no ya hɔ tee asɛm yi, na bebisaa no sɛ, “Yɛ nso yɛyɛ anifirafoɔ anaa?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “If you were blind, you would not be guilty. But now that you claim to see, you will keep on being guilty.”
translation_verse: Na Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Sɛ honyɛ anifirafoɔ a nka bɔne nni ho ho. Na afei ho dɛ, ‘Yehu adeɛ.’ Ho bɔne he aka hɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus said: I tell you for certain only thieves and robbers climb over the fence instead of going in through the gate to the sheep pen.
translation_verse: “Meeka akyerɛ ho nɔkorem paa sɛ, biaa a ɔmfa pono ano nkɔ nnwammuom na mmom ɔforo fa baabi foforɔ he, yɛ korɔmfoɔ ne dwotwafoɔ.

english_verse: But the gatekeeper opens the gate for the shepherd, and he goes in through it. The sheep know their shepherd's voice. He calls each of them by name and leads them out.
translation_verse: Pono ano nwɛmfoɔ he bie no, na nnwane he tie ne nne. Na ɔbɔ ne nnwane he dini frɛ bɛ, na ɔde bɛ firi adi.

english_verse: When he has led out all of his sheep, he walks in front of them, and they follow, because they know his voice.
translation_verse: Sɛ ɔde ne deɛ nyinaa firi adi a, odi bɛ anim ma bedi n’akyi efiri sɛ benim ne nne.

english_verse: The sheep will not follow strangers. They don't recognize a stranger's voice, and they run away.
