english_verse: One of the soldiers stuck his spear into Jesus' side, and blood and water came out.
translation_verse: Na asraafoɔ he mu baako de pea wɔɔ ne mfe, na mogyaa ne nsuo firii mu baeɛ amonom hɔaa.

english_verse: All this happened so that the Scriptures would come true, which say, “No bone of his body will be broken”
translation_verse: Yei siiɛ sɛ ɔbɛyɛ na twerɛsɛm he aba mu sɛ, “Bemmmu ne dompe biaa mu,”

english_verse: and “They will see the one in whose side they stuck a spear.” Jesus Is Buried (Matthew
translation_verse: Na bio twerɛsɛm foforɔ dɛ, “Bɛbɛhwɛ nea bɛde pea awɔ no he.” Yesu amu sie (Mateo

english_verse: The tomb was nearby, and since it was the time to prepare for the Sabbath, they were in a hurry to put Jesus' body there.
translation_verse: Na hɔ ne Yudafoɔ ahosiesie da he nti, bɛde Yesu kɔtoeɛ, efiri sɛ saa nna he a na bɛde boɔ ayɔ he na na ɔbɛne.

english_verse: Joseph from Arimathea was one of Jesus' disciples. He had kept it secret though, because he was afraid of the Jewish leaders. But now he asked Pilate to let him have Jesus' body. Pilate gave him permission, and Joseph took it down from the cross.
translation_verse: Na yeinom akyi he, Yosef a ofiri Arimatea a ɔyɛ Yesu suani nso Yudafoɔ ho hu nti, na ɔyɛ no kokoam he, bɛsrɛɛ Pilato sɛ ɔmma no mfa Yesu amu he nkɔ; na Pilato maa ne kwane na ɔbɛfa kɔeɛ.

english_verse: The two men wrapped the body in a linen cloth, together with the spices, which was how the Jewish people buried their dead.
translation_verse: Afei bɛfaa Yesu amu he, na bɛde nwera a aduhwam yam kyekyere ne ho, sɛnea Yudafoɔ sie bɛ amu he.

english_verse: Thomas replied, “You are my Lord and my God!”
translation_verse: Toma buaa no sɛ, “M’Awurade ne me Nyankopɔn!”

english_verse: On Sunday morning while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene went to the tomb and saw that the stone had been rolled away from the entrance.
translation_verse: Afei nnawɔtwe da a odi kane he, Maria Magdalene kɔɔ da he so anɔpatutuutu na ohuu sɛ boɔ he nna nna he ano.

english_verse: So the two of them went back to the other disciples. Jesus Appears to Mary Magdalene (Mark
translation_verse: Nti asuafoɔ he sane kɔɔ bɛ afie mu. Yesu yii ne ho adi kyerɛɛ Maria Magdalene

english_verse: The disciple who got there first then went into the tomb, and when he saw it, he believed.
translation_verse: Na afei suani foforɔ he a oduruu nna he ho kane he nso kɔɔ mu, na ohuuiɛ, na ɔgye diiɛ.

english_verse: Although Thomas the Twin was one of the twelve disciples, he wasn't with the others when Jesus appeared to them.
translation_verse: Na berɛ a Yesu baeɛ he na Toma, Yesu asuafoɔ dummienu he mu baako a bɛfrɛ no Ata he, nka ho.

english_verse: and saw two angels inside. They were dressed in white and were sitting where Jesus' body had been. One was at the head and the other was at the foot.
translation_verse: Na ohuu abɔfoɔ mmienu bi a bɛhyɛ ntaadeɛ fitaa a na bɛtete baabi a na Yesu amu da he; na baako te baabi a na Yesu amu he da he atifi na baako nso te anafoɔ.

english_verse: She ran to Simon Peter and to Jesus' favorite disciple and said, “They have taken the Lord from the tomb! We don't know where they have put him.”
translation_verse: Nti ɔde mmirika kɔɔ Petro ne suani foforɔ a Awurade pɛ n’asɛm he nkyɛne kɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Bɛafa Awurade afiri nna he mu, na yennim baabi a bɛde no akɔto.”

english_verse: Peter and the other disciple started for the tomb.
translation_verse: Na Petro ne suani foforɔ he firii adi baa nna he ho.

english_verse: They ran side by side, until the other disciple ran faster than Peter and got there first.
translation_verse: Na bɛ mmienu ne beetu mmirika boom nso suani baako he de mmirika tɛntɛ dii Petro anim, na oduruu nna he ho kane.

english_verse: He bent over and saw the strips of linen cloth lying inside the tomb, but he did not go in.
translation_verse: Na ɔbɔɔ ne mu ase hwɛɛ nna he mu, nso wankɔ mu.

english_verse: When Simon Peter got there, he went into the tomb and saw the strips of cloth.
translation_verse: Na Simon Petro dii n’akyi baeɛ, na ɔkɔɔ nna he mu na ohuu nwera he sɛ ogu hɔ,

english_verse: He also saw the piece of cloth that had been used to cover Jesus' face. It was rolled up and in a place by itself.
translation_verse: na afei ntoma a bɛde bɔɔ ne ti he nso bɛabobɔ gu nkyɛne baabi a ɔnka nwere he ho.

english_verse: Mary Magdalene stood crying outside the tomb. She was still weeping, when she stooped down
translation_verse: Maria deɛ, na ɔda soaa gyina da he nkyɛne baabi a oosu. Na oguso oosu he, ɔbɔɔ ne mu ase hwɛɛ da he mu.

english_verse: The angels asked Mary, “Why are you crying?” She answered, “They have taken away my Lord's body! I don't know where they have put him.”
translation_verse: Bebisaa no sɛ, “Baa, ɛɛne nti ne woosu yi?” Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Bɛafa m’Awurade amu kɔ na mennim baabi a a bɛde no akɔto.”

english_verse: Jesus asked her, “Why are you crying? Who are you looking for?” She thought he was the gardener and said, “Sir, if you have taken his body away, please tell me, so I can go and get him.”
translation_verse: Yesu bisaa no sɛ “Baa, Mmɛ nti ne woosu yi? Hwane ne woohwehwɛ no?” Maria deɛ na osusu sɛ ɔyɛ turom hɔ hwɛfoɔ nti ɔkaa sɛ, “Owura, sɛ wone wafa no afiri ha a, kyɛrɛ me baabi a wode no akɔto.”

english_verse: Jesus told her, “Don't hold on to me! I have not yet gone to the Father. But tell my disciples I am going to the one who is my Father and my God, as well as your Father and your God.”
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Mfa wo nsa nka me, efiri sɛ menya nkɔɔ soro, m’Agya nkyɛne. Na kɔ me nuanom nkyɛne kɔka kyerɛ bɛ sɛ, ‘Meekɔ soro, m’Agya ne ho Agya ne me Nyankopɔn ne ho Nyankopɔn nkyɛne.’ ”

english_verse: and showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they became very happy.
translation_verse: Na ɔkaa saa he, ɔde ne nsa ne ne mfem kyerɛɛ asuafoɔ he. Na asuafoɔ he ani gyee sɛ bɛahu Awurade.

english_verse: The disciples were afraid of the Jewish leaders, and on the evening of that same Sunday they locked themselves in a room. Suddenly, Jesus appeared in the middle of the group. He greeted them
translation_verse: Na saa da he yɛ nnawɔtwe he da a odi kane. Na nwunu dwoeɛ he, ewom sɛ na Yudafoɔ ho hu nti asuafoɔ he atoto apono mu baabi a na bɛya he deɛ, nso Yesu ba begyinaa bɛ mfimfini, na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Asomdwie nka ho!”

english_verse: After Jesus had greeted them again, he said, “I am sending you, just as the Father has sent me.”
translation_verse: Na Yesu sane ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Asomdwie nka ho! Sɛnea Agya he asoma me he, saa are ne me nso meesoma ho.”

english_verse: Then he breathed on them and said, “Receive the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Ɔkaa yei wieeɛ he, ɔhome guu bɛ so, kaa sɛ, “Honye Honhom Kronkron.

english_verse: If you forgive anyone's sins, they will be forgiven. But if you don't forgive their sins, they will not be forgiven.” Jesus and Thomas
translation_verse: Na biaa a hode ne bɔne bɛkyɛ no he, na bɛde akyɛ no dada. Na biaa a hoammfa ne bɔne akyɛ no he, bɛmmfa nkyɛɛ no.” Yesu ne Toma

english_verse: So they told him, “We have seen the Lord!” But Thomas said, “First, I must see the nail scars in his hands and touch them with my finger. I must put my hand where the spear went into his side. I won't believe unless I do this!”
translation_verse: Nti asuafoɔ a aka he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Yɛahu Awurade.” Nso ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Sɛ manhu ne nsam nnadewa twa na mamfa me nsa teaa anka dadewa he twa he a mennye nni.”

english_verse: A week later the disciples were together again. This time, Thomas was with them. Jesus came in while the doors were still locked and stood in the middle of the group. He greeted his disciples
translation_verse: Ne nnawɔtwe akyi he, na asuafoɔ he ya dane mu bio a na Toma nso ka ho. Ewom sɛ na bɛatoto apono he mu deɛ, nso Yesu ba begyinaa bɛ mfinfini, na ɔkaa sɛ, “Asomdwie nka ho!”

english_verse: and said to Thomas, “Put your finger here and look at my hands! Put your hand into my side. Stop doubting and have faith!”
translation_verse: Afei ɔka kyerɛɛ Toma sɛ, “Fa wo nsa tea ka ha na bɛhwɛ me nsam, na fa wo nsa bɛka me mfem, na nyɛ akyinnyegyefoɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus said, “Thomas, do you have faith because you have seen me? The people who have faith in me without seeing me are the ones who are really blessed!” Why John Wrote His Book
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Woahu me nti ne woagye adi? Nhyira ne bɛ a benhuiɛ nso bɛagye adi.” Nwoma yi Botaeɛ

english_verse: Jesus worked many other miracles for his disciples, and not all of them are written in this book.
translation_verse: Na afei nsɛnkyerɛnne pii nso ya hɔ a Yesu yɔɔ n’asuafoɔ he anim a bɛantwerɛ no nwoma yi mu.

english_verse: But these are written so that you will put your faith in Jesus as the Messiah and the Son of God. If you have faith in him, you will have true life.
translation_verse: Nso bɛatwerɛ yei ato hɔ na ama hoagye adi sɛ Yesu ne Kristo he, Nyankopɔn Ba, na hogye di a, hoanya nkwa ne dini mu.

english_verse: Jesus later appeared to his disciples along the shore of Lake Tiberias.
translation_verse: Yei akyi he, Yesu yii ne ho adi kyerɛɛ asuafoɔ he bio Tiberia po ho. Na oyii ne ho adi saa kwane yi so.

english_verse: Simon Peter, Thomas the Twin, Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, and the brothers James and John, were there, together with two other disciples.
translation_verse: Na Simon Petro, Toma a bɛfrɛ no Ata, Natanael a ofiri Kana a ɔya Galilea, Sebedeo mma mmeema he ne asuafoɔ he mu mmienu nso ya hɔ.

english_verse: Simon Peter said, “I'm going fishing!” The others said, “We will go with you.” They went out in their boat. But they didn't catch a thing that night.
translation_verse: Simon Petro ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Meekoyi nam.” Bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Yɛ ne wo bɛkɔ.” Bɛkɔtenaa kodoɔ he mu, na anadwo he nyinaa bɛanyi nam biaa.

english_verse: Early the next morning Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples did not realize who he was.
translation_verse: Na adeɛ ɔɔyɛ akye he, Yesu begyinaa po he ano, nso asuafoɔ he ahu sɛ ɔyɛ ɔno a.

english_verse: Jesus shouted, “Friends, have you caught anything?” “No!” they answered.
translation_verse: Yesu bisaa bɛ sɛ, “Mma nkumaa, hoanya nam?” Na bebuaa sɛ, “Daabi.”

english_verse: So he told them, “Let your net down on the right side of your boat, and you will catch some fish.” They did, and the net was so full of fish that they could not drag it up into the boat.
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛ bɛ sɛ, “Honto asau he ngu kodoɔ he nifa na hobenya bi.” Nti bɛtoo asau he na adwene dodoɔ a benyaeɛ nti na bentumi ntwe asau he.

english_verse: Jesus' favorite disciple told Peter, “It's the Lord!” When Simon heard it was the Lord, he put on the clothes he had taken off while he was working. Then he jumped into the water.
translation_verse: Na suani a Yesu dɔ no he ka kyerɛɛ Petro sɛ, “Ɔyɛ Awurade!” Petro tee sɛ ɔyɛ Awurade he, ɔde n’ataadeɛ kyekyeree ne ho, efiri sɛ na ɔda adagya, na ohuri kɔhwee po he mu.

english_verse: When the disciples got out of the boat, they saw some bread and a charcoal fire with fish on it.
translation_verse: Na bebeduru kokoɔ so he, behuu gyabidie a bɛasɔ a nam ne paanoo gu so.

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples, “Bring some of the fish you just caught.”
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Homfa adwene a hoayi he bi mmra.”

english_verse: Simon Peter got back into the boat and dragged the net to shore. In it were
translation_verse: Nti Simon Petro kɔtwee asau a adwene akɛseɛ ha aduonum mmeɛnsa ahyɛ no ma he baa kokoɔ so. Na ɔdɔɔso deɛ, nso asau he ante.

english_verse: Jesus said, “Come and eat!” But none of the disciples dared ask who he was. They knew he was the Lord.
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Hommra mmedidi.” Nso asuafoɔ he mu biaa antumi ammisa sɛ, “Woyɛ hwane?” Na benim sɛ ɔyɛ Awurade.

english_verse: Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave some of it to his disciples. He did the same with the fish.
translation_verse: Yesu ba bɛfaa paanoo he na ode maa bɛ, na ɔfaa nam he nso yɔɔ saa.

english_verse: This was the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from death. Jesus and Peter
translation_verse: Yei ne ne mprɛnsa so a Yesu nyane firii awufoɔ mu he, oyii ne ho adi kyerɛɛ asuafoɔ he. Yesu ne Petro

english_verse: When Jesus and his disciples had finished eating, he asked, “Simon son of John, do you love me more than the others do?” Simon Peter answered, “Yes, Lord, you know I do!” “Then feed my lambs,” Jesus said.
translation_verse: Na bedidi wieeɛ he, Yesu bisaa Simon Petro sɛ, “Simon, Yohane ba, wodɔ me kyɛne yeinom nyinaa?” Ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Ɛɛ, Awurade, wonim sɛ medɔ wo.” Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Ma me nnwane he aduane nni.”

english_verse: Jesus asked a second time, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” Peter answered, “Yes, Lord, you know I love you!” “Then take care of my sheep,” Jesus told him.
translation_verse: Obisaa no mprenu so sɛ, “Simon, Yohane ba, wodɔ me?” Ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Ɛɛ, Awurade, wonim sɛ medɔ wo.” Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Yɛne me nnwane.”

english_verse: Jesus asked a third time, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” Peter was hurt because Jesus had asked him three times if he loved him. So he told Jesus, “Lord, you know everything. You know I love you.” Jesus replied, “Feed my sheep.
translation_verse: Obisaa no mprɛnsa so sɛ, “Simon, Yohane ba, wodɔ me anaa?” Petro werɛ hoo sɛ oobisa no mprɛnsa so sɛ “wodɔ me?” Na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Awurade, wonim bibiaa, wonim sɛ medɔ wo.” Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Nyɛne me nnwane he.”

english_verse: I tell you for certain that when you were a young man, you dressed yourself and went wherever you wanted to go. But when you are old, you will hold out your hands. Then others will wrap your belt around you and lead you where you don't want to go.”
translation_verse: Nɔkwasɛm paa ne sɛ, woyɛ abranteɛ he na wode abɔsoɔ bɔ wo mu nante kɔ baabiaa a w’ankasa wopɛ. Nso sɛ wobɔ akɔkora a, wobɛtene wo nsa na bi de abɔsoɔ abɔ wo mu, na ɔde wo akɔ baabi a wompɛ sɛ wokɔ.

english_verse: Jesus said this to tell how Peter would die and bring honor to God. Then he said to Peter, “Follow me!” Jesus and His Favorite Disciple
translation_verse: Yei akyi he ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Di m’akyi.” Yesu ne suani a ɔdɔ no he

english_verse: Peter turned and saw Jesus' favorite disciple following them. He was the same one who had sat next to Jesus at the meal and had asked, “Lord, who is going to betray you?”
translation_verse: Petro danee ne ho he, ohuu suani a Yesu dɔ no he sɛ odi n’akyi, nea na ɔtweree Yesu kokom adidie he ase a obisaa sɛ, “Awurade, hwane ne beyi wo ama he.”

english_verse: When Peter saw this disciple, he asked Jesus, “Lord, what about him?”
translation_verse: Na Petro huu no he, obisaa Yesu sɛ, “Awurade, na yei nso ɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “What is it to you, if I want him to live until I return? You must follow me.”
translation_verse: Yesu ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Sɛ ɔyɛ mepɛ sɛ ɔtena hɔ kosi sɛ mɛba a, ɔfa wo ho bɛ?” Wo deɛ, kɔ soaa na di m’akyi.

english_verse: So the rumor spread among the other disciples that this disciple would not die. But Jesus did not say he would not die. He simply said, “What is it to you, if I want him to live until I return?”
translation_verse: Nti asɛm yi trɛɛ anuanom he mu sɛ saa suani he onwu; nso Yesu anka sɛ suani he onwu, na mmom ɔkaa sɛ, “Sɛ mepɛ sɛ ɔbɛtena hɔ akosi sɛ mɛba a, ɔfa wo ho bɛ?”

english_verse: This disciple is the one who told all of this. He wrote it, and we know he is telling the truth.
translation_verse: Yei ne suani a oodi nnoɔma yi ho adanseɛ na watwerɛ yeinom, na yenim sɛ adanseɛ a odi he yɛ nɔkorɛ.

english_verse: Jesus did many other things. If they were all written in books, I don't suppose there would be room enough in the whole world for all the books.
translation_verse: Na nnoɔma bebree ya hɔ nso a Yesu yɔeɛ a, sɛ bɛtwerɛɛ ne nyinaa a, nka mesusu sɛ, nwoma a bɛbɛtwerɛ he nkɔ wiase mu mpo.

english_verse: Theophilus, I first wrote to you about all that Jesus did and taught from the very first
translation_verse: Teofilo, nwoma a odi kane a metwerɛeɛ he fa nnoɔma a Yesu firii aseɛ yɔeɛ na ɔkyerɛkyerɛeɛ nyinaa

english_verse: until he was taken up to heaven. But before he was taken up, he gave orders to the apostles he had chosen with the help of the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: de besi da a ɔnam Honhom Kronkron so maa asomafoɔ a oyii bɛ he ahyɛdeɛ, na ɔno akyi he bɛfaa no kɔɔ soro.

english_verse: While he was still with them, he said: Don't leave Jerusalem yet. Wait here for the Father to give you the Holy Spirit, just as I told you he has promised to do.
translation_verse: Na ɔne bɛ hyiaeɛ he, ɔhyɛɛ bɛ sɛ: “Hommfiri Yerusalem nkɔ, na mmom hontwɛne nea Agya he ahyɛ ho bɔ a, hotee sɛ mekaa ho asɛm

english_verse: John baptized with water, but in a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit. Jesus Is Taken to Heaven
translation_verse: sɛ, Yohane deɛ, ɔde nsuo ne bɔɔ asu, na ho deɛ, nna yi mu, ɔnkyɛre bɛbɛbɔ ho asu Honhom Kronkron mu.”

english_verse: While the apostles were still with Jesus, they asked him, “Lord, are you now going to give Israel its own king again?”
translation_verse: Berɛ a behyiaeɛ he, bebisaa no sɛ, “Awurade, berɛ yi mu ne bɛbɛsane de ahennie he ama Israel anaa?”

english_verse: Jesus said to them, “You don't need to know the time of those events that only the Father controls.
translation_verse: Afei obuaa bɛ sɛ, “Ɔnyɛ ho asɛm sɛ hobehu berɛ anaa nna a Agya he ankansa de ne tumi ahyɛ ato hɔ,

english_verse: But the Holy Spirit will come upon you and give you power. Then you will tell everyone about me in Jerusalem, in all Judea, in Samaria, and everywhere in the world.”
translation_verse: na mmom sɛ Honhom Kronkron he nya ba ho so a, hobenya ahoɔdene, na hoayɛ m’adansefoɔ ya Yerusalem, Yudea ne Samaria de akɔpem asaase ano nohoa.”

english_verse: After Jesus had said this and while they were watching, he was taken up into a cloud. They could not see him,
translation_verse: Na ɔkaa yeinom wieeɛ a na bɛɛhwɛ no he, bɛmaa no so, na mununkum bɛkataa no so a afei na benhu no bio.

english_verse: but as he went up, they kept looking up into the sky. Suddenly two men dressed in white clothes were standing there beside them.
translation_verse: Na ɔɔkɔ na asuafoɔ he ɔɔhwɛ soro haa he, mpofiremuaa nnipa mmienu bi a bɛhyɛ ntaadeɛ fitaa begyinaa ne nkyɛne.

english_verse: They said, “Why are you men from Galilee standing here and looking up into the sky? Jesus has been taken to heaven. But he will come back in the same way you have seen him go.” Someone To Take the Place of Judas
translation_verse: Na bɛkaa sɛ, “Galilea mmeema, ɛɛne nti na hogyina ha ɔɔhwɛ soro haa yi? Yesu yi a bɛafa no ho nkyɛne kɔ soro yi, ɔbɛba saa, sɛnea hohu no sɛ ɔɔkɔ soro yi.”

english_verse: The Mount of Olives was about a kilometer from Jerusalem. The apostles who had gone there were Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas the son of James. After the apostles returned to the city, they went upstairs to the room where they had been staying.
translation_verse: Na beduruu hɔ he, bɛforo kɔɔ abrosane so kowuraa dane bi a na Petro ne Yohane ne Yakobo ne Andrea, Filipo ne Toma, Bartolomeo ne Mateo, Alfeo ba Yakobo ne Simon Selote ne Yakobo ba Yuda te mu he mu.

english_verse: The apostles often met together and prayed with a single purpose in mind. The women and Mary the mother of Jesus would meet with them, and so would his brothers.
translation_verse: Na bɛ nyinaa bɛde akoma korɔ tuu bɛ ho sii hɔ maa mpaebɔ, a na mmaa a bɛne Yesu nanteeɛ he ne Yesu maame Maria ne Yesu nuanom nso ka ho.

english_verse: Then Judas bought some land with the money he was given for doing that evil thing. He fell headfirst into the field. His body burst open, and all his insides came out.
translation_verse: (Saa beema yi de amumuyɔ ho akatua tɔɔ asaase, na ne wuo mu he, ɔte hweeɛ ma ne ti besii hɔ, na ne mu paeɛ ma ne yamuadeɛ nyinaa tu guiɛ.

english_verse: When the people of Jerusalem found out about this, they called the place Akeldama, which in the local language means “Field of Blood.”
translation_verse: Na bɛ a bɛte Yerusalem nyinaa teeɛ, na bɛfrɛɛ asaase he bɛaa bɛ kasa mu sɛ Hakeldama, asekyerɛ ne Mogyaa Asaase.)

english_verse: In the book of Psalms it says, “Leave his house empty, and don't let anyone live there.” It also says, “Let someone else have his job.”
translation_verse: “Na bɛatwerɛ no Nnwom nwoma he mu sɛ, ‘Ma ne tenabea nnane amanfõõ, na mma biaa ntena hɔ’; na ‘Ne hwɛfoɔ adwuma he, ma bi foforɔ nyɔ.’

english_verse: Two men were suggested: One of them was Joseph Barsabbas, known as Justus, and the other was Matthias.
translation_verse: Nti besusuu nnipa mmienu, Yosef a bɛfrɛ no Barsaba a bɛtoo ne dini Yusto, ɔne Matia.

english_verse: Then they all prayed, “Lord, you know what everyone is like! Show us the one you have chosen
translation_verse: Na bɛbɔɔ mpaeɛ sɛ, “Awurade, wonim nnipa nyinaa akomam, kyerɛ mmienu yi baako a woayi no

english_verse: to be an apostle and to serve in place of Judas, who got what he deserved.”
translation_verse: sɛ ɔmfa som yi, ne asomfodwuma a Yuda firii mu gya too hɔ, faa ɔno ankasa kwane so he!”

english_verse: They drew names, and Matthias was chosen to join the group of the eleven apostles.
translation_verse: Na bɛtwee bɛ so ntonto; na ntonto he bɔɔ Matia, na bɛde no kaa asomafoɔ dubaako he ho.

english_verse: On the day of Pentecost all the Lord's followers were together in one place.
translation_verse: Na Pentekoste da he duruiɛ he, na bɛ nyinaa ahyia faako.

english_verse: Suddenly there was a noise from heaven like the sound of a mighty wind! It filled the house where they were meeting.
translation_verse: Na mpofiremuaa, dede bi a ɔte sɛ mframa a ɔɔbɔ dendenndene firi soro baeɛ, na ɔbɛhyɛɛ fie a na bɛte mu he ma.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit took control of everyone, and they began speaking whatever languages the Spirit let them speak.
translation_verse: Na Honhom Kronkron bɛhyɛɛ bɛ nyinaa ma ma, na befiri aseɛ sɛ kaa kasa foforɔ, sɛnea Honhom he de maa bɛ sɛ bɛnka.

english_verse: Many religious Jews from every country in the world were living in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Afei na Yudafoɔ bi a bɛyɛ nyamesurofoɔ a befiri wiase amane nyinaa mu te Yerusalem.

english_verse: And when they heard this noise, a crowd gathered. But they were surprised, because they were hearing everything in their own languages.
translation_verse: Na dede he baeɛ he, nnipa pii behyiaeɛ, na bɛ adwene mu yɛɛ bɛ naa sɛ biaa tee nea bɛɛka he ne kurom kasa mu.

english_verse: They were excited and amazed, and said: Don't all these who are speaking come from Galilee?
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛɛ bɛ nyinaa ahodwiri ne nwanwa, na bebisaa sɛ, “Aa! Bɛ a bɛɛkasa yi nyinaa nyɛ Galileafoɔ?

english_verse: Then why do we hear them speaking our very own languages?
translation_verse: Na afei ɔyɛɛ dɛ ne yɛ nyinaa yɛte nea bɛɛka yi yɛ ankasa yɛ kurom kasa mu yi?

english_verse: Some of us are from Parthia, Media, and Elam. Others are from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia,
translation_verse: Ye mu binom firi Partia, Media, Elam, Mesopotamia, Yudea, Kapadokia, Ponto, Asia,

english_verse: Crete, and Arabia. Some of us were born Jews, and others of us have chosen to be Jews. Yet we all hear them using our own languages to tell the wonderful things God has done.
translation_verse: Kretefoɔ ne Arabiafoɔ; na yɛte no yɛ kasa mu sɛ bɛɛka Nyankopɔn tumi ne ne nnwuma akɛseɛ ho asɛm.”

english_verse: Everyone was excited and confused. Some of them even kept asking each other, “What does all this mean?”
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛɛ bɛ nyinaa ahodwiri ne nwanwa, na bebisabisaa bɛ ho bɛ ho sɛ, “Yei kyerɛ sɛ?”

english_verse: Others made fun of the Lord's followers and said, “They are drunk.” Peter Speaks to the Crowd
translation_verse: Na binom nso dii bɛ ho fɛw sɛ “Nsa dɔkɔdɔkɔ aboro bɛ.”

english_verse: Peter stood with the eleven apostles and spoke in a loud and clear voice to the crowd: Friends and everyone else living in Jerusalem, listen carefully to what I have to say!
translation_verse: Na Petro ne dubaako he sɔre gyinaeɛ; afei Petro teaam ka kyerɛɛ nnipa a bɛya hɔ he sɛ, “Yudafoɔ ne ho a hote Yerusalem nyinaa, honyɛ aso ntie nea meebɛka yi!

english_verse: You are wrong to think that these people are drunk. After all, it is only nine o'clock in the morning.
translation_verse: Nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, yeinom mmoro nsa, sɛnea hosusu he, na afei mpo ne abɔ anɔpa nnɔn nkron.

english_verse: But this is what God told the prophet Joel to say,
translation_verse: Na deɛ asi yi yɛ nea diyifoɔ Yoel kaa ho asɛm sɛ,

english_verse: “When the last days come, I will give my Spirit to everyone. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young men will see visions, and your old men will have dreams.
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn dɛ: ‘Nna a odi akyire he mu he, mehwie me Honhom agu honam nyinaa so, na ho mma mmeema ne ho mma mmaa ahyɛ nkɔm, na mmabunu anya anisoadehunu, na ho mpanyini asõsõ adaeɛ.

english_verse: In those days I will give my Spirit to my servants, both men and women, and they will prophesy.
translation_verse: Na me nkoa ne me mfena so ne, nna he mu, mehwie me Honhom agu, na bɛahyɛ nkɔm.

english_verse: “I will work miracles in the sky above and wonders on the earth below. There will be blood and fire and clouds of smoke.
translation_verse: Na mɛma anwanwadeɛ aba soro na nsɛnkyerɛnne aba asaase so, fam, mogyaa ne gya ne wisie kumoo.

english_verse: The sun will turn dark, and the moon will be as red as blood before the great and wonderful day of the Lord appears.
translation_verse: Awia beduru sum, na bosome nso adane sɛ mogyaa, ansa na Awurade da kɛseɛ a ɔhyerɛn he aba.

english_verse: Then the Lord will save everyone who asks for his help.”
translation_verse: Na biaa ɔbɛbɔ Awurade dini he bɛbɛgye no nkwa.’

english_verse: Now, listen to what I have to say about Jesus from Nazareth. God proved he sent Jesus to you by having him work miracles, wonders, and signs. All of you know this.
translation_verse: “Israelfoɔ, hontie nea meeka yi: Yesu, Nasareni he, beema a Nyankopɔn de anwanwadeɛ ne nsɛnkyerɛnne a ɔno Nyankopɔn he ankasa nam ne so yɔɔ no ho mu yii no adi kyerɛ ho pefee, sɛnea hoaa honim he;

english_verse: God had already planned and decided that Jesus would be handed over to you. So you took him and had evil men put him to death on a cross.
translation_verse: ɔnoaa ne ɔnam Nyankopɔn nhyehyɛeɛ ne ne nim a onim dada so yii no maeɛ he, na hofaa no, nam bɛ a benni mmara nsa so bɔɔ no asennua mu kuu no.

english_verse: But God set him free from death and raised him to life. Death could not hold him in its power.
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn anyane no afiri wuom, na waayi wuo-yeyaa afiri ne so, efiri sɛ ontumi mma sɛ ɔbɛkɔ so ahu wuo amaneɛ.

english_verse: What David said are really the words of Jesus, “I always see the Lord near me, and I will not be afraid with him at my right side.
translation_verse: Na Dawid kaa ne ho asɛm sɛ, ‘Mehu Awurade m’anim da biaa, ɔya me nifa nti menhinhim da;

english_verse: Because of this, my heart will be glad, my words will be joyful, and I will live in hope.
translation_verse: Yei nti m’akoma tɔɔ me yam, na me kɛtrɛma ho sanee no; afei nso me nipadua mpo bɛtena anidasoɔ mu,

english_verse: The Lord won't leave me in the grave. I am his holy one, and he won't let my body decay.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ wonnya me kra Asamando, na womma wo Kronkronni he nipadua mporɔ.

english_verse: He has shown me the path to life, and he makes me glad by being near me.”
translation_verse: Woama mahu nkwa kwane; esiane sɛ wo ne me ya hɔ nti, m’anigyeɛ awie pɛyɛ.’

english_verse: My friends, it is right for me to speak to you about our ancestor David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is still here.
translation_verse: “Anuanom, homma me kwane na menka nana Dawid ho asɛm pefee menkyerɛ ho, sɛ owuiɛ, na besiee no, na n’adamena ya yɛ nkyɛne ha de besi nnɛ.

english_verse: But David was a prophet, and he knew that God had made a promise he would not break. He had told David someone from his own family would someday be king.
translation_verse: Ɔnam sɛ ɔyɛ diyifoɔ, na onim sɛ Nyankopɔn de ntam aka akyerɛ no sɛ n’asenemfoɔ he mu baako bɛtena n’ahennwa so nti,

english_verse: David knew this would happen, and so he told us Christ would be raised to life. He said God would not leave him in the grave or let his body decay.
translation_verse: ohuu yei sieeɛ na ɔkaa Kristo ho asɛm sɛ bɛannya no Asamando, saa ara nso ne ne honam amporɔ.

english_verse: All of us can tell you that God has raised Jesus to life!
translation_verse: Yesu yiaa ne Nyankopɔn anyane no; yɛ nyinaa yɛyɛ ho adansefoɔ.

english_verse: Jesus was taken up to sit at the right side of God, and he was given the Holy Spirit, just as the Father had promised. Jesus is also the one who has given the Spirit to us, and this is what you are now seeing and hearing.
translation_verse: Afei bɛmaa no so kɔɔ Nyankopɔn nifa, na onyaa Agya he nkyɛne Honhom bɔhyɛ he nti ne wahwie nea hohu na hote yi agu.

english_verse: David didn't go up to heaven. So he wasn't talking about himself when he said, “The Lord told my Lord to sit at his right side,
translation_verse: Na Dawid amforo ankɔ soro, nso ɔno ankasa kaa sɛ, ‘Awurade ka kyerɛɛ m’Awurade sɛ, Tena me nsa nifa,

english_verse: until he made my Lord's enemies into a footstool for him.”
translation_verse: kosi sɛ mede w’atamfoɔ bɛhyɛ wo nane ase.’

english_verse: Everyone in Israel should then know for certain that God has made Jesus both Lord and Christ, even though you put him to death on a cross.
translation_verse: Nti ma Israel fie nyinaa nhu ampa sɛ Nyankopɔn ayɔ Yesu yi a hobɔɔ no asennua mu he Awurade ne Kristo.”

english_verse: When the people heard this, they were very upset. They asked Peter and the other apostles, “Friends, what should we do?”
translation_verse: Na bɛteeɛ he, asɛm he wowɔɔ bɛ akoma mu, na bebisaa Petro ne Asomafoɔ a bɛaka he sɛ, “Anuanom, ɛɛne ne yɛnyɔ?”

english_verse: Peter said to them, “Turn to God and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Afei Petro ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Honsakra ho adwene, na ho nyinaa homma bɛmmɔ ho asu Yesu Kristo dini mu, na hoanya bɔne fakyɛ, na hobenya akyɛdeɛ a ɔyɛ Honhom Kronkron he.

english_verse: This promise is for you and your children. It is for everyone our Lord God will choose, no matter where they live.”
translation_verse: Na bɔhyɛ yi ya hɔ ma ho ne ho mma ne bɛ a bɛya akyirikyiri, a Awurade yɛ Nyankopɔn bɛfrɛ bɛ aba ne nkyɛne nyinaa.”

english_verse: Peter told them many other things as well. Then he said, “I beg you to save yourselves from what will happen to all these evil people.”
translation_verse: Na ɔde nsɛm foforɔ pii dii adanseɛ, na otuu bɛ fo sɛ, “Homma bɛnnye ho mfiri awoɔ ntoatoasoɔ porɔeɛ yi mu.”

english_verse: They spent their time learning from the apostles, and they were like family to each other. They also broke bread and prayed together. Life among the Lord's Followers
translation_verse: Na betuu bɛ ho sii hɔ maa asomafoɔ he nkyerɛkyerɛ ne ayɔnkofa, paanoo bubuo ne mpaebɔ.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed by the many miracles and wonders that the apostles worked.
translation_verse: Na hu tɔɔ akra nyinaa so, efiri sɛ Asomafoɔ he yɔɔ anwanwadeɛ ne nsɛnkyerɛnne pii.

english_verse: All the Lord's followers often met together, and they shared everything they had.
translation_verse: Na bɛ a bɛgye diiɛ nyinaa hyiaa faako, na bɛ nnoɔma nyinaa yɛɛ kwasafodeɛ.

english_verse: They would sell their property and possessions and give the money to whoever was in need.
translation_verse: Na bɛtɔno nea bɛwɔ ne bɛ agyapadeɛ kyekyɛɛ nea benyaeɛ maa bɛ ho bɛ ho, sɛnea biaa ahiadeɛ teɛ.

english_verse: Day after day they met together in the temple. They broke bread together in different homes and shared their food happily and freely,
translation_verse: Da biaa na behyiam asɔrefie hɔ, na bebubu paanoo afie mu, de anigyeɛ ne akoma a mu te didi bom

english_verse: while praising God. Everyone liked them, and each day the Lord added to their group others who were being saved.
translation_verse: yi Nyankopɔn ayɛ. Na benyaa nnipa he nyinaa anim adom. Na Awurade de bɛ a ɔgyee bɛ he kaa asafo he ho da biaa.

english_verse: The time of prayer was about three o'clock in the afternoon, and Peter and John were going into the temple.
translation_verse: Da koro bi, ɔɔkɔ bɔ awia nnɔn mmeɛnsa, mpaebɔ berɛ he, Petro ne Yohane siim sɛ bɛɛkɔ asɔrefie.

english_verse: A man who had been born lame was being carried to the temple door. Each day he was placed beside this door, known as the Beautiful Gate. He sat there and begged from the people who were going in.
translation_verse: Na nkurofoɔ so beema bi a bɛwoo no he na ɔyɛ bubuani; na daa bɛde no ba asɔrefie pono a bɛfrɛ no Pono Fɛɛfɛ he ano ma no bɛsrɛ bɛ a bɛɛkɔ asɔrefie hɔ he hɔ sika.

english_verse: The man saw Peter and John entering the temple, and he asked them for money.
translation_verse: Berɛ a ohuu Petro ne Yohane sɛ bɛɛkɔ asɔrefie hɔ he, ɔsrɛɛ sɛ bɛnkyɛ no sika.

english_verse: But they looked straight at him and said, “Look up at us!”
translation_verse: Nso Petro ne Yohane hwɛɛ no gyee; na Petro kaa sɛ “Hwɛ yɛ.”

english_verse: The man stared at them and thought he was going to get something.
translation_verse: Nti ɔhwɛɛ bɛ susuu sɛ obenya bibi afiri bɛnkyɛne.

english_verse: But Peter said, “I don't have any silver or gold! But I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ from Nazareth, get up and start walking.”
translation_verse: Nso Petro kaa sɛ, “Sika anaa dwetɛ deɛ menni bi, na mmom nea mewɔ ne mede ma wo yi; sɔre nante Yesu Kristo, Nasareni he, dini mu.”

english_verse: Peter then took him by the right hand and helped him up. At once the man's feet and ankles became strong,
translation_verse: Nti ɔsɔɔ ne nsa nifa maa no so. Na amonom hɔaa ne nane ne ne nanpɔ so yɛɛ dene.

english_verse: and he jumped up and started walking. He went with Peter and John into the temple, walking and jumping and praising God.
translation_verse: Ohuri gyinaeɛ, na ofirii aseɛ nanteeɛ, na ɔne bɛ kɔɔ asɔrefie hɔ a na oohurihuri na ooyi Nyankopɔn ayɛ.

english_verse: Everyone saw him walking around and praising God.
translation_verse: Nnipa he nyinaa huu no sɛ ɔɔnante na ooyi Nyankopɔn ayɛ,

english_verse: They knew he was the beggar who had been lying beside the Beautiful Gate, and they were completely surprised. They could not imagine what had happened to the man. Peter Speaks in the Temple
translation_verse: na behuu sɛ ɔyɛ ɔno ne na ɔtena Pono Fɛɛfɛ he ano srɛsrɛ sika he, na nea asi he yɛɛ bɛ nwanwa ne ahodwiri.

english_verse: While the man kept holding on to Peter and John, the whole crowd ran to them in amazement at the place known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: Berɛ a beema he kita Petro ne Yohane mu he, nnipa a bɛya hɔ he nyinaa de ahodwiri tuu mmirika kɔɔ bɛ nkyɛne bea bi a bɛfrɛ hɔ Salomo abranaa so.

english_verse: Peter saw that a crowd had gathered, and he said: Friends, why are you surprised at what has happened? Why are you staring at us? Do you think we have some power of our own? Do you think we were able to make this man walk because we are so religious?
translation_verse: Na Petro huu yei he, ɔka kyerɛɛ nkurofokuo he sɛ, “Israel mmeema, ɛɛne nti ne yei yɛ ho nwanwa, mmɛ nti ne hoohwɛ yɛ haa te sɛ nea yɛnam yɛ ankasa yɛ tumi anaa yɛ tenenee so ne ama beema yi anante yi?

english_verse: The God that Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and our other ancestors worshiped has brought honor to his Servant Jesus. He is the one you betrayed. You turned against him when he was being tried by Pilate, even though Pilate wanted to set him free.
translation_verse: Abraham Nyankopɔn ne Isak Nyankopɔn ne Yakobo Nyankopɔn, yɛ nananom Nyankopɔn ahyɛ n’akoa Yesu animuonyam, nea ho deɛ, hoyii no maeɛ, na hopaa no Pilato anim, berɛ a Pilato sii gyinaeɛ sɛ obegyaa no he.

english_verse: You rejected Jesus, who was holy and good. You asked for a murderer to be set free,
translation_verse: Na hopoo Kronkronni ne Hoteeni he, na hosrɛɛ sɛ bennyae wudini he ma ɔnkɔ.

english_verse: and you killed the one who leads people to life. But God raised him from death, and all of us can tell you what he has done.
translation_verse: Na hokuu nkwa Farebae he, nea Nyankopɔn nyanee no firii awufuom he. Yɛyɛ yei ho adansefoɔ.

english_verse: You see this man, and you know him. He put his faith in the name of Jesus and was made strong. Faith in Jesus made this man completely well while everyone was watching.
translation_verse: Nti gyidie a yɛwɔ ya ne dini mu ne ama beema a hohu no na honim no yi anya ahoɔdene, na Yesu mu gyidie ne ama beema yi ho atɔ no korakora ya ho nyinaa ho anim yi.

english_verse: My friends, I am sure you and your leaders didn't know what you were doing.
translation_verse: “Afei anuanom, menim sɛ nim a honnim nti ne hoyɔɔ sɛnea ho atumfoɔ yɔɔeɛ he.

english_verse: But God had his prophets tell that his Messiah would suffer, and now he has kept this promise.
translation_verse: Nso Nyankopɔn nam saa kwane yi so ama nnoɔma a ɔnam adiyifoɔ he so kaeɛ he aba mu sɛ, ne Kristo he behu amane.

english_verse: So turn to God! Give up your sins, and you will be forgiven.
translation_verse: Nti ho nsakra ho adwene, na hommra Nyankopɔn nkyɛne na wapopa ho bɔne,

english_verse: Then the time will come when the Lord will give you fresh strength. He will send you Jesus, his chosen Messiah.
translation_verse: na ayɛ ahotɔ nna afiri Awurade nkyɛne aba, na wasoma nea bɛayi no asi hɔ ama ho dada he, Yesu, Kristo he

english_verse: But Jesus must stay in heaven until God makes all things new, just as his holy prophets promised long ago.
translation_verse: a ɔsɛ sɛ ɔtena soro kosi berɛ a bebesiesie nnoɔma nyinaa sɛnea Nyankopɔn nam n’adiyifoɔ kronkron he so firi teteete aka he.

english_verse: Moses said, “The Lord your God will choose one of your own people to be a prophet, just as he chose me. Listen to everything he tells you.
translation_verse: Mose kaa sɛ, ‘Awurade ho Nyankopɔn beyi diyifoɔ a ɔte sɛ me afiri ho nuanom mu ama ho. Ɔsɛ sɛ hotie nea ɔbɛka akyerɛ ho nyinaa.

english_verse: No one who disobeys this prophet will be one of God's people any longer.”
translation_verse: Na ɔbɛba sɛ, biaa a ontie saa diyifoɔ he, bɛbɛsɛe no koraa afiri mane he mu.’

english_verse: Samuel and all the other prophets who came later also spoke about what is now happening.
translation_verse: Na adiyifoɔ a bɛakasa he, ofiri Samuel ne bɛ a bɛbaa n’akyi, nso hyɛɛ saa nna yi ho nkɔm.

english_verse: You are really the ones God told his prophets to speak to. And you were given the promise God made to your ancestors. He said to Abraham, “All nations on earth will be blessed because of someone from your family.”
translation_verse: Hoyɛ adiyifoɔ he ne apam a Nyankopɔn ne ho agyanom pameeɛ he mma, sɛnea ɔka kyerɛɛ Abraham sɛ, ‘W’aseni so ne bɛbɛfa ahyira asaase so mmusua nyinaa he.’

english_verse: God sent his chosen Son to you first, because God wanted to bless you and make each one of you turn away from your sins.
translation_verse: Berɛ a Nyankopɔn somaa n’Akoa he, ho nkyɛne ne ɔmaa no baa kane sɛ ɔmmɛdane ho nyinaa mfiri ho nneyɔeɛ bɔne ho na onhyira ho.”

english_verse: The apostles were still talking to the people, when some priests, the captain of the temple guard, and some Sadducees arrived.
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a Petro ne Yohane ɔɔkasa akyerɛ nkurofoɔ he, asɔfoɔ mpanyimfoɔ ne asɔrefie hwɛfoɔ he ne Sadukifoɔ he baa bɛ so,

english_verse: These men were angry because the apostles were teaching the people that the dead would be raised from death, just as Jesus had been raised from death.
translation_verse: na bɛ bo afu sɛ asuafoɔ he nam Yesu wusɔreɛ he ɔɔkyerɛkyerɛ nkurofoɔ he wusɔreɛ ho asɛm.

english_verse: It was already late in the afternoon, and they arrested Peter and John and put them in jail for the night.
translation_verse: Nti bɛkyee bɛ kɔtoo afiase ma adeɛ kyeeɛ, efiri sɛ na adeɛ asa dada.

english_verse: But a lot of people who had heard the message believed it. So by now there were about
translation_verse: Na bɛ a bɛtee asɛm a Petro ne Yohane kaeɛ he mu bebree gye diiɛ, na bɛ dodoɔ bɛyɛ mpemnum.

english_verse: The next morning the leaders, the elders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Adeɛ kyeeɛ he, bɛ atumfoɔ ne mpanyimfoɔ ne atwerɛfoɔ he hyiaam Yerusalem,

english_verse: The high priest Annas was there, as well as Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and other members of the high priest's family.
translation_verse: a na sɔfo panyini Anas, ne Kaiafa, ne Yohane ne Aleksandro, ne bɛ mu pii a na bɛyɛ sɔfo panyini he abusuafoɔ nso ka ho.

english_verse: They brought in Peter and John and made them stand in the middle while they questioned them. They asked, “By what power and in whose name have you done this?”
translation_verse: Na bɛde Petro ne Yohane begyinaa bɛ mfimfini firi aseɛ bisabisaa bɛ sɛ: “Mmɛ tumi ne hode yɔɔ yei anaa hwane dini mu ne hoyɔɔ saa?”

english_verse: Peter was filled with the Holy Spirit and told the nation's leaders and the elders:
translation_verse: Na Petro a Honhom Kronkron he ahyɛ no ma he kaa sɛ, “Mane atumfoɔ ne mpanyimfoɔ,

english_verse: You are questioning us today about a kind deed in which a man who could not walk was healed.
translation_verse: sɛ nnɛ hogyina papa a yɛayɔ ama yarefoɔ yi so ɔɔhwehwɛ yɛ nsɛm mu ahu nea ɔnam ne so ama beema yi ho atɔ no a,

english_verse: But there is something we must tell you and everyone else in Israel. This man is standing here completely well because of the power of Jesus Christ from Nazareth. You put Jesus to death on a cross, but God raised him to life.
translation_verse: hedeɛ ho ne nnipa a hoya Israel nyinaa nhu sɛ ɔnam Nasareni Yesu Kristo a hobɔɔ no asennua mu a Nyankopɔn nyane no firii awufoɔ mu he dini so ne saa beema yi nyaa ayaresa.

english_verse: He is the stone you builders thought was worthless, and now he is the most important stone of all.
translation_verse: Saa Yesu yi ne, boɔ a ho adansifoɔ poo no a wabɛyɛ tweaso ti boɔ he.

english_verse: Only Jesus has the power to save! His name is the only one in all the world that can save anyone.
translation_verse: Nkwagyeɛ nni bi foforɔ mu, efiri sɛ dini foforɔ biaa nni soro anaa asaase so a bɛde ama nnipa sɛ bɛmfa so nye yɛ nkwa ka Yesu dini ho.”

english_verse: The officials were amazed to see how brave Peter and John were, and they knew that these two apostles were only ordinary men and not well educated. The officials were certain these men had been with Jesus.
translation_verse: Na behuu sɛnea Petro ne Yohane kasa akokoɔduro so he, na behuu sɛ bɛyɛ nnipa a bensuaa nwoma na bɛyɛ mpapahwekwaa he, ɔyɛɛ bɛ nwanwa. Na behuu sɛ na bɛne Yesu ne nanteeɛ.

english_verse: But they could not deny what had happened. The man who had been healed was standing there with the apostles.
translation_verse: Na bɛhwɛɛ nipa a bɛasa no yareɛ he sɛ ogyina Petro ne Yohane nkyɛne he, bɛanya hwee anka antia bɛ.

english_verse: The officials commanded them to leave the council room. Then the officials said to each other,
translation_verse: Na bɛhyɛɛ bɛ sɛ bemfiri dwa he mu mfiri adi, na betuu agyina.

english_verse: “What can we do with these men? Everyone in Jerusalem knows about this miracle, and we cannot say it didn't happen.
translation_verse: Na bɛkaa sɛ, “Ɛɛne ne yɛbɛyɔ nnipa yi? Na nsɛnkyerɛnne a ɔnam bɛ so aba he ada adi ya bɛ a bɛte Yerusalem nyinaa anim; na yentumi nnye ho akyinnye.

english_verse: But to keep this thing from spreading, we will warn them never again to speak to anyone about the name of Jesus.”
translation_verse: Na sɛ ɔbɛyɔ na ɔnkɔ so nterɛ nnipa he mu nti, homma yɛmmara bɛ sɛ bɛnnka dini yi ho asɛm nkyerɛ nipa biaa bio.”

english_verse: So they called the two apostles back in and told them they must never, for any reason, teach anything about the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Nti bɛfrɛɛ bɛ hyɛɛ bɛ sɛ bɛnnka asɛm na bɛnkyerɛkyerɛ Yesu dini mu baabiaa.

english_verse: Peter and John answered, “Do you think God wants us to obey you or to obey him?
translation_verse: Nso Petro ne Yohane buaa bɛ sɛ, “Sɛ ɔtene Nyankopɔn anim sɛ yetie ho sene Nyankopɔn a, ho ankasa hommua.

english_verse: We cannot keep quiet about what we have seen and heard.”
translation_verse: Na yɛ mmom deɛ, nea yɛate na yɛahu he, yentumi nnyae ho asɛm ka da.”

english_verse: The officials could not find any reason to punish Peter and John. So they threatened them and let them go. The man who was healed by this miracle was more than
translation_verse: Na beema a bɛnam anwanwakwane so saa no yadeɛ he adi boro mfie aduannan. Asafo he mpaebɔ

english_verse: As soon as Peter and John had been set free, they went back and told the others everything the chief priests and the leaders had said to them.
translation_verse: Begyae bɛ he, bɛkɔɔ bɛ mfɛfoɔ nkyɛne kɔkaa nea sɔfopanyini ne mpanyimfoɔ he ka kyerɛɛ bɛ he kyerɛɛ bɛ.

english_verse: When the rest of the Lord's followers heard this, they prayed together and said: Master, you created heaven and earth, the sea, and everything in them.
translation_verse: Bɛtee yei he bɛde akoma korɔ maa bɛ nne so bɔɔ mpaeɛ sɛ, “Nyame Tumfoɔ, wo ne wobɔɔ soro ne asaase, po ne mu nnoɔma nyinaa,

english_verse: And by the Holy Spirit you spoke to our ancestor David. He was your servant, and you told him to say: “Why are all the Gentiles so furious? Why do people make foolish plans?
translation_verse: na wonam Honhom Kronkron so faa yɛ nana Dawid a ɔyɛ w’akoa so kaa sɛ: ‘Ɛɛne nti ne Amanamane yɛ gyegyeegye na nkurofoɔ susu ade hunu?

english_verse: The kings of earth prepare for war, and the rulers join together against the Lord and his Messiah.”
translation_verse: Asaase so ahemfo sɔre gyina, na atitire hyiaa faako sɔre tia Awurade ne ne Kristo he.’

english_verse: Here in Jerusalem, Herod and Pontius Pilate got together with the Gentiles and the people of Israel. Then they turned against your holy Servant Jesus, your chosen Messiah.
translation_verse: Na Herode ne Pontio Pilato ne amanamane mufoɔ ne Israelfoɔ ka boom tiaa w’akoa kronkron Yesu a woasra no

english_verse: They did what you in your power and wisdom had already decided would happen.
translation_verse: sɛ ɔmmɛyɔ nea wo nsa ne wo agyina ahyɛ ato hɔ sɛ ɔbɛba he.

english_verse: Lord, listen to their threats! We are your servants. So make us brave enough to speak your message.
translation_verse: Na afei, Awurade, hwɛ bɛ ahunahuna, na ma wo nkoa mfa akokoɔduro nyinaa nka w’asɛm,

english_verse: Show your mighty power, as we heal people and work miracles and wonders in the name of your holy Servant Jesus.
translation_verse: berɛ a wotene wo nsa sa yadeɛ, na wonam w’akoa kronkron Yesu dini so yɔ nsɛnkyerɛnne ne anwanwadeɛ yi.”

english_verse: After they had prayed, the meeting place shook. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and bravely spoke God's message. Sharing Possessions
translation_verse: Berɛ a bewiee mpaebɔ he, baabi a behyiaam he wosoeɛ, na Honhom Kronkron bɛhyɛɛ bɛ nyinaa mama na bɛde akokoɔduro kaa Nyankopɔn asɛm he. Asafo mu anuadɔ

english_verse: The group of followers all felt the same way about everything. None of them claimed that their possessions were their own, and they shared everything they had with each other.
translation_verse: Na afei bɛ a bɛgye diiɛ he mu bebree akoma ne bɛ kra yɛɛ baako, na bɛ mu biaa nka sɛ ne nnoɔma a ɔwɔ yɛ ɔno nkoaa ne dea, na mmom bɛ nnoɔma nyinaa yɛ kwasafodeɛ.

english_verse: In a powerful way the apostles told everyone that the Lord Jesus was now alive. God greatly blessed his followers,
translation_verse: Asomafoɔ he de tumi kɛseɛ dii adanseɛ faa Awurade Yesu wusɔreɛ he ho, na adom kɛseɛ baa bɛ nyinaa bɛ so.

english_verse: and no one went in need of anything. Everyone who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money
translation_verse: Bɛ mu biaa nni hɔ a na bibi hia no, efiri sɛ na bɛ a bɛwɔ nsaase anaa afie he nyinaa tɔno de nea benya he ba

english_verse: to the apostles. Then they would give the money to anyone in need.
translation_verse: bɛhyɛ asomafoɔ he nsa ma bɛkyekyɛ ma biaa sɛnea n’ahiadeɛ teɛ.

english_verse: Ananias and his wife Sapphira also sold a piece of property.
translation_verse: Na beema bi a ɔde Anania, ne ne yerɛ Safira nso tɔnoo bɛ asaase bi.

english_verse: But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves. So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles,
translation_verse: Na Anania yii sika a benyaeɛ he bi sieeɛ a na ne yerɛ nso nim ho bi, na bɛde nkaeɛ he bɛhyɛɛ asomafoɔ he nsa.

english_verse: Peter said, “Why has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit?
translation_verse: Petro bisaa sɛ, “Anania, ɛɛne nti ne Satan apirim w’akoma sɛ yi sika a wonyaeɛ firi asaase he tɔno he mu bi sie na bedi torɔ kyerɛ Honhom Kronkron?

english_verse: The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!”
translation_verse: Berɛ a asaase he ya wo nsam he, na ɔnyɛ wo dea? Na wotɔnoeɛ he nso, na ɔnna so nyɛ wo dea anaa? Mmɛ nti na wobɔɔ pɔ a ɔte saa w’akoma mu? Nyankopɔn ne woadi torɔ akyerɛ no, na mmom ɔnyɛ nnipa.”

english_verse: As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened.
translation_verse: Anania tee asɛm he, ɔhwee ase wuiɛ. Na hu kɛseɛ bɔɔ bɛ a bɛteeɛ he nyinaa.

english_verse: Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
translation_verse: Na mmaranteɛ he sɔre de ntoma kyekyeree no, na bɛsoaa no firii adi kosiee no.

english_verse: Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband.
translation_verse: Afei bɛyɛ nnɔnhwere mmeɛnsa akyi he, ne yerɛ baeɛ a na onnim nea asi.

english_verse: Peter asked her, “Tell me, did you sell the property for this amount?” “Yes,” she answered, “that's the amount.”
translation_verse: Petro bisaa no sɛ, “Ka kyerɛ me, hotɔnoo asaase he de gyee saa boɔ yi anaa?” Na obuaa sɛ, “Ɛɛ, saa ne yɛ tɔnoo no.”

english_verse: Then Peter said, “Why did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!”
translation_verse: Na afei Petro bisaa no sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne ho mmienu ayɔ ho adwene sɛ ho bɛsɔ Nyankopɔn Honhom ahwɛ yi? Hwɛ, bɛ a bekosiee wo kunu he anammɔno ne ya pono ano he, saa ne bɛbɛsoa wo nso akɔ.”

english_verse: At once she fell at Peter's feet and died. When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
translation_verse: Amonom hɔaa ɔhwee Petro nane ase wuiɛ. Na mmeranteɛ he baeɛ a behuu sɛ wawu he, bɛfaa no firi adi kosiee no ne kunu he nkyɛne.

english_verse: The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened. Peter's Unusual Power
translation_verse: Na hu kɛseɛ bɔɔ asafo he ne bɛ a bɛteeɛ nyinaa. Anwanwadeɛ ahodoɔ

english_verse: The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: Bio nso, Nyankopɔn nam asomafoɔ he so yɔɔ nsɛnkyerɛnne ne anwanwadeɛ bebree nnipa he mu, na na bɛ nyinaa de akoma koro ya Salomo abranaa he so.

english_verse: No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
translation_verse: Na nnipa a aka he mu biaa annya akokoɔduro amfa ne he ammɔ bɛ; nso nkurofoɔ de anidie kɛseɛ maa bɛ.

english_verse: Many men and women started having faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Na afei, afei, nea ɔkyɛne so ne sɛ mmeema ne mmaa pii a bɛagye Awurade adi he bɛkaa bɛ ho.

english_verse: Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them.
translation_verse: Na bɛde ayarefoɔ baa abɔntene bɛtotoo mpa ne kɛtɛ so sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a anyeyie koraa he, sɛ Petro ootwam a ne sunsum bɛtɔ bɛ mu bi so.

english_verse: A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed. Trouble for the Apostles
translation_verse: Afei nnipa pii firi nkuro a atwa Yerusalem ho ahyia he mu nso de ayarefoɔ ne bɛ a ahonhom fi ha bɛ baeɛ, na bɛsaa bɛ nyinaa yadeɛ.

english_verse: The high priest and all the other Sadducees who were with him became jealous.
translation_verse: Na sɔfo panyini he ne bɛ a bɛka ne ho nyinaa, Sadukifoɔ fekuo a na bɛya hɔ saa bere he sɔreeɛ, na ahoɔyea a ahyɛ bɛ ma nti,

english_verse: They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail.
translation_verse: bɛkyee asomafoɔ he de bɛ guu afiase.

english_verse: But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said,
translation_verse: Na anadwo he, Awurade bɔfoɔ bi biee afiase apono he de bɛ firii adi kaa sɛ,

english_verse: “Go to the temple and tell the people everything about this new life.”
translation_verse: “Honkɔ, na honkogyina asɔrefie hɔ, nkɔka nkwa yi ho nsɛm nyinaa nkyerɛ nkurofoɔ he.”

english_verse: So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching. The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail.
translation_verse: Na bɛtee yei he, bɛkɔɔ asɔredane he mu ahemakye, na bɛkɔɔ so kyerɛkyerɛeɛ. Na sɔfo panyini he ne bɛ a bɛka ne ho he beduruiɛ he, bɛfrɛɛ abadwakuo he ne Israel mpanyimfoɔ nyinaa behyiaeɛ, na bɛsoma ma bɛkɔɔ afiase hɔ sɛ bɛnkɔfa bɛ mmra.

english_verse: The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said,
translation_verse: Nso bere a awɛmfoɔ he duruu hɔ he, bɛanhu bɛ afiase hɔ. Nti bɛsane kɔbɔɔ amanneɛ sɛ,

english_verse: “We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.”
translation_verse: “Yɛahu sɛ bɛatoto afiase apono he nyinaa mu dendenndene a awɛmfoɔ he gyinagyina apono he ano, nso yebieɛ he yɛanhu biaa hɔ.”

english_verse: The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
translation_verse: Na afei bere a asɔrefie hwɛfoɔ ne asɔfoɔ mpanyimfoɔ he tee nsɛm yi he, bɛ adwene mu yɛɛ bɛ kesenenee; na na bennim baabi a nsɛm yi bɛkɔ akowie.

english_verse: Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!”
translation_verse: Afei nipa bi bɛbɔɔ bɛ amanneɛ sɛ, “Hwɛ! Mmeema a hode bɛ guu afiase he ya asɔrefie hɔ, na begyinagyina hɔ ɔɔkyerɛkyerɛ nkurofoɔ he.” Petro dii wursɔreɛ ho adanseɛ

english_verse: The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
translation_verse: Nti asɔrefie hwɛfoɔ he ne n’awɛmfoɔ kɔfaa bɛ baeɛ, nso bɛanyɔ bɛ gidigidi, efiri sɛ na besuro sɛ nkurofoɔ he besi bɛ aboɔ.

english_verse: When the apostles were brought before the council, the high priest said to them,
translation_verse: Afei bɛde bɛ begyinaa Yudafoɔ abadwakuo kɛseɛ a bɛfrɛ no Sanhedrin he anim. Na sɔfo panyini he bisabisaa bɛ nsɛm

english_verse: “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
translation_verse: na ɔkaa sɛ, “Yɛanhyɛ ho ketee sɛ honyina saa dini yi so nkyerɛkyerɛ? Na afei ɔyɛɛ dɛ ne hode ho nkyerɛkyerɛ yi ahyeta Yerusalem nyinaa, na hoabɔ ho tim pɔ sɛ hode nipa yi mogyaa bebua yɛ atifi.”

english_verse: Peter and the apostles replied: We don't obey people. We obey God.
translation_verse: Petro ne asomafoɔ a bɛaka he buaa sɛ, “Ɔsɛ sɛ yetie Nyankopɔn mmom sene nnipa.

english_verse: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
translation_verse: Yɛ agyanom Nyankopɔn nyanee Yesu a hosɛne no asennua so, na hokuu no he.

english_verse: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
translation_verse: Saa nipa yi ne Nyankopɔn maa no so sɛ Safohene ne Agyenkwa kɔɔ ne nsa nifa so, sɛ ɔmma Israel nnya adwensakra ne bɔne fakyɛ.

english_verse: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
translation_verse: Na yɛyɛ nsɛm he ho adansefoɔ, na Honhom Kronkron a Nyankopɔn de ama bɛ a betie no he nso di adanseɛ.”

english_verse: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
translation_verse: Berɛ a bɛtee yei he, ɔwɔɔ bɛ yie na bɛpɛɛ sɛ beku bɛ.

english_verse: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
translation_verse: Nso Farisini bi a bɛfrɛ no Gamaliel a ɔyɛ mmarakyerɛni a nkurofoɔ he nyinaa de anidie ma no, sɔre gyinaa Sanhedrin he anim, na ɔhyɛɛ sɛ bɛmfa mmeema he mfiri adi kakra.

english_verse: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Israel mmeema, honhwɛ ho ho yie nea hoayɔ ho adwene sɛ hode bɛyɔ nnipa yi ho.

english_verse: Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
translation_verse: Ɔno akyi he, Galileani bi a ɔde Yuda nso sɔree nna a bɛtwerɛtwerɛ dini he mu, na ɔtwee nnipa dii n’akyi. Ɔno nso wuiɛ, na n’akyidifoɔ he nyinaa hweteeɛ.

english_verse: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
translation_verse: Nti me deɛ, nea mewɔ ka fa nea asisi yi ho ne sɛ, hommfa ho ho nhyehyɛ bɛ nsɛm mu, na honyaa bɛ, efiri sɛ, sɛ agyinatuo anaa adwuma yi firi nnipa a obegu;

english_verse: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
translation_verse: nso sɛ ofiri Nyankopɔn a hontumi ntu bɛ ngu koraa. Anyɛ saa ɔbɛyɛ sɛnea ho ne Nyankopɔn ankasa ɔɔko. Bɛgyee ne nsusuiɛ he toom!”

english_verse: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
translation_verse: Na bɛfrɛɛ asomafoɔ he baeɛ, na betwaa bɛ mmaa. Na bɛhyɛɛ bɛ sɛ bɛmmɔ Yesu dini nka asɛm bio, na begyae bɛ.

english_verse: The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus.
translation_verse: Befirii Sanhedrin he anim kɔeɛ, na bɛ ani gyee sɛ Yesu dini nti bɛfata sɛ begu bɛ anim ase.

english_verse: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Na bɛkɔɔ so kyerɛkyerɛeɛ, kaa Kristo Yesu ho asɛmpa ya asɔrefie ne afie afie mu daa a bɛannyae.

english_verse: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
translation_verse: Na afei nna he mu a na asuafoɔ he ɔɔdɔɔso he, Yudafoɔ na beka Hela kasa a ɔno nti ne bɛfrɛ bɛ Helenifoɔ he nwiinwii tia Hebrifoɔ he, efiri sɛ na bebu bɛ ani gu Helenifoɔ akunafoɔ he daa daa aduane ma dwumadie so.

english_verse: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
translation_verse: Nti dummienu he frɛɛ asuafokuo he ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Ɔmfata sɛ yebegya Nyankopɔn asɛm hɔ akɔkyekyɛ aduane apono so.

english_verse: My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things.
translation_verse: Nti anuanom, ho ankasa honhwehwɛ ho mu mmeema nson a hobetumi adi bɛ ho adanse-pa, na Honhom ne nyansa ahyɛ bɛ ma, na yɛmfa saa dwumadie yi nhyɛ bɛ nsa,

english_verse: We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.”
translation_verse: na yɛ nso yebetu yɛ ho asi hɔ ama mpaebɔ ne asɛm he nkyerɛkyerɛ.”

english_verse: This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch.
translation_verse: Nea bɛkaeɛ he sɔɔ nnipakuo he nyinaa ani, na beyii Stefano a gyidie ne Honhom Kronkron ahyɛ no mma, ne Filipo, Prokoro, Nikanor, Timon, Parmena ne Nikolao a ofiri Antiokia a wasakra abɛyɛ Yudani he.

english_verse: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
translation_verse: Bɛde bɛ begyinaa asomafoɔ he anim, na bɛde bɛ nsa guu bɛ so bɔɔ mpaeɛ maa bɛ.

english_verse: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn asɛm kɔɔ so trɛeɛ, na asuafoɔ he kɔɔ so yɛɛ dodo ya Yerusalem, na Yudafoɔ som he mu asɔfoɔ mu pii gyee asɛm he diiɛ.

english_verse: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
translation_verse: Afei Stefano a na adom ne tumi ahyɛ no ma he, yɔɔ anwanwadeɛ ne nsɛnkyerɛnne akɛseɛ nnipa he mu.

english_verse: But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves “Free Men.” They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him.
translation_verse: Nso nnipa bi a befiri nea bɛfrɛɛ no Ahofadiefoɔ Hyiadane he mu ne Kirenefoɔ ne Aleksandriafoɔ ne bɛ a befiri Kilikia ne Asia, sɔree sɛ bɛne Stefano oobedi anobaabaeɛ.

english_verse: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
translation_verse: Nso bɛantumi annyina ne nyansa ne Honhom a ɔde kasaeɛ he ano.

english_verse: So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
translation_verse: Na betutu guu nnipa bi asum sɛ bɛnka sɛ, “Yɛate sɛ ɔɔka abususɛm atia Mose ne Nyankopɔn.”

english_verse: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
translation_verse: Na behwanyanee nnipa he ne mpanyimfoɔ he ne atwerɛfoɔ he maa bɛbaa no so mpofirem bɛkyee no kɔmaa Sanhedrin he.

english_verse: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Na bɛmaa adansekunumufoɔ bɛkaa sɛ, “Beema yi nnyae nsɛm a ɔka tia kronkronbea yi ne Mmara he;

english_verse: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.”
translation_verse: na yɛate sɛ ɔdɛ Yesu yi a ɔyɛ Nasareni he bedwiri bea yi agu fam na wasakra amanneɛ a Mose de maa yɛ he.”

english_verse: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
translation_verse: Berɛ a bɛ a bɛte Sanhedrin badwa he mu nyinaa hwɛɛ no dinn he, behuu sɛ n’anim te sɛ bɔfoɔ anim.

english_verse: The high priest asked Stephen, “Are they telling the truth about you?”
translation_verse: Na sɔfo panyini he bisaa sɛ, “Nsɛm yi te saa anaa?”

english_verse: Stephen answered: Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran.
translation_verse: Na Stefano buaa sɛ: “Anuanom ne agyanom, hontie me. Animuonyam Nyankopɔn he yii ne ho adi kyerɛɛ yɛ nana Abraham, berɛ a na ɔya Mesopotamia ansa na ɔɔkɔtena Haran he,

english_verse: God told him, “Leave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.”
translation_verse: na ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, ‘Firi w’asaase so ne w’abusua mu na kɔ asaase a mɛkyerɛ wo he so.’

english_verse: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
translation_verse: Nti firi Kaldeafoɔ asaase so kɔtena Haran. Afei berɛ a ne papa wuiɛ akyi he, Nyankopɔn ma otu bɛtenaa asaase a hote so seesei he so.

english_verse: God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children.
translation_verse: Nso wamma no bi sɛ n’agyapadeɛ, wannya anammɔno baako mpo, na mmom Nyankopɔn hyɛɛ bɔ sɛ ɔde bɛma no sɛ agyapadeɛ, na ne wuo akyi he ɔde bɛma n’asefoɔ, nso saa berɛ he na onni ba.

english_verse: God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn kaa yei ho asɛm sɛ, n’asefoɔ bɛyɛ ahohoɔ binom mane mu, na nkurofoɔ he de bɛ ayɛ nkoa, ateetee bɛ mfie ahannan.

english_verse: But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn kaa sɛ, ‘Mebu mane a bɛde bɛ bɛyɛ nkoa he atɛne, na yeinom akyi he, bebefiri hɔ na bɛabɛsom me ha.’

english_verse: God said to Abraham, “Every son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.” So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons.
translation_verse: Ɔmaa no twetiatwa apam nso; nti ɔwoo Isak, na da ɔtɔ so nwɔtwe he otwaa no twetia, na Isak woo Yakob, na Yakob woo mmusua dummienu he agyanom.

english_verse: and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
translation_verse: na ɔgyee no firii n’ahohiahia nyinaa mu maa no adom ne nyansa wɔ Misraim hene Farao anim. Na ɔpaa no sɛ ɔnhwɛ Misraim ne ne fie nyinaa so.

english_verse: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
translation_verse: Na afei ɔbaa sɛ kɔm bi baa Misraim ne Kaana asaase nyinaa so, na yei de amaneɛ kɛseɛ baeɛ a na yɛ nananom nya aduane nni.

english_verse: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
translation_verse: Na Yakob tee sɛ aduane ya Misraim he, ɔsomaa yɛ nananom berɛ a odi kane.

english_verse: It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
translation_verse: Na ne mprenu so he, Yosef yii ne ho adi kyerɛɛ ne nuanom, na Farao huu Yosef abusua.

english_verse: Joseph sent for his father and his relatives. In all, there were
translation_verse: Nti Yosef soma ma bɛkɔfaa ne papa Yakob ne n’abusua nyinaa firii hɔ baeɛ, nnipa a na bɛ dodoɔ yɛ aduɔson num.

english_verse: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
translation_verse: Yakob kɔɔ Misraim. Na ɔno ankasa wuiɛ, na yɛ agyanom nso wuwuu saa

english_verse: Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
translation_verse: na bɛde bɛ kɔɔ Sekem kosiee bɛ adamena a Abraham de sika dwetɛ tɔ firii Hamor mma nkyɛne ya Sekem he mu.

english_verse: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
translation_verse: “Na berɛ ɔɔyɛ aso ama bɔhyɛ a Nyankopɔn de maa Abraham he aba mu he, yɛ nkurofoɔ he dɔre yɛɛ bebree ya Misraim

english_verse: Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph.
translation_verse: kosii sɛ hene foforɔ bedii adeɛ Misraim a na onnim Yosef.

english_verse: He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
translation_verse: Ɔno ne de anifire dii yɛ abusua na ɔhyɛɛ yɛ nananom sɛ bɛnto bɛ mmɔfra ngu ma benwuwu.

english_verse: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
translation_verse: Saa berɛ heaa ne bɛwoo Mose, na ne ho ɔfɛ paa. Na bɛtetee no bosome mmeɛnsa ne papa fie

english_verse: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
translation_verse: na begyae no hɔ he, Farao babaa faa no tetee no sɛ n’ankasa ba.

english_verse: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
translation_verse: Na bɛkyerɛɛ Mose Misraim nyansa nyinaa. Na ɔwɔ tumi ne kasa ne nnwuma mu.

english_verse: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
translation_verse: Na ohuu sɛ Misraimni bi ɔɔyɔ Israelni bi bɔne he, ɔgyee Israelni he ti, na okuu Misraimni he de yɛɛ aweretɔ maa Israelni a na ɔɔyɔ no bɔne he.

english_verse: Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
translation_verse: Osusuu sɛ ne nuanom behu sɛ Nyankopɔn nam ne so ɔɔgye bɛ, nso bɛanhu no saa.

english_verse: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
translation_verse: Na adeɛ kyeeɛ he ɔkɔtoo Israelfoɔ binom sɛ bɛɛko, na ɔbɔɔ mmɔdene sɛ ɔbɛka bɛ abom bio ama asomdwie aba bɛ ntam, na ɔkaa sɛ, “Mmeema, ho yɛ anuanom. Mmɛ nti ne hoyɔ ho ho bɔne yi?”

english_verse: But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, “Who made you our ruler and judge?
translation_verse: Na nea ɔɔyɔ ne yɔnko bɔne he summ no kaa sɛ, “Hwane ne yii wo sɛ yɛ sodifoɔ ne temufoɔ?”

english_verse: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?”
translation_verse: Wopɛ sɛ woku me sɛ wokuu Misraimni he nnora he anaa?

english_verse: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
translation_verse: Na Mose tee yei he, odwane kɔeɛ na ɔkɔyɛɛ hɔhoɔ Midian asaase so, na hɔ ne ɔwoo mma mmeema mmienu.

english_verse: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses from a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai.
translation_verse: “Na mfie aduannan akyi he, bɔfoɔ bi yii ne ho kyerɛɛ no Sinai Bepɔ serɛ he so, nwura bi a ɔɔdɛre mu.

english_verse: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,
translation_verse: Mose huiɛ he, ɔyɛɛ no nwanwa. Na ɔtwe pinii ho sɛ ɔɔkɔhwɛ he, Awurade kasaa sɛ:

english_verse: “I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
translation_verse: “Me ne wo agyanom Nyankopɔn, Abraham Nyankopɔn, Isak Nyankopɔn, ne Yakob Nyankopɔn.” Mose ho hyɛɛ aseɛ sɛ ɔɔpopo nti wannya akokoɔduro sɛ ɔɔkɔ so ahwɛ adekorɔ he bio.

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy.
translation_verse: Na Awurade ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Worɔ wo mpaboa, na baabi a wogyina he yɛ kronkron.

english_verse: With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
translation_verse: Nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, maahu amaneɛ a me nkurofoɔ a bɛya Misraim he oohu; na maate bɛ apinisie, na maaba sɛ meebɛgye bɛ. Afei bra, na mensoma wo nkɔ Misraim.”

english_verse: This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
translation_verse: “Ɔyɛ saa Mose yi ne bɛpoo no berɛ a bɛkaa sɛ, Hwane ne yii wo sɛ yɛ sodifoɔ ne temufoɔ?” Ɔno ne afei deɛ Nyankopɔn nam bɔfoɔ a oyii ne ho adi kyerɛɛ no nwuram hɔ he so ɔɔsoma no sɛ sodifoɔ ne gyefoɔ he.

english_verse: In Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the desert, Moses rescued the people by working miracles and wonders for
translation_verse: Oyii bɛ, na ɔyɔɔ anwanwadeɛ ne nsɛnkyerɛnne pii Misriam, Mmɛwa Po he mu ne serɛ he so mfie aduannan.

english_verse: Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.”
translation_verse: Saa Mose yi ne ka kyerɛɛ Israelfoɔ sɛ, ‘Nyankopɔn beyi diyifoɔ bi a ɔte sɛ me afiri ho nuanom mu ama ho.’

english_verse: Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us.
translation_verse: Mose yiaa ne na ɔka bɔfoɔ a ɔkasa kyerɛɛ no Bepɔ Sinai so he ho a ɔbaa asafo he mu ne yɛ nananom nkyɛne serɛ so he, na onyaa nkwa nsɛm kronkron a ɔde bɛma ho.

english_verse: But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
translation_verse: Yɛ nananom anyɛ setie ama no, na mmom bɛpoo no, na bebenya a na nka bɛ ya Misraim,

english_verse: Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
translation_verse: Nti ɔyɔɔ nantwie ba honyini bi, na bɛbɔɔ afɔdeɛ maa no. Na bɛgyee bɛ ani bɛ nsa ano nnwuma ho.

english_verse: God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn yii n’ani firii bɛ so, na ogyae bɛ ma bɛsom soro asafo, sɛnea bɛatwerɛ adiyifoɔ he nwoma mu he sɛ: ‘O Israel fie, ɔyɛ me ne hode afɔrebɔdeɛ ne ayɛyɛdeɛ braa me serɛ so mfie adaduannan he?

english_verse: Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
translation_verse: Daabi, mmom hofaa Molok ntomadane ne ho bosom Refan nsoromma, ahonyini a hoyɔeɛ sɛ hobɛsom bɛ; nti meyi ho akɔ Babel akyi nohoa.’

english_verse: The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen.
translation_verse: “Yɛ nananom nyaa adanseɛ ntomadane he serɛ he so, sɛnea ɔhyɛɛ Mose berɛ a ɔɔkasa akyerɛ no sɛ ɔnyɔ no sɛ nhwɛsodeɛ a ohuiɛ he.

english_verse: Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David.
translation_verse: Na yɛ nananom nsa kaa saa ntomadane yi he, bɛne Yosua de baa amanamane a Nyankopɔn pamoo bɛ firii yɛ nananom anim he asaase so. Na bɛkora noaa de besii Dawid a,

english_verse: He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel.
translation_verse: onyaa Nyankopɔn anim adom na obisaa akwannya a ɔde besi tenabea ama Israel Nyankopɔn, he berɛ so.

english_verse: And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
translation_verse: Nso ɔyɛ Salomo ne sii fie maa no.

english_verse: But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
translation_verse: Na asɛm he ne sɛ, Sorosoroni he nte fie a bɛde nnipa nsa ayɔ mu, sɛnea diyifoɔ he kaa sɛ,

english_verse: “Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me? In what place will I rest?
translation_verse: ‘Soro yɛ m’ahennwa, na asaase yɛ me nane ntiasoɔ. Mmɛ fie ne hobesi ama me, Awurade ne aka, anaa he ne me homebea?’

english_verse: You stubborn and hardheaded people! You are always fighting against the Holy Spirit, just as your ancestors did.
translation_verse: “Nnipa a hoasene ho kɔno, ho a bentwaa ho akoma ne ho aso twetia, hoko tia Honhom Kronkron daa te sɛ ho agyanom yɔeɛ he.

english_verse: Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him.
translation_verse: Adiyifoɔ he mu hwane ne ho agyanom antaa no? Bekuu bɛ a bɛhyɛɛ nkɔm faa Kronkronni he mmaeɛ ho, na afei hoayi no ama bɛaku no.

english_verse: Angels gave you God's Law, but you still don't obey it. Stephen Is Stoned to Death
translation_verse: Hone honyaa mmara he sɛnea abɔfoɔ de maeɛ he, nso hoanni so.

english_verse: When the council members heard Stephen's speech, they were angry and furious.
translation_verse: Bɛtee yeinom he ɔwowɔɔ bɛ akoma mu na bɛ ani beree paa tia Stefano.

english_verse: But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side.
translation_verse: Nso esiane sɛ Honhom Kronkron ahyɛ ne ma nti, ɔhwɛɛ soro gyee huu Nyankopɔn animuonyam ne Yesu sɛ ogyina Nyankopɔn nsa nifa,

english_verse: Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”
translation_verse: na ɔkaa sɛ, “Hwɛ! Mehu sɛ soro abie a Nipa Ba he gyina Nyankopɔn nsa nifa.”

english_verse: The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen
translation_verse: Na bɛteateam dendene, de bɛ nsa sisii bɛ aso, na bɛ nyinaa gyee twi kɔɔ no so.

english_verse: and dragged him out of the city. Then they started throwing stones at him. The men who had brought charges against him put their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
translation_verse: Na bɛde no firii kuro he mu he, befirii aseɛ sii no aboɔ. Na adansefoɔ he de bɛ ntaadeɛ ngugusoɔ guu abranteɛ bi a bɛfrɛ no Saulo nane ase.

english_verse: As Stephen was being stoned to death, he called out, “Lord Jesus, please welcome me!”
translation_verse: Na begu so oosi no aboɔ he ɔsrɛɛ sɛ, “Awurade Yesu, gye me honhom.”

english_verse: He knelt down and shouted, “Lord, don't blame them for what they have done.” Then he died.
translation_verse: Afei, obuu nkotodwe de nne kɛseɛ teaam sɛ, “Awurade, mmu bɔne yi ntia bɛ.” Na ɔkaa saa he ogyaam.

english_verse: Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail. The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
translation_verse: Nso Saulo firii aseɛ tuu n’ani sii asafo he so. Owurawuraa afie mu kɔtwee mmaa ne mmeema de bɛ koguu afiase.

english_verse: The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news.
translation_verse: Na afei bɛ a bɛabɔ ahwete he kyinii asaase he so pae mu kaa asɛmpa he.

english_verse: Philip went to the city of Samaria and told the people about Christ.
translation_verse: Filipo kɔɔ Samaria kuro he mu na ofirii aseɛ pae mu kaa Agyenkwa he ho asɛm kyerɛɛ bɛ.

english_verse: They crowded around Philip because they were eager to hear what he was saying and to see him work miracles.
