translation_verse: Berɛ a nnipakuo he tieeɛ na behuu nsɛnkyerɛnne a Filipo ɔɔyɔ he, bɛde akoma korɔ tiee no.

english_verse: Many people with evil spirits were healed, and the spirits went out of them with a shout. A lot of paralyzed and lame people were also healed.
translation_verse: Na nnipa pii wɔ ahonhom fi, na ahonhom fi he de nne kɛseɛ teateam firii bɛ mu firiiɛ. Afei nso, mmubuafoɔ ne mpakye pii nyaa ayaresa.

english_verse: Everyone in that city was very glad because of what was happening.
translation_verse: Nti anigyeɛ kɛseɛ baa saa kuro he mu.

english_verse: For some time a man named Simon had lived there and had amazed the people of Samaria. He practiced witchcraft and claimed to be somebody great.
translation_verse: Afei na beema bi ya kuro he mu a bɛfrɛ no Simon, a na oyi nkonyaa ma Samaria mane he ho dwirii bɛ, na ɔka sɛ ɔyɛ nipa kɛseɛ.

english_verse: Everyone, rich and poor, crowded around him. They said, “This man is the power of God called ‘The Great Power.’ ”
translation_verse: Na bɛ nyinaa, firi nkumaa so kosi akɛseɛ so tie no ka sɛ, “Yei ne Nyankopɔn Tumi a bɛfrɛ no Kɛseɛ.”

english_verse: For a long time, Simon had used witchcraft to amaze the people, and they kept crowding around him.
translation_verse: Na bɛyɛɛ dinn tie no efiri sɛ na ɔnam nkonyaa so ama bɛ ho adwiri bɛ akyɛre.

english_verse: But when they believed what Philip was saying about God's kingdom and about the name of Jesus Christ, they were all baptized.
translation_verse: Na afei, berɛ a bɛgyee Filipo a na ɔɔka Nyankopɔn ahennie he ho asɛmpa ne Yesu Kristo dini he diiɛ he, ɔbɔɔ bɛ asu, mmaa ne mmeema nyinaa.

english_verse: Even Simon believed and was baptized. He stayed close to Philip, because he marveled at all the miracles and wonders.
translation_verse: Mpo Simon nso gye diiɛ. Na bɛbɔɔ no asu wieeɛ he, na ɔtaa ne Filipo nante. Na nsɛnkyerɛnne ne ahoɔdenneɛ a ohuiɛ he yɛɛ no ahodwiri.

english_verse: The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John.
translation_verse: Na afei asomafoɔ a bɛya Yerusalem tee sɛ Samariafoɔ agye Nyankopɔn asɛm he, bɛsomaa Petro ne Yohane kɔɔ bɛ nkyɛne.

english_verse: When the two apostles arrived, they prayed that these people would be given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Bɛ mmienu he kɔbɔɔ mpaeɛ maa bɛ sɛ bɛ benya Honhom Kronkron he

english_verse: Before this, the Holy Spirit had not been given to any of them, though some of them had been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ obesi saa bere he na Honhom he mmaa bɛ mu biaa so; na mmom asu kɛkɛ na na bɛabɔ no Awurade Yesu dini mu.

english_verse: Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Na afei Petro ne Yohane de bɛ nsa guu bɛ so, na benyaa Honhom Kronkron he.

english_verse: Simon noticed that the Spirit was given only when the apostles placed their hands on the people. So he brought money
translation_verse: Berɛ a Simon huu sɛ asomafoɔ he nsa a bɛde guu binom so he maa Honhom baa bɛ so he, ɔyɔɔ sɛ ɔɔma bɛ sika.

english_verse: and said to Peter and John, “Let me have this power too! Then anyone I place my hands on will also be given the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Na ɔkaa sɛ, “Homma me nso saa tumi yi bi sɛ ɔbɛyɛ na nipa biaa a mede me nsa begu ne so he benya Honhom Kronkron he.”

english_verse: Peter said to him, “You and your money will both end up in hell if you think you can buy God's gift!
translation_verse: Nso Petro ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Wo ne wo sika nyinaa nsɛe, efiri sɛ wodwene sɛ wobetumi de sika atɔ Nyankopɔn akyɛdeɛ!

english_verse: You don't have any part in this, and God sees that your heart isn't right.
translation_verse: Wonni agyapadeɛ anaa kyɛfa ya asɛm yi mu, efiri sɛ w’akoma ntene Nyankopɔn anim.

english_verse: I can see that you are jealous and bound by your evil ways.”
translation_verse: Na maahu sɛ woyɛ anibereɛ paa, na amumuyɔ ahyɛ wo so.

english_verse: Simon said, “Please pray to the Lord, so that what you said won't happen to me.”
translation_verse: Simon buaa sɛ, Honsrɛ Awurade mma me, sɛ na nea hoaka he mu biaa amma me so.”

english_verse: After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria. Philip and an Ethiopian Official
translation_verse: Afei Petro ne Yohane dii adanseɛ na bɛkaa Awurade asɛm wieeɛ he, bɛsane kɔɔ Yerusalem, na bɛkaa asɛm pa he Samaria Nkuraa pii ase.

english_verse: The Lord's angel said to Philip, “Go south along the desert road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.”
translation_verse: Afei Awurade bɔfoɔ bi ka kyerɛɛ Filipo sɛ, “Sɔre na kɔ anaafoɔ fam hɔ kwane a ofiri Yerusalem kɔ Gaza he.” .

english_verse: So Philip left. An important Ethiopian official happened to be going along that road in his chariot. He was the chief treasurer for Candace, the Queen of Ethiopia. The official had gone to Jerusalem to worship
translation_verse: Nti ɔsɔre kɔeɛ. Na hwɛ, Etiopiani panyini bi a odi mu paa di. Na ɔyɛ Etiopiafoɔ hemaa Kandake Fotosanfoɔ. Na waaba Yerusalem akɔsom

english_verse: and was now on his way home. He was sitting in his chariot, reading the book of the prophet Isaiah.
translation_verse: na ɔte ne teaseɛnam mu ɔɔsane akɔ a ɔɔkenkane diyifoɔ Yesaia nwoma he.

english_verse: The Spirit told Philip to catch up with the chariot.
translation_verse: Nti Honhom he ka kyerɛɛ Filipo sɛ, “Twe bɛne teaseɛnam yi.”

english_verse: Philip ran up close and heard the man reading aloud from the book of Isaiah. Philip asked him, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
translation_verse: Nti Filipo tuu mirika kɔɔ ho na ɔtee sɛ ɔɔkenkane diyifoɔ Yesaia nwoma he; na obisaa sɛ, “Wote nea wookane yi ase?”

english_verse: The official answered, “How can I understand unless someone helps me?” He then invited Philip to come up and sit beside him.
translation_verse: Na obuaa sɛ, “Sɛ bi ankyerɛ me a; ɔbɛyɛ dɛ na mate aseɛ?” Na ɔtoo nsa frɛɛ Filipo sɛ ɔmmra teaseɛnam he mu mmɛtena ne nkyɛne.

english_verse: The man was reading the passage that said, “He was led like a sheep on its way to be killed. He was silent as a lamb whose wool is being cut off, and he did not say a word.
translation_verse: Twerɛsɛm mu nsɛm a na ɔɔkane he di: “Bɛde no baeɛ sɛ dwane a beebeku no, na sɛnea dwammaa tɔ mum nea otwitwa ne ho nwi anim he, wammie n’ano.

english_verse: He was treated like a nobody and did not receive a fair trial. How can he have children, if his life is snatched away?”
translation_verse: Berɛ a bɛbrɛɛ no ase he, bebuu no ntɛnkyea. Hwane ne ɔbɛkyerɛ n’abusua? Efiri sɛ bɛafa ne nkwa afiri asaase so kɔ.”

english_verse: The official said to Philip, “Tell me, was the prophet talking about himself or about someone else?”
translation_verse: Etiopiani panyini he bisaa Filipo sɛ, “Mepa wo kyɛw, hwane ne diyifoɔ yi ɔɔka ne ho asɛm yi? Ɔno ankasa anaa nipa foforɔ?”

english_verse: So Philip began at this place in the Scriptures and explained the good news about Jesus.
translation_verse: Na Filipo biee n’ano, na ogyinaa saa twerɛsɛm heaa so kaa Yesu ho asɛm kyerɛɛ no.

english_verse: As they were going along the road, they came to a place where there was some water. The official said, “Look! Here is some water. Why can't I be baptized?”
translation_verse: Na Filipo kaa sɛ, “Sɛ wode w’akoma nyinaa gye di a adeɛ biaa nsi wo kwane.” Na Etiopiani he kaa sɛ, “Megye di sɛ Yesu Kristo yɛ Nyankopɔn Ba.”]

english_verse: He ordered the chariot to stop. Then they both went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
translation_verse: Na Filipo maa teaseɛnam he gyinaeɛ, na Filipo ne Etiopiani panyini he, siane kɔɔ nsuo he mu, na Filipo bɔɔ no asu.

english_verse: After they had come out of the water, the Lord's Spirit took Philip away. The official never saw him again, but he was very happy as he went on his way.
translation_verse: Na berɛ a befiri nsuo he mu pieeɛ he, Awurade Honhom hwim Filipo kɔeɛ, na Etiopiani he anhu no bio, na ɔde anigyeɛ kɔɔ ne kwane.

english_verse: Philip later appeared in Azotus. He went from town to town, all the way to Caesarea, telling people about Jesus.
translation_verse: Na Filipo kopiee Asdod, na ɔkɔfaa asaase he so toaa so pae mu kaa asɛmpa he kyerɛɛ nkuro he nyinaa kosii sɛ oduruu Kaesarea.

english_verse: Saul preached with such power that he completely confused the Jewish people in Damascus, as he tried to show them that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Nso Saulo kɔɔ so nyaa ahoɔdene, na ɔmaa Yudafoɔ a bɛya Damasko he ho dwirii bɛ berɛ a ɔde adanseɛ a mu da hɔ kyerɛeɛ sɛ yei ne Kristo he.

english_verse: Now get up and go into the city, where you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: Sɔre kɔ kuro he mu na bɛbɛka nea ɔsɛ sɛ woyɔ akyerɛ wo.”

english_verse: I will show him how much he must suffer for worshiping in my name.”
translation_verse: Na m’ankasa mɛkyerɛ no amaneɛ a otwa sɛ ɔba no so ɔnam me dini nti.”

english_verse: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower.
translation_verse: Oduruu Yerusalem he, ɔbɔɔ mmɔdene sɛ ɔde ne ho bɛbɔ asuafoɔ he; nso na bɛ nyinaa besuro no, efiri sɛ na bɛnnye nni sɛ ɔyɛ suani nɔkorem.

english_verse: and he has seen a vision. He saw a man named Ananias coming to him and putting his hands on him, so he could see again.”
translation_verse: na waahu beema bi a bɛfrɛ no Anania ya anisoadehunu mu sɛ waba de ne nsa abegu no so sɛnea obehu adeɛ bio”

english_verse: Suddenly something like fish scales fell from Saul's eyes, and he could see. He got up and was baptized.
translation_verse: Na amonom hɔaa bibi a ɔte sɛ ntɛ fifiri n’ani so guu fam na ohuu adeɛ, na ɔsɔree ma bɛbɔɔ no asu,

english_verse: Saul kept on threatening to kill the Lord's followers. He even went to the high priest
translation_verse: Na Saulo a na ɔda so hunahuna Awurade asuafoɔ he na ɔpɛ sɛ okuku bɛ he kɔɔ sɔfo panyini he nkyɛne

english_verse: and asked for letters to the leaders of the synagogues in Damascus. He did this because he wanted to arrest and take to Jerusalem any man or woman who had accepted the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: kɔkaa sɛ ɔmma no nkrataa a ɔde bɛkɔ Damasko hyia adane mu, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a obetumi akye biaa a obehu sɛ ɔnam gyidie Kwane He so, mmeema ne mmaa, de bɛ aba Yerusalem.

english_verse: When Saul had almost reached Damascus, a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around him.
translation_verse: Afei ɔnam kwane he so ɔɔkɔ a ɔbene Damasko he, mpofiremuaa hann bi firi soro hyerɛn twaa ne ho hyiaeɛ.

english_verse: He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying, “Saul! Saul! Why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Na ɔhwee fam, na ɔtee sɛ nne bi ɔɔka kyerɛ no sɛ, “Saulo, Saulo, mmɛ nti ne wotaa me?”

english_verse: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: Obisaa sɛ, “Wo ne hwane, owura?” Na ɔkaa sɛ, “Me ne Yesu, nea wootaa no he.

english_verse: The men with Saul stood there speechless. They had heard the voice, but they had not seen anyone.
translation_verse: Na mmeema a bɛne no nam he gyinagyina hɔ a bentumi nkasa. Nne he faa bɛ asum deɛ nso bɛanhu biaa.

english_verse: Saul got up from the ground, and when he opened his eyes, he could not see a thing. Someone then led him by the hand to Damascus,
translation_verse: Saulo sɔre firii fam, na n’ani gu so deɛ, nso na onhu hwee. Nti bɛsosɔɔ ne nsa de no kɔɔ Damasko.

english_verse: and for three days he was blind and did not eat or drink.
translation_verse: Na wanhu hwee nnansa, wannidi na wannom nso.

english_verse: A follower named Ananias lived in Damascus, and the Lord spoke to him in a vision. Ananias answered, “Lord, here I am.”
translation_verse: Na suani bi ya Damasko a bɛfrɛ no Anania, na Awurade ka kyerɛɛ no anisoadehunu mu sɛ, “Anania” Na ɔkaa sɛ, “Awurade medi.”

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the house of Judas on Straight Street. When you get there, you will find a man named Saul from the city of Tarsus. Saul is praying,
translation_verse: Awurade ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Sɔre na kɔ brɔno a bɛfrɛ no Tee he so, na kɔ Yuda fie kɔhwehwɛ beema bi a bɛfrɛ no Saulo, a ofiri Tarso he. Na hwɛ, ɔɔbɔ mpaeɛ,

english_verse: Ananias replied, “Lord, a lot of people have told me about the terrible things this man has done to your followers in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Nso Anania buaa sɛ, “Awurade maate beema yi ho asɛm afiri nnipa pii hɔ, sɛ ɔde bɔne bebree adi w’ahoteefoɔ a bɛya Yerusalem he.

english_verse: Now the chief priests have given him the power to come here and arrest anyone who worships in your name.”
translation_verse: Afei waanya tumi afiri asɔfoɔ mpanyini he nkyɛne sɛ ɔmfa bɛ a bɛbɔ wo dini he nyinaa ngu mpokyerɛ mu.”

english_verse: The Lord said to Ananias, “Go! I have chosen him to tell foreigners, kings, and the people of Israel about me.
translation_verse: Na Awurade ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Si kwane so kɔ, efiri sɛ beema yi ne nea mayi sɛ ɔmfa me dini nkɔ amanamane ne ahene ne Israel mma nkyɛne.

english_verse: Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, “Saul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Nti Anania kɔeɛ na ɔhyɛne fie he mu, na ɔde ne nsa guu no so kaa sɛ, “Me nua Saulo, Awurade Yesu a oyii ne ho adi kyerɛɛ wo kwane a na wonam so ɔɔba so he ne asoma me, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a wobehu adeɛ bio na Honhom Kronkron ahyɛ wo ma.”

english_verse: Then he ate and felt much better. Saul Preaches in Damascus For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus.
translation_verse: na odidii nyaa ahoɔdene. Na odii nna kakra asuafoɔ a bɛya Damasko he nkyɛne. Saulo kaa asɛm ya Damasko

english_verse: Soon he went to the synagogues and started telling people that Jesus is the Son of God.
translation_verse: Na ntɛmaa ofirii aseɛ hyia adane mu kaa Yesu ho asɛm sɛ “Yesu ne Nyankopɔn Ba he.”

english_verse: Everyone who heard Saul was amazed and said, “Isn't this the man who caused so much trouble for those people in Jerusalem who worship in the name of Jesus? Didn't he come here to arrest them and take them to the chief priests?”
translation_verse: Nso ɔyɛɛ bɛ a beetie no he nyinaa nwanwa, na bɛkaa sɛ, “Ɔnyɛ yei na ɔsɛe bɛ a bɛbɔ dini yi Yerusalem he? Ɔnyɛ ɔno ne ɔde botaeɛ koro heaa baa ha sɛ ɔɔbɛkye bɛ de bɛ akɔma asɔfo mpanyimfoɔ he?”

english_verse: Later some of them made plans to kill Saul,
translation_verse: Na nna pii akyi he, Yudafoɔ he bɔɔ pɔ sɛ bebeku no.

english_verse: but he found out about it. He learned that they were guarding the gates of the city day and night in order to kill him.
translation_verse: Nso Saulo huu sɛ bɛabɔ ne ho pɔ. Na bio nso na bɛwɛne apono he ano awia ne anadwo sɛ bebenya no aku no.

english_verse: Then one night his followers let him down over the city wall in a large basket. Saul in Jerusalem
translation_verse: Nti n’asuafoɔ he faa no anadwo he de no sii kɛntɛne mu de no faa kwane bi a ɔda afasuo he mu, gyaagyaa no nkakrankakra sii fam. Saulo kɔɔ Yerusalem, Kaesarea ne Tarso

english_verse: Then Barnabas helped him by taking him to the apostles. He explained how Saul had seen the Lord and how the Lord had spoken to him. Barnabas also said when Saul was in Damascus, he had spoken bravely in the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Nti Barnaba boaa no de no kɔɔ asomafoɔ he nkyɛne, na ɔkaa sɛnea ɔde akokoɔduro akasa Yesu dini mu Damasko he ho nsɛm nyinaa kyerɛɛ bɛ.

english_verse: Saul moved about freely with the followers in Jerusalem and told everyone about the Lord.
translation_verse: Na ɔne bɛ tenae, dii akɔneaba Yerusalem, kasaa Awurade dini mu akokoɔduro so.

english_verse: He was always arguing with the Jews who spoke Greek, and so they tried to kill him.
translation_verse: Na ɔne Yudafoɔ a bɛka Hela kasa he kasa dii anobaabaeɛ. Na bɛbɔɔ mmɔdene sɛ bebeku no.

english_verse: But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
translation_verse: Berɛ a agyidifoɔ he huu yei he, bɛde no baa Kaesarea; na bɛmaa no kɔɔ Tarso.

english_verse: The church in Judea, Galilee, and Samaria now had a time of peace and kept on worshiping the Lord. The church became stronger, as the Holy Spirit encouraged it and helped it grow. Peter Heals Aeneas
translation_verse: Saa berɛ heaa mu asafo a ɔyeya Yudea ne Galilea ne Samaria nyinaa nyaa asomdwie, na bɛyɛɛ nam. Na bere a bɛde Awurade suro ne Honhom Kronkron he awerekyekyerɛ nanteeɛ he, bɛkɔɔ so dɔreeɛ. Petro saa Aenea yadeɛ

english_verse: While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda.
translation_verse: Afei bere a Petro ookyinikyini mmeammea he, okoduruu ahoteefoɔ a bɛte Lida he nso nkyɛne.

english_verse: There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move.
translation_verse: Hɔ ne ohuu beema bi a bɛfrɛ no Aenia a wabubu ada ne mpa so mfie nwɔtwe.

english_verse: Peter said to Aeneas, “Jesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.” At once he stood up.
translation_verse: Na Petro ka kyerɛ no sɛ, “Aenia, Yesu Kristo asa wo yadeɛ. Sɔre na woaa sɛ wo kɛtɛ.” Na ntɛmaa ɔsɔreeɛ.

english_verse: Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord. Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
translation_verse: Na bɛ a bɛtete Lida ne Saron asaasetam so nyinaa huu no, na bɛdane baa Awurade nkyɛne. Tabita wunyaneeɛ

english_verse: In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means “deer.” She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor.
translation_verse: Na suani bi ya Yopa a bɛfrɛ no Tabita, a Helafoɔ ka no Doka. Na nnwuma pa ne adɔeɛ ahyɛ no ma.

english_verse: But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room.
translation_verse: Na nna he mu he, ɔbaa sɛ ɔyare wuiɛ. Nti bedwaree no de no kɔtoo dane bi mu ya abrosane so.

english_verse: Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, “Please come with us as quickly as you can!”
translation_verse: Na afei na hɔ bɛne Yopa nti, berɛ a asuafoɔ he tee sɛ Petro ya saa kuro he mu he, bɛsomaa mmeema mmienu kɔɔ ne nkyɛne kɔsrɛɛ no sɛ, “Yɛsrɛ wo ntwentwɛne wo nane ase sɛ wobɛba yɛ nkyɛne.”

english_verse: At once, Peter went with them. The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
translation_verse: Na Petro sɔre ne bɛ kɔeɛ. Na oduruu hɔ he, bɛde no kɔɔ dane he mu abrosane he so; na akunafoɔ he nyinaa de suu baa ne nkyɛne de ntaadeɛ nhyɛaseɛ ne ntaadeɛ ngugusoɔ a Doka yɔeɛ berɛ a ɔya bɛ nkyɛne he bebree kyerɛeɛ.

english_verse: After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
translation_verse: Na Petro maa biaa pie kɔɔ abɔntene, na obuu nkotodwe bɔɔ mpaeɛ, na ɔdanee ne ho hwɛɛ amu he kaa sɛ, “Tabita, sɔre!” Obiee n’ani, na n’ani bɔɔ Petro so he, ɔsɔre tenaa ase.

english_verse: He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet. Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death.
translation_verse: Ɔtenee ne nsa na ɔmaa no so, na ɔfrɛɛ ahoteefoɔ ne akunafoɔ he de Doka maa bɛ a ɔya nkwa mu.

english_verse: Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Asɛm yo trɛɛ Yopa mpɔtamu nyinaa sɛ wanyane afiri awufoɔ mu, na bebree begyee Awurade diiɛ.

english_verse: Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
translation_verse: Petro tenaa Yopa dii nna kakra Simon, nhomahyɛfoɔ no nkyɛne.

english_verse: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called “The Italian Unit.”
translation_verse: Na beema bi ya Kaesarea a bɛfrɛ no Konelio, a ɔyɛ Italia asrafodɔm ha so panyini.

english_verse: Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛ nipa a ɔwɔ ahofama na ɔne ne fiefoɔ nyinaa suro Nyankopɔn, na ɔyɛ nkurofoɔ adɔeɛ pii na ɔde adesrɛ to Nyankopɔn anim daa.

english_verse: One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name.
translation_verse: Bɛyɛ awia nnɔn mmeɛnsa berɛ mu he, ohuu Nyankopɔn bɔfoɔ pefee anisoadehunu mu sɛ waba ne nkyɛne na ɔɔfrɛɛ no sɛ, “Konelio.”

english_verse: Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, “What is this all about?” The angel answered, “God has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: Beema he hwɛɛ no dinn, na hu kaa no ma obisaa sɛ, “Awurade, ɛɛne a?” Obuaa sɛ, “Wo mpaebɔ ne w’adɔeɛ aforo soro ayɛ nkaeɛ Nyankopɔn anim.

english_verse: Now send some men to Joppa for a man named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Nti afei soma mmeema ma bɛnkɔ Yopa nkɔfrɛ Simon a bɛfrɛ no Petro he mmra.

english_verse: He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.”
translation_verse: Saa beema yi akɔsoeɛ nwomahyɛni bi a ɔde Simon a ne fie ya po he ano.”

english_verse: After saying this, the angel left. Cornelius called in two of his servants and one of his soldiers who worshiped God.
translation_verse: Berɛ a bɔfoɔ a ɔne no kasaeɛ he kɔeɛaa pɛ, ɔfrɛɛ ne fie nkoa mmienu ne sraani bi a ɔwɔ ahofama firii bɛ a bɛsom no daa he mu,

english_verse: He explained everything to them and sent them off to Joppa.
translation_verse: na ɔkaa bibiaa kyerɛɛ bɛ somaa bɛ kɔɔ Yopa.

english_verse: The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray
translation_verse: Adeɛ kyeeɛ a bɛnam kwane so ɔɔkɔ na bɛɛyɛ aduru kuro he mu he, Petro kɔɔ dane he atifi bɛyɛ awia dummienu berɛ mu kɔbɔɔ mpaeɛ.

english_verse: and became very hungry. While the food was being prepared, he fell sound asleep and had a vision.
translation_verse: Afei kɔm dee no paa ma ɔpɛɛ sɛ odidi. Berɛ a begu so ɔɔyɛ aduane he, onyaa anisoadehunu

english_verse: He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners.
translation_verse: na ohuu sɛ soro abie na adeɛ bi a ɔte sɛ nwera ntoma kɛseɛ bi oosiane firi soro ɔɔba asaase so te sɛ nea nnipa binom kitakita anoano nnan he na beegyaagyaa mu.

english_verse: In it were all kinds of animals, reptiles, and birds.
translation_verse: Na ntɔteboa ne mmoa a bɛwea asaase so ne wiem nnomaa nyinaa bi yam.

english_verse: A voice said to him, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Na ɔtee nne bi sɛ, “Petro, sɔre ku bi na di!”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Nso Petro kaa sɛ, “Daabi da, Awurade, efiri sɛ mennii bibi a ho agu fi anaa ho nte biaa da.”

english_verse: The voice spoke to him again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Na nne he ka kyerɛɛ no ne mprenu so sɛ, “Mfrɛ nnoɔma a Nyankopɔn ate ho sɛ ho agu fi.”

english_verse: This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
translation_verse: Yei baa ne mprɛnsa so, na hɔaa bɛfaa adeɛ a ɔte sɛ nwera he kɔɔ soro.

english_verse: Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a anisoadehunu he nti na Petro tim ayɛ no kesenenee he, hwɛ, na mmeema a Konelio somaa bɛ he abisabisa Simon fie kwane ma bɛabegyina pono he ano.

english_verse: and were asking if Simon Peter was staying there.
translation_verse: Na bɛfrɛ bisaa sɛ ebia Simon a bɛfrɛ no Petro he abɛsoeɛ hɔ.

english_verse: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, “Three men are here looking for you.
translation_verse: Berɛ a Petro gu so ɔɔdwennwene anisoadehunu he ho he, Honhom he kaa sɛ, “Hwɛ, nnipa mmeɛnsa bi ɔɔhwehwɛ wo.

english_verse: Hurry down and go with them. Don't worry, I sent them.”
translation_verse: Wo deɛ, sɔre, si fam na wo ne bɛ nkɔ berɛ a wontwentwɛne wo nane ase, efiri sɛ me ne m’asoma bɛ.”

english_verse: Peter went down and said to the men, “I am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”
translation_verse: Nti Petro sii fam kɔɔ mmeema he nkyɛne kɔkaa sɛ, “Hwɛ, mene nea hoohwehwɛ no he. Ɛɛne nti ne ho baa ha?”

english_verse: They answered, “Captain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.”
translation_verse: Bɛkaa sɛ, “Bɔfoɔ kronkron bi de Nyankopɔn akwankyerɛ maa Konelio, asraafoɔ ha so hwɛfoɔ, teneneeni a osuro Nyankopɔn, a Yudafoɔ nyinaa bɔ no dini pa he sɛ ɔnsoma ma bɛmmɛfa wo mmra ne fie na ontie nea wowɔ ka.”

english_verse: Peter invited them to spend the night. The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius.
translation_verse: Nti ɔde bɛ baa fie na ɔsom bɛ sɛ ahohoɔ. Adeɛ kyeeɛ he ɔsɔre ne bɛ kɔeɛ, na agyidifoɔ binom a befiri Yopa ka bɛ ho.

english_verse: The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
translation_verse: Da a odi hɔ he, oduruu Kaesarea. Na Konelio nso afrɛfrɛ n’abusuafoɔ ne ne nnamfonom nyinaa ɔɔtwɛne bɛ.

english_verse: When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him.
translation_verse: Berɛ a Petro piee hɔ he, Konelio kohyiaa no hwee ne nane ase sɔree no.

english_verse: But Peter took hold of him and said, “Stand up! I am nothing more than a human.”
translation_verse: Nso Petro maa no so kaa sɛ, “Sɔre; me nso meyɛ nipa te sɛ woaa.”

english_verse: As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there,
translation_verse: Na berɛ a ɔne no gu so ɔɔbɔ nkɔmmɔ he, ɔkɔɔ fie he mu kohuu sɛ nnipa bebree ahyiam,

english_verse: and Peter said to them, “You know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit.
translation_verse: na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Ho ankasa honim pefee sɛ mmara mma kwane sɛ Yudani ne abusua foforɔ munii bɛbɔ anaa ɔbɛbɛne no, nso Nyankopɔn akyerɛ me sɛ ɔnsɛ sɛ mefrɛ nipa biaa sɛ ne ho agu fi anaa ne ho nte.

english_verse: I agreed to come here, but I want to know why you sent for me.”
translation_verse: Ne saa nti bere a bɛsoma bɛfrɛɛ me he, manyi ano koraa ne mebaeɛ. Afei, mmɛ nti ne hosoma ma bɛfrɛɛ me.”

english_verse: Cornelius answered: Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me.
translation_verse: Na Konelio kaa sɛ, “Nnannan di, saa berɛ yi muaa, na meya me fie awia nnɔn mmeɛnsa berɛ mu ɔɔbɔ mpaeɛ, na hwɛ, beema bi a ɔhyɛ ataadeɛ hyerɛnhyerɛn begyinaa m’anim.

english_verse: He said, “Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and he knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: Na ɔkaa sɛ, ‘Konelio Nyankopɔn atie wo mpaebɔ na w’adɔeɛ aduru n’anim.

english_verse: Now send to Joppa for Simon Peter. He is visiting in the home of Simon the leather maker, who lives near the sea.”
translation_verse: Nti soma kɔ Yopa kɔfrɛ Simon a bɛfrɛ no Petro he. Saa beema yi abɛsoeɛ Simon a ɔyɛ nwomahyɛni he fie po he ano.’

english_verse: I sent for you at once, and you have been good enough to come. All of us are here in the presence of the Lord God, so that we can hear what he has to say.
translation_verse: Nti ntɛmaa na mesoma ma bɛbaa wo nkyɛne, na woabɔ mmɔdene sɛ woaba. Yei nti yɛ nyinaa ahyiam Nyankopɔn anim saa bere yi sɛ yeebetie nea Awurade ahyɛ wo sɛ ka nyinaa.”

english_verse: Peter then said: Now I am certain that God treats all people alike.
translation_verse: Na Petro biee n’ano kaa sɛ, “Nɔkwasɛm di, maahu sɛ Nyankopɔn nyɛ nyiyimu,

english_verse: God is pleased with everyone who worships him and does right, no matter what nation they come from.
translation_verse: na mmom mane biaa mu he, nipa a osuro no na ɔyɔ adetenenee he, ɔgye no tom.

english_verse: This is the same message that God gave to the people of Israel, when he sent Jesus Christ, the Lord of all, to offer peace to them.
translation_verse: Ɔde asɛm he kɔmaa Israel mma, na ɔkaa asomdwie a ɔnam Yesu Kristo so ba he ho asɛm pa kyerɛɛ bɛ, yei ne nnipa nyinaa wura.

english_verse: You surely know what happened everywhere in Judea. It all began in Galilee after John had told everyone to be baptized.
translation_verse: Saa asɛm yi trɛɛ Yudea nyinaa, ofiri aseɛ Galilea berɛ a Yohane kaa asubɔ ho asɛm he akyi.

english_verse: God gave the Holy Spirit and power to Jesus from Nazareth. He was with Jesus, as he went around doing good and healing everyone who was under the power of the devil.
translation_verse: Yesu Nasareni he, Nyankopɔn de Honhom Kronkron ne tumi hyɛɛ no ma, na okyinikyinii asaase he so yɔɔ papa, na ɔsaa bɛ a Bronsam hyɛ bɛ so nyinaa yadeɛ, efiri sɛ na Nyankopɔn ka ne ho.

english_verse: We all saw what Jesus did both in Israel and in the city of Jerusalem. Jesus was put to death on a cross.
translation_verse: Yɛyɛ nnwuma a ɔyɔeɛ ya Yudea ne Yerusalem nyinaa ho adansefoɔ. Bɛde no sɛnee asennua so kuu no.

english_verse: But three days later, God raised him to life and let him be seen.
translation_verse: Nso Nyankopɔn nyanee no da a ɔtɔ so mmeɛnsa na ɔmaa no kwane sɛ onyi ne ho adi nkyerɛ,

english_verse: Not everyone saw him. He was seen only by us, who ate and drank with him after he was raised from death. We were the ones God chose to tell others about him.
translation_verse: ɔnyɛ nnipa nyinaa, na mmom bɛ a Nyankopɔn ayi bɛ sɛ adansefoɔ he, na ne wusɔreɛ akyi he bɛ ne no didiiɛ, na bɛnomeeɛ.

english_verse: God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead.
translation_verse: Ɔhyɛɛ yɛ sɛ yɛnka nkyerɛ mane he na yenni adanseɛ sɛ ɔno ne Nyankopɔn ayi no sɛ ateasefoɔ ne awufoɔ nyinaa temmufoɔ.

english_verse: Every one of the prophets has said that all who have faith in Jesus will have their sins forgiven in his name.
translation_verse: Adiyifoɔ he nyinaa dii ne ho adanseɛ sɛ biaa a ɔgye no di he benya bɔne fakyɛ ne dini mu.”

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening.
translation_verse: Petro gu so ɔɔkasa he, Honhom Kronkron siane baa bɛ a bɛtee asɛm he nyinaa so.

english_verse: Some Jewish followers of the Lord had come with Peter, and they were surprised that the Holy Spirit had been given to Gentiles.
translation_verse: Agyidifoɔ a bɛatwa bɛ twetia a na bɛ ne Petro nam he ho dwirii bɛ sɛ akyɛdeɛ a ɔyɛ Honhom Kronkron he asiane agu amanamane mufoɔ mpo so,

english_verse: Now they were hearing Gentiles speaking unknown languages and praising God. Peter said,
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, bɛtee sɛ bɛɛka kasa foforɔ na beeyi Nyankopɔn ayɛ. Na Petro bisaa sɛ,

english_verse: “These Gentiles have been given the Holy Spirit, just as we have! I am certain that no one would dare stop us from baptizing them.”
translation_verse: “Hwane ne betumi asi yeinom a bɛanya Honhom Kronkron sɛ yɛaa yi ho kwane sɛ yɛmfa nsuo mmɔ bɛ asu?”

english_verse: Peter ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, and they asked him to stay on for a few days.
translation_verse: Nti ɔhyɛɛ ma bɛbɔɔ bɛ asu Yesu Kristo dini mu. Afei bɛsrɛɛ no sɛ onni nna kakra bɛ nkyɛne.

english_verse: The apostles and the followers in Judea heard that Gentiles had accepted God's message.
translation_verse: Afei asomafoɔ he ne agyidifoɔ a bɛya Yudea he tee sɛ amanamane mufoɔ he nso agye Nyankopɔn asɛm.

english_verse: So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and
translation_verse: Nti berɛ a Petro baa Yerusalem he, bɛ a bekura twetiatwa nhyehyɛeɛ mu he kasakasaeɛ

english_verse: they said, “You stayed in the homes of Gentiles, and you even ate with them!”
translation_verse: sɛ, “Ɛɛne nti ne wokɔɔ bɛ a bentwaa bɛ twetia fie ne bɛ kodidiiɛ yi?”

english_verse: Then Peter told them exactly what had happened:
translation_verse: Na Petro hyɛɛ aseɛ kyerɛkyerɛɛ nsɛm he mu mmaako mmaako kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ,

english_verse: I was in the town of Joppa and was praying when I fell sound asleep and had a vision. I saw heaven open, and something like a huge sheet held by its four corners came down to me.
translation_verse: “Na meya Yopa kuro mu ɔɔbɔ mpaeɛ, na afei bibi tɔɔ me so ma menyaa anisoadehunu bi, adeɛ bi a ɔte sɛ nwera ntoma kɛseɛ bi siane firii soro baa asaase so te sɛ nea nnipa binom kita anoano nnan he na beegyaagyaa mu, na ɔbɛnee me.

english_verse: When I looked in it, I saw animals, wild beasts, reptiles, and birds.
translation_verse: Mehwɛɛ mu he, mehuu asaase so ntɔteboa ne nkekaboa ne mmoa a bɛwea ne wiem nnomaa.

english_verse: I heard a voice saying to me, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Afei metee sɛ nne bi dɛ, ‘Petro, sɔre ku bi na di.’

english_verse: But I said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never taken a bite of anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Na mekaa sɛ, ‘Daabi da, Awurade, efiri sɛ mennii bibiaa ho agu fi anaa ho nte da.’

english_verse: The voice from heaven spoke to me again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Nso ne mprenu so he, nne he kasa firii soro sɛ, ‘Nea Nyankopɔn ate ho he, ɔnsɛ sɛ wofrɛ no sɛ ho agu fi.’

english_verse: This happened three times before it was all taken back into heaven.
translation_verse: Yei sii mprɛnsa, na afei bɛtwee bibiaa sane kɔɔ soro.

english_verse: Suddenly three men from Caesarea stood in front of the house where I was staying.
translation_verse: Saa berɛ heaa, mmeema mmeɛnsa a bɛasoma bɛ firi Kaesarea sɛ bɛmmra me nkyɛne begyinaa fie a yɛte mu he aboboɔ ano.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit told me to go with them and not to worry. Then six of the Lord's followers went with me to the home of a man
translation_verse: Nti Honhom he ka kyerɛɛ me sɛ me ne bɛ nkɔ, menntwentwɛne me nane ase koraa. Na anuanom nsia yi nso ne me kɔeɛ, na yɛhyɛne beema he fie.

english_verse: who told us that an angel had appeared to him. The angel had ordered him to send to Joppa for someone named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Ɔka kyerɛɛ yɛ sɛ, ohuu bɔfoɔ sɛ ogyina ne fie a ɔɔka sɛ, ‘Soma nnipa kɔ Yopa ma bɛnkɔfrɛ Simon a bɛfrɛ no Petro he mmra,

english_verse: Then Peter would tell him how he and everyone in his house could be saved.
translation_verse: na ɔbɛka nea ɔbɛma bɛagye wo ne wo fiefoɔ nyinaa nkwa akyerɛ wo.’

english_verse: After I started speaking, the Holy Spirit was given to them, just as the Spirit had been given to us at the beginning.
translation_verse: Na bere a mefirii aseɛ sɛ meekasa he, Honhom Kronkron baa bɛ so sɛnea yɛ nso ɔbaa yɛ so mfitiaseɛ he.

english_verse: I remembered that the Lord had said, “John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Na mekae Awurade asɛm he, sɛnea na ɔtaa ka sɛ, ‘Yohane deɛ, ɔde nsuo ne bɔɔ asu, nso ho deɛ bɛde Honhom kronkron ne bɛbɔ ho asu.’

english_verse: God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?
translation_verse: Nti sɛ Nyankopɔn maa bɛ akyɛdeɛ korɔ heaa sɛnea ɔmaa yɛ a yɛagye Awurade Yesu Kristo adi he a, mene hwane a metumi asi Nyankopɔn kwane?”

english_verse: When they heard Peter say this, they stopped arguing and started praising God. They said, “God has now let Gentiles turn to him, and he has given life to them!” The Church in Antioch
translation_verse: Bɛtee yei he, bɛgye toom. Na beyii Nyankopɔn ayɛ sɛ, “Hedeɛ Nyankopɔn ama amanamane mufoɔ nso anya adwensakra a ɔkɔ nkwa mu.” Amanamane mu asafo a odi kane

english_verse: Some of the Lord's followers had been scattered because of the terrible trouble that started when Stephen was killed. They went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they told the message only to the Jews.
translation_verse: Afei bɛ a ahohiahia a ɔnam Stefano so baeɛ he maa bɛbɔ hweteeɛ he kɔeɛaa kɔfaa Foinike ne Kipro ne Antiokia, nso bɛanka asɛm he ankyerɛ biaa sɛ Yudafoɔ nkoaa.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Cyprus and Cyrene went to Antioch and started telling Gentiles the good news about the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Na binom ya bɛ mu a bɛyɛ Kiprofoɔ ne Kirenefoɔ a bɛaba Antiokia. Saa nnipa yi pae mu kaa Yesu ho asɛmpa kyerɛɛ nnipa a bɛka Hela kasa he.

english_verse: The Lord's power was with them, and many people turned to the Lord and put their faith in him.
translation_verse: Na Awurade nsa ya bɛ so, na nnipa pii bɛyɛɛ agyidifoɔ dane baa Awurade nkyɛne.

english_verse: News of what was happening reached the church in Jerusalem. Then they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
translation_verse: Bɛ ho asɛm duruu asafo a ɔya Yerusalem he nkyɛne, na bɛsomaa Barnaba kɔɔ Antiokia.

english_verse: When Barnabas got there and saw how God had blessed them with undeserved grace, he was very glad. So he begged them to remain faithful to the Lord with all their hearts.
translation_verse: Berɛ a oduruu hɔ na ohuu Nyankopɔn adom he, odii ahurusie na ofiri aseɛ hyɛɛ bɛ nyinaa nkurane sɛ bɛnkɔ so mfa akoma a onhinhim ntena Awurade mu daa.

english_verse: Barnabas was a good man of great faith, and he was filled with the Holy Spirit. Many more people turned to the Lord.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛ nipa pa na Honhom Kronkron ne gyidie ayɛ no ma. Na nnipadɔm gyee Awurade diiɛ.

english_verse: Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul.
translation_verse: Na afei Barnaba kɔɔ Tarso kɔhwehwɛɛ Saulo,

english_verse: He found Saul and brought him to Antioch, where they met with the church for a whole year and taught many of its people. There in Antioch the Lord's followers were first called Christians.
translation_verse: na ohuu no he, ɔde no baa Antiokia. Nti ɔbaa sɛ afe mũ he nyinaa he, bɛne asafo he hyia, na bɛkyerɛkyerɛɛ nnipadɔm pii, na Antiokia ne bedii kane frɛɛ asuafoɔ he Akristofoɔ.

english_verse: During this time some prophets from Jerusalem came to Antioch.
translation_verse: Saa nna he mu he, adiyifoɔ firi Yerusalem baa Antiokia.

english_verse: One of them was Agabus. Then with the help of the Spirit, he told that there would be a terrible famine everywhere in the world. And it happened when Claudius was Emperor.
translation_verse: Bɛ mu baako a bɛfrɛ no Agabo sɔreeɛ, na ɔnam Honhom so hyɛɛ nkɔm sɛ kɔm kɛseɛ bi ɔɔba asaase nyinaa so, na nkɔmhyɛ yi baa mu Klaudio berɛ so.

english_verse: The followers in Antioch decided to send whatever help they could to the followers in Judea.
translation_verse: Nti asuafoɔ he nyinaa bɔɔ bɛ adwene pɔ sɛ bɛde nnoɔma biaa bebetumi ayi bɛkɔ akɔsom agyidifoɔ a bɛya Yudea he,

english_verse: So they appointed Barnabas and Saul to take their gifts to the church leaders in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: na bɛyɔɔ saa, somaa Barnaba ne Saulo ma bɛde kɔmaa mpanyimfoɔ he.

english_verse: At that time King Herod caused terrible suffering for some members of the church.
translation_verse: Saa berɛ he a mu ne hene Herode tuu n’ani sii nnipa a bɛya asafo he mu binom so dii bɛ ani.

english_verse: He ordered soldiers to cut off the head of James, the brother of John.
translation_verse: Ɔmaa bɛde sekane kuu Yohane nua Yakobo.

english_verse: When Herod saw that this pleased the Jewish people, he had Peter arrested during the Festival of Thin Bread.
translation_verse: Na ohuu sɛ nea wayɔ he asɔ Yudafoɔ he ani he, ɔkɔkyee Petro nso.

english_verse: He put Peter in jail and ordered four squads of soldiers to guard him. Herod planned to put him on trial in public after the festival.
translation_verse: Na ne nsa kaa no he, ɔde no too afiase de no hyɛɛ akuo nnan a asraafoɔ nnan ya mu biaa mu nsa sɛ bɛnwɛne no nnidisoɔ nnidisoɔ, a na wayɛ n’adwene sɛ, Twam Afahyɛ he akyi he, ɔde no bɛma nkurofoɔ he.

english_verse: While Peter was being kept in jail, the church never stopped praying to God for him. Peter Is Rescued
translation_verse: Berɛ a Petro da afiase he, asafo he bɔɔ mpaeɛ aniberɛ so kyerɛɛ Nyankopɔn maa no.

english_verse: The night before Peter was to be put on trial, he was asleep and bound by two chains. A soldier was guarding him on each side, and two other soldiers were guarding the entrance to the jail.
translation_verse: Saa anadwo heaa a Herode de Petro ɔɔbɛma nkurofoɔ he, na Petro da asraafoɔ mmienu ntam a nkɔnsɔnkɔnsɔn mmienu gu ne nsa, na awɛmfoɔ ya pono he ano ɔɔbɔ deduafie he ho bane.

english_verse: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared, and light flashed around in the cell. The angel poked Peter in the side and woke him up. Then he said, “Quick! Get up!” The chains fell off his hands,
translation_verse: Mpofiremuaa Awurade bɔfoɔ yii ne ho adi na hann bi hyerɛnn afiase dane he mu. Ɔbɔɔ Petro nkyɛne mu nyanee no kaa sɛ, “Sɔre ntɛm.” Na ne nkɔnsɔnkɔnsɔn he firii ne nsa guu fam.

english_verse: and the angel said, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” Peter did what he was told. Then the angel said, “Now put on your coat and follow me.”
translation_verse: Bɔfoɔ he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Bɔ w’abɔsoɔ na hyɛ wo mpaboa.” Na Petro yɔɔ saa. Afei ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Fa wo ntoma he kyekyere wo ho na di m’akyi.”

english_verse: Peter left with the angel, but he thought everything was only a dream.
translation_verse: Petro pie dii n’akyi, na wanhu sɛ nea na bɔfoɔ he ɔɔboa no ama no ayɔ he yɛ asɛm paa, na ɔdwene sɛ ɔyɛ anisoadehunu.

english_verse: They went past the two groups of soldiers, and when they came to the iron gate to the city, it opened by itself. They went out and were going along the street, when all at once the angel disappeared.
translation_verse: Na betwaam awɛmfoɔ agyinaeɛ a odi kane ne nea ɔtɔ so mmienu koduruu dadeɛ pono a ɔkɔ kuro he mu he ano he, ɔno ankasa biee maa bɛ. Na bepieeɛ he, bekosii tenpono baako so, na hɔaa bɔfoɔ he gyaa no hɔ kɔeɛ.

english_verse: Peter now realized what had happened, and he said, “I am certain that the Lord sent his angel to rescue me from Herod and from everything the Jewish leaders planned to do to me.”
translation_verse: Na Petro ani baa ne ho so he, ɔkaa sɛ, “Afei ne mahu sɛ Awurade ankasa ne somaa ne bɔfoɔ beyii me firii Herode nsam ne nea na Yudafoɔ he ani da so sɛ bɛde bɛyɔ me nyinaa mu.” Asafo he anigyeɛ

english_verse: Then Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John whose other name was Mark. Many of the Lord's followers had come together there and were praying.
translation_verse: Na ohuu yei he, ntɛmaa ɔkɔɔ Yohane a na ɔsane de Marko he maame Maria fie. Na nnipa pii ahyia hɔ ɔɔbɔ mpaeɛ.

english_verse: Peter knocked on the gate, and a servant named Rhoda came to answer.
translation_verse: Bere a ɔbɔɔ abɔntene pono he mu he, abaawa bi a bɛfrɛ no Rode baeɛ.

english_verse: When she heard Peter's voice, she was too excited to open the gate. She ran back into the house and said Peter was standing there.
translation_verse: Na ɔtee Petro nne he, anigyeɛ nti, wammie pono he na mmom otuu mmirika kɔbɔɔ amaneɛ sɛ Petro gyina pono he akyi.

english_verse: “You are crazy!” everyone told her. But she kept saying it was Peter. Then they said, “It must be his angel.”
translation_verse: Bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “W’adwene mu ka wo!” Nso ɔkɔɔ so kaa sɛ asɛm he yɛ nɔkorɛ. Na bɛkaa sɛ, “ɔyɛ Petro samane.”

english_verse: But Peter kept on knocking, until finally they opened the gate. They saw him and were completely amazed.
translation_verse: Afei Petro kɔɔ soaa bɔɔ pono he mu, na bebiee no. Behuu no he, bɛ ho dwirii bɛ.

english_verse: Peter motioned for them to keep quiet. Then he told how the Lord had led him out of jail. He also said, “Tell James and the others what has happened.” After that, he left and went somewhere else.
translation_verse: Na ɔde ne nsa yɔɔ nsɛnkyerɛnee kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ bɛnyɛ dinn. Na ɔkyerɛɛ bɛ kwane a Awurade nam so yii no firii afiase hɔ. Na ɔkaa bio nso sɛ, “Honkɔka yei nkyerɛ Yakobo ne agyidifoɔ he.” Na ofirii hɔ kɔɔ baabi foforɔ.

english_verse: The next morning the soldiers who had been on guard were terribly worried and wondered what had happened to Peter.
translation_verse: Adeɛ kyeeɛ he, nea na asi fa Petro ho he nti, asraafoɔ he ani so yɛɛ krakrakrakra.

english_verse: Herod ordered his own soldiers to search for him, but they could not find him. Then he questioned the guards and had them put to death. After this, Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while. Herod Dies
translation_verse: Berɛ a Herode hwehwɛeɛ na wanhu Petro he, obisabisa awɛmfoɔ he nea osiiɛ, na ɔhyɛɛ sɛ bɛmfa bɛ nkɔku bɛ. Na Herode firii Yudea kɔɔ Kaesarea kodii nna kakra ya hɔ. Herode wuo a oo’hu

english_verse: Herod and the people of Tyre and Sidon were very angry with each other. But their country got its food supply from the region that he ruled. So a group of them went to see Blastus, who was one of Herod's high officials. They convinced Blastus that they wanted to make peace between their cities and Herod,
translation_verse: Afei nso Herode ani beree Tirofoɔ ne Sidonfoɔ so. Nti bɛyɛɛ anokorɔ baa ne nkyɛne, na berɛ a bɛmaa Blasto a ɔhwɛ hene daberɛ so tee bɛ aseɛ he, befirii aseɛ srɛɛ hwehwɛɛ asomdwie, efiri sɛ na hene he asaase so na mane he nya bɛ aduane.

english_verse: and a day was set for them to meet with him. Herod came dressed in his royal robes. He sat down on his throne and made a speech.
translation_verse: Na da a bɛahyɛ he duriiɛ he, Herode de ahentaadeɛ hyehyɛɛ ne ho, na ɔtenaa atemmuo adwa so firi aseɛ kasa kyerɛɛ bɛ.

english_verse: The people shouted, “You speak more like a god than a man!”
translation_verse: Nkurofoɔ a bɛahyiam he nso firii aseɛ teateaam sɛ, “Ɔnyɛ nipa nne di, na mmom ɔyɛ nyame bi nne!”

english_verse: At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
translation_verse: Hɔaa ne Awurade bɔfoɔ bɔɔ no, efiri sɛ wamfa animuonyam amma Nyankopɔn, na ɔdɔree mmoa wuiɛ. Barnaba ne Saulo sane baa Antiokia

english_verse: God's message kept spreading.
translation_verse: Nso Awurade asɛm nyaa nkɔsoɔ na ɔtrɛɛe.

english_verse: And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Na Barnaba ne Saulo wiee bɛ dwumadie he, bɛsane baa Yerusalem bɛfaa Yohane a bɛfrɛ no Marko he kaa bɛ ho sane baeɛ.

english_verse: The church at Antioch had several prophets and teachers. They were Barnabas, Simeon, also called Niger, Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen, who was Herod's close friend, and Saul.
translation_verse: Antiokia asafo he mu he, na adiyifoɔ ne akyerɛkyerɛfoɔ ya mu, Barnaba ne Simeon a bɛfrɛ no Niger he, ne Lukio a ofiri Kirene, ne Manaen a bɛtetee ɔne Herode a ɔyɛ mantam sodifoɔ he, ne Saulo.

english_verse: While they were worshiping the Lord and going without eating, the Holy Spirit told them, “Appoint Barnabas and Saul to do the work for which I have chosen them.”
translation_verse: Berɛ a bɛɛsom Awurade na bɛɛyɔ akɔmkyene he, Honhom Kronkron he kaa sɛ, “Honyi Barnaba ne Saulo mma me, na bɛnkɔ yɔ adwuma a mafrɛ bɛ sɛ bɛmmɛyɔ he.”

english_verse: After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus.
translation_verse: Nti, saa mmeema yi a Honhom Kronkron asoma bɛ he kɔɔ Seleukia, na befirii hɔ de po so hyɛne kɔɔ Kipro.

english_verse: They arrived at Salamis and began to preach God's message in the synagogues. They also had John as a helper.
translation_verse: Berɛ a beduru Salami he, befirii aseɛ bɔɔ Nyankopɔn asɛm he dawuro Yudafoɔ hyia adane mu. Na Yohane boaa bɛ bɛ dwumadie mu. Sergio Paulo ne Elima

english_verse: Barnabas and Saul went all the way to the city of Paphos on the other end of the island, where they met a Jewish man named Bar-Jesus. He practiced witchcraft and was a false prophet.
translation_verse: Berɛ a bɛfaa supɔ he so koduruu Pafo he, behyiaa Yudani beema bi a ɔyɛ nkonyaayifoɔ, diyifoɔ torɔfoɔ bi a bɛfrɛ no Bar-Yesu,

english_verse: He also worked for Sergius Paulus, who was very smart and was the governor of the island. Sergius Paulus wanted to hear God's message, and he sent for Barnabas and Saul.
translation_verse: na ɔya Amrado Sergio Paulo a ɔyɛ nyansani he nkyɛne. Berɛ a bɛfrɛɛ Barnaba ne Saulo maa no he, amrado he hwehwɛɛ sɛ ɔbɛte Nyankopɔn asɛm.

english_verse: But Bar-Jesus, whose other name was Elymas, was against them. He even tried to keep the governor from having faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Nso na nkonyaayifoɔ Elima ya hɔ; ofirii aseɛ ne bɛ dii asie, pɛɛ sɛ ɔdane amrado he firi gyidie he ho.

english_verse: Then Saul, better known as Paul, was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked straight at Elymas
translation_verse: Afei Saulo a na bɛsane frɛ no Paulo a na Honhom Kronkron ahyɛ no ma he hwɛɛ no gyee

english_verse: and said, “You son of the devil! You are a liar, a crook, and an enemy of everything that is right. When will you stop speaking against the true ways of the Lord?
translation_verse: kaa sɛ, “Wo, beema a asisie ahyɛ wo ma, Bronsam ba, a wotane tenenee nyinaa, wonnyae sɛ wokyea Awurade kwane a ɔtene he?

english_verse: The Lord is going to punish you by making you completely blind for a while.” Suddenly the man's eyes were covered by a dark mist, and he went around trying to get someone to lead him by the hand.
translation_verse: Afei tie! Awurade nsa ya me so, na w’ani befira, a wonhu awia hann kosi berɛ bi.” Amonom hɔaa Bar-Yesu ani so yɛɛ no kusuu na sum duruu no, na okyini hwehwɛɛ nnipa a bɛbɛsɔ ne nsa akyerɛ no kwane.

english_verse: When the governor saw what had happened, he was amazed at this teaching about the Lord. So he put his faith in the Lord. Paul and Barnabas in Antioch of Pisidia
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a amrado he huu nea asi he, ɔgye diiɛ, efiri sɛ Awurade ho nkyerɛkyerɛ he yɛɛ no ahodwiri.

english_verse: Paul and the others left Paphos and sailed to Perga in Pamphylia. But John left them and went back to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Afei mmeema he ne Paulo faa po so firii Pafo kɔɔ Perge a ɔya Pamfilia. Na Yohane deɛ, ɔne bɛ dii ntete mu sane kɔɔ Yerusalem.

english_verse: The rest of them went on from Perga to Antioch in Pisidia. Then on the Sabbath they went to the synagogue and sat down.
translation_verse: Nso bɛde bɛ firii Perge baa Antiokia a ɔya Pisidia, na bɛkɔɔ hyiadane mu homeda kɔtenaa ase.

english_verse: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the leaders sent someone over to tell Paul and Barnabas, “Friends, if you have anything to say that will help the people, please say it.”
translation_verse: Bɛkenkanee mmara ne adiyifoɔ he badwam wieeɛ he, hyiadane mu mpanyimfoɔ he somaa nnipa kɔɔ bɛ nkyɛne sɛ, “Anuanom, sɛ howɔ nkuranhyɛsɛm bi ma nnipa yi a, honka.”

english_verse: Paul got up. He motioned with his hand and said: People of Israel, and everyone else who worships God, listen!
translation_verse: Nti Paulo sɔre nyam ne nsa kaa sɛ: “Ho Israelfoɔ ne ho a hosuro Nyankopɔn, hontie!

english_verse: The God of Israel chose our ancestors, and he let our people prosper while they were living in Egypt. Then with his mighty power he led them out,
translation_verse: Israel mane yi, Nyankopɔn yii yɛ agyanom, na ɔmaa bɛ so berɛ a na bɛyɛ ahɔhoɔ Misraim asaase so he nam ne tumi so yii bɛ firii hɔ.

english_verse: Make sure what the prophets have said doesn't happen to you. They said,
translation_verse: Nti honhwɛ na nea Adiyifoɔ he aka he amma ho so:

english_verse: He destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave their land to our people.
translation_verse: Na ɔsɛee amane nson Kanaan asaase so akyi he, ɔkyekyɛɛ asaase he de maa bɛ sɛ bɛ agyapadeɛ;

english_verse: Later, God removed Saul and let David rule in his place. God said about him, “David the son of Jesse is the kind of person who pleases me most! He does everything I want him to do.”
translation_verse: Na oyii no firi hɔ, na ɔde Dawid bɛyɛɛ bɛ hene, na odii ne ho adanseɛ kaa sɛ, ‘Maahu Yisai ba Dawid, beema a ɔda m’akoma so, ɔno ne bɛyɔ nea mepɛ nyinaa.’

english_verse: God promised that someone from David's family would come to save the people of Israel, and this one is Jesus.
translation_verse: Saa beema yi aseni ne Yesu a Nyankopɔn yii no maa Israel sɛ Agyenkwa, sɛnea ne bɔhyɛ teɛ he.

english_verse: But before Jesus came, John was telling everyone in Israel to turn back to God and be baptized.
translation_verse: Yohane a odii Yesu anim baeɛ he kaa asubɔ a ɔyɛ adwensakra ho nsɛnkyerɛnne ho asɛm badwam kyerɛɛ Israelfoɔ nyinaa.

english_verse: Then, when John's work was almost done, he said, “Who do you people think I am? Do you think I am the Promised One? He will come later, and I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
translation_verse: Na berɛ a Yohane dwumadie ɔɔba awieeɛ he, ɔkaa sɛ, ‘Hosusu sɛ me ne Agyenkwa he anaa? Ɔnnyɛ me ne no. Nso, hwɛ, bi di m’akyi ɔɔba a memfata sɛ mesane ne nane ase mpaboa mpo.’

english_verse: Now listen, you descendants of Abraham! Pay attention, all of you Gentiles who are here to worship God! Listen to this message about how to be saved, because it is for everyone.
translation_verse: “Me nuanom, Abraham asefoɔ ne bɛ a besuro Nyankopɔn, yɛ ne bɛde nkwagyeɛ yi ho asɛm abra yɛ.

english_verse: The people of Jerusalem and their leaders didn't realize who Jesus was. And they didn't understand the words of the prophets they read each Sabbath. So they condemned Jesus just as the prophets had said.
translation_verse: Na osiane sɛ bɛ a bɛtete Yerusalem ne bɛ sodifoɔ he anhu no na bɛante adiyifoɔ he nsɛm a bɛkenkanee no Homeda biaa he ase nti, bebuu no fɔ maa adiyifoɔ he nsɛm he baa mu.

english_verse: But God raised him from death!
translation_verse: Nso Nyankopɔn nyanee no firii awufoɔ mu;

english_verse: Then for many days Jesus appeared to his followers who had gone with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. Now they are telling our people about him.
translation_verse: na bɛ a bɛne no firi Galilea kɔɔ Yerusalem he huu no nna pii, na seesei bedi ne ho adanseɛ kyerɛ nkurofoɔ.

english_verse: God made a promise to our ancestors. And we are here to tell you the good news
translation_verse: Ɔno nti, yɛɛka bɔhyɛ a bɛde maa nananom he ho asɛm akyerɛ ho,

english_verse: that he has kept this promise to us. It is just as the second Psalm says about Jesus, “You are my son because today I have become your Father.”
translation_verse: sɛ Nyankopɔn ama aba mu koraa ama yɛ a yɛyɛ bɛ mma he, efiri sɛ wanyane Yesu, sɛnea bɛatwerɛ ya Dwom a ɔtɔ so mmienu he mu sɛ, ‘Woyɛ me ba, nnɛ maayɛ w’Agya.’

english_verse: God raised Jesus from death and will never let his body decay. It is just as God said, “I will make to you the same holy promises that I made to David.”
translation_verse: Nea waka afa nyane a onyanee no firii awufoɔ mu a, ɔnsane nkɔ porɔeɛ mu he ne sɛ, ‘Mede bɔhyɛ kronkron a Dawid nyaeɛ he bɛ ma wo.’

english_verse: And in another psalm it says, “God will never let the body of his Holy One decay.”
translation_verse: Nti ɔno nso kaa no nnwom foforɔ mu sɛ, ‘Womma wo Kronkronni he honam mporɔ.’

english_verse: When David was alive, he obeyed God. Then after he died, he was buried in the family grave, and his body decayed.
translation_verse: Na Dawid deɛ, ɔyɔɔ Nyankopɔn pɛ a bɛada no adi he ya ɔno ankasa n’awoɔ ntoatoasoɔ mu, na owuiɛ, na besiee no ɔnoaa ne nananom nkyɛne, na ɔporɔeɛ;

english_verse: But God raised Jesus from death, and his body did not decay.
translation_verse: na nea Nyankopɔn ama no so he anhu porɔeɛ.

english_verse: My friends, the message is that Jesus can forgive your sins! The Law of Moses could not set you free from all your sins.
translation_verse: Nti me nuanom, honhu sɛ yɛnam nipa yi so ɔɔpae mu aka bɔne fakyɛ ho asɛm akyerɛ ho.

english_verse: “Look, you people who make fun of God! Be amazed and disappear. I will do something today that you won't believe, even if someone tells you about it!”
translation_verse: ‘Ho a hobu Nyankopɔn asɛm animtia, honhwɛ ma ɔnyɛ ho nwanwa na honyera mfiri m’anim, efiri sɛ meeyɔ adwuma bi ho berɛ so, adwuma a sɛ bi ka ho asɛm nyinaa kyerɛ ho mpo a, honnye nni.’ ”

english_verse: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people begged them to say more about these same things on the next Sabbath.
translation_verse: Paulo ne Barnaba ɔɔkɔ he, nnipa hyɛɛ bɛ nkurane sɛ bɛnka saa nsɛm yiaa homeda a odi hɔ he.

english_verse: After the service, many Jews and a lot of Gentiles who worshiped God went with them. Paul and Barnabas begged them all to remain faithful to God, who had treated them with undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Nti hyiadane he mu nhyiamu he baa awieeɛ he, Yudafoɔ he mu pii ne bɛ a bɛabɛdɔm Yudafoɔ som he na besuro Nyankopɔn he dii Paulo ne Barnaba akyi, na bɛkasa kyerɛɛ bɛ hyɛɛ bɛ nkurane sɛ bɛnkɔ so ntena Nyankopɔn adom mu.

english_verse: The next Sabbath almost everyone in town came to hear the message about the Lord.
translation_verse: Na homeda a odi hɔ he duruiɛ he, kuro mu he nyinaa hyiaa sɛ beebetie Awurade asɛm.

english_verse: When the Jewish people saw the crowds, they were very jealous. They insulted Paul and spoke against everything he said.
translation_verse: Na Yudafoɔ he huu nnipakuo he, ahoɔyea hyɛɛ bɛ ma. Na bɛkaa abususɛm de bɔɔ nea Paulo kaeɛ he abira.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas bravely said: We had to tell God's message to you before we told it to anyone else. But you rejected the message! This proves that you don't deserve eternal life. Now we are going to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Na afei Paulo ne Barnaba kasaa akokoɔduro so sɛ, “Na ohia sɛ yɛka Nyankopɔn asɛm kyerɛ ho kane. Na ɔnam sɛ hoapo asɛm he na hobu ho ho sɛ homfata daa nkwa he nti, yɛde asɛm he ɔɔkɔ Amanamane mufoɔ hɔ.

english_verse: The Lord has given us this command, “I have placed you here as a light for the Gentiles. You are to take the saving power of God to people everywhere on earth.”
translation_verse: Na saa ne Awurade ahyɛ yɛ sɛ, ‘Mede wo asi hɔ sɛ Amanamane mufoɔ kanea, na ama wode nkwagyeɛ akoduru asaase ano nohoa.’ ”

english_verse: This message made the Gentiles glad, and they praised what they had heard about the Lord. Everyone who had been chosen for eternal life then put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Berɛ a Amanamane mufoɔ tee yei he, bɛ ani gyeeɛ, na bɛhyɛɛ Awurade asɛm animuonyam, na dodoɔ a na Nyankopɔn ayi bɛ ama daa nkwa he bɛyɛɛ agyidifoɔ. Taa de bɛ kɔɔ Ikonium

english_verse: The message about the Lord spread all over this region.
translation_verse: Nti Nyankopɔn asɛm he trɛɛ pom hɔ nyinaa.

english_verse: But the Jewish leaders went to some of the important men in the town and to some respected women who were religious. They turned them against Paul and Barnabas and started making trouble for them. They even chased them out of this part of the country.
translation_verse: Na Yudafoɔ he hyɛɛ mmaa a bedi mu, ne mmeema atitire he kutupa ma bɛtaa Paulo ne Barnaba pamoo bɛ firi mpɔtamu hɔ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from that place off their feet and went on to the city of Iconium.
translation_verse: Nti Paulo ne Barnaba proproo bɛ nane ase mfuturo sɛ ɔnyɛ nsɛnkyerɛnne ntia kuro mu hɔfoɔ, na bɛkɔɔ Ikonium.

english_verse: But the Lord's followers in Antioch were very happy and were filled with the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Na anigyeɛ ne Honhom Kronkron hyɛɛ asuafoɔ he ma.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas spoke in the synagogue in Iconium, just as they had done at Antioch, and many Jews and Gentiles put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Saa asɛm korɔ heaa bi sii Ikonium. Berɛ a bɛkɔɔ Yudafoɔ hyiadane mu he bɛkasa maa nnipakuo kɛseɛ bi a befiri Yudafoɔ ne Helafoɔ mu bɛyɛɛ agyidifoɔ.

english_verse: But the Jews who did not have faith in him made the other Gentiles angry and turned them against the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Na Yudafoɔ a bɛannye anni he tutu guu amanamane mufoɔ he asom ma benyaa anuanom ho adwemmɔne.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas stayed there for a while, having faith in the Lord and bravely speaking his message. The Lord gave them the power to work miracles and wonders, and he showed that their message about his gift of undeserved grace was true.
translation_verse: Na bɛtenaa hɔ berɛ pii de akokoɔduro kasa maa Awurade, na Awurade ankasa dii n’adom ho asɛm he ho adanseɛ denam nsɛnkyerɛnne ne anwanwadeɛ ahodoɔ a ɔnam bɛ nsa so yɔeɛ he so.

english_verse: The people of Iconium did not know what to think. Some of them believed the Jewish group, and others believed the apostles.
translation_verse: Na kuro he mufoɔ he mu kyɛɛ mmienu, na binom kɔɔ Yudafoɔ he afa, na binom nso kɔɔ asomafoɔ he afa.

english_verse: Finally, some Gentiles and Jews, together with their leaders, decided to make trouble for Paul and Barnabas and to stone them to death.
translation_verse: Na berɛ a Amanamane mufoɔ ne Yudafoɔ ne bɛ sodifoɔ he nyinaa yɛɛ sɛ bɛɛbɛhyɛ bɛ aniɔyea na bɛasi bɛ aboɔ he,

english_verse: In Lystra there was a man who had been born with paralyzed feet and had never been able to walk.
translation_verse: Na beema bi ya Listra a ɔte hɔ a ontumi nnyina ne nane so, efiri sɛ na ɔyɛ bubuani firi ne maame yam.

english_verse: The man was listening to Paul speak, when Paul saw that he had faith in Jesus and could be healed. So he looked straight at the man
translation_verse: Otiee Paulo bere a na ɔɔkasa he. Na Paulo hwɛɛ no gyee na ohuu sɛ ne gyidie betumi ama ne ho ayɛ no dene,

english_verse: and shouted, “Stand up!” The man jumped up and started walking around.
translation_verse: ɔteaam kaa sɛ, “sɔre gyina hɔ kyirebenn.” Na beema he huri gyina firii aseɛ nanteɛ.

english_verse: When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they yelled out in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have turned into humans and have come down to us!”
translation_verse: Na nnipakuo he huu nea Paulo ayɔ he, bɛteaam Likonia kasa mu sɛ, “abosom he ayɛ bɛ ho sɛ nnipa asane aba yɛ nkyɛne!”

english_verse: The people then gave Barnabas the name Zeus, and they gave Paul the name Hermes, because he did the talking.
translation_verse: Bɛfrɛɛ Barnaba Seus, na Paulo nso bɛfrɛɛ no Hemes, efiri sɛ ɔno ne na ɔyɛ kasamafoɔ.

english_verse: The temple of Zeus was near the entrance to the city. Its priest and the crowds wanted to offer a sacrifice to Barnabas and Paul. So the priest brought some bulls and flowers to the city gates.
translation_verse: Na Seus a n’asɔredane ya kuro he anim he sɔfoɔ de anantwinyini ne nhwiren a bɛakyekyere baa kuro he pono ano pɛɛ sɛ ɔne nnipakuo he de bɛbɔ afɔdeɛ.

english_verse: When the two apostles found out about this, they tore their clothes in horror and ran to the crowd, shouting:
translation_verse: Na asomafoɔ Barnaba ne Paulo teeɛ he, besunsuanee bɛ ntaadeɛ mu tutuu mmirika kɔɔ nkurofoɔ he mu teaam sɛ,

english_verse: Why are you doing this? We are humans just like you. Please give up all this foolishness. Turn to the living God, who made the sky, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
translation_verse: “Anuanom, ɛɛne nti ne ho ɔɔyɔ yei? Yɛ nso yɛyɛ nnipa sɛ hoaa, na yɛka asɛmpa kyerɛ ho sɛ honnane mfiri ahuhudeɛ yi ho nkɔ Nyankopɔn a ɔte ase he nkyɛne; ɔno ne bɔɔ soro ne asaase ne po ne deɛ ɔya mu nyinaa.

english_verse: In times past, God let each nation go its own way.
translation_verse: Ɔno ne tete berɛ a atwam he, ogyae amanamane nyinaa maa bɛfaa bɛaa bɛ akwane so,

english_verse: But he showed he was there by the good things he did. God sends rain from heaven and makes your crops grow. He gives food to you and makes your hearts glad.
translation_verse: nso ɔnam ne nnwuma pa so ma nsuo tɔ firi soro ma ho, ɔma nnɔbaeɛ ba ne berɛ mu, ɔma ho aduane ne anigyeɛ ma ho bo tɔ ho yam, na ɔnam so ada ne ho adi sɛ ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn.”

english_verse: Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
translation_verse: Na nea bɛkaeɛ nyinaa akyi he, bɛka berɛɛ paa ansa na nnipakuo he oogyae afɔdeɛ a bɛpɛɛ sɛ bɛbɔ ama bɛ he bɔ.

english_verse: Some Jewish leaders from Antioch and Iconium came and turned the crowds against Paul. They hit him with stones and dragged him out of the city, thinking he was dead.
translation_verse: Na Yudafoɔ bi firi Antiokia ne Ikonium baeɛ; na nnipakuo he baa bɛ afa. Na besii Paulo aboɔ twee no firii kuro he mu susuu sɛ waawu.

english_verse: But when the Lord's followers gathered around Paul, he stood up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas went to Derbe. Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Nso berɛ a asuafoɔ he twaa ne ho hyiaeɛ he, ɔsɔre kɔɔ kuro he mu. Adeɛkyeeɛ he ɔne Barnaba kɔɔ Derbe.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas preached the good news in Derbe and won some people to the Lord. Then they went back to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch in Pisidia.
translation_verse: Na bɛpae mu kaa asɛmpa he saa kuro he so, na benyaa asuafoɔ bebree he, bɛsane baa Listra, na bɛkɔɔ Ikonium ne Antiokia.

english_verse: They encouraged the followers and begged them to remain faithful. They told them, “We have to suffer a lot before we can get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Hɔ ne bɛhyɛɛ akra he dene na bɛhyɛɛ bɛ nkuranee sɛ bɛntoa gyidie he so. Na afei bɛkaa sɛ, “ɔsɛ sɛ yɛfa taa pii mu na yɛakɔ Nyankopɔn ahennie he mu.”

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas chose some leaders for each of the churches. Then they went without eating and prayed that the Lord would take good care of these leaders who had trusted in the Lord.
translation_verse: Na beyii mpanyimfoɔ maa asafo biaa, na bɛde akɔmkyene bɔɔ mpaeɛ de bɛ hyɛɛ Awurade a bɛabenya ne mu gyidie he nsa.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas went on through Pisidia to Pamphylia,
translation_verse: Na betwaa mu Pisidia he, bɛbaa Pamfilia.

english_verse: where they preached in the town of Perga. Then they went down to Attalia
translation_verse: Bɛkaa asɛm he ya Perge he, bebeduruu Atalia.

english_verse: and sailed to Antioch in Syria. It was there that they had been placed in God's care for the work they had now completed.
translation_verse: Na befiri hɔ faa nsuo so kɔɔ Antiokia, baabi a asuafoɔ he de bɛ hyɛɛ Nyankopɔn adom ase maa adwuma a bɛakowie he.

english_verse: After arriving in Antioch, they called the church together. They told the people what God had helped them do and how he had made it possible for the Gentiles to believe.
translation_verse: Na bɛbaeɛ he, bɛfrɛɛ asafo he boaa ano, na bɛkaa nea Nyankopɔn de bɛ akɔyɛ nyinaa ne sɛnea wabie gyidie pono ama amanamane mufoɔ he.

english_verse: Then they stayed there with the followers for a long time.
translation_verse: Na bɛne asuafoɔ he nyinaa tenaa hɔ mmerɛ kakra.

english_verse: Some people came from Judea and started teaching the Lord's followers that they could not be saved, unless they were circumcised as Moses had taught.
translation_verse: Afei nnipa binom baa Yudea a na bɛɛkyerɛkyerɛ sɛ, “Sɛ bɛantwa wo twetia sɛnea Mose mmara he ka he a, wontumi nya nkwa.”

english_verse: This caused trouble, and Paul and Barnabas argued with them about this teaching. So it was decided to send Paul and Barnabas and a few others to Jerusalem to discuss this problem with the apostles and the church leaders. The Church Leaders Meet in Jerusalem
translation_verse: Na Paulo ne Barnaba ne bɛ dii anobaabaeɛ asɛm he ho wieeɛ he, beyii Paulo ne Barnaba ne nnipa binom kaa ho sɛ bɛnkɔ Yerusalem na bɛ ne asomafoɔ he ne mpanyimfoɔ he nkɔ booboo asɛm he ho.

english_verse: The men who were sent by the church went through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling how the Gentiles had turned to God. This news made the Lord's followers very happy.
translation_verse: Nti asafo he gyaa bɛ kwane he, bɛkɔfaa Foinike ne Samaria kaa adwensakra a amanamane mufoɔ he anya he ho asɛm, na bɛmaa anuanom he nyinaa ani gyee kɛse.

english_verse: When the men arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, including the apostles and the leaders. They told them everything God had helped them do.
translation_verse: Na beduruu Yerusalem he, asafo he ne asomafoɔ ne mpanyimfoɔ he gyee bɛ, na bɛkaa nea Nyankopɔn de bɛ ayɔ nyinaa kyerɛɛ bɛ.

english_verse: But some Pharisees had become followers of the Lord. They stood up and said, “Gentiles who have faith in the Lord must be circumcised and told to obey the Law of Moses.”
translation_verse: Na Farisifoɔ he mu binom a bɛagye Nyankopɔn asɛm adi he sɔre kaa sɛ, “Ɔsɛ sɛ betwa bɛ twetia na bɛhyɛ bɛ sɛ benni Mose mmara so.”

english_verse: The apostles and church leaders met to discuss this problem about Gentiles.
translation_verse: Na asomafoɔ ne mpanyimfoɔ he behyiaa sɛ bɛɛbɛdwendwene asɛm yi ho.

english_verse: They had talked it over for a long time, when Peter got up and said: My friends, you know that God decided long ago to let me be the one from your group to preach the good news to the Gentiles. God did this so they would hear and obey him.
translation_verse: Na bɛgyee ho akyinnye pii he, Petro sɔre ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Anuanom, ho ankasa honim sɛ mfitiaseɛ heaa ne Nyankopɔn yii me yɛ mu sɛ mene amanamane mufoɔ mfa m’anum asɛm so nte asɛmpa he na bɛnnye nni.

english_verse: He knows what is in everyone's heart. And he showed he had chosen the Gentiles, when he gave them the Holy Spirit, just as he had given his Spirit to us.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn a onim akoma mu he maa bɛ Honhom Kronkron sɛnea ɔde maa yɛ heaa sɛ ɔnyɛ adansedie sɛ waagye bɛ atom.

english_verse: God treated them in the same way that he treated us. They put their faith in him, and he made their hearts pure.
translation_verse: Wamma nsonsonoeɛ amma yɛ ne bɛ ntam, na mmom ɔnam bɛ gyidie so tee bɛ akoma ho.

english_verse: Now why are you trying to make God angry by placing a heavy burden on these followers? This burden was too heavy for us or our ancestors.
translation_verse: Afei mmɛ nti ne hosɔ Nyankopɔn hwɛ, sɛ hode konnua a yɛ nananom antumi ansoa to asuafoɔ he kɔno so?

english_verse: But we believe that we will be saved by the gift of undeserved grace from our Lord Jesus Christ, just as the Gentiles are.
translation_verse: Yɛgye di sɛ Awurade Yesu adom so mmom ne yɛnam nya nkwa, sɛ bɛ nso benya heaa.”

english_verse: Everyone kept quiet and listened as Barnabas and Paul told how God had given them the power to work a lot of miracles and wonders for the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Na nnipadɔm he nyinaa yɛɛ komm, na betiee Barnaba ne Paulo berɛ a bɛkaa nsɛnkyerɛnne ne anwanwadeɛ a Nyankopɔn nam bɛ so ayɔ ama amanamane mufoɔ ho asɛm.

english_verse: After they had finished speaking, James said: My friends, listen to me!
translation_verse: Na bɛkasa wieeɛ he, Yakobo buaa sɛ, “Anuanom, hontie me.”

english_verse: Simon Peter has told how God first came to the Gentiles and made some of them his own people.
translation_verse: Simon aka sɛnea kane Nyankopɔn kɔhwɛɛ amanamane he, sɛ ooyi mane bi afiri bɛ mu ama ne dini.

english_verse: This agrees with what the prophets wrote,
translation_verse: Adiyifoɔ he asɛm he si yei so, sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ,

english_verse: “I, the Lord, will return and rebuild David's fallen house. I will build it from its ruins and set it up again.
translation_verse: ‘Yei akyi he mɛsane abesi Dawid ntomadane a ahwe ase he bio, na masi nea bɛadwiri agu he bio,

english_verse: Then other nations will turn to me and be my chosen ones. I, the Lord, say this.
translation_verse: Na nnipa a aka he ne amanamane a bɛbɛbɔ me dini mu he nyinaa ahwehwɛ Awurade akyi kwane.

english_verse: And so, my friends, I don't think we should place burdens on the Gentiles who are turning to God.
translation_verse: Nti m’asɛm a mewɔ ka ne sɛ mma yɛnha amanamane mufoɔ a bɛadane aba Nyankopɔn nkyɛne he;

english_verse: We should simply write and tell them not to eat anything that has been offered to idols. They should be told not to eat the meat of any animal that has been strangled or that still has blood in it. They must also not commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: na yɛntwerɛ nkɔma bɛ sɛ, bɛntwe bɛ ho mfiri abosom afideɛ ne adwamammɔ ne mmoa a bɛanhwie bɛ mogyaa angu na bewuiɛ, ɔne mogyaa ho.

english_verse: We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues. A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
translation_verse: Na ofiri tete na Mose nyaa bɛ a bɛka ne ho asɛm nkuro nyinaa mu, na bɛkenkane no hyia adane mu homeda biaa nso. Bɛde mane nwoma kɔma amanamane asafo he

english_verse: The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Afei asomafoɔ he ne mpanyimfoɔ he ne asafo mu he nyinaa hu sɛ ɔyɛ sɛ beyi bɛ mu binom ka Paulo ne Barnaba ho ma bɛkɔ Antiokia. Bɛ a beyii bɛ he ne Yuda a bɛfrɛ no Barsaba ne Silas, mmeema a na bɛyɛ akannifoɔ anuanom he mu.

english_verse: They wrote a letter that said: We apostles and leaders send friendly greetings to all of you Gentiles who are followers of the Lord in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia.
translation_verse: Na bɛde krataa maa bɛ a na mu nsɛm ne sɛ, “Anuanom, asomafoɔ ne mpanyimfoɔ he twerɛ krataa yi de kɔma agyidifoɔ a befiri amanamane a ɔya Antiokia ne Siria ne Kilikia. Yɛkyea ho.

english_verse: We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them!
translation_verse: Esiane sɛ yɛate sɛ yɛ mu bi de nsɛm ɔɔteetee ho pɛ sɛ bɛsɛeɛ ho adwene berɛ a yɛmmaa bɛ akwankyerɛ biaa nti,

english_verse: So we met together and decided to choose some men and to send them to you along with our good friends Barnabas and Paul.
translation_verse: yɛayɛ adwenkorɔ na ɔyɛ yɛ anigye sɛ yebeyi mmeema aka yɛ adɔfoɔ Barnaba ne Paulo ho aba ho nkyɛne,

english_verse: These men have risked their lives for our Lord Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: mmeema a bɛde bɛ kra ato hɔ, wuo mu oo nkwa mu oo, ama yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo.

english_verse: We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
translation_verse: Nti ne yɛasoma Yuda ne Silas, sɛ bɛmfa bɛ anum asɛm so nka asɛm korɔ heaa nkyerɛ ho.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit has shown us that we should not place any extra burden on you.
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔ Honhom Kronkron he ne yɛ ankasa yɛ ani sɛ yɛmmfa adesoa foforɔ biaa nto ho so, gye sɛ yeinom a ohia yi,

english_verse: But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled. You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well. We send our best wishes.
translation_verse: sɛ hontwe ho ho mfiri nnoɔma a bɛde abɔ afɔdeɛ ama abosom ne mogyaa ne mmoa a bɛanhwie bɛ mogyaa angu ne adwamammɔ ho. Sɛ hotwe ho ho firi yeinom ho a, obesi ho yie. Asomdwie nka ho.”

english_verse: The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter.
translation_verse: Nti bɛsomaa bɛ kɔɔ Antiokia. Bɛfrɛɛ asafo mma he boa ano, de krataa he maa bɛ.

english_verse: When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged.
translation_verse: Na bɛkenkane wieeɛ he, bedii ahurusie nkuranhyɛsɛm he ho.

english_verse: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Na Yuda ne Silas a na bɛ nso bɛyɛ adiyifoɔ he, kaa nsɛm pii de hyɛɛ bɛ nkurane na bɛhyɛɛ bɛ dene.

english_verse: The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well.
translation_verse: Na bɛtenaa hɔ kakra he, agyidifoɔ he gyaa bɛ kwane asomdwie mu ma bɛkɔɔ bɛ a bɛsomaa bɛ he hɔ.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
translation_verse: Na Paulo ne Barnaba kɔɔ so tenaa Antiokia, na bɛ ne nnipa foforɔ binom kyerɛkyerɛeɛ na bɛkaa Awurade asɛm. Silas sii Barnaba ananmu

english_verse: Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, “Let's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.”
translation_verse: Nna bi akyi he Paulo ka kyerɛɛ Barnaba sɛ, “Bra, na yɛnsane nkɔ na yɛnkɔsra agyidifoɔ a bɛya nkuro he mu a yɛkaa Awurade asɛm kyerɛɛ bɛ he na yɛnhwɛ sɛnea bɛ ho teɛ.”

english_verse: Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Barnaba pɛɛ sɛ bɛde Yohane a ɔsane de Marko he ka bɛ ho.

english_verse: But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
translation_verse: Nso Paulo yɔɔ n’adwene sɛ esiane sɛ ogyaa bɛ hɔ berɛ a beduruu Pamfilia na ɔne bɛ ankɔyɔ adwuma he nti, ɔnsɛ sɛ bɛde no ka bɛ ho kɔ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus,
translation_verse: Yei de akasakasaa a mu ɔdene baeɛ, na bedii mpaepaemu; Barnaba faa Marko kaa ne ho ma bɛfaa po so hyɛne de kɔɔ Kipro.

english_verse: but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care.
translation_verse: Paulo nso faa Silas, na berɛ a agyidifoɔ de Paulo hyɛɛ Awurade adom ase he, befirii hɔ kɔeɛ.

english_verse: They traveled through Syria and Cilicia, encouraging the churches.
translation_verse: Na ɔkɔfaa Siria ne Kilikia hyehyɛɛ asafo ahodoɔ he dene.

english_verse: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
translation_verse: Paulo kɔɔ Derbe ne Listra, baabi a na suani bi a bɛfrɛ no Timoteo a na ne maame yɛ Yudani na ne papa nso yɛ Helani ya.

english_verse: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
translation_verse: Na agyidifoɔ a bɛya Listra ne Ikonium he kaa ne ho asɛm pa.

english_verse: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
translation_verse: Na Paulo pɛɛ sɛ Timoteo di n’akyi. Na osiane Yudafoɔ a na bɛya hɔ he nti, ɔfaa no twaa no twetia, efiri sɛ na bɛ nyinaa nim sɛ ne papa yɛ Helani.

english_verse: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a bɛnenam nkuro he so he bɛde mmara a asomafoɔ he ne mpanyimfoɔ a bɛya Yerusalem asi ho gyinaeɛ he maa bɛ sɛ benni so.

english_verse: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
translation_verse: Nti asafo ahodoɔ he kɔɔ so yɛɛ dene gyidie mu na bɛkɔɔ so dɔree da biaa. Paulo anisoadehunu ya Troa

english_verse: Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia.
