translation_verse: Afei bɛkɔfaa Frigia ne Galatia mpɔtamu efiri sɛ Honhom Kronkron he braa bɛ sɛ bɛnnka asɛm he Asia.

english_verse: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
translation_verse: Na berɛ a beduruu Misia he, bɛyɛɛ bɛ adwene sɛ bɛbɛkɔ Bitinia, nso Honhom Kronkron ama bɛ kwane.

english_verse: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us!”
translation_verse: Anadwo he Paulo nyaa anisoadehunu sɛ, Makedoniani bi gyina hɔ ɔɔserɛ no sɛ, “Bra Makedonia na bɛboa yɛ.”

english_verse: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
translation_verse: Na onyaa anisoadehunu heaa pɛ ne yesiim sɛ yɛɛkɔ Makedonia efiri sɛ yehuu sɛ Nyankopɔn ne wafrɛ yɛ sɛ yɛnkɔka asɛmpa he nkyerɛ bɛ.

english_verse: We sailed straight from Troas to Samothrace, and the next day we arrived in Neapolis.
translation_verse: Nti yɛfaa nsuo so firi Troa baa Samotrake tee, na adeɛ kyeeɛ he, yɛkɔɔ Neapoli.

english_verse: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
translation_verse: Na yefirii hɔ he, yɛkɔɔ Filipi a ɔyɛ Roma atubrafoɔ kuro titire ya Makedonia mantam mu. Yɛtenaa saa kuro he mu nna kakra. Lidia adwensakra

english_verse: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
translation_verse: Homeda duruiɛ he, yepie faa kuro he pono ho kɔɔ asubɔntene bi ho, baabi a na yesusu sɛ yebenya baabi a bɛbɔ mpaeɛ. Na yɛtenaa ase kasa kyerɛɛ mmaa a na bɛahyiam hɔ he.

english_verse: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
translation_verse: Na baa bi a bɛfrɛ no Lidia a ɔtɔno ntoma kɔkɔɔ a ofiri Tiatira na ɔsom Nyankopɔn he tiee yɛ asɛm he. Na Awurade biee n’akoma mu ma ɔyɛɛ aso maa nea Paulo kaeɛ he.

english_verse: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, “If you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.” Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a yɛbɔɔ ɔne ne fiefoɔ asu he, ɔde nkotosrɛ kaa sɛ, “Sɛ hoahu me sɛ nɔkwafoɔ ama Awurade a, hommɛtena me fie.” Na ɔkaeɛaa ma yɛkɔɔ ne fie. Afenaa bi ayaresa

english_verse: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
translation_verse: Na dakoro bi, ɔbaa sɛ yɛɛkɔ baabi a bɛbɔ mpaeɛ he, yehyiaa afenaa bi a ɔwɔ akɔm honhom. Na ɔka daakye nsɛm ma ne wuranom nya ne so mfasoɔ pii.

english_verse: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, “These men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.”
translation_verse: Na odii yɛ ne Paulo akyi teateaam sɛ, “Nnipa yi yɛ Sorosoro Nyankopɔn nkoa a bɛɛpae mu aka nkwagyekwane ho asɛm akyerɛ ho.”

english_verse: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, “In the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!” At once the evil spirit left her.
translation_verse: Ɔkɔɔ so yɔɔ saa nna pii. Na ɔyɛɛ Paulo ahomte ma ɔdanee n’ani ka kyerɛɛ honhom he sɛ, “Mehyɛ wo Yesu Kristo dini mu sɛ firi ne mu firi” Na ofirii ne mu saa dɔno heaa.

english_verse: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
translation_verse: Na ne wuranom huu sɛ nea bɛ ani da so a ɔbɛma bɛ mfasoɔ he ahwere bɛ he, bɛsosɔɔ Paulo ne Silas mu twee bɛ kɔɔ dwa so kɔmaa atumfoɔ he.

english_verse: They are telling us to do things we Romans are not allowed to do.”
translation_verse: na bɛɛkyerɛkyerɛ amanneɛ ahodoɔ bi a mmara mma yɛ a yɛyɛ Romafoɔ he kwane sɛ yedi so anaa yɛyɔ.”

english_verse: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
translation_verse: Na nnipakuo he nyinaa sɔre kɔɔ bɛ so; na berɛ a mane he mu mpanyimfoɔ he sunsuanee bɛ ntaadeɛ ngugusoɔ mu he, bɛhyɛɛ sɛ bɛmmɔ bɛ mmaa.

english_verse: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
translation_verse: Na bɛbɔɔ bɛ mmaa pii wieeɛ he, bɛtwee bɛ koguu afiase na bɛhyɛɛ afiasehwɛfoɔ he sɛ ɔnhwɛ bɛ so yie.

english_verse: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
translation_verse: Osiane sɛ bɛhyɛɛ no saa nti, ɔde bɛ koguu afiase dane a ɔhyɛ mfimfini paa he mu kyekyeree bɛ nane bɔɔ dua mu. Afiasehwɛfoɔ he adwensakra

english_verse: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
translation_verse: Na anadwo dasuom he, Paulo ne Silas bɔɔ mpaeɛ too nnwom yii Nyankopɔn ayɛ, na nneduafoɔ he tiee bɛ.

english_verse: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
translation_verse: Mpofiremuaa asaase wosoɔ kɛseɛ bi si maa afiase he fapem wosowosoeɛ. Afei apono he nyinaa biebieɛ amonom hɔaa, na bɛ nkɔnsɔnkɔnsɔn he nyinaa sanesaneeɛ.

english_verse: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
translation_verse: Berɛ a afiasehwɛfoɔ he sɔre firii nna mu a ohuu sɛ afiase apono he abiebie he, ɔtwee ne sekane sɛ nka oobeku ne ho, efiri sɛ na osusu sɛ nneduafoɔ he adwane.

english_verse: But Paul shouted, “Don't harm yourself! No one has escaped.”
translation_verse: Na Paulo de nne kɛseɛ teaam sɛ, “Nyɔ wo ho bɔne biaa, na yɛ nyinaa yɛya ha!”

english_verse: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.
translation_verse: Na ɔkɔgyee kanea di mmirika bɔ wuraa hɔ, na ɔde ahopopoɔ hwee Paulo ne Silas anim.

english_verse: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, “What must I do to be saved?”
translation_verse: Na ɔde bɛ baa abɔntene bisaa sɛ, “me wuranom, mennyɛ dɛ na manya daa nkwa?”

english_verse: They replied, “Have faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.”
translation_verse: Bebuaa sɛ, “Gye Awurade Yesu di, na ɔbɛgye wo ne wo fie nkwa.”

english_verse: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
translation_verse: Bɛkaa Awurade asɛm kyerɛɛ ɔne ne fiefoɔ nyinaa.

english_verse: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
translation_verse: Na ɔde bɛ kɔɔ bea foforɔ bi saa anadwo heaa komiaa bɛ akuro he; na ntɛmaa bɛbɔɔ ɔne ne fiefoɔ nyinaa asu.

english_verse: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
translation_verse: Ɔde bɛ baa fie hɔ, na ɔde aduane besisii bɛ anim, na ɔne ne fiefoɔ nyinaa dii ahurusie sɛ wabɛyɛ Nyankopɔn gyidini.

english_verse: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
translation_verse: Adeɛ kyeeɛ he, mane he mu mpanyimfoɔ somaa awɛmfoɔ sɛ bɛnkɔka nkyerɛ afiasehwɛfoɔ he sɛ, “Gyae saa mmeema yi ma bɛnkɔ.”

english_verse: The jailer told Paul, “The officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.”
translation_verse: Na afiasehwɛfoɔ he kaa asɛm he kyerɛɛ Paulo, kaa sɛ, “Mane mpanyimfoɔ he asoma nnipa aba sɛ mennyae ho. Nti afei hommɛsene nkɔ asomdwie mu.”

english_verse: But Paul told the police, “We are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.”
translation_verse: Na Paulo buaa sɛ, “Bɛhwee yɛ badwam berɛ a yenni fɔ, nnipa a yɛyɛ Romafoɔ, na bɛde yɛ guu afiase, na afei deɛ beeyi yɛ kodɛɛ? Daabi da, na mmom ma bɛ ankasa mmra mmeyi yɛ.”

english_verse: When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid.
translation_verse: Awɛmfoɔ he kɔkaa nsɛm yi kyerɛɛ mpanyimfoɔ he. Berɛ a bɛtee sɛ bɛyɛ Romafoɔ he, bɛbɔɔ hu.

english_verse: So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town.
translation_verse: Nti bɛba bɛpaa bɛ kyɛw. Na beyii bɛ pieeɛ na bɛka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ bemfiri kuro he mu nkɔ.

english_verse: But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
translation_verse: Befirii afiase he bɛkɔɔ Lidia fie. Na berɛ a bɛhyɛɛ anuanom nkurane wieeɛ he, bɛkɔeɛ.

english_verse: After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city.
translation_verse: Afei Paulo ne Silas kɔfaa Amfipoli ne Apolonia baa Tesalonika, baabi a na Yudafoɔ hyiadane bi ya he.

english_verse: So as usual, Paul went there to worship, and on three Sabbaths he spoke to the people. He used the Scriptures
translation_verse: Na sɛnea Paulo taa yɛ he, ɔkɔɔ bɛ nkyɛne, na ɔde homeda mmeɛnsa ne bɛ susuu twerɛsɛm he ho,

english_verse: to show them that the Messiah had to suffer, but that he would rise from death. Paul also told them that Jesus is the Messiah he was preaching about.
translation_verse: na ɔkyerɛkyerɛɛ mu de adanseɛ ahodoɔ kyerɛɛ sɛ na ohia sɛ Kristo he hu amane na ɔsɔre fi awufoɔ mu, na ɔkaa sɛ, “Yei ne Kristo he, Yesu a meeka ne ho asɛm kyerɛ ho he.”

english_verse: Some of them believed what Paul had said, and they became followers with Paul and Silas. Some Gentiles and many important women also believed the message.
translation_verse: Yei maa bɛ mu binom bɛyɛɛ agyidifoɔ, na bɛde bɛ ho bataa Paulo ne Silas ho, na Helafoɔ dɔm kɛseɛ bi a bɛsom Nyankopɔn ne mmaa atitire a bɛdɔɔso pii nso yɔɔ saa.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home.
translation_verse: Na Yudafoɔ he ani bereeɛ, na bɛboaboa nsɛmmɔnefoɔ bi ano dwa so maa kuro he mu yɛɛ bagyabagya. Na bɛto hyɛɛ Yason fie so hwehwɛɛ sɛ bɛde bɛ bɛbra bɛ a bɛɛyɔ basabasa he.

english_verse: But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, “Paul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here,
translation_verse: Berɛ a bɛanhu bɛ he, bɛtwee Yason ne agyidifoɔ binom kɔmaa kuro he mu atumfoɔ, na bɛteateaam sɛ, “Mmeema a bɛadane wiase ani he aba ha nso,

english_verse: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.”
translation_verse: na Yason agye bɛ aba ne fie. Saa nnipa yi nyinaa sɔre tia Kaesare ahyɛdeɛ ka sɛ hene foforɔ bi ya hɔ a ɔde Yesu.”

english_verse: The officials and the people were upset when they heard this.
translation_verse: Berɛ a nnipa he ne kuro he mpanyimfoɔ tee yei he, bɛ ani bereeɛ,

english_verse: So they made Jason and the other followers pay bail before letting them go. People in Berea Welcome the Message
translation_verse: afei berɛ a bɛmaa Yason ne bɛ a bɛaka he dii agyinamu maa bɛ he, begyaa bɛ. Paulo ne Silas kɔɔ Beroia

english_verse: That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Na ntɛmaa agyidifoɔ he maa Paulo ne Silas kɔɔ Beroia, na bekoduruiɛ he, bɛkɔɔ Yudafoɔ hyiadane mu.

english_verse: The people in Berea were much nicer than those in Thessalonica, and they gladly accepted the message. Day after day they studied the Scriptures to see if these things were true.
translation_verse: Na yeinom gyee asɛm he sene bɛ a bɛya Tesalonika he, efiri sɛ bɛde bɛ adwene nyinaa gyee asɛm he, na da biaa na bɛhwehwɛ twerɛsɛm he mu yie hwɛ sɛ nsɛm he te saa anaa.

english_verse: Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
translation_verse: Na bɛ mu pii bɛgye diiɛ, ne Helafoɔ mmaa ne mmeema atitire a bɛdɔɔso pii nso yɔɔ saa.

english_verse: When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
translation_verse: Na Yudafoɔ a bɛya Tesalonika tee sɛ Paulo aka Nyankopɔn asɛm Beroia nso he, bɛbaa hɔ nso betu guu manfoɔ he asom, na bɛhwanyanee bɛ mu.

english_verse: At once the followers sent Paul down to the coast, but Silas and Timothy stayed in Berea.
translation_verse: Na ntɛmaa agyidifoɔ he gyaa Paulo kwane koduruu po he ano nohoa, na Silas ne Timoteo deɛ bɛtenaa hɔ.

english_verse: Some men went with Paul as far as Athens, and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible. Paul in Athens
translation_verse: Bɛ a bekogyaa Paulo kwane he de no koduruu Atene. Na ɔmaa bɛ nkra sɛ bɛnka nkyerɛ Silas ne Timoteo sɛ bɛmmra ne nkyɛne ntɛm so he, befirii hɔ kɔeɛ. Paulo ne Yudafoɔ ne Helafoɔ nkɔmmɔbɔ

english_verse: While Paul was waiting in Athens, he was upset to see all the idols in the city.
translation_verse: Na Paulo gu so ɔɔtwɛne bɛ Atene na ohuu sɛ ahonyini ahyɛ kuro he so ma he, ɔyɛɛ no ahomte.

english_verse: He went to the synagogue to speak to the Jews and to anyone who worshiped with them. Day after day he also spoke to everyone he met in the market.
translation_verse: Nti ɔne Yudafoɔ ne nnipa a aka a bɛsom Nyankopɔn he susuu nsɛm ho hyiadane he mu, na da biaa na ɔkɔ bɛ a obenya bɛ he nso nkyɛne dwa so.

english_verse: Some of them were Epicureans and some were Stoics, and they started arguing with him. People were asking, “What is this know-it-all trying to say?” Some even said, “Paul must be preaching about foreign gods! That's what he means when he talks about Jesus and about people rising from death.”
translation_verse: Nso Epikurofoɔ ne Stoafoɔ nyansapɛfoɔ bi ne no dii anobaabaeɛ, na binom kaa sɛ, “Mmɛ ne kasatentene wura yi pɛ sɛ ɔkyerɛ koraa yi?” Na binom nso kaa sɛ, “Gyama ɔka ananafoɔ abosom ho asɛm.”

english_verse: They brought Paul before a council called the Areopagus, and said, “Tell us what your new teaching is all about.
translation_verse: Nti bɛkyee no de no kɔɔ Areopago asennibea bisaa no sɛ, “Wobetumi ama yɛahu nkyerɛkyerɛ foforɔ a wookyerɛkyerɛ yi mu?

english_verse: We have heard you say some strange things, and we want to know what you mean.”
translation_verse: Nea wooka he yɛ foforɔ yɛ asom. Nti yɛpɛ sɛ yehu nea nsɛm yi kyerɛ.”

english_verse: More than anything else the people of Athens and the foreigners living there loved to hear and to talk about anything new.
translation_verse: Nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, sɛ Atenefoɔ ne ahɔhoɔ a bɛabɛsoɛ hɔ he nya berɛ a, nea bɛde yɛaa ne sɛ bɛbɛka anaa bebetie asɛm foforɔ.

english_verse: So Paul stood up in front of the council and said: People of Athens, I see that you are very religious.
translation_verse: Afei Paulo sɔre gyinaa Areopago mfimfini, kaa sɛ, “Atene mmeema, maahu sɛ adeɛ nyinaa mu hopɛ nyamesom paa,

english_verse: As I was going through your city and looking at the things you worship, I found an altar with the words, “To an Unknown God.” You worship this God, but you don't really know him. So I want to tell you about him.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ menenam kuro he mu na mehwɛɛ nnoɔma a hosom he, mehuu afɔrebokyia bi a bɛatwerɛ ho, ‘Yei yɛ Nyame Bi a Yennim No Dea.’ Nti nea hosom a honnim no he, ɔnoaa ne meeka ne ho asɛm akyerɛ ho yi.

english_verse: This God made the world and everything in it. He is Lord of heaven and earth, and he doesn't live in temples built by human hands.
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn a ɔyɔɔ wiase ne nea ɔya mu nyinaa, ɔno a ɔyɛ soro ne asaase Wura he ntena dane a bɛde nipa nsa asi mu,

english_verse: He doesn't need help from anyone. He gives life, breath, and everything else to all people.
translation_verse: na bɛmfa nipa nsa nsom no, te sɛ nea ohia bibi, efiri sɛ ɔno mmom ne ma nnipa nyinaa nkwa, ahomee ne adeɛ nyinaa.

english_verse: From one person God made all nations who live on earth, and he decided when and where every nation would be.
translation_verse: Na nipa baako mu ne oyii amane nyinaa firiiɛ sɛ bɛntena asaase nyinaa so, na waahyɛ mmerɛ ato hɔ, na ɔde hyeɛ atoto nnipa tenabea nso,

english_verse: God has done all this, so that we will look for him and reach out and find him. He isn't far from any of us,
translation_verse: na ama bɛapɛ Nyankopɔn akyi kwane, ahwehwɛ no anibereɛ so kosi sɛ bebehu no, nso nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ ɔne yɛ mu biaa ntam nware.

english_verse: and he gives us the power to live, to move, and to be who we are. “We are his children,” just as some of your poets have said.
translation_verse: Na ɔnam ne so ne yɛwɔ nkwa, na yɛkeka yɛ ho na yɛte ase’; na ho mu anwensɛm atwerɛfoɔ bi mpo aka sɛ, ‘Yɛ nso yɛyɛ ne mma.’

english_verse: Since we are God's children, we must not think that he is like an idol made out of gold or silver or stone. He isn't like anything that humans have thought up and made.
translation_verse: Nti berɛ a yɛahu sɛ yɛyɛ Nyankopɔn asefoɔ yi, ɔnsɛ sɛ yesusu sɛ Nyankopɔn tebea te sɛ sikakɔkɔɔ anaa dwetɛ anaa boɔ, anaa adwenie bi a nipa de n’adwene adi anaa wabɔ ne tirim ayɔ.

english_verse: In the past, God forgave all this because people did not know what they were doing. But now he says that everyone everywhere must turn to him.
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn buu n’ani guu mmerɛ a na bennim hwee he so, nso afei ɔɔka akyerɛ adasamma a bɛya mmaa nyinaa sɛ biaa nsakra n’adwene,

english_verse: He has set a day when he will judge the world's people with fairness. And he has chosen the man Jesus to do the judging for him. God has given proof of this to all of us by raising Jesus from death.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ waayi da bi asi hɔ a wabɔ ne tim pɔ sɛ ɔnam beema bi a wayi no so bebu wiase atentenee mu, na wanyane no afiri awufoɔ mu de ayɔ nnyinasodeɛ ama nnipa nyinaa.”

english_verse: As soon as the people heard Paul say a man had been raised from death, some of them started laughing. Others said, “We will hear you talk about this some other time.”
translation_verse: Na bɛtee awufoɔ sɔreɛ he ho asɛm he, binom dii fɛw, na binom nso kaa sɛ, “Yebetie yei ho asɛm afiri wo nkyɛne bio berɛ foforɔ nso.”

english_verse: When Paul left the council meeting,
translation_verse: Saa berɛ he Paulo gyaa bɛ hɔ kɔeɛ.

english_verse: some of the men put their faith in the Lord and went with Paul. One of them was a council member named Dionysius. A woman named Damaris and several others also put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Nso mmeema bi de bɛ ho bɔɔ no bɛyɛɛ agyidifoɔ, bɛ mu binom ne Dionisio a ɔyɛ Areopago badwa he temmuafoɔ, ne baa bi a bɛfrɛ no Damari, ne afoforɔ a bɛka bɛ ho.

english_verse: Paul left Athens and went to Corinth,
translation_verse: Yei akyi he, Paulo firii Atene kɔɔ Korinto.

english_verse: where he met Aquila, a Jewish man from Pontus. Not long before this, Aquila had come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Emperor Claudius had ordered the Jewish people to leave Rome. Paul went to see Aquila and Priscilla
translation_verse: Na ohuu Yudani bi a bɛfrɛ no Akwila, Pontoni bi a na ofiri Italia aba ne nkyɛne, ne ne yerɛ Priskila, efiri sɛ na Klaudio ahyɛ sɛ Yudafoɔ nyinaa mfiri Roma. Nti ɔkɔɔ bɛ nkyɛne.

english_verse: and found out they were tent makers. Paul was also a tent maker, so he stayed with them, and they worked together.
translation_verse: Na esiane sɛ na ɔne bɛ yɔ adwuma korɔ nti, ɔtenaa bɛ fie yɔɔ adwuma, efiri sɛ na bɛyɛ ntomadanyɔfoɔ.

english_verse: Every Sabbath, Paul went to the synagogue. He spoke to Jews and Gentiles and tried to win them over.
translation_verse: Nso, homeda biaa, na ɔka asɛm hyiadane mu ma Yudafoɔ ne Helafoɔ gye di.

english_verse: But after Silas and Timothy came from Macedonia, he spent all his time preaching to the Jews about Jesus the Messiah.
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a Silas ne Timoteo nyinaa firii Makedonia beduruiɛ he, Paulo de ne berɛ nyinaa pae mu kaa asɛm he, na odii Yudafoɔ adanseɛ kyerɛɛ sɛ Yesu ne Kristo he.

english_verse: Finally, they turned against him and insulted him. So he shook the dust from his clothes and told them, “Whatever happens to you will be your own fault! I am not to blame. From now on I am going to preach to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Berɛ a bɛ ne no dii asie na bɛkasa tiaa no he, ɔporoporoo ne ntaadeɛ mu mfuturo kaa sɛ, “Ho mogyaa mua hoaa ho atifi. Me deɛ menni ho fɔ. Ofiri nnɛ ɔɔkɔ yi meekɔ amanamane mufoɔ hɔ.”

english_verse: Paul then moved into the house of a man named Titius Justus, who worshiped God and lived next door to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Afei ofirii hyiadane mu hɔ, na ɔkɔɔ beema bi a bɛfrɛ no Tito Yusto a ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn somfoɔ fie. Na ne fie he ya hyiadane he nkyɛne pɛɛ.

english_verse: Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
translation_verse: Krispo a na ɔyɛ hyiadane he mu panyini ne ne fiefoɔ nyinaa gyee Awurade diiɛ. Na Korintofoɔ he a betie Paulo he nso gye diiɛ na bɛbɔɔ bɛ asu.

english_verse: One night, Paul had a vision, and in it the Lord said, “Don't be afraid to keep on preaching. Don't stop!
translation_verse: Da koro anadwo bi, Awurade kaa no anisoadehunu mu kyerɛɛ Paulo sɛ, “Nsuro, kasa na mmua w’ano,

english_verse: I am with you, and you won't be harmed. Many people in this city belong to me.”
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, meka wo ho, na biaa ntumi mfa ne nsa nka wo nyɔ wo bɔne biaa, na mewɔ nnipa pii ya kuro yi mu.”

english_verse: Paul stayed on in Corinth for a year and a half, teaching God's message to the people.
translation_verse: Ɔtenaa hɔ afe ne bosome nsia kyerɛkyerɛɛ bɛ Nyankopɔn asɛm.

english_verse: While Gallio was governor of Achaia, some of the Jewish leaders got together and grabbed Paul. They brought him into court
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a Galio yɛ Akaia amrado he, Yudafoɔ he yɛɛ baako sɔre tiaa Paulo de no kɔɔ atemmuo adwa he anim

english_verse: and said, “This man is trying to make our people worship God in a way that is against our Law!”
translation_verse: kɔkaa sɛ, “Saa nipa yi dane nkurofoɔ adwene ma bɛfa kwane foforɔ a ɔne mmara he bɔ abira so som Nyankopɔn.”

english_verse: Even before Paul could speak, Gallio said, “If you were charging this man with a crime or some other wrong, I would have to listen to you.
translation_verse: Paulo yɛɛ sɛ ɔɔbɛkasa heaa, na Galio ka kyerɛɛ Yudafoɔ he sɛ, “Yudafoɔ, sɛ asɛm yi yɛ bɔne anaa ammumuyɔdeɛ bi a, nka mɛgye ho asɛm he atum.

english_verse: But since this concerns only words, names, and your own law, you will have to take care of it yourselves. I refuse to judge such matters.”
translation_verse: Na mmom sɛ ɔyɛ nsɛm ne dini ne ho mmara ho akyinnyegyeɛ deɛ a, hedeɛ ho ankasa honhwɛ nka. Mempɛ sɛ mɛyɛ nsɛm yi ho temufoɔ.”

english_verse: Then he sent them out of the court.
translation_verse: Na ɔpamoo bɛ firii atemmuo adwa he anim.

english_verse: The crowd grabbed Sosthenes, the Jewish leader, and beat him up in front of the court. But none of this mattered to Gallio. Paul Returns to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Nti bɛ nyinaa to hyɛɛ Sostene a ɔyɛ hyiadane mu panyini he so firii aseɛ hwee no atemmuo adwa he anim. Nso Galio anyɛ yeinom ho hwee. Paulo sane kɔɔ Antiokia

english_verse: After Paul had stayed for a while with the Lord's followers in Corinth, he told them goodbye and sailed on to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla. But before he left, he had his head shaved at Cenchreae because he had made a promise to God.
translation_verse: Nso berɛ a Paulo dii nna kakra kaa ho hɔ he, ɔne agyidifoɔ he dii nkra na ɔde po so hyɛne firii hɔ kɔɔ Siria a na Priskila ne Akwila ka ne ho, na ɔbɔɔ ti kwa ya Kenkrea, efiri sɛ, na berɛ a ɔde asi hɔ sɛ ootua ne ho nnoɔma bi he aba awieeɛ.

english_verse: The three of them arrived in Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. He then went into the synagogue to talk with the people there.
translation_verse: Na beduruu Efeso he, ogyaa bɛ hɔ; na ɔno deɛ ɔkɔɔ hyiadane mu ne Yudafoɔ he kɔyɔɔ nsɛm bi ho mpɛnsɛmpɛnsɛmu.

english_verse: They asked him to stay longer, but he refused.
translation_verse: Na bɛsrɛɛ no sɛ ɔntena hɔ nkyɛre, nso wannye antom,

english_verse: He told them goodbye and said, “If God lets me, I will come back.”
translation_verse: na ɔne bɛ dii nkra ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Sɛ ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn pɛ a, mɛba ho nkyɛne bio.” Yei akyi he ofiri Efeso faa po so kɔeɛ.

english_verse: Paul sailed to Caesarea, where he greeted the church. Then he went on to Antioch.
translation_verse: Na oduruu Kaesarea he, ɔkɔɔ Yerusalem kɔkyeaa asafo he. Na afei ɔkɔɔ Antiokia.

english_verse: After staying there for a while, he left and visited several places in Galatia and Phrygia. He helped the followers there to become stronger in their faith. Apollos in Ephesus
translation_verse: Berɛ a ɔtenaa hɔ kakra he, ofirii hɔ tutuu akwane faa Galatia ne Frigia nsaase so hyehyɛɛ asuafoɔ he nyinaa dene. Apolo kɔɔ Efeso ne Korinto

english_verse: A Jewish man named Apollos came to Ephesus. Apollos had been born in the city of Alexandria. He was a very good speaker and knew a lot about the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Afei Yudani bi a bɛfrɛ no Apolo a ofiri Aleksandria, baa Efeso. Na Apolo yɛ bi a n’ano ate na onim twerɛsɛm mu paa nso.

english_verse: He also knew much about the Lord's Way, and he spoke about it with great excitement. What he taught about Jesus was right, but all he knew was John's message about baptism.
translation_verse: Na bɛakyerɛkyerɛ no Awurade kwane so, na esiane sɛ na Honhom ahyɛ no ma nti, ɔkaa Yesu ho asɛm kyerɛkyerɛɛ mu pɛpɛɛpɛ, nso na Yohane asubɔ he nkoaa ne onim.

english_verse: Apollos started speaking bravely in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him to their home and helped him understand God's Way even better.
translation_verse: Na ɔhyɛɛ aseɛ sɛ ɔde akokoɔduro ɔɔkasa hyiadane he mu, nso berɛ a Priskila ne Akwila tiee no he, bɛfaa no kɔɔ nkyɛne baabi kyerɛkyerɛɛ no Nyankopɔn kwane he mu pɛpɛɛpɛ.

english_verse: Apollos decided to travel through Achaia. So the Lord's followers wrote letters, encouraging the followers there to welcome him. After Apollos arrived in Achaia, he was a great help to everyone who had put their faith in the Lord Jesus because of God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Afei esiane sɛ na ɔpɛ sɛ otware kɔ Akaia nti, agyidifoɔ he hyɛɛ ne nkurane, na bɛtwerɛɛ krataa kɔmaa asuafoɔ he srɛɛ bɛ sɛ bɛnnye no fɛ so. Nti berɛ a oduruu hɔ he, ɔboaa bɛ a bɛnam Nyankopɔn adom so agye adi he paa,

english_verse: He got into fierce arguments with the Jewish people, and in public he used the Scriptures to prove that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: ɔde nnamyɛ daa no adi pefee badwam sɛ Yudafoɔ he afom kwane, na ɔde Twerɛsɛm he kyerɛɛ sɛ Yesu ne Kristo he.

english_verse: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled across the hill country to Ephesus, where he met some of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Ɔbaa sɛ berɛ a Apolo ya Korinto he, Paulo faa mpɔtamu hɔ baabi baa Efeso, bɛtoo asuafoɔ bi.

english_verse: He asked them, “When you put your faith in Jesus, were you given the Holy Spirit?” “No!” they answered. “We have never even heard of the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Na obisaa bɛ sɛ, “Berɛ a hogye diiɛ he honyaa Honhom Kronkron anaa?” Bebuaa sɛ, “Daabi, yɛntee da mpo sɛ Honhom Kronkron bi ya baabi.”

english_verse: “Then why were you baptized?” Paul asked. They answered, “Because of what John taught.”
translation_verse: Nti ɔkaa sɛ, “hedeɛ mmɛ asubɔ ne bɛbɔɔ ho?” Bebuaa sɛ, “Yohane asubɔ a.”

english_verse: Paul replied, “John baptized people so they would turn to God. But he also told them someone else was coming, and they should put their faith in him. Jesus is the one that John was talking about.”
translation_verse: Paulo kaa sɛ, “Yohane bɔɔ asu a ɔyɛ adwensakra ho nsɛnkyerɛnne, na ɔka kyerɛɛ nkurofoɔ he sɛ bennya deɛ odi n’akyi ɔɔba, a ɔne Yesu, he mu gyidie.”

english_verse: After the people heard Paul say this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Na bɛtee yei he, bɛbɔɔ bɛ asu Awurade Yesu dini mu.

english_verse: Then Paul placed his hands on them. The Holy Spirit was given to them, and they spoke unknown languages and prophesied.
translation_verse: Na Paulo de ne nsa guu bɛ so he, Honhom Kronkron he baa bɛ so, na bɛkaa kasa hodoɔ hyɛɛ nkɔm.

english_verse: There were about twelve men in this group.
translation_verse: Na bɛ dodoɔ yɛ du mmienu.

english_verse: For three months Paul went to the synagogue and talked bravely with the people about God's kingdom. He tried to win them over,
translation_verse: Paulo kɔɔ hyiadane he mu, de akokoɔduro kasaeɛ, na bosome mmeɛnsa ne ɔkasaeɛ, na ɔkaa Nyankopɔn ahennie ho asɛm a ɔtɔ asum.

english_verse: but some of them were stubborn and refused to believe. In front of everyone they said terrible things about God's Way. Paul left and took the followers with him to the lecture hall of Tyrannus. He spoke there every day
translation_verse: Nso berɛ a binom prim bɛ akoma na bɛannye anni, na bɛkekaa gyidie Kwane He ho nsɛmmɔne nnipadɔm he anim he, ɔtwee ne ho yii asuafoɔ he firii bɛ mu, na daa na ɔka asɛm Tirano suadane he mu.

english_verse: for two years, until every Jew and Gentile in Asia had heard the Lord's message. The Sons of Sceva
translation_verse: Yei toa so mfie mmienu, na bɛ a bɛte Asia mpɔtamu nyinaa, Yudafoɔ ne Helafoɔ nyinaa, tee Awurade asɛm. Skewa mma nson ho asɛm

english_verse: God gave Paul the power to work great miracles.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn nam Paulo so yɔɔ anwanwadeɛ soronko.

english_verse: People even took handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body, and they carried them to everyone who was sick. All of the sick people were healed, and the evil spirits went out.
translation_verse: Na yei nti bɛde duku ne ntaadeɛ nsɛnanimu mpo ka ne honam na bɛde kogu ayarefoɔ so a bɛ yadeɛ he gyaeɛ, na ahonhommɔne nso he tutu firi bɛ mu.

english_verse: Some Jewish men started going around trying to force out evil spirits by using the name of the Lord Jesus. They said to the spirits, “Come out in the name of that same Jesus that Paul preaches about!”
translation_verse: Na Yudafoɔ a beekyinikyini tutu ahonhommɔne he mu binom nso bɔɔ mmɔdene sɛ bɛbɛbɔ Awurade dini agu bɛ a bɛwɔ ahonhommɔne he so sɛ, “Mehyɛ ho Yesu a Paulo kaa ne ho asɛm he dini mu sɛ hompie.”

english_verse: Seven sons of a high priest named Sceva were doing this,
translation_verse: Na Yudani sɔfo panyini bi a ɔde Skewa wɔ mma nson bi a bɛyɔ saa.

english_verse: when an evil spirit said to them, “I know Jesus! And I have heard about Paul. But who are you?”
translation_verse: Na honhommɔne he buaa sɛ, “Menim Yesu, menim Paulo, na ho nso, hoyɛ hefoɔ?”

english_verse: Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them up. They ran out of the house, naked and bruised.
translation_verse: Na afei beema a honhommɔne ya ne mu he to hyɛɛ bɛ, so dii bɛ so, yɔɔ bɛ pasapasa, na bɛde adagya dwane firii fie hɔ kɔeɛ a na bɛapirapira.

english_verse: When the Jews and Gentiles in Ephesus heard about this, they were so frightened that they praised the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Na Yudafoɔ ne Helafoɔ a bɛte Efeso tee yei he, hu kɛseɛ bɔɔ bɛ nyinaa, na bɛkɔɔ so kamfõõ Awurade Yesu dini.

english_verse: Many who were followers now started telling everyone about the evil things they had been doing.
translation_verse: Na bɛ a bɛbɛyɛɛ agyidifoɔ he mu pii kaa bɛ bɔne ne bɛ nneyɔeɛ kyerɛeɛ pefee.

english_verse: Some who had been practicing witchcraft even brought their books and burned them in public. These books were worth about
translation_verse: Na bɛ a bedi asumansɛm he mu pii de bɛ nwoma ba bɛhye ma biaa huiɛ. Na bebuu ne nyinaa ano huu sɛ ne boɔ yɛ nnwetɛbena apem aduonum.

english_verse: So the Lord's message spread and became even more powerful. The Riot in Ephesus
translation_verse: Saa ara ne Awurade asɛm nam ahoɔdene so kɔɔ so trɛeɛ, na aseɛ timiiɛ.

english_verse: After all this had happened, Paul decided to visit Macedonia and Achaia on his way to Jerusalem. Paul had said, “From there I will go on to Rome.”
translation_verse: Afei bewiee yeinom he, Paulo bɔɔ ne tim pɔ sɛ, sɛ ɔkɔfa Makedonia ne Akaia wie a, ɔbɛkɔ Yerusalem, na ɔkaa sɛ: “Sɛ meduru hɔ a, otwa sɛ mekɔ Roma nso.”

english_verse: So he sent his two helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia. But he stayed on in Asia for a while.
translation_verse: Nti ɔsomaa n’aboɔfoɔ he mu mmienu, Timoteo ne Erasto, kɔɔ Makedonia, na ɔno ankasa deɛ, ɔtenaa Asia mantam he mu kyɛree kakra. Demetrio sakasakayɔ

english_verse: At that time there was serious trouble because of the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: Saa bere no sakasaka kɛseɛ bi sɔreeɛ a na ɔfa gyidie Kwane He ho.

english_verse: A silversmith named Demetrius had a business that made silver models of the temple of the goddess Artemis. Those who worked for him earned a lot of money.
translation_verse: Na dwetɛ dwumfoɔ bi a bɛfrɛ no Demetrio, nam Artemi abosom-adane a ɔde dwetɛ yɔ he so ma adwumfoɔ he nya mfasoɔ kɛseɛ;

english_verse: Demetrius brought together everyone who was in the same business and said: Friends, you know we make a good living at this.
translation_verse: na ɔboaboaa bɛ a bɛyɔ nnoɔma a ɔtete saa ho adwuma he ano kaa sɛ: “Mmeema, honim yie sɛ adwuma yi ne ma yenya yɛ ho.

english_verse: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
translation_verse: Afei nso, hoahu na hoate sɛnea ɔnyɛ Efeso nko na Paulo yi adane nnipakuo pii adwene ama bɛanya adwene foforɔ, na mmom ɔkame ayɛ sɛ Asia mantam mu nyinaa, na ɔka sɛ nea bɛde nsa ayɔ he nyɛ anyame.

english_verse: Everyone will start saying terrible things about our business. They will stop respecting the temple of the goddess Artemis, who is worshiped in Asia and all over the world. Our great goddess will be forgotten!
translation_verse: Bio nso, ɔnyɛ yɛ adwuma a obenya dimmɔne he nkoaa ne asɛm he, na mmom bebebu bosombaa kɛseɛ Artemi asɔrefie nso adehunu, na n’animuonyam a Asia mantam ne asaase sɔfoɔ nyinaa de som no mpo, bɛbrɛ ase.”

english_verse: When the workers heard this, they got angry and started shouting, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Berɛ a nkurofoɔ he tee yei he, bɛ bo fuiɛ. Na bɛteateaam sɛ: “Efesofoɔ bosom Artemi yɛ kɛseɛ!”

english_verse: Soon the whole city was in a riot, and some men grabbed Gaius and Aristarchus, who had come from Macedonia with Paul. Then everyone in the crowd rushed to the place where the town meetings were held.
translation_verse: Nti kurom hɔ nyinaa bɛyɛɛ gyegyeegye, na bɛ nyinaa de koma koro bɔɔ twi kɔɔ adehwɛbea hɔ, na bɛkyee Gaio ne Aristarko a bɛyɛ Makedoniafoɔ ne Paulo akwantuo mu ahokafoɔ he.

english_verse: Paul wanted to go out and speak to the people, but the Lord's followers would not let him.
translation_verse: Paulo deɛ, na ɔpɛ sɛ ɔkɔ nnipa he mu, nso asuafoɔ he amma no kwane.

english_verse: A few of the local officials were friendly to Paul, and they sent someone to warn him not to go.
translation_verse: Mpanyimfoɔ a bɛhwɛ afahyɛ ne agodie so he mu binom mpo a na bɛpɛ n’asɛm he soma bɛsrɛɛ no sɛ ɔmmfa ne ho nto asiane mu nkɔ adehwɛbea hɔ.

english_verse: Some of the people in the meeting were shouting one thing, and others were shouting something else. Everyone was completely confused, and most of them did not even know why they were there.
translation_verse: Nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, sɛ binom teaam ka asɛm baako a, na afoforɔ nso aka asɛm foforɔ, na badwa he mu ayɛ bagyabagya, na bɛ mu dodoɔ heaa nnim nea nti a bɛabehyiam.

english_verse: Several of the Jewish leaders pushed a man named Alexander to the front of the crowd and started telling him what to say. He motioned with his hand and tried to explain what was going on.
translation_verse: Nti bɛde Aleksandro firi nnipakuo he mu baeɛ, na Yudafoɔ he twee no baa anim, na Aleksandro nyam ne nsa pɛɛ sɛ oyi n’ano kyerɛ nkurofoɔ he.

english_verse: But when the crowd saw that he was Jewish, they all shouted for two hours, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Nso berɛ a behuu sɛ ɔyɛ Yudani he, bɛ nyinaa de nne koro teateaam bɛyɛ sɛ nnɔnhwere mmienu sɛ: “Efesofoɔ bosom Artemi yɛ kɛseɛ!”

english_verse: Finally, a town official made the crowd be quiet. Then he said: People of Ephesus, who in the world doesn't know that our city is the center for worshiping the great goddess Artemis? Who doesn't know that her image which fell from heaven is right here?
translation_verse: Afei kuro he twerɛfoɔ maa nnipa he yɛɛ komm, na ɔkaa sɛ: “Efeso mmeema, nipa hwane ne onnim sɛ Efesofoɔ kuro ne hwɛ Artemi kɛseɛ he ne honyini a ɔte firi soro baeɛ he asɔredane so?

english_verse: No one can deny this, and so you should calm down and not do anything foolish.
translation_verse: Hedeɛ esiane sɛ bi ntumi nnye yeinom ho akyinnye nti, ɔfata sɛ hoyɛ komm na homfa ahoperɛ nyɔ bibiaa.

english_verse: You have brought men in here who have not robbed temples or spoken against our goddess.
translation_verse: Efiri sɛ mmeema a hode bɛ aba yi nwiaa asɔredane mu nnoɔma, na bɛnkasa ntiaa yɛ bosombaa he.

english_verse: If Demetrius and his workers have a case against these men, we have courts and judges. Let them take their complaints there.
translation_verse: Nti sɛ Demetrio ne adwumfoɔ a bɛka ne ho he wɔ bi ho asɛm a, nna a bedi asɛm ya hɔ, na amradofoɔ nso ya hɔ; sɛ bi wɔ asɛm tia ne yɔnko a, ɔmfa nkɔ ma badwa he.

english_verse: But if you want to do more than that, the matter will have to be brought before the city council.
translation_verse: Nso sɛ hoohwehwɛ bibi a ɔsene saa a, badwa a ɔya hɔ he ne ɔsɛ sɛ osi ho gyinaeɛ.

english_verse: We could easily be accused of starting a riot today. There is no excuse for it! We cannot even give a reason for this uproar.
translation_verse: Na sɛ anhwɛ a, nea asi nnɛ yi bɛma bɛabɔ yɛ soboɔ sɛ yɛyɛ amammɔfoɔ, efiri sɛ yenni asɛm biaa bɛde bɛkyerɛkyerɛ basabasayɔ yi ase.”

english_verse: After saying this, he told the people to leave.
translation_verse: Na ɔkaa yei he, ɔmaa badwa he tuiɛ.

english_verse: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the followers and encouraged them. He then told them goodbye and left for Macedonia.
translation_verse: Na afei basabasa he ano berɛ ase he, Paulo soma ma bɛkɔfrɛɛ asuafoɔ he, na ɔhyɛɛ bɛ nkurane gyaa bɛ kwane he, ogyaa bɛ hɔ kɔɔ Makedonia.

english_verse: As he traveled from place to place, he encouraged the followers with many messages. Finally, he went to Greece
translation_verse: Berɛ a ɔkɔfaa mpɔtamu hɔ na ɔhyɛɛ bɛ nkurane pii he, ɔbaa Hela,

english_verse: and stayed there for three months. Paul was about to sail to Syria. But some of the Jewish leaders plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of Macedonia.
translation_verse: bedii bosome mmeɛnsa. Na berɛ a ɔɔbɛkɔ Siria he Yudafoɔ he bɔɔ ne ho pɔ. Ne saa nti ɔyɛɛ n’adwene sɛ ɔbɛkɔ akɔfa Makedonia mmom ne akɔ.

english_verse: With him were Sopater, son of Pyrrhus from Berea, and Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica. Gaius from Derbe was also with him, and so were Timothy and the two Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus.
translation_verse: Na bɛ a bɛka ne ho ne Beroiani Piro ba Sopater, Aristako ne Sekundo a bɛyɛ Tesalonikafoɔ, Derbeni Gaio, Timoteo, Asiafoɔ Tihiko ne Trofimo.

english_verse: They went on ahead to Troas and waited for us there.
translation_verse: Yeinom dii yɛ anim kane kɔtwɛnee yɛ Troa.

english_verse: After the Festival of Thin Bread, we sailed from Philippi. Five days later we met them in Troas and stayed there for a week. Paul's Last Visit to Troas
translation_verse: Na Paanoo a Mmɔreka nnim Afahyɛ he akyi he, yɛtenaa hyɛne mu firii Filipi, na nnanum ntam he, yɛkɔtoo bɛ Troa. Na yedii nnanson hɔ. Paulo ne Troas asɔremma dii nkra

english_verse: On the first day of the week we met to break bread together. Paul spoke to the people until midnight because he was leaving the next morning.
translation_verse: Da a odi nnanwɔtwe kane he, berɛ a na yɛahyiam oobubu paanoo he, Paulo firi aseɛ sɛ ɔne bɛ ɔɔkasa, efiri sɛ na waayɛ n’adwene sɛ adeɛ kye a obefiri hɔ. Nti ɔtoaa so kasaeɛ a kosii anadwo dasuom.

english_verse: In the upstairs room where we were meeting, there were a lot of lamps.
translation_verse: Nti na nkanea pii ya abrosane so dane a na yɛahyia mu he mu.

english_verse: A young man by the name of Eutychus was sitting on a window sill. While Paul was speaking, the young man got very sleepy. Finally, he went to sleep and fell three floors all the way down to the ground. When they picked him up, he was dead.
translation_verse: Paulo gu so ɔɔkasa he, abranteɛ bi a bɛfrɛ no Eutiko a na ɔte mpomaa mu faa mu daa hatee, na berɛ he mu he ofirii abrosane a ɔtɔ so mmeɛnsa he so bɛhwee fam, na bɛmaa no so he, na waawu.

english_verse: Paul went down and bent over Eutychus. He took him in his arms and said, “Don't worry! He's alive.”
translation_verse: Nso Paulo siane kɔɔ fam kobutuu ne so, na ɔbamm no kaa sɛ, “Honhaehae, ne kra da so ya ne mu.”

english_verse: After Paul had gone back upstairs, he broke bread, and ate with us. He then spoke until dawn and left.
translation_verse: Afei Paulo foroo abrosane he, na ɔfaa paanoo diiɛ he, ɔtoa so ne bɛ dii nkɔmmɔ kɔpem ahemakye ansa na ɔɔkɔ.

english_verse: Then the followers took the young man home alive and were very happy. The Voyage from Troas to Miletus
translation_verse: Na bɛde abeemaa he baeɛ a na wasane anya nkwa. Na bɛ werɛ kyekyere paa. Paulo firii Troas kɔɔ Mileto

english_verse: Paul decided to travel by land to Assos. The rest of us went on ahead by ship, and we were to take him aboard there.
translation_verse: Afei yedii kane kɔtenaa hyɛne mu kɔɔ Aso, baabi a na yɛayɔ adwene sɛ yɛbɛfa Paulo he, efiri sɛ berɛ a ɔka kyerɛɛ yɛ sɛ yɛnyɔ saa he, ɔno ankasa yɔɔ adwene sɛ ɔbɛnante akɔ.

english_verse: When he met us in Assos, he came aboard, and we sailed on to Mitylene.
translation_verse: Na ohyiaa yɛ Aso he, yɛfaa no tenaa yɛ kodoɔ he mu de no kɔɔ Mitelene.

english_verse: The next day we came to a place near Chios, and the following day we reached Samos. The day after that we sailed to Miletus.
translation_verse: Na yɛkaa kodoɔ he firii hɔ, na adeɛ kyeeɛ he yeduruu bea bi a na ɔne Kio di animhwɛ, na da a odi hɔ he yeduruu Samo, na adeɛ kyee bio he yɛbaa Mileto,

english_verse: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, because he did not want to spend too much time in Asia. He was in a hurry and wanted to be in Jerusalem in time for Pentecost. Paul Says Goodbye to the Church Leaders of Ephesus
translation_verse: efiri sɛ na Paulo asi gyinaeɛ sɛ onnyina Efeso, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a ɔnseɛ berɛ Asia mpɔtamu, efiri sɛ na ne ho pere no sɛ, nka obetumi, akoduru Yerusalem da a bedi Pentekoste afahyɛ he. Paulo ne Efeso mpanyimfoɔ dii nkra

english_verse: From Miletus, Paul sent a message for the church leaders at Ephesus to come and meet with him.
translation_verse: Na ɔya Mileto he ɔsoma nnipa kɔɔ Efeso kɔfrɛɛ asafo mu mpanyimfoɔ he.

english_verse: When they got there, he said: You know everything I did during the time I was with you when I first came to Asia.
translation_verse: Bɛbaeɛ he ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ: “Ho ankasa honim sɛnea me ne ho tenaa berɛ nyinaa ofiri daa odi kane a mebaa Asia,

english_verse: Some of the Jews plotted against me and caused me a lot of sorrow and trouble. But I served the Lord and was humble.
translation_verse: na mede ahobrɛaseɛ kɛseɛ ne nisuo pii, ne nsɔhwɛ a ɔtoo me ɔnam Yudafoɔ pɔ a bɛbɔeɛ so som Awurade.

english_verse: When I preached in public or taught in your homes, I didn't hold back from telling anything that would help you.
translation_verse: Na mamfa nea mfasoɔ ya so a ɔsɛ sɛ meka kyerɛ ho biaa, ne Nyankopɔn asɛm a ɔsɛ sɛ mekyerɛkyerɛ ho badwam ne afie afie he ankame ho.

english_verse: I told Jews and Gentiles to turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Na mmom medii Nyankopɔn fam adwensakra ne yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo mu gyidie ho adanseɛ kyerɛɛ Yudafoɔ ne Helafoɔ nyinaa.

english_verse: I don't know what will happen to me in Jerusalem, but I must obey God's Spirit and go there.
translation_verse: Afei, hwɛ, ɔnam sɛ Honhom he hyɛ me nti meekɔ Yerusalem a mennim adeɛ a ɔbɛto me hɔ,

english_verse: In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: ɔnoaa ne sɛ kuro biaa mu he Honhom Kronkron he di adanseɛ sɛ afiase da ne taa ɔɔtwɛne me.

english_verse: But I don't care what happens to me, as long as I finish the work the Lord Jesus gave me to do. And this work is to tell the good news about God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Nso memmu me kra sɛ adehia sɛ wie a mewie me som ne adwuma a Awurade Yesu de ahyɛ me nsa sɛ menka asɛmpa a ɔfa Nyankopɔn adom he ho he.

english_verse: I have gone from place to place, preaching to you about God's kingdom, but now I know that none of you will ever see me again.
translation_verse: “Na afei menim sɛ ho a me ne ho tenaeɛ na mebɔɔ ahennie he ho dawuro he mu biaa onhu me bio.

english_verse: I tell you today I am no longer responsible for any of you!
translation_verse: Nti meeka akyerɛ ho nnɛ da yi sɛ, menni ho mu biaa mogyaa ho fɔ,

english_verse: I have told you everything God wants you to know.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ mamfa Nyankopɔn nhyehyɛeɛ biaa ansie ho.

english_verse: Look after yourselves and everyone the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be like shepherds to God's church. It is the flock he bought with the blood of his own Son.
translation_verse: Honhwɛ ho ho so yie, na honhwɛ nnipa a Honhom Kronkron he de ho ayɛ bɛ so nhwɛsofoɔ he, na honhwɛ Nyankopɔn asafo a ɔde ɔnoaa ne Ba mogyaa atɔ he so.

english_verse: I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you.
translation_verse: Menim sɛ mekɔ akyi he, mpataku bɔne bɛhyɛne ho mu a bɛɛnyɛ nwankuo he brɛbrɛ.

english_verse: Some of your own people will tell lies to win over the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Hoaa ho mu mmeema bɛsɔre akasa adane nɔkorɛ he de atwe asafo he adi bɛ akyi.

english_verse: Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
translation_verse: Nti honwɛne na honkae sɛ mfie mmeɛnsa di, awia ne anadwo, mannyae sɛ mede nisuo betu ho mu biaa fo.

english_verse: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
translation_verse: Na afei mede ho hyɛ Nyankopɔn ne asɛm a ɔfa n’adom he ho, asɛm a obetumi ahyɛ ho dene na ama hoanya agyapadeɛ bɛ a bɛate bɛ ho nyinaa mu.

english_verse: I have never wanted anyone's money or clothes.
translation_verse: M’ani mmere biaa dwetɛ, sikakɔkɔɔ anaa bi ntoma.

english_verse: You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends.
translation_verse: Ho ankasa honim sɛ mede me nsa ayɔ adwuma ahwɛ me ho ne bɛ a bɛka me ho he.

english_verse: By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, “More blessings come from giving than from receiving.”
translation_verse: Yei nyinaa mu he mede ayɔ nhwɛsoɔ ama ho sɛ ɔsɛ sɛ yɛyɔ adwuma de boa bɛ a bɛayɛ mmerɛ, na yɛkae Awurade Yesu nsɛm a ɔkaa sɛ, ‘Nhyira ya ma mu sene gyeɛ.’ ”

english_verse: After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
translation_verse: Paulo kasa wieeɛ he, ɔne bɛ nyinaa buu nkotodwe bɔɔ mpaeɛ.

english_verse: They were especially sad because Paul had told them, “You will never see me again.” Then they went with him to the ship.
translation_verse: dii awerɛhoɔ ne titire ka a ɔkaa sɛ benhu no bio he ho. Na afei bɛde no kɔɔ kodoɔ he ho.

english_verse: After saying goodbye, we sailed straight to Cos. The next day we reached Rhodes and from there sailed on to Patara.
translation_verse: Afei, yɛne bɛ tetee mu he, yɛfaa nsuo so kɔɔ tẽẽ koduru Ko. Na adeɛ kyeeɛ he yɛkɔɔ Rode, na yefiri hɔ kɔɔ Patara.

english_verse: We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
translation_verse: Na berɛ a yehuu hyɛne bi a ɔɔkɔ Foinike he, yɛkɔforo kɔeɛ.

english_verse: We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo.
translation_verse: Afei yeduruu baabi a na yɛ ani tua Kipro he, yetwaam kɔɔ Siria berɛ a na yɛagya kuro he yɛ benkum so, na yekogyinaa Tiro, efiri sɛ hɔ ne na bebeyi kodoɔ he mu nnoɔma.

english_verse: But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed.
translation_verse: Yɛ nna he soeɛ he, yefirii hɔ toaa yɛ akwantuo he so. Na bɛ nyinaa bɛne bɛ ayerɛ ne bɛ mma kogyaa yɛ kwane kosii kurotia. Na yebuu nkotodwe mpoano hɔ bɔɔ mpaeɛ

english_verse: Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
translation_verse: na yɛ ne bɛ dii nkra. Afei yɛkɔforoo kodoɔ he, na bɛsane kɔɔ fie.

english_verse: We sailed from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the followers and stayed with them for a day.
translation_verse: Afei kwane a yetu firii Tiro he de yɛ koduruu Ptolemai, na yɛkyeakyea agyidifoɔ he na yɛtenaa bɛ nkyɛne da koro.

english_verse: The next day we went to Caesarea and stayed with Philip, the preacher. He was one of the seven men who helped the apostles,
translation_verse: Adeɛ kyeeɛ he, yefirii hɔ kɔɔ Kaesarea; na yɛkɔɔ asɛmpakani Filipo a na ɔyɛ nnipa nson a asomafoɔ he de akunafoɔ aduanema ho dwumadie hyɛɛ bɛ nsa he mu baako fie, na yɛtenaa ne nkyɛne.

english_verse: and he had four unmarried daughters who prophesied.
translation_verse: Na ɔwɔ mma mmaa nnan bi a bɛnwareeɛ a na bɛyɛ nkɔmhyɛfoɔ.

english_verse: We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea.
translation_verse: Na yedii nna kakra hɔ he, diyifoɔ bi a bɛfrɛ no Agabu firi Yudea baa hɔ.

english_verse: He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, “The Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Ɔbaa yɛ nkyɛne; na ɔfaa Paulo aboomu de kyekyeree ne nane ne ne nsa. Na ɔkaa sɛ, “Nea Honhom Kronkron ɔɔka di, ‘Sei ne Yudafoɔ a bɛya Yerusalem bɛkyekyere aboomu yi wura, na bɛde no ama Amanamane mufoɔ.’ ”

english_verse: After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Yɛtee yei he, yɛ ne nkurofoɔ a bɛya hɔ he tuu Paulo fo sɛ ɔnnkɔ Yerusalem.

english_verse: But Paul answered, “Why are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!”
translation_verse: Nso obuaa sɛ, “Mmɛ ne hooyɔ yi, sɛ hode su ɔɔte me nsono mu yi? Masiesie me ho sɛ ɔnyɛ kyekyere a bɛbɛkyekyere me nkoaa, na mmom, sɛ otwa sɛ mewu mpo a mewu ama Awurade Yesu dini ya Yerusalem.”

english_verse: Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, “Lord, please make us willing to do what you want.”
translation_verse: Ɔnam sɛ na wawe ataaso sɛ ɔbɛkɔ nti, yɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Awurade pɛ ne nyɔ hɔ.” Na yɛanka bibiaa bio.

english_verse: Then we got ready to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Na nna yi akyi he, yɛyɔɔ ahoboa siim kɔɔ Yerusalem.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
translation_verse: Asuafoɔ a bɛya Kaesarea nso ne yɛ kɔeɛ de yɛ koduruu Kiproni Minason a na ɔyɛ asuafoɔ adikanfoɔ he mu bi fie. Na yɛ ne no tenaeɛ. Paulo kɔsraa Yakobo ya Yerusalem

english_verse: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us.
translation_verse: Berɛ a yeduruu Yerusalem he, anuanom de anigyeɛ gyee yɛ.

english_verse: Paul went with us to see James the next day, and all the church leaders were present.
translation_verse: Adeɛ kyeeɛ he, Paulo ne yɛ kɔsraa Yakobo, na yɛkɔtoo mpanyimfoɔ he nyinaa nso hɔ.

english_verse: Paul greeted them and told how God had used him to help the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Na ɔkyea bɛ wieeɛ he, ofirii aseɛ kaa nea Nyankopɔn nam ne som adwuma he so ayɔ no Amanamane mufoɔ mu he nyinaa kyerɛɛ bɛ.

english_verse: Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul: My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Na bɛtee yei he, bɛhyɛɛ Nyankopɔn animuonyam. Na afei bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Onua, woahu Yudafoɔ mpempem a bɛyɛ agyidifoɔ; na bɛ nyinaa bɛbɔ bɛ ho mmɔdene mmara he ho.

english_verse: But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
translation_verse: Nea bɛaka afa wo ho akyerɛ bɛ ne sɛ wokyerɛkyerɛ Yudafoɔ a bɛya Amanamane mu nyinaa sɛ bɛmmane mfiri Mose mmara he ho, na woka kyerɛ bɛ sɛ benntwa bɛ mma twetia na bennni amanneɛ he so.

english_verse: What should we do now that our people have heard you are here?
translation_verse: Nti afei, ɛɛne ne yɛbɛyɔ afa saa asɛm yi ho? Nea ɔbɛyɛ biaa bɛbɛte sɛ woaba ha.

english_verse: Please do what we ask, because four of our men have made special promises to God.
translation_verse: Nti yɔ nea yɛbɛka akyerɛ wo yi: Yɛwɔ mmeema nnan bi a bɛahyɛ bɔ soronko sɛ beetua bɛ ho nnoɔma bi berɛ yi mu.

english_verse: Join with them and prepare yourself for the ceremony that goes with the promises. Pay the cost for their heads to be shaved. Then everyone will learn that the reports about you are not true. They will know you do obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Ka saa mmeema yi ho, na homfa nhyehyɛeɛ a ɔfa ahodwira ho he mu, na afei tua sika a ɔsɛ sɛ betua yi beti he kyɛ bɛ. Na yei bɛma biaa ahu sɛ wo ho asɛm a bɛteeɛ he nyɛ nɔkorɛ, na mmom wodi mmara he so na wo bɔ ho bane nso.

english_verse: Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: Na agyidifoɔ a befiri Amanamane mu he deɛ, yɛde gyinaeɛ a yɛasi he akɔ ma bɛ sɛ bɛntwe bɛ ho mfiri bibi a bɛde abɔ afɔdeɛ ama bosom ne mogyaa ne mmoa a bɛaku bɛ a bɛanhwie bɛ mogyaa angu, ne adwamammɔ ho.”

english_verse: The next day Paul took the four men with him and got himself ready at the same time they did. Then he went into the temple and told when the final ceremony would take place and when an offering would be made for each of them. Paul Is Arrested
translation_verse: Nti Paulo faa mmeema he. Na adeɛ kyeeɛ a odwiraa ne ho he, ɔne bɛ kɔɔ asɔrefie hɔ kɔkyerɛɛ ahodwira nna he wie a bebewie he, na afei bɛbɔɔ biaa ti so afɔdeɛ. Nnipakuo he kyee no

english_verse: When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him.
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a nnanson a bɛayi asi hɔ ama ahodwira he ɔɔba awieeɛ he, na Yudafoɔ a befiri Asia huu no asɔrefie hɔ he, befirii aseɛ maa nnipa he yɛɛ gyegyeegye na bɛsosɔɔ ne mu

english_verse: They were shouting, “Friends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.”
translation_verse: teateaam sɛ, “Israel mmeema, hommɛboa yɛ! Yei ne kyerɛkyerɛ nnipa nyinaa ya baabiaa de sɔre tia mane he ne Mmara ne bea yi, na nea ɔkyɛne ne nyinaa ne sɛ, ɔde Helafoɔ baa asɔrefie ha de beguu kronkronbea yi ho fi.”

english_verse: Some of them thought that Paul had brought Trophimus from Ephesus into the temple, because they had seen them together in the city.
translation_verse: Na bɛadi kane ahu Efesoni Trofimo sɛ ɔka ne ho ya kuro he mu, na besusu sɛ Paulo de no baa asɔrefie hɔ.

english_verse: The whole city was in an uproar, and the people turned into a mob. They grabbed Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Then suddenly the doors were shut.
translation_verse: Na kuro hɔ nyinaa yɛɛ gyegyeegye, na nnipa he nyinaa bɔɔ twi baeɛ. Na bɛsosɔɔ Paulo mu twee no firii asɔrefie hɔ. Na ntɛmaa bɛtotoo apono he mu.

english_verse: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
translation_verse: Na berɛ a bɛɛhwehwɛ sɛ bebeku no he, asrafokuo so sahene he nyaa amanebɔ sɛ Yerusalem nyinaa ayɛ gyegyeegye;

english_verse: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
translation_verse: na ntɛmaa ɔfaa asraafoɔ ne bɛ mpanyimfoɔ kɔɔ bɛ so. Berɛ a bɛ ani bɔɔ sahene he ne asraafoɔ he so he, begyae Paulo hweɛ.

english_verse: The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done.
translation_verse: Na sahene he bɛnee hɔ kɔsɔɔ ne mu hyɛɛ sɛ bɛmfa nkɔnsɔnkɔnsɔn mmienu ngu no. Na ofirii aseɛ bisaa nipa ko a ɔyɛ ne nea wayɔ.

english_verse: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
translation_verse: Nso nnipa he mu bi teaam kaa yei, na binom nso ateam aka asɛm foforɔ. Nti berɛ a bɛantumi anhu asɛm he mu yie ɔnam sakasakayɛ he nti, ɔhyɛɛ sɛ bɛmfa no mmra asraafoɔ asoeɛ hɔ.

english_verse: As they reached the steps, the crowd became so wild that the soldiers had to lift Paul up and carry him.
translation_verse: Nso berɛ a oduruu dane-atwedeɛ he so he, osiane nnipakuo he basabasayɔ nti, asraafoɔ he maa no so.

english_verse: The crowd followed and kept shouting, “Kill him! Kill him!” Paul Speaks to the Crowd
translation_verse: Na nnipadɔm he kɔɔ so dii n’akyi teateaam sɛ, “Honyi no mfiri hɔ,” Paulo yii ne ho ano

english_verse: When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, “Can I say something to you?” “How do you know Greek?” the commander asked.
translation_verse: Na berɛ a bɛɛyɛ de no aduru asraafoɔ asoeɛ hɔ he, Paulo bisaa sahene he sɛ, “Mewɔ ho kwane sɛ meka asɛm bi kyerɛ ho anaa?” Obuaa sɛ, “Wobetumi aka Hela kasa?

english_verse: “Aren't you that Egyptian who started a riot not long ago and led
translation_verse: Ɔnnyɛ wo ne Misraimni a nnansa yiaa wokɔfaa amammɔsɛm baeɛ a wode mmeema awudifoɔ mpemnan firii adi kɔɔ serɛ so he?”

english_verse: “No!” Paul replied. “I am a Jew from Tarsus, an important city in Cilicia. Please let me speak to the crowd.”
translation_verse: Paulo buaa sɛ, “Meyɛ Yudani a mefri Tarso a ɔya Kilikia, kuro a odi mu he mu. Nti mesrɛ wo ma me kwane na me ne me manfoɔ he nkasa.”

english_verse: The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
translation_verse: Berɛ a ɔmaa no kwane he, Paulo a na ogyina dane-atwedeɛ so he nyamm ne nsa kyerɛɛ nnipa he. Afei berɛ a hɔ yɛɛ komm he, ɔde Hebri kasa ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ:

english_verse: “My friends and leaders of our nation, listen as I explain what happened!”
translation_verse: “Anuanom ne agyanom, hontie me na menyi nsɛm yi ano nkyerɛ ho.”

english_verse: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
translation_verse: Bɛtee sɛ ɔde Hebri kasa ne ɔɔkasa bɛ ho he, bɛyɛɛ dinn paa. Na ɔkaa sɛ,

english_verse: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
translation_verse: “Meyɛ Yudani a bɛwoo me Tarso a ɔya Kilikia, nso bɛtetee me kuro yi mu, na metenaa Gamaliel nane ase suaa adeɛ. Na ɔkyerɛkyerɛɛ me nananom Mmara he pɛpɛɛpɛ, na mebɔɔ Nyamesom ho mmɔdene sɛnea ho nyinaa yɔ nnɛ yi.

english_verse: I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women.
translation_verse: Na metaa bɛ a bɛfa Yesu nkyerɛkyerɛɛ kwane so kosii sɛ mede binom mpo kɔɔ wuom. Me kyekyee mmeema ne mmaa nyinaa ma bɛde bɛ guu afiase,

english_verse: The high priest and all the council members can tell you this is true. They even gave me letters to the Jewish leaders in Damascus, so that I could arrest people there and bring them to Jerusalem to be punished.
translation_verse: sɔfopanyini ne mpanyimfoɔ badwa he nyinaa di me adanseɛ. Megyegyee tumi-krataa firii bɛ hɔ de kɔmaa anuanom a bɛya Damasko. Na mesiim sɛ meekɔ Damasko akɔkyekye nnipa a bɛya hɔ a bɛnam Yesu nkyerɛkyerɛɛ kwane so de bɛ aba Yerusalem ama bɛatwe bɛ aso.

english_verse: One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
translation_verse: “Na meekɔ a meeyɛ aduru Damasko, berɛ a na abɔ bɛyɛ awia dummienu he, mpofiremuaa, hann kɛseɛ bi hyerɛn firii soro twaa me ho hyiaeɛ,

english_verse: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: na metwa hwee fam, metee nne bi sɛ, ‘Saulo, Saulo, mmɛ nti na wotaa me?’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I answered. The Lord replied, “I am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.”
translation_verse: Mebuaa sɛ, ‘Wo ne hwane, owura?’ Na ɔka kyereɛ; me sɛ, ‘Ɔyɛ me Nasareni Yesu, a wotaa no he.’

english_verse: Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
translation_verse: Afei mmeema a bɛka me ho he huu hann he nso bɛante nne a na ɔne me ɔɔkasa he.

english_verse: I asked, “Lord, what do you want me to do?” Then he told me, “Get up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: Na mekaa sɛ, “Owura, ɛɛne ne menyɔ?” Na Awurade ka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Sɔre, kɔ Damasko na hɔ ne wobɛte bibiaa ɔsɛ sɛ woyɔ.”

english_verse: The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
translation_verse: Na esiane sɛ na hann he hyerɛn kɛse paa nti, manhu hwee, na bɛ a me ne bɛ nam he sosɔɔ me nsa de me kɔɔ Damasko.

english_verse: In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there.
translation_verse: “Afei beema bi a bɛfrɛ no Anania a ɔyɛ nyamesuroni sɛnea Mmara he ɔɔhwehwɛ a Yudafoɔ a bɛtete hɔ nyinaa bɔ no dini pa he

english_verse: He came to me and said, “Saul, my friend, you can now see again!” At once I could see.
translation_verse: baa me nkyɛne, na obegyinaa me nkyɛne ka kyerɛɛ me sɛ “Onua Saulo, sane hu adeɛ!” Saa dɔno heaa mu, me sane huu adeɛ, na mehuu no.

english_verse: Then Ananias told me, “The God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
translation_verse: Na afei ɔkaa sɛ, ‘Yɛ nananom Nyankopɔn ayi wo sɛ hu n’apɛdeɛ, na hu Teneneeni he na tie ɔno ankasa nne,

english_verse: You must tell everyone what you have seen and heard.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ wobedi nea woahu na woate he ho adanseɛ akyerɛ nnipa nyinaa ama no.

english_verse: What are you waiting for? Get up! Be baptized, and wash away your sins by praying to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Na afei mmɛ nti ne wootwentwɛne wo nane ase? Sɔre, ma bɛmmɔ wo asu, nhohoro wo bɔne berɛ a wabɔ Awurade dini yi.’

english_verse: After this I returned to Jerusalem and went to the temple to pray. There I had a vision
translation_verse: “Nso berɛ a mesane baa Yerusalem a meebɔ mpaeɛ asɔrefie hɔ he, menyaa anisoadehunu.

english_verse: of the Lord who said to me, “Hurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.”
translation_verse: Na mehuu Yesu sɛ ɔɔka akyerɛ me sɛ, ‘Ma wo ho nyɛ hare na firi Yerusalem ntɛm, efiri sɛ bɛmpene me ho adanseɛ a wodi he so.’

english_verse: I replied, “Lord, they know that in many of our synagogues I arrested and beat people who had faith in you.
translation_verse: Na mekaa sɛ, ‘Awurade, bɛ ankasa benim pefee sɛ na mekye bɛ a bɛgye wo di he gu afiase hwe bɛ hyiadane ahodoɔ mu.

english_verse: Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.”
translation_verse: Na berɛ a beehwie wo danseni Stefano mogyaa agu he, na m’ankasa nso megyina hɔ bi a mepenee so, na mehwɛɛ bɛ a beeku no he ntaadeɛ ngugusoɔ so.’

english_verse: But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Nso ɔka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, ‘Sɔre kɔ, efiri sɛ, mɛsoma wo akɔ Amanamane a ɔya akyirikyiri mu.’ ” Bɛkyee Paulo

english_verse: The crowd listened until Paul said this. Then they started shouting, “Get rid of this man! He doesn't deserve to live.”
translation_verse: Na betiee no besii saa asɛm yi so, na bɛmaa bɛ nne so kaa sɛ, “Yi nipa yi firi asaase so, efiri sɛ ɔmfata sɛ ɔtena ase!”

english_verse: They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air. Paul and the Roman Army Commander
translation_verse: Na esiane sɛ na bɛɛteateam toto bɛ ntaadeɛ gu na beetu mfuturo gu mframa mu nti,

english_verse: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
translation_verse: sahene he hyɛɛ sɛ bɛmfa no mmra asraafoɔ asoeɛ hɔ, na bentwa no mmaa mfa nhwehwɛ ne nsɛm mu, na ama wahu adeɛ pɔtee a nti bɛɛteateam gu no so saa he.

english_verse: While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, “Is it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?”
translation_verse: Na berɛ a bɛtwee Paulo mu a beebetwa no mmaa he, obisaa sraani panyini a ogyina hɔ he sɛ, “Nti mmara ma kwane sɛ hotwa Romani mmaa berɛ a hommuu no fɔ?”

english_verse: When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and said, “What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!”
translation_verse: Sraani panyini he tee yei he, ɔkɔɔ sahene he nkyɛne kɔbɔɔ no amanneɛ sɛ, “Mmɛ ne wopɛ sɛ woyɔ yi? Saa beema yi yɛ Romani”

english_verse: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” Paul answered.
translation_verse: Nti sahene he baeɛ; bebisaa no sɛ, “Ka kyerɛ me, woyɛ Romani, anaa?” Na ɔkaa sɛ, “Ɛɛ!”

english_verse: The commander then said, “I paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.” But Paul replied, “I was born a Roman citizen.”
translation_verse: Sahene he buaa sɛ, “Mede sika kɛseɛ paa ne tɔɔ Roma manbayɛ yi.” Na Paulo kaa sɛ, “Me mmom deɛ bɛwoo me toom.”

english_verse: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
translation_verse: Amonom hoaa bɛ a bɛɛbɛhwehwɛ ne nsɛm mu he firii ne nkyɛne kɔeɛ. Na berɛ a sahene he huu sɛ ɔyɛ Romani na bɛakyekyere no he, osuroeɛ. Bɛde Paulo kogyinaa asennibea

english_verse: The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.
translation_verse: Nti adeɛ kyeeɛ he, pɛɛ sɛ ohu adekorɔ pɔtee a nti Yudafoɔ he abɔ no so soboɔ he, ɔsanee no na ɔhyɛɛ asɔfo mpanyimfoɔ he ne Sanhedrin he nyinaa ma behyiaam. Na ɔde Paulo begyinaa bɛ mfimfini.

english_verse: Paul looked straight at the council members and said, “My friends, to this day I have served God with a clear conscience!”
translation_verse: Paulo hwɛɛ Sanhedrin badwa he gyee kaa sɛ, “Anuanom, mede ahonim pa abɔ me bra Nyankopɔn anim abesi nnɛ da yi.”

english_verse: Then Ananias the high priest ordered the men standing beside Paul to hit him on the mouth.
translation_verse: Na sɔfo panyini Anania hyɛɛ bɛ a begyinagyina ne nkyɛne he sɛ bɛmmɔ no sotorɔ.

english_verse: Paul turned to the high priest and said, “You whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.”
translation_verse: Na Paulo ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Nyankopɔn bɛbɔ wo, wo a wote sɛ fasuo a bɛaka ho fitaa. Wotena ase bu me atɛne sɛnea Mmara he teɛ, na berɛ koro heaa nso woabu Mmara he so hyɛ sɛ bɛmmɔ me?”

english_verse: The men standing beside Paul asked, “Don't you know you are insulting God's high priest?”
translation_verse: Bɛ a begyinagyina hɔ he kaa sɛ, “Nyankopɔn sɔfo panyini ne wo di no atɛm he?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “Oh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.”
translation_verse: Na Paulo kaa sɛ, “Anuanom, na mennim sɛ ɔyɛ sɔfo panyini. Na bɛatwerɛ sɛ, ‘Nka wo mane sodifoɔ ho asɛmmɔne.’ ”

english_verse: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, “My friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.”
translation_verse: Paulo huu sɛ nnipa he bi yɛ Sadukifoɔ na binom nso yɛ Farisifoɔ he, ɔteaa mu Sanhedrin badwa he mu sɛ, “Anuanom, meyɛ Farisini, Farisifoɔ ba. Wusɔreɛ ho anidasoɔ ho nti ne beebu me atɛne yi.”

english_verse: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
translation_verse: Ɔkaa yei he, akasakasaa baa Farisifoɔ ne Sadukifoɔ he mu, na nnipa he mu kyɛeɛ.

english_verse: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, “We don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.”
translation_verse: Nti hɔ yɛɛ gyegyeegye, na atwerɛfoɔ a befiri Farisifoɔ kuo mu binom sɔre kasakasa dendenndene sɛ, “Yenhu mfomsoɔ biaa nipa yi ho, efiri sɛ ebia na honhom anaa bɔfoɔ bi ne akasa kyerɛ no.”

english_verse: The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a akasakasaa he mu yɛɛ dene he, na sahene he suro sɛ bɛbɛtete Paulo mu asisinasini, nti ɔhyɛɛ asraafoɔ he sɛ bɛnkɔgye no mfiri bɛ nsa mu mfa no mmra asraafoɔ asoeɛ hɔ.

english_verse: That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, “Don't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.” A Plot To Kill Paul
translation_verse: Saa anadwo he Awurade begyinaa ne nkyɛne kaa sɛ, “Nya akokoɔduro! Sɛnea wo adi me ho adanseɛ Yerusalem he, saa ara nso ne otwa sɛ wodi me ho adanseɛ Roma nso.” Bɛbɔɔ Paulo ti so pɔ

english_verse: The next morning more than
translation_verse: Nnipa bɛboro aduannan ne kɔɔ saa apam yi.

english_verse: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, “We have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
translation_verse: Bɛkɔɔ sɔfo panyini ne mpanyimfoɔ he hɔ kɔkaa sɛ, “Yɛaka ntam dendenndene sɛ, sɛ yenkuu Paulo a, yɛmfa aduane nka yɛ ano.

english_verse: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.”
translation_verse: Nti afei, ho ne badwa he mma sahene nte sɛ ho pɛ sɛ ɔde no bra ho na hohwehwɛ n’asɛm he mu fann. Na yɛ deɛ, yɛmma no nnuru ha mpo na yɛaku no.”

english_verse: When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it.
translation_verse: Na Paulo wɔfaseɛ tee sɛ nkurofoɔ he akɔtɛ no; nti ɔkɔ kɔhyɛnee abane he mu koyii Paulo asotire.

english_verse: So Paul said to one of the army officers, “Take this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.”
translation_verse: Na Paulo frɛɛ asraafoɔ ha so mpanyimfoɔ he mu baako ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Fa abranteɛ yi kɔ safohene nkyɛne, na ɔwɔ asɛm bi ka kyerɛ no.”

english_verse: The officer took him to the commander and said, “The prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.”
translation_verse: Na ɔfaa no de no kɔɔ safohene he nkyɛne kaa sɛ, “Deduani Paulo frɛɛ me, na ɔka kyerɛɛ me sɛ memfa abranteɛ yi mmra wo, na ɔwɔ asɛm bi ka kyerɛ wo.”

english_verse: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, “What do you want to tell me?”
translation_verse: Na safohene he sɔɔ ne nsa na ɔne no no kɔɔ kokoam baabi. Na obisaa no sɛ, “Ɛɛne ne wowɔ ka kyerɛ me?”

english_verse: He answered, “Some men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him.
translation_verse: Obuaa sɛ, “Yudafoɔ he ayɔ adwene sɛ bɛbɛsrɛ wo na wode Paulo aba dwam kyena sɛnea bebebisa ne ho asɛm mu yie.

english_verse: The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, “Don't let anyone know you told me this.” Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
translation_verse: Na safohene he gyaa abranteɛ he kwane na ɔhyɛɛ no sɛ, “Nka nkyerɛ biaa sɛ woayi me asɛm yi ho asotire.” Bɛde Paulo kɔmaa Felike ya Kaesarea

english_verse: The commander called in two of his officers and told them, “By nine o'clock tonight have
translation_verse: Afei ɔfrɛɛ asraafoɔ ha so mpanyimfoɔ he mu mmienu bi kaa sɛ, “Honsiesie asraafoɔ ahaanu, apɔnkɔsotefoɔ aduɔson, ne peakurafoɔ ahanu, anadwo nnɔnkron berɛ mu nkɔ Kaesarea.

english_verse: Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.”
translation_verse: Afei nso bensiesie mmoa na bɛmfa Paulo ntena so mfa no dwoodwoo nkɔma Amrado Felike.”

english_verse: The commander wrote a letter that said:
translation_verse: Na ɔtwerɛɛ nwoma a mu asɛm ne sɛ:

english_verse: Greetings from Claudius Lysias to the Honorable Governor Felix:
translation_verse: “Klaudio Lisia de krataa yi kyea Amrado pa Felike.

english_verse: Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
translation_verse: Beema yi a Yudafoɔ kɔfaa no sɛ nka bebeku no na me ne m’asraafoɔ kɔgyee no he, maate sɛ ɔyɛ Romani.

english_verse: I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council
translation_verse: Na mepɛɛ sɛ nka mehu adekodeɛ a bɛde ɔɔbɔ no kwaadu he, na mede no kɔɔ bɛ badwam.

english_verse: and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
translation_verse: Na mehuu sɛ bɛ mmara ho nsemmisa nti ne bɛne no wɔ nkurobɔ, na bɛamfa bibi a ɔsɛ wuo anaa kyeɛ anto no so.

english_verse: As soon as I learned that there was a plot against him, I sent him to you and told their leaders to bring charges against him in your court.
translation_verse: Nso bɛayi me asotire sɛ nnipa bi ɔɔtɛ beema he. Nti meema bɛde no abra wo, na maahyɛ ne nkurobɔfoɔ he sɛ bɛmmɛka nea bɛwɔ fa ne ho w’anim.”

english_verse: The soldiers obeyed the commander's orders, and that same night they took Paul to the city of Antipatris.
translation_verse: Nti asraafoɔ he faa Paulo, sɛnea bɛhyɛɛ bɛ he, na bɛde no kɔɔ Antipatri anadwo he.

english_verse: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
translation_verse: Na adeɛ kyeeɛ he, bɛmaa apɔnkɔsotefoɔ he ne no toaa so kɔeɛ, na asraafoɔ he sane baa abane he mu bio.

english_verse: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
translation_verse: Na beduruu Kaesarea he, beyii nwoma he maa amrado he, na bɛde Paulo nso kogyinaa n’anim.

english_verse: The governor read the letter. Then he asked Paul and found out that he was from Cilicia.
translation_verse: Na amrado he kenkane nwoma he wieeɛ he, obisaa mane korɔ a ofiri mu; na ɔtee sɛ ofiri Kilikia he,

english_verse: The governor said, “I will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.” After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
translation_verse: ɔkaa sɛ, “Wo nkurobɔfoɔ he ba a, na metie wo.” Na ɔhyɛɛ sɛ bɛmfa no nsie Herode abane mu.

english_verse: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.
translation_verse: Nnanum akyi he, sɔfo panyini Anania ne mpanyimfoɔ bi ne Tertulo a ogyina badwa anim kasa he baeɛ. Na bɛde Paulo ho nsɛm bɛtoo amrado he anim.

english_verse: So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him: Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off.
translation_verse: Berɛ a bɛfrɛɛ Paulo baeɛ he, Tertulo hyɛɛ aseɛ bɔɔ no kwaadu sɛ: Nimuonyamfoɔ Felike, esiane sɛ ɔnam wo so ne yenya asomdwie, na wo, ne wo nhunumu ama nsakraeɛ aba mane yi mu nti,

english_verse: All of us are always grateful for what you have done.
translation_verse: yɛde aseda a ɔkyɛne so gye yeinom berɛ nyinaa ya baabiaa.

english_verse: I don't want to bother you, but please be patient with us and listen to me for just a few minutes.
translation_verse: Nso sɛ ɔbɛyɔ na mennsɛe wo brɛ pii bio nti, mesrɛ wo sɛ firi w’anyamyɛ mu tie yɛ kakra.

english_verse: This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes.
translation_verse: Yɛahu beema yi sɛ busuyefoɔ a otutu gu Yudafoɔ nyinaa asom ma bɛte atua asaase nyinaa so, na ɔyɛ Nasarefoɔ kuo he mu panyini.

english_verse: When he tried to disgrace the temple, we arrested him. If you question him, you will find out for yourself that our charges are true.
translation_verse: Wo ankasa wobetumi abisabisa no ahu nea yɛde ɔɔbɔ no soboɔ yi nyinaa mu.

english_verse: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
