translation_verse: Yudafoɔ he nso de bɛ ho gyee mu bi sɛ, nsɛm yi te saa. Paulo yii ne ho ano Felike anim

english_verse: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
translation_verse: Berɛ a amrado he maa Paulo kwane sɛ ɔnkasa he, obuaa sɛ, “Esiane sɛ menim yie sɛ woayɔ mane yi temmufoɔ mfie pii nti, ɔyɛ me anigyeɛ sɛ meeyi m’ano m’asɛm yi ho.

english_verse: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
translation_verse: Sɛnea wobetumi anyɔ nhwehwemu ahu he, mekɔɔ Yerusalem kɔsomeeɛ he, onni mmoroo nnafua dummienu.

english_verse: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
translation_verse: Ɔnyɛ adeɛ a behuu me sɛ me ne bi agye akyinnye ya asɔrefie hɔ anaa meehyɛ nnipakuo bi kutupa de basabasayɔ aba hyia adane anaa kuro he mu.

english_verse: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
translation_verse: Na bentumi nso nya soboɔ a bɛɛbɔ me seeseiaa yi ho adanseɛ mma wo.

english_verse: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
translation_verse: Nso megye nɔkwasɛm yi tom ya w’anim sɛ nea bɛfrɛ no fekusɛm yiaa ne Kwane a menam so som me nananom Nyankopɔn, efiri sɛ megye nea Mmara he ka na bɛatwerɛ Adiyifoɔ nwoma he mu he di.

english_verse: I am just as sure as these people are that God will raise from death everyone, good or evil.
translation_verse: Na mewɔ anidasoɔ ya Nyankopɔn mu, a bɛ nso bɛwɔ saa anidasoɔ heaa bi, sɛ berɛ bi bɛba a bɛ a bɛtene ne bɛ a bɛntene nyinaa bɛsɔre afiri awufoɔ mu.

english_verse: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
translation_verse: Nti berɛ nyinaa mehwɛ hu sɛ menyɔ bɔne biaa ntiaa Nyankopɔn ne nnipa.

english_verse: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
translation_verse: Nti mekokyinkyinii Amaneamane mu mfie kakra he, mesane baa Yerusalem de adɔeyɛdeɛ ne ayɛyɛdeɛ braa me mane.

english_verse: This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
translation_verse: Berɛ a meeyɔ yei he behuu me asɔrefie hɔ sɛ meedwira me ho, nso na nnipakuo anaa hooyɔ biaa nni hɔ.

english_verse: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
translation_verse: Mmom, na Yudafoɔ bi a befiri Asia mantam he mu ne bɛya hɔ, na nka ɔsɛ sɛ bɛba w’anim na sɛ bɛwɔ bibi tia me a, bɛde ato dwa.

english_verse: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
translation_verse: Anaa, ma mmeema yi ankasa nka bɔne a behuu sɛ meyɔeɛ berɛ a na megyina Sanhedrin he anim he.

english_verse: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, “I am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!”
translation_verse: Menkaa hwee gye asɛm baako yi a meteaa mu kaa sɛ, ‘Awufoɔ sɔreeɛ nti ne beebu me atɛne ho anim nnɛ yi!’ ”

english_verse: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, “I will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.”
translation_verse: Nso esiane sɛ na Felike nim Kwane he ho asɛm yie nti, otuu nkurofoɔ he nsɛm he hyɛɛ da kaa sɛ, “Berɛ biaa sahene Lisia bɛba he, mebu nsɛm yi ho atɛne.”

english_verse: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
translation_verse: Na ɔhyɛɛ asraafoɔ ha so panyini he sɛ ɔnhwɛ Paulo so na bɛannha no, na bɛmmara ne ho nipa biaa a ɔbɛba abɛsra no. Felike tuu asɛm he hyɛɛ mfie mmienu akyi

english_verse: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Nna bi akyi he, Felike ne ne yerɛ Drusila a ɔyɛ Yudani he baeɛ, na ɔsoma ma bɛkɔfrɛɛ Paulo baeɛ, na betiee nea ɔwɔ ka fa Kristo Yesu mu gyidie ho.

english_verse: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, “That's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.”
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a Paulo kaa tenenee, ahohyɛsoɔ ne atemmuo a ɔbɛba ho asɛm he, hu kaa Felike ma ɔkaa sɛ, “Firi m’anim ha kɔ, na sɛ menya berɛ a mɛsane asoma abɛfrɛ wo.”

english_verse: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
translation_verse: Nso saa berɛ yi nyinaa na n’ani da so sɛ Paulo bɛma no sika. Nti na ɔtaa soma kɔfrɛ no ne no bɛbɔ nkɔmmɔ.

english_verse: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
translation_verse: Mfie mmienu akyi he, Porkio Festo bedii Felike adeɛ, na esiane sɛ na Felike pɛ sɛ onya Yudafoɔ he anim adom nti, ɔmaa Paulo kɔɔ soaa daa afiase.

english_verse: Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Afei Festo baa ne mane a odi so amrado he mu. Na nnansa akyi, ofiri Kaesarea kɔɔ Yerusalem.

english_verse: There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus
translation_verse: Na asɔfoɔ mpanyimfoɔ ne Yudafoɔ atitire he bɛkaa Paulo ho nsɛmmɔne kyerɛɛ no. Na befirii aseɛ paa no kyɛw

english_verse: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
translation_verse: srɛɛ sɛ ɔnyɔ bɛ adom nsoma nkɔfrɛ beema he mmra Yerusalem. Na bɛabɔ pɔ paa sɛ bɛbɛtɛ kwane ho aku no.

english_verse: But Festus told them, “Paul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
translation_verse: Nso Festo buaa sɛ bɛde Paulo ato afiase ya Kaesarea na ɔɔnnkyɛre biaa ɔnoaa nso bɛkɔ.

english_verse: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.”
translation_verse: Ɔkaa sɛ, “Nti homma bɛ a bɛya ho mu a bɛyɔ tumi he ne me nkɔ, na sɛ bibi a ɔmfa kwane mu ya beema he ho a, bɛabɔ no soboɔ.”

english_verse: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
translation_verse: Nti berɛ a odii bɛ nkyɛne bɛyɛ nnawɔtwe anaa dadu he, ɔsane kɔɔ Kaesarea, na adeɛ kyeeɛ he, ɔtenaa atemmuo adwa so hyɛɛ sɛ bɛmfa Paulo mmra.

english_verse: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.
translation_verse: Berɛ a Paulo baeɛ he, Yudafoɔ a na befiri Yerusalem he betwaa ne ho hyiaeɛ, kaa nsɛm akɛseɛ bebree guu ne so a bɛantumi amfa ho adanseɛ biaa amma.

english_verse: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, “I have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.”
translation_verse: Paulo yii ne ho ano sɛ, “Menyɔɔ bɔne biaa a otia Yudafoɔ Mmara anaa asɔrefie he anaa Kaesare.”

english_verse: Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, “Are you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?”
translation_verse: Afei esiane sɛ Festo pɛ sɛ onya Yudafoɔ he anim adom nti, obisaa Paulo sɛ, “Wopɛ sɛ wokɔ Yerusalem na bedi asɛm he m’anim anaa?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “I am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
translation_verse: Na Paulo buaa sɛ, “Megyina Kaesare atemmuo adwa anim, na ha na ɔsɛ sɛ bedi m’asɛm. Menyɛɛ Yudafoɔ bɔne biaa, sɛnea wo ankasa wonnim no pefee he.

english_verse: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.”
translation_verse: Nso sɛ meyɛ bɔnefoɔ a maayɔ bibiaa a ɔsɛ sɛ mewu a, mennwane wuo he; nso sɛ nea bɛde ato me so yi mu biaa nnim a, biaa ntumi mfa me nnhyɛ bɛ nsa mfa mpata bɛ. Mede asɛm he dane Kaesare.”

english_verse: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, “You have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!” Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
translation_verse: Afei Festo ne abadwafoɔ he de bɛ ti sɛɛ ho he, Festo buaa sɛ, “Kaesare ne wode asɛm he akɔdane no nti, Kaesare hɔ ne wobɛkɔ.” Festo kaa Paulo ho asɛm kyerɛ Herode Agripa

english_verse: A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus.
translation_verse: Nna bi akyi he, Hene Agrippa ne Berenike baa Kaesarea bɛkyeaa Festo.

english_verse: They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said: Felix left a man here in jail,
translation_verse: Na esiane sɛ na bebedi nna kakra ya hɔ nti, Festo de Paulo asɛm he too hene he anim sɛ, “Beema bi ya ha a Felike gyae no afiase,

english_verse: and when I went to Jerusalem, the chief priests and the Jewish leaders came and asked me to find him guilty.
translation_verse: na berɛ a meya Yerusalem he, asɔfo mpanyimfoɔ ne Yudafoɔ mpanyimfoɔ de ne ho asɛm baeɛ, srɛɛ sɛ memmu no fɔ.

english_verse: I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
translation_verse: Na mebuaa bɛ sɛ ɔnnyɛ Romafoɔ amanneɛ sɛ bebu bi fɔ ama bɛ a bɛabɔ no soboɔ berɛ a onii he nyaa ho kwane nyii ne ho ano bɛ anim.

english_verse: So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in.
translation_verse: Nti berɛ a bebehyia ha he, mantwentwɛne me nane ase koraa, na adeɛ kyeeɛaa na metenaa atemmuo adwa so hyɛɛ sɛ bɛmfa beema he mmra.

english_verse: But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would.
translation_verse: Ɔsɔre gyinaeɛ he, bɛ a bɛɛbɔ no soboɔ he antumi anka ne ho asɛmmɔne biaa sɛnea mesusuiɛ he.

english_verse: Instead, they argued with him about some of their beliefs and about a dead man named Jesus, who Paul said was alive.
translation_verse: Ɔne sɛ, bɛne no dii anobaabaeɛ faa bɛ ankasa bɛ som ne Yesu bi a owuiɛ a Paulo ka dendenndene sɛ ɔte ase he ho.

english_verse: Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there.
translation_verse: Afei na menhu nea menyɔ mfa anobaabaeɛ yi ho, nti mebisaa sɛ ɔpɛ sɛ ɔkɔ Yerusalem na bekodi asɛm he ya hɔ anaa.

english_verse: But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
translation_verse: Nso berɛ a Paulo srɛɛ sɛ bɛmfa no nkɔto afiase nkosi berɛ a Tumfoɔ he bebua asɛm he, mehyɛɛ sɛ bɛnhwɛ ne so nkosi sɛ mede no bɛkɔ akɔma Kaesare.”

english_verse: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would also like to hear what this man has to say.” Festus answered, “You can hear him tomorrow.”
translation_verse: Na Agrippa ka kyerɛɛ Festo sɛ, “Mɛpɛ sɛ me ankasa metie beema he ano asɛm.” Ɔkaa sɛ, “Kyena wobɛte n’ano asɛm.” Bɛde Paulo kogyinaa Agripa anim

english_verse: The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in
translation_verse: Nti adeɛ kyeeɛ he Agrippa ne Benike baeɛ a na bɛahyehyɛ bɛ ho dɔsɔɔ, na bɛ ne asahene ne kuro he mu atitire he kɔɔ baabi a betie asɛm he, na Festo maa bɛde Paulo baeɛ.

english_verse: and said: King Agrippa and other guests, look at this man! Every Jew from Jerusalem and Caesarea has come to me, demanding for him to be put to death.
translation_verse: Na Festo kaa sɛ, “Hene Agrippa ne nnipa a hoka yɛ ho ya ha nyinaa, beema a ho ani tua no yi yɛ bi a Yudafoɔ dɔm he srɛɛ me Yerusalem ne ha nso, teateaam sɛ ɔnsɛ sɛ ɔtena nkwa mu bio.

english_verse: I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
translation_verse: Nso mehuu sɛ ɔnyɔɔ bibiaa ɔfata sɛ owu. Nti berɛ a ɔde asɛm he danee Tumfoɔ he, meyɔɔ m’adwene sɛ mede no bɛkɔ.

english_verse: I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write.
translation_verse: Nso mennim asɛm a mentwerɛ mfa ne ho nkɔma yɛ Wura. Nti mede no baa ho anim, titire wo Hene Agrippa, na sɛ wohwehwɛ asɛm he mu wie a, manya bibi atwerɛ,

english_verse: It makes no sense to send a prisoner to the Emperor without stating the charges against him.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ nyansa biaa nnim sɛ mede deduani bɛkɔ a menkyerɛ asɛm a bɛde ato no so he.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Paul, “You may now speak for yourself.” Paul stretched out his hand and said:
translation_verse: Agrippa ka kyerɛɛ Paulo sɛ, “Wowɔ ho kwane sɛ woyi w’ano.” Na Paulo tenee ne nsa firi aseɛ yii ne ho ano sɛ,

english_verse: King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me.
translation_verse: “Ɔyɛ me dɛ sɛ sommoɔ a Yudafoɔ de abɔ me nyinaa he, wo Hene Agrippa w’anim ne meebeyi m’ano nnɛ da yi,

english_verse: You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
translation_verse: ne titire ne sɛ wonim amanneɛ nyinaa ne akyinnyegyeɛ a ɔya Yudafoɔ mu. Ne saa nti mesrɛ wo, to wo bo ase na tie me.

english_verse: Now I am on trial because I believe the promise God made to our people long ago.
translation_verse: Na seesei bɔhyɛ a Nyankopɔn de maa yɛ agyanom ho adanseɛ nti ne megyina atemmuo adwa anim yi;

english_verse: Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me.
translation_verse: na yɛ mmusuakuo dummienu he som Nyankopɔn anibereɛ so, awia ne anadwo a bɛ ani da so sɛ bɛ nsa bɛka saa bɔhyɛ yi. Nimuonyamfoɔ, saa anidasoɔ yi ho na Yudafoɔ abɔ me soboɔ yi.

english_verse: Why should any of you doubt that God raises the dead to life?
translation_verse: “Mmɛ nti ne honnye nni sɛ Nyankopɔn nyane awufoɔ?

english_verse: I once thought that I should do everything I could to oppose Jesus from Nazareth.
translation_verse: “Me nso, na medwene me mu sɛ ɔsɛ sɛ meyɔ nnoɔma pii de ko tia Nasareni Yesu dini,

english_verse: I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed.
translation_verse: na saa pɛpɛɛpɛ ne meyɔeɛ Yerusalem, na menyaa tumi firii asɔfo mpanyimfoɔ he hɔ de ahoteefoɔ bebree guu afiase, na sɛ beeku bɛ a mefoa so.

english_verse: I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
translation_verse: Na metwee bɛ aso mprɛ pii hyia adane nyinaa mu sɛnea bɛbɛka bibi de apo bɛ gyidie. Na esiane m’ani a na abere bɛ so paa nti, mekɔɔ nkuro foforɔ so mpo kɔtaa bɛ hɔ. Paulo adwensakra ho asɛm

english_verse: King Agrippa, one day I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and permission of the chief priests.
translation_verse: “Berɛ a megu so ɔɔyɔ yie he mede tumi ne ahyɛdeɛ a menya firii asɔfoɔ mpanyimfoɔ hɔ he siim sɛ meekɔ Damasko.

english_verse: About noon I saw a light brighter than the sun. It flashed from heaven on me and on everyone traveling with me.
translation_verse: Na oduruu awigyina he, me wura Nimuonyamfoɔ, mehuu hann bi a ano ɔdene sene awia sɛ ahyerɛn firi soro atwa me ne bɛ a bɛne me ɔɔkɔ he ho ahyia kwane he so.

english_verse: We all fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice say to me in Aramaic, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me? It's foolish to fight against me!”
translation_verse: Na berɛ a yɛ nyinaa hwehwee fam he, metee sɛ nne bi ka kyerɛɛ me Hebri kasa mu sɛ, ‘Saulo, Saulo, mmɛ nti ne wootaa me? Sɛ woto boɔ bɔ dane a ɔba woaa wo so.’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I asked. Then the Lord answered, “I am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: Na mebisaa sɛ, ‘Ɔyɛ wo hwane, owura?’ Na Awurade ka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, ‘Ɔyɛ me Yesu a wotaa no he.

english_verse: Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.”
translation_verse: Afei, sɔre gyina. Na yei nti ne mama woahu me, sɛ meeyi wo sɛ somfoɔ ne adansefoɔ nea woahu ne nea mɛma woahu afa me ho nyinaa ho.

english_verse: The Lord also said, “I will protect you from the Jews and from the Gentiles that I am sending you to.
translation_verse: Na mɛgye wo nso afiri mmane yi ne Amanamane a meesoma wo akɔ bɛ nkyɛne he nsam,

english_verse: I want you to open their eyes, so they will turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God. Then their sins will be forgiven, and by faith in me they will become part of God's holy people.”
translation_verse: na woabie bɛ ani, adane bɛ afiri sum mu aba hann mu, na bɛafiri Satan tumi mu aba Nyankopɔn nkyɛne, na bɛanya bɔne fakyɛ ne agyapadeɛ bɛ a bɛnam me mu gyidie so ate bɛ ho mu.’

english_verse: King Agrippa, I obeyed this vision from heaven.
translation_verse: “Yei akyi he, Hene Agrippa, manyɔ anisoadeɛ a menya firii soro he ho asoɔdene,

english_verse: First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, “Stop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.”
translation_verse: na mmom, medii kane kasa kyerɛɛ Damaskofoɔ. Na afei mekɔɔ Yerusalem, ne Yudea mantam nyinaa mu, ne amanamane mufoɔ hɔ kɔkaa sɛ, sɛ bɛnsakra bɛ adwene na bɛmfa nnwuma a ɔkyerɛ adwensakra so nane mmra Nyankopɔn nkyɛne.

english_verse: That is why some men grabbed me in the temple and tried to kill me.
translation_verse: Yei nti ne Yudafoɔ kyee me asɔrefie hɔ pɛɛ sɛ beku me.

english_verse: But all this time God has helped me, and I have preached both to the rich and to the poor. I have told them only what the prophets and Moses said would happen.
translation_verse: Nso mmoa a manya afiri Nyankopɔn nkyɛne nti ne makɔ so atena hɔ abesi nnɛ a megyina ha oodi adanseɛ akyerɛ nipasu ahodoɔ nyinaa yi, menkaa hwee, gye nsɛm a Adiyifoɔ he ne Mose kaeɛ sɛ ɔbɛba he,

english_verse: I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: sɛ Kristo he behu amane, na esiane sɛ bɛadi kane anyane no afiri awufoɔ mu nti, wabɔ hann ho dawuro akyerɛ, nkurofoɔ yi ne Amanamane he.” Paulo tuu Agripa fo sɛ ɔnnye Kristo nni

english_verse: Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, “Paul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.”
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a ɔɔka nsɛm yi de ayi n’ano he, Festo de nne kɛseɛ teaam sɛ, “Paulo, woobɔ dam! Adesua pii ɔɔyɛ ama woabɔ dam!”

english_verse: But Paul replied, “Honorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense.
translation_verse: Na Paulo kaa sɛ, “Ɔnyɛ dam ne meebɔ, Nimuonyamfoɔ Festo, na mmom meeka nsɛm a ɔyɛ nɔkorɛ na ɔtɔ asom.

english_verse: None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.”
translation_verse: Nɔkwasɛm di, hene a mede akokoɔduro ɔɔkasa akyerɛ no yi nim saa nsɛm yi mu yie, na menim sɛ nsɛm yi mu biaa nni hɔ a ahinta no, efiri sɛ ɔnyɛ adeɛ a bɛyɔɔ no kokoam.

english_verse: Then Paul said to Agrippa, “Do you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.”
translation_verse: Hene Agrippa, nti wogye Adiyifoɔ he di? Menim sɛ wogye bɛ di.”

english_verse: Agrippa asked Paul, “In such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?”
translation_verse: Na Agrippa ka kyerɛɛ Paulo sɛ, “Nti wodwene sɛ wobetumi de berɛ tiaa yi ama me abɛyɛ Kristoni anaa?”

english_verse: Paul answered, “Whether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.”
translation_verse: Na Paulo buaa sɛ, “Sɛ menya a, nka berɛ tiaa bi oo, bere tentene bi oo, ɔnyɛ wo nkoa, na mmom nka Nyankopɔn nyɔ bɛ a beetie me nnɛ yi nyinaa nso sɛnea meteɛ yi, na mmom bengu ho mpokyerɛ sɛ me.”

english_verse: Then King Agrippa, Governor Festus, Bernice, and everyone who was with them got up.
translation_verse: Na hene he ne amrado he ne Berenike ne mmeema a bɛne bɛ tete hɔ he sɔreeɛ.

english_verse: But before they left, they said, “This man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.”
translation_verse: Na berɛ a bɛɛkɔ he, asɛm a na bɛkaa ne sɛ, “Aa! Nipa yi nyɔɔ bibiaa a ɔsɛ sɛ owu anaa begu no mpokyerɛ.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Festus, “Paul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.”
translation_verse: Afei nso Agrippa ka kyerɛɛ Festo sɛ, “Sɛ ɔnyɛ sɛ nipa yi de asɛm he akɔdane Kaesare a, nka bebetumi agyae.”

english_verse: When it was time for us to sail to Rome, Captain Julius from the Emperor's special troops was put in charge of Paul and the other prisoners.
translation_verse: Na afei besii gyinaeɛ wieeɛ sɛ yɛnkɔ Italia he, bɛde Paulo ne nneduafoɔ bi hyɛɛ panyini bi a ne dini de Yulio a ofiri Roma Tumfoɔ asraafoɔ kuo he mu nsa.

english_verse: We went aboard a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to some ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus from Thessalonica in Macedonia sailed on the ship with us.
translation_verse: Yɛkɔtenaa Adramitio hyɛne bi a na ɔɔkɔ Asia fam he mu. Na Makedoniani Aristarko a ofiri Tesalonika he ka yɛ ho.

english_verse: The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed.
translation_verse: Adeɛ kyeeɛ he, yekoduruu Sidon. Yulio yii dɔ adi kyerɛɛ Paulo sɛ ɔmaa ne kwane maa no kɔsraa ne nnamfo sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a bɛbɛma no nnoɔma a ho hia no he.

english_verse: When we left Sidon, the winds were blowing against us, and we sailed close to the island of Cyprus to be safe from the wind.
translation_verse: Esiane sɛ yetuu hɔ he, na mframa ɔɔbɔ hyia yɛ nti, yɛka kɔɔ Kipro nifa fam.

english_verse: Then we sailed south of Cilicia and Pamphylia until we came to the port of Myra in Lycia.
translation_verse: Yɛnam po so kɔfaa Kilikia ne Pamfilia koduruu Mira a ɔya Likia mantam mu he mu.

english_verse: There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
translation_verse: Hɔ ne asraafoɔ panyini he huu hyɛne bi a ofiri Aleksandria ɔɔkɔ Italia. Nti ɔde yɛ kɔtenaa mu.

english_verse: We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind.
translation_verse: Hyɛne he kɔɔ bɔkɔɔ ma yedii nna pii ya po he so. Yɛbrɛeɛ ansa na yeeduru Kinido. Esiane sɛ na mframa ɔɔbɔ hyia yɛ nti, yɛfaa Kreta anaafoɔ fam baabi a na hɔ dwo sanee Salmone ho.

english_verse: We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
translation_verse: Yɛka kɔɔ mpoano nkakrankakra kosii sɛ yebeduruu baabi a bɛfrɛ hɔ Hyɛne-Agyinaeɛ-Pa a ɔbɛne kuro Lasea.

english_verse: By now we had already lost a lot of time, and sailing was no longer safe. In fact, even the Great Day of Forgiveness was past.
translation_verse: Yedii nna pii po he so, na osiane sɛ na mmuadadie he mpo atwam, na saa berɛ he ɔɔkɔ afe he awieeɛ a po so fa ayɛ hu nti, yɛtenaa hɔ kyɛreeɛ. Paulo tuu bɛ fo sɛ,

english_verse: Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, “Men, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.”
translation_verse: “Anuanom, mehu sɛ, sɛ yɛtoa yɛ akwantuo yi so a, yebehu amane ama ebia hyɛne he mpo abɔ ama mu nnoɔma asɛe ama mu nnipa ahwere bɛ nkwa.”

english_verse: But Julius listened to the captain of the ship and its owner, rather than to Paul.
translation_verse: Nso asraafoɔ panyini he antie Paulo afutuo he, na mmom otiee nsɛm a hyɛnkafoɔ he ne hyɛne he wura kaeɛ he.

english_verse: The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there. The Storm at Sea
translation_verse: Osiane sɛ na hyɛngyinabea hɔ nyɛ mma bɛ sɛ bɛbɛtena hɔ awɔ berɛ mu he nti, nnipa he pii pɛɛ sɛ, sɛ ɔbɛyɛ yie a, nka hyɛne he betu afiri hɔ akɔ Foinike, a ɔyɛ hyɛngyinabea a ɔya Kreta a ɔda atifi ne anafoɔ ntam ya atɔeɛ fam, ɔyɛ baabi a bebetumi atena hɔ awɔberɛ mu. Ahum tuu po he so

english_verse: When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete.
translation_verse: Mframa a ano ɔdene bɔ firii anafoɔ fam. Yei maa nnipa he susuu sɛ bebetumi atoa bɛ akwantuo he so sɛnea bɛahyehyɛ he. Nti betuu sɛkyɛ he de hyɛne he faa Kreta mpoano.

english_verse: days you have been so worried that you haven't eaten a thing.
translation_verse: Ankyɛre koraa ne mframa kɛseɛ bi a bɛfrɛ no “Atifi Apieɛ Mframa” bɔ firii supɔ he so.

english_verse: The wind struck the ship, and we could not sail against it. So we let the wind carry the ship.
translation_verse: Mframa he bɔ bunkam faa yɛ hyɛne he so twee no kɔɔ po he mu. Yɛbɔɔ mmɔdene sɛ yɛbɛdane hyɛne he ani na anyɛ yie he, yegyae maa mframa he twee no kɔeɛ.

english_verse: We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place,
translation_verse: Yeduruu supɔ ketewa bi a bɛfrɛ no Klauda ho a na hɔ mframa ano nyɛ dene mpo he, yɛbrɛeɛ ansa na yeetumi atwe kodoɔ a ɔsɛne hyɛne he akyi he aba mu.

english_verse: but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
translation_verse: Hyɛne he mu adwumayɔfoɔ maa so baa hyɛne he mu, kyekyeree no dendenndene. Osiane sɛ na besuro sɛ bɛbɛkɔ akotim anwea mu nti, beyiyii hyɛne dua he so ntoma maa mframa he bɔɔ hyɛne he kɔɔ faako a ɔpɛ.

english_verse: The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard.
translation_verse: Ahum he kɔɔ so tuu dendenndene kosii sɛ adeɛ kyeeɛ mpo, beyiyii hyɛne he mu nnoɔma he bi to guu po he mu.

english_verse: Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear.
translation_verse: Ne nnansa so he, bɛsesaa hyɛne he ho nnoɔma he bi to guiɛ.

english_verse: For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
translation_verse: Nna bebree twaa mu a na yenhu awia anaa nsoromma a na ahum he gu so ootu. Yei maa yɛ anidasoɔ nyinaa saeɛ. Paulo kyekyeree bɛ werɛ

english_verse: Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men: You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss.
translation_verse: Nna bebree twaa mu a na biaa nnidiiɛ. Afei Paulo ka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Anuanom, sɛ hotiee me na yɛantu amfiri Kreta a, nka ɔnyɛ ɔno ne sɛ yeehu amane yi.

english_verse: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
translation_verse: Nso homma ho bo ntɔ ho yam, efiri sɛ, ho mu biaa oonwu na mmom hyɛne he ne bɛbɔ.

english_verse: I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel
translation_verse: Nnora anadwo, Nyankopɔn a meyɛ ne deɛ a mesom no he bɔfoɔ baa me nkyɛne

english_verse: to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.”
translation_verse: bɛka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, ‘Paulo, nsuro! Nea obesi biaa wobeduru Kaesare anim ama wadi w’asɛm. Bɛ a bɛka wo ho yi nso, Nyankopɔn adom nti, bibiaa ɔnyɔ bɛ.’

english_verse: Cheer up! I am sure that God will do exactly what he promised.
translation_verse: Anuanom, homma ho bo ntɔ ho yam, efiri sɛ, megye Nyankopɔn di na asɛm a waka akyerɛ me he bɛba mu pɛpɛɛpɛ.

english_verse: But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
translation_verse: Nso nea ɔbɛyɛ biaa, mframa bɛbɔ hyɛne he de yɛ akogu supɔ bi mpoano.”

english_verse: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
translation_verse: Yei nti betwitwaa hyɛne he asɛkyɛ ahoma mu maa asɛkyɛ he guu po mu. Afei bɛsansanee homa a okita akyerɛkyerɛkwane he mu na afei besii ntoma a ɔya hyɛne he anim he sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a mframa betumi abɔ hyɛne he akɔ mpoano.

english_verse: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
translation_verse: Hyɛne he mu adwumayɔfoɔ pɛɛ sɛ bedwane firi hyɛne he mu. Nti begyaa kodoɔ a na ɔsene hyɛne akyi he sii po he ani boapa yɛɛ sɛnea bɛɛkɔto sɛkyɛ ya hyɛne he anim.

english_verse: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
translation_verse: Osiane sɛ na besuro sɛ anhwɛ a hyɛne he bɛpem abotane bi nti, bɛtoo asɛkyɛ nnan firii hyɛne he akyi, na bɛtwɛne sɛ adeɛ bɛkye.

english_verse: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.”
translation_verse: Paulo ka kyerɛɛ asraafoɔ panyini he ne asraafoɔ a bɛka ne ho he sɛ, “Sɛ hyɛne yi mu adwumayɔfoɔ he dwane a, hobewuwu.”

english_verse: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
translation_verse: Yei nti, asraafoɔ he twaa homa a ɔkyekyere kodoɔ he mu he ma ɔkɔeɛ.

english_verse: Just before daylight Paul begged the people to eat something. He told them, “For
translation_verse: Adeɛ ɔɔyɛ akye he, Paulo srɛɛ bɛ nyinaa sɛ bennidi. Nea ɔkaeɛ ne sɛ, “Nnawɔtwe mmienu di, ho mu biaa nni hwee.

english_verse: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
translation_verse: Mesrɛ ho, biaa mfa bibi nka n’ano sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a hobenya ahoɔdene, na bibiaa ɔnyɔ ho mu biaa.”

english_verse: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
translation_verse: Paulo kasa wieeɛ he, ɔfaa paanoo bɔɔ mpaeɛ, daa Nyankopɔn ase bɛ nyinaa bɛ anim, na obuu bi diiɛ.

english_verse: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
translation_verse: Amonom hɔaa nnipa he nyinaa bo tɔɔ bɛ yam ma befirii aseɛ didiiɛ.

english_verse: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
translation_verse: Biaa didi meeɛ he, hyɛne he mu adwumayɔfoɔ he too aburo a ɔya hyɛne he mu he guu po he mu maa hyɛne he mu yɛɛ hare. Suhyɛn he bɔeɛ

english_verse: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
translation_verse: Adeɛ kyeeɛ he, hyɛne he mu adwumayɔfoɔ he anhu sɛ bɛaduru mpoano. Behuu baabi a po he adidi akɔ asaase he mu a nsuo taa hɔ. Nti bɛyɔɔ bɛ adwene sɛ bɛbɛka hyɛne he akogyina.

english_verse: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
translation_verse: Nso hyɛne he kɔpemm anweatam bi ma otimiiɛ. Hyɛne he anim tim dendenndene na n’akyi he nso, asorɔkye a na ɔɔbɔ no he maa obubuiɛ.

english_verse: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping.
translation_verse: Asraafoɔ he yɛɛ bɛ adwene sɛ bebekuku nneduafoɔ he nyinaa sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a biaa oonnya kwane nnwane.

english_verse: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
translation_verse: Nso osiane sɛ na asraafoɔ panyini he pɛ sɛ ɔgye Paulo nkwa he nti, wamma bɛ ho kwane. Mmom, ɔhyɛɛ sɛ bɛ a bebetumi adware he mfiti aseɛ nnware nkɔ mpoano.

english_verse: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
translation_verse: Bɛ a bɛaka he nso mu binom tetaree ntaaboo ne nnua asisinasini a abubu firi hyɛne he mu he so kɔɔ mpoano. Saa kwane yi so ne yɛnam beduruu mpoano hɔ dwoodwoo.

english_verse: When we came ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta.
translation_verse: Berɛ a yekoduruu dwodwo he, yehuu sɛ bɛfrɛ supɔ he Malta.

english_verse: The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
translation_verse: Na nnipa a bɛya hɔ he yɛ pɔtɔfoɔ; na bɛdaa ayamyɛ sononko adi kyerɛɛ yɛ. Na nsuo a na ɔɔtɔ ne awɔ a na ɔyam nti, bɛsɔɔ gya maa yɛ de gyee yɛ nyinaa fɛ so.

english_verse: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
translation_verse: Nso berɛ a Paulo boaboaa mmabaa bi ano a na ɔde oogu gya he mu he, ɔnam hyeɛ a na ofiri gya he mu ɔɔba nti, wɔ bi firii mmabaa he mu bɛto momanee ne nsa ho.

english_verse: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
translation_verse: Berɛ a pɔtɔfoɔ he ani bɔɔ wɔ he so sɛ ɔsɛne ne nsa ho he, bɛka kyerɛɛ bɛ ho bɛ ho sɛ, “Akyinnye biaa nni hɔ sɛ nipa yi yɛ wudifoɔ, na ɔyam sɛ waafiri po he mu afiri dwoodwoo deɛ, nso aweretɔ nti, ɔmma sɛ obenya ne ti adidi mu.”

english_verse: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed.
translation_verse: Nso Paulo petee wɔ he too gya he mu a hwee anyɔ no.

english_verse: The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, “This man is a god.”
translation_verse: Na bɛ deɛ, na bɛhwɛ kwane sɛ ɔɔbɛhonhono anaa oobetwa ahwe awu amonom hɔaa. Berɛ a bɛtwɛne berɛ tentene a bɛanhu sɛ bɔne bi ato no he, bɛsesaa bɛ adwene firii aseɛ kaa sɛ ɔyɛ nyame.

english_verse: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
translation_verse: Afei na supɔ he so kunyini a bɛfrɛ no Publio he wɔ nsaase ya mpɔtamu hɔ. Na ɔgyee yɛ fɛ so de nnansa som yɛ hɔhoɔ fɛ so.

english_verse: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
translation_verse: Nso ɔbaa sɛ na Publio papa da hɔ a abunu ne konkoriwa akye no, na Paulo kɔɔ ne nkyɛne kɔbɔɔ mpaeɛ de ne nsa guu ne so saa no yadeɛ.

english_verse: After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed.
translation_verse: Yei akyi he, supɔ he so nnipa a aka a bɛwɔ nyarewa nso firii aseɛ baa ne nkyɛne maa ɔsaa bɛ yadeɛ.

english_verse: The people were very respectful to us, and when we sailed, they gave us everything we needed. From Malta to Rome
translation_verse: Na bɛ nso bɛde akyɛdeɛ pii dii yɛ ni, na berɛ a yeefiri hɔ akɔ he, bɛmaa yɛ nnoɔma pii a ho behia yɛ. Paulo koduruu Roma

english_verse: Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as “The Twin Gods.”
translation_verse: Abosome mmeɛnsa akyi he, yɛforoo hyɛne bi a ofiri Aleksandria a obedii awɔberɛ supɔ he so a n’agyinahyɛdeɛ ne “Po so Abosom Ntaafoɔ”.

english_verse: We arrived in Syracuse and stayed for three days.
translation_verse: Na yekogyinaa Sirakuse dii hɔ nnansa,

english_verse: From there we sailed to Rhegium. The next day a south wind began to blow, and two days later we arrived in Puteoli.
translation_verse: hɔ ne yefiri kontono koduruu Regio. Na da a odi hɔ he, anaafoɔ mframa bi bɔeɛ, na ne nnanu so he yekoduruu Puteoli.

english_verse: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
translation_verse: Yehuu agyidifoɔ bi ya hɔ, na bɛsrɛɛ yɛ sɛ yɛntena bɛ nkyɛne nnanson. Na afei yɛkɔɔ Roma.

english_verse: Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged. Paul in Rome
translation_verse: Na agyidifoɔ tee yɛ nka he, befirii hɔ behyiaa yɛ Apia Dwa so ne bea bi a bɛfrɛ hɔ Nsadane-Mmeɛnsa, na Paulo ani bɔɔ bɛ so pɛ na ɔdaa Nyankopɔn ase na otwaa n’akoma too ne yam.

english_verse: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
translation_verse: Afei berɛ a yɛkɔɔ Roma he, bɛmaa Paulo kwane maa ɔno nkoaa kɔtenaeɛ, na na sraani a ɔwɛne no ka ne ho. Paulo ne Yudafoɔ mpanimfoɔ nkɔmmɔbɔ

english_verse: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
translation_verse: Nso, nnansa akyi he, ɔfrɛfrɛɛ Yudafoɔ he mu atitire he ma bebehyiaeɛ. Berɛ a behyiaeɛ he, ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Anuanom, ɔyam sɛ menyɔɔ bibiaa a otia manfoɔ anaa yɛ nananom amanneɛ deɛ, nso bɛde me hyɛɛ Romafoɔ nsa sɛ deduani ya Yerusalem.

english_verse: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
translation_verse: Na berɛ a bɛhwehwɛɛ asɛm he mu wieeɛ he, na bɛpɛ sɛ begyaa me efiri sɛ na menyɛɛ hwee a ɔsɛ wuo.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders disagreed, so I asked to be tried by the Emperor. But I don't have anything to say against my own nation.
translation_verse: Na pene a Yudafoɔ he ampene so he nti, mede asɛm he kɔdane Kaesare, na ɔnyɛ sɛ ebia mewɔ bibi a mede ɔɔbɔ me mane soboɔ.

english_verse: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
translation_verse: Nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, asɛm yi ho nti ne mesrɛɛ sɛ mepɛ sɛ mehu ho ne ho kasa, efiri sɛ Israel anidasoɔ nti ne bɛagu me nkɔnsɔnkɔnsɔn yi.”

english_verse: The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
translation_verse: Bɛka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Yennyaa wo ho krataa biaa mfirii Yudea, na anuanom a bɛaba he mu biaa mmɛbɔɔ amanneɛ, na bɛmmɛkaa wo ho nsɛmmɔne biaa.

english_verse: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
translation_verse: Nso yesusuu sɛ, ɔyɛ sɛ yebetie w’adwene, efiri sɛ, nɔkwasɛm di, yenim sɛ bɛkasa tia saa fekuo yi baabiaa.” Paulo kaa asɛm ya Roma

english_verse: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
translation_verse: Bɛhyɛɛ da a bebehyia he wieeɛ he, nnipa pii baa baabi a na ɔda he. Na ɔkɔkyerɛɛ asɛm he mu kyerɛɛ bɛ dii Nyankopɔn ahennie he ho adanseɛ, na ɔnam Mose mmara ne Adiyifoɔ he so kaa Yesu ho asɛm a ɔtɔ asom, firi anɔpa kosi anwummerɛ.

english_verse: Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not.
translation_verse: Na binom gyee nea ɔkaeɛ he diiɛ, binom nso annye anni.

english_verse: Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, “The Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet
translation_verse: Na osiane sɛ na bɛ adwene nhyia nti, befirii aseɛ sɛ bɛɛkɔ, na Paulo de asɛm yi kaa ho sɛ, “Honhom Kronkron faa diyifoɔ Yesaia so kaa no yei kyerɛɛ ho nananom sɛ:

english_verse: to tell our ancestors, ‘Go to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
translation_verse: ‘Kɔ na kɔka kyerɛ mane yi sɛ: Te deɛ, hobɛte nso honte aseɛ na hwɛ deɛ hobɛhwɛ nso honhu,

english_verse: All of you have stubborn hearts. Your ears are stopped up, and your eyes are covered. You cannot see or hear or understand. If you could, you would turn to me, and I would heal you.’ ”
translation_verse: efiri sɛ mane yi akoma apirim, na bɛde bɛ aso tie kwa, na bɛakata bɛ ani, na bɛamfa bɛ ani anhu, amfa bɛ aso antie, na bɛamfa bɛ akoma ante aseɛ na bɛansakra amma mansa bɛ yadeɛ.’

english_verse: Paul said, “You may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.”
translation_verse: [Na ɔkaa yei nyinaa wieeɛ he, Yudafoɔ he firii hɔ kɔeɛ na bɛgyegyee akyinnye bɛ mu.]

english_verse: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
translation_verse: Ɔtenaa hɔ mfie mmienu a na ɔnoaa hwɛ ne ho. Na ɔgyee bɛ a bɛbaa ne nkyɛne nyinaa fɛ so,

english_verse: He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
translation_verse: na ɔkaa Nyankopɔn ahennie ho asɛm de nnamyɛ kɛseɛ kyerɛkyerɛɛ bɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo ho nsɛm a bibiaa ansi ho kwane.

english_verse: From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus. God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news
translation_verse: Paulo, Yesu Kristo akoa a bɛafrɛ no sɛ ɔmmɛyɛ somafoɔ na bɛayi no asi hɔ ama Nyankopɔn asɛmpa he

english_verse: that he promised long ago by what his prophets said in the holy Scriptures.
translation_verse: a ɔnam n’adiyifoɔ so hyɛɛ ho bɔ siee twerɛnsɛm kronkron he mu he,

english_verse: This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
translation_verse: na bɛde tumi ayi no adi akyerɛ sɛ ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn Ba a ɔnam honhom ahoteɛ kronkron so awufoɔ sɔreeɛ mu; ɔno ne Yesu Kristo, yɛ Awurade.

english_verse: Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith.
translation_verse: Ɔno so ne yɛnam anya adom ne somafodwuma sɛ yɛde setie mu gyidie ɔɔbra Amanamane mufoɔ nyinaa ne dini nti,

english_verse: You are some of those people chosen by Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: ho a bɛabɔ ho dini afrɛ ho ama Yesu Kristo he, ho nso hoka ho bi.

english_verse: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
translation_verse: Me Paulo ne meetwerɛ krataa yi de abra ho, Nyankopɔn adɔfoɔ, a hoya Roma nyinaa, ho a bɛafrɛ ho sɛ hommɛyɛ ahoteefoɔ he: Adom ne asomdwie a ofiri yɛ Agya Nyankopɔn ne Awurade Yesu Kristo nka ho. Aseda mpaebɔ

english_verse: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
translation_verse: Nea odi kane paa ne sɛ, menam Yesu Kristo so da me Nyankopɔn ase ma ho nyinaa, efiri sɛ bɛka ho gyidie ho asɛm wiase baabiaa.

english_verse: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn a mede me honhom som no a mepae mu ka ne Ba he ho asɛmpa he, ɔno ne me danseni sɛ mennyae ho nkaeɛ wɔ me mpaebɔ mu,

english_verse: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
translation_verse: na mesrɛ sɛ Nyankopɔn pɛ a menya kwane aba ho nkyɛne.

english_verse: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
translation_verse: Na m’ani agyina sɛ mehu ho sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a metumi ne ho akyɛ honhom adom akyɛdeɛ bi ama ahyɛ ho dene,

english_verse: What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
translation_verse: ɔne sɛ yɛ nyinaa gyidie bɛhyehyɛ yɛ ho yɛ ho dene.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places.
translation_verse: Anuanom agyidifoɔ, mepɛ sɛ hohu sɛ mpɛn pii ne maabɔ me tim pɔ sɛ mɛba ho nkyɛne , na ama matwa aba bi afiri ho hɔ sɛnea mayɔ no Amanamane a aka he hɔ.

english_verse: It doesn't matter if people are civilized and educated, or if they are uncivilized and uneducated. I must tell the good news to everyone. That's why I am eager to visit all of you in Rome. The Power of the Good News
translation_verse: saa nti ne me ho pere me sɛ mɛpaem aka asɛmpa he akyerɛ ho nso a hoya Roma. Asɛmpa he mu tumi

english_verse: I am proud of the good news! It is God's powerful way of saving all people who have faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Na m’ani nwu asɛmpa he ho; efiri sɛ ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn tumi a ɔnam so gye biaa a ɔwɔ gyidie nkwa, Yudafoɔ ne bedi kane, na afei Helafoɔ nso.

english_verse: The good news tells how God accepts everyone who has faith, but only those who have faith. It is just as the Scriptures say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.” Everyone Is Guilty
translation_verse: Na asɛmpa he mu ne Nyankopɔn tenenee da adi firi gyidie mu kɔ gyidie mu; sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Nea ɔtene he nam gyidie so ne benya nkwa.” Nnipa nyinaa fobuo

english_verse: From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn abufuo ayi adi atia bɛ a bɛde nnoɔma a ɔntene ne amumuyɔ berɛ nɔkorɛ he ase nyinaa.

english_verse: They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them.
translation_verse: Na nea bebetumi ahu afa Nyankopɔn ho he da adi pefee, efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn ayi no adi akyerɛ bɛ.

english_verse: God's eternal power and character cannot be seen. But from the beginning of creation, God has shown what these are like by all he has made. That's why those people don't have any excuse.
translation_verse: Ewom sɛ Nyankopɔn daa tumi ne ne nyamesu yɛ adeɛ a ani nhu deɛ, nso ofiri adebɔ mfitiaseɛ he, ne nnoɔma a wayɔ he ada no adi pefee, ama bɛde adwene ahu mu. Nti benni anoyie biaa;

english_verse: They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ benim Nyankopɔn deɛ, nso bɛamfa anidie amma no sɛ Nyankopɔn na bɛanna no ase, nso nea bɛdwene he yɛ nsemhunu, na bɛ adwene hunu he yɛɛ sum.

english_verse: They claim to be wise, but they are fools.
translation_verse: Besusuu sɛ bɛwɔ nyansa, nso bɛdanee nkwaseafoɔ;

english_verse: They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
translation_verse: na bɛsesaa Nyankopɔn a onwu da he animuonyam maa nipa a owu anaa nnomaa, ntɔteboa ne mmoa a bɛwea fam nsɛso.

english_verse: So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies.
translation_verse: Ne saa nti Nyankopɔn gyae bɛ maa bɛ akoma akɔnnɔ, na akɔnnɔ he de bɛ kɔɔ nnoɔma a ho nte ho, na nnoɔma a ho nte he maa bɛsɛe bɛ nipadua,

english_verse: They gave up the truth about God for a lie, and they worshiped God's creation instead of God, who will be praised forever. Amen.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ bɛde Nyankopɔn ho nɔkorɛ sesaa torɔ na bɛsomm abɔdeɛ sene sɛ bɛbɛsom Bɔadeɛ, ɔno ne nhyira nka no daa! Amen.

english_verse: God let them follow their own evil desires. Women no longer wanted to have sex in a natural way, and they did things with each other that were not natural.
translation_verse: Yei nti Nyankopɔn gyaa bɛ maa akɔnnɔ porɔeɛ. Bɛ mmaa de bɛ honam fam nhyiamudie a ɔnam ne kwane so he sesaa nea ɔnnam ne kwane so,

english_verse: Men behaved in the same way. They stopped wanting to have sex with women and had strong desires for sex with other men. They did shameful things with each other, and what has happened to them is punishment for their foolish deeds.
translation_verse: saa ara nso ne bɛ mmeema nso, gyaa nhyiamudie a ɔsɛ sɛ bɛ ne mmaa hyiam, maa benyaa akɔnnɔ maa bɛ yɔnko mmeema. Mmeema ne mmeema bɔɔ aniwuo bra na benyaa bɛ bɔne he so akatua.

english_verse: Since these people refused even to think about God, he let their useless minds rule over them. That's why they do all sorts of indecent things.
translation_verse: Na esiane sɛ bɛammu Nyankopɔn nti, Nyankopɔn gyae bɛ maa adwene a ɔmfata na bɛyɔɔ nnoɔma hunu a ɔnsɛ sɛ bɛyɔ koraa.

english_verse: They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip,
translation_verse: Na amumuyɔ: bɔne, anibereɛ ne nitane nyinaa bi hyɛɛ bɛ ma. Bɔne bi te sɛ: ahoɔyeyaa, awudie, apereapereɛ, ntɔkwa, torɔ, nnaadaa ne nkontompo hyɛɛ bɛ ma, bɛyɛɛ nsekurofoɔ,

english_verse: They are stupid, unreliable, and don't have any love or pity for others.
translation_verse: nkwaseafoɔ, nnipa a benni gyidie, akomadenfoɔ, atimuɔdenfoɔ.

english_verse: They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
translation_verse: Benim Nyankopɔn ahyɛdeɛ, sɛ bɛ a bɛyɔ saa bɔne yi nyinaa fata wuo, nso nyɛ ne yɔ nkoaa ne bɛyɔeɛ na mmom bɛfoaa bɛ a bɛyɔ saa nso so.

english_verse: Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things.
translation_verse: Ne saa nti, adasamma nni anoyie biaa, sɛnea woteeɛ biaa, woobu bi atɛne he, na woobu woaa wo ho atɛne, efiri sɛ wo a woyɛ temmufoɔ he, woyɔ ade korɔ heaa.

english_verse: We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way.
translation_verse: Hoka sɛ, “Yenim sɛ Nyankopɔn atemmuo a ɔba bɛ a bɛyɔ saa he so he tene.”

english_verse: Do you really think God won't punish you, when you behave exactly like the people you accuse?
translation_verse: Nti, wo nipa, wosusu sɛ, sɛnea woteeɛ biaa, sɛ wobu bɛ a bɛyɔ saa he atɛne na wo nso woyɔ saa bi a, wobɛdwane Nyankopɔn atemmuo anaa?

english_verse: You surely don't think much of God's wonderful goodness or of his patience and willingness to put up with you. Don't you know that the reason God is good to you is because he wants you to turn to him?
translation_verse: Anaa wobu N’ayamyɛ pii a ɔno ne boasetɔ ne n’abodwokyɛre he animtia? Wonnim sɛ Nyankopɔn ayamyɛ botae ne sɛ ɔde wo bɛkɔ adwensakram?

english_verse: But you are stubborn and refuse to turn to God. So you are making things even worse for yourselves on that day when he will show how angry he is and will judge the world with fairness.
translation_verse: Nso w’akoma pirim ɔne sesa a wompɛ sɛ wosesa nti, wooboaboa Nyankopɔn abufuo ano ama wo ho ama abufuo da he, berɛ a Nyankopɔn atemmuo a ɔtene he beyi ne ho adi;

english_verse: God will reward each of us for what we have done.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ obetua biaa ka sɛnea biaa nnwuma teɛ:

english_verse: He will give eternal life to everyone who has patiently done what is good in the hope of receiving glory, honor, and life that lasts forever.
translation_verse: bɛ a bɛde ntoboaseɛ yɔ papa hwehwɛ animuonyam ne anidie ne nea ɔmporɔ he, ɔbɛma bɛ daa nkwa;

english_verse: But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil.
translation_verse: nso bɛ a bedi ntuturasɛm; na bɛpo nɔkorɛ na bɛfa kwane a ɔntene so he, bebenya abufuo ne asotwe.

english_verse: All who are wicked will be punished with trouble and suffering. It doesn't matter if they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Na ahohiahia ne ahokyerɛ bɛba biaa a ɔyɔ bɔne so, Yudani kane, na afei Helani nso,

english_verse: But all who do right will be rewarded with glory, honor, and peace, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: nso animuonyam ne anidie ne asomdwie bɛba biaa a ɔyɔ papa so, Yudani kane, na afei Helani nso,

english_verse: God doesn't have any favorites!
translation_verse: efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn nyɛ nyiyimu.

english_verse: Those people who don't know about God's Law will still be punished for what they do wrong. And the Law will be used to judge everyone who knows what it says.
translation_verse: Dodoɔ a benni mmara na bɛyɔɔ bɔne he, bɛbɛkwati mmara he nso ayera, na bɛ a bɛya mmara ase na bɛyɔɔ bɔne nyinaa bɛde mmara he bebu bɛ atɛne.

english_verse: God accepts those who obey his Law, but not those who simply hear it.
translation_verse: Na ɔnyɛ bɛ a betie mmara he ne bɛyɛ ateneneefoɔ Nyankopɔn ani so, na mmom bɛ a bɛyɛ mmara he yɔfoɔ nkoaa ne benya bembuo.

english_verse: This proves that the conscience is like a law written in the human heart. And it will show whether we are forgiven or condemned,
translation_verse: Bɛda no adi sɛ deɛ mmara he hyɛ sɛ bɛnyɔ he bɛatwerɛ no bɛ akomam, sɛnea bɛ ahonim nso di ho adanseɛ; na bɛ nsusuiɛ a ayɛ ntanta bɛtoto bɛ ano anaa obetwitwa agye bɛ

english_verse: when God appoints Jesus Christ to judge everyone's secret thoughts, just as my message says. The Jews and the Law
translation_verse: da a Nyankopɔn a ɔnam Yesu Kristo so, bebu nnipa nyinaa kokoam nsusuiɛ atɛne sɛnea asɛmpa a maka he teɛ. Yudafoɔ ne mmara he

english_verse: Some of you call yourselves Jews. You trust in the Law and take pride in God.
translation_verse: Nso sɛ wofrɛ wo ho Yudani, na wode wo ho to mmara he so, na wohoahoa wo ho Nyankopɔn mu,

english_verse: By reading the Scriptures you learn how God wants you to behave, and you discover what is right.
translation_verse: na wonim n’apɛdeɛ na wokyerɛ nea ɔyɛ, efiri sɛ bɛatete wo mmara he mu,

english_verse: You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for all who are in the dark.
translation_verse: na wogye di sɛ wokyerɛ anifirafoɔ kwane, woyɛ hann ma bɛ a bɛnam sum mu,

english_verse: And since there is knowledge and truth in God's Law, you think you can instruct fools and teach young people.
translation_verse: wotene nkwaseafoɔ, woyɛ mmɔfra bɛ kyerɛkyerɛfoɔ, wowɔ mmara he a nimdeɛ ne nɔkorɛ ahyɛ no ma;

english_verse: But how can you teach others when you refuse to learn? You preach it is wrong to steal. But do you steal?
translation_verse: wo a afei deɛ, wokyerɛkyerɛ afoforɔ, wonkyerɛkyerɛ wo ho? Wo a wokasa tia korɔnobɔ he, wommɔ korɔno?

english_verse: You take pride in the Law, but you disobey the Law and bring shame to God.
translation_verse: Wo a wohoahoa wo ho mmara he mu, wommu mmara he so mfa mmu Nyankopɔn animtia?

english_verse: It is just as the Scriptures tell us, “You have made foreigners say insulting things about God.”
translation_verse: Saa nti ne bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Wo nti bɛaka Nyankopɔn dini ho abususɛm Amanamane mu.”

english_verse: Being circumcised is worthwhile, if you obey the Law. But if you don't obey the Law, you are no better off than people who are not circumcised.
translation_verse: Na mmom sɛ wodi mmara he so a twetiatwa ho hia; nso sɛ wobu mmara he so a wo twetiatwa he dane bɛyɛ te sɛnea wontwaa twetia.

english_verse: In fact, if they obey the Law, they are as good as anyone who is circumcised.
translation_verse: Nti sɛ bɛ a bentwaa twetia he di mmara he so a, twetia a bentwaeɛ he nti bemmu bɛ sɛ bɛ a bɛatwa bɛ twetia anaa?

english_verse: So everyone who obeys the Law, but has never been circumcised, will condemn you. Even though you are circumcised and have the Law, you still don't obey its teachings.
translation_verse: Nti bɛ a honam fam bentwaa bɛ twetia na nso bedi mmara he so he bebu ho a bɛatwerɛ mmara he amma ho, na bɛatwa ho twetia nso hoto mmara he fɔ.

english_verse: Just because you live like a Jew and are circumcised doesn't make you a real Jew.
translation_verse: Ɔnyɛ honam fam nnoɔma ne ma bi yɛ Yudani, saa ara nso ne twetiatwa kann nso nna adi anisoɔ ne honam fam nnoɔma mu.

english_verse: To be a real Jew you must obey the Law. True circumcision is something that happens deep in your heart, not something done to your body. And besides, you should want praise from God and not from humans.
translation_verse: na mmom, sɛ bi yɛ Yudani mapa a, ɔyɛ no ne mu, na twetiatwa ankasa yɛ akoma mu asɛm; ɔyɛ honhom mu adeɛ, na mmom ɔnyɛ honam mu adeɛ. Nea ɔyɛ Yudani mapa he, n’ayeyie firi Nyankopɔn hɔ, na mmom ommfiri nnipa hɔ.

english_verse: What good is it to be a Jew? What good is it to be circumcised?
translation_verse: Afei, mmɛ ne Yudani wɔ boro so? Anaa mmɛ mfasoɔ ne ya twetiatwa so?

english_verse: It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews.
translation_verse: Ɔdɔɔso kwane hodoɔ bebree so, efiri sɛ nea odi kane he, Yudafoɔ he ne bɛde Nyankopɔn nsɛm hyɛɛ bɛ nsa.

english_verse: It is true that some of them did not believe the message. But does this mean that God cannot be trusted, just because they did not have faith?
translation_verse: Na sɛ bɛ mu binom annye anni ɛ? Nti bɛgye a bɛannye anni he bɛsesa Nyankopɔn nɔkorɛdie he anaa?

english_verse: No, indeed! God tells the truth, even if everyone else is a liar. The Scriptures say about God, “Your words will be proven true, and in court you will win your case.”
translation_verse: Daabi da! Ewom sɛ nnipa biaa nni nɔkorɛ, ma Nyankopɔn nyɛ nɔkwafoɔ, sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ: “Sɛnea wobedi bem wo nsɛm mu. Na woadi nkunim w’atemmuo mu.”

english_verse: If our evil deeds show how right God is, then what can we say? Is it wrong for God to become angry and punish us? What a foolish thing to ask.
translation_verse: Nti sɛ tene a yɛntene da Nyankopɔn tenenee adi a, mmɛ ne yɛbɛka? Nti Nyankopɔn ntene sɛ obehwie n’abufuo agu yɛ so?

english_verse: But the answer is, “No.” Otherwise, how could God judge the world?
translation_verse: Daabi da! Saa deɛ a mmɛ kwane ne Nyankopɔn bɛfa so abu wiase atɛne?

english_verse: Since your lies bring great honor to God by showing how truthful he is, you may ask why God still says you are a sinner.
translation_verse: Nso sɛ tene a mentene ma Nyankopɔn nɔkorɛdie bu so ma n’animuonyam da adi a, mmɛ nti ne bɛda so bu me sɛ debɔneyɛni?

english_verse: You might as well say, “Let's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!” Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well. No One Is Good
translation_verse: Na ɛɛne nti ne yɛnka sɛ , “Homma yɛnyɔ nnebɔne na nnepa aba?” Bɛ afobuo fata bɛ! Nipa biaa ntene Nyankopɔn anim

english_verse: What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said.
translation_verse: Hedeɛ asɛm he ne sɛ? Nti yɛ Yudafoɔ he kyɛne bɛ a bɛaka he anaa? Daabi, ɔnte saa koraa; efiri sɛ yɛadi kane aka sɛ, Yudafoɔ oo, Helafoɔ oo, biaa hyɛ bɔne tumi ase,

english_verse: The Scriptures tell us, “No one is acceptable to God!
translation_verse: sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ: “Biaa nni hɔ a ɔtene, baako mpo nni hɔ;

english_verse: Not one of them understands or even searches for God.
translation_verse: Biaa nni nteaseɛ, biaa nni hɔ a ɔhwehwɛ Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: They have all turned away and are worthless. There isn't one person who does right.
translation_verse: Biaa amane afiri kwanpa he so, bɛ nyinaa mfasoɔ nni bɛ so; biaa nni hɔ a ɔda ayamyɛ adi, biaa nni hɔ, baako mpo nni hɔ.”

english_verse: Their words are like an open pit, and their tongues are good only for telling lies. Each word is as deadly as the fangs of a snake,
translation_verse: “Bɛ mene mu yɛ awufoɔ adakamena a ano abie; bɛde bɛ kɛtrɛma di torɔ.” “Nankatia borɔ ya bɛ ano.”

english_verse: and they say nothing but bitter curses.
translation_verse: “Duabɔ ne adenwono ahyɛ bɛ anum ma.”

english_verse: These people quickly become violent.
translation_verse: “Bɛ nane ɔhare mogyahwieguo ho;

english_verse: Wherever they go, they leave ruin and destruction.
translation_verse: sɛeɛ ne awerɛhoɔ ya bɛ kwane so,

english_verse: They don't know how to live in peace.
translation_verse: na asomdwie kwane deɛ, bennim.”

english_verse: They don't even fear God.”
translation_verse: “Nyamesuro nni bɛ ani so.”

english_verse: We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty.
translation_verse: Afei yenim sɛ, nea mmara he ka biaa, ɔkasa kyerɛ bɛ a bɛya mmara he ase, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a ano biaa bɛka atom, na wiase nyinaa abu nkonta akyerɛ Nyankopɔn,

english_verse: God doesn't accept people simply because they obey the Law. No, indeed! All the Law does is to point out our sin. God's Way of Accepting People
translation_verse: efiri sɛ nipa biaa nni hɔ a obedi bem Nyankopɔn anim, sɛnea mmara he nnwuma teɛ, efiri sɛ yɛnam mmara he so ne yenya bɔne ho nimdeɛ. Tenenee a ɔnam gyidie so ba

english_verse: Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Na afei, bɛakwati mmara he ayi Nyankopɔn tenenee adi, na mmara he ne adiyifoɔ he di ho adanseɛ,

english_verse: God treats everyone alike. He accepts people only because they have faith in Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn tenenee a ɔnam Yesu Kristo mu gyidie so ma bɛ a bɛgye di nyinaa. Na nyiyimu nnim,

english_verse: All of us have sinned and fallen short of God's glory.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ nnipa nyinaa ayɔ bɔne na yɛtɔ sini Nyankopɔn animuonyam ho;

english_verse: But God treats us much better than we deserve, and because of Christ Jesus, he freely accepts us and sets us free from our sins.
translation_verse: na afei ɔyɛ akyɛdeɛ kwa sɛ Nyankopɔn nam n’adom ne gyeɛ a ɔya Kristo Yesu mu he so abu nnipa bem.

english_verse: God sent Christ to be our sacrifice. Christ offered his life's blood, so by faith in him we could come to God. And God did this to show that in the past he was right to be patient and forgive sinners. This also shows that God is right when he accepts people who have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: sɛnea ɔbɛyɔ a ɔbɛda n’ankasa ne tenenee adi saa berɛ yi mu, na berɛ a obu nipa a ɔwɔ Yesu mu gyidie bem he, ada adi sɛ ɔyɛ teneneeni mapa.

english_verse: What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith.
translation_verse: Afei mmɛ ne ahohoahoa yi? Wɔayi afiri hɔ. Mmɛ nhyehyɛeɛ so na ɔnam? Mmara nnwuma so? Daabi, na mmom ɔnam gyidie nhyehyɛeɛ so.

english_verse: We see that people are acceptable to God because they have faith, and not because they obey the Law.
translation_verse: Na yesi no pi sɛ ɔnam gyidie so ne bebu bi bem, na mmom omfiri mmara nnwuma mu.

english_verse: Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is!
translation_verse: Anaa Nyankopɔn yɛ Yudafoɔ nkoaa bɛ Nyankopɔn? Ɔnnyɛ Amanamane mufoɔ nso Nyankopɔn anaa? Ampa, ɔyɛ Amanamane mufoɔ nso Nyankopɔn,

english_verse: There is only one God, and he accepts Gentiles as well as Jews, simply because of their faith.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, Nyankopɔn yɛ baako; na ɔnam gyidie korɔ he a so ne bebu twetiatwafoɔ ne bɛ a bentwaa bɛ twetia bem.

english_verse: Do we destroy the Law by our faith? Not at all! We make it even more powerful.
translation_verse: Ɔsɛ sɛ yɛde saa gyidie he tu mmara he gu anaa? Daabi da! Na mmom, yɛma mmara he ase tim.

english_verse: Well then, what can we say about our ancestor Abraham?
translation_verse: Ɔte saa a afei mmɛ ne yɛbɛka sɛ ɔyɛ mfasoɔ a Abraham a ɔyɛ yɛ honam fam agya-panyini he nyaeɛ?

english_verse: If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God.
translation_verse: Sɛ nnwuma mu ne bebuu Abraham bem a, nka ɔwɔ nea ɔde bɛhoahoa ne ho, nso ɔnyɛ Nyankopɔn anim.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.”
translation_verse: Na mmɛ twerɛsɛm he ka? “Abraham gyee Nyankopɔn diiɛ, na Nyankopɔn buu ne gyidie he sɛ tenenee maa no.”

english_verse: Money paid to workers isn't a gift. It is something they earn by working.
translation_verse: Na afei sɛ bi yɔ adwuma a, bemmu n’akatua sɛ akyɛdeɛ, na mmom bebu no sɛ adeɛ ɔfata no.

english_verse: But you cannot make God accept you because of something you do. God accepts sinners only because they have faith in him.
translation_verse: Nso bi a wannyɔ adwuma he gye nea obu debɔnyɛni bem he di a, obu saa gyidie he sɛ tenenee ma no.

english_verse: In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
translation_verse: Nti Dawid nso kasa faa nhyira a ɔba bɛ a Nyankopɔn kwati bɛ nnwuma bu bɛ bem he ho sɛ:

english_verse: Are these blessings meant for circumcised people or for those who are not circumcised? Well, the Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.
translation_verse: Nti saa nhyira yi, kɔma bɛ a bɛatwa bɛ twetia nkoaa, anaa bɛ a bentwaa bɛ twetia nso ka ho? Yɛpae mu ka sɛ, Gyidie ne bɛde buu Abraham sɛ ɔtene.

english_verse: But when did this happen? Was it before or after Abraham was circumcised? Of course, it was before.
translation_verse: Mmɛ kwane so ne bɛde buu no saa? Ɔyɛ berɛ a na bentwaa no twetia he anaa betwaa no twetia wieeɛ he a? Ɔnnyɛ twetiatwa he akyi, na mmom yei siiɛ ansa na betwaa no twetia.

english_verse: Abraham let himself be circumcised to show he had been accepted because of his faith even before he was circumcised. This makes Abraham the father of all who are acceptable to God because of their faith, even though they are not circumcised.
translation_verse: Onyaa twetiatwa he ho nsɛnkyerɛnne sɛ tenenee a ɔnam gyidie so nyaeɛ he ho nsɔano berɛ a na bentwaa no twetia. Nyankopɔn yɔɔ yei nam so ama Abraham abɛyɛ bɛ a bentwaa bɛ twetia, na nso bɛgye Nyankopɔn di he nyinaa agya, na ne saa nti bɛabu bɛ nso sɛ ateneneefoɔ,

english_verse: This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised. The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
translation_verse: saa ara nso ne ɔyɛ agya ma twetiafoɔ a, ɔnyɛ bɛ twetiatwa nkoaa, na mmom bɛnam yɛ agya Abraham a onyaa gyidie ansa na betwaa no twetia he anammɔno mu. Nyankopɔn nhwɛsoɔ a ɔnam gyidie so

english_verse: God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable.
translation_verse: Na bɔ a bɛhyɛɛ no sɛ obedi adeɛ wiase he annyɛ bɔhyɛ a ɔnam mmara so maa Abraham anaa n’asefoɔ na mmom ɔnam gyidie tenee so.

english_verse: If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
translation_verse: Sɛ mmara sodifoɔ he ne ɔsɛ sɛ bɛyɛ adedifoɔ a, hedeɛ na gyidie yɛ kwa na bɔhyɛ he nso nni mu;

english_verse: God becomes angry when his Law is broken. But where there isn't a law, it cannot be broken.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ mmara he twetwe Nyankopɔn abufuo ba; na sɛ mmara nni hɔ a, mmarato biaa nso nni hɔ.

english_verse: Everything depends on having faith in God, so that God's promise is assured by his gift of undeserved grace. This promise isn't only for Abraham's descendants who have the Law. It is for all who are Abraham's descendants because they have faith, just as he did. Abraham is the ancestor of us all.
translation_verse: Ne saa nti ogyina gyidie so, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a bɔhyɛ he begyina adom so na adi agyinamu ama n’asefoɔ nyinaa, ɔnnyɛ mmara sodifoɔ he nkoaa, na mmom bɛ a bekura Abraham gyidie he bi, efiri sɛ ɔyɛ yɛ nyinaa yɛ agya,

english_verse: The Scriptures say that Abraham would become the ancestor of many nations. This promise was made to Abraham because he had faith in God, who raises the dead to life and creates new things.
translation_verse: sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ “Maayɔ wo amane bebree bɛ agya,” Nyankopɔn a ɔgye no di he anim, nea ɔma awufoɔ nkwa, na ɔfrɛ adeɛ a onni hɔ ma no ba.

english_verse: God promised Abraham a lot of descendants. And when it all seemed hopeless, Abraham still had faith in God and became the ancestor of many nations.
translation_verse: Berɛ a na anidasoɔ nni hɔ he, Abraham gyinaa anidasoɔ so gye dii sɛ ɔbɛyɛ amane bebree agya sɛnea bɛaka sɛ he, “Saa ara ne w’asefoɔ bɛyɛ bebree.”

english_verse: But Abraham never doubted or questioned God's promise. His faith made him strong, and he gave all the credit to God.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn bɔhyɛ he ho deɛ wamma annyeannie biaa amma n’akwane mu, amma no annye akyinnye, na mmom ogyinaa pintinn gyidie mu na ɔhyɛɛ Nyankopɔn animuonyam,

english_verse: Abraham was certain that God could do what he had promised.
translation_verse: onyaa awerɛhyɛmu paa sɛ Nyankopɔn betumi ayɔ nea w’ahyɛ ho bɔ he.

english_verse: just as we read in the Scriptures. But these words were not written only for Abraham.
translation_verse: Afei nsɛm a ɔka sɛ, “Nyankopɔn buu ne gyidie sɛ tenenee maa no” he, bɛantwerɛ amma ɔno nkoaa,

english_verse: They were written for us, since we will also be accepted because of our faith in God, who raised our Lord Jesus to life.
translation_verse: na mmom bɛtwerɛ maa yɛ nso; na bɛabu yɛ a yɛgye nea ɔnyanee Yesu a ɔyɛ yɛ Awurade firii awufoɔ mu he di nso sɛ yɛ tene.

english_verse: God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Yesus ne yɛ bɔne nti beyii no maa wuo na yɛ bembuo nti bɛyanee no he.

english_verse: By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God.
translation_verse: Afei ɔnam sɛ bɛabu yɛ bem gyidie mu nti, yɛnam yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo so anya asomdwie Nyankopɔn mu,

english_verse: Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God.
translation_verse: ɔno so ne yɛnam anya saa adom a yegyina so yi; na yɛhoahoa yɛ ho anidasoɔ a yɛ wɔ no Nyankopɔn animuonyam mu.

english_verse: But that's not all! We gladly suffer, because we know that suffering helps us to endure.
translation_verse: Na ɔnyɛ yei nkoaa, na mmom yɛhoahoa yɛ ho ya yɛ amanehunu mu, yenim sɛ amanehunu de yɔkoduru ba,

english_verse: And endurance builds character, which gives us a hope
translation_verse: na yɔkoduru de subane pa ba, na subane pa de anidasoɔ ba,

english_verse: that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
translation_verse: na anidasoɔ nni hwammɔ, efiri sɛ bɛnam Honhom Kronkron a bɛde ama yɛ he so ahwie Nyankopɔn dɔ agu yɛ akoma mu.

english_verse: Christ died for us at a time when we were helpless and sinful.
translation_verse: Na berɛ a yɛda so yɛ mmerɛ he, berɛ a ɔsɛ mu he Kristo wu maa nnebɔnyɛfoɔ.

english_verse: No one is really willing to die for an honest person, though someone might be willing to die for a truly good person.
translation_verse: Nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, ɔbɛyɛ dene sɛ bi bewu ama deɛ ɔyɛ nipa tenenee mpo; na deɛ ɔyɛ nipa pa he deɛ, ebia nka bi besi ne bo sɛ obewu ama no.

english_verse: But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
translation_verse: Nso Nyankopɔn daa ne dɔ adi kyerɛɛ yɛ sɛ yɛda so yɛ nnebɔnyɛfoɔ he, Kristo wu maa yɛ.

english_verse: But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger.
translation_verse: Nea ɔka ho koraa ne sɛ, seesei bɛde ne mogyaa abu yɛ bem, bɛnam ne so bɛgye yɛ afiri Nyankopɔn abufuo mu.

english_verse: Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son.
translation_verse: Na sɛ berɛ a yɛyɛ Nyankopɔn atamfoɔ he, bɛnam ne Ba he wuo so apata yɛ ne Nyankopɔn ntam a, ɔbɛyɛ dɛ ne bɛmfa ne nkwa so nnye yɛ.

english_verse: And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us. Adam and Christ
translation_verse: Nso ɔnyɛ yei nkoaa, na mmom yɛnam yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo so hoahoa yɛ ho Nyankopɔn mu, seesei deɛ, yɛnam ne so anya mpata yi. Adam ne Kristo

english_verse: Adam sinned, and that sin brought death into the world. Now everyone has sinned, and so everyone must die.
translation_verse: Saa nti ɔnam nipa baako so ne bɔne baa wiase, na wuo nam bɔne so baeɛ, maa wuo trɛ kaa nnipa nyinaa, efiri sɛ nnipa nyinaa ayɔ bɔne;

english_verse: Sin was in the world before the Law came. But no record of sin was kept, because there was no Law.
translation_verse: ansa na mmara he ɔɔba he na bɔne ya wiase, nso sɛ mmara nni hɔ a bemmu biaa fɔ bɔne ho.

english_verse: Yet death still had power over all who lived from the time of Adam to the time of Moses. This happened, though not everyone disobeyed a direct command from God, as Adam did. In some ways Adam is like Christ who came later.
translation_verse: Nso wuo faa nnipa nnommum firi Adam so kosii Mose so, mpo bɛ a bɛ bɔne nte sɛ Adam mmarato he, saa Adam yi yɛ nea na ɔɔba he ho nsɛso.

english_verse: But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
translation_verse: Na ɔnyɛ sɛdeɛ mmarato he teɛ ne adom akyɛdeɛ he nso teɛ, efiri sɛ baakofoɔ mmarato nti nnipa bebree awu a, Nyankopɔn Adom ne nipa baako a ɔno ne Yesu Kristo he mu adom akyɛdeɛ aboro so ama nnipa bebree akyɛne saa.

english_verse: There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times.
translation_verse: Na akyɛdeɛ a bɛde kyɛ kwa he nte sɛ nipa baako bɔne ho nsɛso he. Na atemmuo a ɔnam mmarato baako so he de afobuo baeɛ, nso akyɛdeɛ a bɛde kyɛ kwa he, a odi mmarato bebree akyi he de bembuo ba.

english_verse: Death ruled like a king because Adam had sinned. But that cannot compare with what Jesus Christ has done. God has treated us with undeserved grace, and he has accepted us because of Jesus. And so we will live and rule like kings.
translation_verse: Sɛ ɔnam baakofoɔ mmarato so maa wuo faa nnipa nnommum saa kwane yi so a, mprɛ meɛhe ne bɛ a benyaa adom ne tenenee akyɛdeɛ a ɔboro so he mmom ɔmmfa nipa baako, Yesu Kristo, so nya daa nkwa.

english_verse: Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life.
translation_verse: Nti sɛnea nipa baako mmarato de bebree kɔɔ afobuo mu he, saa ne ɔnam baakofoɔ tenenee so de bembuo ne nkwa bɛma nnipa nyinaa.

english_verse: Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Sɛnea ɔnam baakofoɔ asoɔdene so maa nnipa bebree yɛɛ nnebɔnyɛfoɔ he, saa ara ne nipa baako setie bɛma nnipa bebree ayɛ ahoteefoɔ.

english_verse: The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful.
translation_verse: Nso mmara he baa sɛ mmarato bɛdɔɔso; nso sɛ bɔne dɔɔso a, adom broo so kyɛne saa.

english_verse: Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
translation_verse: Na sɛnea bɔne dii hene wuo mu he, adom so nam tenenee so adi hene afa yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo so kɔ daa nkwa mu.

english_verse: What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better?
translation_verse: Afei ɛɛne ne yɛnka? Yɛnkɔ so nyɔ bɔne sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a adom bɛdɔɔso anaa?

english_verse: No, we should not! If we are dead to sin, how can we go on sinning?
translation_verse: Daabi da, ɔnsɛ sɛ yɛyɔ saa koraa! Mmɛ nti ne ɔsɛ sɛ yɛ a yɛawu ama bɔne kɔ so tena bɔne mu?

english_verse: Don't you know that all who share in Christ Jesus by being baptized also share in his death?
translation_verse: Anaa honnim sɛ yɛ mu dodoɔ a bɛbɔɔ yɛ asu Kristo Yesu mu he, bɛbɔɔ yɛ asu kɔɔ ne wuom?

english_verse: When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
translation_verse: Nti besiee yɛ ne no ya asubɔ mu kɔɔ wuom, sɛ, sɛnea bɛde Agya he animuonyam nyanee Kristo firii awufoɔ mu he, saa ne ɔsɛ sɛ yɛ nso yɛnante nkwa foforɔ mu.

english_verse: If we shared in Jesus' death by being baptized, we will be raised to life with him.
translation_verse: Nti sɛ yɛ ne no ayɛ baako ne wuo a ɔte sei mu a, yɛ ne no nso bɛyɛ baako ya wusɔreɛ a ɔte sɛ ne deɛ he mu.

english_verse: We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin.
translation_verse: Yenim sɛ bɛabɔ yɛ nipadua dada ne no asennua mu sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a bɔne nipadua he bɛsɛe, na yɛankɔ so anyɔ bɔne nkoa.

english_verse: We know sin doesn't have power over dead people.
translation_verse: Na nea wawu he ade ne ho afiri bɔne ho.

english_verse: As surely as we died with Christ, we believe we will also live with him.
translation_verse: Nso sɛ yɛ ne Kristo awu a, yɛgye di sɛ yɛ ne no bɛtena ase nso.
