english_verse: We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die.
translation_verse: Yenim sɛ Kristo a bɛanyane no afiri awufuom he, onwu bio; efiri sɛ wuo ntumi mfa no nommum bio.

english_verse: When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God.
translation_verse: Wuo a owuiɛ he, owuu prɛko maa bɔne, maa nnipa nyinaa; nso ase a ɔte he, ɔte ma Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: In the same way, you must think of yourselves as dead to the power of sin. But Christ Jesus has given life to you, and you live for God.
translation_verse: Nti ho nso ho mu ho ho sɛ hoawu ama bɔne ampa na hote ase ma Nyankopɔn, Kristo mu.

english_verse: Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires
translation_verse: Nti, hommma bɔne mmfa ho nipadua a obewu yi nommum, sɛ hobɛyɛ setie ama honam akɔnnɔ.

english_verse: or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God.
translation_verse: Hommfa ho nipadua mma bɔne sɛ akodeɛ amumuyɔ kwane so, na mmom homfa ho ho ma Nyankopɔn sɛ bɛ a bɛagye bɛ afiri wuom aba nkwa mu, na homfa ho ho ma sɛ tenenee akodeɛ.

english_verse: Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law. Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
translation_verse: Na bɔne amfa ho nommum bio, efiri sɛ honni mmara ase, na mmom hoya adom ase. Bɔne nkoa

english_verse: What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not!
translation_verse: Afei mmɛ bio? Yɛnyɔ bɔne efiri sɛ yenni mmara ase na mmom adom ase anaa? Daabi da!

english_verse: Don't you know that you are slaves of anyone you obey? You can be slaves of sin and die, or you can be obedient slaves of God and be acceptable to him.
translation_verse: Honnim sɛ nea hode ho ho ma no na hotie no sɛ nkoa he, hoyɛ ne nkoa, sɛ bɔne a ɔkɔ wuom, anaa setie a ɔkɔ tenenee mu?

english_verse: You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received.
translation_verse: Nso yɛda Nyankopɔn ase sɛ, hoayɛ bɔne nkoa da, na hofiri akomam abɛyɛ setie ama nkyerɛkyerɛ a bɛde hyɛɛ ho nsa,

english_verse: Now you are set free from sin and are slaves who please God.
translation_verse: na afei, berɛ a bɛagye ho afiri bɔne mu he, hoabɛyɛ tenenee nkoa.

english_verse: I am using these everyday examples, because in some ways you are still weak. You used to let the different parts of your body be slaves of your evil thoughts. But now you must make every part of your body serve God, so that you will belong completely to him.
translation_verse: Meeka no nipafam esiane ho honam mmerɛeɛ nti. Sɛnea hode ho honam akwaa maa fi nkoa na hoyɔɔ amumuyɔ toaa amumuyɔ so he, nti saa mmerɛ yi deɛ homfa ho ho ma sɛ tenenee nkoa nkɔ ahoteɛ mu.

english_verse: When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God.
translation_verse: Berɛ a hoyɛ bɔne nkoa he, na hode ho ho tenenee ho.

english_verse: But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death.
translation_verse: Nti mmɛ mfasoɔ ne honyaa no nnoɔma a afei deɛ ho fɛre ho he mu? Saa nnoɔma he awieeɛ ne wuo.

english_verse: Now you have been set free from sin, and you are God's slaves. This will make you holy and will lead you to eternal life.
translation_verse: Nso afei a hoade ho ho afiri bɔne ho yi, ho abɛyɛ Nyankopɔn nkoa, mfasoɔ a honya ne ahoteɛ. N’awieeɛ ne daa nkwa.

english_verse: Sin pays off with death. But God's gift is eternal life given by Jesus Christ our Lord.
translation_verse: Na bɔne akatua a ne wuo, nso Nyankopɔn adom akyɛdeɛ ne daa nkwa a ɔya Awurade Kristo Yesu mu.

english_verse: My friends, you surely understand enough about law to know that laws only have power over people who are alive.
translation_verse: Anuanom, honnim sɛ, meekasa akyerɛ bɛ a benim mmara he, sɛ mmara he kyekyere bi berɛ a onii he te ase?

english_verse: For example, the Law says that a man's wife must remain his wife as long as he lives. But once her husband is dead, she is free
translation_verse: Nti baa warefoɔ hyɛ ne kunu mmara ase berɛ a ne kunu he te ase; nso sɛ okunu he wu a, ɔde ne ho firi ne kunu mmara he ase.

english_verse: to marry someone else. However, if she goes off with another man while her husband is still alive, she is said to be unfaithful.
translation_verse: Saa ne ɔteɛ, sɛ baa warefoɔ ne beema foforɔ te a, na ne kunu he nwuiɛ a, bɛbɛfrɛ no dwammanmɔfoɔ. Nso sɛ ne kunu he wu a, baa he de ne ho firi saa mmara he ho; sɛ ɔware beema foforɔ a ɔnyɛ dwammanmɔfoɔ.

english_verse: That is how it is with you, my friends. You are now part of the body of Christ and are dead to the power of the Law. You are free to belong to Christ, who was raised to life so we could serve God.
translation_verse: Annuanom, saa kwane he so ne honam Kristo nipadua so awu mmara he fam, saa nti, hohyɛ nipa foforɔ ase, nea benyanee no firi awufoɔ mu na ɔnam so ama yɛ aso aba ama Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: When we thought only of ourselves, the Law made us have sinful desires. It made every part of our bodies into slaves who are doomed to die.
translation_verse: Berɛ a na yɛte honam mu he, mmara he yɛnee yɛ bɔne akɔnnɔ he, na ɔyɔɔ adwuma yɛ nnipadua mu maa yɛsoo aba maa wuo.

english_verse: But the Law no longer rules over us. We are like dead people, and it cannot have any power over us. Now we can serve God in a new way by obeying his Spirit, and not in the old way by obeying the written Law. The Battle with Sin
translation_verse: Na afei deɛ, yɛade yɛ ho afiri mmara he mu, yɛawu afiri nea ɔfaa yɛ nommum he ase, na yɛnam Honhom he so ayɛ nkoa kwane foforɔ so, na ɔnyɛ kwane dadaa a ogyina atwerɛeɛ dadaa he so he. Mmara he ne bɔne

english_verse: Does this mean that the Law is sinful? Certainly not! But if it had not been for the Law, I would not have known what sin is really like. For example, I would not have known what it means to want something that belongs to someone else, unless the Law had told me not to do this.
translation_verse: Ne saa nti mmɛ ne yɛnka? Mmara he yɛ bɔne anaa? Daabi da! Nso, sɛ ɔnyɛ mmara he a, nka na menim bɔne. Sɛ mmara he anka sɛ, “Mma w’ani mmere adeɛ” a, nka na mennim nea bɛfrɛ no anibereɛ.

english_verse: It was sin that used this command as a way of making me have all kinds of desires. But without the Law, sin is dead.
translation_verse: Nso bɔne, nyaa kwane, de anibereɛ nyinaa hyɛɛ me mu; na sɛ mmara nni hɔ a, na bɔne nso awu.

english_verse: Before I knew about the Law, I was alive. But as soon as I heard that command, sin came to life,
translation_verse: Na mekwatii mmara he tenaa ase berɛ bi, nso berɛ a mmaransɛm he baeɛ he, bɔne nyaneɛ

english_verse: and I died. The very command that was supposed to bring life to me, instead brought death.
translation_verse: na mewuiɛ, na mmaransɛm korɔ he a ɔhyɛɛ nkwa ho bɔ he mmaa mehuu sɛ ɔyɛ wuo ma me.

english_verse: Sin used this command to trick me, and because of it I died.
translation_verse: Nso bɔne nam mmaransɛm he so nyaa akwannya, daadaa me na ɔnam so kuu me.

english_verse: Still, the Law and its commands are holy and correct and good.
translation_verse: Nti mmara he yɛ kronkron, na mmaransɛm he yɛ kronkron, ɔtene na ɔyɛ papa nso.

english_verse: Am I saying something good caused my death? Certainly not! It was sin that killed me by using something good. Now we can see how terrible and evil sin really is.
translation_verse: Nti nea ɔyɛ papa he, adane wuo ama me anaa? Daabi da! Na mmom ɔyɛ bɔne a ɔnam nea ɔyɛ papa so de wuo di dwuma me mu, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a ɔbɛda adi sɛ bɔne yɛ bɔne, na ɔnam mmaransɛm he so ama bɔneyɔ aboroso. Ahutaeɛ mu apereapereɛ

english_verse: We know that the Law is spiritual. But I am merely a human, and I have been sold as a slave to sin.
translation_verse: Na yenim sɛ mmara he yɛ honhom; nso meya honam mu a, bɛatɔno me ahyɛ bɔne nkoasom ase.

english_verse: In fact, I don't understand why I act the way I do. I don't do what I know is right. I do the things I hate.
translation_verse: Mennte me ankasa me nneyɔeɛ ase, efiri sɛ mennyɔ nea mepɛ, nso meyɔ adeɛ pɔtee a metane.

english_verse: Although I don't do what I know is right, I agree that the Law is good.
translation_verse: Nti sɛ meyɔ adeɛ a mempɛ a, megye tom sɛ mmara he yɛ.

english_verse: So I am not the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
translation_verse: Nso afei ɔnnyɛ me ne meyɔ saa, na mmom bɔne a ɔte me mu he a.

english_verse: I know that my selfish desires won't let me do anything that is good. Even when I want to do right, I cannot.
translation_verse: Na menim sɛ bibi papa biaa nte me mu, mekyerɛ, me honam mu. Mewɔ pɛ pa sɛ mɛyɔ nea ɔyɛ, nso mentumi nyɔ,

english_verse: Instead of doing what I know is right, I do wrong.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ menyɔ papa a mepɛ he, nso bɔne a mempɛ he ne meyɔ.

english_verse: And so, if I don't do what I know is right, I am no longer the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
translation_verse: Nti sɛ nea mempɛ ne meyɔ deɛ a, hedeɛ ɔnyɛ me ne me yɔ bio, na mmom bɔne a ɔte me mu he a.

english_verse: The Law has shown me that something in me keeps me from doing what I know is right.
translation_verse: Nti mehu sɛ ɔyɛ mmara sɛ berɛ a mepɛ sɛ meyɔ nea ɔyɛ he, bɔne bɛne me.

english_verse: With my whole heart I agree with the Law of God.
translation_verse: Na m’ani gye Nyankopɔn mmara a ɔte me mu he ho,

english_verse: But in every part of me I discover something fighting against my mind, and it makes me a prisoner of sin that controls everything I do.
translation_verse: nso mehu mmara foforɔ me honam akwaa mu a ɔne m’adwene mmara di asie, ɔma meyɔ bɔne a ɔya me nipadua mu he akoa.

english_verse: What a miserable person I am. Who will rescue me from this body that is doomed to die?
translation_verse: Me, mmɔborɔwa! Hwane ne bɛgye me afiri wuo nipadua yi mu?

english_verse: Thank God! Jesus Christ will rescue me. So with my mind I serve the Law of God, although my selfish desires make me serve the law of sin.
translation_verse: Aseda mfa yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo so nka Nyankopɔn! Nti afei, m’adwene mu he meyɛ akoa ma Nyankopɔn mmara, nso me honam mu he meyɛ bɔne akoa.

english_verse: If you belong to Christ Jesus, you won't be punished.
translation_verse: Nti afei afobuo biaa nni hɔ ma bɛ a bɛya Kristo Yesu mu,

english_verse: The Holy Spirit will give you life that comes from Christ Jesus and will set you free from sin and death.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ Honhom mu nkwa mmara he ama ho faahodie Kristo Yesu mu afiri bɔne ne wuo mmara he mu.

english_verse: The Law of Moses cannot do this, because our selfish desires make the Law weak. But God set you free when he sent his own Son to be like us sinners and to be a sacrifice for our sin. God used Christ's body to condemn sin.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn ayɔ nea mmara he antumi anyɔ, ɔne sɛ ɔnam honam mmerɛyɛ nti ɔsomaa ɔnoaa ne Ba, honam subane mu maa no bɛyɛɛ nipa te sɛ yɛ nnipa adebɔneyɛfoɔ yi sɛ ɔne bɔne nni asie, na odii bɔne so nkunim honam mu,

english_verse: He did this, so that we would do what the Law commands by obeying the Spirit instead of our own desires.
translation_verse: sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a nnepa a mmara he hwehwɛ sɛ yɛyɔ he bɛba mu, ya yɛ a yɛnnante honam fam, na mmom yɛnante Honhom fam he abrabɔ mu;

english_verse: People who are ruled by their desires think only of themselves. Everyone who is ruled by the Holy Spirit thinks about spiritual things.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ bɛ a bɛbɔ bɛ bra sɛnea honam pɛ he de bɛ adwene si honam fam nnoɔma so, nso bɛ a bɛbɔ bɛ bra Honhom mu he de bɛ adwene si Honhom mu nnoɔma so.

english_verse: If our minds are ruled by our desires, we will die. But if our minds are ruled by the Spirit, we will have life and peace.
translation_verse: Nea ɔde n’adwene si honam nnoɔma so he kɔ wuom, na nea ɔde n’adwene si honhom nnoɔma so he nya nkwa ne asomdwie.

english_verse: Our desires fight against God, because they do not and cannot obey God's laws.
translation_verse: Saa nti adwene a osi honam nnoɔma so he tane Nyankopɔn; ɔmmrɛ ne ho ase nhyɛ Nyankopɔn mmara ase, nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ ontumi nyɔ saa mpo,

english_verse: If we follow our desires, we cannot please God.
translation_verse: na bɛ a bɛya honam mu he ntumi nsɔ Nyankopɔn ani.

english_verse: You are no longer ruled by your desires, but by God's Spirit, who lives in you. People who don't have the Spirit of Christ in them don't belong to him.
translation_verse: Nso honni honam mu; hoya Honhom mu, esiane Nyankopɔn Honhom a ɔte ho mu nti. Biaa a onni Kristo Honhom he nyɛ Kristo dea.

english_verse: But Christ lives in you. So you are alive because God has accepted you, even though your bodies must die because of your sins.
translation_verse: Sɛ Kristo te ho mu a, ewom sɛ honam he awu deɛ, ɔnam bɔne nti, nso honhom he wɔ nkwa ɔnam tenenee nti.

english_verse: Yet God raised Jesus to life! God's Spirit now lives in you, and he will raise you to life by his Spirit.
translation_verse: Na afei sɛ nea ɔnyanee Yesu firii awufoɔ mu he Honhom te ho mu a, hedeɛ deɛ ɔnyanee Kristo firii awufoɔ mu he bɛma ho nnipadua a owu he nkwa ɔnam ne Honhom a ɔte ho mu he nti.

english_verse: My dear friends, we must not live to satisfy our desires.
translation_verse: Nti, me nuanom, ɔnyɛ honam ne yɛde no ka na yɛatena ase sɛnea honam pɛ;

english_verse: If you do, you will die. But you will live, if by the help of God's Spirit you say “No” to your desires.
translation_verse: na sɛ hotena ase sɛnea honam pɛ a, hobewu; nso sɛ hode Honhom he ku honam he a, hobɛtena ase.

english_verse: Only those people who are led by God's Spirit are his children.
translation_verse: Na dodoɔ a Nyankopɔn Honhom kyerɛ bɛ kwane he, bɛ ne bɛyɛ Nyankopɔn mma;

english_verse: God's Spirit doesn't make us slaves who are afraid of him. Instead, we become his children and call him our Father.
translation_verse: Na sɛ hoannya akoayɛ honhom a ɔbɛma ho asane akɔ hu mu bio, na mmom hoanya abayɛ honhom. Nti yetumi team sɛ, “Abba! Agya!”

english_verse: God's Spirit makes us sure that we are his children.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛ saa Honhom he ankasa ne yɛ honhom ne di adanseɛ sɛ yɛyɛ Nyankopɔn mma,

english_verse: His Spirit lets us know that together with Christ we will be given what God has promised. We will also share in the glory of Christ, because we have suffered with him. A Wonderful Future for God's People
translation_verse: na sɛ yɛyɛ mma a, hedeɛ yedi adeɛ nso, yɛyɛ Nyankopɔn adedifoɔ, na yɛ ne Kristo bom di adeɛ; nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, yɛ ne no hu amaneɛ a, saa ara nso ne bɛbɛhyɛ yɛne no animuonyam abom. Daakye animuonyam

english_verse: I am sure what we are suffering now cannot compare with the glory that will be shown to us.
translation_verse: Menim sɛ yentumi mfa wiase yi mu amanehunu ntoto animuonyam a bɛde bɛhyɛ yɛ daakye wiase he mu he ho.

english_verse: In fact, all creation is eagerly waiting for God to show who his children are.
translation_verse: Na abɔdeɛ he de anigyina ɔɔtwɛne Nyankopɔn mma ahoyie dendenndene;

english_verse: Meanwhile, creation is confused, but not because it wants to be confused. God made it this way in the hope
translation_verse: na bɛde abɔdeɛ he ahyɛ nnoɔma funu ase a ɔnyɛ ɔnoaa ne pɛ so, na mmom nea ɔde no hyɛɛ aseɛ he pɛ so, abɔdeɛ he anidasoɔ ne

english_verse: that creation would be set free from decay and would share in the glorious freedom of his children.
translation_verse: sɛ ɔbɛde ne ho afiri nkyekyereɛ ne porɔeɛ a bɛde no ahyɛ aseɛ he mu na obenya ahofadie ya Nyankopɔn mma animuonyam he mu.

english_verse: We know that all creation is still groaning and is in pain, like a woman about to give birth.
translation_verse: Yenim sɛ abɔdeɛ he nyinaa asi apinie awoko yeyaa mu de besi nnɛ;

english_verse: The Spirit makes us sure about what we will be in the future. But now we groan silently, while we wait for God to show that we are his children. This means that our bodies will also be set free.
translation_verse: na ɔnyɛ abɔdeɛ he nkoaa, na mmom yɛ nso a yɛanya aba a odi kane a ɔno ne Honhom he, yesi apinie yɛ mu dendenndene berɛ a yɛɛtwɛne sɛ bɛbɛgye yɛ ayɛ mma he, gyeɛ a bɛnam agyedeɛ so bɛgye yɛ afiri yɛ nnipadua mu he.

english_verse: And this hope is what saves us. But if we already have what we hope for, there is no need to keep on hoping.
translation_verse: Na saa anidasoɔ yi mu ne bɛgyee yɛ nkwa. Na hwane ne hwɛ nea ne nsa aka kwane? Na sɛ nea yɛwɔ ho anidasoɔ he, yɛ nsa aka a, nka ɔnyɛ adeɛ sɛ yɛbɛkɔ so atwɛne sɛ ɔbɛba mu.

english_verse: However, we hope for something we have not yet seen, and we patiently wait for it.
translation_verse: Nso sɛ yɛ ani da nea yɛ nsa nkaeɛ so a, yɛde ntoboaseɛ twɛne.

english_verse: In certain ways we are weak, but the Spirit is here to help us. For example, when we don't know what to pray for, the Spirit prays for us in ways that cannot be put into words.
translation_verse: Saa ara nso ne Honhom he boa yɛ yɛ mmerɛyɛ mu; na yennim sɛnea ɔsɛ sɛ yɛbɔ mpaeɛ a ɔsɛ, nso Honhom he ankasa si apini a mu dɔ kyɛne nsɛm de di ma yɛ.

english_verse: All of our thoughts are known to God. He can understand what is in the mind of the Spirit, as the Spirit prays for God's people.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn a, ɔhwehwɛ akoma mu pefee he, nim Honhom he adwene, efiri sɛ Honhom he di ma ahoteefoɔ he sɛnea Nyankopɔn pɛ.

english_verse: We know that God is always at work for the good of everyone who loves him. They are the ones God has chosen for his purpose,
translation_verse: Yenim sɛ bɛ a bɛdɔ Nyankopɔn he, bɛ a ɔnam ne pɛ so afrɛ bɛ he, ɔfa adeɛ nyinaa so di boa bɛ ma no wie bɛ yie.

english_verse: and he has always known who his chosen ones would be. He had decided to let them become like his own Son, so his Son would be the first of many children.
translation_verse: Na bɛ a ohuu bɛ sieeɛ he, oyii bɛ too hɔ sɛ bɛnsɛ ne Ba he, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a ne Ba he bɛyɛ abakane nnipa bebree mu.

english_verse: God then accepted the people he had already decided to choose, and he has shared his glory with them. God's Love
translation_verse: Na bɛ a oyii bɛ sii hɔ he, ɔfrɛɛ bɛ nso; na bɛ a ɔfrɛɛ bɛ he, buu bɛ bem nso; na bɛ a obuu bɛ bem he, hyɛɛ bɛ animuonyam nso. Nyankopɔn dɔ a ɔya Kristo Yesu mu

english_verse: What can we say about all this? If God is on our side, can anyone be against us?
translation_verse: Hedeɛ mmɛ ne yɛbɛka afa yeinom ho? Sɛ Nyankopɔn ya yɛ afa a, hwane ne betumi ne yɛ adi asie?

english_verse: God did not keep back his own Son, but he gave him for us. If God did this, won't he freely give us everything else?
translation_verse: Nea wamfa ɔno ankasa ne Ba ankame yɛ na ɔde no maa yɛ nyinaa he, ɔmmfa nnoɔma nyinaa nka ne ho mma yɛ kwa anaa?

english_verse: If God says his chosen ones are acceptable to him, can anyone bring charges against them?
translation_verse: Hwane ne bɛbɔ Nyankopɔn mma a waayi bɛ he kwaadu? Nyankopɔn ne nea obu bi bem.

english_verse: Or can anyone condemn them? No indeed! Christ died and was raised to life, and now he is at God's right side, speaking to him for us.
translation_verse: Hwane ne ɔsɛ sɛ obu atɛne? Kristo Yesu ne nea obu atɛne, nea owuiɛ, na benyanee no, nea ɔte Nyankopɔn nsa nifa, nea odi ma yɛ nɔkorem he.

english_verse: Can anything separate us from the love of Christ? Can trouble, suffering, and hard times, or hunger and nakedness, or danger and death?
translation_verse: Hwane ne bɛte yɛ afiri Kristo dɔ ho? Ahokyerɛ mu oo, ateetee mu oo, taa mu oo, kɔm mu oo, adagyada mu oo, amaneɛ mu oo, wuo mu oo; bibiaa nni hɔ a obetumi ate yɛ afiri Kristo dɔ ho.

english_verse: It is exactly as the Scriptures say, “For you we face death all day long. We are like sheep on their way to be butchered.”
translation_verse: Sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Wo nti beku yɛ daa; bebu yɛ sɛ dwane a bɛɛkɔ ku no.”

english_verse: In everything we have won more than a victory because of Christ who loves us.
translation_verse: Nso, yeinom nyinaa mu he yɛnam nea ɔdɔ yɛ he so adi nkunim.

english_verse: I am sure that nothing can separate us from God's love—not life or death, not angels or spirits, not the present or the future,
translation_verse: Na megye di sɛ mpo wuo, anaa nkwa, anaa abɔfoɔ, anaa atumfoɔ, anaa nnoɔma a ɔya hɔ saa mmerɛ yi, anaa nnoɔma a ɔbɛba, anaa ahoɔdene,

english_verse: and not powers above or powers below. Nothing in all creation can separate us from God's love for us in Christ Jesus our Lord!
translation_verse: anaa nea ɔya soro, anaa nea ɔya bunum, asaase ase, anaa abɔdeɛ biaa, mpo betumi ntete yɛ mmfiri Nyankopɔn dɔ a ɔya yɛ Awurade Kristo Yesu mu he ho.

english_verse: my heart is broken and I am in great sorrow.
translation_verse: Medi awerɛhoɔ paa ne yeyaa m’akomam daa.

english_verse: I would gladly be placed under God's curse and be separated from Christ for the good of my own people.
translation_verse: Nka mepɛ sɛ bɛde me ankasa yɛ abusudeɛ na beyi me firi Kristo mu, me mane mufoɔ a bɛyɛ me honam fam abusuafoɔ nti.

english_verse: They have those famous ancestors, who were also the ancestors of the Christ. I pray that God, who rules over all, will be praised forever! Amen.
translation_verse: bɛyɛ tete agyanom asefoɔ, na ofiri bɛ abusua mu ne Kristo he baa honam fam. Nhyira nka Nyankopɔn a ɔyɛ adeɛ nyinaa sodifoɔ daa. Amen.

english_verse: It cannot be said that God broke his promise. After all, not all of the people of Israel are the true people of God.
translation_verse: Na ɔnyɛ sɛ ebia Nyankopɔn asɛm he adi hwammɔ. Na ɔnyɛ bɛ a befiri Israel nyinaa ne bɛyɛ Israelfoɔ mapa,

english_verse: In fact, when God made the promise to Abraham, he meant only Abraham's descendants by his son Isaac. God was talking only about Isaac when he promised
translation_verse: Yei kyerɛ sɛ ɔnnyɛ honam fam mma ne yɛ Nyankopɔn mma, na mmom bɔhyɛ mma ne bebu bɛ sɛ n’asefoɔ ankasa.

english_verse: Sarah, “At this time next year I will return, and you will already have a son.”
translation_verse: Na sei ne bɔhyɛ he kaeɛ, “Berɛ sei mu mɛba, na Sara anya ba beema.”

english_verse: Don't forget what happened to the twin sons of Isaac and Rebekah.
translation_verse: Na ɔnyɛ yei nkoaa; saa berɛ a Rebeka nso nam ne kunu, yɛ nana Isak so nyinsɛneeɛ he, bibi siiɛ.

english_verse: That's why the Scriptures say that the Lord liked Jacob more than Esau.
translation_verse: Sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Medɔɔ Yakob, nso metanee Esau.”

english_verse: Are we saying God is unfair? Certainly not!
translation_verse: Hedeɛ mmɛ ne yɛbɛka? Atɛntenenee nni Nyankopɔn fam? Daabi da!

english_verse: The Lord told Moses that he has pity and mercy on anyone he wants to.
translation_verse: Efiri sɛ ɔka kyerɛɛ Mose sɛ, “Mehu nea mehu no mmɔbɔ mmɔbɔ, na me yam bɛhyehye me ama nea meyam bɛhyehye no ama no.”

english_verse: Everything then depends on God's mercy and not on what people want or do.
translation_verse: Ne saa nti onyina nipa pɛ anaa nhyɛ so, na mmom Nyankopɔn a ohu mmɔbɔ he so.

english_verse: In the Scriptures the Lord says to the king of Egypt, “I let you become king, so that I could show you my power and be praised by all people on earth.”
translation_verse: Na twerɛsɛm he ka kyerɛ Farao sɛ,” Maama wo so sɛ me botae a ɔkyerɛ me tumi he bɛda adi wɔ wo mu, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a bɛbɛbɔ me dini asaase nyinaa so.”

english_verse: Everything depends on what God decides to do, and he can either have pity on people or make them stubborn. God's Anger and Mercy
translation_verse: Ne saa nti ohu biaa a waayi no mmɔbɔ, na opirim biaa ɔpɛ akoma. Nyankopɔn abufuo ne n’ahummɔborɔ

english_verse: Someone may ask, “How can God blame us, if he makes us behave in the way he wants us to?”
translation_verse: Hedeɛ wobebisa me sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne ɔda so hu mfomsoɔ? Na hwane ne betumi asi Nyankopɔn pɛ kwane?”

english_verse: But, my friend, I ask, “Who do you think you are to question God? Does the clay have the right to ask the potter why he shaped it the way he did?
translation_verse: Nso ampa wone hwane, o! Nipa dasani, sɛ wo ne Nyankopɔn yiyi nsɛm ano? Nti nea bɛanwono no he betumi aka akyerɛ nea ɔnwonoo no he sɛ, “Mmɛ nti ne woyɔɔ me sei?”

english_verse: Doesn't a potter have the right to make a fancy bowl and a plain bowl out of the same lump of clay?”
translation_verse: Nwonfoɔ nni tumi nɛteɛ so, sɛ ɔde nɛteɛ korɔ heaa bi bɛyɔ bibi a anidie yam na foforɔ so nea anidie nnim?

english_verse: God wanted to show his anger and reveal his power against everyone who deserved to be destroyed. But instead, he patiently put up with them.
translation_verse: Na sɛ, Nyankopɔn pɛ sɛ ɔda n’abufuo adi na ɔma behu ne tumi, nso ɔde abodwokyɛre bebree kura abufuo adeɛ a ɔda hɔ ma ɔsɛe he mu a;

english_verse: He did this by showing how glorious he is when he has pity on the people he has chosen to share in his glory.
translation_verse: na sɛ ɔnam saa kwane he so da n’animuonyam ahonya adi ma mmɔborɔhunu adeɛ a wasiesie ato hɔ dadaada ma animuonyam a—

english_verse: just as the Lord says in the book of Hosea, “Although they are not my people, I will make them my people. I will treat with love those nations that have never been loved.
translation_verse: Sɛnea Hosea mu nso ɔka sɛ, “Bɛ a na bɛnnyɛ me nkurofoɔ he mɛfrɛ bɛ ‘me nkurofoɔ,’ na nea ɔnnyɛ me dɔfo he mɛfrɛ no ‘medɔfo.’ ”

english_verse: “Once they were told, ‘You are not my people.’ But in that very place they will be called children of the living God.”
translation_verse: “Na baabi a bɛka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, ‘Honnyɛ me nkurofoɔ,’ he hɔaa nso ne bɛbɛfrɛ bɛ Nyankopɔn Teasefoɔ he mma.”

english_verse: And this is what the prophet Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “The people of Israel are as many as the grains of sand along the beach. But only a few who are left will be saved.
translation_verse: Na Yesaia teaa Israel ho sɛ, “Ewom sɛ na Israel mma te sɛ mpoano anwea, nso bɛ a bɛbɛka kakraa bi ne bɛbɛgye bɛ nkwa;

english_verse: The Lord will be quick and sure to do on earth what he has warned he will do.”
translation_verse: efiri sɛ Awurade bedi n’asɛm asaase yi so awie atwa no tiaa.”

english_verse: Isaiah also said, “If the Lord All-Powerful had not spared some of our descendants, we would have been destroyed like the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah.” Israel and the Good News
translation_verse: Na sɛnea Yesaia kaa too hɔ he, “Sɛ Nyankopɔn a ɔya soro annya nkaeɛfoɔ amma yɛ a, nka yɛ ase hyee sɛ Sodom ama yɛayɛ sɛ Gomora.” Paulo mpaeɛ a ɔbɔ ma Israel

english_verse: What does all of this mean? It means that the Gentiles were not trying to be acceptable to God, but they found that he would accept them if they had faith.
translation_verse: Afei deɛ ɛɛne ne yɛbɛka? Amanamane mufoɔ, a bɛanhwehwɛ tenenee he, bɛ nsa aka, ɔne sɛ, tenenee a ofiri gyidie mu;

english_verse: just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a stone to make people stumble and fall. But those who have faith in that one will never be disappointed.”
translation_verse: sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Hwɛ, meeto fapem bi Sion a ɔbɛto nnipa bebree sintidua, botane a ɔbɛma bɛahwe ase, na biaa a ɔbɛgye no adi biaa nsa onsi fam.”

english_verse: Dear friends, my greatest wish and my prayer to God is for the people of Israel to be saved.
translation_verse: Anuanom, m’akoma so adeɛ ne mpaeɛ a mebɔ kyerɛ Nyankopɔn ne sɛ ɔbɛgye me mane Israel nkwa.

english_verse: I know they love God, but they don't understand
translation_verse: Metumi adi adanseɛ sɛ bɛwɔ Nyankopɔn mu mmɔdemmɔ, nso benni nimdeɛ,

english_verse: what makes people acceptable to him. So they refuse to trust God, and they try to be acceptable by obeying the Law.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, benni tenenee a ofiri Nyankopɔn hɔ he ho nimdeɛ, esiane sɛ bɛpɛ sɛ bɛde bɛ ankasa bɛ tenenee si hɔ nti, bɛmfa bɛ ho nhyɛ Nyankopɔn tenenee mu.

english_verse: But Christ makes the Law no longer necessary for those who become acceptable to God by faith. Anyone Can Be Saved
translation_verse: Na Kristo mu ne mmara he nyinaa wie pɛyɛ, nti bɛnam ne so de tenenee ma nea ɔbɛgye adi biaa. Nkwagyeɛ ya hɔ ma biaa

english_verse: Moses said a person could become acceptable to God by obeying the Law. He did this when he wrote, “If you want to live, you must do all the Law commands.”
translation_verse: Mose twerɛ faa tenenee a ofiri mmara he ho sɛ, “nipa a ɔyɔ saa nnoɔma yi benya nkwa.”

english_verse: But people whose faith makes them acceptable to God will never ask, “Who will go up to heaven to bring Christ down?”
translation_verse: Nso tenenee a ofiri gyidie mu he dɛ, “Nka no w’akomam sɛ, Hwane ne bɛforo akɔ soro?” ,

english_verse: So you will be saved, if you honestly say, “Jesus is Lord,” and if you believe with all your heart that God raised him from death.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, sɛ wode w’ano pae mu ka sɛ Yesu ne Awurade na wode w’akoma gye di sɛ Nyankopɔn anyane no afiri awufoɔ mu a, wobenya nkwa.

english_verse: God will accept you and save you, if you truly believe this and tell it to others.
translation_verse: Na akoma ne bɛde gye di ma bebu bɛ bem, na ano ne bɛde pae mu ka nya nkwa.

english_verse: The Scriptures say no one who has faith will be disappointed,
translation_verse: Twerɛsɛm he dɛ, “Nea ɔgye no di biaa nsa onsi fam.”

english_verse: no matter if that person is a Jew or a Gentile. There is only one Lord, and he is generous to everyone who asks for his help.
translation_verse: Na yei ho he, nsosonoeɛ biaa nni Yudani anaa Helani ntam; Awurade korɔ heaa ne di bɛ nyinaa bɛ so, ne yam yɛ ma bɛ a bɛbɔ ne dini nyinaa,

english_verse: All who call out to the Lord will be saved.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, “Nea ɔbɛbɔ Awurade dini biaa benya nkwa.”

english_verse: How can people have faith in the Lord and ask him to save them, if they have never heard about him? And how can they hear, unless someone tells them?
translation_verse: Nso ɔbɛyɛ dɛ na bi atumi abɔ nea ɔnnye no nni dini? Na ɔbɛyɛ dɛ na bi agye nea ɔntee ne ho asɛm adi? Na sɛ bi anka Awurade ho asɛm ankyerɛ bɛ a bɛbɛyɛdɛ ate?

english_verse: And how can anyone tell them without being sent by the Lord? The Scriptures say it is a beautiful sight to see even the feet of someone coming to preach the good news.
translation_verse: Na sɛ bɛansoma asɛmpakafoɔ a, ɔbɛyɛdɛ na bi akɔka asɛm he? Nti bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Hwɛ bɛ a bɛde asɛmpa he ba he bɛ nane ase ɔfɛ fa!”

english_verse: Yet not everyone has believed the message. For example, the prophet Isaiah asked, “Lord, has anyone believed what we said?”
translation_verse: Nso nyɛ bɛ nyinaa ne bɛayɔ setie ama asɛmpa he; efiri sɛ Yesaia dɛ, “Awurade, hwane ne agye yɛ asɛm adi?”

english_verse: No one can have faith without hearing the message about Christ.
translation_verse: Nti gyidie firi nea yɛate mu ne ba, na nea yɛate nso firi Kristo asɛm he mu.

english_verse: But am I saying that the people of Israel did not hear? No, I am not! The Scriptures say, “The message was told everywhere on earth. It was announced all over the world.”
translation_verse: Nso meebisa sɛ, bɛntee koraa? Nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ bɛate; efiri sɛ “Bɛapae mu aka no asaase nyinaa so, na bɛ nsɛm nso aduru wiase afanaa nyinaa.”

english_verse: Did the people of Israel understand or not? Moses answered this question when he told that the Lord had said, “I will make Israel jealous of people who are a nation of nobodies. I will make them angry with people who don't understand a thing.”
translation_verse: Meebisa bio sɛ, nti Israel ante aseɛ? Nea odi kane, Mose dɛ, “Mɛma ho atwe bɛ a bɛnyɛ mane he ho ninkunu; mɛma ho bo afu atia amane nkwaseafoɔ.”

english_verse: Isaiah was fearless enough to tell that the Lord had said, “I was found by people who were not looking for me. I appeared to the ones who were not asking about me.”
translation_verse: Hedeɛ, Yesaia nyaa akokoɔduro paa sɛ ɔkaa sɛ, “Bɛ a bɛanhwehwɛ me he, bɛahu me; Maayi me ho adi akyerɛ bɛ a bɛanhwehwɛ me.”

english_verse: And Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “All day long the Lord has reached out to people who are stubborn and refuse to obey.”
translation_verse: Nso Israel ho deɛ ɔkaa sɛ, “Da nyinaa metrɛɛ me nsam maa mane asoɔdenfoɔ ne nyiyianofoɔ yi.”

english_verse: Am I saying that God has turned his back on his people? Certainly not! I am one of the people of Israel, and I myself am a descendant of Abraham from the tribe of Benjamin.
translation_verse: Afei meebisa sɛ, nti Nyankopɔn apo ne nkurofoɔ? Daabi da! M’ankasa meyɛ Israelni a meyɛ Abraham aseni a mefiri Benyamin abusuakuo mu.

english_verse: God did not turn his back on his chosen people. Don't you remember reading in the Scriptures how Elijah complained to God about the people of Israel?
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn mpoo ne nkurofoɔ a oyii bɛ sieeɛ he. Wonnim deɛ twerɛsɛm he ka fa Elia ho, sɛnea ɔbɔɔ Isreal soboɔ kyerɛɛ Nyankopɔn?

english_verse: He said, “Lord, they killed your prophets and destroyed your altars. I am the only one left, and now they want to kill me.”
translation_verse: “Awurade bɛaku w’adiyifoɔ, bɛabubu w’afɔrebokyia; me nkoaa ne maaka, na bɛɛhwehwɛ me aku me.”

english_verse: This means that only a chosen few of the people of Israel found what all of them were searching for. And the rest of them were stubborn,
translation_verse: Afei mmɛ bio? Israel nsa anka nea ɔɔhwehwɛ he. Bɛ a bɛayi bɛ he deɛ bɛ nsa kaeɛ, nso bɛ a bɛaka he deɛ beprim bɛ akoma,

english_verse: It is the same way now. God treated the people of Israel with undeserved grace, and so a few of them are still his followers.
translation_verse: Nti saa ne berɛ yi mu nso nnipa nkaeɛ bi ya hɔ a bɛnam adom so ayi bɛ.

english_verse: This happened because of God's undeserved kindness and not because of anything they have done. It could not have happened except for God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Na sɛ ɔyɛ adom a, ɔnna so nyinaa nnwuma so, anyɛ saa a, hedeɛ na adom he nyɛ adom ankasa.

english_verse: just as the Scriptures say, “God made them so stupid that their eyes are blind, and their ears are still deaf.”
translation_verse: sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Nyankopɔn ama bɛ honhom a ɔtɔ nko, ani a onhu adeɛ ne aso a ɔnte asɛm, de besi nnɛ.”

english_verse: Then David said, “Turn their meals into bait for a trap, so that they will stumble and be given what they deserve.
translation_verse: Na Dawid dɛ, “Ma bɛ adidipono nyɛ fidisum ne fidie, sintiduatoɔ ne akatua mma bɛ;

english_verse: Blindfold their eyes! Don't let them see. Bend their backs beneath a burden that will never be lifted.” Gentiles Will Be Saved
translation_verse: ma bɛ ani nyɛ kusuu sɛnea benhu adeɛ, na ma bɛ akyi nkuntunu daa.” Amanamane nkwagyeɛ

english_verse: Do I mean that the people of Israel fell, never to get up again? Certainly not! Their failure made it possible for the Gentiles to be saved, and this will make the people of Israel jealous.
translation_verse: Nti meebisa sɛ, bɛasonti sɛ bɛbɛhwe ase? Daabi da! Nso ɔnam bɛ sintidua he so ama Amanamane mufoɔ anya nkwagyeɛ, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a Israel bɛtwe ninkunu.

english_verse: But if the rest of the world's people were helped so much by their sin and loss, they will be helped even more by their full return.
translation_verse: Na afei sɛ bɛ sintiduatoɔ he kyerɛ wiase ahonya a, na sɛ bɛ nkoguo de ahonya maa Amanamane mufoɔ a, hedeɛ na gye a bɛbɛgye bɛ atom he bɛboro so!

english_verse: I am now speaking to you Gentiles, and as long as I am an apostle to you, I will take pride in my work.
translation_verse: Afei meekasa akyerɛ ho Amanamane mufoɔ. Esiane sɛ meyɛ somafoɔ ma Amanamane mufoɔ nti, mehyɛ me som animuonyam

english_verse: I hope in this way to make some of my own people jealous enough to be saved.
translation_verse: sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a m’ankasa me nkurofoɔ bɛtwe ninkunu, na bɛagye binom.

english_verse: When Israel rejected God, the rest of the people in the world were able to turn to him. So when God makes friends with Israel, it will be like bringing the dead back to life.
translation_verse: Na sɛ bɛpo a bɛpoo bɛ he bɛyɛ wiase mpataa a, hedeɛ bɛ gyeɛ nso bɛyɛ nkwa a ofiri wuom!

english_verse: If part of a batch of dough is made holy by being offered to God, then all of the dough is holy. If the roots of a tree are holy, the rest of the tree is holy too.
translation_verse: Afei sɛ dua aba a odi kane a bɛde bɔɔ afɔdeɛ he yɛ kronkron a, aba mu he nyinaa nso yɛ kronkron; na sɛ ne nhini he yɛ kronkron a, ne mmane he nso nyinaa bɛyɛ kronkron.

english_verse: You Gentiles are like branches of a wild olive tree made to be part of a cultivated olive tree. You have taken the place of some branches that were cut away from it. And because of this, you enjoy the blessings that come from being part of that cultivated tree.
translation_verse: Nso sɛ mmane he bi bu firii ho a, na wo, wuram ngodua, bɛde wo hyɛɛ ananem sɛ wobenya ahonya a ɔya ngodua nhini he mu bi a,

english_verse: But don't think you are better than the branches that were cut away. Just remember you are not supporting the roots of this tree. Its roots are supporting you.
translation_verse: nhoahoa wo ho mmane he ho. Sɛ wohoahoa wo ho a, kae sɛ ɔnyɛ wo ne wokita nhini he mu, na mmom nhini he ne kita wo mu.

english_verse: Maybe you think those branches were cut away, so you could be put in their place.
translation_verse: Saa nti wobɛka sɛ, “Mmane bi bubuiɛ sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a bɛde me besi ananem.”

english_verse: That's true enough. But they were cut away because they did not have faith, and you are where you are because you do have faith. So don't be proud, but be afraid.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ, yei yɛ nɔkorɛ. Gye a bɛannye anni nti ne betwaa bɛtweneeɛ, nso gyidie nkoaa so ne wobegyina. Nti nyɛ ahantan, na mmom fa suro gyina pintinn.

english_verse: If God cut away those natural branches, couldn't he do the same to you?
translation_verse: Na sɛ Nyankopɔn ankora mmane he ankasa a, ebia wo nso ɔnkora wo.

english_verse: Now you see both how kind and how hard God can be. He was hard on those who fell, but he was kind to you. And he will keep on being kind to you, if you keep on trusting in his kindness. Otherwise, you will be cut away too.
translation_verse: Hyɛ Nyankopɔn ayamyɛ ne ne dene yɛ nso: ɔdene ma bɛ a bɛhwee ase, nso Nyankopɔn ayamyɛ ya hɔ ma wo, gye sɛ wo toa so n’ayamyɛ he mu; anyɛ saa a wo nso bebetwa wo atwene.

english_verse: If those other branches will start having faith, they will be made a part of that tree again. God has the power to put them back.
translation_verse: Na mpo Israel nso, sɛ bɛantoa so ampirim bɛ akoma annyeannie ho a, bɛde bɛ bɛhyɛ mu bio, efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn wɔ tumi sɛ ɔde bɛ hyɛ mu bio.

english_verse: After all, it wasn't natural for branches to be cut from a wild olive tree and to be made part of a cultivated olive tree. So it is much more likely that God will join the natural branches back to the cultivated olive tree. The People of Israel Will Be Brought Back
translation_verse: Sɛ wo mpo, betwaa wo firii wuram ngodua he ho, na bɛakwati wɔawosuo de wo atɛ ngodua pa he mu a, ɔbɛyɛ dɛ ne ontumi mfa ne ngodua pa he ankasa mmane he ɔmmɛtɛ mu bio? Bɛbɛgye Israel nyinaa nkwa

english_verse: My friends, I don't want you Gentiles to be too proud of yourselves. So I will explain the mystery of what has happened to the people of Israel. Some of them have become stubborn, and they will stay like that until the complete number of you Gentiles has come in.
translation_verse: Nti, anuanom, mepɛ sɛ hote saa ahintasɛm yi ase, na hoammu ho ho anyansafoɔ ho ankasa ho ani so: akomapirim bi aba Israelfoɔ bi so, kosi sɛ Amanamane mufoɔ bebree bɛba mu.

english_verse: In this way all of Israel will be saved, as the Scriptures say, “From Zion someone will come to rescue us. Then Jacob's descendants will stop being evil.
translation_verse: Ne saa nti bɛbɛgye Israelfoɔ nyinaa nkwa; sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Sion mu ne Gyefoɔ befiri aba; obetu amumuyɔ afiri Yakob.”

english_verse: This is what the Lord has promised to do when he forgives their sins.”
translation_verse: “Na yei ne m’apam a me ne bɛ bɛpam, berɛ a meyi bɛ bɔne afiri hɔ.”

english_verse: The people of Israel are treated as God's enemies, so the good news can come to you Gentiles. But they are still the chosen ones, and God loves them because of their famous ancestors.
translation_verse: Ɔfa asɛmpa he ho deɛ, ho nti bɛyɛ Nyankopɔn atamfoɔ; nso ɔfa yie he ho deɛ, bɛyɛ adɔfoɔ, bɛ nananom nti;

english_verse: God doesn't take back the gifts he has given or disown the people he has chosen.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, Nyankopɔn ntwe n’akyɛdeɛ ne ne frɛ nsane da.

english_verse: At one time you Gentiles rejected God. But now Israel has rejected God, and you have been shown mercy.
translation_verse: Sɛnea mmerɛ bi hoyɔɔ asoɔdene tiaa Nyankopɔn nso bɛ asoɔdene nti hoanya mmɔborɔhunu he,

english_verse: And because of the mercy shown to you, they will also be shown mercy.
translation_verse: saa nso nti bɛayɛ asoɔdene a, ahummɔborɔ a hoanya nti, bɛ nso saa berɛ he bebenya mmɔborɔhunu.

english_verse: Who can measure the wealth and wisdom and knowledge of God? Who can understand his decisions or explain what he does?
translation_verse: O! Nyankopɔn ahonya ne ne nyansa ɔne ne nimdeɛ a mu dɔ! Yentumi nhu n’atemmuo ne n’akwane mu nwie da!

english_verse: “Has anyone known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?
translation_verse: “Na hwane ne ahu Awurade adwene? Anaa hwane ne ayɛ ne fotufoɔ?”

english_verse: Has anyone loaned something to the Lord that must be repaid?”
translation_verse: “Anaa hwane ne ama no akyɛdeɛ, a ɔbɛgye akyɛdeɛ asi ananem?”

english_verse: Everything comes from the Lord. All things were made because of him and will return to him. Praise the Lord forever! Amen.
translation_verse: Biaa nni hɔ efiri sɛ, nnoɔma nyinaa firi ne mu, na ɔnam ne so, na ɔyɛ ne dea. Ɔno ne animuonyam nka no daa daa. Amen.

english_verse: Dear friends, God is good. So I beg you to offer your bodies to him as a living sacrifice, pure and pleasing. That's the most sensible way to serve God.
translation_verse: Nti anuanom, megyina Nyankopɔn mmɔborɔhunu so ɔɔsrɛ ho sɛ, homfa ho nipadua nsi hɔ sɛ afɔdeɛ a ɔte ase, a ɔyɛ kronkron a ɔsɔ Nyankopɔn ani. Yei ne nyamesom pa.

english_verse: Don't be like the people of this world, but let God change the way you think. Then you will know how to do everything that is good and pleasing to him.
translation_verse: Na homfa wiase nhyehyɛeɛ yi so, na mmom honsakra ho adwene na honyɛ foforɔ, na hoasɛ ahwɛ ahu nea Nyankopɔn pɛ a ɔyɛ na ɔsɔ ani na odi mu.

english_verse: I realize God has treated me with undeserved grace, and so I tell each of you not to think you are better than you really are. Use good sense and measure yourself by the amount of faith that God has given you.
translation_verse: Na menam adom a bɛde ama me he so, meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, ho mu biaa mmu ne ho ntra sɛnea ɔsɛ sɛ obu ne ho, na mmom, biaa nya anidahɔ adwene, sɛnea Nyankopɔn asusu gyidie ama ho he.

english_verse: A body is made up of many parts, and each of them has its own use.
translation_verse: Na sɛnea honamdua baako wɔ akwaa bebree he, na biaa wɔ dwuma sonoko a odi he,

english_verse: That's how it is with us. There are many of us, but we each are part of the body of Christ, as well as part of one another.
translation_verse: saa nso ne yɛ a yɛdɔɔso yi, yɛyɛ nipadua baako Kristo mu, na yɛyeyɛ yɛ ho akwaa mmaako mmaako.

english_verse: God has also given each of us different gifts to use. If we can prophesy, we should do it according to the amount of faith we have.
translation_verse: Yɛwɔ adom akyɛdeɛ a nsonsonoeɛ deda mu, sɛnea adom a bɛde adom yɛ he teɛ. Sɛ ɔyɛ nkɔmhyɛ a, ma no mfata sɛnea bɛasusu ho gyidie;

english_verse: If we can serve others, we should serve. If we can teach, we should teach.
translation_verse: som adwuma a, ma no nsom; kyerɛkyerɛni a, ma no nkyerɛkyerɛ;

english_verse: If we can encourage others, we should encourage them. If we can give, we should be generous. If we are leaders, we should do our best. If we are good to others, we should do it cheerfully. Rules for Christian Living
translation_verse: fotufoɔ a, ma ontu fo; ɔma a, ma no mma no adɔeɛ mu; dikanfoɔ a, ma ɔmmɔ ne ho mmɔdene akannie mu; ahummɔborɔ a, ma ɔnyɔ no animteɛ mu. Nɔkorɛ kristoni ahyɛnsodeɛ

english_verse: Be sincere in your love for others. Hate everything that is evil and hold tight to everything that is good.
translation_verse: Hommma nyaatwom mfra dɔ mu; hontane nea ɔyɛ bɔne, honkura nea ɔyɛ papa mu;

english_verse: Love each other as brothers and sisters and honor others more than you do yourself.
translation_verse: homfa nteaseɛ nnodɔ ho ho yie; homfa anidie mmɔho mma ho ho.

english_verse: Never give up. Eagerly follow the Holy Spirit and serve the Lord.
translation_verse: Honnyɛ mmɔdenmmɔ ho nyaa, honyɛ hye honhom mu, honsom Awurade!

english_verse: Let your hope make you glad. Be patient in time of trouble and never stop praying.
translation_verse: Homma ho ani nnye anidasoɔ mu, honto ho boase amaneɛ mu, hontu ho ho nsi hɔ ma mpaebɔ.

english_verse: Take care of God's needy people and welcome strangers into your home.
translation_verse: Hommoa ahoteefoɔ ma bɛ nsa nka bɛ ahiadeɛ; homma ho ani nye ahɔhoyɛ ho.

english_verse: When others are happy, be happy with them, and when they are sad, be sad.
translation_verse: Ho ne bɛ a bɛ ani agye ani nnye mmom, ho ne bɛ a besu nso nsu mmom.

english_verse: Be friendly with everyone. Don't be proud and feel that you know more than others. Make friends with ordinary people.
translation_verse: Ho ne nnipa nyinaa ntena asomdwie mu; honnnyɛ ahantan, na mmom ho ne ahobrɛaseɛfoɔ nante; honnyɛ ahomasoɔ.

english_verse: Don't mistreat someone who has mistreated you. But try to earn the respect of others,
translation_verse: Hommmfa bɔne ntua bɔne so ka, na mmom honwene nea odi mu nnipa nyinaa ani so.

english_verse: and do your best to live at peace with everyone.
translation_verse: Sɛ obetumi a, ho fam deɛ, ho ne nnipa nyinaa ntena asomdwiem.

english_verse: Dear friends, don't try to get even. Let God take revenge. In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I am the one to take revenge and pay them back.”
translation_verse: Adɔfo, honnntotɔ ho ho ho ho werɛ, na mmom honnyae ma Nyankopɔn abufuo; efiri sɛ bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Awerɛtɔ yɛ me dea, meaa ne metua ka, Awurade ne ɔɔka.”

english_verse: The Scriptures also say, “If your enemies are hungry, give them something to eat. And if they are thirsty, give them something to drink. This will be the same as piling burning coals on their heads.”
translation_verse: Ne saa nti, sɛ kɔm de wo tamfoɔ a, ma no bibi nni; sɛ sukɔm de no a, ma no nsuo nom; na sɛ woyɔ saa a, wobɛhyɛ no aniwuo.

english_verse: Don't let evil defeat you, but defeat evil with good.
translation_verse: Mma bɔne nnni wo so, na mmom fa papa di bɔne so nim!

english_verse: Obey the rulers who have authority over you. Only God can give authority to anyone, and he puts these rulers in their places of power.
translation_verse: Homma nnipa nyinaa mmrɛbrɛ bɛ ho ase nhyɛ mane mpanyimfoɔ tumi he ase; efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn ne ma nnipa tumi ma ɔde di dwuma, na Nyankopɔn ne de saa tumidie he sii hɔ.

english_verse: People who oppose the authorities are opposing what God has done, and they will be punished.
translation_verse: Saa nti biaa a ɔte atua tumidie biaa so he te nea Nyankopɔn de no asi hɔ he so atua, na bɛ a bɛyɔ saa he bɛkɔ afobuo mu.

english_verse: Rulers are a threat to evil people, not to good people. There is no need to be afraid of the authorities. Just do right, and they will praise you for it.
translation_verse: Efiri sɛ bɛ a bedi mpanyinnie he ho nyɛ hu mma bɛ a bɛbɔ bra pa, na mmom bɛho ohu ma bɛ a bɛyɔ bɔne. Sɛ wompɛ sɛ wosuro tumfoɔ he a, hedeɛ yɔ nea ɔyɛ papa, na wobenya n’anim adom;

english_verse: After all, they are God's servants, and it is their duty to help you. If you do something wrong, you ought to be afraid, because these rulers have the right to punish you. They are God's servants who punish criminals to show how angry God is.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn somfoɔ ma wo yiedie. Nso sɛ woyɔ bɔne a, ɔsɛ sɛ wosuro no, efiri sɛ tumidie he nni hɔ kwa, na mmom atumfoɔ he yɛ Nyankopɔn asomfoɔ a bɛtɔ were de abufuo ba deɛ ɔyɛ bɔne he so.

english_verse: But you should obey the rulers because you know it is the right thing to do, and not just because of God's anger.
translation_verse: Ne saa nti ɔsɛ sɛ nipa brɛ ne ho ase, nyɛ abufuo nkoaa nti na mmom suro a osuro atumfoɔ he ɔnoaa n’ahonim nti.

english_verse: You must also pay your taxes. The authorities are God's servants, and it is their duty to take care of these matters.
translation_verse: Yei nti ne ɔsɛ sɛ wo tua toɔ nso, efiri sɛ atumfoɔ yi yɛ Nyankopɔn asomfoɔ, na mane he som ti ne bɛya hɔ.

english_verse: Let love be your only debt! If you love others, you have done all that the Law demands.
translation_verse: Honnne biaa ka, gye sɛ dɔ a hode bɛdodɔ ho ho; na nea ɔdɔ foforɔ he awie mmara he so di,

english_verse: In the Law there are many commands, such as, “Be faithful in marriage. Do not murder. Do not steal. Do not want what belongs to others.” But all of these are summed up in the command that says, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: efiri sɛ mmaransɛm a ɔka sɛ, “Mmɔ adwamane; Nni awu; Mmɔ korɔno; Mma w’ani mmere” ne mmaransɛm foforɔ biaa, bɛde ne nyinaa abom asɛm baako yi mu, “Dɔ wo yɔnko sɛ wo ho.”

english_verse: No one who loves others will harm them. So love is all that the Law demands. The Day When Christ Returns
translation_verse: Deɛ ɔdɔ ne yɔnko nyɔ no bɔne; nti dɔ ne ma mmara he nyinaa ba mu. Adesrɛ a ohia

english_verse: You know what sort of times we live in, and so you should live properly. It is time to wake up. You know that the day when we will be saved is nearer now than when we first put our faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Yeinom nyinaa akyi he, honim sɛnea berɛ he teɛ, sɛ saa berɛ yi ne ɔsɛ sɛ hosɔre firi ho nna mu; efiri sɛ nkwagyeɛ bɛne yɛ kyɛne berɛ a yɛbɛyɛɛ agyidifoɔ;

english_verse: Night is almost over, and day will soon appear. We must stop behaving as people do in the dark and be ready to live in the light.
translation_verse: anadwo akɔ n’anim, adekyeɛ abɛne. Saa nti ho ma yɛnto sum nnwuma ngu na yɛmfa hann akodeɛ;

english_verse: So behave properly, as people do in the day. Don't go to wild parties or get drunk or be vulgar or indecent. Don't quarrel or be jealous.
translation_verse: homma yɛmmɔ bra a ɔfata hann mma, ɔnyɛ agobɔne ne nsaboro mu, nyɛ adwamammɔ ne akohwie mu, nyɛ ntɔkwakoo ne aniberebereɛ mu.

english_verse: Let the Lord Jesus Christ be as near to you as the clothes you wear. Then you won't try to satisfy your selfish desires.
translation_verse: Na mmom, homfa Awurade Yesu Kristo nhyɛ , na homma kwane mma honam, akɔnnɔ nni ho so!.

english_verse: Welcome all the Lord's followers, even those whose faith is weak. Don't criticize them for having beliefs that are different from yours.
translation_verse: Honnye nea ne gyidie ɔmmerɛ he na hommmu atɛne ya adwene nnaayɛ ho.

english_verse: Some think it is all right to eat anything, while those whose faith is weak will eat only vegetables.
translation_verse: Binom gye di sɛ bɛwɔ ho kwane sɛ bedi nnoɔma nyinaa, na nea ɔmmerɛ he deɛ odi nhahama nkoaa.

english_verse: What right do you have to criticize someone else's servants? Only their Lord can decide if they are doing right, and the Lord will make sure that they do right.
translation_verse: Wo ne hwane sɛ wobebu bi akoa atɛne? Ɔyɛ bɛ wura he ankasa ne bɛkyerɛ sɛ nea bɛayɔ he yɛ anaa ɔnyɛ; efiri sɛ nɔkorem he Awurade betumi ama bɛagyina

english_verse: Some of the Lord's followers think one day is more important than another. Others think all days are the same. But each of you should make up your own mind.
translation_verse: Binom bu atɛne sɛ da bi di mu kyɛne bi, binom nso bu nna nyinaa pɛ. Homma biaa nte ne ho ase sɛnea n’adwene kyerɛ no.

english_verse: Any followers who count one day more important than another day do it to honor their Lord. And any followers who eat meat give thanks to God, just like the ones who don't eat meat.
translation_verse: Bɛ a bebu da bi he, bɛde hyɛ Awurade animuonyam. Na bɛ a bedi he nso, bedi hyɛ Awurade animuonyam, esiane sɛ bɛda Nyankopɔn ase; bɛ a benni he nso, di a benni he bɛde hyɛ Awurade animuonyam, na bɛda Nyankopɔn ase.

english_verse: Whether we live or die, it must be for God, rather than for ourselves.
translation_verse: Na yɛnnte ase mma yɛ ho, saa so ne yenwu mma yɛ ho.

english_verse: Whether we live or die, it must be for the Lord. Alive or dead, we still belong to the Lord.
translation_verse: Sɛ yɛte ase a, yɛte ase ma Awurade, na sɛ yewu nso a, yewu ma Awurade; nti, sɛ yɛte ase oo, anaa sɛ yewu oo, yɛyɛ Awurade dea.

english_verse: This is because Christ died and rose to life, so that he would be the Lord of the dead and of the living.
translation_verse: Na yei nti Kristo wuiɛ na ɔte ase bio, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a ɔbɛyɛ awufoɔ ne ateasefoɔ nyinaa bɛ Awurade.

english_verse: Why do you criticize other followers of the Lord? Why do you look down on them? The day is coming when God will judge all of us.
translation_verse: Mmɛ nti ne wobu wo ankasa wo nua atɛne? Anaa wo, ɛɛne nti ne wobu wo nua animtia?

english_verse: In the Scriptures God says, “I swear by my very life that everyone will kneel down and praise my name!”
translation_verse: Na bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Awurade dɛ, sɛ mete ase yi, kotodwe nyinaa bɛkoto me, na kɛtrɛma nyinaa beyi Nyankopɔn ayɛ.”

english_verse: And so, each of us must give an account to God for what we do. Don't Cause Problems for Others
translation_verse: Ne saa nti, yemu biaa bebu n’ankasa akonta akyerɛ Nyankopɔn. Honto biaa sintidua

english_verse: We must stop judging others. We must also make up our minds not to upset anyone's faith.
translation_verse: Afei hommma yemmu biaa atɛne, nso homma yɛnyɔ yɛadwene sɛ yɛmmfa akwansideɛ nto biaa kwane mu, saa ara nso ne yɛnnto biaa sintidua.

english_verse: The Lord Jesus has made it clear to me that God considers all foods fit to eat. But if you think some foods are unfit to eat, then for you they are not fit.
translation_verse: Menim na megye di Awurade Yesu mu sɛ bibi biaa nni hɔ a honnte ankasa; nso honnte mma nea ɔdwene sɛ honnte.

english_verse: If you are hurting others by the foods you eat, you are not guided by love. Don't let your appetite destroy someone Christ died for.
translation_verse: Sɛ aduane a wodi sɛe wo nua ahonim a, hedeɛ na ɔnyɛ dɔ mu ne woonante he. Mma aduane a wodi he nto bi a Kristo wu maa no he sintidua.

english_verse: Don't let your right to eat bring shame to Christ.
translation_verse: Nti hommma bɛmmu ho papayɔ sɛ ɔyɛ bɔne;

english_verse: God's kingdom isn't about eating and drinking. It is about pleasing God, about living in peace, and about true happiness. All this comes from the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn ahennie he mfa adidie ne nom ho, na mmom ne nyinasoɔ ne tenenee ne asomdwie ne Honhom Kronkron mu anigyeɛ.

english_verse: If you serve Christ in this way, you will please God and be respected by people.
translation_verse: Na nea ɔsom Kristo saa kwane so he ɔno ne sɔ Nyankopɔn ani na nnipa gye no tom.

english_verse: We should try to live at peace and help each other have a strong faith.
translation_verse: Nti homma yenni asomdwie ɔne nnoɔma a ɔma nkɔsoɔ akyi.

english_verse: Don't let your appetite destroy what God has done. All foods are fit to eat, but it is wrong to cause problems for others by what you eat.
translation_verse: Aduane nti, honnsɛe Nyankopɔn adwuma. Adeɛ nyinaa ho te deɛ, nso ɔnnyɛ sɛ aduane a wodi nti woma bi hwe ase;

english_verse: It is best not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything else that causes problems for other followers of the Lord.
translation_verse: ɔyɛ sɛ wonnwe nam anaa wonnom nsa anaa sɛ wonnyɔ bibi a ɔbɛto wo nua sintidua.

english_verse: What you believe about these things should be kept between you and God. You are fortunate, if your actions don't make you have doubts.
translation_verse: Gyidie a wowɔ he, ma ɔnyɛ sɛnea w’ahonim teɛ Nyankopɔn anim. Nhyira ne nea a ɔmma nea wasi ho gyinaeɛ he mmu ne fɔ.

english_verse: But if you do have doubts about what you eat, you are going against your beliefs. And you know that is wrong, because anything you do against your beliefs is sin.
translation_verse: Nso bɛ a bɛ adwene nsi bɛ pi he sɛ bedi a bedi fɔ, efiri sɛ bɛannyɔ no gyidie mu, na bibiaa a omfiri gyidie mu biaa yɛ bɔne.

english_verse: If our faith is strong, we should be patient with the Lord's followers whose faith is weak. We should try to please them instead of ourselves.
translation_verse: Afei yɛ a yɛ ho ɔdene he ɔsɛ sɛ yɛboa bɛ a bɛyɛ mmerɛ he, na yɛansɔ yɛ ankasa yɛ ani.

english_verse: We should think of their good and try to help them by doing what pleases them.
translation_verse: Ɔsɛ sɛ yemmu biaa sɔ ne yɔnko ani a ne botae ne sɛ yɛbɛboa yɛ yɔnko ama no anyini honhom mu;

english_verse: Even Christ did not try to please himself. But as the Scriptures say, “The people who insulted you also insulted me.”
translation_verse: efiri sɛ Kristo ammɛsɔ ɔnoaa ankasa ani; na mmom, sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Atɛm a bedii wo he abegu me so.”

english_verse: God is the one who makes us patient and cheerful. I pray that he will help you live at peace with each other, as you follow Christ.
translation_verse: Boasetɔ ne awerekyekyerɛ Nyankopɔn he mma ho ntena asomdwie mu, sɛnea ɔfata Kristo Yesu,

english_verse: Then all of you together will praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. The Good News Is for Jews and Gentiles
translation_verse: na hode anokorɔ ahyɛ Nyankopɔn a ɔyɛ yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo agya he animuonyam. Asɛmpa a ɔyɛ Yudafoɔ ne Amanamane mufoɔ nyinaa deɛ

english_verse: Honor God by accepting each other, as Christ has accepted you.
translation_verse: Nti, honnye biaa mma ɔnhyɛ Nyankopɔn animuonyam, sɛnea Kristo nso agye ho he.

english_verse: I tell you that Christ came as a servant of the Jews to show that God has kept the promises he made to their famous ancestors. Christ also came,
translation_verse: Na meeka kyerɛ ho sɛ Kristo gyina Nyankopɔn nɔkorɛ so ayɛ akoa ama twetiatwafoɔ sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a ɔbɛhyɛ bɔhyɛ a bɛde maa nananom he dene,

english_verse: so that the Gentiles would praise God for being kind to them. It is just as the Scriptures say, “I will tell the nations about you, and I will sing praises to your name.”
translation_verse: na wɔama Amanamane mufoɔ ahyɛ Nyankopɔn animuonyam ne mmɔborɔhunu nti. Sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Nti mɛbɔ wo dini Amanamane mufoɔ mu, na mato wo dini ho ayeyie nnwomu”;

english_verse: The Scriptures also say to the Gentiles, “Come and celebrate with God's people.”
translation_verse: na bio so ɔdɛ, “Amanamane mufoɔ, ho ne ne nkurofoɔ mma ho ani nye”;

english_verse: Again the Scriptures say, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles. All you nations, come and worship him.”
translation_verse: na bio, “Amanamane mufoɔ nyinaa, ho nyi Awurade ayɛ, na homma nnipa nyinaa nyi n’ayɛ”;

english_verse: Isaiah says, “Someone from David's family will come to power. He will rule the nations, and they will put their hope in him.”
translation_verse: na afei nso Yesaia kaa sɛ, “Yisai aseni bi bɛba, nea ɔbɛsɔre adi hene Amanamane mufoɔ so; ne mu ne Amanamane mufoɔ benya anidasoɔ.”

english_verse: I pray that God, who gives hope, will bless you with complete happiness and peace because of your faith. And may the power of the Holy Spirit fill you with hope. Paul's Work as a Missionary
translation_verse: Anidasoɔ Nyankopɔn ankasa nhyɛ ho ma anigyeɛ ne asomdwie nyinaa mu gye a hoogye adi ho, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a hobɛboro so anidasoɔ nyinaa mu Honhom Kronkron tumi mu. Deɛ nti a Paulo de akokoɔduro ɔɔtwerɛ

english_verse: My friends, I am sure that you are very good and that you have all the knowledge you need to teach each other.
translation_verse: Me ankasa mewɔ awerɛhyɛmu ma ho, me nuanom, ho ankasa hoayɛ ma nnepa a ɔyɛ nyinaa mu, nimdeɛ nyinaa ahyɛ ho ma, na hotumi kyerɛkyerɛ ho ho.

english_verse: But I have spoken to you plainly and have tried to remind you of some things. God treated me with undeserved grace!
translation_verse: Nso ne nyinaa mu he, deɛ ntia meetwerɛ ho penpen ya nsɛm yi bi ho, te sɛ deɛ mede ɔɔkae ho bio he ne adom a Nyankopɔn de adom me

english_verse: He chose me to be a servant of Christ Jesus for the Gentiles and to do the work of a priest in the service of his good news. God did this so that the Holy Spirit could make the Gentiles into a holy offering, pleasing to him.
translation_verse: sɛ mɛnyɛ Kristo somfoɔ mma Amanamane mufoɔ sɛ Nyankopɔn asɛmpa he ho asɔfodwuma, na Amanamane mufoɔ afɔrebɔ ayɛ nea Honhom Kronkron ate ho a ɔsɔ ani.

english_verse: Because of Christ Jesus, I can take pride in my service for God.
translation_verse: Kristo Yesu mu deɛ, metumi hoahoa me ho Nyankopɔn adwuma a meyɔ ho.

english_verse: In fact, all I will talk about is how Christ let me speak and work, so that the Gentiles would obey him.
translation_verse: Nti menhyɛ me ho nka asɛm biaa gye nea Kristo nam me so ayɔ ama Amanamane mufoɔ setie ya asɛm ne adwuma mu

english_verse: Indeed, I will tell how Christ worked miracles and wonders by the power of the Holy Spirit. I have preached the good news about him all the way from Jerusalem to Illyricum.
translation_verse: tumi a ɔyɔ anwanwadeɛ ne nsɛnkyerɛnne, Nyankopɔn Honhom tumi mu, sɛ maawie Kristo asɛmpa he ka firi Yerusalem de kosi Iliriko.

english_verse: But I have always tried to preach where people have never heard about Christ. I am like a builder who doesn't build on someone else's foundation.
translation_verse: Nti mede ayɛ me botae sɛ mɛka asɛmpa he, nyɛ baabi a bɛabɔ Kristo dini dada, na manto bi fapem so,

english_verse: It is just as the Scriptures say, “All who haven't been told about him will see him, and those who haven't heard about him will understand.” Paul's Plan To Visit Rome
translation_verse: na mmom sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Bɛ a bɛnkaa ne ho asɛm nkyerɛɛ bɛ he behu, na bɛ a bɛntee ne ho asɛm he bɛte aseɛ.” Paulo pɛɛ sɛ ɔbɛkɔ Roma

english_verse: My work has always kept me from coming to see you.
translation_verse: Yei nti ne bɛasi kwane a mantumi amma ho nkyɛne.

english_verse: Now there is nothing left for me to do in this part of the world, and for years I have wanted to visit you.
translation_verse: Nso seesei deɛ, baabiaa nni hɔ a aka ma me a mɛkɔ mpɔtamu ha, ɔyɛ mepɛ, sɛnea mayɔ sɛ mfie bebree di, mɛba ho nkyɛne.

english_verse: So I plan to stop off on my way to Spain. Then after a short, but refreshing, visit with you, I hope you will quickly send me on.
translation_verse: Da biaa a metu kwane akɔ Spania he, m’ani da so sɛ, mɛfa hɔ a, mehu ho anim, na sɛ megye m’ani kakra ho nkyɛne a, hoagya me kwane akɔ hɔ.

english_verse: I am now on my way to Jerusalem to deliver the money that the Lord's followers in Macedonia and Achaia collected for God's needy people.
translation_verse: na aasɔ Makedonia ne Akaia ani sɛ bɛde nea bɛwɔ he bi bɛma ahoteefoɔ a bɛya Yerusalem he mu ahiafoɔ.

english_verse: This is something they really wanted to do. But sharing their money with the Jews was also like paying back a debt, because the Jews had already shared their spiritual blessings with the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Ɔsɔɔ bɛ ani sɛ bɛbɛyɔ saa, nɔkorɛ mu ɔfata; efiri sɛ, sɛ ɔnam bɛ so ama Amanamane mufoɔ he anya honhom fam nhyira a, otwa sɛ bɛ nso bɛma bɛ honam fam nnoɔma de som bɛ bi.

english_verse: After I have safely delivered this money, I will visit you and then go on to Spain.
translation_verse: Nti, sɛ mewie saa adeɛ yi a, na mede nea bɛagyegye he ma bɛ a, mɛfa ho nkyɛne akɔ Spania;

english_verse: And when I do arrive in Rome, I know it will be with the full blessings of Christ.
translation_verse: na menim sɛ, sɛ meba ho nkyɛne a, mede Kristo mu nhyira a ɔboro so bɛbra ho.

english_verse: My friends, by the power of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the love that comes from the Holy Spirit, I beg you to pray sincerely with me and for me.
translation_verse: Nso anuanom, megyina yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo ne Honhom he dɔ so, srɛ ho sɛ hommoa me, na homfa mpaebɔ nsrɛ Nyankopɔn aniberɛ so mma me,

english_verse: Pray that God will protect me from the unbelievers in Judea, and that his people in Jerusalem will be pleased with what I am doing.
translation_verse: sɛ bɛbɛgye me afiri Yudeafoɔ a bɛnnye asɛmpa he nni he nsam, na me som a mɛsom Yerusalem he asɔ ahoteefoɔ he ani,

english_verse: Ask God to let me come to you and have a pleasant and refreshing visit.
translation_verse: sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a ɔnam Nyankopɔn pɛ so mede anigyeɛ bɛba ho nkyɛne na me ho adwo me ho nkyɛne.

english_verse: I pray that God, who gives peace, will be with all of you. Amen.
translation_verse: Asomdwie Nyankopɔn he ne ho nyinaa ntena. Amen.

english_verse: I have good things to say about Phoebe, who is a leader in the church at Cenchreae.
translation_verse: Afei medi yɛ nuabaa Foibe, somfoɔ a ɔya Kenkrea asafo mu ho adanse-pa kyerɛ ho,

english_verse: Welcome her in a way that is proper for someone who has faith in the Lord and is one of God's own people. Help her in any way you can. After all, she has proved to be a respected leader for many others, including me.
translation_verse: sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a hobɛgye no Awurade mu sɛ anidie a ɔfata ahoteefoɔ, na hommoa no nea obebisa ho nyinaa ho, efiri sɛ waboa nnipa bebree a meaa nso meka ho bi.

english_verse: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila. They have not only served Christ Jesus together with me,
translation_verse: Honkyea Priska ne Akwila, bɛ a bɛ ne meyɔ adwuma Kristo Yesu mu,

english_verse: but they have even risked their lives for me. I am grateful for them and so are all the Gentile churches.
translation_verse: na bekuu bɛ ho maa me, me nkwa ho, na meda bɛ ase, ɔnyɛ bɛ nkoa na mmom Amanamane mufoɔ asafo he nyinaa nso ka ho bi.

english_verse: Greet the church that meets in their home. Greet my dear friend Epaenetus, who was the first person in Asia to have faith in Christ.
translation_verse: Honkyea asafo a ɔya bɛ fie he nso. Honkyea me dɔfo Epaineto, a ɔyɛ Kristo mu abakane ya Asia he.

english_verse: Greet Mary, who has worked so hard for you.
translation_verse: Honkyea Maria a, wayɔ adwuma dene paa ho mu he.

english_verse: Greet my relatives Andronicus and Junia, who were in jail with me. They are highly respected by the apostles and were followers of Christ before I was.
translation_verse: Honkyea Androniko ne Yunia, m’abusuafoɔ a na bɛka me ho afiase; bɛ a bedi mu asomafoɔ he mu, na bɛya Kristo mu ansa na meeba.

english_verse: Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend whose faith is in the Lord.
translation_verse: Honkyea Amplia a ɔyɛ me dɔfo Awurade mu.

english_verse: Greet Urbanus, who serves Christ along with us. Greet my dear friend Stachys.
translation_verse: Honkyea Urbano a ɔyɛ me yɔnko Kristo mu dwumayɔni, ne me dɔfoɔ Staki.

english_verse: Greet Apelles, a faithful servant of Christ. Greet Aristobulus and his family.
translation_verse: Honkyea Apele, a ɔsɔ ani Kristo mu. Honkyea bɛ a befiri Aristobulo abusua mu.

english_verse: Greet Herodion, who is a relative of mine. Greet Narcissus and the others in his family, who have faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Honkyea me busuani Herodion. Honkyea Narkiso abusuafoɔ a bɛya Awurade mu.

english_verse: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who work hard for the Lord. Greet my dear friend Persis. She also works hard for the Lord.
translation_verse: Honkyea adwumayɔfoɔ a bɛya Awurade mu, Trifaina ne Trifosa. Honkyea dɔfoɔ Persi a wayɔ adwuma dene Awurade mu.

english_verse: Greet Rufus, that special servant of the Lord, and greet his mother, who has been like a mother to me.
translation_verse: Honkyea Rufo a, bɛayi no Awurade mu; na honkyea ne maame—a ɔyɛ me nso me maame.

english_verse: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, and Hermas, as well as our friends who are with them.
translation_verse: Honkyea Asinkrito, Flegon, Herme, Patroba, Herma, ne anuanom a bɛka bɛ ho.

english_verse: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all of God's people who are with them.
translation_verse: Honkyea Filologo, Yulia, Nereo ɔne ne nuabaa, ne Olimpa, ne ahoteefoɔ a bɛ ne bɛya hɔ he.

english_verse: Be sure to give each other a warm greeting. All of Christ's churches greet you.
translation_verse: Honkyeakyea ho ho dɔ kwane so. Kristo asafo he nyinaa kyea ho. Nkyerɛkyerɛ a otwa toɔ

english_verse: My friends, I beg you to watch out for anyone who causes trouble and divides the church by refusing to do what all of you were taught. Stay away from them!
translation_verse: Anuanom, mehyɛ ho nkurane sɛ, honhwɛ ho ho yie bɛ a bɛde mpaepaemu ne sintiduatoɔ, de ko tia nkyerɛkyerɛ a hoasua he; hontwe ho ho mfiri bɛ ho,

english_verse: They want to serve themselves and not Christ the Lord. Their flattery and fancy talk fool people who don't know any better.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ nnipa a bɛte saa he nsom yɛ Awurade Kristo, na mmom bɛsom bɛ a bɛ akɔnnɔ, na bɛde kɛtrɛma dɛ ne adɛfɛdɛfɛsɛm daadaa bɛ a bennim bɔne he akoma.

english_verse: I am glad that everyone knows how well you obey the Lord. But still, I want you to understand what is good and not have anything to do with evil.
translation_verse: Nti ɔma m’ani gye ho ho, mepɛ sɛ honya nnepa ho nyansa, na honte ho ho mfiri bɔne ho koraa.

english_verse: Then God, who gives peace, will soon crush Satan under your feet. I pray that our Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
translation_verse: Asomdwie Nyankopɔn he bɛpɛkyɛ Satan ho nane ase ntɛm. Yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo adom nka ho ho.

english_verse: Timothy, who works with me, sends his greetings, and so do my relatives, Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater.
translation_verse: Timoteo, me yɔnko dwumayɔni, ne Lukio ne Yason ne Sosipatro a bɛyɛ m’abusuafoɔ kyea ho.

english_verse: I, Tertius, also send my greetings. I am a follower of the Lord, and I wrote this letter.
translation_verse: Me Tertio a meetwerɛ Paulo ano asɛm yi, me nso mekyea ho Awurade mu.

english_verse: Praise God! He can make you strong by means of my good news, which is the message about Jesus Christ. For ages and ages this message was kept secret,
translation_verse: Afei Nyankopɔn a otumi hyɛ ho dene sɛnea m’asɛmpa he teɛ ne Yesu Kristo ho asɛnka, sɛnea adiyie ahintasɛm a bɛde siee teteete

english_verse: but now at last it has been told. The eternal God commanded his prophets to write about the good news, so that all nations would obey and have faith.
translation_verse: nso afei deɛ bɛayi no adi, na ɔnam adiyifoɔ ntwerɛe so a bɛama Amanamane mufoɔ bɛahu, sɛnea daa daa Nyankopɔn hyɛeɛ he, sɛ ɔde setie a ɔkɔ gyidie mu—

english_verse: And now, because of Jesus Christ, we can praise the only wise God forever! Amen.
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn a ɔno ne nyansafoɔ korɔ he, animuonyam mfa Yesu Kristo so, nka no daa! Amen.

english_verse: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from Sosthenes, who is also a follower.
translation_verse: Paulo, Kristo Yesu somafoɔ a bɛnam Nyankopɔn pɛ so afrɛ no, ɔne yɛ nua Sostene,

english_verse: To God's church in Corinth. Christ Jesus chose you to be his very own people, and you worship in his name, as we and all others do who call him Lord.
translation_verse: twerɛ krataa yi de kɔma Nyankopɔn asafo a ɔya Korinto, ɔne bɛ a Nyankopɔn ate bɛ ho Kristo Yesu mu na wafrɛ bɛ sɛ bɛmmɛyɛ ahoteefoɔ, ɔne bɛ a bɛbɔ yɛ ne ho nyinaa Awurade Yesu Kristo dini baabiaa.

english_verse: My prayer is that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
translation_verse: Adom ne asomdwie a ofiri Agya Nyankopɔn ne Awurade Yesu hɔ nka ho. Aseda

english_verse: I never stop thanking my God for treating you with undeserved grace by giving you Christ Jesus,
translation_verse: Meda me Nyankopɔn ase daa ma ho esiane Nyankopɔn adom a ɔde ama ho Yesu Kristo mu,

english_verse: who helps you speak and understand so well.
translation_verse: sɛ nnoɔma nyinaa mu hoadi yie Kristo mu, kasa ne nimdeɛ nyinaa mu—

english_verse: You are not missing out on any blessings, as you wait for him to return.
translation_verse: na amma adom akyɛdeɛ biaa ammɔ ho berɛ a hootwɛne yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo adiyie he dendenndene yi.

english_verse: And until the day Christ does return, he will keep you completely innocent.
translation_verse: Ɔno Nyankopɔn ankasa nso bɛhyɛ ho dene akosi awieeɛ, na ho ho amma asɛm Awurade Yesu Kristo da he.

english_verse: God can be trusted, and he chose you to be partners with his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ. Taking Sides
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn a wafrɛ ho aba ne Ba, yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo ayɔnkofa mu, he yɛ nɔkwafoɔ. Mpaepaemu a ɔya Asafo he mu

english_verse: My dear friends, as a follower of our Lord Jesus Christ, I beg you to get along with each other. Don't take sides. Always try to agree in what you think.
translation_verse: Afei anuanom, menam yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo dini so ootu ho fo sɛ, ho nyinaa honya adwene korɔ; na hommma mpaepaemu mma ho mu, na mmom honka mmom adwene korɔ mu ne nsusuiɛ korɔ mu.

english_verse: Several people from Chloe's family have already reported to me that you keep arguing with each other.
translation_verse: Me nuanom, asɛm he ne sɛ, Kloe fiefoɔ atia me nane so sɛ akasakasaa ya ho mu.

english_verse: They have said that some of you claim to follow me, while others claim to follow Apollos or Peter or Christ.
translation_verse: Nea mate ne sɛ ho mu bi ka sɛ, “Meya Paulo afa,” bi nso “Meya Apolo afa,” saa ara nso ne bi nso ka sɛ, “Meya Kefa afa,” anaa “meya Kristo afa.”

english_verse: Has Christ been divided up? Was I nailed to a cross for you? Were you baptized in my name?
translation_verse: Kristo mu akyekyɛ anaa? Paulo ne bɛbɔɔ no asennuam maa ho anaa? Anaa Paulo dini mu ne bɛbɔɔ ho asu hyɛeɛ?

english_verse: I thank God that I didn't baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius.
translation_verse: Meda Nyankopɔn ase sɛ mammɔ ho mu biaa asu ka Krispo ne Gaio ho,

english_verse: Not one of you can say that you were baptized in my name.
translation_verse: na amma bi anka sɛ bɛabɔ no asu ahyɛ me dini mu.

english_verse: Christ did not send me to baptize. He sent me to tell the good news without using words that sound wise and would make the cross of Christ lose its power. Christ Is God's Power and Wisdom
translation_verse: Ɔnyɛ asubɔ nti ne Kristo somaa me, na mmom ɔsomaa me sɛ memmɛka asɛmpa he, ɔnyɛ kasaa mu nyansa mu, na Kristo asennua he anyɛ kwa. Kristo yɛ Nyankopɔn Nyansa ne ne Tumi

english_verse: The message about the cross doesn't make any sense to lost people. But for those of us who are being saved, it is God's power at work.
translation_verse: Na asennua he ho asɛm he yɛ nkwaseasɛm ma bɛ a bɛɛyera, na yɛ a bɛɛgye yɛ nkwa he deɛ, ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn tumi ma yɛ;

english_verse: What happened to those wise people? What happened to those experts in the Scriptures? What happened to the ones who think they have all the answers? Didn't God show that the wisdom of this world is foolish?
translation_verse: Na afei nyansani ya he? Twerɛfoɔ ya he? Wiase yi kyinnyefoɔ nso ya he? Nti Nyankopɔn nnanee wiase nyansa nkwaseasɛm anaa?

english_verse: God was wise and decided not to let the people of this world use their wisdom to learn about him. Instead, God chose to save only those who believe the foolish message we preach.
translation_verse: Na esiane sɛ, Nyankopɔn nyansa mu he wamma nnipa amfa wiase nyansa so anhu no nti, ɔsɔɔ n’ani sɛ ɔbɛfa asɛnka he nkwaseasɛm so agye bɛ a bɛgye di he nkwa.

english_verse: Jews ask for miracles, and Greeks want something that sounds wise.
translation_verse: Na Yudafoɔ bisa nsɛnkyerɛnne; ne Helafoɔ nso hwehwɛ nyansa.

english_verse: But we preach that Christ was nailed to a cross. Most Jews have problems with this, and most Gentiles think it is foolish.
translation_verse: Na yɛ deɛ, yɛka Kristo a bɛbɔɔ no asennuam he ho asɛm, ɔyɛ sintidua ma Yudafoɔ na ɔyɛ nkwaseasɛm ma Helafoɔ;

english_verse: Our message is God's power and wisdom for the Jews and the Greeks that he has chosen.
translation_verse: nso bɛ a bɛafrɛ bɛ he deɛ, Yudafoɔ oo, Helafoɔ oo, Kristo yɛ Nyankopɔn tumi ne Nyankopɔn nyansa ma bɛ;

english_verse: Even when God is foolish, he is wiser than everyone else, and even when God is weak, he is stronger than everyone else.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ nyansa ya Nyankopɔn nkwaseasɛm mu kyɛne nnipa nyansa, na Nyankopɔn mmerɛyɛ mu wɔ ahoɔdene kyɛne nnipa.

english_verse: But God chose the foolish things of this world to put the wise to shame. He chose the weak things of this world to put the powerful to shame.
translation_verse: na Nyankopɔn fa deɛ wiase bu no sɛ nkwaseadeɛ de hyɛ anyansafoɔ aniwuo; Nyankopɔn fa deɛ ɔmmerɛ wiase de hyɛ ahoɔdenfoɔ aniwuo.

english_verse: What the world thinks is worthless, useless, and nothing at all is what God has used to destroy what the world considers important.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn fa wiase yi mu adeɛ a ɔnsɛ hwee ne nea bebu no animtia, nnoɔma a onni hɔ de brɛ deɛ ɔya hɔ he ase,

english_verse: God did all this to keep anyone from bragging to him.
translation_verse: sɛ ɔbɛyɔ a biaa ɔnhoahoa ne ho Nyankopɔn anim.

english_verse: You are God's children. He sent Christ Jesus to save us and to make us wise, acceptable, and holy.
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn yɛ ho nkwa a howɔ no Kristo Yesu mu he farebae. Na Yesu Kristo yi ne bɛyɛɛ nyansadeɛ a ofiri Nyankopɔn hɔ maa yɛ he, ɔno ne tenenee ne ahoteɛ ne gyeɛ,

english_verse: So if you want to brag, do what the Scriptures say and brag about the Lord.
translation_verse: sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Ma deɛ ɔhoahoa ne ho he, nhoahoa ne ho Awurade mu.”

english_verse: Friends, when I came and told you the mystery that God had shared with us, I didn't use big words or try to sound wise.
translation_verse: Na me nso, anuanom, berɛ a mebaa ho nkyɛne he, mamfa nyansa a ɔtra so ne bɛkaa Nyankopɔn ahintasɛm he.

english_verse: In fact, while I was with you, I made up my mind to speak only about Jesus Christ, who had been nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Na mmom nɛbɔɔ me tim pɔ sɛ memmfa m’adwene nsi bibiaa so ka Yesu Kristo, ne bɔ a bɛbɔɔ no asennua mu he ho.

english_verse: At first, I was weak and trembling with fear.
translation_verse: Na mebaa ho nkyɛne mmerɛyɛ ne suro ne ahopopoɔ mu.

english_verse: When I talked with you or preached, I didn't try to prove anything by sounding wise. I simply let God's Spirit show his power.
translation_verse: Me kasaa ne m’asɛnka anyɛ nnipa nyansa adɛfɛdɛfɛsɛm kwane so, na mmom Honhom ne tumi yikyerɛ mu,

english_verse: That way you would have faith because of God's power and not because of human wisdom.
translation_verse: na amma ho gyidie fapem annyina nnipa nyansa so na mmom, ama no agyina Nyankopɔn tumi so.

english_verse: We do use wisdom when speaking to people who are mature in their faith. But it isn't the wisdom of this world or of its rulers, who will soon disappear.
translation_verse: Nso yɛka nyansa ho asɛm kyerɛ bɛ a bɛanyini honhom fam, na ɔyɛ nyansa a ɔnyɛ wiase yi deɛ anaa wiase atumfoɔ a bɛbɛsɛe bɛ he deɛ.

english_verse: We speak of God's hidden and mysterious wisdom that God decided to use for our glory long before the world began.
translation_verse: Na mmom yɛka Nyankopɔn ho ahintasɛm ne nyansasɛm a bɛde asie, deɛ Nyankopɔn ka too hɔ ansa na ɔɔbɛhyɛ wiase ase, sɛ ɔde bɛhyɛ yɛ animuonyam he.

english_verse: The rulers of this world didn't know anything about this wisdom. If they had known about it, they would not have nailed the glorious Lord to a cross.
translation_verse: Wiase yi anyansafoɔ biaa ante yei ase; sɛ bɛtee aseɛ a, nka bɛammɔ animuonyam Wura he asennua mu.

english_verse: But it is just as the Scriptures say, “What God has planned for people who love him is more than eyes have seen or ears have heard. It has never even entered our minds!”
