translation_verse: Nso sɛnea bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Deɛ ani biaa nhuu da, anaa aso ntee da, na nnipa akoma nwenee ho, deɛ Nyankopɔn asiesie ama bɛ a bɛdɔ no,”

english_verse: God's Spirit has shown you everything. His Spirit finds out everything, even what is deep in the mind of God.
translation_verse: nnoɔma yei ne Nyankopɔn nam Honhom he so ada no adi; efiri sɛ Honhom he hwehwɛ nnoɔma nyinaa mu, mpo Nyankopɔn nnoɔma a mu dɔ nso mu.

english_verse: You are the only one who knows what is in your own mind, and God's Spirit is the only one who knows what is in God's mind.
translation_verse: Na hwane ne nim deɛ ɔya nipa mu ka nnipa honhom a ɔya ne mu he ho? Saa nso ne bi ntumi nte Nyankopɔn ase gye sɛ Nyankopɔn Honhom heaa.

english_verse: But God has given us his Spirit. This is why we don't think the same way that the people of this world think. This is also why we can recognize the blessings God has given us.
translation_verse: Na yɛ deɛ, ɔnyɛ wiase honhom ne yɛanya, na mmom honhom a ofiri Nyankopɔn mu, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a yɛbɛte akyɛdeɛ a Nyankopɔn de ama yɛ he ase.

english_verse: Every word we speak was taught to us by God's Spirit, not by human wisdom. And this same Spirit helps us teach spiritual things to spiritual people.
translation_verse: Na yɛka nnoɔma yi ho asɛm, ɔnyɛ deɛ nnipa nyansa kyerɛ, na mmom deɛ Honhom he akyerɛ, de kyerɛkyerɛ honhom nnoɔma ase kyerɛ bɛ a bɛya honhom mu.

english_verse: This is why only someone who has God's Spirit can understand spiritual blessings. Anyone who doesn't have God's Spirit thinks these blessings are foolish.
translation_verse: Bɛ a Honhom he nni bɛ mu he, bennya Nyankopɔn honhom akyɛdeɛ, efiri sɛ ɔyɛ nkwaseasɛm ma bɛ, na bentumi nte aseɛ efiri sɛ saa akyɛdeɛ he mu nhunumu yɛ honhom mu asɛm.

english_verse: People who are guided by the Spirit can make all kinds of judgments, but they cannot be judged by others.
translation_verse: Bɛ a bɛya honhom mu he wɔ adeɛ nyinaa mu nhunumu, nso bɛ deɛ, afoforɔ nni bɛ nnoɔma mu nhunumu.

english_verse: The Scriptures ask, “Has anyone ever known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?” But we understand what Christ is thinking.
translation_verse: Na hwane ne ahu Awurade adwene na wakyerɛ no nea ɔnyɔ? Nso yɛ deɛ, yɛwɔ Kristo adwene.

english_verse: My friends, you are acting like the people of this world. That's why I could not speak to you as spiritual people. You are like babies as far as your faith in Christ is concerned.
translation_verse: Ne saa nti, anuanom, mantumi ne ho ankasa sɛ honhom mufoɔ, na mmom sɛ honam mufoɔ, Kristo mu nkokoa.

english_verse: So I had to treat you like babies and feed you milk. You could not take solid food, and you still cannot,
translation_verse: Nofosuo ne mede maa ho, na mmom ɔnyɛ aduane a mu oduru, efiri sɛ na hontumi nnye, na seeseiaa mpo, hontumi nnye.

english_verse: because you are not yet spiritual. You are jealous and argue with each other. This proves you are not spiritual and you are acting like the people of this world.
translation_verse: Na hoda soaa yɛ honam mufoɔ. Na mprɛ dodoɔ a ahoɔyea ne apereapereɛ da so ya ho mu he, honyɛ honam mufoɔ a hobɔ ho bra sɛnea honam pɛ?

english_verse: Some of you say you follow me, and others claim to follow Apollos. Isn't this how ordinary people behave?
translation_verse: Na sɛ bi ka sɛ, “Meya Paulo afa,” na foforɔ nso ka sɛ, “Meya Apolo afa” a, na ɔnnyɛ nnipasɛm ne hoodi?

english_verse: Apollos and I are merely servants who helped you to have faith. It was the Lord who made it all happen.
translation_verse: Na Apolo ne hwane? Paulo [nso] ne hwane? Bɛyɛ asomfo a honam bɛ so bɛgye diiɛ, sɛnea Awurade de maa biaa.

english_verse: I planted the seeds, Apollos watered them, but God made them sprout and grow.
translation_verse: Meduaeɛ, Apolo guguu so nsuo, na ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn ne maa ne nyiniiɛ.

english_verse: What matters isn't those who planted or watered, but God who made the plants grow.
translation_verse: Ne saa nti, ɔnyɛ nea odua anaa nea ogugu so he ne ayɔ bibi, na mmom ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn a ɔma no nyini he a.

english_verse: The one who plants is just as important as the one who waters. And each one will be paid for what they do.
translation_verse: Nea odua ne nea ogugu so nsuo nyinaa wɔ botaeɛ baako, na biaa benya akatua sɛnea n’adwuma teɛ,

english_verse: Apollos and I work together for God, and you are God's garden and God's building. Only One Foundation
translation_verse: efiri sɛ yɛyɛ Nyankopɔn asomfoɔ a yɛyɔ adwuma bom. Hote sɛ Nyankopɔn afuo, ne ne dane.

english_verse: God treated me with undeserved grace and let me become an expert builder. I laid a foundation on which others have built. But we must each be careful how we build,
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn adom a manya he te sɛ dansifoɔ nyansani a metoo fapem, na bi foforɔ oosi bi asi so. Ɔsɛ sɛ biaa hwɛ yie sɛdeɛ ɔto so fa;

english_verse: Whatever we build on this foundation will be tested by fire on the day of judgment. Then everyone will find out if we have used gold, silver, and precious stones, or wood, hay, and straw.
translation_verse: dansifoɔ biaa adwuma bɛda adi, na Da he bɛda no adi, efiri sɛ gya ne bɛde beyi no adi, na gya he bɛsɔ biaa adwuma a waayɔ ahwɛ.

english_verse: We will be rewarded if our building is left standing.
translation_verse: Sɛ nea bɛasi no fapem he so he gyina a, dansifoɔ he benya akatua.

english_verse: But if it is destroyed by the fire, we will lose everything. Yet we ourselves will be saved, like someone escaping from flames.
translation_verse: Sɛ dansifoɔ bi adwuma hye a, ɔbɛhwere adeɛ; na mmom, ɔno ankasa deɛ bɛbɛgye no nkwa, nso ɔte sɛ deɛ ɔnam gya mu.

english_verse: All of you surely know you are God's temple and his Spirit lives in you.
translation_verse: Honnim sɛ hoyɛ Nyankopɔn asɔredane na Nyankopɔn Honhom te ho mu?

english_verse: Together you are God's holy temple, and God will destroy anyone who destroys his temple.
translation_verse: Sɛ bi sɛe Nyankopɔn asɔredane a, Nyankopɔn bɛsɛe saa nipa he nso, efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn asɔredane yɛ kronkron, na hoaa ne saa asɔredane he.

english_verse: Don't fool yourselves! If any of you think you are wise in the things of this world, you will have to become foolish before you can be truly wise.
translation_verse: Biaa nnnaadaa ne ho. Sɛ bi susu sɛ ɔyɛ nyansafoɔ wiase yi mu a, ɔnyɛ sɛ kwasea na wayɛ nyansafoɔ ampa,

english_verse: This is because God considers the wisdom of this world to be foolish. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God catches the wise when they try to outsmart him.”
translation_verse: efiri sɛ wiase yi mu nyansa yɛ nkwaseasɛm ma Nyankopɔn. Na bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Ɔkye anyansafoɔ bɛ aniteɛ mu”

english_verse: The Scriptures also say, “The Lord knows that the plans made by wise people are useless.”
translation_verse: na bio, “Awurade nim nyansani nsusuiɛ, sɛ ne nyinaa yɛ kwa.”

english_verse: So stop bragging about what anyone has done. Paul and Apollos and Peter all belong to you. In fact, everything is yours, including the world, life, death, the present, and the future. Everything belongs to you,
translation_verse: sɛ Paulo oo, Apolo oo, Kefa oo, wiase oo, nkwa oo, wuo oo, seesei oo anaa daakye oo, ne nyinaa yɛ ho dea,

english_verse: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
translation_verse: na hoyɛ Kristo dea, na Kristo nso yɛ Nyankopɔn dea.

english_verse: Think of us as servants of Christ who have been given the work of explaining God's mysterious ways.
translation_verse: Saa ne yɛ nso, hommu yɛ sɛ Kristo asomfoɔ ne Nyankopɔn ahintasɛm so ahwɛsofoɔ.

english_verse: And since our first duty is to be faithful to the one we work for,
translation_verse: Afei nso, ɔsɛ fiehwɛfoɔ sɛ behu no sɛ ɔyɛ nɔkwafoɔ.

english_verse: it doesn't matter to me if I am judged by you or even by a court of law. In fact, I don't judge myself.
translation_verse: Na me deɛ, ɔyɛ me adeɛ ketewa bi sɛ ho anaa nipa bi hyɛ da bu me atɛne, nso memmu me ho atɛne,

english_verse: I don't know of anything against me, but this doesn't prove I am right. The Lord is my judge.
translation_verse: na mennim bibiaa a otia me, nso ɔno mma menni bem. Ɔyɛ Awurade ne bu me atɛne.

english_verse: So don't judge anyone until the Lord returns. He will show what is hidden in the dark and what is in everyone's heart. Then God will be the one who praises each of us.
translation_verse: Nti mpɛ ntɛm mmu atɛne ansa na berɛ he aso, ansa na Awurade aba, ɔno ne de nnoɔma a ahinta sum mu bɛba hann mu na wayi akoma mu botaeɛ adi, ansa na ɔde biaa abasobɔ ama no.

english_verse: Friends, I have used Apollos and myself as examples to teach you the meaning of the saying, “Follow the rules.” I want you to stop saying one of us is better than the other.
translation_verse: Anuanom, ho nti ne mede asɛm yi totoo me ne Apolo ho kyerɛɛ ho, na hoasua asɛm a ɔdɛ, “Honyɔ bibi ntera nea bɛatwerɛ,” he asekyerɛ afiri yɛ hɔ, na ho mu bi ansɔre ne bi anni asie.

english_verse: What is so special about you? What do you have that you were not given? And if it was given to you, how can you brag?
translation_verse: Na hwane ne hu bibi sononko ya ho mu? Ɛɛne ne wowɔ a wo nsa anka? Na sɛ wo nsa kaeɛ a, mmɛ nti ne hoahoa woho te sɛ ɔnyɛ akyɛdeɛ?

english_verse: Are you already satisfied? Are you now rich? Have you become kings while we are still nobodies? I wish you were kings. Then we could have a share in your kingdom.
translation_verse: Howɔ deɛ hohia nyinaa dada! Hoanya hoho dada! Hoakwati yɛ adi ahene! Nɔkorɛ, sɛ menya a nka hoyɛ ahene, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a yɛ ne ho nyinaa bedi ahene!

english_verse: It seems to me that God has put us apostles in the worst possible place. We are like prisoners on their way to death. Angels and the people of this world just laugh at us.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛ me sɛ Nyankopɔn de yɛ asomafoɔ adi akyire sɛ bɛabua yɛ kumfɔ, efiri sɛ yɛayɛ ahwɛdeɛ ama wiase, abɔfoɔ ne nnipa.

english_verse: Because of Christ we are thought of as fools, but Christ has made you wise. We are weak and hated, but you are powerful and respected.
translation_verse: Kristo nti yɛyɛ nkwaseafoɔ, nso ho deɛ hoyɛ anyansafoɔ Kristo mu. Yɛyɛ mmerɛ, nso ho ɔdene. Howɔ animuonyam, yɛ deɛ bebu yɛ animtia.

english_verse: Even today we go hungry and thirsty and don't have anything to wear except rags. We are mistreated and don't have a place to live.
translation_verse: Ɔde besi saa dɔno yi kɔm ne sukɔm de yɛ, yɛdeda adagya, bɛhwe yɛ na yenni baabi te.

english_verse: We work hard with our own hands, and when people abuse us, we wish them well. When we suffer, we are patient.
translation_verse: Na yɛbrɛ de yɛ ankasa yɛ nsa yɔ adwuma. Bɛdome yɛ a, yehyira bɛ, bɛtaa yɛ a, yegyina;

english_verse: When someone curses us, we answer with kind words. Until now we are thought of as nothing more than the trash and garbage of this world.
translation_verse: bɛsɛe yɛ dini a, yɛka abodwosɛm. Yɛayɛ sɛ wiase afidɛ nnipa nyinaa mu, te sɛ sumina de besi nnɛ.

english_verse: I am not writing to embarrass you. I want to help you, just as parents help their own dear children.
translation_verse: Mentwerɛ yei mfa nhyɛ ho aniwuo, na mmom mede ootu ho fo sɛ adɔfoɔ.

english_verse: Ten thousand people may teach you about Christ, but I am your only father. You became my children when I told you about Christ Jesus,
translation_verse: Na mpo sɛ howɔ ahwɛfoɔ mpem du Kristo mu a, honni agyanom bebree. Nɔkorɛ, menam asɛmpa he so awo ho Kristo Yesu mu.

english_verse: and I want you to be like me.
translation_verse: Afei, metu ho fo sɛ, honsua me.

english_verse: This is why I sent Timothy to you. I love him like a son, and he is a faithful servant of the Lord. Timothy will tell you what I do to follow Christ and how it agrees with what I always teach about Christ in every church.
translation_verse: Yei nti ne mesomaa Timoteo maa no baa honkyɛne, ɔno ne yɛ me ba dɔfoɔ ne nɔkwafoɔ Awurade mu, sɛ ɔnkae ho m’akwane Kristo Yesu mu, sɛdeɛ mekyerɛkyerɛɛ hyiadane biaa mu ya baabiaa he.

english_verse: Some of you think I am not coming for a visit, and so you are bragging.
translation_verse: Nso ɔnam sɛ ho mu binom dwene sɛ memma honkyɛne nti, bɛayɛ ahomasoɔ.

english_verse: But if the Lord lets me come, I will soon be there. Then I will find out if the ones who are doing all this bragging really have any power.
translation_verse: Nso sɛ Awurade pɛ a, ɔnkyɛre mɛba honkyɛne, na mabehu bɛ a bɛama bɛ ho so he tumi farebae, na ɔnyɛ bɛ kasaa nkoaa,

english_verse: God's kingdom isn't just a lot of words. It is power.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn ahennie he nnyina kasaa kɛkɛ so, na mmom ogyina tumi so.

english_verse: What do you want me to do when I arrive? Do you want me to be hard on you or to be kind and gentle?
translation_verse: Deɛ ɔya he ne hopɛ? Memfa abaa memmra honkyɛne, anaa dɔ deɛ ɔya dwoɔ honhom mu?

english_verse: I have heard terrible things about some of you. In fact, you are behaving worse than Gentiles. A man is even sleeping with his own stepmother.
translation_verse: Bɛabɛka akyerɛ me sɛ adwamammɔ ya ho mu, bɛdɛ bi afa ne papa yerɛ; adwammammɔ a ɔte saa a bi nni annyeanniefoo mpo mu!

english_verse: You are proud, when you ought to feel bad enough to chase away anyone who acts like this.
translation_verse: Na hoyɛ ahomasoɔ! Ɔnsɛ sɛ nka hosu? Na nea wayɔ saa he honyi no mfiri ho mu.

english_verse: I am with you only in my thoughts. But in the name of our Lord Jesus I have already judged this man, as though I were with you in person. So when you meet together and the power of the Lord Jesus is with you, I will be there too.
translation_verse: ya yɛ Awurade Yesu dini mu ya deɛ ɔyɔɔ saa he ho. Sɛ hohyia a, na me honhom ne yɛ Awurade Yesu tumi ya hɔ a,

english_verse: You must then hand that man over to Satan. His body will be destroyed, but his spirit will be saved when the Lord Jesus returns.
translation_verse: ɔsɛ sɛ hode saa nipa he hyɛ Satan nsa na ama ne honam he sɛe, na ne kra anya nkwa Awurade da he.

english_verse: Stop being proud! Don't you know how a little yeast can spread through the whole batch of dough?
translation_verse: Ho ahohoahoa he nyɛ ade pa. Honnim sɛ mmɔreka kakraa bi ne tu mmɔre he nyinaa?

english_verse: Get rid of the old yeast! Then you will be like fresh bread made without yeast, and this is what you are. Our Passover lamb is Christ, who has already been sacrificed.
translation_verse: Honyi mmɔreka dadaa he nyinaa sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a hobɛyɛ foforɔ, efiri sɛ mmɔreka biaa nni ho mu, efiri sɛ bɛaya de Kristo a ɔyɛ yɛ twam dwane he abɔ afɔdeɛ.

english_verse: So don't celebrate the festival by being evil and sinful, which is like serving bread made with yeast. Be pure and truthful and celebrate by using bread made without yeast.
translation_verse: Nti homma yɛmmmfa mmɔreka dadaa anaa bɔneyɛ ne amumuyɔ mmɔreka nnhyɛ fa he, na mmom yɛmfa paanoo a mmɔreka nnim a ɔno ne adwenemuteɛ ne nɔkorɛdie knhyɛ.

english_verse: In my other letter I told you not to have anything to do with immoral people.
translation_verse: Metwerɛɛ no nwoma a mede braa ho he mu sɛ, hommmfa ho ho mmɔ adwamammɔfoɔ,

english_verse: But I wasn't talking about the people of this world. You would have to leave this world to get away from everyone who is immoral or greedy or who cheats or worships idols.
translation_verse: nso menkyerɛ sɛ, hontwe ho ho koraa mfiri adwamammɔfoɔ a bɛya wiase, anaa aniberefoɔ ne apoobɔfoɔ, anaa abosonsomfoɔ he ho, ɔno deɛ, nka ɔsɛ sɛ hofiri wiase yi mu kɔ.

english_verse: I was talking about your own people who are immoral or greedy or worship idols or curse others or get drunk or cheat. Don't even eat with them!
translation_verse: Na afei metwerɛ ho sɛ, sɛ bi a ɔfrɛ ne ho gyidini yɛ dwamanfoɔ anaa aniberefoɔ anaa bosonsonni anaa ntɔkwapɛfoɔ anaa nsadweam anaa korɔmfoɔ a, ho ne saa foɔ he mmɔ fekuo mpo, na ho ne no nnidi nso.

english_verse: God judges everyone else. The Scriptures say, “Chase away any of your own people who are evil.”
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn bebu bɛ a bɛnka yɛ ho he atene. “Hontu tirimuɔdenfoɔ he mfiri ho mu.”

english_verse: When one of you has a complaint against another, do you take your complaint to a court of sinners? Or do you take it to God's people?
translation_verse: Sɛ ho mu bi ne yɔnko nya asɛm a, obesi ne bo de asɛm he akɔ asennibea, nipa a ɔntene anim, sene sɛ ɔde bɛkɔ teneneeni anim anaa?

english_verse: Don't you know that God's people will judge the world? And if you are going to judge the world, can't you settle small problems?
translation_verse: Honnim sɛ ahoteefoɔ ne bebu wiase atene? Na sɛ hobebu wiase atene a, ɔmfata ho sɛ hodi nsɛm nketewa anaa?

english_verse: Don't you know we will judge angels? And if this is so, we can surely judge everyday matters.
translation_verse: Honnim sɛ yebebu abɔfoɔ atene? Na sɛ ɔte saa a, daa daa asetena yi mu nnoɔma ne yentumi mmu ho atene anaa?

english_verse: Why do you take everyday complaints to judges who are not respected by the church?
translation_verse: Na sɛ howɔ daa daa asetena yi mu nsɛm a ɔsɛ sɛ hodi a, nti annyeanniefoɔ a benni asafo he mu he ne hode bɛ bɛyɛ atemmuafoɔ?

english_verse: I say this to your shame. Aren't any of you wise enough to act as a judge between one follower and another?
translation_verse: Meka yei de hyɛ wo aniwuo. Nti nyansani baako pɛ mpo nni ho mu a obetumi abu nua ne nua ntam atɛne anaa?

english_verse: Why should one of you take another to be tried by unbelievers?
translation_verse: Mmɛ nti ne nua de nua kɔ asennibea, bɛ a bɛnnye nni anim?

english_verse: When one of you takes another to court, all of you lose. It would be better to let yourselves be cheated and robbed.
translation_verse: Nɔkorɛ, mfomsoɔ anya aba ho mu sɛ, hone ho ho wɔ nsɛm. Mmɛ nti ne homma bɛnyɔ bɔne ntia ho mmom? Ɛɛne nti ne homma bensisi ho mmom?

english_verse: But instead, you cheat and rob other followers.
translation_verse: Nso ho mmom a hoyɛ anuanom he ne hosisi ho ho ho ho.

english_verse: Don't you know that evil people won't have a share in the blessings of God's kingdom? Don't fool yourselves! No one who is immoral or worships idols or is unfaithful in marriage or is a pervert or behaves like a homosexual
translation_verse: Honnim sɛ bɛ a bɛntene he, bennya Nyankopɔn ahennie he? Homma bɛnnaadaa ho! Adwamammɔfoɔ, abosonsomfoɔ, awaresɛefoɔ, mmeema a bɛbɔ ahuhubra, mmeema bɛ ne mmeema da,

english_verse: will share in God's kingdom. Neither will any thief or greedy person or drunkard or anyone who curses and cheats others.
translation_verse: akorɔmfoɔ, aniberefoɔ, nsaborofoɔ, ntɔkwapɛfoɔ, akwamukafoɔ, yei no mu bi a onnya Nyankopɔn ahennie.

english_verse: Some of you used to be like that. But now the name of our Lord Jesus Christ and the power of God's Spirit have washed you and made you holy and acceptable to God. Honor God with Your Body
translation_verse: Saa ne nka ho mu bi teɛ. Na afei bɛnam yɛ Nyankopɔn Honhom so adware ho, ate ho ho, abu ho bem Awurade Yesu Kristo dini mu. Fa wo nipadua hyɛ Nyankopɔn animuonyam

english_verse: Some of you say, “We can do anything we want to.” But I tell you not everything is good for us. So I refuse to let anything have power over me.
translation_verse: “Mewɔ adeɛ nyinaa ho kwane deɛ,” nso ɔnyɛ ne nyinaa ne fata. “Mewɔ adeɛ nyinaa ho kwane deɛ,” nso memma bibiaa mfa me nommum.

english_verse: You also say, “Food is meant for our bodies, and our bodies are meant for food.” But I tell you that God will destroy them both. We are not supposed to do indecent things with our bodies. We are to use them for the Lord who is in charge of our bodies.
translation_verse: “Aduane ya hɔ ma yafunu, na yafunu nso ya hɔ ma aduane dea,” na Nyankopɔn bɛsɛe ne nyinaa. Nipadua nni hɔ ma adwamammɔ, na mmom ɔya hɔ ma Awurade, na Awurade nso ya hɔ ma nipadua he.

english_verse: God will raise us from death by the same power he used when he raised our Lord to life.
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn nyanee Awurade, na ɔnam ne tumi nso so benyane yɛ bi.

english_verse: Don't you know that your bodies are part of the body of Christ? Is it right for me to join part of the body of Christ to a prostitute? No, it isn't!
translation_verse: Honnim sɛ ho nnipadua he yɛ Kristo akwaa? Ɔsɛ sɛ mede Kristo akwaa yɛ adwamammɔ akwaa anaa? Daabi da!

english_verse: Don't you know that a man who does that becomes part of her body? The Scriptures say, “The two of them will be like one person.”
translation_verse: Honnim sɛ deɛ ɔde ne ho abɔ dwamanmɔfoɔ he ne no yɛ baako? Efiri sɛ, bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Na bɛ mmienu bɛyɛ honam baako.”

english_verse: But anyone who is joined to the Lord is one in spirit with him.
translation_verse: Nso deɛ ɔde ne ho abɔ Awurade he ne no yɛ honhom baako.

english_verse: Don't be immoral in matters of sex. That is a sin against your own body in a way no other sin is.
translation_verse: Honnwane adwamammɔ! Na bɔne biaa a nipa yɔ he nni nipadua he, nso deɛ ɔbɔ adwamane he yɔ bɔne de tia nipadua he ankasa.

english_verse: You surely know that your body is a temple where the Holy Spirit lives. The Spirit is in you and is a gift from God. You are no longer your own.
translation_verse: Anaa honnim sɛ ho nipadua yɛ Honhom Kronkron a honya firii Nyankopɔn hɔ a ɔte ho mu he asɔredane, na honne ho ho?

english_verse: God paid a great price for you. So use your body to honor God.
translation_verse: Efiri sɛ ɔtɔɔ ho aboɔdene; nti homfa ho nipadua nhyɛ no animuonyam.

english_verse: Now I will answer the questions you asked in your letter. You asked, “Is it best for people not to marry?”
translation_verse: Na afei hontie nea mewɔ ka fa nsɛm a hotwerɛeɛ he ho sɛ, “Ɔyɛ ma beema sɛ ɔmfa ne ho nka baa.”

english_verse: Well, having your own husband or wife should keep you from doing something immoral.
translation_verse: Nka saa yɛ deɛ, nso adwamammɔ nti, beema biaa nnya ɔnoaa ne yerɛ, na baa biaa nnya ɔnoaa ne kunu.

english_verse: Husbands and wives should be fair with each other about having sex.
translation_verse: Kunu mma ne yerɛ nea ɔsɛ, na yerɛ nso mma kunu saa.

english_verse: A wife belongs to her husband instead of to herself, and a husband belongs to his wife instead of to himself.
translation_verse: Na yerɛ nni ɔnoaa ne nipadua so tumi, na mmom ne kunu ne wɔ so tumi, saa ne beema nso nni ɔnoaa ne nipadua so tumi, na mmom ne yerɛ ne wɔ so tumi.

english_verse: So don't refuse sex to each other, unless you agree not to have sex for a little while, in order to spend time in prayer. Then Satan won't be able to tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
translation_verse: Hommfa ho ho nkame ho ho gye ho mmienu apene so sɛ hobɛtena ase berɛ kakra bi, de anya adaagye ama mpaebɔ, na hoabehyia bio. Honyɔ ne saa na Satan ansɔ ho nhwɛ, ɔnam ahohyɛsoɔ a honni nti.

english_verse: In my opinion that is what should be done, though I don't know of anything the Lord said about this matter.
translation_verse: Nso nea meeka yi yɛ kwanma bi kɛkɛ, na ɔnyɛ nhyɛ so.

english_verse: I wish all of you were like me, but God has given different gifts to each of us.
translation_verse: Menya a nka ho nyinaa hote sɛ me, nso biaa wɔ akyɛdeɛ sononko firi Nyankopɔn hɔ, bi wɔ yei, ne foforɔ nso wɔ yei.

english_verse: My advice for the unmarried and for widows is that it is acceptable for them to remain single, just as I am.
translation_verse: Afei meeka akyerɛ asugyafoɔ ne akunafoɔ sɛ, ɔyɛ ma bɛ sɛ bɛnnware te sɛ me.

english_verse: But if you don't have enough self-control, then go ahead and get married. After all, it is better to marry than to burn with desire.
translation_verse: Na sɛ bentumi nhyɛ bɛ ho so a, hedeɛ bɛnware. Na ɔyɛ sɛ bɛbɛware sene sɛ honam akɔnnɔ beku bɛ.

english_verse: I instruct married couples to stay together, and this is exactly what the Lord himself taught. A wife who leaves her husband
translation_verse: Afei mehyɛ awarefoɔ—ɔnyɛ me na mmom Awurade—sɛ baa nnyae ne kunu

english_verse: I don't know of anything else the Lord said about marriage. All I can do is to give you my own advice. If your wife isn't a follower of the Lord, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce her.
translation_verse: Na bɛ a bɛaka he nso meeka akyerɛ bɛ—me ne meeka na ɔnyɛ Awurade—sɛ: Sɛ gyidini bi ware baa a ɔnyɛ gyidini, na baa he te aseɛ ne no tena a, mma onnyae no.

english_verse: If your husband isn't a follower, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce him.
translation_verse: Na sɛ baa bi ware beema a ɔnyɛ gyidini, na beema he te aseɛ sɛ ɔne no bɛtena a, mma baa he nnyae no,

english_verse: Your husband or wife who isn't a follower is made holy by having you as a partner. This also makes your children holy and keeps them from being unclean in God's sight.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ ɔnam baa gyidini he so bɛma ne kunu a ɔnnye nni he abɛyɛ gyidini, na yerɛ a ɔnnye nni he nso abɛyɛ gyidini, ɔnam ne kunu a ɔgye di he nti. Sɛ ɔnte saa, nka ho mma he yɛ annyeanniefoɔ mma, nso, nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ Nyankopɔn agye bɛ.

english_verse: If your husband or wife isn't a follower of the Lord and decides to divorce you, then you should agree to it. You are no longer bound to that person. After all, God chose you and wants you to live at peace.
translation_verse: Nso sɛ deɛ ɔnnye nni he ka sɛ ɔɔkɔ a mmara biaa nkyekyere gyidini beema anaa gyidini baa he sɛ ɔntena saa awadeɛ he mu. Na Nyankopɔn afrɛ yɛ sɛ hontena asomdwie mu.

english_verse: And besides, how do you know if you will be able to save your husband or wife who isn't a follower? Obeying the Lord at All Times
translation_verse: Yerɛ, wonnim sɛ wo kunu betumi afa wo so anya nkwa? Kunu, wonnim sɛ wo yerɛ betumi afa wo so anya nkwa?

english_verse: In every church I tell the people to stay as they were when the Lord Jesus chose them and God called them to be his own. Now I say the same thing to you.
translation_verse: Nea ɔsɛ sɛ hoyɔ ne sɛ biaa bɛbɔ ne bra sɛdeɛ Awurade de maa no berɛ a Nyankopɔn frɛɛ no sɛ ɔmmɛyɛ gyidini he. Saa ara ne mahyɛ ama asafo ahodoɔ he nyinaa.

english_verse: Being circumcised or uncircumcised isn't really what matters. The important thing is to obey God's commands.
translation_verse: twetiatwa nka hwee, saa ara nso ne sɛ bentwaa bi twetia a ɔno nso nseɛ bibiaa; mmom Nyankopɔn mmara so die ne hia.

english_verse: So don't try to change what you were when God chose you.
translation_verse: Tebea biaa a bɛfrɛ wo mu he, tena mu.

english_verse: Are you a slave? Don't let that bother you. But if you can win your freedom, you should.
translation_verse: Bɛfrɛɛ wo he na woyɛ akoa? Mma no nha wo koraa. Nso sɛ wobetumi ade wo ho nso a, de wo ho.

english_verse: When the Lord chooses slaves, they become his free people. And when he chooses free people, they become slaves of Christ.
translation_verse: Nipa a bɛfrɛɛ no Awurade mu he na ɔyɛ akoa he, waade ne ho a ɔyɛ Awurade dea, saa ara nso ne nea bɛfrɛ no he na ɔde ne ho he nso abɛyɛ akoa ama Kristo.

english_verse: God paid a great price for you. So don't become slaves of anyone else.
translation_verse: Waatɔ wo aboɔdene; nti nyɛ akoa mma nnipa.

english_verse: Stay what you were when God chose you. Unmarried People
translation_verse: Tebea biaa a bɛfrɛɛ ho mu he, anuanom, ho ne Nyankopɔn ntena.

english_verse: I don't know of anything the Lord said about people who have never been married. But I will tell you what I think. And you can trust me, because the Lord has treated me with kindness.
translation_verse: Afei ɔfa mmabunu ho he, Awurade mmaa me ahyɛdeɛ biaa, nso meeka m’adwene sɛ bi a Awurade mmɔborɔhunu nti ɔyɛ nɔkwafoɔ.

english_verse: We are now going through hard times, and I think it is best for you to stay as you are.
translation_verse: Mesusu sɛ, ahohia a ɔɔba he nti, ɔyɛ sɛ wobɛyɛ sɛnea woteeɛ heaa.

english_verse: If you are married, stay married. If you are not married, don't try to get married.
translation_verse: Wobata yerɛ ho anaa? Nhwehwɛ sɛ wobɛde wo ho. Wade wo ho afiri yerɛ ho anaa? Nhwehwɛ yerɛ.

english_verse: It isn't wrong to marry, even if you have never been married before. But those who marry will have a lot of trouble, and I want to protect you from this.
translation_verse: Na sɛ woware a, wonyɔɔ bɔne, na sɛ babunu ware a, ɔnyɔɔ bɔne. Na bɛ a bɛware he fa abrabɔ mu ahohiahiam, na mempɛ sɛ hobɛfa mu bi.

english_verse: My friends, what I mean is that the Lord will soon come, and it won't matter if you are married or not.
translation_verse: Anuanom, nea meeka ne sɛ, esiane sɛ bɛatwa berɛ no so tia nti, ofiri saa berɛ yi, ma bɛ a bɛwɔ yerɛnom nyɛ sɛ benni bi he,

english_verse: It will be all the same if you are crying or laughing, or if you are buying or are completely broke.
translation_verse: na bɛ a beesu nyɛ sɛ bennsu, na bɛ a beedi ahurusie he nso nyɛ sɛ benni ahurusie, na bɛ a bɛɛtɔ nyɛ sɛ bɛnntɔ hwee,

english_verse: It won't make any difference how much good you are getting from this world or how much you like it. This world as we know it is now passing away.
translation_verse: na bɛ a bɛ ne wiase wɔ bibi yɔ he nyɛ sɛ bɛ ne no nni hwee yɔ, efiri sɛ wiase yi ootwam.

english_verse: I want all of you to be free from worry. An unmarried man worries about how to please the Lord.
translation_verse: Mepɛ sɛ hode ho ho firi adwendwene ho. Deɛ ɔnwareɛ he dwene Awurade dwumadie ho, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛsɔ Awurade ani;

english_verse: But a married man has more worries. He must worry about the things of this world, because he wants to please his wife.
translation_verse: nso warefoɔ he dwene daa daa asetena ho, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛsɔ ne yerɛ ani,

english_verse: So he is pulled in two directions. Unmarried women and women who have never been married worry only about pleasing the Lord, and they keep their bodies and minds pure. But a married woman worries about the things of this world, because she wants to please her husband.
translation_verse: na n’adwene yɛ ntanta. Na deɛ ɔnwareɛ anaa babunu dwene Awurade adwuma ho, sɛ ɔbɛyɛ kronkron honam ne honhom mu; nso baa warefoɔ dwene daa daa asetena asɛm ho, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛsɔ ne kunu ani.

english_verse: What I am saying is for your own good—it isn't to limit your freedom. I want to help you to live right and to love the Lord above all else.
translation_verse: Hoaa ho yiedie nti ne meeka yei, ɔnyɛ sɛ ebia mede bibi ɔkyekyere ho, na mmom sɛdeɛ ɔbɛma hoayɔ deɛ ɔfata na hoatumi asom Awurade daa a bibiaa ɔnye ho adwene.

english_verse: But suppose you are engaged to someone old enough to be married, and you want her so much that all you can think about is getting married. Then go ahead and marry. There is nothing wrong with that.
translation_verse: Sɛ bi hu sɛ nneyɔeɛ a ɔda no adi ya n’asiwa a waso awareɛ ho mfata a, na n’akɔnnɔ ano ayɛ dene a, ma bɛnware, na saa nyɛ bɔne.

english_verse: It is perfectly all right to marry, but it is better not to get married at all.
translation_verse: Nti, sɛ bi ware n’asiwa a, ɔnyɔɔ bɔne biaa, saa ara nso ne sɛ bi yɔ n’adwene sɛ ɔnware n’asiwa kosi mmerɛ bi a, ɔno nso nyɔɔ bɔne biaa.

english_verse: A wife should stay married to her husband until he dies. Then she is free to marry again, but only to a man who is a follower of the Lord.
translation_verse: Baa kunu te ase he, mmara kyekyere no. Nso sɛ ne kunu he wu a, otumi ware biaa a ɔpɛ, na mmom ɔsɛ sɛ ɔware Awurade mu.

english_verse: However, I think I am obeying God's Spirit when I say she would be happier to stay single.
translation_verse: Na mesusu sɛ ɔbɛyɛ nhyira sɛ ɔbɛtena ase. Na menim nso sɛ, mewɔ Nyankopɔn honhom.

english_verse: In your letter you asked me about food offered to idols. All of us know something about this subject. But knowledge makes us proud of ourselves, while love makes us helpful to others.
translation_verse: Na afei, nea mepɛ sɛ meka fa afɔdeɛ a bɛbɔ ma abosom ho di: yenim sɛ “yɛ nyinaa wɔ nimdeɛ.” Na saa nimdeɛ he ma ahomasoɔ, na dɔ deɛ, ɔma nkɔsoɔ.

english_verse: In fact, people who think they know so much don't know anything at all.
translation_verse: Na sɛ ɔyɛ bi sɛ onim bibi a, onnya nhuu sɛnea ɔsɛ sɛ ohu;

english_verse: But God has no doubts about who loves him.
translation_verse: nso Nyankopɔn nim bɛ a bɛdɔ no.

english_verse: Even though food is offered to idols, we know that none of the idols in this world are alive. After all, there is only one God.
translation_verse: Nso, ɔfa sɛ bi oodi aduane a bɛde ama abosom ho he, yenim sɛ “bosom nyɛ ade titire biaa,” efiri sɛ “Nyame biaa nni hɔ sɛ Nyankopɔn baako pɛ he.”

english_verse: Many things in heaven and on earth are called gods and lords, but none of them really are gods or lords.
translation_verse: Ewom sɛ nnoɔma bi ya soro anaa asaase so a bɛfrɛ bɛ abosom deɛ— mpo yɛwɔ abosom bebree ne awuranom bebree—

english_verse: We have only one God, and he is the Father. He created everything, and we live for him. Jesus Christ is our only Lord. Everything was made by him, and by him life was given to us.
translation_verse: nso yɛ deɛ, Nyankopɔn baako pɛ ne yenim sɛ ɔya hɔ, ɔno ne Agya he a nnoɔma nyinaa firi ne mu na ɔnam ɔno nti yɛ ya hɔ; saa ara nso ne yɛwɔ Awurade baako, Yesu Kristo a nnoɔma nyinaa nam ne so ba, na yɛ te ase ne mu he.

english_verse: Not everyone knows these things. In fact, many people have grown up with the belief that idols have life in them. So when they eat meat offered to idols, they are bothered by a weak conscience.
translation_verse: Mmom, ɔnyɛ biaa ne wɔ saa nimdeɛ yi. Esiane sɛ binom de bɛ ho ahyɛ abosom mu de besi nnɛ nti, bɛdwene sɛ aduane a bedi he yɛ deɛ bɛde ma abosom; na ɔnam sɛ bɛ ahonim ɔmmerɛ nti, bɛ ho agu fi.

english_verse: But food doesn't bring us any closer to God. We are no worse off if we don't eat, and we are no better off if we do.
translation_verse: “Aduane mma yɛ mmɛne Nyankopɔn.” Yɛanni a yɛntɔ sini, na ne sɛ yedi nso a, yɛmmoro so.

english_verse: Don't cause problems for someone with a weak conscience, just because you have the right to eat anything.
translation_verse: Nso honhwɛ yie sɛ ho faahodie he ɔnyɛ sintidua mma bɛ a bɛyɛ mmerɛ he.

english_verse: You know all this, and so it doesn't bother you to eat in the temple of an idol. But suppose a person with a weak conscience sees you and decides to eat food that has been offered to idols.
translation_verse: Na sɛ bi hu wo a wowɔ nimdeɛ he sɛ wote adidiiɛ abosom dane mu a, ɔmma n’ahonim a ɔmmerɛ he nnya nnyinasoɔ nkodi abosom afɔrebɔdeɛ anaa?

english_verse: Then what you know has destroyed someone Christ died for.
translation_verse: Hedeɛ na ɔnam wo nimdeɛ so na nipa ɔmmerɛ, a ne nti Kristo wuiɛ he bɛyera.

english_verse: When you sin by hurting a follower with a weak conscience, you sin against Christ.
translation_verse: Na sɛ hoyɔ ho nuanom bɔne de sɛe bɛ ahonim a ɔmmerɛ he a, na hooyɔ Kristo bɔne.

english_verse: So if I hurt one of the Lord's followers by what I eat, I will never eat meat as long as I live.
translation_verse: Nti sɛ nam weɛ to me nua sintidua a, hedeɛ mennwe nam koraa na manto me nua sintidua.

english_verse: I am free. I am an apostle. I have seen the Lord Jesus and have led you to have faith in him.
translation_verse: Menne me ho anaa? Menyɛ somafoɔ anaa? Menhuu Yesu yɛ Awurade he anaa? Honnyɛ m’adwuma so aba Awurade mu anaa?

english_verse: Others may think that I am not an apostle, but you are proof that I am an apostle to you.
translation_verse: Sɛ menyɛ somafoɔ mma afoforɔ a, anyɛ hwee he, meyɛ bi ma ho; efiri sɛ ho yɛ me somafoyɛ ho adansedie, Awurade mu.

english_verse: When people question me, I tell them
translation_verse: M’anoyie a meyi kyerɛ bɛ a bɛhwehwɛ me dwumadie mu di:

english_verse: that Barnabas and I have the right to our food and drink.
translation_verse: Yenni ho kwane sɛ yedidi na yɛnom nsuo?

english_verse: We each have the right to marry one of the Lord's followers and to take her along with us, just as the other apostles and the Lord's brothers and Peter do.
translation_verse: Yenni ho kwane sɛ yɛ mu biaa ware gyidini baa ne no nante, sɛdeɛ asomafoɔ ne Awurade mu anuanom ne Kefa yɔ he?

english_verse: Are we the only ones who have to support ourselves by working at another job?
translation_verse: Anaa me ne Barnaba deɛ, yenni ho kwane sɛ yegyae adwuma yɛyɔ pɛ yɛ ano aduane he?

english_verse: Do soldiers pay their own salaries? Don't people who raise grapes eat some of what they grow? Don't shepherds get milk from their own goats?
translation_verse: Sraani hwane ne ɔno ankasa ne tua ne ho ka? Hwane ne yɔ bobeturo na onni mu aduaba he bi? Anaa hwane ne hwɛ mmoa na ɔnnom bɛ nofosuo he bi?

english_verse: I am not saying this on my own authority. The Law of Moses tells us not to muzzle an ox when it is grinding grain. But was God concerned only about an ox?
translation_verse: Na bɛatwerɛ no Mose mmara he mu sɛ, “Nkyekyere nantwie berɛ a ɔɔporo aburo.” Nantwie he ne ho hia Nyankopɔn a nti twerɛnsɛm he ka saa he?

english_verse: No, he wasn't! He was talking about us. This was written in the Scriptures so that all who plow and all who grind the grain will look forward to sharing in the harvest.
translation_verse: Anaa nyɛ yɛ nti ne ɔka saa? Nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, yɛ nti ne ɔtwerɛɛ saa, ɔsɛ deɛ ofuntum asaase he sɛ ofuntum no anidasoɔ mu, na deɛ ɔporo aburo he nso poro no anidasoɔ mu sɛ obenya bi.

english_verse: When we told the message to you, it was like planting spiritual seed. So we have the right to accept material things as our harvest from you.
translation_verse: Sɛ yeduaa honhom nnepa ya ho mu a, ɔboro so sɛ yebetwa honam mu mfasoɔ afiri ho mu anaa?

english_verse: If others have the right to do this, we have an even greater right. But we haven't used this right of ours. We are willing to put up with anything to keep from causing trouble for the message about Christ.
translation_verse: Sɛ nnipa binom wɔ ho kwane sɛ betwa honam mu mfasoɔ firi ho mu a, yenni ho kwane nkyɛne bɛ anaa? Nso ne nyinaa mu he, yɛmfaa saa akwanya yi so mpɛɛ mfasoɔ mfirii hɔ nkyɛne, yɛhyɛ yɛho so sene sɛ yɛde bibi besi Kristo asɛmpa he kwane.

english_verse: Don't you know that people who work in the temple make their living from what is brought to the temple? Don't you know that a person who serves at the altar is given part of what is offered?
translation_verse: Honnim sɛ bɛ a bɛyɔ asɔre he mu adwuma nya bɛ aduane firi asɔrefie hɔ, na bɛ a bɛte afɔrebokyia he ho he nso didi afɔrebokyia he ho bi?

english_verse: In the same way, the Lord wants everyone who preaches the good news to make a living from preaching this message.
translation_verse: Saa kwane heaa so ne Awurade hyɛɛ sɛ, bɛ a bɛka asɛmpa he nso nnya bɛ ano aduane.

english_verse: But I have never used these privileges of mine, and I am not writing this because I want to start now. I would rather die than have someone rob me of the right to take pride in this.
translation_verse: Nso memfaa saa akwannya yi biaa nnii dwuma, na mentwerɛ yei sɛ bɛde bɛyɛ adwuma me fã mu. Mmom, nka mewu sene sɛ bi bɛma nea mede hoahoa me ho he ayɛ kwa!

english_verse: I don't have any reason to brag about preaching the good news. Preaching is something God told me to do, and if I don't do it, I am doomed.
translation_verse: Sɛ meka asɛmpa he a, ɔnsɛ sɛ mede hoahoa me ho, efiri sɛ ɔyɛ ahyɛdeɛ a ɔda me so sɛ meyɔ. Na sɛ manka asɛmpa he a, mennue!

english_verse: If I preach because I want to, I will be paid. But even if I don't want to, it is still something God has sent me to do.
translation_verse: Sɛ meyi me yam yɔ a, mewɔ akatua, sɛ nso meyɔ no amemenemfe mu a, ɔyɛ dɛaa asɛmpa he ho asodie da me so.

english_verse: What pay am I given? It is the chance to preach the good news free of charge and not to use the privileges that are mine because I am a preacher.
translation_verse: Ɔne sɛ berɛ a meeka asɛmpa he, mede asɛmpa he bɛma kwa, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɔ a memfa asɛmpa he ho tumi a mewɔ he nni dwuma kwammɔne so

english_verse: I am not anyone's slave. But I have become a slave to everyone, so I can win as many people as possible.
translation_verse: Ɔyam sɛ mede me ho adeɛ nyinaa mu deɛ, maayɛ me ho akoa ama biaa, na manya bɛ nyinaa.

english_verse: When I am with the Jews, I live like a Jew to win Jews. They are ruled by the Law of Moses, and I am not. But I live by the Law to win them.
translation_verse: Meyɛɛ me ho sɛ Yudani maa Yudafoɔ, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɔ a menya bɛ ama Kristo, meyɛɛ me ho sɛdeɛ mehyɛ mmara ase maa bɛ a bɛhyɛ mmara ase, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɔ a menya bɛ ama Kristo.

english_verse: And when I am with people who are not ruled by the Law, I forget about the Law to win them. Of course, I never really forget about the law of God. In fact, I am ruled by the law of Christ.
translation_verse: Bɛ a benni mmara he meyɛɛ me ho sɛ menni mmara he ase maa bɛ sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɔ a mɛgye bɛ a benni mmara he.

english_verse: When I am with people whose faith is weak, I live as they do to win them. I do everything I can to win everyone I possibly can.
translation_verse: Bɛ a bɛyɛ mmerɛ he meyɛ sɛ maayɛ mmerɛ, sɛdeɛ mɛgye bɛ a bɛyɛ mmerɛ. Meyɛ me ho adeɛ nyinaa ma nipa nyinaa, sɛdeɛ metumi agye binom.

english_verse: I do all this for the good news, because I want to share in its blessings. A Race and a Fight
translation_verse: Asɛmpa he nti ne meyɛ saa, na manya mu nhyira he bi.

english_verse: You know that many runners enter a race, and only one of them wins the prize. So run to win!
translation_verse: Honnim sɛ mmirikatufoɔ nyinaa si akane akansie mu, nso baako pɛ ne nya nkunim abotire he? Saa ara nso ne hontu mmirika sɛdeɛ hobedi nkunim.

english_verse: Athletes work hard to win a crown that cannot last, but we do it for a crown that will last forever.
translation_verse: Agokansiefoɔ hyɛ bɛ ho so nnoɔma nyinaa mu; bɛyɔ saa de nya abotire deɛ ɔsɛe, na yɛdeɛ yebenya deɛ ɔnsɛe.

english_verse: I don't run without a goal. And I don't box by beating my fists in the air.
translation_verse: Nti mentu mmirika kwa, memmɔ akuturuku te sɛ nea meeboro mframa mu kwa;

english_verse: I keep my body under control and make it my slave, so I won't lose out after telling the good news to others.
translation_verse: na mmom mehyɛ me nipadua he dene na ama matumi ahyɛ me ho so, sɛ ɔbɛyɔ a meeka asɛm akyerɛ binom he, ankɔba sɛ bebeyi me totwene.

english_verse: Friends, I want to remind you that all our ancestors walked under the cloud and went through the sea.
translation_verse: Anuanom, mempɛ sɛ mede sie ho sɛ agyanom hyɛɛ mununkum ase, na bɛ nyinaa bɛfaa po he mu,

english_verse: This was like being baptized and becoming followers of Moses.
translation_verse: na bɛbɔɔ bɛ ne Mose nyinaa asu mununkum ne po he mu.

english_verse: All of them also ate the same spiritual food
translation_verse: Na bɛ nyinaa bedii honhom fam aduane baako,

english_verse: and drank the same spiritual drink, which flowed from the spiritual rock that followed them. That rock was Christ.
translation_verse: na bɛ nyinaa bɛnomm honhom fam anonneɛ baako. Na bɛnom firii honhom fam botane a na odi bɛ akyi he mu, na botane he ne Kristo.

english_verse: But most of them did not please God. So they died, and their bodies were scattered all over the desert.
translation_verse: Nso bɛ mu bebree ansɔ Nyankopɔn ani, na ɔtɔre bɛ ase serɛ he so.

english_verse: What happened to them is a warning to keep us from wanting to do the same evil things.
translation_verse: Na nnoɔma yei siiɛ, de yɔɔ nhwɛsodeɛ maa yɛ, sɛdeɛ yɛ kɔno ɔnnɔ bɔne sɛnea bɛ akɔnnɔ maa bɛ yɔeɛ he.

english_verse: They worshiped idols, just as the Scriptures say, “The people sat down to eat and drink. Then they got up to dance around.” So don't worship idols.
translation_verse: Honnyɛ abosonsomfoɔ sɛ bɛ mu binom yɔeɛ he; sɛdeɛ bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Na nkurofoɔ he tenaa ase didiiɛ na bɛ nomeeɛ, na bɛsɔre di agorɔ.”

english_verse: Some of those people did shameful things, and in a single day about
translation_verse: Hommma yɛmmmɔ adwamane sɛ bɛ mu binom bɔeɛ maa bɛ mu mpem aduonu mmeɛnsa tɔɔ da koro he.

english_verse: Some of you say, “We can do whatever we want to!” But I tell you not everything may be good or helpful.
translation_verse: “Yɛwɔ nnoɔma nyinaa ho kwane deɛ,” nso ɔnyɛ ne nyinaa ne mfasoɔ ya so ma yɛ. “Yɛwɔ nnoɔma nyinaa ho kwane deɛ,” nso ɔnyɛ ne nyinaa ne ma nkɔsoɔ.

english_verse: And don't try to test Christ, as some of them did and were later bitten by poisonous snakes.
translation_verse: Hommma yɛnsɔ Kristo nhwɛ, sɛdeɛ bemu bi yɔeɛ he, na awɔ kukuu bɛ he.

english_verse: Don't even grumble, as some of them did and were killed by the destroying angel.
translation_verse: Na honnnwiinwii sɛ binom yɔeɛ na sɛefoɔ he kukuu bɛ he.

english_verse: These things happened to them as a warning to us. All this was written in the Scriptures to teach us who live in these last days.
translation_verse: Nnoɔma yeinom nyinaa too bɛ sɛ ɔbɛyɛ nhwɛsodeɛ, na bɛatwerɛ sɛ ɔbɛyɛ kɔkɔbɔ ama yɛ, yɛ a yɛya awieeɛ berɛ mu yi.

english_verse: Even if you think you can stand up to temptation, be careful not to fall.
translation_verse: Nti nea ɔyɛ no sɛ ogyina hɔ he, ɔnhwɛ na wanhwe ase.

english_verse: You are tempted in the same way that everyone else is tempted. But God can be trusted not to let you be tempted too much, and he will show you how to escape from your temptations.
translation_verse: Nsɔhwɛ biaa nni hɔ a ato ho a bi ntoo nipa bi da. Na Nyankopɔn yɛ nɔkwafoɔ; na ɔmma bɛnsɔ ho nhwɛ ntra deɛ hobetumi, na mmom ho sɔhwɛ berɛ mu he obebie kwane ama ho, ama hoatumi agyina.

english_verse: My friends, you must keep away from idols.
translation_verse: Nti adɔfoɔ, honwane mfiri abosonsom ho.

english_verse: I am speaking to you as people who have enough sense to know what I am talking about.
translation_verse: Mene ho kasa sɛ animdefoɔ. Honhwehwɛ nea meeka yi mu nya mu nhunumuu.

english_verse: When we drink from the cup we ask God to bless, isn't that sharing in the blood of Christ? When we eat the bread we break, isn't that sharing in the body of Christ?
translation_verse: Nhyira kuruwaa a yehyira so he, ɔnyɛ ayɔnkoyɛ a yenya firi Kristo mogyaa he mu anaa? Na paanoo a yebubu he nso, ɔnyɛ ayɔnkoyɛ a yenya firi Kristo nipadua he mu anaa?

english_verse: By sharing in the same loaf of bread, we become one body, even though there are many of us.
translation_verse: Esiane sɛ paanoo he yɛ baako nti, yɛ a yɛyɛ dodoɔ he nso yɛyɛ nipadua baako, efiri sɛ yɛ nyinaa yedi paanoo korɔ he bi.

english_verse: Aren't the people of Israel sharing in the worship when they gather around the altar and eat the sacrifices offered there?
translation_verse: Honhwɛ Israel, ɔnyɛ bɛ a bedi afɔdeɛ aduane he ne bɛ ne afɔrebokyia wɔ ayɔnkofa?

english_verse: Am I saying that either the idols or the food sacrificed to them is anything at all?
translation_verse: Afei mmɛ ne mepɛ sɛ mekyerɛ? Sɛ abosom afɔdeɛ yɛ bibi anaa sɛ bosom yɛ bibi?

english_verse: No, I am not! This food is really sacrificed to demons and not to God. I don't want you to have anything to do with demons.
translation_verse: Daabi, mekyerɛ sɛ afɔdeɛ a bɛbɔ he, bɛbɔ de ma ahonhommɔne na bɛmmɔ mma Nyankopɔn. Memmpɛ sɛ ho ne ahonhommɔne nya ayɔnkofa.

english_verse: You cannot drink from the cup of demons and still drink from the Lord's cup. You cannot eat at the table of demons and still eat at the Lord's table.
translation_verse: Honntumi nnom Nyankopɔn kuruwaa ano na honsane nnom ahonhommɔne kuruwaa nso ano. Hontumi nnidi Awurade pono so na honsane nnidi ahonhommɔne pono nso so.

english_verse: We would make the Lord jealous if we did this. And we are not stronger than the Lord. Always Honor God
translation_verse: Anaa yɛpɛ sɛ yɛma Awurade twe ninkunu? Yɛ ho ɔdene kyɛne no anaa?

english_verse: We should think about others and not about ourselves.
translation_verse: Honnhwehwɛ ho nkoaa ho yiedie, na mmom honhwehwɛ afoforɔ nso deɛ.

english_verse: However, when you buy meat in the market, go ahead and eat it. Keep your conscience clear by not asking where the meat came from.
translation_verse: Honni bibiaa a bɛtɔno no nam dwaso, na hommisabisa ho asɛm, ɔnam ho ahonim a obu ho fɔ nti,

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “The earth and everything in it belong to the Lord.”
translation_verse: na “wiase ne mu nnoɔma deɛ ahyɛ no ma nyinaa yɛ Awurade dea.”

english_verse: If an unbeliever invites you to dinner, and you want to go, then go. Eat whatever you are served. Don't cause a problem for someone's conscience by asking where the food came from.
translation_verse: Na sɛ deɛ ɔnye nni frɛ ho adidie na hopɛ sɛ hokɔ a, deɛ bɛde besi ho anim biaa he honni, na hommmisa ho asɛm, ho ahonim nti.

english_verse: If I give thanks for what I eat, why should anyone accuse me of doing wrong?
translation_verse: Na sɛ mede aseda di a mmɛ nti ne ɔsɛ sɛ bɛkasa tia me ɔnam bibi a mada ho ase ho?

english_verse: When you eat or drink or do anything else, always do it to honor God.
translation_verse: Nti sɛ hodidi oo, honom oo, hoyɛ bibiaa oo, honyɔ ne nyinaa nhyɛ Nyankopɔn animuonyam.

english_verse: Don't cause problems for Jews or Greeks or anyone else who belongs to God's church.
translation_verse: Honnyɔ deɛ ɔbɛto Yudafoɔ anaa Helafoɔ anaa Nyankopɔn asafo he sintidua,

english_verse: I always try to please others instead of myself, in the hope that many of them will be saved.
translation_verse: sɛdeɛ meyɔ sɛ mɛsɔ nnipa nyinaa ani nea meyɛ biaa mu he, ɔnyɛ me nkoaa me yieyɔ nti, na mmom, nnipa nyinaa deɛ nso, sɛdeɛ bebenya nkwa.

english_verse: You must follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ. Rules for Worship
translation_verse: Sɛnea mesua Kristo he, homma ɔnyɛ nhwɛsoɔ mma ho. Abrabɔ a ɔsɛ sɛ mma da no adi asafo he mu

english_verse: I am proud of you, because you always remember me and obey the teachings I gave you.
translation_verse: Na meyi ho ayɛ sɛ hokae me nnoɔma nyinaa mu na hokura amammerɛ he mu sɛdeɛ mede gyaa ho he.

english_verse: Now I want you to know that Christ is the head of all men, and a man is the head of a woman. But God is the head of Christ.
translation_verse: Nso mepɛ sɛ hote aseɛ sɛ Kristo ne beema biaa ti, na baa ti ne ne kunu, na Nyankopɔn ne Kristo ti.

english_verse: This means that any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head brings shame to his head.
translation_verse: Beema biaa ɔɔbɔ mpaeɛ anaa ɔɔhyɛ nkɔm na waakata ne ti so he hyɛ ne ti aniwuo,

english_verse: But any woman who prays or prophesies without something on her head brings shame to her head. In fact, she may as well shave her head.
translation_verse: na baa biaa a ɔbɔ mpaeɛ anaa ɔhyɛ nkɔm, na ɔnkata ne ti so he hyɛ ne ti aniwuo; ɔte sɛ wabɔ tikwa.

english_verse: A woman should wear something on her head. It is a disgrace for a woman to shave her head or cut her hair. But if she refuses to wear something on her head, let her cut off her hair.
translation_verse: Na sɛ baa ankata ne ti so a, hedeɛ ma no mmɔ tikwa; na sɛ ɔyɛ aniwuo ma baa sɛ ɔbɛbɔ tikwa a, hedeɛ ɔnkata ne ti so.

english_verse: Men were created to be like God and to bring honor to God. This means a man should not wear anything on his head. Women were created to bring honor to men.
translation_verse: Na beema deɛ, ɔmfata sɛ ɔkata ne ti so, efiri sɛ ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn subane ne n’animuonyam; na baa nso yɛ beema animuonyam.

english_verse: He wasn't created for her. She was created for him.
translation_verse: Na ɔnyɛ baa nti ne bɛbɔɔ beema, na mmom beema nti ne bɛbɔɔ baa.

english_verse: And so, because of this, and also because of the angels, a woman ought to wear something on her head, as a sign of her authority.
translation_verse: Ne saa nti ɔsɛ sɛ baa kata ne ti so ma no yɛ tumi ho nsɛnkyerɛnne, ɔnam abɔfoɔ he nti.

english_verse: As far as the Lord is concerned, men and women need each other.
translation_verse: Nso Awurade mu he, baa nne ne ho mfiri beema ho na beema nso nne ne ho mfiri baa ho.

english_verse: It is true that the first woman came from a man, but all other men have been given birth by women. Yet God is the one who created everything.
translation_verse: Na sɛnea baa firi beema mu baeɛ he, saa ara nso ne beema nso nam baa so baeɛ; nso adeɛ nyinaa firi Awurade.

english_verse: Ask yourselves if it is proper for a woman to pray without something on her head.
translation_verse: Hoaa hommu atɛne ho mu: Ɔyɛ sɛ baa bɔ mpaeɛ kyerɛ Nyankopɔn berɛ a ɔnkataa ne ti so anaa?

english_verse: But long hair is a beautiful way for a woman to cover her head.
translation_verse: na mmom baa deɛ ogyina ne ti kusuu a, ɔhyɛ no animuonyam? Efiri sɛ bɛde nwi he ama baa he sɛ nkatasoɔ.

english_verse: This is how things are done in all of God's churches, and this is why none of you should argue about what I have said. Rules for the Lord's Supper
translation_verse: Na sɛ bi pɛ sɛ ɔgye yei ho akyinnye a, me deɛ nea mewɔ ka ne sɛ, yɛ deɛ yemmu saa bra he, na Nyankopɔn asafo ahodoɔ he nso nyɔ saa. Asɔre mu mpaepaemu

english_verse: Your worship services do you more harm than good. I am certainly not going to praise you for this.
translation_verse: Nso, berɛ a meema ho ahyɛdeɛ yi he, ɔnyɛ kamfo ne meekamfo ho, efiri sɛ, sɛ hohyiam a ɔnyɛ adepa ne ho yɔ, na mmom adebɔne

english_verse: I am told you can't get along with each other when you worship, and I am sure that some of what I have heard is true.
translation_verse: Na deɛ odi kane he, sɛ hohyia sɛ asafo a, mete sɛ mpaepaemu ya ho mu, na megye di kwane bi so.

english_verse: You are bound to argue with each other, but it is easy to see which of you have God's approval.
translation_verse: Ɔkame ayɛ sɛ, ɔsɛ sɛ nkyekyemu ba ho mu, na ama bɛ a bɛyɛ agyidifoɔ mapa he ada adi ho mu. Awurade adidie asekɔ mu basabasayɔ

english_verse: You even start eating before everyone gets to the meeting, and some of you go hungry, while others get drunk.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ binom di kane di bɛ aduane mee berɛ a kɔm de binom nso, na binom nso abro nsa.

english_verse: The Lord meant that when you eat this bread and drink from this cup, you tell about his death until he comes.
translation_verse: Na da biaa a hobedi paanoo he na hobɛnom kuruwaa yi ano he, hopae mu ka Awurade wuo he kosi sɛ ɔbɛba. Awurade adidie asekyerɛ

english_verse: If you fail to understand that you are the body of the Lord, you will condemn yourselves by the way you eat and drink.
translation_verse: Na biaa a onni na ɔnnom no kwanpa so he, odi nom de atemmuo ba ɔnoaa ne so, efiri sɛ wammu Awurade nipadua he yie.

english_verse: After the meal, Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and said, “This is my blood, and with it God makes his new agreement with you. Drink this and remember me.”
translation_verse: Saa ara nso ne adidie he akyi he, ɔfaa kuruwaa he nso; na ɔkaa sɛ, “Kuruwaa yi yɛ Apam foforɔ a ɔnam me mogyaa so he. Honyɔ yei, da biaa a hobɛnom he, homfa nkae me.”

english_verse: I have already told you what the Lord Jesus did on the night he was betrayed. And it came from the Lord himself. He took some bread in his hands.
translation_verse: Na me deɛ, nea menya firii Awurade hɔ heaa ne mede maa ho, sɛ Awurade Yesu anadwo a beyii no maeɛ he ɔfaa paanoo,

english_verse: Then after he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body, which is given for you. Eat this and remember me.”
translation_verse: na ɔdaa ase he, obuu mu ka sɛ, “Me nnipadua a beebubu ama ho di. Honyɔ yei mfa nkae me.”

english_verse: But if you eat the bread and drink the wine in a way that isn't worthy of the Lord, you sin against his body and blood.
translation_verse: Ne saa nti, deɛ onni paanoo he na ɔnnom Awurade kruwaa he ano kwanpa so he, obedi Awurade nipadua ne ne mogyaa he ho fɔ.

english_verse: This is why you must examine the way you eat and drink.
translation_verse: Ɔsɛ sɛ biaa sɔ ne ho nhwɛ ansa na wadi paanoo he, na wanom kuruwaa he ano bi.

english_verse: This is why many of you are sick and weak and why a lot of others have died.
translation_verse: Yei nti ne ho mu bebree ayɛ mmerɛ, na binom yare, na binom nso awuwu he.

english_verse: If we carefully judge ourselves, we won't be punished.
translation_verse: Na sɛ yetumi hu nipa ko a yɛyɛ a, hedeɛ nka bemmu yɛ atɛne.

english_verse: But when the Lord judges and punishes us, he does it to keep us from being condemned with the rest of the world.
translation_verse: Nso sɛ Awurade bu yɛ atɛne a, ɔtwe yɛaso sɛdeɛ yɛ ne wiase ɔɔnsɛe.

english_verse: My dear friends, you should wait until everyone gets there before you start eating.
translation_verse: Nti me nuanom, hohyiam sɛ hoobedidi a, hontwɛne ho nuanom.

english_verse: If you really are hungry, you can eat at home. Then you won't condemn yourselves when you meet together. After I arrive, I will instruct you about the other matters.
translation_verse: Sɛ kɔm de wo a, didi fie, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɔ a sɛ hohyia mu a, hoammenya afobuo. Na nnoɔma a aka he, me ba a, metoto no pɛpɛɛpɛ.

english_verse: My friends, you asked me about spiritual gifts.
translation_verse: Na deɛ ɔfa honhom fam nsɛm ho deɛ, anuanom, mempɛ sɛ mede bibiaa sie ho.

english_verse: I want you to remember that before you became followers of the Lord, you were led in all the wrong ways by idols that cannot even talk.
translation_verse: Honim sɛ kane he a na hoyɛ abosonsomfoɔ he, homa bɛdaadaa ho de ho kɔɔ abosom a bentumi nkasa hɔ.

english_verse: Now I want you to know that if you are led by God's Spirit, you will say that Jesus is Lord, and you will never curse Jesus.
translation_verse: Nti mepɛ sɛ hote aseɛ sɛ biaa a ɔnam Nyankopɔn honhom mu kasa he nka sɛ, “Yesu yɛ abusudeɛ,” na biaa ntumi nka sɛ “Yesu yɛ Awurade” gye sɛ ɔnam Honhom Kronkron so.

english_verse: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but they all come from the same Spirit.
translation_verse: Afei, adom akyɛdeɛ he gu ahodoɔ, nso Honhom korɔ heaa ne de ma;

english_verse: There are different ways to serve the same Lord,
translation_verse: na som nnwuma he nso gu ahodoɔ nso Awurade baako heaa ne de ma;

english_verse: and we can each do different things. Yet the same God works in all of us and helps us in everything we do.
translation_verse: na dwumadie nso gu ahodoɔ, nso Nyankopɔn baako he a ne di dwuma he nyinaa bɛ nyinaa bɛ mu.

english_verse: The Spirit has given each of us a special way of serving others.
translation_verse: Nso ɔma Honhom he da adi bɛ mu biaa mu na aba mfasoɔ ama bɛ.

english_verse: Some of us can speak with wisdom, while others can speak with knowledge, but these gifts come from the same Spirit.
translation_verse: Na ɔnam Honhom he so ma bi anyansasɛnka, bi nso ɔma no nimdesɛnka sɛdeɛ Honhom korɔ heaa pɛ.

english_verse: To others the Spirit has given great faith or the power to heal the sick
translation_verse: Foforɔ nso ɔma no gyidie Honhom korɔ he a mu, bi nso ɔma no ayaresa adom akyɛdeɛ Honhom baako he a,

english_verse: or the power to work mighty miracles. Some of us are prophets, and some of us recognize when God's Spirit is present. Others can speak different kinds of languages, and still others can tell what these languages mean.
translation_verse: bi nso ɔma no nsɛnkyerɛnne, foforɔ nso nkɔmhyɛ, na foforɔ nso honhom ahodoɔ mu nhunumu, bi nso ɔma no kasa hodoɔ, na bi nso ɔma no kasa hodoɔ nkyerɛaseɛ.

english_verse: But it is the Spirit who does all this and decides which gifts to give to each of us. One Body with Many Parts
translation_verse: Na yeinom nyinaa Honhom korɔ he a ne yɔ, na ɔkyekyɛ ma biaa sɛdeɛ ɔpɛ. Asafo he baakoyɛ

english_verse: Some of us are Jews, and others are Gentiles. Some of us are slaves, and others are free. But God's Spirit baptized each of us and made us part of the body of Christ. Now we each drink from that same Spirit.
translation_verse: Na Honhom baako mu ne bɛbɔɔ yɛ asu kɔɔ nnipadua baako mu, Yudafoɔ oo, Helafoɔ oo, nkoa oo, adehyeɛ oo—na bɛmaa yɛ nyinaa nomm Honhom baako.

english_verse: Our bodies don't have just one part. They have many parts.
translation_verse: Nɔkorɛ, nipadua he nyɛ akwaa baako, na mmom ɔyɛ akwaa bebree.

english_verse: Suppose a foot says, “I'm not a hand, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the foot still belong to the body?
translation_verse: Sɛ nane ka sɛ, “ɔnam sɛ menyɛ nsa nti, menka nnipadua he ho” a, ɔno nkyerɛ sɛ ɔnka nipadua he ho.

english_verse: Or suppose an ear says, “I'm not an eye, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the ear still belong to the body?
translation_verse: Na sɛ aso bɛka sɛ, “ɔnam sɛ menyɛ ani nti, menka nnipadua he ho a,” ɔnkyerɛ sɛ ɔnka nnipadua he ho.

english_verse: If our bodies were only an eye, we couldn't hear a thing. And if they were only an ear, we couldn't smell a thing.
translation_verse: Sɛ nipadua he nyinaa yɛ ani a, nka deɛ yɛde bɛte ya he? Nka nipadua nyinaa yɛ deɛ bɛde te asɛm a, nka deɛ yɛde behua adeɛ ya he?

english_verse: But God has put all parts of our body together in the way that he decided is best.
translation_verse: Na sɛdeɛ ɔteɛ yi, Nyankopɔn hyehyɛ nipadua akwaa he biaa sɛdeɛ ɔpɛ.

english_verse: A body isn't really a body, unless there is more than one part.
translation_verse: Sɛ ne nyinaa yɛ akwaa baako a, nka nipadua he ya he?

english_verse: It takes many parts to make a single body.
translation_verse: Nso sɛdeɛ ɔteɛ he, yɛwɔ akwaa bebree, nso nipadua baako.

english_verse: That's why the eyes cannot say they don't need the hands. That's also why the head cannot say it doesn't need the feet.
translation_verse: Ani ntumi nka nkyerɛ nsa sɛ, “Menhia wo,” na tire nso ntumi nka nkyerɛ nane sɛ, “Menhia wo.”

english_verse: In fact, we cannot get along without the parts of the body that seem to be the weakest.
translation_verse: Mmom, nipadua akwaa a akɔyɛ sɛ ɔmmerɛ he, yentumi nkwati no,

english_verse: We take special care to dress up some parts of our bodies. We are modest about our personal parts,
translation_verse: na yɛde anidie pii hyehyɛ nipadua akwaa a yɛdwene sɛ ne ho nhia papa he, na yebu akwaa a bemmu no he kyɛne so;

english_verse: but we don't have to be modest about other parts. God put our bodies together in such a way that even the parts that seem the least important are valuable.
translation_verse: berɛ a yɛ akwaa a ɔwɔ anidie kɛseɛ he nhia yei. Nso Nyankopɔn ahyehyɛ nipadua he de anidie a ɔkyɛne so ama akwaa a bemmu no he,

english_verse: He did this to make all parts of the body work together smoothly, with each part caring about the others.
translation_verse: na ntawantawadie amma nipadua he mu, na mmom, akwaa he anya tema korɔ amema bɛ ho bɛ ho.

english_verse: If one part of our body hurts, we hurt all over. If one part of our body is honored, the whole body will be happy.
translation_verse: Sɛ akwaa baako hu amane a, bɛ nyinaa bɛ ne no hu amane; sɛ akwaa bi nya anidie a, bɛ nyinaa bɛ ne no ani gye.

english_verse: Together you are the body of Christ. Each one of you is part of his body.
translation_verse: Afei ho yɛ Kristo nipadua na ho yɛ n’akwaa mmaako mmaako.

english_verse: First, God chose some people to be apostles and prophets and teachers for the church. But he also chose some to work miracles or heal the sick or help others or be leaders or speak different kinds of languages.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn de binom asi asafo he mu, deɛ odi kane, Asomafoɔ, deɛ ɔtɔ so mmienu, adiyifoɔ, deɛ ɔtɔ so mmeɛnsa, akyerɛkyerɛfoɔ; ansa na tumideɛyɔ, ansa na ayaresa akyɛdeɛ, mmoa ahodoɔ, akannie ahodoɔ, kasa hodoɔ.

english_verse: Not everyone is an apostle. Not everyone is a prophet. Not everyone is a teacher. Not everyone can work miracles.
translation_verse: Yɛ nyinaa yɛ asomafoɔ anaa? Yɛ nyinaa yɛ adiyifoɔ anaa? Yɛ nyinaa yɛ akyerɛkyerɛfoɔ anaa? Yɛ nyinaa yɔ nsɛnkyerɛnne anaa?

english_verse: Not everyone can heal the sick. Not everyone can speak different kinds of languages. Not everyone can tell what these languages mean.
translation_verse: Yɛ nyinaa wɔ ayaresa akyɛdeɛ anaa? Yɛ nyinaa ka kasa ahodoɔ anaa? Yɛ nyinaa kyerɛ kasa ahodoɔ ase anaa?

english_verse: I want you to desire the best gifts. So I will show you a much better way.
translation_verse: Na hompre mpɛ akyɛdeɛ a ɔkyɛne so he. Na mɛkyerɛ ho kwane a odi mu kyɛne so he.

english_verse: What if I could speak all languages of humans and even of angels? If I did not love others, I would be nothing more than a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
translation_verse: Sɛ mede nnipa ne abɔfoɔ kɛtrɛma kasa na menni dɔ a, mete sɛ twene a ɔyɔ dede anaa akasaa a ɔɔwoso.

english_verse: What if I could prophesy and understand all mysteries and all knowledge? And what if I had faith that moved mountains? I would be nothing, unless I loved others.
translation_verse: Na sɛ mewɔ nkɔmhyɛ tumi, na meyɔ ahintasɛm ne nimdeɛ nyinaa mu nteaseɛ, na mewɔ gyidie nyinaa ma metumi tutu mmepɔ, na menni dɔ a, mensɛ hwee.

english_verse: What if I gave away all that I owned and let myself be burned alive? I would gain nothing, unless I loved others.
translation_verse: Sɛ mede m’ahodeɛ nyinaa kyɛ, na mede me nipadua mma sɛ bɛnhye a, na menni dɔ a, menya ho mfasoɔ biaa.

english_verse: Love is patient and kind, never jealous, boastful, proud, or
translation_verse: Dɔ yɛ abodwokyɛre; dɔ yam yɛ, dɔ nyɛ anibereɛ anaa ahohoahoa, anaa ahomasoɔ

english_verse: rude. Love isn't selfish or quick tempered. It doesn't keep a record of wrongs that others do.
translation_verse: anaa dwɛɛ; ɔnyɔ deɛ ɔnoaa pɛ, onni koko bɔne anaa abufuo;

english_verse: Love rejoices in the truth, but not in evil.
translation_verse: n’ani nnye bɔne ho, na mmom n’ani gye nɔkorɛ ho.

english_verse: Love never fails! Everyone who prophesies will stop, and unknown languages will no longer be spoken. All that we know will be forgotten.
translation_verse: Dɔ to ntwa da. Na nkɔmhyɛ deɛ, obetwam akɔ, kasa ahodoɔ nso bɛgyae; nimdeɛ nso bɛba awieeɛ,

english_verse: We don't know everything, and our prophecies are not complete.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ yɛ nimdeɛ nwie pɛyɛ, na yɛhyɛ nkɔm fa ne fa.

english_verse: But what is perfect will someday appear, and what isn't perfect will then disappear.
translation_verse: Na sɛ nea owie pɛyɛ he ba a, deɛ onwie pɛyɛ he bɛba awieeɛ.

english_verse: When we were children, we thought and reasoned as children do. But when we grew up, we quit our childish ways.
translation_verse: Meyɛ abɔfra he, mekasaa sɛ abɔfra, medwenee sɛ abɔfra, mesusuu sɛ abɔfra; na mebɛyɛɛ panyini he, magyae me mmɔfrasɛm he.

english_verse: Now all we can see of God is like a cloudy picture in a mirror. Later we will see him face to face. We don't know everything, but then we will, just as God completely understands us.
translation_verse: Seesei deɛ, yehu nnoɔma tosee ahwehwem, na daakye deɛ he deɛ, yebehu bibiaa pefee. Seesei menim fa kakra bi; na daakye deɛ, mehu no yie, sɛdeɛ benim me yie he.

english_verse: For now there are faith, hope, and love. But of these three, the greatest is love.
translation_verse: Na afei gyidie, anidasoɔ ne dɔ, nnoɔma mmeɛnsa yi ne ya hɔ, na mu kɛseɛ paa ne dɔ.

english_verse: Love should be your guide. Be eager to have the gifts that come from the Holy Spirit, especially the gift of prophecy.
translation_verse: Honhwehwɛ dɔ akyi kwane na hommɔ honhom akyɛdeɛ ho mmɔdene, na ne titire honhwehwɛ sɛ hobetumi ahyɛ nkɔm.

english_verse: If you speak languages that others don't know, God will understand what you are saying, though no one else will know what you mean. You will be talking about mysteries that only the Spirit understands.
translation_verse: Deɛ ɔka kasa foforɔ bi he nkasa nkyerɛ nnipa, na mmom ɔkasa kyerɛ Nyankopɔn; efiri sɛ biaa nte deɛ ɔka he ase, na ɔnam honhom he so ka ahintasɛm.

english_verse: But when you prophesy, you will be understood, and others will be helped. They will be encouraged and made to feel better.
translation_verse: Kwane bi so he, deɛ ɔhyɛ nkɔm he kasa ma nipa yiedie, afotuo ne awerɛkyekyerɛ.

english_verse: By speaking languages that others don't know, you help only yourself. But by prophesying you help everyone in the church.
translation_verse: Deɛ ɔka kasa foforɔ bi he, ɔka de ma ɔnoaa ne nkɔsoɔ, nso deɛ hyɛ nkɔm he deɛ, ɔhyɛ ma asafo he nyinaa nkɔsoɔ.

english_verse: I am glad for you to speak unknown languages, although I prefer that you would prophesy. In fact, prophesying does much more good than speaking unknown languages, unless someone can help the church by explaining what you mean.
translation_verse: Menya a nka ho nyinaa hoka kasa ahodoɔ, nso deɛ nka mepɛ paa ne sɛ hobɛhyɛ nkɔm. Deɛ hyɛ nkɔm he so kyɛne deɛ ka kasa ahodoɔ, gye sɛ bi kyerɛ aseɛ, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a asafo he benya nkɔsoɔ.

english_verse: My friends, what good would it do, if I came and spoke unknown languages to you and didn't explain what I meant? How would I help you, unless I told you what God had shown me or gave you some knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
translation_verse: Afei, anuanom, sɛ meba ho nkyɛne bɛka kasa ahodoɔ, na mankasa ankyerɛ ho adiyie anaa nimdeɛ anaa nkɔmhyɛ anaa nkyerɛkyerɛ mu a, mmɛ mfasoɔ ne ho benya ne me so?

english_verse: If all musical instruments sounded alike, how would you know the difference between a flute and a harp?
translation_verse: Sɛnea ɔteɛ he, nnoɔma a nkwa nnim wɔ nnyegyeɛ bi; sɛ ɔyɛ atɛntɛnbɛne anaa sankuo, nso sɛ nsonsonoyɛ biaa amma nnyegyeɛ he mu a, ɔbɛyɛ dɛ ne bebehu nea woobɔ ya atɛntɛnbɛne anaa sankuo he so?

english_verse: If a bugle call isn't clear, how would you know to get ready for battle?
translation_verse: Na sɛ totorobɛnto he annyegye yie a, hwane ne besiesie ne ho akɔ sa?

english_verse: This is how it is when you speak unknown languages. If no one can understand what you are talking about, you will only be talking to the wind.
translation_verse: Saa ara nso ne ɔteɛ, sɛ hode ho kɛtrɛma ka nsɛm a nteaseɛ nni mu a, bi bɛyɛ dɛ ate deɛ hooka he ase? Ɔte sɛ hookasa gu mframa mu kwa.

english_verse: But if I don't understand the language that someone is using, we will be like foreigners to each other.
translation_verse: Nti sɛ mente kasaa he ase a, mebu deɛ ɔkasa he sɛ pɔtɔni ne deɛ ɔkasa he nso bu me pɔtɔni,

english_verse: If you really want spiritual gifts, choose the ones that will be most helpful to the church.
translation_verse: Na ho nso, sɛdeɛ ho ani abere ɔɔpɛ honhom akyɛdeɛ he, hommɔ ho ho mmɔdene sɛ hobenya abu so na asafo he anya nkɔsɔ.

english_verse: When we speak languages that others don't know, we should pray for the power to explain what we mean.
translation_verse: Nti deɛ ɔka kasa foforɔ he, ma no mmɔ mpaeɛ nserɛ akyɛdeɛ a bɛde kyerɛ kasa foforɔ ase he nso.

english_verse: For example, if I use an unknown language in my prayers, my spirit prays but my mind is useless.
translation_verse: Na mede kasa foforɔ bɔ mpaeɛ a, me honhom ne bɔ mpaeɛ he, na ɔnyɛ m’adwene ne mede oodi dwuma.

english_verse: Then what should I do? There are times when I should pray with my spirit, and times when I should pray with my mind. Sometimes I should sing with my spirit, and at other times I should sing with my mind.
translation_verse: Sɛ ɔte saa, ɛɛne ne ɔsɛ sɛ meyɔ? Mede honhom he bɛbɔ mpaeɛ, na mede adwene nso abɔ mpaeɛ. Mede honhom bɛto dwom, na mede adwene nso ato dwom.

english_verse: Suppose some strangers are in your worship service, when you are praising God with your spirit. If they don't understand you, how will they know to say, “Amen”?
translation_verse: Sɛ anyɛ saa a, wode honhom hyira a, ɔbɛyɛ dɛ na deɛ ɔnte aseɛ he agye w’aseda he so sɛ “Amen”, bere a onnim deɛ wooka?

english_verse: You may be worshiping God in a wonderful way, but no one else will be helped.
translation_verse: Ewom, wada ase yie deɛ, nso ɔnyɛ foforɔ dene.

english_verse: I thank God that I speak unknown languages more than any of you.
translation_verse: Meda Nyankopɔn ase sɛ meka kasa hodoɔ sene ho nyinaa;

english_verse: But words that make sense can help the church. This is why in church I would rather speak five words that make sense than to speak
translation_verse: Nso asafo he mu deɛ, mɛpɛ sɛ mede m’adwene bɛka nsɛm num de akyerɛkyerɛ binom sene sɛ mɛka nsɛm mpem du kasa foforɔ mu.

english_verse: My friends, stop thinking like children. Think like mature people and be as innocent as tiny babies.
translation_verse: Anuanom, honnyɛ asɛm he nteaseɛ mu mmɔfra; na mmom, honyɛ bɔne mu nkokoa; ne nyinaa mu he, honyɛ nnipa a bɛanyini nsɛm ho nteaseɛ mu.

english_verse: In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I will use strangers who speak unknown languages to talk to my people. They will speak to them in foreign languages, but still my people won't listen to me.”
translation_verse: Mmara he mu he, bɛatwerɛ sɛ, “Mɛfa nnipa a bɛka kasa foforɔ ne ahɔhoɔ ano so ne bɛkasa nnipa yi ho; nso ne nyinaa mu he, bentie me,” Awurade ne ɔɔka.

english_verse: Languages others don't know may mean something to unbelievers, but not to the Lord's followers. Prophecy, on the other hand, is for followers, not for unbelievers.
translation_verse: Ne saa nti, kasa foforɔ nyɛ nsɛnkyerɛnne mma agyidifoɔ, na mmom ɔyɛ nsɛnkyerɛnne ma bɛ a bɛnnye nni, na nkɔmhyɛ ya hɔ ma bɛ a bɛnnye nni na ɔnyɛ bɛ a bɛgye di.

english_verse: Suppose everyone in your worship service started speaking unknown languages, and some outsiders or some unbelievers come in. Won't they think you are crazy?
translation_verse: Na sɛ asafo he nyinaa hyia na sɛ bɛka kasa foforɔ a, na bɛ a bennim anaa abɔntensofoɔ ya hɔ a, bɛnka sɛ hoabobɔ adam?

english_verse: But suppose all of you are prophesying when those unbelievers and outsiders come in. They will realize that they are sinners, and they will want to change their ways because of what you are saying.
translation_verse: Na sɛ ho nyinaa hohyɛ nkɔm na nea ɔnye nni anaa nipa foforɔ bi ba hɔ a, nkɔm a hohyɛ he ma ne tiboa bu no fɔ; na ho bɛma n’ani aba ne ho so.

english_verse: They will tell what is hidden in their hearts. Then they will kneel down and say to God, “We are certain that you are with these people.” Worship Must Be Orderly
translation_verse: Na deɛ ɔnye nni he, sɛ n’ahintasɛm da adi a, ɔkoto de n’anim butu fam, Nyankopɔn anim ne ɔsom no pae mu ka sɛ, ampa Nyankopɔn ne ho ya hɔ.

english_verse: My friends, when you meet to worship, you must do everything for the good of everyone there. That's how it should be when someone sings or teaches or tells what God has said or speaks an unknown language or explains what the language means.
translation_verse: Afei mmɛ ne yɛnyɔ, anuanom? Sɛ hohyia mu a, biaa wɔ nnwom, nkyerɛkyerɛ, adiyie, kasa bi, anaa nkyerɛaseɛ bi. Homma ne nyinaa nyɛ deɛ ɔma nkɔsoɔ.

english_verse: No more than two or three of you should speak unknown languages during the meeting. You must take turns, and someone should always be there to explain what you mean.
translation_verse: Sɛ binom ka kasa foforɔ bi a, homma bemu nnipa mmienu anaa aboro so a mmeɛnsa, ne nka; homma biaa nka no mmaako mmaako, na nipa baako nkyerɛ aseɛ.

english_verse: If no one can explain, you must keep silent in church and speak only to yourself and to God.
translation_verse: Na sɛ biaa nni hɔ a ɔbɛkyerɛ kasa foforɔ he ase a, ma bɛ mmua bɛano asafo he mu, na bɛnkasa nkyerɛ bɛ ho ne Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: Two or three persons may prophesy, and everyone else must listen carefully.
translation_verse: Homma adiyifoɔ mmienu anaa mmeɛnsa nkasa, na nkaeɛ he nkari adiyie he nhwɛ.

english_verse: If someone sitting there receives a message from God, the speaker must stop and let the other person speak.
translation_verse: Sɛ ɔkɔ ba sɛ begu so ɔɔhyɛ nkɔm he, bɛ a bɛtete hɔ he bi nya adiyie a, ma deɛ ɔɔkasa he mmua n’ano.

english_verse: Let only one person speak at a time, then all of you will learn something and be encouraged.
translation_verse: Na ho nyinaa ho betumi ahyɛ nkɔm, baako wie a na baako nso ahyɛ, na ho nyinaa hoasua na ahyɛ ho nkuranee,

english_verse: A prophet should be willing to stop and let someone else speak.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ adiyifoɔ he wɔ tumi ya adiyie adom akyɛdeɛ he so.

english_verse: God wants everything to be done peacefully and in order. When God's people meet in church,
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn nyɛ sakasaka Nyankopɔn na mmom asomdwie Nyankopɔn. (Sɛdeɛ ɔteɛ ahoteefoɔ asafo nyinaa mu he,

english_verse: the women must not be allowed to speak. They must keep quiet and listen, as the Law of Moses teaches.
translation_verse: ɔsɛ sɛ mmaa mmua bɛ ano asafo he mu. Na benni ho kwane sɛ bɛkasa, mmom bɛmmerɛ bɛ ho ase, sɛdeɛ mmara he nso ka he.

english_verse: If there is something they want to know, they can ask their husbands when they get home. It is disgraceful for women to speak in church.
translation_verse: Sɛ bibi ne bɛpɛ sɛ behu a, bemmisa bɛ kununom berɛ a bɛya fie; efiri sɛ ɔyɛ aniwuo ma baa sɛ ɔbɛkasa asafo he mu.

english_verse: God's message did not start with you people, and you are not the only ones it has reached.
translation_verse: Mmɛ di! Ho mu ne Nyankopɔn asɛm firi baeɛ? Anaa ho nkoaa ne hoate Nyankopɔn asɛm.)

english_verse: If you think of yourself as a prophet or a spiritual person, you will know I am writing only what the Lord has commanded.
translation_verse: Sɛ bi ka sɛ ɔyɛ diyifoɔ, anaa ɔwɔ honhom mu tumi a, ɔsɛ sɛ ohu sɛ deɛ meetwerɛ ama ho yi yɛ ahyɛdeɛ firi Nyankopɔn hɔ.

english_verse: So don't pay attention to anyone who ignores what I am writing.
translation_verse: Na sɛ bi anhu yei a, mma biaa nnye onii he ntom.

english_verse: My friends, be eager to prophesy and don't stop anyone from speaking languages that others don't know.
translation_verse: Nti anuanom, hommɔ adiyie ho mmɔdene na honnsi kasa ahodoɔ ka ho kwane;

english_verse: But do everything properly and in order.
translation_verse: Na honyɔ nnoɔma nyinaa fɛfɛɛfɛ, ne kwane so, ne kwane so.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to remember the message I preached and that you believed and trusted.
translation_verse: Anuanom, afei mepɛ sɛ ho hu sɛ, asɛmpa a mepae mu ka kyerɛɛ ho a ho nso hogyeɛ na hogyina mu nso he,

english_verse: You will be saved by this message, if you hold firmly to it. But if you don't, your faith was all for nothing.
translation_verse: sɛ ho sɔ mu yie a, ɔno ne ɔnam so bɛgye ho nkwa— gye sɛ ebia hogye dii kwa deɛ.

english_verse: I told you the most important part of the message exactly as it was told to me. This part is: Christ died for our sins, as the Scriptures say.
translation_verse: Na me deɛ, deɛ ohia pa ara a me nsa kaeɛ a mede hyɛɛ ho nsa: ɔno ne sɛ yɛ bɔne nti ne Kristo wuiɛ, sɛdeɛ twerɛm he ka he

english_verse: He was buried, and three days later he was raised to life, as the Scriptures say.
translation_verse: na besiee no, na nnansa so ɔsɔreeɛ sɛdeɛ twerɛ nsɛm ka he,

english_verse: Christ appeared to Peter, then to the twelve.
translation_verse: na oyii ne ho adi kyerɛɛ Kefa, ɔne dummienu he.

english_verse: After this, he appeared to more than five hundred other followers. Most of them are still alive, but some have died.
translation_verse: Afei oyii ne ho adi kyerɛɛ anuanom bɛboro ahenum berɛ korɔ mu, bemu dodoɔ da so te ase, ɔyam sɛ binom adeda deɛ.

english_verse: He also appeared to James, then to all of the apostles.
translation_verse: Afei oyii ne ho adi kyerɛɛ Yakobo, ɔne asomafoɔ henom.

english_verse: Finally, he appeared to me, even though I am like someone who was born at the wrong time.
translation_verse: Deɛ otwa toɔ he, me ne oyii ne ho adi kyerɛɛ me a me te sɛ deɛ bɛapatu awo no.

english_verse: I am the least important of all the apostles. In fact, I caused so much trouble for God's church that I don't even deserve to be called an apostle.
translation_verse: Na me ne asomafoɔ he mu kaakyire, na mensɛ sɛ bɛfrɛ me somafoɔ mpo, efiri sɛ metaa Nyankopɔn asafo.

english_verse: But God treated me with undeserved grace! He made me what I am, and his grace wasn't wasted. I worked much harder than any of the other apostles, although it was really God's grace at work and not me.
translation_verse: Nso Nyankopɔn adom nti ne mete sɛdeɛ meteɛ yi, na n’adom a ɔya me so he nyɛ kwa. Mmom meyɛ adwuma sene bɛ nyinaa— ɔyam sɛ na ɔnyɛ me deɛ, na mmom sɛdeɛ Nyankopɔn adom a ɔte me mu he teɛ.

english_verse: But it doesn't matter if I preached or if they preached. All of you believed the message just the same. God's People Will Be Raised to Life
translation_verse: Nti sɛ me oo, sɛ bɛ oo, saa ne yɛpae mu kaeɛ, na saa ne hogye diiɛ. Yesu wuo ne ne wusɔreɛ

english_verse: If we preach that Christ was raised from death, how can some of you say the dead will not be raised to life?
