english_verse: The Scriptures say, “If you hear his voice today, don't be stubborn like those who rebelled.”
translation_verse: Sɛdeɛ bɛkaa sɛ, “Nnɛ, sɛ hote ne nne a, hommpirim ho akoma sɛ berɛ a bɛhyɛɛ me abufuo he.”

english_verse: Who were those people that heard God's voice and rebelled? Weren't they the same ones that came out of Egypt with Moses?
translation_verse: Na hefoɔ ne bɛtee asɛm he nso bɛhyɛɛ no abufuo he? Ɔnyɛ bɛ a Mose dii bɛ anim de bɛfirii Misraim he nyinaa?

english_verse: And who did God say would never enter his place of rest? Weren't they the ones that disobeyed him?
translation_verse: Na ɔnyɛ asoɔdenfoɔ he ho ne ɔkaa ntam sɛ bɛnhyɛne n’ahomegyeɛ mu he?

english_verse: We see that those people did not enter the place of rest because they did not have faith.
translation_verse: Nti yɛahu sɛ annyeannie nti ne bɛantumi anhyɛne n’ahomegyeɛ mu.

english_verse: The promise to enter the place of rest is still good, and we must take care that none of you miss out.
translation_verse: Nti, esiane sɛ bɔhyɛ da so ya hɔ sɛ yebetumi ahyɛne n’ahomegyeɛ mu nti, homma yɛnhwɛ yie sɛ ho mu bi ɔnntɔ ho sini daakye;

english_verse: We have heard the message, just as they did. But they failed to believe what they heard, and the message did not do them any good.
translation_verse: na bɛaka asɛmpa he akyerɛ yɛ sɛnea bɛka kyerɛɛ bɛ heaa; nso bɛannya mfasoɔ asɛm a bɛka kyerɛɛ bɛ he mu, efiri sɛ bɛ a bɛteeɛ he amfa gyidie annye.

english_verse: Only people who have faith will enter the place of rest. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God became angry and told the people, ‘You will never enter my place of rest!’ ” God said this, even though everything has been ready from the time of creation.
translation_verse: Na yɛ a yɛagye adi he nya n’ahomegyeɛ he mu kyɛfa, sɛdeɛ Nyankopɔn aka he, “Sɛnea m’abufuo mu mekaa ntam sɛ, ‘Bennya m’ahomegyeɛ mu kyɛfa,’ ” nso owiee ne nnwuma he ansa na ɔɔto wiase fapem.

english_verse: In fact, somewhere the Scriptures say that by the seventh day, God had finished his work, and so he rested.
translation_verse: Na mmom baabi he na ɔɔka da a ɔtɔ so nson he ho asɛm sɛ, “Na da a ɔtɔ so nson he Nyankopɔn home firii ne nnwuma nyinaa mu.”

english_verse: We also read that he later said, “You people will never enter my place of rest!”
translation_verse: Ɔsane kaa no ha nso sɛ, “Bennya m’ahomegyeɛ mu kyɛfa da.”

english_verse: This means that the promise to enter is still good, because those who first heard about it disobeyed and did not enter.
translation_verse: Afei esiane sɛ ɔda so abie kwane ama binom sɛ bennya n’ahomegyeɛ mu kyɛfa na nso asoɔdene amma bɛ a bedii kane nyaa asɛmpa he bɛannya n’ahomegyeɛ mu kyɛfa nti,

english_verse: Much later God told David to make the promise again, just as I have already said, “If you hear his voice today, don't be stubborn!”
translation_verse: ɔsane hyɛɛ da foforɔ berɛ a ɔkaa berɛ tentenntene akyi ya David dwom mu sɛ, “Nnɛ”, he; sɛnea bɛaka dada sɛ, “Nnɛ, sɛ hote ne nne a, hommpirim ho akoma.”

english_verse: If Joshua had really given the people rest, there would not be any need for God to talk about another day of rest.
translation_verse: Na sɛ Yosua maa bɛ ahomegyeɛ a, nka Nyankopɔn ɔɔnka da foforɔ bi ho asɛm.

english_verse: But God has promised us a Sabbath when we will rest, even though it has not yet come.
translation_verse: Ne saa nti, homedadie bi da soaa ya hɔ ma Nyankopɔn nkurofoɔ;

english_verse: On that day God's people will rest from their work, just as God rested from his work.
translation_verse: na bɛ a bɛhyɛne Nyankopɔn ahomegyeɛ mu he home firi bɛ nnwuma ho sɛdeɛ Nyankopɔn nso yɔeɛ he.

english_verse: We should do our best to enter the place of rest, so none of us will disobey and miss going there, as they did.
translation_verse: Nti homma yɛmmɔ mmɔdene nhyɛne saa ahomegyeɛ he mu bi, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a biaa ɔnyɔ asoɔdene korɔ heaa bi nhwe ase sɛdeɛ bɛyɔeɛ he.

english_verse: God's word is alive and powerful! It is sharper than any double-edged sword. His word can cut through our spirits and souls and through our joints and marrow, until it discovers the desires and thoughts of our hearts.
translation_verse: Nɔkwasɛm di, nkwa na ahoɔdene ya Nyankopɔn asɛm mu, ano ɔnam sene sekane anofanu biaa, otumi hwire bibiaa mu koduru sɛ ɔpaepae kra firi honhom ho, akwaa firi kyim ho; na otumi bu adwene ne akoma mu nsusuiɛ atɛne.

english_verse: Nothing is hidden from God! He sees through everything, and we will have to tell him the truth. Jesus Is the Great High Priest
translation_verse: Na abɔdeɛ biaa ntumi nhinta no, ne nyinaa da adagya na so abie a ɔda deɛ yɛ nyinaa bebu nkonta akyerɛ no he anim pefee. Yesu a ɔyɛ yɛ sɔfopanyini

english_verse: We have a great high priest, who has gone into heaven, and he is Jesus the Son of God. This is why we must hold on to what we have said about him.
translation_verse: Esiane sɛ yɛwɔ sɔfopanyini kɛseɛ a waforo akɔ soro, Yesu a ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn Ba he nti, homma yɛnsɔ yɛ gyidie asɛm a yɛpae mu ka he mu,

english_verse: Jesus understands every weakness of ours, because he was tempted in every way that we are. But he did not sin!
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, sɔfopanyini a yɛwɔ he ɔnyɛ nea onni tema mma yɛ ya yɛ mmerɛyɛ mu, na ɔyɛ nea bɛasɔ no ahwɛ akwane nyinaa mu sɛ yɛ, na ne nyinaa mu he wannyɔ bɔne da.

english_verse: So whenever we are in need, we should come bravely before the throne of our merciful God. There we will be treated with undeserved grace, and we will find help.
translation_verse: Saa deɛ a homma yɛmfa akokoɔduro mmra adom ahennwa he anim, na yɛanya mmɔborɔhunu ne adom a ɔbɛboa yɛ berɛ a ɔsɛ mu.

english_verse: Every high priest is appointed to help others by offering gifts and sacrifices to God because of their sins.
translation_verse: Na sɔfopanyini biaa a beyi no firi nnipa mu he, bɛhyɛ no sɔfo ma ɔyɔ Nyankopɔn som adwuma ma nnipa na ɔde bɔne ho ayɛyɛdeɛ ne afɔrebɔdeɛ ama.

english_verse: A high priest has weaknesses of his own, and he feels sorry for foolish and sinful people.
translation_verse: Esiane sɛ mmerɛyɛ atwa ɔno nso ho ahyia nti, otumi ne bɛ a bennim ne bɛ a bɛafom kwane he di no bɔkɔɔ;

english_verse: This is why he must offer sacrifices for his own sins and for the sins of others.
translation_verse: ne saa nti ɔsɛ sɛ ɔbɔ ɔno ankasa ne bɔne ho afɔreɛ ne nnipa he nyinaa deɛ nso.

english_verse: But no one can have the honor of being a high priest simply by wanting to be one. Only God can choose a priest, and God is the one who chose Aaron.
translation_verse: Na bi mfa saa anidie he mma ne ho, gye sɛ deɛ Nyankopɔn afrɛ no te sɛ nea ɔfrɛɛ Aaron he.

english_verse: This is how it was with Christ. He became a high priest, but not just because he wanted the honor of being one. It was God who told him, “You are my Son, because today I have become your Father!”
translation_verse: Saa nso ne Kristo amfa anidie ama ne ho sɛ sɔfopanyini, na mmom deɛ ɔfrɛɛ no he ka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Wo ne me Ba, Nnɛ maayi wo kwane sononko so”;

english_verse: In another place, God says, “You are a priest forever just like Melchizedek.”
translation_verse: sɛdeɛ ɔka no baabi foforɔ sɛ, “Woyɛ sɔfo daa, ya Melkisedek asɔfoyɛ kwane so.”

english_verse: God had the power to save Jesus from death. And while Jesus was on earth, he begged God with loud crying and tears to save him. He truly worshiped God, and God listened to his prayers.
translation_verse: Berɛ a Yesu ya honam mu he, ɔde nteamu ne nisuo ne nkotosrɛ mpaebɔ kɔɔ deɛ obetumi agye no afiri wuom he anim, na ne nyamesuro nti otiee no.

english_verse: Jesus is God's own Son, but still he had to suffer before he could learn what it really means to obey God.
translation_verse: Ɔyam sɛ na ɔyɛ Ba he deɛ, nso osuaa amaneɛ a ohuiɛ he mu setie,

english_verse: Suffering made Jesus perfect, and now he can save forever all who obey him.
translation_verse: na owiee pɛyɛ he, ɔbɛyɛɛ daa nkwa farebae maa bɛ a bɛyɛ setie ma no he nyinaa,

english_verse: This is because God chose him to be a high priest like Melchizedek. Warning against Turning Away
translation_verse: mmerɛ a Nyankopɔn yii no ma no bɛyɛɛ sɔfopanyini ya Melkisedek asɔfoyɛ kwane so. Akyirisane ho kɔkɔbɔ

english_verse: Much more could be said about this subject. But it is hard to explain, and all of you are slow to understand.
translation_verse: Yɛwɔ nsɛm pii ka fa yei ho, nso ɔnam sɛ ho yɛ nyaa nteaseɛ mu nti ɔdene ma yɛ sɛ yɛbɛkyerɛkyerɛ mu.

english_verse: By now you should have been teachers, but once again you need to be taught the simplest things about what God has said. You need milk instead of solid food.
translation_verse: Ɔyam sɛ ɔsɛ sɛ nka saa mmerɛ yi ho tumi kyerɛkyerɛ afoforɔ, nso ho da soaa hia sɛ bɛkyerɛkyerɛ ho Nyankopɔn nsɛm mu mfitiasesɛm he. Ho hia nofosuo, na ɔnyɛ aduane a mu ɔdene;

english_verse: People who live on milk are like babies who don't really know what is right.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ nipa biaa a nofosuo yɛ n’aduane he nyiniiɛ, ne ho nkokwaa tenenee asɛm he mu.

english_verse: Solid food is for mature people who have been trained to know right from wrong.
translation_verse: Na mmom bɛde aduane a mu oduru ma nipa a wayini, deɛ bɛatete no a n’adwene ma no tumi hu papa ne bɔne ntam nsonsonoeɛ.

english_verse: We must try to become mature and start thinking about more than just the basic things we were taught about Christ. We shouldn't need to keep talking about why we ought to turn from deeds that bring death and why we ought to have faith in God.
translation_verse: Nti afei, homma yɛmfa Kristo ho mfitiasesɛm he nto nkyɛne, na yɛmpere nkɔ nkyerɛkyerɛ a ɔfata bɛ a bɛ anyini Kristo mu he, na yɛansane anto fapem a ɔfa yeinom ho bio: nnwuma funu ho adwensakra ne Nyankopɔn mu gyidie,

english_verse: And we shouldn't need to keep teaching about baptisms or about the laying on of hands or about people being raised from death and the future judgment.
translation_verse: nkyerɛkyerɛ a ɔfa asubɔ hodoɔ ho, nsa a bɛde gu nkurofoɔ so, awufoɔ sɔreeɛ, ne daa atemmuo ho.

english_verse: Let's grow up, if God is willing.
translation_verse: Na sɛ Nyankopɔn ma kwane a, saa nnoɔma yi ne yɛbɛyɔ.

english_verse: A field is useful to farmers, if there is enough rain to make good crops grow. In fact, God will bless such a field.
translation_verse: Asaase a nsuo tɔ gu so daa, na ɔsõ aba ma bɛ a bɛyɔ so adwuma he, nya nhyira firi Nyankopɔn hɔ.

english_verse: But land that produces only thornbushes is worthless. It is likely to fall under God's curse, and in the end it will be set on fire.
translation_verse: Na mmom sɛ ofifiri nkasɛe ne hwerɛmo a, onni mfasoɔ biaa na ɔbɛne nnome ho; n’awieeɛ ne sɛ bɛde bɛbɛhye no.

english_verse: My friends, we are talking this way. But we are sure that you are doing those really good things people do when they are being saved.
translation_verse: Adɔfonom, ɔyam sɛ yɛkasa saa kwane yi so deɛ, nso yɛwɔ awerɛhyɛmu sɛ ho fam deɛ nnoɔma pa bi ya hɔ ma ho, nnoɔma a ɔma nkwagyeɛ.

english_verse: God is always fair. He will remember how you helped his people in the past and how you are still helping them. You belong to God, and he won't forget the love you have shown his people.
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn nyɛ deɛ ɔntene; na ommu n’ani ngu nwuma pa a ho yɔ ne dɔ a ɔno nti ho da no adi ya ahoteefoɔ som ho, na hogu so yɔ he ho.

english_verse: We wish each of you would always be eager to show how strong and lasting your hope really is.
translation_verse: Na yɛpɛ sɛ ho mu biaa da mmɔdemmɔ korɔ he adi sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a hobenya anidasoɔ mu ahotɔsoɔ akosi awieeɛ,

english_verse: Then you would never be lazy. You would be following the example of those who had faith and were patient until God kept his promise to them. God's Promise Is Sure
translation_verse: na hoannyɛ nyaa, na mmom hoasua bɛ a bɛnam gyidie ne aboterɛ so nyaa bɔhyɛ he. Nyankopɔn bɔhyɛ a ɔyɛ nɔkorɛ

english_verse: No one is greater than God. So he made a promise in his own name when he said to Abraham,
translation_verse: Mmerɛ a Nyankopɔn hyɛɛ Abraham bɔ he, esiane sɛ na bi nni hɔ a ɔsõ sene ɔno Nyankopɔn a ɔde no bɛka ntam nti, ɔno Nyankopɔn ankasa kaa ne ho ntam,

english_verse: “I, the Lord, will bless you with many descendants!”
translation_verse: ɔkaa sɛ, “Nɔkorɛ di mehyira wo na mama wo ase atrɛ.”

english_verse: Then after Abraham had been very patient, he was given what God had promised.
translation_verse: Nti Abraham de ntoboaseɛ twene saa he, bɔhyɛ he baa mu maa no.

english_verse: When anyone wants to settle an argument, they make a vow by using the name of someone or something greater than themselves.
translation_verse: Ɔyɛ nɔkorɛ sɛ nipa biaa de deɛ ɔsõ kyɛne no ne ka ntam, na ntam hyɛ asɛm biaa mu dene na otwa akyingyegyeɛ biaa so.

english_verse: So when God wanted to prove for certain his promise to his people could not be broken, he made a vow.
translation_verse: Ɔno nti ne mmerɛ a Nyankopɔn yɔɔ n’adwene sɛ ɔbɛkyerɛ bɔhyɛ he ho diadeɛ he pefee ne botaeɛ a ɔnsesa he, ɔde ntam ne dii agyinamu,

english_verse: God cannot tell lies! And so his promises and vows are two things that can never be changed. We have run to God for safety. Now his promises should greatly encourage us to take hold of the hope that is right in front of us.
translation_verse: sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a ɔnam nnoɔma mmienu a ɔnsesa so, sɛ Nyankopɔn ntumi nni torɔ, na yɛ a yɛadwane abɛtoa no he benya nkuranhyɛ a mu ɔdene na yɛasɔ anidasoɔ a ɔda yɛ anim he mu.

english_verse: This hope is like a firm and steady anchor for our souls. In fact, hope reaches behind the curtain and into the most holy place.
translation_verse: Yɛwɔ saa anidasoɔ a otim hɔ pintinn sɛ kra sɛkyɛ, anidasoɔ a ɔhyɛne mu de kosii ntwamutam he mu,

english_verse: Jesus has gone there ahead of us, and he is our high priest forever, just like Melchizedek.
translation_verse: baabi a yɛ nti Yesu adi kane ahyɛne hɔ na wayɛ sɔfopanyini ya daa Sɔfopanyini Melkisedek asɔfoyɛ kwane so.

english_verse: Melchizedek was both king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He was the one who went out and gave Abraham his blessing, when Abraham returned from killing the kings.
translation_verse: Na saa Melkisedek yi, Salem hene, Sorosoro Nyankopɔn sɔfo he, berɛ a Abraham dii ahemfo he so nkunim a ɔɔsane aba he, okohyiaa Abraham hyiraa no;

english_verse: Then Abraham gave him a tenth of everything he had. The meaning of the name Melchizedek is “King of Justice.” But since Salem means “peace,” he is also “King of Peace.”
translation_verse: ɔno ne Abraham maa no nnoɔma nyinaa so ntotosoɔ du du. Melkisedek dini he asekyerɛ a odi kane ne sɛ, “tenenee hene”; afei nso ɔyɛ Salem hene a ne nkyerɛaseɛ ne “asomdwie hene.”

english_verse: We are not told he had a father or mother or ancestors or beginning or end. He is like the Son of God and will be a priest forever.
translation_verse: Twerɛsɛm he anka bibiaa amfa n’agya, ne na, anaa n’awoɔntoatoasoɔ ho, saa nso ne wɔanka bibiaa amfa n’ ahyɛaseɛ ne n’awieeɛ ho, yei ma bɛde no toto Nyankopɔn Ba he ho, na ɔkɔ so di sɔfo daa.

english_verse: Notice how great Melchizedek was! Our famous ancestor Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had taken from his enemies.
translation_verse: Hwɛ di a odi mu! Abraham mpo a ɔyɛ nanapanyini he maa no asadeɛ he so ntotosoɔ du du.

english_verse: The Law teaches that even Abraham's descendants must give a tenth of what they possess. And they are to give this to their own relatives, who are the descendants of Levi and are priests.
translation_verse: Ɔyam sɛ Lewi asefoɔ he yɛ Abraham asefoɔ he bi deɛ, nso ɔnam sɛ benyaa sɔfodwuma he nti mmara he maa bɛ kwane sɛ bɛnnye ntotosoɔ du du mfiri nkorɔfoɔ, mekyerɛ, bɛ nuanom hɔ.

english_verse: Although Melchizedek wasn't a descendant of Levi, Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had. Then Melchizedek blessed Abraham, who had been given God's promise.
translation_verse: Nso saa beema yi a omfiri Lewi abusua he mu he gyee ntotosoɔ du du firii Abraham a ne nsa kaa bɔhyɛ he hɔ; na ohyiraa Abraham.

english_verse: Everyone agrees a person who gives a blessing is greater than the one who receives the blessing.
translation_verse: Akyinnye biaa nni hɔ sɛ nipa kɛseɛ he ne hyira deɛ ɔyɛ kumaa he.

english_verse: Priests are given a tenth of what people earn. But all priests die, except Melchizedek, and the Scriptures teach that he is alive.
translation_verse: Lewi asefoɔ he fam deɛ, ɔyɛ nnipa a bewu ne bɛgye ntotosoɔ du du he; nso Melkisedek fam deɛ, ɔyɛ deɛ a bedi ne ho adanseɛ sɛ ɔte ase daa he ne gye ntotosoɔ dudu he.

english_verse: Levi's descendants are now the ones who receive a tenth from people. We could even say that when Abraham gave Melchizedek a tenth, Levi also gave him a tenth.
translation_verse: Mpo bi bɛka sɛ Lewi ankasa a ɔgye ntotosoɔ du du he nam Abraham so tua ntotosoɔ du du,

english_verse: This is because Levi was born later into the family of Abraham, who gave a tenth to Melchizedek.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, Lewi a ɔbɛyɛɛ Abraham aseni he, berɛ a Abraham hyiaa Melkisedek he, na ɔya Abraham asenem a benya nwoo no. Sɔfo foforɔ a ɔsɛ Melkisedek

english_verse: Even though the Law of Moses says the priests must be descendants of Levi, those priests cannot make anyone perfect. So there needs to be a priest like Melchizedek, rather than one from the priestly family of Aaron.
translation_verse: Na afei sɛ pɛyɛ nam Lewifoɔ sɔfodwuma so a, mmɛ hia ne ohia sɛ bɛkwati Aaron abusua he yi sɔfo foforɔ bi ya Melkisedek asɔfoyɛ kwane so.

english_verse: And when the rules for selecting a priest are changed, the Law must also be changed.
translation_verse: Na sɛ nsakraeɛ ba sɔfodwuma he mu a, hedeɛ na ɔsɛ sɛ nsakraeɛ ba mmara he mu nso.

english_verse: The person we are talking about is our Lord, who came from a tribe that had never had anyone to serve as a priest at the altar.
translation_verse: Na afei deɛ, nea bɛɛka yei fa ne ho he firi abusua foforɔ bi a mu nipa biaa mfirii mu mmɛyɔɔ afɔrebokyia so adwuma da.

english_verse: Everyone knows he came from the tribe of Judah, and Moses never said priests would come from that tribe.
translation_verse: Na ɔda hɔ pefee sɛ yɛ Awurade he firi Yuda abusua mu, na saa abusua he deɛ Mose anka sɔfodie ho hwee amfa bɛ ho.

english_verse: All of this becomes clearer, when someone who is like Melchizedek is appointed to be a priest.
translation_verse: Yei da adi pefee sɛ, berɛ a sɔfo foforɔ sɔre ya Melkisedek asɔfoyɛ kwane so he,

english_verse: That person wasn't appointed because of his ancestors, but because his life can never end.
translation_verse: bɛamfa honam ahyɛdeɛ mmara so, na mmom bɛnam nkwa a ɔnsɛeɛ ahoɔdene so yɔɔ no sɔfo.

english_verse: The Scriptures say about him, “You are a priest forever, just like Melchizedek.”
translation_verse: Na bɛadi ne ho adanseɛ sɛ, “Wo yɛ sɔfo daa, te sɛ Melkisedek asɔfoyɛ.”

english_verse: In this way a weak and useless command was put aside,
translation_verse: Na kwane bi so he, beyii mmara dadaa he sii nkyɛne, efiri sɛ na ɔmerɛ, na na onni mu nso

english_verse: because the Law cannot make anything perfect. At the same time, we are given a much better hope, and it can bring us close to God.
translation_verse: ; na mmom, anidasoɔ a ɔyɛ kyɛne yei so na yɛnam bɛne Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: God himself made a promise when this priest was appointed. But he did not make a promise like this when the other priests were appointed. The promise he made is, “I, the Lord, promise that you will be a priest forever! And I will never change my mind!”
translation_verse: Lewifoɔ he deɛ bɛkwatii ntanka yɛɛ asɔfoɔ, nso Yesu deɛ, deɛ ɔka faa ne ho ne sɛ, “Awurade aka ntam na ɔnsesa n’adwene, ‘Woyɛ sɔfo daa,’ ”

english_verse: This means that Jesus guarantees us a better agreement with God.
translation_verse: yei ma apam a Yesu adi mu agyinamu yɛ papa kyɛne kane apam he.

english_verse: There have been a lot of other priests, and all of them have died.
translation_verse: Bio nso, na ohia sɛ nnipa pii toatoa so yɛ asɔfoɔ, esiane sɛ wuo amma bɛantoa so anni sɔfo he daa nti;

english_verse: But Jesus will never die, and so he will be a priest forever!
translation_verse: nso Yesu deɛ ɔnam sɛ ɔte hɔ daa nti, odi sɔfo afebɔɔ.

english_verse: He is forever able to save the people he leads to God, because he always lives to speak to God for them.
translation_verse: Ne saa nti berɛ biaa obetumi agye bɛ a bɛnam ne so ba Nyankopɔn nkyɛne he, efiri sɛ ɔte ase daa sɛ obedi ama bɛ.

english_verse: Jesus is the high priest we need. He is holy and innocent and faultless, and not at all like us sinners. Jesus is honored above all beings in heaven,
translation_verse: Nti na ohia sɛ yenya sɔfo panyini a ɔte saa, ne ho te, ne ho nni asɛm, nkekaeɛ nni ne ho, bɛate ne ho afiri nnebɔnyɛfoɔ ho, na bɛama no so mmorosoɔ.

english_verse: and he is better than any other high priest. Jesus doesn't need to offer sacrifices each day for his own sins and then for the sins of the people. He offered a sacrifice once for all, when he gave himself.
translation_verse: Na Yesu deɛ ɔnte sɛ asɔfoɔ mpayimfoɔ a bɛaka he, bɛ deɛ ohia sɛ bɛbɔ afɔreɛ daa, deɛ odi kane, bɛbɔ bɛ a bɛ bɔne ho afɔdeɛ, na afei mane he nso bɔne ho afɔdeɛ; nso ɔno deɛ, ɔbɔɔ afɔreɛ he prɛko pɛ mmerɛ a ɔde ne ho maeɛ he.

english_verse: The Law appoints priests who have weaknesses. But God's promise, which came later than the Law, appoints his Son. And he is the perfect high priest forever.
translation_verse: Na mmara he hyɛ nnipa a bɛyɛ mmerɛ asɔfoɔ, na mmom, asɔfodie a ntanka ka ho a ɔbaa mmara he akyi he hyɛ Ba a ɔyɛ pɛ he sɔfo panyini afebɔɔ.

english_verse: What I mean is we have a high priest who sits at the right side of God's great throne in heaven.
translation_verse: Na afei yɛ asɛm a yɛɛka he titire paa ne sɛ, yɛwɔ saa sɔfo panyini yi a ɔte deɛ ɔte Tumi kɛseɛ he ahennwa nifa so ya soro,

english_verse: He also serves as the priest in the most holy place inside the real tent there in heaven. This tent of worship was set up by the Lord, not by humans.
translation_verse: a ɔyɔ kronkronbea ne ntomadane a owie pɛyɛ a ɔnnyɛ nipa ne yɔeɛ, na mmom ɔyɛ Awurade he ankasa ne yɔeɛ he mu adwuma.

english_verse: Since all priests must offer gifts and sacrifices, Christ also needed to have something to offer.
translation_verse: Na bɛ de sɔfo panyini biaa si hɔ sɛ ɔmfa ayɛyɛdeɛ ne afɔrebɔdeɛ mmra afɔrebokyia so; ne saa nti ohia sɛ ɔno nso nya bibi bɔ afɔreɛ.

english_verse: If he were here on earth, he would not be a priest at all, because here the Law appoints other priests to offer sacrifices.
translation_verse: Na afei, sɛ nka ɔya asaase yi so a, nka ɔnyɛ sɔfo koraa, esiane sɛ asɔfoɔ bebree ya hɔ daa a bɛbɔ afɔreɛ sɛnea mmara he ka he.

english_verse: But the tent where they serve is just a copy and a shadow of the real one in heaven. Before Moses made the tent, he was told, “Be sure to make it exactly like the pattern you were shown on the mountain!”
translation_verse: Saa asɔfoɔ he som kronkronbea a ɔyɛ soro deɛ he nhwɛsoɔ ne ne mfonyini; efiri sɛ berɛ a Mose oobesi ntomadane he, Nyankopɔn hyɛɛ no sɛ, “Hwɛ sɛ wobɛyɔ bibiaa sɛdeɛ mfonyini a mede kyerɛɛ wo ya mmepɔ he so he teɛ he.”

english_verse: Now Christ has been appointed to serve as a priest in a much better way, and he has given us much assurance of a better agreement.
translation_verse: Nso Yesu deɛ, waanya asɔfodwuma a ɔkyɛne so koraa, ne saa nti ɔyɛ ntamgyinafoɔ mma apam a ɔkyɛne so he, deɛ ɔnam bɔhyɛ a ɔkyɛne so he so baeɛ he. Apam foforɔ bɔhyɛ

english_verse: If the first agreement with God had been all right, there would not have been any need for another one.
translation_verse: Na sɛ apam a odi kane he ho nni asɛm a, nka ho ankohia sɛ apam a ɔtɔ so mmienu he bɛba.

english_verse: But the Lord found fault with it and said, “I tell you the time will come, when I will make a new agreement with the people of Israel and the people of Judah.
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn nyaa bɛ ho mfomsoɔ mmerɛ a ɔkaa sɛ: “Nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ nna bi ɔɔba, Awurade ne ɔɔka, sɛ me ne Israel fie ne Yuda fie bɛpam apam foforɔ

english_verse: It won't be like the agreement that I made with their ancestors, when I took them by the hand and led them out of Egypt. They broke their agreement with me, and I stopped caring about them!
translation_verse: a ɔnte sɛ apam a me ne bɛ nananom pameɛ he, da he a mesɔɔ bɛ nsa yii bɛ firii Misraim asaase so he; efiri sɛ bɛantena m’apam he mu, ne saa nti na memfa bɛ ho koraa, Awurade ne ɔɔka.

english_verse: “But now I tell the people of Israel this is my new agreement: ‘The time will come when I, the Lord, will write my laws on their minds and hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
translation_verse: Apam a me ne Israel fie bɛpam di, Awurade dɛ nna he akyi he: Mede me mmara bɛhyɛ bɛ adwenem, na matwerɛ no bɛ akoma so, na mɛyɛ bɛ Nyankopɔn, na bɛbɛ yɛ me mane.

english_verse: Not one of them will have to teach another to know me, their Lord.’ “All of them will know me, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Na bɛnkyerɛkyerɛ bɛ ho bɛ ho, saa nso ne bɛnnka nkyerɛ bɛ ho bɛ ho sɛ, ‘Ɔsɛ sɛ wo hu Awurade,’ efiri sɛ bɛ nyinaa behu me, ofiri bɛ mu kumaa paa so de kosi kɛseɛ paa so.

english_verse: I will treat them with kindness, even though they are wicked. I will forget their sins.”
translation_verse: Na mehu bɛ mmɔbɔ bɛ ammumuyɔ ho, na menkae bɛ bɔne bio.”

english_verse: When the Lord talks about a new agreement, he means that the first one is out of date. And anything that is old and useless will soon disappear.
translation_verse: Na ɔɔka “apam foforɔ,” bi ho asɛm he, na ɔɔma kane deɛ he atwam. Na deɛ atwam na ɔɔyɛ dadaa he, ɔnkyɛre biaa ɔbɛyera afiri hɔ.

english_verse: The first promise included rules for worship and a tent for worship here on earth.
translation_verse: Na afei kane apam he wɔ nhyehyɛeɛ ya hɔ ma som ne asaase so kronkronbea.

english_verse: The first part of the tent was called the holy place, and a lampstand, a table, and the sacred loaves of bread were kept there.
translation_verse: Na besii ntomadane he mu pie, mekyerɛ deɛ bedi kane wuram ansa na bɛawura nea ɔtɔ so mmienu he mu. Saa dane yi mu ne na kaneadua, didi-pono ne Kyerɛ-paanoo he ya; hɔ ne bɛfrɛ no Kronkronbea he.

english_verse: The gold altar for burning incense was in this holy place. The gold-covered sacred chest was also there, and inside it were three things. First, there was a gold jar filled with manna. Then there was Aaron's walking stick that sprouted. Finally, there were the flat stones with the Ten Commandments written on them.
translation_verse: Mu ne sikakɔkɔɔ aduhwamhyeɛ bokyea ne apam adaka a bɛde sikakɔkɔɔ adura ho nyinaa, mu ne sika kuruwaa a mana yam ne Aaron poma a ɔfefɛeɛ ne apam aboɔpono he ya;

english_verse: On top of the chest were the glorious creatures with wings opened out above the place of mercy. Now isn't the time to go into detail about these things.
translation_verse: so ne animuonyam Kerubim he kata mpata adwa he so. Saa nnoɔma yi deɛ yentumi nka ho asɛm nkɔ akyiri seesei.

english_verse: But this is how everything was when the priests went each day into the first part of the tent to do their duties.
translation_verse: Sɛ bewie saa nnoɔma yi siesie a, asɔfo he kɔ ntomadane a odi kane he mu daa kɔyɔ bɛsom adwuma;

english_verse: However, only the high priest could go into the second part of the tent, and he went in only once a year. Each time he carried blood to offer for his sins and for any sins that the people had committed without meaning to.
translation_verse: Na deɛ ɔtɔ so mmienu he deɛ, sɔfo panyini he nkoaa ne afe biaa ɔkɔ hɔ prɛko, berɛ a ɔde mogyaa kɔbɔ afɔreɛ, ɔnam ɔnoaa ne mfomsoɔ ne mane he deɛ nti.

english_verse: All of this is the Holy Spirit's way of saying no one could enter the most holy place while the tent was still the place of worship.
translation_verse: Nti Honhom Kronkron da no adi pefee sɛ mmerɛ tentene a na ntomadane a odi kane he da so si hɔ he, na bennya nyii kwane a ɔkɔ kronkronbea he adi.

english_verse: This also has a meaning for today. It shows we cannot make our consciences clear by offering gifts and sacrifices.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛ mfatoho de ma yɛ nnɛ mmerɛ yi. Saa nhyehyɛeɛ mu he bɛde ayɛyɛdeɛ ne afɔdeɛ nso mma a ontumi mma deɛ ɔsom he nni mu n’ahonim fam,

english_verse: These rules are merely about such things as eating and drinking and ceremonies for washing ourselves. And rules about physical things will last only until the time comes to change them for something better.
translation_verse: na mmom, ɔyɛ nhyehyɛeɛ a ɔfa aduane ne anonneɛ ne asubɔ hodoɔ ho, nhyehyɛeɛ a ɔfa nipadua he ho nkoaa kosi sɛ mmerɛ bɛbɛba a bɛtoto nnoɔma nyinaa yie. Kristo asɔfodie a odi mu

english_verse: Christ came as the high priest of the good things that are now here. He also went into a much better tent that wasn't made by humans and that doesn't belong to this world.
translation_verse: Nso Kristo deɛ, berɛ a ɔbaa sɛ nnoɔma pa a aba he ho sɔfo panyini he, na afei ɔnam ntomadane a ɔkyɛne so na odi mu he mu he ,

english_verse: Then Christ went once for all into the most holy place and freed us from sin forever. He did this by offering his own blood instead of the blood of goats and bulls.
translation_verse: ɔhyɛnee Kronkron mu Kronkron hɔ prɛko pɛ. Kristo amfa mmirekyie ne anantwie mogyaa, na mmom ɔde ɔno ankasa ne mogyaa ne nyaa daa gyeɛ maa yɛ.

english_verse: According to the Law of Moses, those people who become unclean are not fit to worship God. Yet they will be considered clean, if they are sprinkled with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a sacrificed calf.
translation_verse: Na sɛ mmirekyie ne anantwinyini mogyaa ne nantwibedeɛ mma nso mogyaa a bɛde petee bɛ a na bɛ ho agu fi ho maa bɛ honam ho teeɛ a,

english_verse: But Christ was sinless, and he offered himself as an eternal and spiritual sacrifice to God. This is why his blood is much more powerful and makes our consciences clear. Now we can serve the living God and no longer do things that lead to death.
translation_verse: ɔbɛyɛ dɛ ne Kristo a ɔnam daa honhom so de ne ho bɔɔ afɔdeɛ, a ho nni dɛm maa Nyankopɔn he mogyaa ontumi nte ho ahonim ho mfiri nnwuma funu nyinaa mu ma ho nkɔsom Nyankopɔn a ɔte ase he? Kristo yɛ Apam foforɔ ntamgyinafoɔ

english_verse: Christ died to rescue those who had sinned and broken the old agreement. Now he brings his chosen ones a new agreement with its guarantee of God's eternal blessings!
translation_verse: Sɛ saa deɛ a, Kristo yɛ apam foforɔ ho ntamgyinafoɔ, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a bɛ a bɛafrɛ bɛ he benya daa agyapadeɛ a bɛahyɛ ho bɔ he, efiri sɛ wuo a owuiɛ he agye bɛ afiri apam a odi kane he mmarato ase.

english_verse: In other words, a will doesn't go into effect as long as the one who made it is still alive.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ wuo akyi ne bɛ de nsamanseɛ di dwuma, mprɛ dodoɔ a nea ɔhyɛeɛ he te ase he deɛ ne nsamanseɛ he ntumi nni dwuma.

english_verse: Blood was also used to put the first agreement into effect.
translation_verse: Yei nti woankwati mogyaa amfa apam a odi kane he ansi hɔ,

english_verse: Moses told the people all the Law said they must do. Then he used red wool and a hyssop plant to sprinkle the people and the book of the Law with the blood of bulls and goats and with water.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ mmerɛ a Mose kaa ahyɛdeɛ biaa ne ahyɛdeɛ ho asɛm kyerɛɛ mane he, sɛdeɛ mmara he teɛ he, ɔfaa nantwie mma ne mmirekyie mogyaa, ne nsuo ne ntoma kɔkɔɔ a sommɛ bɔ ano na ɔde petee nwoma heaa ne mane he nyinaa so,

english_verse: He told the people, “With this blood God makes his agreement with you.”
translation_verse: kaa sɛ, “Yei ne apam he mogyaa a Nyankopɔn ahyɛ ama ho.”

english_verse: Moses also sprinkled blood on the tent and on everything else used in worship.
translation_verse: Na saa kwane heaa so ne ɔde mogyaa he petee ntomadane ne nnoɔma a bɛde yɔ som adwuma he nyinaa ho.

english_verse: The Law says that almost everything must be sprinkled with blood, and no sins can be forgiven unless blood is offered. Christ's Great Sacrifice
translation_verse: Nɔkwasɛm, bɛde mogyaa te bibiaa ho sɛdeɛ mmara he teɛ he, na sɛ mogyaa angu a bɔne fakyɛ nni hɔ. Kristo afɔrebɔ yi bɔne hɔ

english_verse: These things are only copies of what is in heaven, and so they had to be made holy by these ceremonies. But the real things in heaven must be made holy by something better.
translation_verse: Nti ohia sɛ soro nnoɔma he ho nhwɛsoɔ he, bɛde saa amanneɛ yi te ho, nso soro nnoɔma he ankasa hia afɔreɛ a odi mu kyɛne yeinom.

english_verse: This is why Christ did not go into a tent made by humans and was only a copy of the real one. Instead, he went into heaven and is now there with God to help us.
translation_verse: Na Kristo anhyɛne kronkronbea a bɛde nsa ayɔ a ɔyɛ kronkronbea kann he ho nhwɛsoɔ he mu, na mmom ɔhyɛnee soro ankasa sɛ afei ookoyi ne ho adi Nyankopɔn anim ama yɛ.

english_verse: Christ did not have to offer himself many times. He wasn't like a high priest who goes into the most holy place each year to offer the blood of an animal.
translation_verse: Saa ara nso ne wanhyɛne hɔ sɛ ɔde ne ho bɛbɔ afɔdeɛ mprɛ pii, sɛdeɛ sɔfo panyini hyɛne kronkronbea hɔ de mogyaa a ɔnyɛ ɔno ankasa ne mogyaa kɔ bɔ afɔdeɛ afe biaa he;

english_verse: If he had offered himself every year, he would have suffered many times since the creation of the world. But instead, near the end of time he offered himself once and for all, so he could be a sacrifice that does away with sin.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ nka obehu amane da biaa firi wiase ahyɛaseɛ. Nso sɛdeɛ ɔteɛ yi deɛ, waayi ne ho adi prɛko ama biaa kosi wiase awieeɛ sɛ ɔde ɔno ankasa ne ho bɛbɔ afɔreɛ ayi bɔne hɔ.

english_verse: We die only once, and then we are judged.
translation_verse: Na sɛdeɛ ɔda hɔ sɛ nipa bewu prɛko, na wuo he akyi atemmuo ya hɔ he,

english_verse: So Christ died only once to take away the sins of many people. But when he comes again, it will not be to take away sin. He will come to save everyone who is waiting for him.
translation_verse: saa nso ne Kristo a ɔde ne ho bɔɔ afɔreɛ prɛko sɛ ɔbɛsoa nnipa bebree bɔne prɛko he, beyi ne ho adi ne mprenu so a, ɔmmɛyɔ bɔne ho adwuma, na mmom ɔɔbɛgye bɛ a bɛhwɛ no kwane dendenndene he nkwa.

english_verse: The Law of Moses is like a shadow of the good things to come. This shadow isn't the good things themselves, because it cannot free people from sin by the sacrifices offered year after year.
translation_verse: Na esiane sɛ mmara he kura nnoɔma pa a ɔɔba he nsɛsoɔ na mmom onni nnoɔma he ankasa nti, ontumi mfa saa daa daa afɔreɛ a bɛbɔ no afe biaa he so mma bɛ a bɛde bɛ ho to so he nwie pɛyɛ.

english_verse: If there were worshipers who already have their sins washed away and their consciences made clear, there would not be any need to go on offering sacrifices.
translation_verse: Sɛ asɔrefoɔ he afɔreɛ a bɛbɔeɛ he tumi popaa bɛ bɔne na ɔtee bɛ ahonim ho ampa, nti nka bɛannyae afɔrebɔ anaa?

english_verse: When Christ came into the world, he said to God, “Sacrifices and offerings are not what you want, but you have given me my body.
translation_verse: Nti, berɛ a Kristo baa wiase he, ɔkaa sɛ, “Woampɛ afɔrebɔ ne ayɛyɛdeɛ, na mmom nipadua ne woasiesie ama me;

english_verse: No, you are not pleased with animal sacrifices and offerings for sin.”
translation_verse: hyeɛ afɔreɛ ne bɔne afɔreɛ ansɔ w’ani

english_verse: Then Christ said, “And so, my God, I have come to do what you want, as the Scriptures say.”
translation_verse: Na afei mekaa sɛ, ‘Nyankopɔn, hwɛ, maaba sɛ meebɛyɔ deɛ wopɛ, O me Nyankopɔn’ .”

english_verse: The Law teaches that offerings and sacrifices must be made because of sin. But why did Christ mention these things and say that God did not want them?
translation_verse: Mmerɛ a odii kane kaa sɛ, “Afɔrebɔ ne ayɛyɛdeɛ anaa hyeɛ afɔreɛ ne bɔne afɔreɛ ansɔ w’ani he” ,

english_verse: Well, it was to do away with offerings and sacrifices and to replace them. This is what he meant by saying to God, “I have come to do what you want.”
translation_verse: na ɔka kaa ho sɛ, “Hwɛ, maaba sɛ meebɛyɔ deɛ wopɛ.” Oyii deɛ odi kane he hɔ, na ɔde deɛ ɔtɔ so mmienu he besii hɔ.

english_verse: So we are made holy because Christ obeyed God and offered himself once for all.
translation_verse: Na ɔnam Nyankopɔn pɛ so ne yɛ ho ate ɔnam Yesu Kristo nipadua a ɔde bɔɔ afɔre-prɛko he nti.

english_verse: The priests do their work each day, and they keep on offering sacrifices that can never take away sins.
translation_verse: Na sɔfo biaa gyina hɔ sɛ ɔyɔ ne som adwuma da biaa, na ɔtaa bɔ afɔreɛ korɔ heaa a ontumi nyi bɔne hɔ.

english_verse: But Christ offered himself as a sacrifice that is good forever. Now he is sitting at God's right side,
translation_verse: Nso mmerɛ a Kristo bɔɔ afɔreɛ baako maa bɔne afebɔɔ he, “ɔtenaa Nyankopɔn nifa so,”

english_verse: and he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power.
translation_verse: na ɔɔtwɛne “kosi sɛ bɛde n’atamfoɔ bɛyɛ ne nane ase ntiasoɔ.”

english_verse: By his one sacrifice he has forever set free from sin the people he brings to God.
translation_verse: Na ɔnam afɔreɛ baako pɛ so ama bɛ a bɛate bɛ ho nyinaa awie pɛyɛ afebɔɔ.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit also speaks of this by telling us that the Lord said,
translation_verse: Na Honhom Kronkron so di adanseɛ Twerɛsɛm he mu sɛ,

english_verse: “When the time comes, I will make an agreement with them. I will write my laws on their minds and hearts.
translation_verse: “Yei ne apam a me ne bɛ bɛpam he Awurade dɛ: nna he akyi he Mede me mmara bɛhyɛ bɛ akomam, na mɛtwerɛ no bɛ adwenem,”

english_verse: Then I will forget about their sins and no longer remember their evil deeds.”
translation_verse: na ɔsane ka kaa ho sɛ, “Menkae bɛ bɔne ne bɛ mmarato bio.”

english_verse: When sins are forgiven, there is no more need to offer sacrifices. Encouragement and Warning
translation_verse: Baabiaa a yeinom ho fakyɛ ya he, bɔne ho afɔrebɔ nni hɔ bio. Nyankopɔn anim korɔ kwane

english_verse: My friends, the blood of Jesus gives us courage to enter the most holy place
translation_verse: Nti, me nuanom, esiane sɛ yɛnam Yesu mogyaa so anya awerɛhyɛmu a yɛde bɛhyɛne kronkronbea hɔ,

english_verse: by a new way that leads to life! And this way takes us through the curtain that is Christ himself.
translation_verse: na ɔnam kwane foforɔ a nkwa yam so abie ntomadane he ama yɛ yi ,

english_verse: We have a great high priest who is in charge of God's house.
translation_verse: na yɛwɔ Nyankopɔn fie so sɔfopanyini yi nti,

english_verse: So let's come near God with pure hearts and a confidence that comes from having faith. Let's keep our hearts pure, our consciences free from evil, and our bodies washed with clean water.
translation_verse: homma yɛmfa akoma a mu yɛ fann a gyidie mu ahotɔsoɔ ahyɛ no ma, akoma a bɛapete ho ate ho afiri ahonim bɔne ho ne yɛ nipadua a bɛde nsuo a ho te adware he ntwe mmɛne no.

english_verse: We must hold tightly to the hope we say is ours. After all, we can trust the one who made the agreement with us.
translation_verse: Homma yɛnhimhim na mmom yenkura yɛ anidasoɔ asɛm a yɛka he mu dene, efiri sɛ deɛ waahyɛ bɔ he yɛ nɔkwafoɔ.

english_verse: We should keep on encouraging each other to be thoughtful and to do helpful things.
translation_verse: Na homma yɛnhwɛ sɛ yɛbɛkanyane yɛ ho yɛ ho na yɛadodɔ yɛ ho yɛ ho; na yɛmfa nnwuma pa nsom yɛ ho yɛ ho,

english_verse: Some people have given up the habit of meeting for worship, but we must not do that. We should keep on encouraging each other, especially since you know that the day of the Lord's coming is getting closer.
translation_verse: hommma yennyae yɛ ho yɛ ho nhyiamu, sɛdeɛ binom agyae bɛ ho bɛ ho nhyiamu he, na mmom homma yɛnhyehyɛ yɛ ho yɛ ho nkurane, ne titire mmerɛ a yehu sɛ Da he abɛne.

english_verse: No sacrifices can be made for people who decide to sin after they find out about the truth.
translation_verse: Na sɛ yɛboapa kɔ so yɔ bɔne mmerɛ a yɛanya nɔkorɛ he ho nimdeɛ a, bɔne ho afɔrebɔ foforɔ biaa nni hɔ bio,

english_verse: They are God's enemies, and all they can look forward to is a terrible judgment and a furious fire.
translation_verse: na mmom atemmuo a ohu akwanhwɛ, ne abufuo gya a ɔbɛhye Nyankopɔn atamfoɔ pasaa he ne ya hɔ ma yɛ.

english_verse: If two or more witnesses accused someone of breaking the Law of Moses, that person could be put to death.
translation_verse: Nipa biaa a ɔto Mose mmara he gyina adansefoɔ mmienu anaa mmeɛnsa adanseɛ wu berɛ a onnya mmɔborɔhunu biaa.

english_verse: But it is much worse to dishonor God's Son and to disgrace the blood of the promise that made us holy. And it is just as bad to insult the Holy Spirit, who shows us mercy.
translation_verse: Mmɛ asotwe koraa ne ɔboro so a hosusu sɛ ɔfata sɛ bɛde bɛtwe bɛ a bɛde Nyankopɔn Ba he anim atwitwi fam na bɛabu apam mogyaa a bɛde tee bɛ ho he animtia, na bɛasopa adom Honhom he?

english_verse: We know that God has said he will punish and take revenge. We also know that the Scriptures say the Lord will judge his people.
translation_verse: Na yenim deɛ ɔkaa sɛ “Mene metɔ werɛ, metua ka.” Na ɔkaa bio sɛ, “Awurade bebu ne mane atɛne.”

english_verse: It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God!
translation_verse: Nti ohu ma bi sɛ ɔbɛtɔ Nyankopɔn teasefoɔ he abufuom.

english_verse: Don't forget all the hard times you went through when you first received the light.
translation_verse: Nso honkae kane nna he a hohuu hann he, sɛ hode animia gyinaeɛ, kõ dendene amanehunu mu,

english_verse: Sometimes you were abused and mistreated in public, and at other times you shared in the sufferings of others.
translation_verse: maa bɛbɔɔ ho adapaa na bɛtaa ho badwam, na da bi nso ho ne bɛ a bɛfaa saa tebea he mu he nyaa ayɔnkofa.

english_verse: You were kind to people in jail. And you gladly let your possessions be taken away, because you knew you had something better, something that would last forever.
translation_verse: Na honyaa tema maa nneduafoɔ, na hode anigyeɛ penee so maa bɛfom ho agyapadeɛ, efiri sɛ honim sɛ ho ankasa howɔ agyapadeɛ a odi mu, na ɔbɛtena hɔ daa.

english_verse: Keep on being brave! It will bring you great rewards.
translation_verse: Nti honnto ho nnamyɛ a ɔma akatua kɛseɛ he ntwene;

english_verse: Learn to be patient, so you will please God and be given what he has promised.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ ho hia boasetɔ, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a sɛ hoyɔ deɛ Nyankopɔn pɛ wie a, ho nsa bɛka deɛ wahyɛ ho bɔ he.

english_verse: As the Scriptures say, “God is coming soon! It won't be very long.
translation_verse: Na “aka kakraa bi, deɛ ɔɔba he bɛba, na ɔnkyɛre koraa;

english_verse: The people God accepts will live because of their faith. But he isn't pleased with anyone who turns back.”
translation_verse: Na deɛ ɔtene he firi gyidie mu benya nkwa, na me kra ani nsɔ deɛ ɔtwe ne ho sane akyi he.”

english_verse: We are not like those people who turn back and get destroyed. We will keep on having faith until we are saved.
translation_verse: Nso yɛ deɛ, yɛnnka bɛ a bɛtwe bɛ ho sane akyi na bɛayera he ho, na mmom yɛka bɛ a bɛwɔ gyidie na bɛanya nkwa he ho.

english_verse: Faith makes us sure of what we hope for and gives us proof of what we cannot see.
translation_verse: Na afei, gyidie ne nnoɔma a yɛ ani da so ho awerɛhyemu a osi pi, ne nnoɔma a yenhu ho adansegyeɛ.

english_verse: It was their faith that made our ancestors pleasing to God.
translation_verse: Ɔnam yei so ne maa bedii tetefoɔ he ho adanseɛ.

english_verse: Because of our faith, we know that the world was made at God's command. We also know that what can be seen was made out of what cannot be seen.
translation_verse: Gyidie ne ma yɛ te aseɛ sɛ Nyankopɔn nam n’asɛm so ne bɔɔ nnoɔma nyinaa, na nnoɔma yehu yi firi deɛ onni hɔ mu ne baeɛ. Habel, Henok ne Noah ho nhwɛsoɔ

english_verse: Because Abel had faith, he offered God a better sacrifice than Cain did. God was pleased with him and his gift, and even though Abel is now dead, his faith still speaks for him.
translation_verse: Gyidie ne Habel de bɔɔ afɔreɛ maa Nyankopɔn a na ɔsɔ ani kyɛnee Kain deɛ he. Ɔnam yei so maa bebuu Habel sɛ ɔtene, na Nyankopɔn dii n’afɔreɛ he ho adanseɛ. Ɔyam sɛ Habel awu deɛ, nso ɔnam ne gyidie he so da so kasa kyerɛ yɛ.

english_verse: Enoch had faith and did not die. He pleased God, and God took him up to heaven. This is why his body was never found.
translation_verse: Gyidie nti ne Nyankopɔn faa Henok kɔeɛ a wanhu wuo; na “bɛanhu no bio, efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn faa no kɔeɛ.” Na ansa na ɔɔbɛfa no akɔ he, bedii ne ho adanseɛ sɛ “ɔsɔ Nyankopɔn ani.”

english_verse: But without faith no one can please God. We must believe that God is real and rewards everyone who searches for him.
translation_verse: Na sɛ gyidie nni hɔ a, biaa ntumi nsɔ Nyankopɔn ani, efiri sɛ ɔsɛ sɛ deɛ ɔba ne nkyɛne biaa gye di sɛ ɔya hɔ na ɔyɛ bɛ a bɛhwehwɛ n’akyi kwane he katuafoɔ.

english_verse: Because Noah had faith, he was warned about something that had not yet happened. He obeyed and built a boat that saved him and his family. In this way the people of the world were judged, and Noah was given the blessings that come to everyone who pleases God.
translation_verse: Gyidie so ne Noah, mmerɛ a Nyankopɔn bɔɔ no kɔkɔ faa nnoɔma a obesi ho he, ɔde anidie gyee kɔkɔbɔ he too mu na ɔyɔɔ adaka de gyee ne fiefoɔ nkwa; Noah nam saa so buu wiase fɔ, na ɔnam ne gyidie so bɛyɛɛ tenenee adedifoɔ. Abraham gyidie

english_verse: Abraham had faith and obeyed God. He was told to go to the land that God had said would be his, and he left for a country he had never seen.
translation_verse: Gyidie nti ne bɛfrɛɛ Abraham sɛ ontu nkɔ baabi a daakye bɛde bɛ ma no sɛ n’agyapadeɛ he, ɔyɔɔ setie kɔeɛ berɛ a na onnim baabi a na ɔɔkɔ he.

english_verse: Because Abraham had faith, he lived as a stranger in the promised land. He lived there in a tent, and so did Isaac and Jacob, who were later given the same promise.
translation_verse: Abraham nam gyidie so tenaa asaase a bɛahyɛ no ho bɔ he so sɛ hɔhoɔ, tenaa ntomadane mu, sɛdeɛ Isak ne Yakob nso yɔeɛ he, a bɛ nso bɛka ne ho sɛ bɔhyɛ korɔ heaa ho adedifoɔ.

english_verse: Abraham did this, because he was waiting for the eternal city God had planned and built.
translation_verse: Na ɔɔtwɛne kuro a ɔwɔ fapem a ne kyekyerefoɔ ne ne yɔfoɔ ne Nyankopɔn. Sara

english_verse: Even when Sarah was too old to have children, she had faith that God would do what he had promised, and she had a son.
translation_verse: Gyidie nti Sara tumi nyinsɛneeɛ, mpo berɛ a na n’awoɔ atwam, efiri sɛ obuu deɛ na wahyɛ no awoɔ ho bɔ he nɔkwafoɔ.

english_verse: Her husband Abraham was almost dead, but he became the ancestor of many people. In fact, there are as many of them as there are stars in the sky or grains of sand along the seashore.
translation_verse: Nti ɔnam nipa baako a na ɔte sɛ deɛ waawu he so ne maa nipa, “na bɛdɔɔso sɛ soro nsoromma ne mpoano anwea a bentumi nkane mpo” baeɛ.

english_verse: Every one of those people died. But they still had faith, even though they had not received what they had been promised. They were glad just to see these things from far away, and they agreed that they were only strangers and foreigners on this earth.
translation_verse: Yeinom nyinaa wuu gyidie mu a bɛ nsa anka bɔhyɛ he, na mmom behuu no akyirikyiri gye tomm, kaa sɛ, bɛyɛ ahɔhoɔ ne amanfrafoɔ asaase yi so,

english_verse: When people talk this way, it is clear they are looking for a place to call their own.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ bɛ a bɛkaa saa he daa no adi pefee sɛ bɛ ani da bɛ ankasa bɛ mane so.

english_verse: If they had been talking about the land where they had once lived, they could have gone back at any time.
translation_verse: Sɛ na bɛ akoma da kuro a befirii mu baeɛ he so a, nka bɛwɔ akwannya sɛ bɛbɛsane akɔ hɔ.

english_verse: But they were looking forward to a better home in heaven. This is why God wasn't ashamed for them to call him their God. He even built a city for them.
translation_verse: Na afei, bɛ ani gyinaa mane papa bi, mekyerɛ, soro mane he. Ne saa nti ɔnyɛ Nyankopɔn aniwuo sɛ bɛbɛfrɛ no bɛ Nyankopɔn; nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, wasiesie kuro bi ama bɛ.

english_verse: because he was sure that God could raise people to life. This was just like getting Isaac back from death.
translation_verse: Osusuu sɛ Nyankopɔn betumi anyane no afiri awufoɔ mu mpo. Nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, sɛ wofa nea osiiɛ he kasabebuom a, wobetumi aka sɛ, ne nsa kaa Isak bio firii wuom. Isak, Yakob ne Yosef

english_verse: Isaac had faith, and he promised blessings to Jacob and Esau.
translation_verse: Gyidie nti ne Isak hyiraa Yakob ne Esau ya nnoɔma a ɔbɛba daakye ho.

english_verse: Later, when Jacob was about to die, he leaned on his walking stick and worshiped. Then because of his faith he blessed each of Joseph's sons.
translation_verse: Afei ɔnam gyidie so ne Yakob, mmerɛ a na ɔda awusu mpo he, ohyiraa Yosef mmammeema he mu biaa. Yakob de ne ho tweree ne poma, na ɔkoto sɔree Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: And right before Joseph died, he had faith that God would lead the people of Israel out of Egypt. So he told them to take his bones with them.
translation_verse: Bio nso, ɔnam gyidie nti, ne Yosef, mmerɛ a ne nna ɔɔba awieeɛ he, ɔkaa nkorɔeɛ a Israelfoɔ betu akɔ he ho asɛm, na ɔhyɛɛ ne nnompe sie ho nsamanseɛ nso. Mose gyidie

english_verse: Because Moses' parents had faith, they kept him hidden until he was three months old. They saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid to disobey the king's orders.
translation_verse: Afei gyidie nti ne bɛwoo Mose he n’awofoɔ de no siee bosome mmeɛnsa n’awoɔ akyi, efiri sɛ, behuu sɛ abɔfra he ho ɔfɛ; na bɛansuro mmara a na hene he ahyɛ he.

english_verse: Then after Moses grew up, his faith made him refuse to be called the king's grandson.
translation_verse: Gyidie nti ne Mose nyiniiɛ he, wampɛ sɛ bɛbɛfrɛ no Farao babaa ba, na mmom

english_verse: He chose to be mistreated with God's people instead of having the good time that sin could bring for a little while.
translation_verse: ɔpɛɛ sɛ ɔne Nyankopɔn mane behu amaneɛ kyɛne sɛ ɔbɛte bɔne mu dɛ mmerɛ tiaa bi.

english_verse: Moses knew that the treasures of Egypt were not as wonderful as what he would receive from suffering for the Messiah, and he looked forward to his reward.
translation_verse: Obuu amaneɛ a Kristo nti obehu he kyɛnee Misraim ahonyadeɛ, efiri sɛ na ɔɔhwɛ akatua a ɔda n’anim he kwane.

english_verse: Because of his faith, Moses left Egypt. Moses had seen the invisible God and wasn't afraid of the king's anger.
translation_verse: Gyidie nti ne ofirii Misraim he wansuro hene he abufuo, na omiaa n’ani te sɛ deɛ wahu Nyankopɔn a benhu no he.

english_verse: His faith also made him celebrate Passover. He sprinkled the blood of animals on the doorposts, so that the first-born sons of the people of Israel would not be killed by the destroying angel.
translation_verse: Gyidie nti ne odii Twam ne mogya-peteɛ afahyɛ he, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a deɛ ɔsɛe mmakane he ɔmfa ne nsa nka Israel mmakane he. Israel akofofoɔ binom nso gyidie

english_verse: Because of their faith, the people walked through the Red Sea on dry land. But when the Egyptians tried to do it, they were drowned.
translation_verse: Gyidie nti ne mane he faa Mmɛwa Po he mu te sɛ bɛnam asaase pɛsɛɛ so, nso Misraimfoɔ he yɔɔ sɛ bɛɛfa mu he, Po he faa bɛ nyinaa.

english_verse: God's people had faith, and when they had walked around the city of Jericho for seven days, its walls fell down.
translation_verse: Gyidie nti ne Yeriko afasuo he bubu guu fam mmerɛ a Israelfoɔ twaa ho hyiaa nna nson he.

english_verse: Rahab had been a prostitute, but she had faith and welcomed the spies. So she wasn't killed with the people who disobeyed.
translation_verse: Ɔnam dwamanfoɔ Rahab gyidie so ne maa no gyee akwansrafoɔ he asomdwie mu a ne saa nti bɛ anku, na mmom bekuu asoɔdenfoɔ he.

english_verse: What else can I say? There isn't enough time to tell about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
translation_verse: Na mmɛ ne menka bio? Efiri sɛ mmerɛ ɔmma kwane mma me nka Gideon, Barak, Samson, Yefta, Dawid ne Samuel ne adiyifoɔ he ho asɛm—

english_verse: Their faith helped them conquer kingdoms, and because they did right, God made promises to them. They closed the jaws of lions
translation_verse: yeinom ne nam gyidie so dii ahennie so nkunim, hwɛɛ sɛ atɛntenenee bɛyɔ adwuma, bɛ nsa kaa bɔhyɛ, mmua gyata ano,

english_verse: and put out raging fires and escaped from the swords of their enemies. Although they were weak, they were given the strength and power to chase foreign armies away.
translation_verse: dum gya a ɔɔdɛre, dwane sekane ano, nyaa ahoɔdene berɛ a bɛyɛɛ mmerɛ mu, yɛɛ nam kõ mu, kaa amanfrafoɔ dɔm guiɛ.

english_verse: Some women received their loved ones back from death. Many of these people were tortured, but they refused to be released. They were sure they would get a better reward when the dead are raised to life.
translation_verse: Nnanom nsa kaa bɛ awufoɔ a bɛasɔre. Bɛyɛɛ bebree ayakayakadeɛ, nso bɛampene sɛ bɛbɛgye bɛ, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a bebenya wusɔreɛ a odi mu.

english_verse: Others were made fun of and beaten with whips, and some were chained in jail.
translation_verse: Bedii binom ho fɛw na bɛbɔɔ bɛ mmaa, na mpo bɛde nkɔnsɔnkɔnsɔn guu binom de bɛ too afiase.

english_verse: Still others were stoned to death or sawed in two or killed with swords. Some had nothing but sheep skins or goat skins to wear. They were poor, mistreated, and tortured.
translation_verse: Besii bɛ aboɔ kuu bɛ, bɛde asradaa paee bɛ mu mmienu, bɛde sekane kuu bɛ; na bɛakɔneabadie nyinaa mu he, ntaadeɛ a na bɛhyɛ nyinaa yɛ nnwane ne mmirekyie nwoma, ohiaa bɛ, bɛ ho hiahia bɛ na bedii bɛ ani.

english_verse: The world did not deserve these good people, who had to wander in deserts and on mountains and had to live in caves and holes in the ground.
translation_verse: Na bɛyɛɛ sɛ nnipa a na wiase so nni mfasoɔ mma bɛ. Nti bekyinkyinnii serɛ ne mmepɔ so, ne abodane mu asaase mu monku mu.

english_verse: All of them pleased God because of their faith! But still they died without being given what had been promised.
translation_verse: Nso yei nyinaa mu he, ɔyam sɛ bɛ gyidie nyaa adanse-pa deɛ, nso bɛ nsa anka bɔhyɛ he,

english_verse: This was because God had something better in store for us. And he did not want them to reach the goal of their faith without us.
translation_verse: esiane sɛ na Nyankopɔn asiesie bibi a ɔyɛ kyɛne so asie amma yɛ, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a bɛnkwati yɛ nyɛ bɛ pɛ.

english_verse: Such a large crowd of witnesses is all around us! So we must get rid of everything that slows us down, especially the sin that just won't let go. And we must be determined to run the race that is ahead of us.
translation_verse: Saa nti yɛ nso, esiane sɛ adansefoɔ bebree atwa yɛ ho ahyia yi, homma yɛnto adesoa ne bɔne biaa a ɔkyekyere yɛ na ɔtwe yɛ he ngu, na yɛmfa animia ntu akansie a ɔda yɛ anim he mu mmirika,

english_verse: We must keep our eyes on Jesus, who leads us and makes our faith complete. He endured the shame of being nailed to a cross, because he knew later on he would be glad he did. Now he is seated at the right side of God's throne!
translation_verse: nhwɛ, Yesu a ɔyɛ yɛ gyidie farebae na ɔno nso ne ma no wie pɛyɛ, ɔno ne hwɛɛ anigyeɛ a ɔda n’anim he na ɔde boasetɔ huu asennua he ho amaneɛ, na obuu n’ani guu ho aniwuo so. Ne saa nti, waagye n’adwa Nyankopɔn ahennie he nifa.

english_verse: So keep your mind on Jesus, who put up with many insults from sinners. Then you won't get discouraged and give up.
translation_verse: Na honhwɛ deɛ ɔde boasetɔ faa nnebɔnyɛfoɔ nitane a bɛde tiaa no he mu, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a hoommerɛ anaa hoompa aba, ho akomam.

english_verse: None of you have yet been hurt in your battle against sin.
translation_verse: Ampa, apereapereɛ a ho ne bɔne oodi yi, onnya nkoduru sɛ hode ho mogyaa betwa so koraa.

english_verse: But you have forgotten that the Scriptures say to God's children, “When the Lord punishes you, don't make light of it, and when he corrects you, don't be discouraged.
translation_verse: Na ho werɛ afiri afutusɛm a bɛde maa ho ɔnam sɛ ho yɛ mma nti he anaa? Ɔne sɛ, “Me ba, mmu wo ani ngu Awurade asotwe so, anaa ɔtwe w’aso a mma waba mu mmu;

english_verse: The Lord corrects the people he loves and disciplines those he calls his own.”
translation_verse: efiri sɛ bɛ a Awurade dɔ bɛ he ne ɔtwe bɛ aso, na ɔtea ne ba biaa a ɔgye no.”

english_verse: Be patient when you are being corrected! This is how God treats his children. Don't all parents correct their children?
translation_verse: Honya atenetene a bɛtenetene ho he mu boasetɔ, efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn yɛ ho sɛ mma. Na ba deɛ ɔya he ne n’agya ntenetene no?

english_verse: God corrects all his children, and if he doesn't correct you, then you don't really belong to him.
translation_verse: Sɛ hoanya atenetene a mma nyinaa fa mu he bi a, hedeɛ na honnyɛ mma mmara kwane so sɛ ɔno ankasa ne mma nɔkorem.

english_verse: Our earthly fathers correct us, and we still respect them. Isn't it even better to be given true life by letting our spiritual Father correct us?
translation_verse: Afei nso, yɛwɔ honam fam awofoɔ a bɛtenetene yɛ, na yɛde buo ma bɛ. Sɛ saa deɛ a, nti ɔnsɛ sɛ yɛbrɛ yɛ ho ase mpo kyɛne saa hyɛ honhom nyinaa Agya he ase na yɛanya nkwa anaa?

english_verse: Our human fathers correct us for a short time, and they do it as they think best. But God corrects us for our own good, because he wants us to be holy, as he is.
translation_verse: Na yɛ honam fam awofoɔ he deɛ, bɛtenetene yɛ mmerɛ tiaa bi mu sɛnea ɔsɔ bɛ ani, nso ɔno deɛ, yɛ yiedie nti ne ɔtenetene yɛ, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a yɛbɛ yɛ kronkron sɛdeɛ ɔno nso yɛ kronkron he.

english_verse: It is never fun to be corrected. In fact, at the time it is always painful. But if we learn to obey by being corrected, we will do right and live at peace.
translation_verse: Na seeseiaa deɛ atenetene biaa nyɛ anigyeɛ, na mmom ɔyɛ haw, nso akyire yi ɔsõ asomdwie aba a ɔyɛ tenenee ma bɛ a bɛfa saa nteteɛ he mu.

english_verse: and walk a straight path. Then lame people will be healed, instead of getting worse. Warning against Turning from God
translation_verse: na homma ho akwane ntene, na adeɛ biaa a abubu he amfiri ne nyinasoɔ so, na mmom anya ayaresa. Kɔkɔbɔ a otia bɛ a bɛpo Nyankopɔn adom

english_verse: Try to live at peace with everyone! Live a clean life. If you don't, you will never see the Lord.
translation_verse: Honhwehwɛ asomdwie ho ne nnipa nyinaa ntam, ɔne kronkronyɛ a deɛ ɔkwati biaa ntumi nhu Awurade he.

english_verse: Make sure no one misses out on God's wonderful kindness. Don't let anyone become bitter and cause trouble for the rest of you.
translation_verse: Honhwɛ yie na Nyankopɔn adom he ammɔ biaa; na nhini biaa a nwonwono yam amfifiri ho mu na ammɛyɛ haw amma ho, na angu nnipa bebree ho fi.

english_verse: Watch out for immoral and ungodly people like Esau, who sold his future blessing for only one meal.
translation_verse: Honhwɛ yie sɛ ho mu bi ɔnnyɛ sɛ Esau, dwamamfoɔ ne huhuni a aduane baako nti ɔtɔnoo ne mpanyinnie.

english_verse: You know how he later wanted it back. But there was nothing he could do to change things, even though he begged his father and cried.
translation_verse: Honnim sɛ akyire yi, ɔyam sɛ ɔde nisuo hwehwɛɛ nhyira he deɛ, nso bɛpoo no, efiri sɛ wanhwehwɛ adwensakra akyi kwane.

english_verse: You have not come to a place like Mount Sinai that can be seen and touched. There is no flaming fire or dark cloud or storm
translation_verse: Na ɔnyɛ mmepɔ a bɛde nsa ka he ho, ne gya a ɔɔhye ne mununkum kusuu ne sum ne mframa a ɔɔbɔ huu,

english_verse: or trumpet sound. The people of Israel heard a voice speak. But they begged it to stop,
translation_verse: ne mmɛne nnyegyeɛ ne nsɛm nne a bɛ a bɛteeɛ he srɛɛ sɛ ɔmmfa asɛm bi nka bɛ ho bio he ho ne hoaba;

english_verse: because they could not obey its commands. They were even told to kill any animal that touched the mountain.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, ahyɛdeɛ a bɛde maa bɛ he mu yɛɛ duru maa bɛ, ɔne sɛ, “Sɛ aboa mpo ho kɔka mmepɔ he a, bensi no aboɔ ma onwu.”

english_verse: The sight was so frightening that Moses said he shook with fear.
translation_verse: Nɔkwasɛm ne sɛ, ɔnam sɛ na ohu paa he nti, Mose kaa sɛ, “Mebɔ hu na me ho popo.”

english_verse: You have now come to Mount Zion and to the heavenly Jerusalem. This is the city of the living God, where thousands and thousands of angels have come to celebrate.
translation_verse: Na mmom, ho deɛ hoaba Sion Bepɔ so ne Nyankopɔn teasefoɔ he kuro, soro Yerusalem, ne abɔfoɔ bebree nhyiamu,

english_verse: Here you will find all of God's dearest children, whose names are written in heaven. And you will find God himself, who judges everyone. Here also are the spirits of those good people who have been made perfect.
translation_verse: ne mmakane a bɛatwerɛ bɛ dini soro ahennie mu nhyiamu ase ne Nyankopɔn a ɔyɛ nnipa nyinaa temufoɔ, ne ahoteefoɔ ahonhom a bɛayɛ no pɛ,

english_verse: And Jesus is here! He is the one who makes God's new agreement with us, and his sprinkled blood says much better things than the blood of Abel.
translation_verse: na ho aba Yesu hɔ, apam foforɔ he ho ntamgyinafoɔ, ne mogyaa a bɛapete a ɔkasa yie kyɛne Habel mogyaa he.

english_verse: Make sure you obey the one who speaks to you. The people did not escape, when they refused to obey the one who spoke to them at Mount Sinai. Do you think you can possibly escape, if you refuse to obey the one who speaks to you from heaven?
translation_verse: Honhwɛ yie na hoampo deɛ ɔɔkasa he; na sɛ bɛ a bɛpoo kɔkɔbɔ a bɛte firii asaase yi so he antumi annwane a, ɔbɛyɛ dɛ ne yɛ a yɛate kɔkɔbɔ a ofiri soro he betumi adwane?

english_verse: When God spoke the first time, his voice shook only the earth. This time he has promised to shake the earth once again, and heaven too.
translation_verse: Saa mmerɛ he ne nne wosoo asaase; nso afei deɛ waahyɛ bɔ sɛ, “Mesane awoso asaase bio, ɔnyɛ asaase nkoaa na mmom soro nso ka ho.”

english_verse: The words “once again” mean that these created things will someday be shaken and removed. Then what cannot be shaken will last.
translation_verse: Saa asɛm a ɔka sɛ, “Mɛwoso no bio”, he kyerɛ sɛ obeyi nnoɔma a ɔwosoeɛ he afiri hɔ, ɔne sɛ, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a deɛ bentumi nwoso he bɛtena hɔ daa.

english_verse: We should be grateful we were given a kingdom that cannot be shaken. And in this kingdom we please God by worshiping him and by showing him great honor and respect.
translation_verse: Nti esiane sɛ yɛanya ahennie a ɔnwoso yi, homma yɛmfa aseda mmra mfa so nsom Nyankopɔn sɛdeɛ ɔsɔ n’ani anidie ne ahodasoɔ mu,

english_verse: Our God is like a destructive fire!
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, yɛ Nyankopɔn te sɛ gya a ɔdɛre.

english_verse: Be sure to welcome strangers into your home. By doing this, some people have welcomed angels as guests, without even knowing it.
translation_verse: Hommmu ho ani ngu ahɔhoyɛ so. Na binom nam ahɔhoyɛ so yɔɔ abɔfoɔ papa a na bennim sɛ bɛyɛ abɔfoɔ mpo.

english_verse: Remember the Lord's people who are in jail and be concerned for them. Don't forget those who are suffering, but imagine you are there with them.
translation_verse: Honkae bɛ a bɛda afiase, te sɛ deɛ ho ne bɛ ne da hɔ ɔne bɛ a bɛyɔ bɛ ayakayakadeɛ te sɛ deɛ ho ne bɛ ne ohu amaneɛ.

english_verse: Have respect for marriage. Always be faithful to your partner, because God will punish anyone who is immoral or unfaithful in marriage.
translation_verse: Homma nnipa nyinaa nni awareɛ nni, na honngu awareɛ mpa ho fi; efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn bebu adwamammɔfoɔ ne awaresɛefoɔ atɛne.

english_verse: Don't fall in love with money. Be satisfied with what you have. The Lord has promised that he will not leave us or desert us.
translation_verse: Hommma sikaniberɛ mmma ho abrabɔ mu, na homma ho ani nsɔ deɛ ho wɔ; efiri sɛ waaka sɛ, “Mennyae ho na mempo ho.”

english_verse: This should make you feel like saying, “The Lord helps me! Why should I be afraid of what people can do to me?”
translation_verse: Ne saa nti, yebetumi de akokoɔduro aka sɛ, “Nyankopɔn ne me boafoɔ; Mensuro. Ɛɛne ne nipa betumi de ayɔ me?”

english_verse: Don't forget about your leaders who taught you God's message. Remember what kind of lives they lived and try to have faith like theirs.
translation_verse: Honkae ho mpayimfoɔ, bɛ a bɛkaa Nyankopɔn asɛm he kyerɛɛ ho; honhwɛ sɛdeɛ bɛ abrabɔ wieeɛ, na honsua bɛ gyidie.

english_verse: Jesus Christ never changes! He is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
translation_verse: Sɛnea Yesu Kristo teɛ nnora he, ɔte saa nnɛ, na ɔbɛyɛ saa ara daa.

english_verse: Don't be fooled by any kind of strange teachings. It is better to receive strength from God's gift of undeserved grace than to depend on certain foods. After all, these foods don't really help the people who eat them.
translation_verse: Hommma nkyerɛkyerɛ ahodoɔ ne nsɛm foforɔ mmfa ho nkyinkyini, na ɔyɛ sɛ bɛ de adom hyɛ akoma dene, na ɔnyɛ nnuane a bɛ a bediiɛ he nni ho mfasoɔ biaa.

english_verse: But we have an altar where even the priests who serve in the place of worship have no right to eat.
translation_verse: Yɛwɔ afɔrebokyia bi a bɛ a bɛyɔ som adwuma ntomadane he mu he nni ho kwane sɛ bedi so bibi.

english_verse: After the high priest offers the blood of animals as a sin offering, the bodies of those animals are burned outside the camp.
translation_verse: Na mmoa a sɔfo panyini he de bɛ mogyaa bɔɔ bɔne ho afɔreɛ he bɛhye bɛ nsrabane he akyi.

english_verse: Jesus himself suffered outside the city gate, so his blood would make people holy.
translation_verse: Saa nti ne Yesu nso huu amane kuro he akyi sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a ɔde ɔno ankasa ne mogyaa bɛte mane he ho.

english_verse: This is why we should go outside the camp to Jesus and share in his disgrace.
translation_verse: Saa deɛ a, homma yɛnkɔ ne nkyɛne kuro he akyi na yɛasoa n’ahohorabɔ a ohuu mu amaneɛ he;

english_verse: On this earth we don't have a city that lasts forever, but we are waiting for such a city.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ yenni kuro a ɔtena hɔ daa ya ha yi, na mmom nea ɔɔba he na yɛɛhwɛ no kwane dendene.

english_verse: Our sacrifice is to keep offering praise to God in the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Nti homma yɛmfa ne so mmɔ aseda afɔreɛ ma Nyankopɔn, ɔne sɛ, aba a ofiri anofafaa a ɔbɔ ne dini mu.

english_verse: But don't forget to help others and to share your possessions with them. This too is like offering a sacrifice that pleases God.
translation_verse: Hommmu ho ani ngu papayɔ ne adekyɛ so, efiri sɛ, saa afɔreɛ he sɔ Nyankopɔn ani.

english_verse: Obey your leaders and do what they say. They are watching over you, and they must answer to God. So don't make them sad as they do their work. Make them happy. Otherwise, they won't be able to help you at all.
translation_verse: Homfa buo ne ahobrɛaseɛ ma ho mpayimfoɔ, efiri sɛ bɛwɛne ho kra sɛ bɛ a bebu ho akonta. Homma bɛmfa anigye nyɔ na ɔnyɛ ahomeguo so, sɛ bɛde ahomeguo a, ɔnyɛ mfasoɔ biaa mma ho.

english_verse: Pray for us. Our consciences are clear, and we always try to live right.
translation_verse: Hommɔ mpaeɛ ma yɛ; efiri sɛ yɛwɔ ahotɔsoɔ sɛ yɛwɔ ahonim a ho te, yɛasiesie yɛ ho sɛ yebedi nɔkorɛ nnoɔma nyinaa mu.

english_verse: I especially want you to pray that I can visit you again soon. Final Prayers and Greetings
translation_verse: Metu ho fo sɛ honyɔ saa adeɛ yi anibereɛ so, na ama bɛde asane abɛka ho ho animuanimu yiaa. Nhyirasɛm

english_verse: God gives peace, and he raised our Lord Jesus Christ from death. Now Jesus is like a Great Shepherd whose blood was used to make God's eternal agreement with his flock.
translation_verse: Na asomdwie Nyankopɔn a ɔnam daa apam he mogyaa so, ayi nnwane he hwɛfoɔ kɛseɛ a ɔne yɛ Awurade Yesu afiri awufoɔ mu he,

english_verse: I pray God will make you ready to obey him and that you will always be eager to do right. May Jesus help you do what pleases God. To Jesus Christ be glory forever and ever! Amen.
translation_verse: mma ho nni mu nnoɔma nyinaa mu na hoayɔ nea ɔpɛ, deɛ ɔyɔ ho mu adwuma he mfa Yesu Kristo so, sɛdeɛ ɔsɔ n’ani. Ɔno ne animuonyam nka no daa daa. Amen. Afotusɛm a otwatoɔ ne nkyea

english_verse: My friends, I have written only a short letter to encourage you, and I beg you to pay close attention to what I have said.
translation_verse: Anuanom, metu ho fo sɛ honkura m’afotusɛm he mu, efiri sɛ m’atwerɛ no tiaa bi ne abra ho.

english_verse: By now you surely must know that our friend Timothy is out of jail. If he gets here in time, I will bring him with me when I come to visit you.
translation_verse: Mepɛ sɛ hohu sɛ bɛagyae yenua Timoteo; na sɛ ɔba mmerɛ pa mu a me ne no bɛba abehu ho.

english_verse: Please give my greetings to your leaders and to the rest of the Lord's people. His followers from Italy send you their greetings.
translation_verse: Honkyea ho mpayimfoɔ ne ahoteefoɔ he nyinaa ma me. Bɛ a befiri Italia he kyea ho.

english_verse: From James, a servant of God and of our Lord Jesus Christ. Greetings to the twelve tribes scattered all over the world. Faith and Wisdom
translation_verse: Yakobo, Nyankopɔn ne Awurade Yesu Kristo akoa, twerɛ krataa yi de kɔma abusuakuo dummienu a bɛabɔ ahwete he de nkyea mma bɛ nyinaa. Kristoni ne Sɔhwɛ

english_verse: My friends, be glad, even if you have a lot of trouble.
translation_verse: Me nuanom, sɛ hohyia sɔhwɛ ahodoɔ biaa a, hommu no anigyeɛ,

english_verse: You know you learn to endure by having your faith tested.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ honim sɛ, ho gyidie he sɔhwɛ yɛ boasetɔ ho adwuma.

english_verse: But you must learn to endure everything, so you will be completely mature and not lacking in anything.
translation_verse: Na homma boasetɔ nwie n’adwuma, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a ho bewie honhom mu nyini na ho ayɛ pɛ, a bibiaa ɔnka. Nyansa a ofiri Nyankopɔn

english_verse: If any of you need wisdom, you should ask God, and it will be given to you. God is generous and won't correct you for asking.
translation_verse: Sɛ ho mu bi hia nyansa a, ma no mmisa Nyankopɔn a ɔma nnipa nyinaa adeɛ kwa na ahohora biaa nni mu he, na ɔde bɛma nipa korɔ he.

english_verse: But when you ask for something, you must have faith and not doubt. Anyone who doubts is like an ocean wave tossed around in a storm.
translation_verse: Na mmom ɔsɛ sɛ obisa no gyidie mu, a n’adwene ɔnyɛ no ntanta, efiri sɛ nea n’adwene yɛ no ntanta he te sɛ po asorɔkye a mframa bɔ no di akɔneaba.

english_verse: Any of God's people who are poor should be glad he thinks so highly of them.
translation_verse: Ɔsɛ sɛ gyidini a ɔyɛ hiani he ma n’ani gye, efiri sɛ, ɔwɔ honhom mu ahonyadeɛ,

english_verse: But any who are rich should be glad when God makes them humble. Rich people will disappear like wild flowers
translation_verse: na defoɔ a ɔnyɛ gyidini he mma ne werɛ nho, efiri sɛ, ɔbetwam akɔ sɛ wiram nhwiren.

english_verse: scorched by the burning heat of the sun. The flowers lose their blossoms, and their beauty is destroyed. This is how the rich will disappear, as they go about their business. Trials and Temptations
translation_verse: Na sɛdeɛ awia de ne hyeɛ pie, na ɔhye wira mma ne nhwiren te tɔ, ma nhwiren he ahoɔfɛ sɛe he, saa ne defoɔ nso betwam akɔ berɛ a ogu so ɔɔyɔ ne nnwuma he. Amaneɛ ne sɔhwɛ

english_verse: God will bless you, if you don't give up when your faith is being tested. He will reward you with a glorious life, just as he rewards everyone who loves him.
translation_verse: Nhyira ne nipa a ɔto ne bo ase sɔhwɛ mu. Na sɛ otumi gyina sɔhwɛ berɛ he mu a, obenya nkwa abotire a Awurade ahyɛ bɔ sɛ ɔde bɛma bɛ a bɛdɔ no he.

english_verse: Don't blame God when you are tempted! God cannot be tempted by evil, and he doesn't use evil to tempt others.
translation_verse: Sɛ sɔhwɛ ba bi so a, mma onii he nka sɛ, “Nyankopɔn asɔ me ahwɛ”, efiri sɛ bi ntumi mfa bɔne nsɔ Nyankopɔn nhwɛ, na ɔno nso nsɔ bi nhwɛ.

english_verse: We are tempted by our own desires that drag us off and trap us.
translation_verse: Na mmom bi akɔnnɔ ne gyegye no, daadaa no na ɔsɔ onii he hwɛ;

english_verse: Our desires make us sin, and when sin is finished with us, it leaves us dead.
translation_verse: na sɛ saa akɔnnɔ he nyinsɛne a, ɔwo bɔne, na sɛ bɔne he nso nyini a, ɔwo wuo.

english_verse: Don't be fooled, my dear friends.
translation_verse: Me nuanom adɔfo, hommma bɛnnaadaa ho.

english_verse: Every good and perfect gift comes down from the Father who created all the lights in the heavens. He is always the same and never makes dark shadows by changing.
translation_verse: Akyɛdeɛ pa ne nnoɔma a ɔyɛ pɛ nyinaa firi soro, ofiri hann Agya he hɔ ne ba. Ɔno ne nsakraeɛ anaa adanedaneɛ sunsum biaa nni ne mu.

english_verse: He wanted us to be his own special people, and so he sent the true message to give us new birth. Hearing and Obeying
translation_verse: Ɔnam n’asɛm a ɔyɛ nɔkorɛ so ne woo yɛ, sɛdeɛ ɔbɛyɛ a ɔno ankasa ne pɛ bɛba mu, na yɛabɛyɛ sɛ n’abɔdeɛ mu aba a odi kane. Asɛm he tie ne ne yɔ

english_verse: My dear friends, you should be quick to listen and slow to speak or to get angry.
translation_verse: Anuanom adɔfo, honhu yei sɛ: Ɔsɛ sɛ nipa biaa yɔ krado wɛne n’aso tie asɛm; ɔnsɛ sɛ ɔpere ne ho kasa; saa nso ne ɔnsɛ sɛ ɔpere ne ho fa abufuo;

english_verse: You must stop doing anything immoral or evil. Instead be humble and accept the message planted in you to save you.
translation_verse: Nti honto fi ne bɔne nyinaa ngu, na homfa ahobrɛaseɛ nye Nyankopɔn asɛm a bɛadua ne ho mu a obetumi agye ho kra nkwa he.

english_verse: Obey God's message! Don't fool yourselves by just listening to it.
translation_verse: Honyɛ asɛm he yɔfoɔ, na mmom honnyɛ atiefoɔ kɛkɛ, nnaadaa ho ho.

english_verse: If you hear the message and don't obey it, you are like people who stare at themselves in a mirror
translation_verse: Na sɛ bi tie asɛm he kɛkɛ na wamfa anni dwuma a, ɔte sɛ nipa a ɔhwɛ n’ anim ahwehwɛ mu;

english_verse: and forget what they look like as soon as they leave.
translation_verse: na sɛ ɔhwɛ n’anim na ofiri hɔ kɔ a, hɔaa na ne werɛ afiri sɛdeɛ na ɔteɛ he.

english_verse: But you must never stop looking at the perfect law that sets you free. God will bless you in everything you do, if you listen and obey, and don't just hear and forget.
translation_verse: Nso deɛ ɔhwɛ mmara a ɔyɛ pɛ mu he, mmara a ɔma ahofadie he tena ne mu, ɔnyɛ tiefoɔ werɛfirefoɔ na mmom ɔyɛ deɛ otie na ɔyɔ, bebehyira no deɛ ɔyɔ nyinaa mu.

english_verse: If you think you are being religious, but can't control your tongue, you are fooling yourself, and your religion is useless.
translation_verse: Sɛ bi dwene sɛ ɔyɛ nyamesomni, na woanto ne kɛtrɛma nnareka na mmom ɔdaadaa n’akoma a, na ne nyamesom he yɛ kwa.

english_verse: Religion that pleases God the Father must be pure and spotless. You must help needy orphans and widows and not let this world make you evil.
translation_verse: Nyamesom a ho te na nkekaeɛ nni mu a Agya Nyankopɔn ani sɔ di: Sɛ bi bɛdwene nyanka ne akunafoɔ ho, bɛ ahohiahia mmerɛ mu, na ɔbɛte ne ho afiri wiase nkekaeɛ ho.

english_verse: My friends, if you have faith in our glorious Lord Jesus Christ, you won't treat some people better than others.
translation_verse: Me nuanom a hoyɛ yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo a ɔyɛ animuonyam wura he mu agyidifoɔ he, ɔnsɛ sɛ honyɔ nyinyimu.

english_verse: Suppose a rich person wearing fancy clothes and a gold ring comes to one of your meetings. And suppose a poor person dressed in worn-out clothes also comes.
translation_verse: Sɛ nipa bi a ɔhyɛ sikakɔkɔɔ nkaa ne ataade-pa ba ho nhyiamu ase, na sɛ hiani bi nso a ɔnhyɛ ataade-pa nso ba hɔ a,

english_verse: You must not give the best seat to the one in fancy clothes and tell the one who is poor to stand at the side or sit on the floor.
translation_verse: na sɛ hohu deɛ ɔhyɛ ataade pa he na hoka kyerɛ no sɛ, “Mepa wo kyɛw, bɛtena ha,” na mmom hiani he deɛ hoka kyerɛ no sɛ, “Gyina hɔ,” anaa, “Tena me nane ase fam ha,” a na

english_verse: This is the same as saying that some people are better than others, and you would be acting like a crooked judge.
translation_verse: honyɔɔ nyiyimu ho mu anaa, na homfaa adwemmɔne mmu atɛne anaa?

english_verse: My dear friends, pay attention. God has given a lot of faith to the poor people in this world. God has also promised them a share in his kingdom that he will give to everyone who loves him.
translation_verse: Anuanom adɔfo, hontie! Nyankopɔn nyii bɛ a wiasefoɔ bu bɛ sɛ ahiafoɔ he sɛ bɛnyɛ gyidie mu adefoɔ ne ahennie a ɔhyɛɛ ho bɔ ma bɛ a bɛdɔ no he ho adedifoɔ?

english_verse: You mistreat the poor. But isn't it the rich who boss you around and drag you off to court?
translation_verse: Nso hoabu saafoɔ he animtia. Ɔnyɛ wiasefoɔ adefoɔ ne bɛhyɛ ho so he? Ɔnyɛ bɛ ne bɛtwe ho kɔ asennibea he anaa?

english_verse: Aren't they the ones who make fun of your Lord?
translation_verse: Ɔnyɛ bɛ ne bɛka dini pa a bɛde frɛ ho he ho nsɛmmɔne he?

english_verse: You will do all right, if you obey the most important law in the Scriptures. It is the law that commands us to love others as much as we love ourselves.
translation_verse: Sɛ hodi twerɛsɛm he mu adehye-mmara a ɔne sɛ, “Ɔsɛ sɛ wodɔ wo yɔnko sɛ wo ho” he so a, hoyɔ no yie.

english_verse: But if you treat some people better than others, you have done wrong, and the Scriptures teach that you have sinned.
translation_verse: Na mmom sɛ hoyɛ nyiyimu a, hoayɔ bɔne na mmara he bu ho atɛne sɛ hoyɛ mmaratofoɔ.

english_verse: If you obey every law except one, you are still guilty of breaking them all.
translation_verse: Na sɛ bi di mmara mũ he nyinaa so na ɔto baako a, waadi mmara he nyinaa ho fɔ,

english_verse: The same God who told us to be faithful in marriage also told us not to murder. So even if you are faithful in marriage, but murder someone, you still have broken God's Law.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, deɛ ɔkaa sɛ, “Nsɛe awareɛ” heaa nso ne kaa sɛ, “Nni awu.” Saa nti, nea ɔnsɛe awareɛ na nso odi awu he, yɛ mmara he tofoɔ.

english_verse: Speak and act like people who will be judged by the law that sets us free.
translation_verse: Sɛ ɔte saa deɛ a, homma ho kasaa ne ho dwumadie nyɛ nea ɔfata nnipa a bɛde adehyedie mmara he bebu ho atɛne;

english_verse: Do this, because on the day of judgment there will be no pity for those who have not had pity on others. But even in judgment, God is merciful! Faith and Works
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, atemmuo berɛ he, Nyankopɔn onhu biaa a wanhu afoforɔ mmɔbɔ he mmɔbɔ, nso mmɔborɔhunu di atemmuo so nkunim. Gyidie a nnwuma nni mu he awu

english_verse: My friends, what good is it to say you have faith, when you don't do anything to show you really do have faith? Can this kind of faith save you?
translation_verse: Anuanom, mmɛ mfasoɔ ne ya so sɛ bi bɛka sɛ ɔwɔ gyidie na onni nnwuma? Saa nipa he gyidie betumi agye no nkwa anaa?

english_verse: If you know someone who doesn't have any clothes or food,
translation_verse: Sɛ wo nuabeema anaa wo nuabaa bi da adagya na onya n’ano aduane a,

english_verse: you shouldn't just say, “I hope all goes well for you. I hope you will be warm and have plenty to eat.” What good is it to say this, unless you do something to help?
translation_verse: na ho mu baako ka kyerɛ no sɛ, “Fa asomdwie kɔ; wo ho nyɛ wo hye, na didi mee,” na woama no deɛ ohia honam fam a, mmɛ mfasoɔ ne ya so?

english_verse: Faith that doesn't lead us to do good deeds is all alone and dead!
translation_verse: Saa ara ne gyidie nkoaa ya hɔ na onni dwuma a, na waawu.

english_verse: Suppose someone disagrees and says, “It is possible to have faith without doing kind deeds.” I would answer, “Prove that you have faith without doing kind deeds, and I will prove that I have faith by doing them.”
translation_verse: Nso bi bɛka sɛ, “Wowɔ gyidie, na me nso mewɔ nnwuma.” Kyerɛ me wo gyidie a nwuma nka ho, na mede me nnwuma bɛkyerɛ wo me gyidie.

english_verse: You surely believe there is only one God. That's fine. Even demons believe this, and it makes them shake with fear.
translation_verse: Wogyidi sɛ Nyankopɔn yɛ baako; woyɛ no yie. Mpo ahonhommɔne nso gye saa di, na bɛ ho popo.

english_verse: Does some stupid person want proof that faith without deeds is useless?
