translation_verse: Yei ne bɛma yɛahu sɛ yefiri nɔkorɛ he mu, na ama yɛ akoma atɔ yɛ yam Nyankopɔn anim,

english_verse: But even if we don't feel at ease, God is greater than our feelings, and he knows everything.
translation_verse: ya bibiaa a yɛ akoma bu yɛ fɔ mu, efiri sɛ, Nyankopɔn so sene yɛ akoma, na onim adeɛ nyinaa.

english_verse: Dear friends, if we feel at ease in the presence of God, we will have the courage to come near him.
translation_verse: Nti anuanom adɔfo, sɛ yɛ akoma ammu yɛ fɔ a, hedeɛ na yɛde akokoɔduro kɔ Nyankopɔn anim.

english_verse: He will give us whatever we ask, because we obey him and do what pleases him.
translation_verse: Bibiaa a yebisa no he, yɛ nsa ka efiri sɛ, yedi ne mmara so na yɛyɔ nea ɔsɔ n’ani.

english_verse: God wants us to have faith in his Son Jesus Christ and to love each other. This is also what Jesus taught us to do.
translation_verse: Na yei ne n’ahyɛdeɛ, sɛ yɛnnye ne Ba Yesu Kristo dini he nni, na yɛnnodɔ yɛ ho yɛ ho, sɛnea ɔhyɛɛ yɛ he.

english_verse: If we obey God's commandments, we will stay one in our hearts with him, and he will stay one with us. The Spirit he has given us is proof that we are one with him.
translation_verse: Biaa a odi Nyankopɔn ahyɛdeɛ so he te Nyankopɔn mu, na Nyankopɔn nso te ne mu. Na yei ne ma yehu sɛ Nyankopɔn te yɛ mu. Na deɛ yɛde hu sɛ ɔte yɛ mu ne Honhom a ɔde ama yɛ he.

english_verse: Dear friends, don't believe everyone who claims to have the Spirit of God. Test them all to find out if they really do come from God. Many false prophets have already gone out into the world,
translation_verse: Adɔfo, honnye ahonhom nyinaa nnni, na mmom honsɔ ahonhom he nhwɛ sɛ befiri Nyankopɔn anaa; efiri sɛ, atorɔ adiyifoɔ bebree aba wiase.

english_verse: and you can know which ones come from God. His Spirit says that Jesus Christ had a truly human body.
translation_verse: Kwane a hobefa so ahu Nyankopɔn honhom di: Honhom biaa a ɔpae mu ka sɛ Yesu Kristo aba honam mu he firi Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: But when someone doesn't say this about Jesus, you know this person has a spirit that doesn't come from God and is the enemy of Christ. You knew this enemy was coming into the world and now is already here.
translation_verse: Na honhom a ɔnka saa mfa Yesu ho he mfiri Nyankopɔn. Saa honhom he yɛ Kristo-kotiafoɔ a hotee sɛ ɔbɛba he, na afei wanya aba wiase he.

english_verse: Children, you belong to God, and you have defeated these enemies. God's Spirit is in you and is more powerful than the one who is in the world.
translation_verse: Mma nkumaa, hoyɛ Nyankopɔn deɛ, na hoadi bɛ so nkunim, efiri sɛ, deɛ ɔya ho mu he wɔ tumi sene deɛ ɔya wiase mu.

english_verse: These enemies belong to this world, and the world listens to them, because they speak its language.
translation_verse: Bɛkasa sɛ wiasefoɔ; wiase tie bɛ efiri sɛ, bɛyɛ wiase deɛ.

english_verse: We belong to God, and everyone who knows God will listen to us. But the people who don't know God won't listen to us. This is how we can tell the Spirit that speaks the truth from the one that tells lies.
translation_verse: Nso yɛ deɛ, yefiri Nyankopɔn. Biaa a onim Nyankopɔn he tie yɛ. Na biaa a onnim Nyankopɔn he ntie yɛ. Yei mu ne yehu nɔkorɛ honhom ne atorɔ honhom. Nyankopɔn yɛ dɔ

english_verse: My dear friends, we must love each other. Love comes from God, and when we love each other, it shows we have been given new life. We are now God's children, and we know him.
translation_verse: Anuanom, homma yɛnnodɔ yɛ ho yɛ ho, efiri sɛ, dɔ firi Nyankopɔn. Nipa biaa a ɔwɔ dɔ he yɛ Nyankopɔn ba na onim Nyankopɔn nso.

english_verse: God is love, and anyone who doesn't love others has never known him.
translation_verse: Na nipa biaa a onni dɔ he nnim Nyankopɔn; efiri sɛ, Nyankopɔn yɛ dɔ.

english_verse: God showed his love for us when he sent his only Son into the world to give us life.
translation_verse: Kwane a Nyankopɔn nam so daa ne dɔ adi kyerɛɛ yɛ ne sɛ: Ɔsomaa ne Ba koro he baa wiase, sɛnea yɛbɛfa ne so anya nkwa.

english_verse: Real love isn't our love for God, but his love for us. God sent his Son to be the sacrifice by which our sins are forgiven.
translation_verse: Yei mu ne dɔ he ya, ɔnyɛ sɛ yɛ ne yɛdɔɔ Nyankopɔn, na mmom ɔno ne dɔɔ yɛ na ɔnam so somaa ne Ba sɛ ɔmmɛyɛ yɛ bɔne ho mpatadeɛ.

english_verse: Dear friends, since God loved us this much, we must love each other.
translation_verse: Adɔfonom, sɛ saa ne Nyankopɔn dɔ yɛ deɛ a, hedeɛ ɔsɛ sɛ yɛ nso yɛdodɔ yɛ ho yɛ ho.

english_verse: No one has ever seen God. But if we love each other, God lives in us, and his love is truly in our hearts.
translation_verse: Biaa nhuu Nyankopɔn da. Sɛ yɛdodɔ yɛ ho yɛ ho a, na Nyankopɔn te yɛ mu, na ne dɔ he awie pɛyɛ yɛ mu nso.

english_verse: God has given us his Spirit. This is how we know we are one with him, just as he is one with us.
translation_verse: Nea ɔma yehu sɛ yɛte Nyankopɔn mu, na ɔno nso te yɛ mu ne sɛ, waama yɛ ne Honhom.

english_verse: God sent his Son to be the Savior of the world. We saw his Son and are now telling others about him.
translation_verse: Yɛahu, na yedi adanseɛ sɛ Agya he somaa ne Ba ma ɔbɛyɛɛ wiase Agyenkwa.

english_verse: God stays one with everyone who openly says Jesus is the Son of God. This is how we stay one with God
translation_verse: Biaa a ɔpae mu ka sɛ Yesu yɛ Nyankopɔn Ba he Nyankopɔn te ne mu, na ɔno nso te Nyankopɔn mu.

english_verse: and are sure God loves us. God is love. If we keep on loving others, we will stay one in our hearts with God, and he will stay one with us.
translation_verse: Na yɛ ankasa yenim na yɛgye Nyankopɔn dɔ a ɔde dɔ yɛ he di. Nyankopɔn yɛ dɔ, na biaa a ɔte dɔ mu he te Nyankopɔn mu na Nyankopɔn nso te ne mu.

english_verse: If we truly love others and live as Christ did in this world, we won't be worried about the day of judgment.
translation_verse: Saa kwane yi so ne dɔ wie pɛyɛ yɛ mu, na ama yɛatumi akasa akokoɔduro mu atemmuda he, efiri sɛ sɛnea ɔteɛ he, saa nso ne yɛteɛ wiase yi mu.

english_verse: A real love for others will chase those worries away. The thought of being punished is what makes us afraid. It shows we have not really learned to love.
translation_verse: Hu biaa nni dɔ mu, na mmom dɔ a odi mu he pam hu nyinaa, efiri sɛ, hu ne atemmuo ne nam, na deɛ osuro atemmuo he nwie pɛyɛ ya dɔ mu.

english_verse: We love because God loved us first.
translation_verse: Yɛdɔ, efiri sɛ, Nyankopɔn ne dii kane dɔɔ yɛ.

english_verse: But if we say we love God and don't love each other, we are liars. We cannot see God. So how can we love God, if we don't love the people we can see?
translation_verse: Sɛ bi ka sɛ, “Medɔ Nyankopɔn” na ɔtane ne nua a, ɔyɛ torɔfoɔ, efiri sɛ, bi ntumi nnɔ Nyankopɔn a onhuu no, berɛ a ɔtane ne nua a ohu no he.

english_verse: The commandment that God has given us is: “Love God and love each other!”
translation_verse: Yei ne ahyɛdeɛ a yɛanya no ne nkyɛne: nea ɔdɔ Nyankopɔn he, ɔsɛ sɛ ɔdɔ ne nua nso.

english_verse: If we believe Jesus is truly Christ, we are God's children. Everyone who loves the Father will also love his children.
translation_verse: Nea ɔgye di sɛ Yesu ne Kristo he, Nyankopɔn ne awo no. Biaa nni hɔ a ɔdɔ bi na ɔnnɔ saa nipa he ba nso.

english_verse: If we love and obey God, we know we will love his children.
translation_verse: Sɛ yɛdɔ Nyankopɔn na yedi n’ahyɛdeɛ so a, ɔnoaa ne yɛde hu sɛ yɛdɔ Nyankopɔn mma.

english_verse: We show our love for God by obeying his commandments, and they are not hard to follow.
translation_verse: Dɔ a yɛdɔ Nyankopɔn he, kyerɛ di a yedi ne mmara so. Na ne mmara he nso so di nyɛ adesoa mma yɛ,

english_verse: Every child of God can defeat the world, and our faith is what gives us this victory.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, biaa a Nyankopɔn awo no di wiase so nkunim, na ɔnam yɛ gyidie so ama yɛadi wiase so nkunim.

english_verse: No one can defeat the world without having faith in Jesus as the Son of God. Who Jesus Is
translation_verse: Hwane ne betumi adi wiase so nkunim ka dea ɔgye di sɛ Yesu yɛ Nyankopɔn Ba he ho? Nyankopɔn Ba he ho adansedie

english_verse: Water and blood came out from the side of Jesus Christ. It wasn't just water, but water and blood. The Spirit tells about this, because the Spirit is truthful.
translation_verse: Yesu Kristo ne deɛ a ɔnam nsuo ne mogyaa so baeɛ. Ɔnyɛ nsuo nkoaa so ne ɔnam baeɛ na mmom nsuo ne mogyaa so. Na ɔyɛ Honhom he ne di ho adanseɛ.

english_verse: In fact, there are three who tell about it.
translation_verse: Na Honhom he ne nɔkorɛ he.

english_verse: They are the Spirit, the water, and the blood, and they all agree.
translation_verse: Adansefoɔ mmeɛnsa ne di adanseɛ, Honhom, nsuo ne mogyaa. Saa adansefoɔ yi adwene hyia.

english_verse: We believe what people tell us. But we can trust what God says even more, and God is the one who has spoken about his Son.
translation_verse: Sɛ yɛgye nnipa adanseɛ di a adanseɛ a Nyankopɔn di he kyɛne no. Na Nyankopɔn adanseɛ he ne adanseɛ a wadi afa ne Ba he ho.

english_verse: If we have faith in God's Son, we have believed what God has said. But if we don't believe what God has said about his Son, it is the same as calling God a liar.
translation_verse: Nti biaa a ɔgye Nyankopɔn Ba he di he wɔ saa adanseɛ yi ya n’akoma mu. Na nea ɔnnye Nyankopɔn nni he ama Nyankopɔn ayɛ torɔfoɔ, efiri sɛ, wannye adanseɛ a Nyankopɔn di faa ne Ba he ho he anni.

english_verse: God has also said he gave us eternal life and this life comes to us from his Son.
translation_verse: Yei ne adanseɛ he: Nyankopɔn ama yɛ nkwa a onni awieeɛ; na saa nkwa he ya ne Ba he mu.

english_verse: And so, if we have God's Son, we have this life. But if we don't have the Son, we don't have this life. Knowing about Eternal Life
translation_verse: Biaa a ɔwɔ Ba he wɔ saa nkwa he, na biaa a onni Ba he nni nkwa he.

english_verse: All of you have faith in the Son of God, and I have written to let you know you have eternal life.
translation_verse: Meetwerɛ yei abra ho, ho a hogye Nyankopɔn ba he dini di he, na ama hoahu sɛ howɔ nkwa a onni awieeɛ.

english_verse: We are certain God will hear our prayers when we ask for what pleases him.
translation_verse: Na nnam a yɛde kɔ ne nkyɛne ne sɛ, yɛsrɛ bibiaa sɛdeɛ ɔpɛ a, ootie yɛ.

english_verse: And if we know God listens when we pray, we are sure our prayers have already been answered.
translation_verse: Na sɛ yenim sɛ otie yɛ abisadeɛ a, hedeɛ, yenim sɛ nnoɔma a yɛasrɛ no ne nkyɛne he, yɛ nsa bɛka.

english_verse: Suppose you see one of our people commit a sin that isn't a deadly sin. You can pray, and this person will be given eternal life. But the sin must not be one that is deadly.
translation_verse: Sɛ bi hu sɛ ne nua ayɔ bɔne bi a ɔmfa no nkɔ wuo mu a, ɔmmɔ Nyankopɔn a ɔbɛma no nkwa he mpaeɛ mma no. Yei ka bɛ a bɛ bɔne mfa bɛ nkɔ wuo mu he ho. Bɔne bi ya hɔ a ɔde nipa kɔ wuo mu na ɔno ho deɛ, menka sɛ hommɔ ho mpaeɛ nkyerɛ Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: Everything that is wrong is sin, but not all sins are deadly.
translation_verse: Deɛ ɔntene nyinaa yɛ bɔne, na bɔne bi ya hɔ a ɔnkɔ wuo mu.

english_verse: We are sure God's children do not keep on sinning. God's own Son protects them, and the devil cannot harm them.
translation_verse: Yenim sɛ Nyankopɔn ba biaa ntena bɔne mu, efiri sɛ, nea Nyankopɔn awo no he hwɛ ne ho so yie, na bɔnefoɔ he ntumi mfa ne nsa nka no.

english_verse: We are certain we come from God and the rest of the world is under the power of the devil.
translation_verse: Yenim sɛ wiase nyinaa hyɛ bɔnefoɔ he tumi ase, nso yɛ deɛ yɛyɛ Nyankopɔn nkurofoɔ.

english_verse: We know that Jesus Christ the Son of God has come and has shown us the true God. And because of Jesus, we now belong to the true God who gives eternal life.
translation_verse: Na yenim sɛ Nyankopɔn Ba he aba, na waama yɛ nteaseɛ ama yɛahu Nyankopɔn trodoo he. Yɛ abrabɔ ya Nyankopɔn trodoo he ne ne Ba Yesu Kristo he mu. Yei ne Nyankopɔn trodoo he ne nkwa a onni awieeɛ he.

english_verse: Children, you must stay away from idols.
translation_verse: Me mma nkumaa, hontwe ho ho mfiri ahonyini ho.

english_verse: From the church leader. To a very special woman and her children. I truly love all of you, and so does everyone else who knows the truth.
translation_verse: Me, Asafo Panyini, twerɛ krataa yi de kɔma Awuraa a bɛayi no ne ne mma a medɔ bɛ nɔkorɛ he mu. Ɔnyɛ me nkoaa, na mmom bɛ a benim nɔkorɛ he nyinaa nso dɔ ho,

english_verse: We love you because the truth is now in our hearts, and it will be there forever.
translation_verse: ɔnam nɔkorɛ he a ɔte yɛ mu na ɔbɛtena yɛ mu akosi daa daa he nti:

english_verse: I pray that God the Father and Jesus Christ his Son will be kind and merciful to us! May they give us peace and truth and love. Truth and Love
translation_verse: Adom, mmɔborɔhunu ne asomdwie mfiri Agya Nyankopɔn ne Awurade Yesu Kristo, Agya he Ba he, nkyɛne mmra yɛ so nɔkorɛ ne dɔ mu. Nɔkorɛ ne dɔ

english_verse: I was very glad to learn that some of your children are obeying the truth, as the Father told us to do.
translation_verse: M’ani agye kɛse sɛ mahu sɛ wo mma he bi nam nɔkorɛ he mu sɛnea Agya he ahyɛ yɛ heaa.

english_verse: Dear friend, I am not writing to tell you and your children to do something you have not done before. I am writing to tell you to love each other, which is the first thing you were told to do.
translation_verse: Nti afei, Awuraa, meeka akyerɛ wo sɛ, yɛnnodɔ yɛ ho yɛ ho. Yei nyɛ mmara foforɔ a meehyɛ ama wo, na mmom ɔyɛ mmara a yenya firii mfitiaseɛ heaa.

english_verse: Love means we do what God tells us. And from the beginning, he told you to love him.
translation_verse: Na dɔ a meeka ho asɛm yi di: Sɛ yɛbɛnante sɛdeɛ ahyɛdeɛ he teɛ. Yei ne ahyɛdeɛ a hote firii mfitiaseɛ he sɛ, honnante dɔ mu.

english_verse: Many liars have gone out into the world. These deceitful liars are saying Jesus Christ did not have a truly human body. But they are liars and the enemies of Christ.
translation_verse: Na nnaadaafoɔ bebree aba wiase, nipa a bɛmpae mu nka sɛ Yesu Kristo baa honam mu. Nipa biaa a ɔte saa he yɛ daadaafoɔ he ne nea ɔko tia Kristo he.

english_verse: So be sure not to lose what we have worked for. If you do, you won't be given your full reward.
translation_verse: Honhwɛ ho ho so yie na hoanhwere nea hoayɔ ho adwuma, sɛ ɔbɛyɛ a hobenya ho akatua nyinaa.

english_verse: Don't keep changing what you were taught about Christ, or else God will no longer be with you. But if you hold firmly to what you were taught, both the Father and the Son will be with you.
translation_verse: Nipa biaa a ɔfom na ɔntena Kristo nkyerɛkyerɛ he mu he, onnim Nyankopɔn. Na nea ɔtena nkyerɛkyerɛ he mu he, ɔno mmom ne nea onim Agya he ne ne Ba he.

english_verse: If people won't agree to this teaching, don't welcome them into your home or even greet them.
translation_verse: Sɛ bi ba ho nkyɛne na wamfa saa nkyerɛkyerɛ yi ammra ho a, honnnye no nkɔ ho fie, na honnkyea no nka sɛ, “Asomdwie nka wo,”

english_verse: Greeting them is the same as taking part in their evil deeds. Final Greetings
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, biaa a ɔde Nyankopɔn asomdwie bɛma no he nso de ne ho bata bɔne a saa nipa he yɔ he ho. Awieeɛ

english_verse: I have much more to tell you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink. I want to come and talk to you in person, because this will make us really happy.
translation_verse: Ɔyɛ nɔkorɛ sɛ, mewɔ nsɛm pii ka kyerɛ ho, nso memfa krataa ne twerɛdua so nka. Na mmom, mewɔ anidasoɔ sɛ mɛba ne ho atena ase akasa, na yɛ anigyeɛ awie pɛyɛ.

english_verse: Greetings from the children of your very special sister.
translation_verse: Wo nuabaa a bɛayi no he mma he kyea wo.

english_verse: From the church leader. To my dear friend Gaius. I love you because we follow the truth,
translation_verse: Me, Asafo Panyini, metwerɛ krataa yi de kɔma dɔfo Gaio a medɔ no nɔkorɛ mu.

english_verse: dear friend, and I pray all goes well for you. I hope you are as strong in body, as I know you are in spirit.
translation_verse: Me dɔfo pa, mebɔ mpaeɛ sɛ adeɛ nyinaa mu, onsi wo yie, na nya ahoɔdene sɛnea asi wo kra yie he.

english_verse: It makes me very happy when the Lord's followers come by and speak openly of how you obey the truth.
translation_verse: Berɛ a anuanom bi baeɛ na bebedii wo nɔkorɛdie ho adanseɛ sɛnea wonam nɔkorɛ he mu he, m’ani gyee paa.

english_verse: Nothing brings me greater happiness than to hear that my children are obeying the truth. Working Together
translation_verse: Bibiaa nyɛ me anigye nkyɛne sɛ mɛte sɛ me mma he nam nɔkorem.

english_verse: Dear friend, you have always been faithful in helping other followers of the Lord, even the ones you didn't know before.
translation_verse: Me dɔfo pa, wodi nɔkorɛ yie adwuma a woyɔ ma anuanom, ahɔhoɔ mpo.

english_verse: They have told the church about your love. They say you were good enough to welcome them and to send them on their mission in a way God's servants deserve.
translation_verse: Bɛadi wo dɔ he ho adanseɛ asafo he anim. Mesrɛ wo, ma nea wode begya bɛ kwane he nyɛ adeɛ a ɔbɛsɔ Nyankopɔn ani,

english_verse: When they left to tell others about the Lord, they decided not to accept help from anyone who wasn't a follower.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, Kristo dini mu ne bɛnam, na bɛnhwehwɛ mmoa biaa mfiri bɛ a bɛnyɛ agyidifoɔ he nkyɛne.

english_verse: We must support people like them, so we can take part in what they are doing to spread the truth.
translation_verse: Ɔsɛ sɛ yɛ Akristofoɔ mmom ne yɛboa saa nnipa he sɛnea yɛ ne bɛ bɛyɛ nɔkorɛ he ho adwuma ayɔnkofoɔ. Diotrefe ne Demetrio

english_verse: I wrote to the church. But Diotrephes likes to be the number-one leader, and he won't pay any attention to us.
translation_verse: Metwerɛɛ krataa bi kɔmaa asafo he, nso Diotrefe a ɔpɛ sɛ odi kane bɛ mu he antie nea mekaeɛ he, efiri sɛ, ɔnnye me mpanyinnie ntom.

english_verse: So if I come, I will remind him of how he has been attacking us with gossip. Not only has he been doing this, but he refuses to welcome any of the Lord's followers who come by. And when other church members want to welcome them, he puts them out of the church.
translation_verse: Yei nti sɛ meba a, mede bibiaa a wayɔ he ho asɛm bɛto ho anim pefee, nsɛmmɔne a ɔka fa me ho nyinaa. Ɔmma no nso hɔaa, na mmom sɛ anuanom he ba a, ɔnnye bɛ, na bɛ a bɛpɛ sɛ bɛgye bɛ he nso, ɔbɔ mmɔdene sɛ obesi bɛ kwane na watu bɛ afiri asafo he mu.

english_verse: Dear friend, don't imitate the evil deeds of others, but follow the example of people who do kind deeds. They are God's children, but those who are always doing evil have never seen God.
translation_verse: Me dɔfo, nhwɛ bɔne nsua, na mmom hwɛ papa sua. Biaa a ɔyɛ papa he yɛ Nyankopɔn deɛ. Na biaa a ɔyɛ bɔne he nso nhuu Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: Everyone speaks well of Demetrius, and so does the true message that he teaches. I also speak well of him, and you know what I say is true. Final Greetings
translation_verse: Demetrio deɛ, nnipa nyinaa ne nɔkorɛ he ankasa di ne ho adanse-pa. Na yɛ nso yedi ne ho adanse-pa na honim sɛ nea yɛka he yɛ nɔkorɛ. Awieeɛ

english_verse: I have much more to say to you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink.
translation_verse: Nka mewɔ nsɛm bebree ka kyerɛ ho nso memfa krataa ne twerɛdua so nka.

english_verse: I hope to see you soon, and then we can talk in person.
translation_verse: Na mewɔ anidasoɔ sɛ ɔnkyɛre biaa mehu ho ne ho atena ase akasa.

english_verse: I pray that God will bless you with peace! Your friends send their greetings. Please give a personal greeting to each of our friends.
translation_verse: Asomdwie nka ho. Ho nnamfonom nyinaa kyeakyea ho. Bobɔ nnamfonom nyinaa dini mmaako mmaako kyeakyea bɛ.

english_verse: From Jude, a servant of Jesus Christ and the brother of James. To all who are chosen and loved by God the Father and are kept safe by Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Yesu Kristo akoa Yuda a ɔyɛ Yakobo nua twerɛ krataa yi de kɔma bɛ a bɛafrɛ bɛ a Agya Nyankopɔn dɔ bɛ na bɛakora bɛ ama Yesu Kristo he.

english_verse: I pray that God will greatly bless you with mercy, peace, and love! False Teachers
translation_verse: Mmɔborɔhunu, asomdwie ne dɔ nnɔɔso mma ho. Kristofoɔ gyidie ho pere

english_verse: My dear friends, I really wanted to write you about God's saving power at work in our lives. But instead, I must write and ask you to defend the faith that God has once for all given to his people.
translation_verse: Adɔfonom, berɛ a meebɔ mmɔdene atwerɛ ho afa nkwagyeɛ a yɛ nyinaa te mu he ho he, mehuu sɛ ohia sɛ metwerɛ de fa hia ohia sɛ hoyere ho ho gyina gyidie a Nyankopɔn de ahyɛ ahoteefoɔ nsa prɛko he ho.

english_verse: Some godless people have sneaked in among us and are saying, “God treats us much better than we deserve, and so it is all right to be immoral.” They even deny we must obey Jesus Christ as our only Master and Lord. But long ago the Scriptures warned that these godless people were doomed.
translation_verse: Na nnipa bi awiawia bɛ ho aba mu a befiri tete atwerɛ bɛ ho asɛm ato hɔ ama saa atemmuo yi, amumuyɔfoɔ a bɛde akohwibra bu Nyankopɔn adom he animtia, na bɛpo Owura korɔ a ɔne Awurade Yesu Kristo he. Torɔ akyerɛkyerɛfoɔ atemmuo

english_verse: Don't forget what happened to those people the Lord rescued from Egypt. Some of them did not have faith, and he later destroyed them.
translation_verse: Ɔyɛ nɔkorɛ sɛ honim yei dada, nso mepɛ sɛ mekae ho adwene fa gyeɛ a Awurade gyee Israelfoɔ firii Misraim asaase so prɛko na akyire yi he, ɔsɛee bɛ a bɛannye asɛm he anni he.

english_verse: You also know about the angels who didn't do their work and left their proper places. God chained them with everlasting chains and is now keeping them in dark pits until the great day of judgment.
translation_verse: Na abɔfoɔ a bɛtraa bɛ tumi, na begyaa bɛ atenaeɛ he, bɛde daa nkɔnsɔnkɔnsɔn agugu bɛ ya sum kabii mu a Nyankopɔn de bɛ asie ɔɔtwɛne da kɛseɛ he atemmuo.

english_verse: We should also be warned by what happened to the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah and the nearby towns. Their people became immoral and committed all sorts of sexual sins. Then God made an example of them and punished them with eternal fire.
translation_verse: Saa ara ne Sodom ne Gomora ne nkuro a atwa bɛ ho ahyia a bɛyɔɔ sɛ yeinom, de bɛ ho maa adwamammɔfoɔ, na bekodii honam akɔnnɔ akyi a na ɔnyɛ ne kwane so, na bɛde daa gya twee bɛ aso de ayɔ nsɛnnahɔ ama yɛ.

english_verse: The people I am talking about are behaving just like those dreamers who destroyed their own bodies. They reject all authority and insult angels.
translation_verse: Saa nnipa he nam saa kwane korɔ heaa so sosoo daeɛ ma bɛyɔɔ bɔne tiaa bɛ nipadua. Bemmu tumidie, na bɛkasa tiaa animuonyamfoɔ.

english_verse: Even Michael, the chief angel, didn't dare to insult the devil, when the two of them were arguing about the body of Moses. All Michael said was, “The Lord will punish you!”
translation_verse: Nso berɛ a bɔfo-panyini Mikael ne bronsam ɔɔpere Mose amu so a akasakasaa sii bɛ ntam he mpo, bɔfoɔ Mikael anyɔ n’adwene sɛ ɔbɛka asɛmmɔne abu no atɛne, na mmom asɛm a ɔkaeɛaa ne sɛ, “Awurade nka w’anim!”

english_verse: But these people insult powers they don't know anything about. They are like senseless animals that end up getting destroyed, because they live only by their feelings.
translation_verse: Nso saa nnipa yi ka nea bennim he ho nsɛmmɔne, na deɛ befiri bɛ nnipa su mu nim sɛ mmoa a benni adwene he nso nim he, ɔno mu ne begu bɛ ho fi.

english_verse: Now they are in for real trouble. They have followed Cain's example and have made the same mistake that Balaam did by caring only for money. They have also rebelled against God, just as Korah did. Because of all this, they will be destroyed.
translation_verse: Mmusuo nka bɛ! Efiri sɛ, kwane a Kain faa so heaa so ne bɛnam, na mfasoɔ nti, bɛde bɛ ho ahyɛ mfomsoɔ a Bileam yɔeɛ he bi ase. Bɛayɔ dɔm sɛnea Kora yɔɔ dɔm he nam so ma bɛasɛe bɛ sɛ ɔno.

english_verse: These people are filthy minded, and by their shameful and selfish actions they spoil the meals you eat together. They are like clouds blown along by the wind, but never bringing any rain. They are like leafless trees, uprooted and dead, and unable to produce fruit.
translation_verse: Yeinom yɛ ho dɔ adidie mu nkekaeɛ, bɛne ho bom oodidi, bɛmfɛre adeɛ, bɛyɛne bɛ ho; bɛte sɛ mununkum a nsuo nni mu a mframa twetwe kyinkyini, anaa nnua a so nhahane apere a onni aba, na awu mprenu a ne nhini atutu.

english_verse: Their shameful deeds show up like foam on wild ocean waves. They are like wandering stars forever doomed to the darkest pits of hell.
translation_verse: Bɛ aniwuo da adi te sɛ po asorɔkye a opu ahuro fi gu, bɛte sɛ nsoromma a okyinkyini a Nyankopɔn de bɛ asie sum kabii mu afebɔɔ.

english_verse: Enoch was the seventh person after Adam, and he was talking about these people when he said: Look! The Lord is coming with thousands and thousands of holy angels
translation_verse: Yeinom ho ne Henok nso a ɔtɔ Adam so nson awoɔ ntoatoasoɔ mu hyɛɛ saa nnipa yi ho nkɔm teteete sɛ, “Hwɛ, Awurade ne n’abɔfoɔ kronkron mpempem bɛba,

english_verse: to judge everyone. He will punish all those ungodly people for all the evil things they have done. The Lord will surely punish those ungodly sinners for every evil thing they have ever said about him.
translation_verse: na bɛabebu ho nyinaa atɛne na bɛabu nnebɔnyɛfoɔ nyinaa atɛne bɛ bɔne a bɛayɔ ne nsɛm a ano ɔdene a bɛaka atia Nyankopɔn he ho.”

english_verse: These people grumble and complain and live by their own selfish desires. They brag about themselves and flatter others to get what they want. More Warnings
translation_verse: Yeinom yɛ anwiinwiifoɔ, yɔnaafoɔ a bedi bɛaa bɛ akɔnnɔ akyi, na bɛyɛ anumdwafoɔ a mfasoɔ nti, bɛdɛfɛdɛfɛ nnipa. Kɔkɔbɔ ne afutuo ahodoɔ

english_verse: My dear friends, remember the warning you were given by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Adɔfonom, honkae nsɛm a tete he asomafoɔ he ka too hɔ faa Awurade Yesu Kristo ho,

english_verse: They told you that near the end of time, selfish and godless people would start making fun of God.
translation_verse: bɛkaa sɛ, “Sɛ awieeɛ he duru a, fɛwdifoɔ bɛba a, bebedi bɛaa bɛ amumuyɔ akɔnnɔ akyi.”

english_verse: And now these people are already making you turn against each other. They think only about this life, and they don't have God's Spirit.
translation_verse: Yeinom ne nnipa a bɛnam bɛ akɔnnɔ so de mpaepaemu ba he. Na benni Honhom he.

english_verse: Dear friends, keep building on the foundation of your most holy faith, as the Holy Spirit helps you to pray.
translation_verse: Nti adɔfonom, metu ho fo sɛ, honkɔ so nyini ho gyidie kronkron mu, hommɔ mpaeɛ Honhom Kronkron he tumi mu,

english_verse: And keep in step with God's love, as you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to show how kind he is by giving you eternal life.
translation_verse: na honkɔ so ntena Nyankopɔn dɔ mu berɛ a hootwɛne yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo mmɔborɔhunu mu ama wama ho nkwa a onni awieeɛ he.

english_verse: Be helpful to all who may have doubts.
translation_verse: Na akyinnyegyefoɔ nso, honhu bɛ mmɔbɔ

english_verse: Rescue any who need to be saved, as you would rescue someone from a fire. Then with fear in your own hearts, have mercy on everyone who needs it. But hate even the clothes of those who have been made dirty by their filthy deeds. Final Prayer
translation_verse: berɛ a hoontwe bɛ afiri gya mu agye bɛ nkwa, homfa suro nhu binom mmɔbɔ, na hontane nipasu a ɔte sɛ ataadeɛ a nkekaeɛ akeka mu he nso. Nhyira nsɛm

english_verse: Offer praise to God our Savior because of our Lord Jesus Christ! Only God can keep you from falling and make you pure and joyful in his glorious presence. Before time began and now and forever, God is worthy of glory, honor, power, and authority. Amen.
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn koro a ɔnam yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo so yɛ yɛ Agyenkwa he, ɔno ne animuonyam ne kɛseyɛ ne ahoɔdene ne tumi nka no mmerɛ santene, seesei ne daa nyinaa. Amen.

english_verse: This is what God showed to Jesus Christ, so that he could tell his servants what must happen soon. Christ then sent his angel with the message to his servant John.
translation_verse: Saa nwoma yi fa Yesu Kristo adiyisɛm a Nyankopɔn de maa no sɛ ɔnkyerɛ ne nkoa nnoɔma a otwa sɛ ɔba ntɛm. Kristo somaa ne bɔfoɔ kɔɔ ne n’akoa Yohane nkyɛne kɔdaa asɛm yi adi kyerɛɛ no.

english_verse: And John told everything he had seen about God's message and about what Jesus Christ had said and done.
translation_verse: Na Yohane dii adanseɛ faa Nyankopɔn asɛm ne Yesu Kristo ne nea ohuiɛ nyinaa ho.

english_verse: God will bless everyone who reads this prophecy to others, and he will bless everyone who hears and obeys it. The time is almost here.
translation_verse: Nhyira ne nipa a ɔkenkane saa nwoma yi, ne bɛ a betie adiyisɛm he na bedi so sɛnea bɛatwerɛ no saa nwoma yi mu he, efiri sɛ berɛ he abɛne.

english_verse: From John to the seven churches in Asia. I pray that you will be blessed with kindness and peace from God, who is and was and is coming. May you receive kindness and peace from the seven spirits before the throne of God.
translation_verse: Nsɛm a Yohane de kɔmaa asafo ahodoɔ nson a na bɛya Asia he di: Adom mmra ho so. Afei nso, asomdwie a ofiri nea ɔya hɔ, seesei, na ɔya hɔ firi tetentredee, na ɔya hɔ daa he, ne ahonhom nson a ɔya n’ahennwa anim,

english_verse: May kindness and peace be yours from Jesus Christ, the faithful witness. Jesus was the first to conquer death, and he is the ruler of all earthly kings. Christ loves us, and by his blood he set us free from our sins.
translation_verse: ne Yesu Kristo a ɔyɛ danseni nɔkwafoɔ ne wusɔreɛ mu dikanfoɔ ne asaase so ahene sodifoɔ he nkyɛne nka ho. Ɔno a ɔdɔ yɛ, na ɔnam ne mogyaa he so agye yɛ afiri yɛ bɔne mu he

english_verse: He lets us rule as kings and serve God his Father as priests. To him be glory and power forever and ever! Amen.
translation_verse: ayɔ yɛ ahemmane ne asɔfoɔ ama ne Nyankopɔn a ɔyɛ n’agya. Ɔno ne animuonyam ne tumi nka no daa daa. Amen!

english_verse: Look! He is coming with the clouds. Everyone will see him, even the ones who stuck a sword through him. All people on earth will weep because of him. Yes, it will happen! Amen.
translation_verse: Hwɛ! Ɔnam mununkum mu ɔɔba; ani nyinaa behu no, na bɛ a bɛwɔɔ no pea he nso behu no bi. Na ɔno nti asaase so nnipa nyinaa bɛbɔ bena. Ampa, saa ne ɔbɛyɔ. Amen!

english_verse: The Lord God says, “I am Alpha and Omega, the one who is and was and is coming. I am God All-Powerful!” A Vision of the Risen Lord
translation_verse: “Mene ahyɛaseɛ ne awieeɛ,” Awurade Nyankopɔn ne ɔɔka, ɔno ne ya hɔ seesei na ɔya hɔ firi tetentredee na ɔya hɔ daa, ade nyinaa so tumfoɔ, he. Kristo ho anisoadehunu

english_verse: I am John, a follower together with all of you. We suffer because Jesus is our king, but he gives us the strength to endure. I was sent to Patmos Island, because I had preached God's message and had told about Jesus.
translation_verse: Me, Yohane, ho nua a me ne ho bom Yesu mu hunu amane n’ahennie ne ne boasetɔ mu, na meya supɔ Patmo so, esiane Nyankopɔn asɛm ne Yesu adanseɛ nti.

english_verse: On the Lord's day the Spirit took control of me, and behind me I heard a loud voice that sounded like a trumpet.
translation_verse: Awurade da he, na meya Honhom mu, na metee nne dendene bi ya m’akyi a ɔte sɛ totorobɛnto.

english_verse: The voice said, “Write in a book what you see. Then send it to the seven churches in Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.”
translation_verse: Ɔkaa sɛ, “Twerɛ bibiaa a wohu gu nwoma mu, na fa nwoma he kɔma asafo ahodoɔ nson yi: Deɛ ɔya Efeso, Smirna, Pergamo, Tiatira, Sardi, Filadelfia ne Laodikea.”

english_verse: When I turned to see who was speaking to me, I saw seven gold lampstands.
translation_verse: Afei, medanee m’ani pɛɛ sɛ mehu nea ɔɔkasa akyerɛ me he, mehuu sikakɔkɔɔ nkaneadua nson.

english_verse: There with the lampstands was someone who seemed to be the Son of Man. He was wearing a robe that reached down to his feet, and a gold cloth was wrapped around his chest.
translation_verse: Na bi a ɔte sɛ nipa ba he hyɛ ataadeɛ yuu a osi ne nane ase a sikakɔkɔɔ aboomu bɔ ne koko gyina nkaneadua nson he mfimfini.

english_verse: His head and his hair were white as wool or snow, and his eyes looked like flames of fire.
translation_verse: Na ne tinwi yɛ fitaa te sɛ asaawafufuo, te sɛ akaadoo, na n’ani dɛre sɛ gyaframa.

english_verse: His feet were glowing like bronze being heated in a furnace, and his voice sounded like the roar of a waterfall.
translation_verse: Na ne nane ase hyerɛn sɛ yaawa fɛɛfɛ a adɔ ya fononoo mu, na afei bɛatwi ho na ne nne woro so te sɛ nsuo ɔɔworo dendene.

english_verse: He held seven stars in his right hand, and a sharp double-edged sword was coming from his mouth. His face was shining as bright as the sun at noon.
translation_verse: Na okita nsoromma nson ne nsa nifa mu, na nkrantɛ anofanu nnamnnam firi n’anum baeɛ. Na n’anim hyerɛn sɛ awia a ɔɔhyerɛn dendene.

english_verse: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead person. But he put his right hand on me and said: Don't be afraid! I am the first, the last,
translation_verse: Mehuu no he, mekɔhwee ne nane ase yɛɛ te sɛ wufoɔ. Na ɔde ne nsa nifa too me so kaa sɛ, “Nsuro! Mene Kannifoɔ ne Kyidifoɔ.

english_verse: and the living one. I died, but now I am alive forever, and I have the keys to death and the world of the dead.
translation_verse: Mene teasefoɔ he! Mewuiɛ, na hwɛ, mete ase daa daa. Mekita Wuo ne asamando nsafoa.

english_verse: Write what you have seen and what is and what will happen after these things.
translation_verse: Nti afei twerɛ nnoɔma a wohu; nea wohu no seesei ne nea ɔbɛba akyire he.

english_verse: I will explain the mystery of the seven stars you saw at my right side and the seven gold lampstands. The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the lampstands are the seven churches.
translation_verse: Nsoromma nson a wohu no me nsa nifa mu ne sika nkaneadua he nkyerɛaseɛ a ɔyɛ ahintasɛm he di: Nsoromma nson he yɛ asafo nson he abɔfoɔ, na nkaneadua nson he nso yɛ asafo nson he.”

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Ephesus: I am the one who holds the seven stars in my right hand, and I walk among the seven gold lampstands. Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: “Twerɛ krataa kɔma Efeso asafo mu bɔfoɔ he, na ka kyerɛ no sɛ: Yei ne nsɛm a ofiri nea a okita nsoromma nson ne nsa nifa mu a, ɔnante sika nkaneadua nson he ntam he nkyɛne.

english_verse: I know everything you have done, including your hard work and how you have endured. I know you won't put up with anyone who is evil. When some people pretended to be apostles, you tested them and found out they were liars.
translation_verse: “Menim wo nnwuma. Menim nsiyɛ a wode ayɔ adwuma ne boasetɔ a wowɔ. Menim sɛ wonnye nnebɔnyɛfoɔ, na afei woasɔ bɛ a bɛfrɛ bɛ ho asomafoɔ nso bɛnyɛ bi he ahwɛ, na woahu sɛ bɛyɛ atorɔfoɔ.

english_verse: You have endured and gone through hard times because of me, yet you have not given up.
translation_verse: Wowɔ boasetɔ na me dini nti, woahu amane bebree, na ne nyinaa mu he, wompaa aba.

english_verse: But I do have something against you! And it is this: You don't have as much love as you used to.
translation_verse: Nso mewɔ nsɛm bi tia wo: Wonnɔ me sɛnea kane he na wodɔ me he.

english_verse: Think about where you have fallen from, and then turn back and do as you did at first. If you don't turn back, I will come and take away your lampstand.
translation_verse: Kae baabi a wofiri hwee ase. Sakra w’adwene firi bɔne ho na yɔ sɛnea kane he na woyɔ he. Sɛ woansakra amfiri wo bɔne ho a, mɛba wo nkyɛne abeyi wo kaneadua he afiri ne sibrɛ.

english_verse: But there is one thing you are doing right. You hate what the Nicolaitans are doing, and so do I.
translation_verse: “Nso adepa a mahu ne wo ho ne sɛ, wokyiri Nikolaifoɔ nwuma he, na saa ne me nso mekyiri.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. I will let everyone who wins the victory eat from the life-giving tree in God's wonderful garden. The Letter to Smyrna
translation_verse: Nea ɔwɔ aso he ntie nea Honhom he ka kyerɛ asafo ahodoɔ he. Bɛ a bɛadi nkunim he, mɛma bɛ akwanya na bɛadi nkwa dua a ɔya Nyankopɔn turo he mu aba he.’ Krataa a ɔtwerɛ kɔmaa Smirna asafo he

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Smyrna: I am the first and the last. I died, but now I am alive! Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: “Twerɛ krataa yi kɔma Smirna asafo mu bɔfoɔ he sɛ: ‘Saa asɛm yi firi kannifoɔ ne kyidifoɔ he nkyɛne. Ɔno ne wuiɛ, na ɔte ase bio he.

english_verse: I know how much you suffer and how poor you are, but you are rich. I also know the cruel things being said about you by people who claim to be God's people. But they are not really God's people. They are a group that belongs to Satan.
translation_verse: “Menim amaneɛ a wohu maa Awurade na menim wo hia nso, nso woyɛ defoɔ honhom mu. Menim nsɛm a bɛ a bɛka sɛ bɛyɛ Yudafoɔ, nso bɛnyɛ Yudafoɔ he ka tia wo. Bɛyɛ Satan apamfoɔ.

english_verse: Don't worry about what you will suffer. The devil will throw some of you into jail, and you will be tested and made to suffer for ten days. But if you are faithful until you die, I will reward you with a glorious life.
translation_verse: Nsuro haw a ɔɔbɛto wo he. Tie, bɛde ho mu bi bɛto afiase na Bronsam nam so asɔ ho ahwɛ. Na ho haw he bedi dadu. Di nɔkorɛ ma me kosi wuo mu mpo, na mɛma wo nkwa abotire.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. Whoever wins the victory will not be hurt by the second death. The Letter to Pergamum
translation_verse: “Ma nea ɔwɔ aso he ntie nea Honhom he ka kyerɛ asafo he. Deɛ odi nkunim he, wuprenu he ɔnka no da.’ Krataa a ɔtwerɛ kɔmaa Pergamo asafo he

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Pergamum: I am the one who has the sharp double-edged sword! Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: “Twerɛ krataa yi kɔma bɔfoɔ a ɔya Pergamo asafo mu sɛ: Saa krataa yi firi nea ɔwɔ nkrantɛ anofanu a ɔnam he nkyɛne.

english_verse: I know you live where Satan has his throne. But you have kept true to my name. Right there where Satan lives, my faithful witness Antipas was taken from you and put to death. Even then you did not give up your faith in me.
translation_verse: “Menim sɛ faako a wote he, hɔ ne Satan ahennwa si, nso wokuraa me dini mu, na woampa me gyidie, nna a bekuu me danseni nɔkwafoɔ Antipa a na ɔne ho ya hɔ, deɛ a Satan te he.

english_verse: I do have a few things against you. Some of you are following the teaching of Balaam. Long ago he told Balak to teach the people of Israel to eat food that had been offered to idols and to be immoral.
translation_verse: Nso nsɛm kakra a mewɔ tia wo ne sɛ, nnipa bi ya wo mu a bedi Bileam nkyerɛkyerɛ akyi. Saa Bileam he ne kyerɛɛ Balak ma ɔmaa Israelfoɔ dii abosom so nnuane, kyerɛɛ bɛ adwamammɔ, nam so ma bɛyɔɔ bɔne he.

english_verse: Now some of you are following the teaching of the Nicolaitans.
translation_verse: Saa ara nso ne wowɔ nnipa bi ka wo ho a bedi Nikolaifoɔ nkyerɛkyerɛ akyi.

english_verse: Turn back! If you don't, I will come quickly and fight against these people. And my words will cut like a sword.
translation_verse: “Sakra w’adwene firi wo bɔne ho. Anyɛ saa a, ɔɔnkyɛre biaa mɛba wo nkyɛne de nkrantɛ a ofiri m’anum he abɛko atia saa nnipa he.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. To everyone who wins the victory, I will give some of the hidden food. I will also give each one a white stone with a new name written on it. No one will know that name except the one who is given the stone. The Letter to Thyatira
translation_verse: “Ma nea ɔwɔ aso he ntie nea Honhom he ka kyerɛ asafo ahodoɔ he. “Na nea odi nkunim he, mɛma no mana a ahinta he bi. Afei mɛma no boɔ fitaa a bɛatwerɛ dini foforɔ so a biaa nnim, gye nipa a bɛde bɛma no he nko.” Krataa a ɔtwerɛ kɔmaa Tiatira asafo he

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Thyatira: I am the Son of God! My eyes are like flames of fire, and my feet are like bronze. Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: “Twerɛ krataa kɔma bɔfoɔ a ɔya Tiatira asafo mu sɛ: Yei ne asɛm a ofiri Nyankopɔn Ba a n’ani dɛre sɛ gyaframa a ne nane ase hyerɛn sɛ yaawa he nkyɛne.

english_verse: I know everything about you, including your love, your faith, your service, and how you have endured. I know you are doing more now than you have ever done before.
translation_verse: “Menim wo nnwuma. Menim wo dɔ, wo gyidie, wo som ne wo boasetɔ. Menim sɛ wooyɔ adwuma sene kane he.

english_verse: But I still have something against you because of that woman Jezebel. She calls herself a prophet, and you let her teach and mislead my servants to do immoral things and to eat food offered to idols.
translation_verse: Nso nea mewɔ nsɛm bi tia wo: Wososɔɔ baa bi a ne dini de Isebel, a ɔfrɛ ne ho diyifoɔ he so. Saa baa yi kyerɛkyerɛ, na odi m’asomfoɔ anim ma bɛbɔ adwamane di abosom-nnuane.

english_verse: I gave her a chance to turn from her sins, but she did not want to stop doing these immoral things.
translation_verse: Memaa no berɛ sɛ ɔnsakra mfiri ne bɔne mu nso wansakra amfiri n’adwamammɔ mu.

english_verse: I am going to strike down Jezebel. Everyone who does these immoral things with her will also be punished, if they don't stop.
translation_verse: Yei nti, mede no bɛto ahohiahia mpa so na ɔne bɛ a bɛsɛe awareɛ he ahu amane a ano ɔdene yie, gye sɛ ebia bɛsesa firi ne nnwuma he ho.

english_verse: I will even kill her followers. Then all the churches will see that I know everyone's thoughts and feelings. I will treat each of you as you deserve.
translation_verse: Afei, meku n’akyidifoɔ he, na asafo he nyinaa behu sɛ mene deɛ ɔhwehwɛ adwene ne akoma mu. Metua ho mu biaa ka sɛnea ne nwuma teɛ.

english_verse: Some of you in Thyatira don't follow Jezebel's teaching. You don't know anything about what her followers call the “deep secrets of Satan.” So I won't burden you down with any other commands.
translation_verse: Nso ho a hoya Tiatira a honni saa nkyerɛkyerɛ bɔne he akyi he, na honnsua nea afoforɔ frɛ no Satan asumansɛm he deɛ, meeka makyerɛ ho sɛ, memmfa adesoa biaa nto ho so.

english_verse: But until I come, you must hold firmly to the teaching you have.
translation_verse: Na honsɔ nea howɔ he mu dene nkosi sɛ mɛba.

english_verse: I will give power over the nations to everyone who wins the victory and keeps on obeying me until the end.
translation_verse: “Deɛ odi nkunim na ɔkɔ so yɔ nea mepɛ kosi awieeɛ he, mɛma no amane so tumi,

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
translation_verse: Nea ɔwɔ aso he ntie nea Honhom he ka kyerɛ asafo ahodoɔ he.

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Sardis: I have the seven spirits of God and the seven stars. Listen to what I say. I know what you are doing. Everyone may think you are alive, but you are dead.
translation_verse: “Twerɛ krataa kɔma Sardi asafo he mu bɔfoɔ sɛ: “Yei ne nsɛm a ofiri nea ɔwɔ Nyankopɔn ahonhom nson ne nsoromma nson he nkyɛne. “Menim wo nnwuma; wowɔ dini sɛ wote ase deɛ, nso woawu.

english_verse: Wake up! You have only a little strength left, and it is almost gone. So try to become stronger. I have found that you are not completely obeying God.
translation_verse: Nti nyane na kanyane nea aka wo nkyɛne he na anwu koraa, na menhuu wo nnwuma sɛ ɔyɛ pɛ me Nyankopɔn anim.

english_verse: Remember the teaching you were given and heard. Hold firmly to it and turn from your sins. If you don't wake up, I will come when you least expect it, just as a thief does.
translation_verse: Kae nea wonyaeɛ na woteeɛ he, di so na sakra w’adwene firi wo bɔne ho. Sɛ woanwɛne a, mɛba wo so te sɛ korɔmfoɔ, na wonhu dɔnhwere korɔ a mɛba wo nkyɛne.

english_verse: A few of you in Sardis have not dirtied your clothes with sin. You will walk with me in white clothes, because you are worthy.
translation_verse: “Nso ho mu kakraa bi a bɛya Sardi he mfaa wiase nkekaeɛ nkekaa bɛ ntaadeɛ mu. Na bɛ ne me bɛnante berɛ a bɛhyɛ ntaade fitaa, efiri sɛ, bɛfata sɛ bɛyɔ saa.

english_verse: Everyone who wins the victory will wear white clothes. Their names will not be erased from the book of life, and I will tell my Father and his angels that they are my followers.
translation_verse: Na deɛ odi nkunim he bɛbɛhyɛ no ataade-fitaa, na mempopa ne dini mfiri nkwa nwoma he mu. Mɛbɔ ne dini ahyɛ no animuonyam m’Agya ne n’abɔfoɔ anim.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. The Letter to Philadelphia
translation_verse: Nea ɔwɔ aso he ntie nea Honhom he ka kyerɛ asafo ahodoɔ he. Krataa a ɔtwerɛ kɔmaa Filadelfia asafo he

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Philadelphia: I am the one who is holy and true, and I have the keys that belonged to David. When I open a door, no one can close it. And when I close a door, no one can open it. Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: “Twerɛ krataa kɔma Filadelfia asafo he mu bɔfoɔ he sɛ: Yei ne asɛm a ofiri nea ɔyɛ kronkron, a ɔyɛ nɔkwafoɔ a okita Dawid safoa a sɛ obie a, bi ntumi nto mu, na ɔto mu nso a, bi ntumi mmie, he nkyɛne.

english_verse: I know everything you have done. And I have placed before you an open door no one can close. You were not very strong, but you obeyed my message and did not deny you are my followers.
translation_verse: “Menim wo nnwuma. Hwɛ, mede pono bi a mabie a bi ntumi ntom asi w’anim. Menim sɛ wowɔ tumi ketewa bi deɛ, nso woadi me nkyerɛkyerɛ he so na woampa me dini.

english_verse: Now you will see what I will do with those people who belong to Satan's group. They claim to be God's people, but they are liars. I will make them come and kneel down at your feet. Then they will know that I love you.
translation_verse: Hwɛ! Mɛma bɛ a befiri Satan hyiadane mu, a bedi torɔ ka sɛ bɛyɛ Yudafoɔ, nso bɛnyɛ bi he abɛkoto wo nane ase, na bɛ nyinaa ahu sɛ medɔ wo.

english_verse: You obeyed my message and endured. So I will protect you from the time of testing everyone in all the world must go through.
translation_verse: Esiane sɛ woadi m’ahyɛdeɛ so boasetɔ mu nti, sɛ haw a ɔɔba wiase nyinaa abɛsɔ asaase so nnipa nyinaa ahwɛ he ba a, mɛgye wo afiri mu.

english_verse: I am coming soon. So hold firmly to what you have, and no one will take away the crown you will be given as your reward.
translation_verse: Ɔnkyɛre biaa mɛba. Sɔ nea wowɔ he mu dene, na bi annye wo nkunim abotire he.

english_verse: Everyone who wins the victory will be made into a pillar in the temple of my God, and they will stay there forever. I will write on each of them the name of my God and the name of his city. It is the new Jerusalem my God will send down from heaven. I will also write on them my own new name.
translation_verse: Nea obedi nkunim he, mɛma no ayɛ me Nyankopɔn asɔredane he fapem titire, na ommfiri mu bio da. Mɛtwerɛ me Nyankopɔn dini ne me Nyankopɔn kuro dini a ɔyɛ Yerusalem foforɔ a obefiri soro me Nyankopɔn nkyɛne aba he agu ne ho. Bio, mɛtwerɛ me dini foforɔ agu ne ho.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. The Letter to Laodicea
translation_verse: Nea ɔwɔ aso he ntie nea Honhom he ka kyerɛ asafo ahodoɔ he. Krataa a ɔtwerɛ kɔmaa Laodikea asafo he

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Laodicea: I am the one called Amen! I am the faithful and true witness and the source of God's creation. Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: “Twerɛ krataa kɔma Laodikea asafo mu bɔfoɔ he sɛ: Yei ne asɛm a ofiri Amen a ɔyɛ danseni nɔkwafoɔ a ɔyɛ bibiaa a Nyankopɔn bɔeɛ he abɔseɛ nkyɛne.

english_verse: I know everything you have done, and you are not cold or hot. I wish you were either one or the other.
translation_verse: “Menim wo nnwuma. Wo a wonyɛ nwunu, wo nso a wonyɛ hye. Sɛ woyɛ nwunu anaa hye a, nka ɔyɛ!

english_verse: But since you are lukewarm and neither cold nor hot, I will spit you out of my mouth.
translation_verse: Nso esiane sɛ woyɛ botrobodwo na wonyɛ hye, wo so a wonyɛ nwunu nti, mɛfe wo afiri m’anum.

english_verse: You claim to be rich and successful and to have everything you need. But you don't know how bad off you really are. You are pitiful, poor, blind, and naked.
translation_verse: Woka sɛ, ‘Meyɛ defoɔ na maanya me ho, na mewɔ nea ohia me nyinaa.’ Nso wonnim hia a ahia wo ne mmɔbɔ a woyɛ. Woyɛ hiani. Woda adagya, na w’ani nso afira.

english_verse: Buy your gold from me. It has been refined in a fire, and it will make you rich. Buy white clothes from me. Wear them and you can cover up your shameful nakedness. Buy medicine for your eyes, so you will be able to see.
translation_verse: Nti metu wo fo sɛ tɔ sikakɔkɔɔ a bɛabere ho gya mu, firi me nkyɛne sɛnea ɔbɛma woayɛ defoɔ. Afei tɔ ntaadeɛ fitaa hyɛ fa kata w’adagya a ama w’anim agu ase he so. Afei tɔ aniduro sra w’ani so sɛnea wobehu adeɛ.

english_verse: I correct and punish everyone I love. So make up your minds to turn away from your sins.
translation_verse: Mekɔ so twe bɛ a medɔ bɛ he nyinaa aso. Nti bɔ mmɔdene na sakra w’adwene.

english_verse: Listen! I am standing and knocking at your door. If you hear my voice and open the door, I will come in and we will eat together.
translation_verse: Hwɛ! Megyina pono he akyi ɔɔbɔ mu, sɛ bi te me nne na obie pono he a, mɛba mu na me ne no abedidi na ɔno nso ne me adidi.

english_verse: Everyone who wins the victory will sit with me on my throne, just as I won the victory and sat with my Father on his throne.
translation_verse: Mɛma nkunimdifoɔ he atena m’ahennwa he so sɛnea madi nkunim nti mete m’Agya ahennwa so seesei he.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
translation_verse: Ma nea ɔwɔ aso he ntie nea Honhom he ka kyerɛ asafo ahodoɔ he.”

english_verse: After this, I looked and saw a door that opened into heaven. Then the voice that had spoken to me at first and that sounded like a trumpet said, “Come up here! I will show you what must happen next.”
translation_verse: Yei akyi he, mehwɛeɛ, na hwɛ, mehuu soro pono sɛ abie. Na nne bi teaam sɛ totorobɛnto a mate sɛ bi akasa akyerɛ me pɛn he; na ɔkaa sɛ, “Bra soro ha, na mɛkyerɛ wo nea ɔbɛba yei akyi.”

english_verse: Right then the Spirit took control of me, and there in heaven I saw a throne and someone sitting on it.
translation_verse: Prɛko pɛ! Na meya honhom mu. Na hwɛ, na bɛde ahennwa bi asi soro a bi te so.

english_verse: The one who was sitting there sparkled like precious stones of jasper and carnelian. A rainbow that looked like an emerald surrounded the throne.
translation_verse: Na nea ɔte he anim hyerɛn te sɛ ahwehwɛboɔ ne bogyanamboɔ. Na nyankontɔno a ɔte sɛ ahabamonoboɔ atwa ahennwa he ho ahyia.

english_verse: Twenty-four other thrones were in a circle around that throne. And on each of these thrones there was an elder dressed in white clothes and wearing a gold crown.
translation_verse: Na ahennwa aduonu nnan atwa ahennwa he ho ahyia a mpanyimfoɔ aduonu nnan tete so a bɛhyehyɛ ntaadeɛ fitaa na sikakɔkɔɔ abotire bobɔ bɛ ti.

english_verse: Flashes of lightning and roars of thunder came out from the throne in the center of the circle. Seven torches, which are the seven spirits of God, were burning in front of the throne.
translation_verse: Na anyinam ne nne ahodoɔ ne apranaa boboom firi ahennwa he mu. Na nkanea nson a bɛasosɔ a ɔɔdɛre ya ahennwa he anim. Na yeinom ne Nyankopɔn ahonhom nson.

english_verse: Also in front of the throne was something that looked like a glass sea, clear as crystal. Around the throne in the center were four living creatures covered front and back with eyes.
translation_verse: Na bibi te sɛ ahwehwɛ po a ɔyɛ krɔgyee sɛ ahwehwɛ da ahennwa he anim. Na ateasefoɔ nnan a ani tuatua bɛ anim ne bɛ akyi atwa ahennwa he ho ahyia.

english_verse: The first creature was like a lion, the second one was like a bull, the third one had the face of a human, and the fourth was like a flying eagle.
translation_verse: Na teasefoɔ a odi kane he te sɛ gyata; nea ɔtɔ so mmienu he te sɛ nantwie ba; nea ɔtɔ so mmeɛnsa he anim te sɛ nipa; na nea ɔtɔ so nnan he te sɛ kɔdeɛ a ootu.

english_verse: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and their bodies were covered with eyes. Day and night they never stopped singing, “Holy, holy, holy is the Lord, the all-powerful God, who was and is and is coming!”
translation_verse: Na ateasefoɔ nnan he mu biaa wɔ ntabane nsia a ani tuatua akyire ne animu nyinaa. Anadwo ne awia nyinaa bɛto nnwom sɛ, “Kronkron, Kronkron, Kronkron ne Awurade Nyankopɔn Tumfoɔ a na ɔya hɔ tetentredee, na seesei nso ɔya hɔ, na ɔya hɔ daa.”

english_verse: The living creatures kept praising, honoring, and thanking the one who sits on the throne and who lives forever and ever.
translation_verse: Saa ateasefoɔ nnan he to nnwom de hyɛ nea ɔte ahennwa he so a ɔte hɔ daa daa he animuonyam de ma no anidie da no ase.

english_verse: “Our Lord and God, you are worthy to receive glory, honor, and power. You created all things, and by your decision they are and were created.”
translation_verse: “Yɛ Awurade ne Nyankopɔn; wofata sɛ wonya animuonyam, anidie ne tumi, efiri sɛ, wo ne wobɔɔ nnoɔma nyinaa, na wo pɛ mu nti na bɛya hɔ, ne bɛbɔeɛ.”

english_verse: In the right hand of the one sitting on the throne I saw a scroll that had writing on the inside and on the outside. And it was sealed in seven places.
translation_verse: Na mehuu nwoma bi ya deɛ ɔte ahennwa so he nsa nifa mu. Na bɛatwerɛ mu ne akyire nyinaa a na bɛde nsɔano nson asosɔ ano.

english_verse: I saw a mighty angel ask with a loud voice, “Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals?”
translation_verse: Na mehuu bɔfoɔ hoɔdenfoɔ bi a ɔde nne kɛseɛ kaa sɛ, “Hwane ne fata sɛ ɔte nsɔano he ano na obie nwoma he mu?”

english_verse: No one in heaven or on earth or under the earth was able to open the scroll or look inside it.
translation_verse: Na bɛannya biaa a ɔya soro anaa asaase so, anaa asaase ase a otumi biee nwoma he mu na ohwɛɛ mu.

english_verse: I cried hard because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or look inside it.
translation_verse: Mesuu paa efiri sɛ, bɛannya biaa a ɔfata sɛ obie nwoma he mu na ɔhwɛ mu.

english_verse: Then one of the elders said to me, “Stop crying and look! The one who is called both the ‘Lion from the Tribe of Judah’ and ‘King David's Great Descendant’ has won the victory. He will open the scroll and its seven seals.”
translation_verse: Afei mpanyimfoɔ he mu baako ka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Nsu. Hwɛ! Yuda abusuakuo mu Gyata a ɔyɛ Dawid aseni he adi nkunim, na ɔno ne betumi ate nsɔano nson he ano na wabie nwoma he mu.”

english_verse: Then I looked and saw a Lamb standing in the center of the throne surrounded by the four living creatures and the elders. The Lamb looked as if it had once been killed. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven spirits of God, sent out to all the earth.
translation_verse: Afei mehuu adwamaa bi sɛ ogyina ahennwa he mfimfini a ateasefoɔ nnan he ne mpanyimfoɔ he atwa ne ho ahyia. Wohwɛ a, na ɔte sɛ nea bɛaku no. Na ɔwɔ mmɛne nson ne ani nson a ogyina hɔ ma Nyankopɔn ahonhom nson a bɛasoma bɛ aba wiase he.

english_verse: The Lamb went over and took the scroll from the right hand of the one who sat on the throne.
translation_verse: Adwamaa he kɔgyee nwoma he firii nea ɔte ahennwa so he nsa nifa mu.

english_verse: Then they sang a new song, “You are worthy to receive the scroll and open its seals, because you were killed. And with your own blood you bought for God people from every tribe, language, nation, and race.
translation_verse: Bɛtoo nnwom foforɔ bi sɛ, “Wofata sɛ wofa nwoma he, na wote nsɔano he ano, efiri sɛ, bekuu wo, na bɛde wo mogyaa atɔ nnipa afiri abusua, kasa, mane ne nkurofoɔ ahodoɔ mu ama Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: You let them become kings and serve God as priests, and they will rule on earth.”
translation_verse: Woama bɛayɛ ahemmane ne asɔfoɔ ama yɛ Nyankopɔn, na bebedi asaase so ahene.”

english_verse: As I looked, I heard the voices of a large number of angels around the throne and the voices of the living creatures and of the elders. There were millions and millions of them,
translation_verse: Bio mehwɛeɛ, na metee abɔfoɔ mpempem buburugyaa nka. Na bɛatwa ahennwa he ne ateasefoɔ nnan he ne mpanyimfoɔ he ho ahyia

english_verse: and they were saying in a loud voice, “The Lamb who was killed is worthy to receive power, riches, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and praise.”
translation_verse: a na bɛɛto nnwom dendene sɛ, “Adwamaa a bekuu no he fata sɛ ɔgye tumi, ahonya, nyansa ne ahoɔdene ne anidie ne animuonyam ne nhyira!”

english_verse: Then I heard all beings in heaven and on the earth and under the earth and in the sea offer praise. Together, all of them were saying, “Praise, honor, glory, and strength forever and ever to the one who sits on the throne and to the Lamb!”
translation_verse: Na metee sɛ abɔdeɛ a ɔya soro, asaase so, asaase ase, po mu ne dea ɔya wiase nyinaa ɔɔto nnwom sɛ, “Nhyira ne anidie ne animuonyam ne tumi nka nea ɔte ahennwa he so ne adwamaa he daa daa!”

english_verse: The four living creatures said “Amen,” while the elders knelt down and worshiped.
translation_verse: Ateasefoɔ nnan he gyee so sɛ, “Amen!” Na mpanyimfoɔ he butubutuiɛ, na bɛkoto sɔreeɛ.

english_verse: At the same time I saw the Lamb open the first of the seven seals, I heard one of the four living creatures shout with a voice like thunder. It said, “Come out!”
translation_verse: Afei mehuu sɛ adwamaa he ate nsɔano nson he mu nea odi kane he ano, na metee sɛ ateasefoɔ nnan he mu baako de nne a ɔte sɛ apranaa ɔɔka sɛ, “Bra!”

english_verse: Then I saw a white horse. Its rider carried a bow and was given a crown. He had already won some victories, and he went out to win more.
translation_verse: Afei mehwɛeɛ, na hwɛ, na pɔnkɔ fitaa bi di. Na deɛ ɔte ne so he kita agyane, na bɛmaa no ahenkyɛ. Ofiri adi sɛ nkunimdifoɔ a ookodi nkunim bio.

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, “Come out!”
translation_verse: Na ɔtee nsɔano a ɔto so mmienu he ano, na metee sɛ teasefoɔ a ɔtɔ so mmienu he ɔɔka sɛ, “Bra!”

english_verse: Then another horse came out. It was fiery red. And its rider was given the power to take away all peace from the earth, so people would slaughter one another. He was also given a big sword.
translation_verse: Afei pɔnkɔ foforɔ bi a ɔyɛ kɔkɔɔ pie baeɛ. Bɛmaa nea ɔte ne so he tumi sɛ ɔmfa kõ mmra asaase so, na nnipa nkuku bɛ ho. Bɛmaa no nkrantɛ kɛseɛ bi.

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, “Come out!” Then I saw a black horse, and its rider had a balance scale in one hand.
translation_verse: Na ɔtee nsɔano a ɔtɔ so mmeɛnsa he ano, na metee sɛ teasefoɔ a ɔtɔ so mmeɛnsa he ɔɔka sɛ, “Bra!” Mehwɛeɛ, na hwɛ, pɔnkɔ tuntum bi di. Na nea ɔte ne so he kita nsania ya ne nsam.

english_verse: I heard what sounded like a voice from somewhere among the four living creatures. It said, “A liter of wheat will cost you a whole day's wages! Three liters of barley will cost you a day's wages too. But don't ruin the olive oil or the wine.”
translation_verse: Metee bibi te sɛ nne bi firi ateasefoɔ nnan he nkyɛne. Na nne he kaa sɛ, “Ayuo susukora yɛ paani da koro akatua, na aburo susukora mmeɛnsa nso yɛ paani da koro akatua. Nso, nsɛe ngo he ne nsa he!”

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, “Come out!”
translation_verse: Afei adwamaa he tee nsɔano a ɔtɔ so nnan he ano, na metee sɛ teasefoɔ a ɔtɔ so nnan he ɔɔka sɛ, “Bra!”

english_verse: Then I saw a pale green horse. Its rider was named Death, and Death's Kingdom followed close behind. They were given power over one fourth of the earth, and they could kill its people with swords, famines, diseases, and wild animals.
translation_verse: Mehwɛeɛ na hwɛ, pɔnkɔ nsõnsõ bi di. Bɛtoo ne sotefoɔ he dini sɛ Wuo. Na Asamando nso di n’akyi pɛɛ. Bɛmaa bɛ asaase nkyemu nnan mu baako so tumi sɛ bɛmfa afena ne kɔm ne wuo ne asaase so nkekaboa nkuku bɛ.

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of everyone who had been killed for speaking God's message and telling about their faith.
translation_verse: Afei ɔtee nsɔano a ɔto so num he ano. Na mehuu bɛ a Nyankopɔn asɛm ne bɛ adanseɛ a bediiɛ nti bɛakuku bɛ he akra, afɔrebokyia he ase.

english_verse: They shouted, “Master, you are holy and faithful! How long will it be before you judge and punish the people of this earth who killed us?”
translation_verse: Bɛde nne a ano ɔdene kaa sɛ, “O Awurade Tumfoɔ, kronkronni ne nɔkwafoɔ! Wobɛtwentwɛne wo nane ase akosi da bɛ na woabu atɛne atua nnipa a bɛya asaase so ka yɛ mogyaa ho?”

english_verse: Then each of those who had been killed was given a white robe and told to rest for a little while. They had to wait until the complete number of the Lord's other servants and followers would be killed.
translation_verse: Bɛmaa bɛ mu biaa ataade tentene fitaa, na bɛka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ bɛntwɛne kakra nkosi sɛ bɛ mfɛfoɔ asomfo ne bɛ nuanom a bebekuku bɛ sɛnea bekuu bɛ he bɛba abɛka bɛ ho ama bɛ dodoɔ awie pɛyɛ.

english_verse: When I saw the Lamb open the sixth seal, I looked and saw a great earthquake. The sun turned as dark as sackcloth, and the moon became red as blood.
translation_verse: Na mehuu sɛ ɔtee nsɔano a ɔtɔ so nsia he ano. Na asaasewosoɔ a ano ɔdene ba maa awia yɛɛ tumm sɛ birisie, na bosome yɛɛ kɔɔ sɛ mogyaa.

english_verse: The stars in the sky fell to earth, just like figs shaken loose by a windstorm.
translation_verse: Na nsoromma tete firi soro bɛhwee asaase so te sɛ borɔdoma a mframa kɛseɛ woso no tete n’aba bunu gu fam.

english_verse: Then the sky was rolled up like a scroll, and all mountains and islands were moved from their places.
translation_verse: Afei soro yeraeɛ te sɛ nwoma a bɛabobɔ, na mmepɔ, ne nsupɔ firii baabi a na bɛyeya.

english_verse: The kings of the earth, its famous people, and its military leaders hid in caves or behind rocks on the mountains. They hid there together with the rich and the powerful and with all the slaves and free people.
translation_verse: Na asaase so ahemfo ne nnipa akɛseɛ ne asafohene ne adefoɔ ne ahoɔdenfoɔ ne nkoa ne adehyeɛ nyinaa hintahintaa abodane ne mmepɔ mu abotane mu.

english_verse: Then they shouted to the mountains and the rocks, “Fall on us! Hide us from the one who sits on the throne and from the anger of the Lamb.
translation_verse: Na bɛka kyerɛɛ mmepɔ ne abotane he sɛ, “Honkata yɛ so na homfa yɛ nsie sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a nea ɔte ahennwa he so he ani onhu yɛ na yemfiri Adwamaa he abufuo ano,

english_verse: That terrible day has come! God and the Lamb will show their anger, and who can face it?”
translation_verse: efiri sɛ n’abufuo da kɛseɛ he aduru; na hwane ne betumi agyina ano?”

english_verse: “Don't harm the earth or the sea or any tree! Wait until I have marked the foreheads of the servants of our God.”
translation_verse: Bɔfoɔ he kaa sɛ, “Honnsɛe asaase ne po anaa nnua kosi sɛ yɛbɛhyɛ Nyankopɔn nkoa he nso bɛ moma so.”

english_verse: Then I heard how many people had been marked on the forehead. There were
translation_verse: Na bɛkyerɛɛ me bɛ a bɛahyɛ bɛ nsõ he dodoɔ. Na bɛ nyinaa bɛ dodoɔ yɛ mpemha aduannan nnan a, befiri Israel mmusuakuo nyinaa mu.

english_verse: and said, “Amen! Praise, glory, wisdom, thanks, honor, power, and strength belong to our God forever and ever! Amen!”
translation_verse: kaa sɛ, “Amen! Nhyira ne animuonyam ne nyansa ne aseda ne anidie ne tumi ne ahoɔdene yɛ Nyankopɔn dea daa daa! Amen!” Nkwagyeɛ a ɔnam adwammaa he so ba

english_verse: After this, I saw a large crowd with more people than could be counted. They were from every race, tribe, nation, and language, and they stood before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands,
translation_verse: Yei akyi he, mehwɛeɛ, na mehuu nnipakuo a biaa ntumi nkane bɛ dodoɔ a befiri amane ne mmusuakuo ne nkurofoɔ ne kasa nyinaa mu a begyinagyina ahennwa he ne Adwamaa he anim a bɛhyehyɛ ntaadeɛ fitaa kitakita berɛɛ.

english_verse: as they shouted, “Our God, who sits upon the throne, has the power to save his people, and so does the Lamb.”
translation_verse: Bɛteaam dendene sɛ, “Nkwagyeɛ firi yɛ Nyankopɔn a ɔte ahennwa he so ne Adwamaa he hɔ.”

english_verse: The angels who stood around the throne knelt in front of it with their faces to the ground. The elders and the four living creatures knelt there with them. Then they all worshiped God
translation_verse: Na abɔfoɔ he atwa ahennwa he ne mpanyimfoɔ he ne ateasefoɔ nnan he ho ahyia. Afei bebutubutuu ahennwa he anim, sɔree Nyankopɔn

english_verse: One of the elders asked me, “Do you know who these people are that are dressed in white robes? Do you know where they come from?”
translation_verse: Afei mpanyimfoɔ he mu baako bisaa me sɛ, “Hefoɔ ne bɛhyehyɛ ntaadeɛ fitaa yi, na befiri he?”

english_verse: “Sir,” I answered, “you must know.” Then he told me: “These are the ones who have gone through the great suffering. They have washed their robes in the blood of the Lamb and have made them white.
translation_verse: Mebuaa sɛ “Owura, wo ne wonim.” Ɔka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Yeinom ne bɛ a bɛafiri taa mu, na bɛde Adwamaa he mogyaa ahohoro bɛ ntaadeɛ ama ayɛ fitaa.

english_verse: And so they stand before the throne of God and worship him in his temple day and night. The one who sits on the throne will spread his tent over them.
translation_verse: Na yei nti ne begyina Nyankopɔn ahennwa anim a bɛɛsom awia ne anadwo ne hyiadane mu yi. Nea ɔbɛtena ahennwa he so he bɛbɔ bɛ ho bane.

english_verse: They will never hunger or thirst again, and they won't be troubled by the sun or any scorching heat.
translation_verse: Kɔm ɔnne bɛ bio. Awia anaa ahuhuro biaa ɔnnhye bɛ bio,

english_verse: “The Lamb in the center of the throne will be their shepherd. He will lead them to streams of life-giving water, and God will wipe all tears from their eyes.”
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, Adwamaa he a ɔya ahennwa he mfinimfini he bɛyɛ bɛ dwanhwɛfoɔ de bɛ akɔ nkwa asuti ho. Na Nyankopɔn bɛpopa bɛ aniwa mu nisuo nyinaa.”

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
translation_verse: Berɛ a ɔtee nsɔano a ɔto so nson he ano he, soro nyinaa tam dinn bɛyɛ dɔnhwere fa.

english_verse: I noticed that the seven angels who stood before God were each given a trumpet.
translation_verse: Afei mehuu abɔfoɔ nson a begyinagyina Nyankopɔn anim. Bɛmaa bɛ ntotorobɛnto nson.

english_verse: Another angel, who had a gold container for incense, came and stood at the altar. This one was given a lot of incense to offer with the prayers of God's people on the gold altar in front of the throne.
translation_verse: Bɔfoɔ foforɔ a na okita sikakɔkɔɔ aduhwam adaka begyinaa afɔrebokyia he ho. Bɛmaa no aduhwam bebree sɛ ɔmfa mmɔ mpaeɛ nka Nyankopɔn nkurofoɔ he deɛ ho na ɔmmɔ sikakɔkɔɔ afɔrebokyia a osi ahennwa he anim he so afɔdeɛ.

english_verse: Then the smoke of the incense, together with the prayers of God's people, went up to God from the hand of the angel.
translation_verse: Aduhwam he hua ne Nyankopɔn nkurofoɔ mpaeɛ firi bɔfoɔ he nsam kɔɔ Nyankopɔn anim.

english_verse: After this, the angel filled the incense container with fire from the altar and threw it on the earth. Thunder roared, lightning flashed, and the earth shook. The Trumpets
translation_verse: Afei bɔfoɔ he faa aduhwam adaka he de gya a ofiri afɔrebokyia he so hyɛɛ no ma too twenee asaase so. Na apranaa ne nne ahoroɔ ne anyinam ne asaasewosoɔ baeɛ.

english_verse: The seven angels now got ready to blow their trumpets.
translation_verse: Na abɔfoɔ nson a bekitakita ntotorobɛnto nson he siesiee bɛ ho sɛ bɛɛbɛhyɛne.

english_verse: When the first angel blew his trumpet, hail and fire mixed with blood were thrown down on the earth. A third of the earth, a third of the trees, and a third of all green plants were burned.
translation_verse: Bɔfoɔ a odi kane he hyɛne ne totorobɛnto he. Na asukɔtwea ne gya a ɔne mogyaa adi afra tɔ guu asaase so. Asaase nkyekyɛmu mmeɛnsa mu baako hyeeɛ. Nnua nkyekyɛmu mmeɛnsa mu baako hyeeɛ, na nwura mono nyinaa nso hyeeɛ.

english_verse: When the second angel blew his trumpet, something like a great fiery mountain was thrown into the sea. A third of the sea turned to blood,
translation_verse: Afei bɔfoɔ a ɔtɔ so mmienu he hyɛnee ne totorobɛnto he. Na bɛtoo bibi a ɔte sɛ bepɔ kɛseɛ a ɔɔdɛre too po mu.

english_verse: a third of the living creatures in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
translation_verse: Po he nkyekyɛmu mmeɛnsa mu baako danee mogyaa. Na abɔdeɛ a bɛwɔ nkwa a bɛya po mu he, bemu nkyekyɛmu mmeɛnsa mu baako wuwuiɛ maa ahyɛne mu nkyekyɛmu mmeɛnsa mu baako nso sɛeɛ.

english_verse: When the third angel blew his trumpet, a great star fell from heaven. It was burning like a torch, and it fell on a third of the rivers and on a third of the springs of water.
translation_verse: Afei bɔfoɔ a ɔto so mmeɛnsa he hyɛnee ne totorobɛnto he. Na nsoromma kɛseɛ bi a ɔɔdɛre sɛ gyatanaa te firii soro bɛhwee nsuo nkyekyɛmu mmeɛnsa mu baako mu.

english_verse: The name of the star was Bitter, and a third of the water turned bitter. Many people died because the water was so bitter.
translation_verse: Nsoromma he dini de Nwononwono. Nsuo he nkyekyɛmu mmeɛnsa mu baako yɛɛ nwono, na nnipa a bɛnomeeɛ he mu bebree wuwuiɛ, efiri sɛ, na ayɛ nwono.

english_verse: When the fourth angel blew his trumpet, a third of the sun, a third of the moon, and a third of the stars were struck. They each lost a third of their light. So during a third of the day there was no light, and a third of the night was also without light.
translation_verse: Afei bɔfoɔ a ɔto so nnan he hyɛnee ne totorobɛnto he. Na bɛbɔɔ awia nkyekyɛmu mmeɛnsa mu baako ne bosome nkyekyɛmu mmeɛnsa mu baako, ne nsoromma nkyekyem mmeɛnsa mu baako, na biaa nkyekyɛmu mmeɛnsa mu baako he duduruu sum, na adekyeɛ mu nkyekyem mmeɛnsa mu baako anhyerɛn, na anadwo nso saa.

english_verse: Then I looked and saw a lone eagle flying across the sky. It was shouting, “Trouble, trouble, trouble to everyone who lives on earth! The other three angels are now going to blow their trumpets.”
translation_verse: Na mehuu kɔdeɛ bi sɛ a watu nam sorosoro, na metee sɛ ɔɔteam sɛ, “Bɛ a bɛte asaase so he nnue; honnue, honnue ɔnam totorobɛnto a aka a abɔfoɔ mmeɛnsa he ɔɔbɛhyɛne he nti.”

english_verse: When the fifth angel blew his trumpet, I saw a star fall from the sky to earth. It was given the key to the tunnel that leads down to the deep pit.
translation_verse: Afei bɔfoɔ a ɔto so nnum he hyɛnee ne totorobɛnto he, na mehuu nsoromma bi sɛ ofiri soro abɛhwe asaase so. Bɛmaa no amena donkodonko he ano safoa.

english_verse: As it opened the tunnel, smoke poured out like the smoke of a great furnace. The sun and the air turned dark because of the smoke.
translation_verse: Nsoromma he biee amena donkodonko he, na wusie kumoo firi mu sɛ fononoo kɛseɛ bi, na wusie he maa awia hann ne mframa a ɔya hɔ he yɛɛ tumm.

english_verse: Locusts came out of the smoke and covered the earth. They were given the same power that scorpions have.
translation_verse: Ntutummɛ firii wusie he mu baa asaase so, na bɛmaa bɛ tumi a ɔte sɛ asaase so naanantwi tumi.

english_verse: The locusts were told not to harm the grass on the earth or any plant or any tree. They were to punish only those people who did not have God's mark on their foreheads.
translation_verse: Afei bɛka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ bɛnsɛe serɛ anaa nnua anaa afideɛ biaa. Na mmom nnipa a Nyankopɔn ahyɛnsodeɛ he bi nni bɛ moma so he deɛ, betumi ha bɛ.

english_verse: The locusts were allowed to make them suffer for five months, but not to kill them. The suffering they caused was like the sting of a scorpion.
translation_verse: Na bɛamma ntutummɛ he kwane sɛ bekuku saa nnipa he, na mmom bɛnha bɛ abosome nnum. Bɛka bɛ a, bɛ ano yeyaa he te sɛ naanantwi ano yeyaa.

english_verse: In those days people will want to die, but they will not be able to. They will hope for death, but it will escape from them.
translation_verse: Saa bosome num he mu he, saa nnipa he bɛpere wuo, nso bennya. Bɛbɛpɛ sɛ bewu, nso wuo bedwane afiri bɛ nkyɛne.

english_verse: These locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads they wore something like gold crowns, and they had human faces.
translation_verse: Ntutummɛ he te sɛ apɔnkɔ a bɛasiesie bɛ ama ko. Bɛwɔ bibi te sɛ sikakɔkɔɔ ahenkyɛ hyɛ bɛ ti. Na bɛ anim te sɛ nnipa anim.

english_verse: Their hair was like a woman's long hair, and their teeth were like those of a lion.
translation_verse: Bɛ tinwi te sɛ mmaa tinwi, na bɛ sẽ nso te sɛ gyata sẽ.

english_verse: On their chests they wore armor made of iron. Their wings roared like an army of horse-drawn chariots rushing into battle.
translation_verse: Bɛde bibi te sɛ dadeɛ nkataboɔ akatakata bɛ bo. Na bɛ ntabane yɛ dede te sɛ apɔnkɔ pii a bɛɛtwe nteaseɛnam akɔ ko.

english_verse: Their tails were like a scorpion's tail with a stinger that had the power to hurt someone for five months.
translation_verse: Bɛwɔ dua a ne nwowɔeɛ te sɛ naanantwi. Na bɛ dua he ne maa bɛ tumi a bɛde ha nnipa bosome num he.

english_verse: Their king was the angel in charge of the deep pit. In Hebrew his name was Abaddon, and in Greek it was Apollyon.
translation_verse: Bɛwɔ hene a odi bɛ so a ɔno ne bɔfoɔ a ɔhwɛ amena donkodonko he so. Ne dini ya Hebri kasa mu ne Abadon; na Hela kasa mu nso ɔde Apolion .

english_verse: The first horrible thing has now happened! But wait. Two more horrible things will happen soon.
translation_verse: Haw baako atwam. Yei akyi he, haw mmienu bi bɛba.

english_verse: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet. I heard a voice speak from the four corners of the gold altar that stands in the presence of God.
translation_verse: Afei bɔfoɔ a ɔtɔ so nsia he hyɛne ne totorobɛnto he, na metee nne bi firii sikakɔkɔɔ afɔrebokyia a osi Nyankopɔn anim he mmɛne he mu.

english_verse: The voice spoke to this angel and said, “Release the four angels who are tied up beside the great Euphrates River.”
translation_verse: Nne he ka kyerɛɛ bɔfoɔ a ɔto so nsia a okita totorobɛnto he sɛ, “Sansane abɔfoɔ nnan a bɛakyekyere bɛ asubɔntene Eufrate ho he.”

english_verse: The four angels had been prepared for this very hour and day and month and year. Now they were set free to kill a third of all people.
translation_verse: Besansanee abɔfoɔ nnan he a bɛasiesie bɛ ama saa dɔnhwere yi, saa da yi, saa bosome yi ne saa afe yi sɛ benkuku adasamma mu nkyekyɛmu mmeɛnsa mu baako.

english_verse: By listening, I could tell there were more than
translation_verse: Na metee sɛ dɔm a bɛtete apɔnkɔ so he ano si mpem du ahodoɔ mpem aduonu.

english_verse: In my vision their riders wore fiery-red, dark-blue, and yellow armor on their chests. The heads of the horses looked like lions, with fire and smoke and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
translation_verse: Na m’anisoadehunu he mu he, mehuu apɔnkɔ he ne bɛ akafoɔ. Na bɛ nkataboɔ he ahosu te sɛ gya, tumm te sɛ afasebiri, na ɔte sɛ sɔfe nso. Na apɔnkɔ he ti te sɛ gyata ti a gya, wusie ne sɔfe firi bɛ anum.

english_verse: One third of all people were killed by the three terrible troubles caused by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur.
translation_verse: Saa haw ahodoɔ mmeɛnsa he a ɔyɛ gya, wusie ne sɔfe he a ofiri apɔnkɔ he anum baeɛ he kuu adasamma nkyekyɛmu mmeɛnsa mu baako,

english_verse: The horses had powerful mouths, and their tails were like poisonous snakes that bite and hurt.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, na apɔnkɔ he tumi nyinaa firi bɛ anum ne bɛ dua. Bɛ dua te sɛ awɔ a bɛwɔ ti a ɔno ne na bɛde pirepira nnipa.

english_verse: The people who lived through these terrible troubles did not turn away from the idols they had made, and they did not stop worshiping demons. They kept on worshiping idols that were made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood. Not one of these idols could see, hear, or walk.
translation_verse: Adasamma a bɛkaeɛ he a haw he anku bɛ he antwe bɛ ho amfiri bɛ nnwuma ho. Bɛannyae ahohommɔne ne ahoni ne sika ne dwetɛ ne ayowa ne aboɔ ne nnua a onhu adeɛ na ɔnte asɛm na ontumi nnante he som.

english_verse: No one stopped murdering or practicing witchcraft or being immoral or stealing.
translation_verse: Saa nso ne bɛansakra bɛ adwene amfiri bɛ awudie, bɛ adutoɔ, bɛ adwamammɔ ne bɛ korɔnobɔ ho.

english_verse: I saw another powerful angel come down from heaven. This one was covered with a cloud, and a rainbow was over his head. His face was like the sun, his legs were like columns of fire,
translation_verse: Afei mehuu bɔfoɔ tumfoɔ bi a ofiri soro oosiane ba fam. Na ɔhyɛ mununkum mu a nyankontɔno bɔ ne ti. Na n’anim te sɛ awia, na ne nane te sɛ gya fadum.

english_verse: and with his hand he held a little scroll that had been unrolled. He stood there with his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
translation_verse: Na nwoma ketewa bi kita no a wabie mu. Ɔde ne nane nifa sii po so a na benkum he nso si asaase so.

english_verse: Then he shouted with a voice that sounded like a growling lion. Thunder roared seven times.
translation_verse: Ɔteaam maa ne nne woroo so sɛ gyata. Ɔfrɛeɛ he, apranaa nson he woroo so gyee so.

english_verse: After the thunder stopped, I was about to write what it had said. But a voice from heaven shouted, “Keep it secret! Don't write these things.”
translation_verse: Bɛka saa pɛ, na mepɛ sɛ metwerɛ. Nso metee nne bi firi soro a ɔkaa sɛ, “Ma nsɛm a apranaa nson he kaeɛ he nka wo tim. Ntwerɛ!”

english_verse: The angel I had seen standing on the sea and the land then held his right hand up toward heaven.
translation_verse: Hɔaa, bɔfoɔ a mehuu sɛ ogyina hɔ a ne nane baako si po so na baako nso si asaase so he maa ne nsa nifa so kɔɔ soro,

english_verse: He made a promise in the name of God who lives forever and who created heaven, earth, the sea, and every living creature. The angel said, “You won't have to wait any longer.
translation_verse: na ɔde deɛ ɔte hɔ daa daa a ɔbɔɔ soro ne asaase ne po ne nea ɔya mu nyinaa he dini kaa ntam. Bɔfoɔ he kaa sɛ, “Bɛntwentwɛne bɛ nana ase bio.

english_verse: God told his secret plans to his servants the prophets, and it will all happen by the time the seventh angel sounds his trumpet.”
translation_verse: Na sɛ bɔfoɔ a ɔto so nson he hyɛ ne totorobɛnto he a, ne Nyankopɔn bewie n’ahintasɛm mu nhyehyɛeɛ he, sɛnea ɔka kyerɛɛ n’asomfoɔ a bɛyɛ adiyifoɔ he.” Bɔfoɔ he nwoma

english_verse: Once again the voice from heaven spoke to me. It said, “Go and take the open scroll from the hand of the angel standing on the sea and the land.”
translation_verse: Afei nne a metee sɛ ofiri soro he kasa kyerɛɛ me bio sɛ, “Kɔ na kɔgye nwoma a bɛabie mu a okita bɔfoɔ a ogyina po ne asaase so he.”

english_verse: When I went over to ask the angel for the little scroll, the angel said, “Take the scroll and eat it! Your stomach will turn sour, but the taste in your mouth will be sweet as honey.”
translation_verse: Mekɔɔ bɔfoɔ he nkyɛne kɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ ɔmfa nwoma ketewa he mma me. Ɔka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Gye na we. Ɔbɛkeka wo yam, nso w’anum deɛ, ɔbɛyɛ dɛ te sɛ woɔ.”

english_verse: I took the little scroll from the hand of the angel and ate it. The taste was sweet as honey, but my stomach turned sour.
translation_verse: Megyee nwoma ketewa he firii ne nsam weeɛ, na ɔyɛɛ dɛ te sɛ woɔ ya m’anum. Nso memeneeɛ he akyi, ɔkekaa me yam.

english_verse: Then some voices said, “Keep on telling what will happen to the people of many nations, races, and languages, and also to kings.”
translation_verse: Afei bɛka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Ɔsɛ sɛ woka nkurofoɔ ne amane ne kasa ne ahemfo bebree ho adiyisɛm bio.”
