english_verse: But don't measure the courtyard outside the temple building. Leave it out. It has been given to those people who don't know God, and they will trample all over the holy city for
translation_verse: Nso mfa asɔredane he mfikyiri nka ho, efiri sɛ, bɛde hɔ ama amanamane mufoɔ, na bebetiatia kuro kronkron he so abosome aduannan mmienu.

english_verse: My two witnesses will wear sackcloth, while I let them preach for
translation_verse: Mɛsoma m’adansefoɔ mmienu a befirafira ayitoma, na bɛaka adiyisɛm nna apem ahanu ne aduosia.”

english_verse: These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand in the presence of the Lord who rules the earth.
translation_verse: Yeinom ne ngodua mmienu he ne kaneadua mmienu a osisi asaase a ɔya Awurade anim he so he.

english_verse: Any enemy who tries to harm them will be destroyed by the fire that comes out of their mouths.
translation_verse: Gya firi bɛ anum bekuku atamfoɔ biaa a bɛpɛ sɛ bɛha bɛ he. Ɔno nti, biaa a ɔpɛ sɛ ɔha bɛ he, bebeku no.

english_verse: They have the power to lock up the sky and to keep rain from falling while they are prophesying. And whenever they want to, they can turn water to blood and cause all kinds of terrible troubles on earth.
translation_verse: Yeinom wɔ tumi sɛ bɛto soro mu, na nsuo ɔnntɔ ya nna a bɛde hyɛ nkɔm he mu, na bɛwɔ tumi ya nsuo so sɛ bɛma ɔdane mogyaa na bɛde haw nyinaa bi ha asaase berɛ dodoɔ biaa bɛ pɛ.

english_verse: After the two witnesses have finished preaching God's message, the beast that lives in the deep pit will come up and fight against them. It will win the battle and kill them.
translation_verse: Na sɛ bɛka bɛ asɛm he wie a, aboa a obefiri amena donkodonko he mu apie he ne bɛ bɛko. Obedi bɛ so aku bɛ.

english_verse: Their bodies will be left lying in the streets of the same great city where their Lord was nailed to a cross. And this city is spiritually like the city of Sodom or the country of Egypt.
translation_verse: Na bɛ afunu bɛdeda kuro kɛseɛ he abɔntene so, kuro a honhom mu bɛfrɛ no Sodom ne Misraim, baabi a bɛbɔɔ bɛ Awurade nso asennua mu he

english_verse: For three and a half days the people of every nation, tribe, language, and race will stare at the bodies of these two witnesses and refuse to let them be buried.
translation_verse: Na nnipa a befiri amane so ne mmusuakuo ne kasa hodoɔ ne amane bi so bɛhwɛ bɛ afunu he nnansa ne fa, na bɛmma kwane mma bensie bɛ.

english_verse: Everyone on earth will celebrate and be happy. They will give gifts to each other, because of what happened to the two prophets who caused them so much trouble.
translation_verse: Asaase so nnipa ani bɛgye sɛ saa nnipa mmienu he awuwu. Bɛbɛgye bɛ ani na bɛakyɛ bɛ ho akyɛdeɛ, efiri sɛ, saa adiyifoɔ mmienu he ne bɛde haw bɛtoo asaase sofoɔ so.

english_verse: But three and a half days later, God will breathe life into their bodies. They will stand up, and everyone who sees them will be terrified.
translation_verse: Na nnansa ne fa akyi he, honhom a ofiri Nyankopɔn bewuraa bɛ mu na bɛsɔre gyinaeɛ. Na hu kɛseɛ kãã biaa a ohuu bɛ.

english_verse: The witnesses then heard a loud voice from heaven, saying, “Come up here.” And while their enemies were watching, they were taken up to heaven in a cloud.
translation_verse: Afei nne a ano ɔdene firi soro bɛkaa sɛ, “Homforo mmra soro ha.” Na berɛ a na bɛ atamfoɔ ɔɔhwɛ bɛ he, bɛfaa mununkum mu kɔɔ soro.

english_verse: At that same moment there was a terrible earthquake that destroyed a tenth of the city. Seven thousand people were killed, and the rest were frightened and praised the God who rules in heaven.
translation_verse: Saa mmerɛ heaa, asaase wosoo dendenndene, kuro he nkyekyɛmu du mu baako sɛeɛ, na nnipa mpemnson wuwuiɛ ɔnam asaasewosoɔ he nti. Nnipa a bɛaka he bɔɔ hu na bɛkamfoo Nyankopɔn a ɔya soro he kɛseyɛ.

english_verse: The second horrible thing has now happened! And the third one will be here soon. The Seventh Trumpet
translation_verse: Haw a ɔtɔ so mmienu he atwam, nso, hwɛ, ɔnkyɛre na haw a ɔto so mmeɛnsa nso aba. Totorobɛnto a ɔto so nson

english_verse: At the sound of the seventh trumpet, loud voices were heard in heaven. They said, “Now the kingdom of this world belongs to our Lord and to his Chosen One! And he will rule forever and ever!”
translation_verse: Afei bɔfoɔ a ɔto so nson he hyɛne ne totorobɛnto he, na nne dendene bi firi soro kaa sɛ, “Wiase ahennie ayɛ yɛ Awurade ne ne Kristo he dea, na obedi hene daa daa.”

english_verse: They said, “Lord God All-Powerful, you are and you were, and we thank you. You used your great power and started ruling.
translation_verse: kaa sɛ: Yɛda wo ase, Awurade Nyankopɔn a woyɛ adeɛ nyinaa so Tumfoɔ a na woya hɔ firi tetentredee, na woya hɔ nnɛ nso, sɛ woafa wo tumi kɛseɛ, na woadi hene.

english_verse: When the nations got angry, you became angry too! Now the time has come for the dead to be judged. It is time for you to reward your servants the prophets and all your people who honor your name, no matter who they are. It is time to destroy everyone who has destroyed the earth.”
translation_verse: Na amanamane he bo fuiɛ, na w’abufuo ne awufoɔ berɛ he aduru sɛ wobebu atɛne, na woama wo nkoa, adiyifoɔ ne ahoteefoɔ ne bɛ a besuro wo dini, nketewa ne akɛseɛ nyinaa, he akatua, na woasɛe bɛ a bɛasɛe asaase he.

english_verse: The door to God's temple in heaven was then opened, and the sacred chest could be seen inside the temple. I saw lightning and heard roars of thunder. The earth trembled and huge hailstones fell to the ground.
translation_verse: Afei Nyankopɔn asɔredane a ɔya soro he bieeɛ ma behuu adaka a apam he ya mu he asɔredane he mu. Afei anyinam ne apranaa paepaeɛ, na asaase wosoeɛ, na asukɔtwea pii nso baeɛ.

english_verse: She was about to give birth, and she was crying because of the great pain.
translation_verse: Na ɔnyem a ɔɔbɛwo, na awoyeya nti, na oosu.

english_verse: Something else appeared in the sky. It was a huge red dragon with seven heads and ten horns, and a crown on each of its seven heads.
translation_verse: Afei nsɛnkyerɛnne kɛseɛ foforɔ bi daa ne ho adi soro. Wɔ kɔkɔɔ kakraka bi pieeɛ a na ne ti yɛ nson a na ɔwɔ mmɛne du; na ahemmotire bobɔ ne ti he biaa so.

english_verse: With its tail, it dragged a third of the stars from the sky and threw them down to the earth. Then the dragon turned toward the woman, because it wanted to eat her child as soon as it was born.
translation_verse: Ɔde ne dua twee nsoromma a ɔya soro he mu nkyemu mmeɛnsa mu baako too guu fam. Na ogyinaa baa a ɔɔwo he anim sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a ɔwo abɔfra he to hɔaa pɛ, ɔbɛfa abɔfra he awe.

english_verse: The woman gave birth to a son, who would rule all nations with an iron rod. The boy was snatched away. He was taken to God and placed on his throne.
translation_verse: Afei baa he woo abeemaa a ɔde dadeɛ poma bedi amane nyinaa so. Nso behwim abɔfra he kɔɔ Nyankopɔn ne n’ahennwa ho.

english_verse: The woman ran into the desert to a place that God had prepared for her. There she would be taken care of for
translation_verse: Baa he dwane kɔɔ serɛ so baabi a Nyankopɔn asiesie ama no a bɛbɛhwɛ no hɔ nnafua apem ahanu ne aduosia mu. Mikael dii wɔ he so nkunim

english_verse: A war broke out in heaven. Michael and his angels were fighting against the dragon and its angels.
translation_verse: Afei ko sii soro hɔ. Mikael ne n’abɔfoɔ ko tiaa tweaseɛ kɛseɛ he, na wɔ kɛseɛ he nso ne n’abɔfoɔ ko tia bɛ.

english_verse: But the dragon lost the battle. It and its angels were forced out of their places in heaven
translation_verse: Nso bedii bɛ so nkunim na bɛamma ɔne n’abɔfoɔ antena soro bio.

english_verse: and were thrown down to the earth. Yes, that old snake and his angels were thrown out of heaven! That snake, who fools everyone on earth, is known as the devil and Satan.
translation_verse: Bɛtoo tweaseɛ kɛseɛ he tweneeɛ. Ɔno ne wɔ dadaa he a bɛfrɛ no Bronsam ne Satan he, ɔno ne daadaa wiase nyinaa he. Na bɛtoo no twenee asaase so a na n’abɔfoɔ nyinaa ka ne ho.

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven shout, “Our God has shown his saving power, and his kingdom has come! God's own Chosen One has shown his authority. Satan accused our people in the presence of God day and night. Now he has been thrown out!
translation_verse: Afei metee nne dendene bi soro a ɔkaa sɛ, “Afei Nyankopɔn nkwagyeɛ, ne tumi, n’ahennie ne ne Kristo he tumi he aba, efiri sɛ, bɛapam nea ogyina Nyankopɔn anim toto yɛ nuanom ano awia ne anadwo he afiri soro.

english_verse: “Our people defeated Satan because of the blood of the Lamb and the message of God. They were willing to give up their lives.
translation_verse: Nso bɛnam Adwamaa he mogyaa so ne bɛ adanseɛ asɛm adi no so nkunim, na bɛannɔ bɛ kra, mpo de kosii wuo mu.

english_verse: “The heavens should rejoice, together with everyone who lives there. But pity the earth and the sea, because the devil was thrown down to the earth. He knows his time is short, and he is very angry.”
translation_verse: Nti ho a hote soro homma ho ani nnye. Afei asaase ne po nnue! Efiri sɛ, Bronsam he atoa ho, na ne bo afu efiri sɛ, onim sɛ, ne mmerɛ a aka sua!” Tweaseɛ he tuu sa bio asaase so

english_verse: When the dragon realized it had been thrown down to the earth, it tried to make trouble for the woman who had given birth to a son.
translation_verse: Mmerɛ a tweaseɛ he huu sɛ bɛato no atwene asaase so he, ɔtaa baa a wawo abeemaa he.

english_verse: But the woman was given two wings like those of a huge eagle, so she could fly into the desert. There she would escape from the snake and be taken care of for a time, two times, and half a time.
translation_verse: Nso bɛmaa baa he kɔdeɛ ntabane akɛseɛ mmienu sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a obetumi atu akɔ serɛ so baabi a hɔ yɛ ne tenaberɛ, dea bɛbɛhwɛ no mfie mmeɛnsa ne fa sɛnea wɔ he oonni n’akyi bio.

english_verse: The snake then spewed out water like a river to sweep the woman away.
translation_verse: Afei wɔ he puu n’anum nsuo te sɛ asubɔntene guu baa he akyi, sɛ nka ɔmfa no.

english_verse: But the earth helped her and swallowed the water that had come from the dragon's mouth.
translation_verse: Nso asaase boaa baa he. Obiee n’anum nom nsuo a tweaseɛ he pu guu hɔ he nyinaa.

english_verse: This made the dragon terribly angry with the woman. So it started a war against the rest of her children. They are the people who obey God and are faithful to what Jesus did and taught.
translation_verse: Na tweaseɛ he bo fuu baa he, na okotuu n’asefoɔ nkaeɛ a bedi Nyankopɔn ahyɛdeɛ so na bekura Yesu adanseɛ he so sa.

english_verse: The dragon stood on the beach beside the sea.
translation_verse: Na okogyinaa mpoano anwea so.

english_verse: I looked and saw a beast coming up from the sea. This one had ten horns and seven heads, and a crown was on each of its ten horns. On each of its heads were names that were an insult to God.
translation_verse: Afei mehuu sɛ aboa bi apie po he mu ɔɔba. Na ɔwɔ mmɛne du ne tire nson a, na ahemmotire du bobɔ ne mmɛne he so a abusudini bobɔ tire he ho.

english_verse: The beast I saw had the body of a leopard, the feet of a bear, and the mouth of a lion. The dragon handed over its own power and throne and great authority to this beast.
translation_verse: Aboa a mehuu no he, na ɔte sɛ sebɔ a ne nane te sɛ sisire, na n’ano nso te sɛ gyata ano. Na tweaseɛ he de ɔno ankasa ne tumi ne n’ahennwa ne ne tumi kɛseɛ maa aboa he.

english_verse: One of its heads seemed to have been fatally wounded, but now it was well. Everyone on earth marveled at this beast,
translation_verse: Na mehuu ne ti he mu baako a ɔte sɛ deɛ bɛatwa ama ɔɔyɛ awu, nso bɛsaa ne pirakuro he, na aboa he ho yɛɛ wiasefoɔ nyinaa nwanwa maa bedii n’akyi.

english_verse: and they worshiped the dragon who had given its authority to the beast. They also worshiped the beast and said, “No one is like this beast! No one can fight against it.”
translation_verse: Nnipa nyinaa som tweaseɛ he efiri sɛ, ɔde ne tumi nyinaa ama aboa he. Bɛsom aboa he nso kaa sɛ, “Hwane ne te sɛ aboa he? Na hwane ne betumi ne no ako?”

english_verse: The beast was allowed to brag and claim to be God, and for
translation_verse: Bɛmaa aboa he kwane ma ɔkaa nsɛm akɛseɛ ne abususɛm, na bɛmaa no kwane ma onyaa tumi bosome aduannan mmienu.

english_verse: The beast cursed God, and it cursed the name of God. It even cursed the place where God lives, as well as everyone who lives in heaven with God.
translation_verse: Na obiee n’ano kaa abususɛm tiaa Nyankopɔn, sɛ ɔbɛka ne dini ne ne ntomadane ne bɛ a bɛtete soro he ho abususɛm.

english_verse: It was allowed to fight against God's people and defeat them. It was also given authority over the people of every tribe, nation, language, and race.
translation_verse: Bɛmaa no kwane maa ɔne ahoteefoɔ koeɛ sɛ obedi bɛ so. Na bɛmaa no mmusuakuo ne nkurofoɔ ne kasa hodoɔ ne amane nyinaa so tumi.

english_verse: The beast was worshiped by everyone whose name wasn't written before the time of creation in the book of the Lamb who was killed.
translation_verse: Na bɛ a bɛte asaase so nyinaa bɛkoto no, meekyerɛ bɛ a bɛamfiri wiase ahyɛaseɛ antwerɛ bɛ dini Adwammaa a bekuu no he nkwa nwoma mu he.

english_verse: If you have ears, then listen!
translation_verse: Sɛ bi wɔ aso a, ma no ntie.

english_verse: If you are doomed to be captured, you will be captured. If you are doomed to be killed by a sword, you will be killed by a sword. This means God's people must learn to endure and be faithful!
translation_verse: Biaa a ɔsɛ sɛ bɛkye no he, bɛbɛkye no. Biaa a ɔsɛ sɛ beku no nkrantɛ ano he, bebeku no. Yei mu ne ahoteefoɔ boasetɔ ne gyidie ho hia.

english_verse: I now saw another beast. This one came out of the ground. It had two horns like a lamb, but spoke like a dragon.
translation_verse: Afei mehuu aboa foforɔ a oopie afiri asaase mu. Na ɔwɔ mmɛne mmienu te sɛ adwamaa, nso na ɔkasa te sɛ wɔ.

english_verse: It worked for the beast whose fatal wound had been healed. And it used all its authority to force the earth and its people to worship that beast.
translation_verse: Na ɔwɔ aboa a odi kane he ahoɔdenneɛ nyinaa ya n’anim, na ɔmaa asaase ne bɛ a bɛtete so koto aboa a odi kane a bɛasa ne wuopira he.

english_verse: It worked mighty miracles, and while people watched, it even made fire come down from the sky.
translation_verse: Aboa a ɔto so mmienu yi yɔɔ anwanwadeɛ akɛseɛ bebree. Ɔmaa gya firii soro baa asaase so a nnipa nyinaa huiɛ.

english_verse: This second beast fooled people on earth by working miracles for the first one. Then it talked them into making an idol in the form of the beast that did not die after being wounded by a sword.
translation_verse: Na ɔnam anwanwadeɛ a bɛmaa no ho kwane maa no yɔeɛ ya aboa a odi kane he anim he so daadaa nnipa a bɛte asaase so nyinaa. Aboa he ka kyerɛɛ wiase nnipa nyinaa sɛ, bensi honyini mma aboa a bɛde nkrantɛ piraa no na nso n’apirakuro he awu na ɔte ase bio he.

english_verse: It was allowed to put breath into the idol, so it could speak. Everyone who refused to worship the idol of the beast was put to death.
translation_verse: Bɛmaa aboa a ɔto so mmienu he kwane ma ɔhomee nkwa guu aboa a odi kane honyini he mu, sɛnea ɔbɛyɛ a honyini he betumi akasa, na waku biaa a ɔnsom no he.

english_verse: All people were forced to put a mark on their right hand or forehead. Whether they were powerful or weak, rich or poor, free people or slaves,
translation_verse: Afei aboa he hyɛɛ nnipa nyinaa, nketewa ne akɛseɛ, adefoɔ ne ahiafoɔ, bɛ a bɛde bɛ ho ne nkoa sɛ bɛnhyɛ bɛ nsa nifa ho ne bɛ moma so agyinaeɛ.

english_verse: they all had to have this mark, or else they could not buy or sell anything. This mark stood for the name of the beast and for the number of its name.
translation_verse: Biaa nni ho kwane sɛ ɔtɔ anaa ɔtɔno gye sɛ bɛde aboa he dini anaa ne dini akonta ntwerɛeɛ he hyɛ ne ho agyinaeɛ.

english_verse: You need wisdom to understand the number of the beast! But if you are smart enough, you can figure this out. Its number is
translation_verse: Yei hia ma nimdeɛ. Biaa a ɔwɔ nimdeɛ he betumi ahu nsɛnkyerɛnne a ogyina hɔ ma aboa he ase, efiri sɛ, nsɛnkyerɛnne he gyina hɔ ma nipa dini. Ne nsɛnkyerɛnne yɛ ahansia aduosia nsia.

english_verse: Then I heard a sound from heaven that was like a roaring flood or loud thunder or even like the music of harps.
translation_verse: Na metee nne bi firi soro te sɛ nsuworoeɛ a ano ɔdene, na ɔte sɛ apranaa nso. Nne a meteeɛ he te sɛ nnwom a sankubɔfoɔ bi ɔɔbɔ no ne sankuo so.

english_verse: And a new song was being sung in front of God's throne and in front of the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn that song, except the
translation_verse: Na bɛtoo dwom foforɔ bi, ahennwa ne ateasefoɔ nnan ne mpanyimfoɔ he anim, na biaa antumi ansua dwom he, gye nnipa mpem ha aduannan nnan a bɛagye bɛ afiri asaase so he nko.

english_verse: All of these are pure virgins, and they follow the Lamb wherever he leads. They have been rescued to be presented to God and the Lamb as the most precious people on earth.
translation_verse: Yeinom ne nnipa a bɛamfa adwamammɔ angu bɛ ho fi, na bɛyɛ mmabunu. Baabiaa a Adwamaa he bɛkɔ he, na bedi n’akyi. Bɛagye bɛ nkwa afiri nnipa mu, na bɛne nnipa a bedi kane a bɛde bɛ ama Nyankopɔn ne Adwamaa he.

english_verse: They never tell lies, and they are innocent. The Messages of the Three Angels
translation_verse: Na bɛante torɔsɛm biaa amfiri bɛ anum; na dɛm biaa nni bɛ ho. Abɔfoɔ mmeɛnsa he

english_verse: I saw another angel. This one was flying across the sky and had the eternal good news to announce to the people of every race, tribe, language, and nation on earth.
translation_verse: Afei mehuu bɔfoɔ foforɔ bi sɛ watu nam soro soro a ɔɔkɔka Asɛmpa a ontwam da he akyerɛ asaase so nnipa a amane ne mmusuakuo ne kasa ne nkurofoɔ nyinaa ka ho.

english_verse: The angel shouted, “Worship and honor God! The time has come for him to judge everyone. Kneel down before the one who created heaven and earth, the oceans, and every stream.”
translation_verse: Ɔde nne kɛseɛ teaam sɛ, “Honsuro Nyankopɔn na honkamfo ne kɛseyɛ, efiri sɛ, berɛ he aduru sɛ obu adasamma atɛne. Honsom nea ɔbɔɔ soro, asaase, po ne nsuti he!”

english_verse: A second angel followed and said, “The great city of Babylon has fallen! This is the city that made all nations drunk and immoral. Now God is angry, and Babylon has fallen.”
translation_verse: Bɔfoɔ a ɔto so mmienu dii akyire kaa sɛ, “Wahwe ase! Babilon kɛseɛ ahwe ase, efiri sɛ waama amanamane nyinaa anom n’adwamammɔ nsa dendene he bi.”

english_verse: Finally, a third angel came and shouted: Here is what will happen if you worship the beast and the idol and have the mark of the beast on your hand or forehead.
translation_verse: Bɔfoɔ a ɔto so mmeɛnsa dii bɛ akyi teaam kaa sɛ, “Biaa a ɔsom aboa he ne ne sɛso honyini a na ɔma bɛhyɛ ne nsa ho anaa ne moma so agyinaeɛ

english_verse: You will have to drink the wine that God gives to everyone who makes him angry. You will feel his mighty anger, and you will be tortured with fire and burning sulfur, while the holy angels and the Lamb look on.
translation_verse: he ankasa bɛnom Nyankopɔn abufuo nsa korɔgyee a bɛahwie agu n’abufuo kuruwa mu he bi. Bɛ a bɛbɛyɔ saa he nyinaa, bɛbɛhyɛ no aniɔyeyaa gya ne sɔfe mu, abɔfoɔ ne Adwamaa he anim.

english_verse: If you worship the beast and the idol and accept the mark of its name, you will be tortured day and night. The smoke from your torture will go up forever and ever, and you will never be able to rest.
translation_verse: Na gya he wusie a ɔhyɛ bɛ aniɔyeyaa he foro kɔ soro daa daa. Na bɛ a bɛsomm aboa he ne ne sɛso honyini he onnya home biaa, awia anaa anadwo.”

english_verse: God's people must learn to endure. They must also obey his commands and have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Yei nti ne ɔsɛ sɛ Nyankopɔn nkurofoɔ ne bɛ a bedi Nyankopɔn mmara so na bɛgye Yesu di he nya boasetɔ he.

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven say, “Put this in writing. From now on, the Lord will bless everyone who has faith in him when they die.” The Spirit answered, “Yes, they will rest from their hard work, and they will be rewarded for what they have done.” The Earth Is Harvested
translation_verse: Afei metee nne bi firi soro a ɔkaa sɛ, “Twerɛ yei: Honhom he dɛ, Nhyira ne awufoɔ a bewu Awurade mu firi seesei ɔɔkɔ yi, ampa, bɛbɛhome afiri bɛ brɛ he mu; na bɛ nnwuma di bɛ akyi.”

english_verse: I looked and saw a bright cloud, and someone who seemed to be the Son of Man was sitting on the cloud. He wore a gold crown on his head and held a sharp sickle in his hand.
translation_verse: Na mehwɛeɛ, na hwɛ, mununkum fitaa bi a na abɔdeɛ bi a na ɔte so he te sɛ nipa a sikakɔkɔɔ abotire bɔ ne ti na okita kantankrankyi nnamnnam bi di.

english_verse: An angel came out of the temple and shouted, “Start cutting with your sickle! Harvest season is here, and all crops on earth are ripe.”
translation_verse: Na bɔfoɔ foforɔ bi firii asɔredane he mu pieeɛ, teaam kyerɛɛ nea ɔte mununkum he so he sɛ, “Fa wo kantankrankyi he twa, efiri sɛ, twaberɛ he aduru; asaase he aso aba a ɔsɛ sɛ betwa!”

english_verse: The one on the cloud swung his sickle and harvested the crops.
translation_verse: Nti nea ɔte mununkum so he hwimm ne kantankrankyi asaase so ma otwaa asaase so nnɔbae.

english_verse: Another angel with a sharp sickle then came out of the temple in heaven.
translation_verse: Afei mehuu bɔfoɔ foforɔ bi a ofiri soro asɔredane mu apie a ɔno nso kita kantankrankyi nnamnnam bi.

english_verse: After this, an angel with power over fire came from the altar and shouted to the angel who had the sickle. He said, “All grapes on earth are ripe! Harvest them with your sharp sickle.”
translation_verse: Bɔfoɔ foforɔ bi a ɔno nso hwɛ gya so firi afɔrebokyia he mu pieeɛ. Ɔteaa bɔfoɔ a okita kantankrankyi nnamnnam he sɛ, “Fa wo kantankrankyi nnamnnam he twitwa asaase so bobe aba akuoakuo he, efiri sɛ, n’aba he abere.”

english_verse: The angel swung his sickle on earth and cut off its grapes. He threw them into a pit where they were trampled on as a sign of God's anger.
translation_verse: Nti bɔfoɔ he himm ne kantankrankyi he asaase so de twitwaa bobe aba he na ɔde koguu Nyankopɔn abufuo nsa-kyi-amena kɛseɛ he mu.

english_verse: The pit was outside the city, and when the grapes were mashed, blood flowed out. The blood turned into a river that was about
translation_verse: Na betiatiaa nsa-kyi-amena he so kuro he akyi, na mogyaa firii nsa-kyi-amena he mu taa hɔ foro koduru bɛyɛ sɛ apɔnkɔ nnareka ho, na ɔtenee kɔɔ bɛyɛ nkwansini ha aduowɔtwe nnan.

english_verse: After this, I looked at the sky and saw something else that was strange and important. Seven angels were bringing the seven last terrible troubles. When these are ended, God will no longer be angry.
translation_verse: Na mehuu nsɛnkyerɛnne foforɔ bi a ɔsõ na ɔyɛ nwanwa ya soro. Na abɔfoɔ nson kitakita haw nson a ɔyɛ haw a otwa toɔ, efiri sɛ, ɔno ne kyerɛ Nyankopɔn abufuo a otwa toɔ.

english_verse: Then I saw something that looked like a glass sea mixed with fire, and people were standing on it. They were the ones who had defeated the beast and the idol and the number that tells the name of the beast. God had given them harps,
translation_verse: Afei mehuu bibi te sɛ ahwehwɛ po a bɛde gya afra mu, ne bɛ a bɛadi aboa he ne ne honyini ne n’akontahyɛdeɛ he so nkunim. Na begyinagyina ahwehwɛ po he ho a bekitakita Nyankopɔn nsankuo.

english_verse: and they were singing the song his servant Moses and the Lamb had sung. They were singing, “Lord God All-Powerful, you have done great and marvelous things. You are the ruler of all nations, and you do what is right and fair.
translation_verse: Na bɛɛto Nyankopɔn akoa Mose nnwom ne Adwamaa he nnwom sɛ, Awurade Nyankopɔn, adeɛ nyinaa so tumfoɔ, wo nnwuma yɛ akɛseɛ na ɔyɛ nwanwa. O! meresantene hene, w’akwane tene na ɔyɛ nɔkorɛ.

english_verse: Lord, who doesn't honor and praise your name? You alone are holy, and all nations will come and worship you, because you have shown that you judge with fairness.”
translation_verse: Awurade, hwane na oonsuro wo na ɔɔnhyɛ wo dini animuonyam? Efiri sɛ wo nkoaa ne woyɛ kronkron, na amane nyinaa bɛba abɛkoto w’anim, efiri sɛ w’atɛne tenenee ada adi.

english_verse: After this, I noticed something else in heaven. The sacred tent used for a temple was open.
translation_verse: Na yeinom akyi he, mehuu sɛ asɔredane a ɔyɛ adanseɛ ntomadane a ɔya soro he abie.

english_verse: And the seven angels who were bringing the terrible troubles were coming out of it. They were dressed in robes of pure white linen and wore belts made of pure gold.
translation_verse: Abɔfoɔ nson a bɛmaa bɛ tumi sɛ bɛmfa haw nson mmra he pie firii asɔredane he mu a na bɛhyehyɛ ntaadeɛ fitaa a sikakɔkɔɔ aboomu bɔ bɛ mu.

english_verse: One of the four living creatures gave each of the seven angels a bowl made of gold. These bowls were filled with the anger of God who lives forever and ever.
translation_verse: Afei ateasefoɔ nnan he mu baako maa abɔfoɔ nson he sikakɔkɔɔ nkuruwaa nson a, Nyankopɔn a ɔte ase daa daa he abufuo ahyɛ no ma.

english_verse: The temple quickly filled with smoke from the glory and power of God. No one could enter it until the seven angels had finished pouring out the seven last troubles.
translation_verse: Wusie a ofiri Nyankopɔn animuonyam ne ne tumi mu he hyɛɛ asɔredane he ma. Na biaa antumi ankɔ asɔredane he mu kosii sɛ haw nson a abɔfoɔ nson he de baeɛ he to twaeɛ.

english_verse: From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, “Go and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.”
translation_verse: Afei metee nne kɛseɛ bi a ofiri asɔredane he mu a ɔka kyerɛɛ abɔfoɔ nson he sɛ, “Kɔ na kohwie Nyankopɔn abufuo nson a ɔya nkuruwaa nson mu he gu asaase so.”

english_verse: The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
translation_verse: Bɔfoɔ a odi kane he kohwiee nea ɔya ne kuruwa he mu guu asaase so. Akuro a ohu na ɔyeya tutuu bɛ a bɛde aboa he ahyɛ bɛ ho agyinaeɛ he ne bɛ a bɛsomm ne sɛso honyini he nso.

english_verse: The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
translation_verse: Bɔfoɔ a ɔto so mmienu he hwiee nea ɔya ne kuruwa he mu guu po mu. Afei nsuo he danee te sɛ awufoɔ mogyaa, na abɔdeɛ biaa a nkwa yam ya po he mu he wuiɛ.

english_verse: The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood.
translation_verse: Bɔfoɔ a ɔto so mmeɛnsa he hwiee nea ɔya ne kuruwa he mu guu nsubɔntene mu maa nsuo he nyinaa danee mogyaa.

english_verse: Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say, “You have always been, and you always will be the holy God. You had the right to judge in this way.
translation_verse: Metee sɛ bɔfoɔ a ɔhwɛ nsuo so he ɔɔka sɛ, “O Kronkronni a wotene, na woya hɔ, na woya ha firi tetentredee, w’atɛne a wobuiɛ he yɛ atɛntenenee.

english_verse: They poured out the blood of your people and your prophets. So you gave them blood to drink as they deserve!”
translation_verse: Na bɛahwie Nyankopɔn ahoteefoɔ ne adiyifoɔ mogyaa agu, nti woama bɛ mogyaa sɛ bɛ nom. Nea ɔfata bɛaa ne beenya yi.”

english_verse: After this, I heard the altar shout, “Yes, Lord God All-Powerful, your judgments are honest and fair.”
translation_verse: Na metee sɛ nne bi firi afɔrebokyia he mu a ɔdɛ: Ɛɛ! Awurade Nyankopɔn, adeɛ nyinaa so tumfoɔ, w’atemmuo yɛ nɔkorɛ ne tenenee.

english_verse: The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire.
translation_verse: Afei bɔfoɔ a ɔto so nnan he hwiee nea ɔya ne kuruwa he mu guu awia so na bɛmaa awia tumi sɛ ɔmfa ne hyeɛ yaayaa he nhye nnipa.

english_verse: Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
translation_verse: Awia he hyeɛ yaayaa he hyee nnipa ma bɛkaa Nyankopɔn a ɔwɔ saa haw yi nyinaa so tumi he dini he ho abususɛm. Nso bɛannu bɛ ho anhyɛ no animuonyam.

english_verse: The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain.
translation_verse: Afei bɔfoɔ a ɔto so num he hwiee nea ɔya ne kuruwa he mu guu aboa he ahennwa he so. Sum duruu aboa he ahemmane mu, na osiane yeya nti, nnipa wee bɛ kɛtrɛma,

english_verse: And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
translation_verse: na esiane saa yeyaa he ne akuro a otutuu bɛ he nti, bɛdomee soro Nyankopɔn. Nso bɛansakra bɛ adwene amfiri bɛ bɔne ho.

english_verse: The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east.
translation_verse: Bɔfoɔ a ɔto so nsia he hwiee nea ɔya ne kuruwa he mu guu asubɔntene Eufrate mu. Nsuo he weweeɛ na ɔdanee kwane maa ahene a befiri awia apieeɛ.

english_verse: An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet.
translation_verse: Afei mehuu ahohommɔne mmeɛnsa bi a na bɛte sɛ atwerɛ a na befifiri tweaseɛ he ne aboa he ne atorɔ diyifoɔ he anum.

english_verse: These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
translation_verse: Bɛyɛ ahohommɔne a bɛyɔ anwanwadeɛ. Saa ahohom mmeɛnsa yi kɔɔ ahene a bɛya asaase so nyinaa nkyɛne kɔka bɛ nyinaa boa ano ma Tumfoɔ Nyankopɔn da kɛseɛ he mu ko he.

english_verse: Those armies came together in a place that in Hebrew is called Armagedon.
translation_verse: Na bɛboaboaa ahene he ano kɔɔ baabi a Hebri kasa mu bɛfrɛ hɔ Armagedon.

english_verse: As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, “It's done!”
translation_verse: Afei bɔfoɔ a ɔto so nson he hwiee nea ɔya ne kuruwa he mu guu mframa mu. Na nne dendene bi firi ahennwa a ɔya asɔredane he mu kaa sɛ, “Bɛawie.”

english_verse: The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger.
translation_verse: Kuro kɛseɛ he mu paee mmeɛnsa, na amane nyinaa nkuro akɛseɛ sɛesɛee. Nyankopɔn kaee Babel kɛseɛ he maa ɔnom nsa a ano ɔdene he, firii ne kuruwa ano.

english_verse: Every island ran away, and the mountains disappeared.
translation_verse: Nsupɔ ne mmepɔ nyinaa yeraeɛ.

english_verse: One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, “Come on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans.
translation_verse: Afei abɔfoɔ nson he mu baako a okita nkuruwaa nson he ka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Bra na menkyerɛ wo dwamanfoɔ kɛseɛ a ɔte nsuo pii so he atemmuo.

english_verse: Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.”
translation_verse: Ɔno ne wiase ahemfo ne no abɔ adwamane, na n’adwamammɔ nsa he aboroboro bɛ a bɛtete asaase so he.”

english_verse: With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
translation_verse: Honhom he faa me maa bɔfoɔ he de me kɔɔ serɛ so. Hɔ ne mehuu baa bi sɛ ɔte aboa kɔkɔɔ bi a abususɛm dini ahyɛ no mã so. Na aboa he wɔ ti nson ne mmɛne du.

english_verse: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet robes, and she wore jewelry made of gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup filled with the filthy and nasty things she had done.
translation_verse: Na baa he hyɛ ataadeɛ a n’ahosuo yɛ afasebiri ne kɔkɔɔ, afei nso ɔde sikakɔkɔɔ aboɔ ne nhwene pa ahyehyɛ ne ho. Na okita sika kuruwa a afideɛ ne adwamammɔ ho nnoɔma ayɛ no ma tɔ.

english_verse: On her forehead a mysterious name was written: I AM THE GREAT CITY OF BABYLON, THE MOTHER OF EVERY IMMORAL AND FILTHY THING ON EARTH.
translation_verse: Na bɛatwerɛ dini bi ya ne moma so a ne nkyerɛaseɛ yɛ ahintasɛm: “Babel kɛseɛ he, asaase so adwamanfoɔ ne akyiwadeɛ na.”

english_verse: I could tell that the woman was drunk on the blood of God's people who had given their lives for Jesus. This surprising sight amazed me,
translation_verse: Na mehuu sɛ ahoteefoɔ ne Yesu akyidifoɔ a bekuu bɛ he mogyaa aboro baa he. Mehu no he, ɔyɛɛ me nwanwa paa.

english_verse: and the angel said: Why are you so amazed? I will explain the mystery about this woman and about the beast she is sitting on, with its seven heads and ten horns.
translation_verse: Bɔfoɔ he bisaa me sɛ, “Mmɛ nti na ɔyɛ wo nwanwa? Mɛkyerɛ wo baa he ne aboa a ɔsõ no a ɔwɔ tire nson ne mmɛne du he ho ahintasɛm he.

english_verse: The beast you saw is one that used to be and no longer is. It will come back from the deep pit, but only to be destroyed. Everyone on earth whose names were not written in the book of life before the time of creation will be amazed. They will see this beast that used to be and no longer is, but will be once more.
translation_verse: Aboa a wohuu no he, na ɔte ase nso ɔnte ase bio. Oobefiri amena donkodonko he mu aba ama bɛasɛe no. Nnipa a bɛte asaase so a bɛantwerɛ bɛ dini angu ateasefoɔ nwoma he mu ansa na bɛɛbɔ wiase he, sɛ wohu aboa he a, ɔbɛyɛ bɛ nwanwa, efiri sɛ mmerɛ bi na ɔte asɛ, nso seesei deɛ, ɔnte ase na ɔbɛsane aba.

english_verse: Anyone with wisdom can figure this out. The seven heads that the woman is sitting on stand for seven hills. These heads are also seven kings.
translation_verse: “Yei ho ne nimdeɛ ne nteaseɛ ho hia. Ti nson he gyina hɔ ma mmepɔ nson a baa he tena so he. Bɛyɛ ahemfo nson nso,

english_verse: Five of the kings are dead. One is ruling now, and the other one has not yet come. But when he does, he will rule for only a little while.
translation_verse: bɛ mu nnum awu, na bɛ mu baako da so di hene, na baako a ɔka bɛ ho he mmaeɛ. Na sɛ ɔba a, ɔsɛ sɛ ɔtwɛne kakra bi.

english_verse: You also saw a beast that used to be and no longer is. That beast is one of the seven kings who will return as the eighth king, but only to be destroyed.
translation_verse: Aboa he a nka na ɔte ase he na afei deɛ ɔnte ase he yɛ hene a ɔto so nwɔtwe a ɔka nson a bedi kane he ho a ɔɔkɔ ne sɛeɛ mu.

english_verse: The ten horns you saw are ten more kings, who have not yet come into power, and they will rule with the beast for only a short time.
translation_verse: “Na mmɛne du a wohuiɛ he yɛ ahemfo du a bennyaa ahennie, nso bɛne aboa he benya tumi sɛ ahemfo dɔnhwere baako.

english_verse: They all think alike and will give their power and authority to the beast.
translation_verse: Yeinom wɔ adwene korɔ, na bɛde bɛ tumi ne bɛ ahoɔdene bɛma aboa he.

english_verse: These kings will go to war against the Lamb. But he will defeat them, because he is Lord over all lords and King over all kings. His followers are chosen and special and faithful.
translation_verse: Bɛbɛko atia Adwamaa he ne n’akyidifoɔ, agyidifoɔ a bɛafrɛ bɛ he ne no bedi bɛ so, efiri sɛ, ɔyɛ Awurade mu Awurade ne Ahemfo mu Hene.”

english_verse: The oceans you saw the prostitute sitting on are crowds of people from all races and languages.
translation_verse: Bɔfoɔ he nso ka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Nsuo a wohuiɛ a dwamanfoɔ he te so he yɛ nkuro ne nkurofoɔ akuoakuo ne amane ne kasa ahodoɔ.

english_verse: The ten horns and the beast will start hating the shameless woman. They will strip off her clothes and leave her naked. Then they will eat her flesh and throw the rest of her body into a fire.
translation_verse: Mmɛne du ne aboa he a wohuiɛ he bɛtane dwamanfoɔ he. Bɛbɛfa bibiaa a ɔya ne ho ama wada adagya. Bɛbɛwe ne nam na bɛde gya ahye no,

english_verse: God is the one who made these kings all think alike and decide to give their power to the beast. And they will do this until what God has said comes true.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, Nyankopɔn de ahyɛ bɛ akoma mu sɛ bɛbɛyɔ adwene korɔ de bɛ ahennie ama aboa he na wadi hene akosi sɛ Nyankopɔn asɛm he bɛba mu.

english_verse: The woman you saw is the great city that rules over all kings on earth.
translation_verse: Afei baa a wohuu no he yɛ kuro kɛseɛ a odi asaase so ahemfo so hene he.”

english_verse: I saw another angel come from heaven. This one had great power, and the earth was bright because of his glory.
translation_verse: Yei akyi he, mehuu bɔfoɔ foforɔ sɛ ofiri soro ɔɔba fam. Na ɔwɔ tumi kɛseɛ na n’animuonyam hyerɛn asaase so nyinaa.

english_verse: The angel shouted, “Fallen! Powerful Babylon has fallen and is now the home of demons. It is the den of every filthy spirit and of all unclean birds, and every dirty and hated animal.
translation_verse: Ɔteaam dendenndene sɛ, “Wahwe ase! Babel kɛseɛ ahwe ase, na adane ahonhommɔne tenaberɛ ne ahonhom fi nyinaa nso atenaeɛ. Nnomaa fi a bekyiri bɛ nyinaa te ne mu.

english_verse: Babylon's evil and immoral wine has made all nations drunk. Every king on earth has slept with her, and every merchant on earth is rich because of her evil desires.”
translation_verse: Efiri sɛ, amanamane nyinaa anom n’adwamammɔ nsa dendene he bi, na asaase so ahemfo ne no abɔ adwamane, na asaase so adwadifoɔ anya bɛ ho ɔnam n’akɔnnɔbɔne ntrasoɔ nti.”

english_verse: Then I heard another voice from heaven shout, “My people, you must escape from Babylon. Don't take part in her sins and share her punishment.
translation_verse: Afei metee nne foforɔ firii soro sɛ, “Me mane, homfiri ne mu, na hoanyɔ ne nneyɔeɛ bɔne he bi, na hoannya ne haw he bi;

english_verse: Her sins are piled as high as heaven. God has remembered the evil she has done.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, ne nneyɔeɛ bɔne atoatoa abeduru soro, na Nyankopɔn kae n’akwammɔne he.

english_verse: Treat her as she has treated others. Make her pay double for what she has done. Make her drink twice as much of what she mixed for others.
translation_verse: Sɛnea ɔyɔɔ ho he, honyɔ no saa pɛpɛɛpɛ; nea ɔyɔɔ ho biaa he, hontua no so ka mprenu. Nsa a ɔde maa ho he, honhyɛ no bi a ano ɔdene mmɔho mmienu.

english_verse: That woman honored herself with a life of luxury. Reward her now with suffering and pain. “Deep in her heart Babylon said, ‘I am the queen! Never will I be a widow or know what it means to be sad.’
translation_verse: Homma onhu amane na ne werɛ nho ɔnam animuonyam ne n’ahonya a ɔpɛ maa ne ho he nti, efiri sɛ, daa ɔka sɛ, ‘Mete ha sɛ hemaa. Menyɛ kunafoɔ, me werɛ ɔnho da!’

english_verse: And so, in a single day she will suffer the pain of sorrow, hunger, and death. Fire will destroy her dead body, because her judge is the powerful Lord God.”
translation_verse: Esiane yei nti, n’amanehunu bɛba ne so da bi, yadeɛ, awerɛhoɔ ne kɔm, na bɛde gya ahye no; efiri sɛ Awurade Nyankopɔn a obu no atɛne he yɛ hoɔdenfoɔ.”

english_verse: Every king on earth who slept with her and shared in her luxury will mourn. They will weep, when they see the smoke from that fire.
translation_verse: Sɛ asaase so ahemfo a bɛne no bɔɔ adwamane, dii dɛ he hu ne hyeɛ wusie a, bebesu, atwa agyaadwoɔ.

english_verse: Her sufferings will frighten them, and they will stand at a distance and say, “Pity that great and powerful city! Pity Babylon! In a single hour her judgment has come.”
translation_verse: N’amanehunu he hu a ɔyɛ nti, bebegyina akyirikyiri asu sɛ, “Due! Due! O! kuro kɛseɛ, O Babel, kuro a ɔwɔ tumi. Hwɛ! dɔnhwere baako pɛ mu, na w’atemmuo aba.”

english_verse: Every merchant on earth will mourn, because there is no one to buy their goods.
translation_verse: Adwadifoɔ a bɛya asaase so nso su no efiri sɛ, biaa ntɔ bɛ nnoɔma bio.

english_verse: There won't be anyone to buy their gold, silver, jewels, pearls, fine linen, purple cloth, silk, scarlet cloth, sweet-smelling wood, fancy carvings of ivory and wood, as well as things made of bronze, iron, or marble.
translation_verse: Biaa ntɔ sikakɔkɔɔ ne dwetɛ, ne aboɔdeneboɔ ne nhwene pa, ne nwera pa ne ntoma afasebiri ne serekye ne ntoma kɔkɔɔ, ne nnua a ohwam nyinaa ne asommɛne adwinnie nyinaa, ne adwinnie a bɛde nnua a ɔwɔ dini ayɔ, ne yaawa ne dadeɛ ne abohemmaa,

english_verse: No one will buy their cinnamon, spices, incense, myrrh, frankincense, wine, olive oil, fine flour, wheat, cattle, sheep, horses, chariots, slaves, and other humans.
translation_verse: ne pɛprɛ ne mmorɔngo, ne nnuhwam ne kurobo, ne hwamfufuo ne nsa ne ngo, ne siam muhumuhu ne ayuo, ne nyɛmmoa bi te sɛ nnwane ne apɔnkɔ ne nteaseɛnam ne nnipadua ne nnipa akra bio.

english_verse: Babylon, the things your heart desired have all escaped from you. Every luxury and all your glory will be lost forever. You will never get them back.
translation_verse: Na aduaba a wo kra kɔno dɔ he afiri wo nkyɛne, na nnepa ne animuonyanneɛ nyinaa afiri wo ho ayera, na wonhu bio koraa.”

english_verse: The merchants had become rich because of her. But when they saw her sufferings, they were terrified. They stood at a distance, crying and mourning.
translation_verse: Nnoɔma yi ho adwadifoɔ a kuro he nti bɛanya adeɛ he, begyinagyina akyirikyiri n’ayayadeɛ he suro nti, na bɛasu

english_verse: Then they shouted, “Pity the great city of Babylon! She dressed in fine linen and wore purple and scarlet cloth. She had jewelry made of gold and precious stones and pearls.
translation_verse: na bɛagyam sɛ, “Due! Due! O! kuro kɛseɛ a ofira nwera pa ne ntoma afasebiri ne ntoma kɔkɔɔ na ɔde sika ne aboɔdenboɔ ne nhwene pa abobɔ mu,

english_verse: Yet in a single hour her riches disappeared.” Every ship captain and passenger and sailor stood at a distance, together with everyone who does business by traveling on the sea.
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, dɔnhwere pɛ mu, na saa ahonya dodoɔ yi asa.” Nsuomhyɛnkafoɔ, ne hyɛne he mu nnipa, ne hyɛne he mu adwadifoɔ, ne bɛ a bɛka ho a benya bɛ ano aduane firi hyɛne adwumayɔ mu he nyinaa gyinaa akyirikyiri

english_verse: When they saw the smoke from her fire, they shouted, “This was the greatest city ever!”
translation_verse: na behuu ne hyeɛ wusie he, bɛteaam sɛ: Abɛ kuro ne te sɛ kuro kɛseɛ yi?”

english_verse: They cried loudly, and in their sorrow they threw dust on their heads, as they said, “Pity the great city of Babylon! Everyone who sailed the seas became rich from her treasures. But in a single hour the city was destroyed.
translation_verse: Na bɛde mfuturo guu bɛ atifi, na bɛde sũ ne gyam teaa mu sɛ: Due! Due! Kuro kɛseɛ yi a n’aboɔdenneɛ nti bɛ a bɛya hyɛne mu ya po so nyinaa anyinya bɛ ho ya mu, hwɛ! Dɔnhwere baako pɛ mu na bɛayɛ no pasaa.

english_verse: The heavens should be happy with God's people and apostles and prophets. God has punished her for them.”
translation_verse: Soro ne ho ahoteefoɔ ne asomafoɔ ne adiyifoɔ, ɔno nti homma ho ani nnye ya ne ho, efiri sɛ Nyankopɔn abu no atɛne ama ho.

english_verse: A powerful angel then picked up a huge stone and threw it into the sea. The angel said, “This is how the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, never to rise again.
translation_verse: Afei bɔfoɔ hoɔdenfoɔ bi faa boɔ a ne kɛseɛ bɛyɛ sɛ ayuyammoɔ to twenee po mu kaa sɛ, “Saa ahoɔdene yiaa ne bɛde bɛto Babilon kɛse he atwene fam a bennhu no bio.

english_verse: The music of harps and singers and of flutes and trumpets will no longer be heard. No workers will ever set up shop in that city, and the sound of grinding grain will be silenced forever.
translation_verse: Bɛnnte sankubɔfoɔ ne nwomtofoɔ, atɛntɛbɛnbɔfoɔ ne totorobɛntohyɛnfoɔ nka bio mfiri ho mu. Bennhu dwumfoɔ biaa ya wo mu bio. Na bɛnte ayuyammoɔ biaa nka ho mu bio.

english_verse: Lamps will no longer shine anywhere in Babylon, and couples will never again say wedding vows there. Her merchants ruled the earth, and by her witchcraft she fooled all nations.
translation_verse: Kanea hann onhyerɛn ya ho mu bio. Na bɛnnte ayefokunu ne ayeforɔ nne ya ho mu bio. W’adwadifoɔ yɛ wiase mu nnipa titire. Na bɛde wo ntafowayie adaadaa amane nyinaa.

english_verse: On the streets of Babylon is found the blood of God's people and of his prophets, and everyone else.”
translation_verse: Na ɔno mu ne behuu adiyifoɔ ne ahoteefoɔ ne bɛ a bɛaku bɛ asaase so he nyinaa mogyaa.”

english_verse: After this, I heard what sounded like a lot of voices in heaven, and they were shouting, “Praise the Lord! To our God belongs the glorious power to save,
translation_verse: Yei akyi he, metee bibi a ɔte sɛ nnipakuo bebree nne firii soro sɛ: “Haleluya! Nkwagyeɛ ne animuonyam ne tumi yɛ yɛ Nyankopɔn dea

english_verse: because his judgments are honest and fair. That filthy prostitute ruined the earth with shameful deeds. But God has judged her and made her pay the price for murdering his servants.”
translation_verse: efiri sɛ, n’atemmuo yɛ nɔkorɛ na ɔtene sɛ obuu dwamanfoɔ kɛseɛ a ɔde n’adwamammɔ asɛe asaase he atɛne, na ɔtɔɔ ne nkoa mogyaa so werɛ ne nsa mu.”

english_verse: Then the crowd shouted, “Praise the Lord! Smoke will never stop rising from her burning body.”
translation_verse: Na afei bɛteaam bio sɛ, “Haleluya! Na wusie a ofiri ne mu he kɔ soro daa daa.”

english_verse: From the throne a voice said, “If you worship and fear our God, give praise to him, no matter who you are.”
translation_verse: Afei nne bi firi ahennwa he so baa sɛ, “Honkamfo yɛ Nyankopɔn, ho a hoyɛ ne nkoa, ne ho a hosuro no nyinaa, akɛseɛ ne nkumaa!”

english_verse: Then I heard what seemed to be a large crowd that sounded like a roaring flood and loud thunder all mixed together. They were saying, “Praise the Lord! Our Lord God All-Powerful now rules as king.
translation_verse: Afei metee nne bi te sɛ nnipadɔm nne, ne nsuo nworosoɔ ne apranaa a ɔɔpaepae a ɔɔteam sɛ, “Haleluya! Yɛ Tumfoɔ Awurade Nyankopɔn di hene.

english_verse: So we will be glad and happy and give him praise. The wedding day of the Lamb is here, and his bride is ready.
translation_verse: Homma yɛ ho nsɛpɛ yɛ na yɛ ani nnye na yɛnhyɛ no animuonyam, efiri sɛ, dwammaa he ayefohyia aduru, na ne yerɛ asiesie ne ho.

english_verse: She will be given a wedding dress made of pure and shining linen. This linen stands for the good things God's people have done.”
translation_verse: Bɛde nwera pa a ani hyerɛn na ani te maa no sɛ ɔnhyɛ.” Nwera pa he ne ahoteefoɔ tenenee adeyɔ.

english_verse: Then the angel told me, “Put this in writing. God will bless everyone who is invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb.” The angel also said, “These things God has said are true.”
translation_verse: Afei bɔfoɔ he ka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Twerɛ sɛ, Nhyira nka bɛ a bɛato nsa afrɛ bɛ dwammaa he ayefohyia apontoɔ ase.” Na ɔka kaa ho sɛ, “Yei yɛ Nyankopɔn asɛm turodoo.”

english_verse: I knelt at the feet of the angel and began to worship him. But the angel said, “Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you and everyone else who tells about Jesus. Don't worship anyone but God. Everyone who tells about Jesus does it by the power of the Spirit.” The Rider on the White Horse
translation_verse: Afei mebutuu ne nane ase sɔree no. Nso ɔka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Nyɔ saa, efiri sɛ, meyɛ akoa te sɛ wo ne wo nuanom a bedi Yesu ho adanseɛ heaa, Nyankopɔn mmom ne koto sɔre no! Na Yesu adanseɛ ne yɛ adiyie honhom he.” Gyefoɔ he ho anisoadehunu kɛseɛ

english_verse: I looked and saw heaven was open, and a white horse was there. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he is always fair when he judges or goes to war.
translation_verse: Afei mehuu sɛ soro abie a pɔnkɔ fitaa ya hɔ. Na nea ɔte ne so he dini de “Ɔseadeɛyɔ ne Nɔkwafoɔ,” na tenenee ne ogyina so bu atɛne ko ne ko nso.

english_verse: He had eyes like flames of fire, and he was wearing a lot of crowns. His name was written on him, but he was the only one who knew what the name meant.
translation_verse: N’ani te sɛ gya a ɔɔdɛre, na na ɔbɔ ahemmotire bebree. Na bɛatwerɛ ne dini bi a biaa nnim gye sɛ ɔno nkoaa.

english_verse: The rider wore a robe that was covered with blood, and he was known as “The Word of God.”
translation_verse: Na bɛde n’ataadeɛ a ɔhyɛ he anu mogyaa mu. Dini a bɛde frɛ no ne “Nyankopɔn Asɛm.”

english_verse: He was followed by armies from heaven that rode on white horses and were dressed in pure white linen.
translation_verse: Soro asraafoɔ a bɛhyehyɛ nwera fitaa a ani te a bɛtete pɔnkɔ fitaa so dii n’akyi.

english_verse: From his mouth a sharp sword went out to attack the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod and will show the fierce anger of God All-Powerful by trampling the grapes in the pit where wine is made.
translation_verse: Afei nkrantɛ nnamnnam a ɔde bedi amane so firi n’anum baeɛ. Ɔde dadeɛ poma ne bedi bɛ so. Obetiatia Nyankopɔn a ɔyɛ adeɛ nyinaa so tumfoɔ he abufuo a ɔdene he nsa-kyi-amena he so.

english_verse: On the part of the robe that covered his thigh was written, “KING OF KINGS AND Lord OF LordS.”
translation_verse: Na bɛatwerɛ dini “Ahene mu Hene ne awuranom mu Awurade” agu n’ataadeɛ mu ne ne nane ho. Bedii aboa he ne n’asraafoɔ he so nkunim

english_verse: I then saw an angel standing on the sun, and he shouted to all the birds flying in the sky, “Come and join in God's great feast!
translation_verse: Afei mehuu bɔfoɔ bi a ogyina awia mu. Ɔteaam guu nnomaa a bɛnam wiem nyinaa so sɛ, “Hommra mmehyiam Nyankopɔn apontoɔ kɛseɛ he ase.

english_verse: You can eat the flesh of kings, rulers, leaders, horses, riders, free people, slaves, important people, and everyone else.”
translation_verse: Hommedi ahemfo nam ne asafohene nam ne atumfoɔ nam ne apɔnkɔ ne bɛ a bɛtete bɛ so nam ne adehyeɛ ne nkoa ne akɛseɛ ne nkumaa nyinaa nam.”

english_verse: I also saw the beast and all kings of the earth come together. They fought against the rider on the white horse and against his army.
translation_verse: Afei mehuu aboa he ne asaase so ahemfo ne bɛ asraafoɔ a bɛaboa bɛ ano ɔɔkɔko atia nea ɔte pɔnkɔ he so he ne ne dɔm.

english_verse: But the beast was captured and so was the false prophet. This is the same prophet who had worked miracles for the beast, so he could fool everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol. The beast and the false prophet were thrown alive into a lake of burning sulfur.
translation_verse: Bɛkyee aboa he ne ne diyifoɔ torɔfoɔ a ɔyɔɔ anwanwadeɛ n’anim he de no too afiase. Saa anwanwadeɛ he so ne ɔnam daadaa bɛ a bɛde aboa he ahyɛ bɛ agyinaeɛ ne bɛ a bɛsom aboa he honyini he. Bɛtoo aboa he ne diyifoɔ torɔfoɔ he anikann guu gya tadeɛ a sɔfe ɔɔdɛre mu he mu.

english_verse: But the rest of their army was killed by the sword that came from the mouth of the rider on the horse. Then birds stuffed themselves on the dead bodies.
translation_verse: Bɛde nkrantɛ a ofiri pɔnkɔkafoɔ he anum kukuu bɛ asraafoɔ, na nnomaa he dii bɛ nam meeɛ.

english_verse: I saw thrones, and sitting on those thrones were the ones who had been given the right to judge. I also saw the souls of the people who had their heads cut off because they had told about Jesus and preached God's message. They were the same ones who had not worshiped the beast or the idol, and they had refused to let its mark be put on their foreheads or hands. They will come to life and rule with Christ for
translation_verse: Afei mehuu nhennwa bi ne bɛ a bɛtete so a na bɛama bɛ tumi sɛ bemmu atɛne. Na mesane huu bɛ a Yesu adanseɛ ne Nyankopɔn asɛm nti betwitwaa bɛ ti he kra, ne bɛ a bɛankoto aboa he anaa ne honyini na bɛannye agyinaeɛ he ammɔ bɛ moma ne bɛ nsa so he. Na benyaa nkwa bio, na bɛne Kristo dii hene mfie apem.

english_verse: These people are the first to be raised to life, and they are especially blessed and holy. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and Christ and will rule with them for
translation_verse: Nhyira ne bɛ a bɛate bɛ ho ama Awurade a benya kyɛfa sɔreeɛ a odi kane he mu. Saa nkurofoɔ he deɛ, wuprenu he nni bɛ so tumi, na mmom bɛbɛyɛ Nyankopɔn ne Kristo asɔfoɔ, na bɛne no bedi ahene mfie apem.

english_verse: At the end of the
translation_verse: Mfie apem he akyi he, bebesane Satan afiri afiase hɔ;

english_verse: He will fool the countries of Gog and Magog, which are at the far ends of the earth, and their people will follow him into battle. They will have as many followers as there are grains of sand along the beach,
translation_verse: na ɔbɛkɔ akɔdaadaa amane a bɛya wiase nyinaa, Gog ne Magog, akɔboaboa nnipa a bɛ dodoɔ te sɛ mpoano anwea de bɛ akɔ sa.

english_verse: and they will march all the way across the earth. They will surround the camp of God's people and the city God loves. But fire will come down from heaven and destroy the whole army.
translation_verse: Betuu sa faa asaase so nyina kotwaa kuro a Nyankopɔn dɔ no he, na n’ahoteefoɔ tete mu he ho hyiaeɛ. Nso gya firi soro bɛhyee bɛ.

english_verse: Then the devil who fooled them will be thrown into the lake of fire and burning sulfur. He will be there with the beast and the false prophet, and they will be in pain day and night forever and ever. The Judgment at the Great White Throne
translation_verse: Na bɛtoo Bronsam a ɔdaadaa bɛ he too gya ne sɔfe tadeɛ a bɛde aboa he ne diyifoɔ torɔfoɔ he ato mu he mu. Na bɛbɛyɛ bɛ ayayadeɛ awia ne anadwo daa daa. Bebuu awufoɔ atɛne

english_verse: I saw a great white throne with someone sitting on it. Earth and heaven tried to run away, but there was no place for them to go.
translation_verse: Afei mehuu ahennwa kɛseɛ fitaa bi ne nea ɔte ahennwa he so a asaase ne soro dwane firii n’anim, na bɛanya baabiaa amma bɛ.

english_verse: I also saw all the dead people standing in front of that throne. Every one of them was there, no matter who they had once been. Several books were opened, and then the book of life was opened. The dead were judged by what those books said they had done.
translation_verse: Na mehuu awufoɔ akɛseɛ ne nketewa sɛ begyinagyina ahennwa he anim. Bebiebiee nwoma bebree mu, na bebiee nwoma foforɔ bi a ɔyɛ nkwa nwoma he mu. Bebuu awufoɔ he atɛne, sɛnea bɛ nnwuma teɛ, ne sɛnea bɛatwerɛ ya nwoma ahoroɔ he mu he.

english_verse: The sea gave up the dead people who were in it, and death and its kingdom also gave up their dead. Then everyone was judged by what they had done.
translation_verse: Afei po nso yii n’awufoɔ maeɛ. Wuo ne Asamando nso yii bɛ awufoɔ maeɛ. Na bebuu bɛ nyinaa atɛne sɛnea bɛ nnwuma teɛ.

english_verse: Afterwards, death and its kingdom were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
translation_verse: Na bɛtoo Wuo ne Asamando guu gya tadeɛ he mu. Saa gya tadeɛ yi ne wuprenu he.

english_verse: Anyone whose name wasn't written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
translation_verse: Na biaa a bɛantwerɛ ne dini nkwa nwoma he mu he, bɛtoo no twenee gya tadeɛ he mu.

english_verse: I saw a new heaven and a new earth. The first heaven and the first earth had disappeared, and so had the sea.
translation_verse: Afei mehuu soro foforɔ ne asaase foforɔ, na kane soro ne kane asaase he nni hɔ bio, na po nso nni hɔ bio.

english_verse: I heard a loud voice shout from the throne: God's home is now with his people. He will live with them, and they will be his own. Yes, God will make his home among his people.
translation_verse: Na metee nne dendene bi firi ahennwa he so sɛ: “Hwɛ! Nyankopɔn ntomadane ya nnipa mu. Ɔne bɛ bɛtena na bɛayɛ ne mane. Na Nyankopɔn he ankasa ne bɛ bɛtena

english_verse: He will wipe all tears from their eyes, and there will be no more death, suffering, crying, or pain. These things of the past are gone forever.
translation_verse: Na ɔbɛpepa bɛ aniwa mu nisuo nyinaa. Na wuo nni hɔ bio; na awerɛhoɔ ne su ne yeyaa biaa nni hɔ bio, efiri sɛ, kane nnoɔma he atwam.”

english_verse: Then the one sitting on the throne said: I am making everything new. Write down what I have said. My words are true and can be trusted.
translation_verse: Afei nea ɔte ahennwa he so he kaa sɛ, “Hwɛ! Meeyɔ nnoɔma nyinaa foforɔ!” Ɔka kyerɛɛ me nso sɛ, “Twerɛ yei, efiri sɛ, saa nsɛm yi yɛ nɔkorɛ, na wobetumi de wo ho ato so.”

english_verse: Everything is finished! I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will freely give water from the life-giving fountain to everyone who is thirsty.
translation_verse: Na ɔkɔɔ so kaa sɛ, “Bɛawie! Mene Ahyɛaseɛ ne Awieeɛ, kyerɛ sɛ, mfitiaseɛ ne awieeɛ he. Biaa a sukɔm de no he, mɛma no nsuo a ofiri nkwa asuti mu anom kwa.

english_verse: All who win the victory will be given these blessings. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
translation_verse: Biaa a obedi nkunim he benya yei afiri me nkyɛne. Mɛyɛ ne Nyankopɔn na ɔno nso ayɛ me ba.

english_verse: But I will tell you what will happen to cowards and to everyone who is unfaithful or dirty-minded or who murders or is sexually immoral or uses witchcraft or worships idols or tells lies. They will be thrown into that lake of fire and burning sulfur. This is the second death. The New Jerusalem
translation_verse: Nso ahufoɔ ne bɛ a bɛnnye nni ne akyiwadeyɔfoɔ ne awudifoɔ ne adwamanfoɔ ne adutofoɔ ne abosonsomfoɔ ne atorɔfoɔ nyinaa benya bɛ kyɛfa ya tadeɛ a gya ne sɔfe ɔɔdɛre mu he. Yei ne wuprenu he.” Yerusalem foforɔ he

english_verse: I saw one of the seven angels who had the bowls filled with the seven last terrible troubles. The angel came to me and said, “Come on! I will show you the one who will be the bride and wife of the Lamb.”
translation_verse: Abɔfoɔ nson a bekitakita nkuruwaa nson a haw nson a otwa toɔ he ahyɛ no ma he mu baako baa me nkyɛne bɛkaa sɛ, “Bra, na mɛkyerɛ wo Ayeforɔ a ɔyɛ Dwammaa he yerɛ he.”

english_verse: Then with the help of the Spirit, he took me to the top of a very high mountain. There he showed me the holy city of Jerusalem coming down from God in heaven.
translation_verse: Na ɔde me nam honhom mu kɔɔ bepɔ kɛseɛ tentene bi atifi. Ɔkyerɛɛ me Yerusalem, Kuro Kronkron a ofiri soro, Nyankopɔn hɔ ɔɔba fam,

english_verse: The glory of God made the city bright. It was dazzling and crystal clear like a precious jasper stone.
translation_verse: a ɔɔhyerɛn Nyankopɔn animuonyam mu. Na kuro he hyerɛn sɛ aboɔdenboɔ, sɛ ahwehwɛboɔ a mu te sɛ ahwehwɛ.

english_verse: The city had a high and thick wall with twelve gates, and each one of them was guarded by an angel. On each of the gates was written the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Na ne fasuo he wɔ ahoɔdene na ɔware yie, na ɔwɔ pono dummienu gu ano a, abɔfoɔ dummienu na bɛhwɛ apono he so. Apono he so ne bɛatwerɛ Israel mmusuakuo dummienu he mu nnipa dini.

english_verse: Three of these gates were on the east, three were on the north, three more were on the south, and the other three were on the west.
translation_verse: Na apono mmeɛnsa ya apieɛ; mmeɛnsa ya anafoɔ; mmeɛnsa ya atifi na mmeɛnsa nso ya atɔeɛ.

english_verse: The city was built on twelve foundation stones. On each of the stones was written the name of one of the Lamb's twelve apostles.
translation_verse: Besii kuro he afasuo sii aboɔ dummienu a bɛatwerɛ Dwammaa he asomafoɔ dummienu he dini ya so he so.

english_verse: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring stick to measure the city and its gates and its walls.
translation_verse: Bɔfoɔ a ɔkasa kyerɛɛ me he wɔ sikakɔkɔɔ poma susudua a bɛde besusu kuro he, n’apono ne ne fasuo.

english_verse: The city was shaped like a cube, because it was just as high as it was wide. When the angel measured the city, it was about
translation_verse: Na kuro he yɛ ahinanan, ne tentene ne ne tɛtrɛtɛ yɛ pɛ. Bɔfoɔ he de ne susudua he susu kuro he. Na ne tentene yɛ nkwansini apem ahanum na ne tɛtrɛtɛ, ne tentene ne sorokɔ nyinaa yɛ pɛ.

english_verse: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
translation_verse: Ahwehwɛboɔ ne ɔde too afasuo he. Na kuro he deɛ, sikakɔkɔɔ mapa a ɔyɛ hyerɛnn te sɛ ahwehwɛ na bɛde toeɛ.

english_verse: Each of the twelve foundations was a precious stone. The first was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was agate, the fourth was emerald,
translation_verse: Bɛde aboɔdenboɔ ahodoɔ a ɔfɛ ne hyehyɛɛ kuro he fapem. Fapem a odi kane he yɛ ahwehwɛboɔ. Fapem a ɔto so mmienu he yɛ hoaboɔ. Fapem a ɔto so mmeɛnsa he yɛ mununkumboɔ.

english_verse: Each of the twelve gates was a solid pearl. The streets of the city were made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
translation_verse: Na bɛde nhwene pa dummienu ne yɔɔ apono dummienu he. Apono he mu biaa, bɛde ahwene pa baako ne yɔeɛ. Na kuro he abɔntene kɛseɛ yɛ sika mapa, kronkron a ɔte sɛ ahwehwɛ.

english_verse: I did not see a temple there. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were its temple.
translation_verse: Na manhu asɔredane ya kuro he mu baabiaa, efiri sɛ, na Awurade Nyankopɔn, ade nyinaa so tumfoɔ, ne Adwammaa he ne yɛ n’asɔredane.

english_verse: And the city did not need the sun or the moon. The glory of God was shining on it, and the Lamb was its light.
translation_verse: Na kuro he nhia awia anaa bosome hann, efiri sɛ, na Nyankopɔn animuonyam hyerɛn mu, na kuro he kanea ne Dwammaa he.

english_verse: Nations will walk by the light of that city, and kings will bring their riches there.
translation_verse: Ne hann he bɛhyerɛn asaase so amane nyinaa so, na asaase so amane sodifoɔ de bɛ animuonyam bɛba mu.

english_verse: Its gates are always open during the day, and night never comes.
translation_verse: Bɛntoto kuro he apono mu koraa awia, efiri sɛ adeɛ nsa hɔ da.

english_verse: The glorious treasures of nations will be brought into the city.
translation_verse: Bɛde amane he animuonyam ne anidie bɛba kuro he mu.

english_verse: But nothing unworthy will be allowed to enter. No one who is dirty-minded or who tells lies will be there. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will be in the city.
translation_verse: Nso bibiaa a ho nte nkɔ kuro he mu. Saa ara ne bi a ɔyɛ aniwudeɛ anaa odi atorɔ nkɔ mu. Bɛ a bɛatwerɛ bɛ dini ahyɛ Dwammaa he nkwa nwoma he mu he nkoaa na bɛbɛkɔ kuro he mu.

english_verse: The angel showed me a river that was crystal clear, and its waters gave life. The river came from the throne where God and the Lamb were seated.
translation_verse: Afei bɔfoɔ he kyerɛɛ me nkwa asubɔntene a ani te krɔgyee sɛ ahwehwɛ a ofiri Nyankopɔn ne Dwammaa he ahennwa so

english_verse: Then it flowed down the middle of the city's main street. On each side of the river are trees that grow a different kind of fruit each month of the year. The fruit gives life, and the leaves are used as medicine to heal the nations.
translation_verse: a, ɔtene fa kuro he abɔntene mfinimfini. Na nkwa dua a ɔsõ aba ahodoɔ dummienu sisi Asubɔntene he afanu nyinaa, na ɔsoso n’aba bosome biaa. Na dua he ahahane ne bɛde sa amane yadeɛ.

english_verse: God's curse will no longer be on the people of that city. He and the Lamb will be seated there on their thrones, and its people will worship God
translation_verse: Bibiaa a ɔhyɛ Nyankopɔn nnome ase he nkɔ kuro he mu. Bebehu Nyankopɔn ne Dwammaa he ahennwa ya kuro he mu na ne nkoa bɛsom no.

english_verse: and will see him face to face. God's name will be written on the foreheads of the people.
translation_verse: Bebehu n’anim na bɛbɛtwerɛ ne dini agu bɛ moma so.

english_verse: Never again will night appear, and no one who lives there will ever need a lamp or the sun. The Lord God will be their light, and they will rule forever. The Coming of Christ
translation_verse: Adeɛ ɔnnsa hɔ bio na nkanea anaa awia ho nhia, efiri sɛ, Awurade Nyankopɔn bɛyɛ bɛ hann na bebedi ahene daa daa.

english_verse: Then I was told: These words are true and can be trusted. The Lord God controls the spirits of his prophets, and he is the one who sent his angel to show his servants what must happen very soon.
translation_verse: Afei bɔfoɔ he ka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Saa nsɛm yi yɛ nɔkorɛ a wode wo ho to so a, ɔyɛ. Na Awurade Nyankopɔn a ɔnam adiyifoɔ so kasaeɛ he somaa ne bɔfoɔ sɛ ɔmmɛkyerɛ ne nkoa adeɛ a ɔbɛba nnansa yiaa he.

english_verse: Remember, I am coming soon! God will bless everyone who pays attention to the message of this book.
translation_verse: Hwɛ! Ɔnkyɛre koraa mɛba.” Nhyira nka bɛ a betie adiyisɛm a ɔya saa nwoma yi mu he!

english_verse: My name is John, and I am the one who heard and saw these things. Then after I had heard and seen all this, I knelt down and began to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown it to me.
translation_verse: Na me, Yohane, mene mehuu nnoɔma yi na meteeɛ. Na meteeɛ na mehuiɛ he, mehwee ase sɛ meekoto asɔre bɔfoɔ a ɔkyerɛɛ me nnoɔma yi.

english_verse: But the angel said, Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you. I am the same as a follower or a prophet or anyone else who obeys what is written in this book. God is the one you should worship.
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ me sɛ: Nyɔ saa, meyɛ akoa te sɛ wo ne wo nuanom adiyifoɔ, ne bɛ a bedi nwoma yi mu nsɛm mu he soaa pɛ. Nyankopɔn nkoaa ne koto no sɔre no!

english_verse: Don't keep the prophecies in this book a secret. These things will happen soon.
translation_verse: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ me sɛ, “Nsɔ nwoma yi mu adiyisɛm he ano, efiri sɛ berɛ he abɛne sɛ nea bɛtwerɛ ya mu nyinaa ba mu.

english_verse: Evil people will keep on being evil, and everyone who is dirty-minded will still be dirty-minded. But good people will keep on doing right, and God's people will always be holy.
translation_verse: Ee! Deɛ ɔyɔ bɔne he nkɔso nyɔ bɔne ara, na nea ɔyɔ fi he nyɔ fi ara. Na deɛ ɔtene he nkɔso ntene ara, na deɛ ɔyɛ kronkron he nkɔso nyɛ kronkron ara.”

english_verse: Then I was told: I am coming soon! And when I come, I will reward everyone for what they have done.
translation_verse: “Hwɛ! Ɔnkyɛre koraa mɛba! Na mekita m’akatua sɛ mede bra biaa sɛnea n’adwuma teɛ.

english_verse: I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
translation_verse: Mene Alpha ne Omega, kannifoɔ ne kyidifoɔ, mfitiaseɛ ne awieeɛ he.”

english_verse: God will bless all who have washed their robes. They will each have the right to eat fruit from the tree that gives life, and they can enter the gates of the city.
translation_verse: Nhyira nka bɛ a bɛahoro bɛ ntaadeɛ na afiri, na bɛ a bɛwɔ ho kwane sɛ bedi nkwa dua he aba na bɛfa kuro he pono he mu.

english_verse: But outside the city will be dogs, witches, immoral people, murderers, idol worshipers, and everyone who loves to tell lies and do wrong.
translation_verse: Na kuro he akyi ne nkramane ne adutofoɔ ne adwamanfoɔ ne awudifoɔ ne abosonsomfoɔ ne bɛ a bɛpɛ torɔ na bedi he nyinaa ya.

english_verse: I am Jesus! And I am the one who sent my angel to tell all of you these things for the churches. I am David's Great Descendant, and I am also the bright morning star.
translation_verse: “Me, Yesu, ne masoma me bɔfoɔ sɛ ɔmmɛbɔ ho saa nwoma yi nyinaa ho amanneɛ ya asafo mu. Mene Dawid nhini ne n’aseni, mene anɔpa nsoromma a ɔhyerɛn he.”

english_verse: The Spirit and the bride say, “Come!” Everyone who hears this should say, “Come!” If you are thirsty, come! If you want life-giving water, come and take it. It's free!
translation_verse: Honhom he ne ayeforɔ he dɛ, “Bra!” Na biaa a ɔte saa asɛm yi nso ka sɛ, “Bra!” Na deɛ sukɔm de no he mmra; na nea ɔpɛ he nso mmɛgye nkwa nsuo he kwa. Awieeɛ

english_verse: Here is my warning for everyone who hears the prophecies in this book: If you add anything to them, God will make you suffer all the terrible troubles written in this book.
translation_verse: Me, Yohane, mebɔ biaa a ɔte saa adiyisɛm a ɔya nwoma yi mu kɔkɔ sɛ: Sɛ bi de bi ka ho a, Nyankopɔn de asotwe bɛka haw a bɛatwerɛ no saa nwoma yi mu he ho de ama no.

english_verse: If you take anything away from these prophecies, God will not let you have part in the life-giving tree and in the holy city described in this book.
translation_verse: Na sɛ bi nso yi bi firi adiyisɛm a ɔya saa nwoma yi mu he mu a, Nyankopɔn beyi ne kyɛfa a obenya afiri nkwa dua he aba he ne kuro Kronkron a bɛakyerɛkyerɛ mu nwoma yi mu he.

english_verse: The one who has spoken these things says, “I am coming soon!” So, Lord Jesus, please come soon!
translation_verse: Nea odi nwoma yi ho adanseɛ he ka sɛ, “Nɔkorɛ! Ɔnkyɛre koraa mɛba!” Ɔmmra mu saa. Awurade Yesu, bra!

english_verse: I pray that the Lord Jesus will be kind to all of you.
translation_verse: Yɛ Awurade Yesu Kristo adom nka ho nyinaa. Amen.
